attributes.title: Pṛthivī (Earth) — The Ground of Stability
attributes.aspect: Earth is the densest and most manifest of the five elements — the solid state of matter that gives form, shape, and structure to the body. It manifests as bone, muscle, skin, tissue, and hair; its tanmātra is smell (Gandha) and its sense organ the nose; its quality is roughness and solidity (Kharatwa); its bīja mantra is ‘Laṁ,’ and it is seated in the Mūlādhāra (root) chakra. Earth is the ground of stability — the principle of permanence, support, and belonging.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical (somatic) · Ontic mode: Constituent / elemental substrate · Valence: Neutral faculty (balance is the aim) · Guṇa: Tamas-dominant · Scale: Individual (constitutional), with environmental correspondence · Causal status: Material substrate · Key relation: Cross-dimensional correspondence — Mūlādhāra chakra, Kapha dosha (with Water), bīja Laṁ, tanmātra smell.
coordinate.key_relation: Cross-dimensional correspondence — Mūlādhāra chakra, Kapha dosha (with Water), bīja Laṁ, tanmātra smell.

balanced.label: Balanced — the element well-proportioned

facets.overview: Well-proportioned Earth gives groundedness, physical stability, and a secure sense of belonging — the down-to-earth steadiness that brings reliability to any situation and can weather emotional storms without losing its foundation.
facets.somatic: Structural integrity: strong bones and teeth, firm musculature, resilient skin, and a body that feels solid, supported, and at home in itself.
facets.emotional: Rooted, patient, and reliable; able to relate to the material world without being consumed by it, and to stay steady when feelings run high.
facets.cognitive: Practical, detail-oriented, methodical thinking; a stable foundation from which to build coherent understanding and follow things patiently through.
facets.psychic: The soul finds in groundedness a stable seat from which to meet life; rooted in the body and the earth, it can turn toward the depths without fear of losing itself.

balanced.text:
Well-proportioned Earth gives groundedness, physical stability, and a secure sense of belonging — the down-to-earth steadiness that brings reliability to any situation and can weather emotional storms without losing its foundation.

Somatic: Structural integrity: strong bones and teeth, firm musculature, resilient skin, and a body that feels solid, supported, and at home in itself.

Emotional: Rooted, patient, and reliable; able to relate to the material world without being consumed by it, and to stay steady when feelings run high.

Cognitive: Practical, detail-oriented, methodical thinking; a stable foundation from which to build coherent understanding and follow things patiently through.

Psychic: The soul finds in groundedness a stable seat from which to meet life; rooted in the body and the earth, it can turn toward the depths without fear of losing itself.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has run the same small furniture workshop for twenty years, and at the end of a long day he stands among the wood shavings feeling solid, unhurried, and entirely at home in his hands. When a big order falls through, he does not spiral; he sweeps the floor, makes tea, and begins again tomorrow. His steadiness is not stubbornness—it is a ground that others lean on. People say that being near him is like resting against a warm stone wall.

excess.label: In excess — the element overwhelming the system

facets.overview: Disproportionate Earth brings heaviness and stagnation — a density that pervades body, feeling, and mind, leaving the person ‘stuck,’ clinging to the familiar well past its usefulness.
facets.somatic: Weight gain, lethargy, congestion, sluggish digestion (mandāgni), edema, and stiffening joints; the body feels dense, heavy, and resistant to movement (a Kapha vitiation).
facets.emotional: Attachment, possessiveness, dull stubbornness, and a marked resistance to change; the person clings to old routines, relationships, and beliefs.
facets.cognitive: Mental rigidity and fixed thinking; a fog of dullness settles over the mind, learning slows, creativity stagnates, and new perspectives feel impossible to entertain.
facets.psychic: The soul over-identifies with form and security, clinging to the solid and the familiar; weighed down by matter, it forgets it is more than the ground it stands on.

excess.text:
Disproportionate Earth brings heaviness and stagnation — a density that pervades body, feeling, and mind, leaving the person ‘stuck,’ clinging to the familiar well past its usefulness.

Somatic: Weight gain, lethargy, congestion, sluggish digestion (mandāgni), edema, and stiffening joints; the body feels dense, heavy, and resistant to movement (a Kapha vitiation).

Emotional: Attachment, possessiveness, dull stubbornness, and a marked resistance to change; the person clings to old routines, relationships, and beliefs.

Cognitive: Mental rigidity and fixed thinking; a fog of dullness settles over the mind, learning slows, creativity stagnates, and new perspectives feel impossible to entertain.

Psychic: The soul over-identifies with form and security, clinging to the solid and the familiar; weighed down by matter, it forgets it is more than the ground it stands on.
excess.vignette: Tom has sat in the same chair, in the same room, watching the same evening news for fifteen years, and the mere idea of change—a new job, a different city, even rearranging the furniture—brings a physical heaviness to his chest. His daughter suggests a trip; he finds nine reasons to stay home. The routines that once steadied him have set like concrete, and anything new now feels less like possibility than threat. He calls it being grounded; it has quietly become being stuck.

deficient.label: Deficient — the element depleted

facets.overview: Depleted Earth removes the ground from under the person — a loss of anchoring that leaves body and psyche unsupported and insecure.
facets.somatic: Low bone density, weak teeth and structural tissues, instability in the legs and feet, and a pervasive sense of being physically ‘unanchored’ (osteoporosis, joint instability).
facets.emotional: Insecurity, rootlessness, anxiety, and difficulty trusting others or the world; an underactive Mūlādhāra produces fearfulness and a persistent sense of not belonging, driving a constant search for external validation.
facets.cognitive: Scattered, unanchored thinking with no stable foundation from which to build; difficulty settling or following a line of thought to completion.
facets.psychic: Cut off from its base, the soul feels it has no place in the world; without roots it cannot rest, and the inner work has no foundation on which to build.

deficient.text:
Depleted Earth removes the ground from under the person — a loss of anchoring that leaves body and psyche unsupported and insecure.

Somatic: Low bone density, weak teeth and structural tissues, instability in the legs and feet, and a pervasive sense of being physically ‘unanchored’ (osteoporosis, joint instability).

Emotional: Insecurity, rootlessness, anxiety, and difficulty trusting others or the world; an underactive Mūlādhāra produces fearfulness and a persistent sense of not belonging, driving a constant search for external validation.

Cognitive: Scattered, unanchored thinking with no stable foundation from which to build; difficulty settling or following a line of thought to completion.

Psychic: Cut off from its base, the soul feels it has no place in the world; without roots it cannot rest, and the inner work has no foundation on which to build.
deficient.vignette: Since the divorce, Mara has moved four times in three years, and half the boxes in the new apartment are still taped shut. Nothing feels like hers; the floor never seems quite solid underfoot. She over-researches every small decision, checks the locks twice, and lies awake with a low hum of not-belonging. What she is missing is not a place but a ground—some inner floor to stand on that no new address has yet given her.

attributes.title: Apas (Water) — The Flow of Feeling
attributes.aspect: Water is the liquid state of matter — the element of fluidity, cohesion, and binding. It manifests as plasma, blood, lymph, reproductive fluids, fat, urine, and saliva; its tanmātra is taste (Rasa) and its sense organ the tongue; its qualities are adhesion, cooling, and liquidity; its bīja mantra is ‘Vaṁ,’ and it is seated in the Svādhiṣṭhāna (sacral) chakra, associated with joy and well-being. Water is the flow of feeling — the principle that carries, cleanses, and connects.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital (somatic fluids / emotional flow) · Ontic mode: Constituent / elemental substrate · Valence: Neutral faculty (balance is the aim) · Guṇa: Sattva + Tamas · Scale: Individual (constitutional), with environmental correspondence · Causal status: Material substrate · Key relation: Cross-dimensional correspondence — Svādhiṣṭhāna chakra, Kapha dosha (with Earth), bīja Vaṁ, tanmātra taste.
coordinate.key_relation: Cross-dimensional correspondence — Svādhiṣṭhāna chakra, Kapha dosha (with Earth), bīja Vaṁ, tanmātra taste.

balanced.label: Balanced — the element well-proportioned

facets.overview: Balanced Water gives emotional fluidity — the capacity to feel deeply without being overwhelmed, to adapt with grace, and to sustain healthy intimacy and creative expression.
facets.somatic: Fluids circulate properly: smooth digestion, sound reproductive health, supple tissues, and skin with a healthy luster.
facets.emotional: Joy, sensuality, empathy, and creative vitality; deep feeling that flows and connects without excessive attachment.
facets.cognitive: Fluid, adaptive reasoning and a gift for deep, patient analysis — moving through complexity with cohesion and care.
facets.psychic: The soul lets feeling flow and move it without being swept away; emotion becomes a current that carries it toward connection and creative life rather than a flood that drowns it.

balanced.text:
Balanced Water gives emotional fluidity — the capacity to feel deeply without being overwhelmed, to adapt with grace, and to sustain healthy intimacy and creative expression.

Somatic: Fluids circulate properly: smooth digestion, sound reproductive health, supple tissues, and skin with a healthy luster.

Emotional: Joy, sensuality, empathy, and creative vitality; deep feeling that flows and connects without excessive attachment.

Cognitive: Fluid, adaptive reasoning and a gift for deep, patient analysis — moving through complexity with cohesion and care.

Psychic: The soul lets feeling flow and move it without being swept away; emotion becomes a current that carries it toward connection and creative life rather than a flood that drowns it.
balanced.vignette: When her friend’s mother died, Leena simply sat with her for an afternoon—feeling the grief alongside her, letting the tears come and go, present and soft without being pulled under. She could hold the sorrow because it moved through her like a current rather than a flood. Later she made soup and they even laughed a little, the feeling having done its quiet work. Her warmth connects without drowning; people bring her their hardest days.

excess.label: In excess — the element overwhelming the system

facets.overview: Excess Water creates emotional flooding and waterlogged heaviness — the person drowns in feeling and cannot easily release it.
facets.somatic: Edema, fluid retention, excessive mucus and congestion, heaviness and coldness in the lower abdomen, sluggish lymphatic function, and weakened digestive fire with accumulation of āma (a Kapha vitiation).
facets.emotional: Hypersensitivity, sentimentality, over-attachment, and clinging in relationships; grief and melancholy that become persistent and hard to release; an overactive Svādhiṣṭhāna with emotional and sexual preoccupation.
facets.cognitive: Over-analysis and rumination — ‘paralysis by analysis’ — the mind clouded and slowed, unable to come to a clear conclusion.
facets.psychic: The soul is submerged in its own feeling, identity dissolving into sentiment and attachment; it mistakes the intensity of emotion for the depth of being.

excess.text:
Excess Water creates emotional flooding and waterlogged heaviness — the person drowns in feeling and cannot easily release it.

Somatic: Edema, fluid retention, excessive mucus and congestion, heaviness and coldness in the lower abdomen, sluggish lymphatic function, and weakened digestive fire with accumulation of āma (a Kapha vitiation).

Emotional: Hypersensitivity, sentimentality, over-attachment, and clinging in relationships; grief and melancholy that become persistent and hard to release; an overactive Svādhiṣṭhāna with emotional and sexual preoccupation.

Cognitive: Over-analysis and rumination — ‘paralysis by analysis’ — the mind clouded and slowed, unable to come to a clear conclusion.

Psychic: The soul is submerged in its own feeling, identity dissolving into sentiment and attachment; it mistakes the intensity of emotion for the depth of being.
excess.vignette: Daniel cries at the insurance advert with the old dog, then at a half-remembered song, then at a text from someone he left years ago. Every feeling arrives at full volume and lingers well past its welcome; he is forever awash, unable to tell today’s sadness from a decade of stored-up ones. Friends have learned that any conversation may turn into a tide. The tenderness that is his gift has become a sea with no shore.

deficient.label: Deficient — the element depleted

facets.overview: Depleted Water brings dryness and rigidity — a barrenness in body and feeling where flow should be.
facets.somatic: Dry skin, parched mouth, creaking joints, dehydration, strained elimination, and impaired taste (which depends directly on the water element).
facets.emotional: Coldness, distance, and emotional dryness — an inability to feel deeply, connect empathically, or experience the joy that is balanced Water’s signature; an underactive Svādhiṣṭhāna producing impassivity and withdrawal.
facets.cognitive: Rigid, brittle thinking that lacks the fluidity needed for creative problem-solving and adaptive reasoning.
facets.psychic: The soul’s feeling-life runs dry; walled off from tenderness and joy, it cannot connect, and the heart’s natural sweetness is forgotten.

deficient.text:
Depleted Water brings dryness and rigidity — a barrenness in body and feeling where flow should be.

Somatic: Dry skin, parched mouth, creaking joints, dehydration, strained elimination, and impaired taste (which depends directly on the water element).

Emotional: Coldness, distance, and emotional dryness — an inability to feel deeply, connect empathically, or experience the joy that is balanced Water’s signature; an underactive Svādhiṣṭhāna producing impassivity and withdrawal.

Cognitive: Rigid, brittle thinking that lacks the fluidity needed for creative problem-solving and adaptive reasoning.

Psychic: The soul’s feeling-life runs dry; walled off from tenderness and joy, it cannot connect, and the heart’s natural sweetness is forgotten.
deficient.vignette: At his father’s funeral, Karan stood dry-eyed and composed while everyone around him wept, feeling not grief so much as a glassy distance—as though watching the scene through a window. He wanted to feel it; he simply could not reach the water. In his relationships it is the same: he registers that he ought to be moved and finds only a cool, level surface. The heart’s flow has run dry, and he has forgotten the way back to it.

attributes.title: Agni (Fire) — The Flame of Transformation
attributes.aspect: Fire is the element of transformation, penetration, and illumination — governing digestion, metabolism, and the conversion of experience into understanding. It manifests as hunger, thirst, body heat, and the light of perception; its tanmātra is form/vision (Rūpa) and its sense organ the eye; its bīja mantra is ‘Raṁ,’ and it is seated in the Maṇipūra (solar plexus) chakra, associated with wisdom and personal power. Personified as Agni Deva, fire is both life-giver and life-taker — indispensable, and dangerous when uncontrolled.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Mental (metabolic heat / discernment) · Ontic mode: Constituent / transformative principle · Valence: Neutral faculty (balance is the aim) · Guṇa: Sattva + Rajas · Scale: Individual (constitutional), with environmental correspondence · Causal status: Transformative agent (efficient-causal principle) · Key relation: Cross-dimensional correspondence — Maṇipūra chakra, Pitta dosha, bīja Raṁ, tanmātra form/vision.
coordinate.key_relation: Cross-dimensional correspondence — Maṇipūra chakra, Pitta dosha, bīja Raṁ, tanmātra form/vision.

balanced.label: Balanced — the element well-proportioned

facets.overview: Balanced Fire produces clear vision, sharp discrimination, and decisive action — the warmth and natural authority that come from inner clarity rather than domination.
facets.somatic: Strong digestion and metabolism (balanced Jāṭharāgni), good appetite, clear eyesight, and the capacity to transform food and experience into nourishment.
facets.emotional: Healthy self-esteem, courage, warmth, and the passion to inspire; the fire of discernment that cuts through doubt and insecurity with clarity.
facets.cognitive: Keen discrimination, clear perception, and the ability to decide and illuminate situations — turning experience into wisdom.
facets.psychic: The soul’s inner flame illumines without consuming; discernment and warmth let it transform experience into wisdom and meet the world with clear, steady purpose.

balanced.text:
Balanced Fire produces clear vision, sharp discrimination, and decisive action — the warmth and natural authority that come from inner clarity rather than domination.

Somatic: Strong digestion and metabolism (balanced Jāṭharāgni), good appetite, clear eyesight, and the capacity to transform food and experience into nourishment.

Emotional: Healthy self-esteem, courage, warmth, and the passion to inspire; the fire of discernment that cuts through doubt and insecurity with clarity.

Cognitive: Keen discrimination, clear perception, and the ability to decide and illuminate situations — turning experience into wisdom.

Psychic: The soul’s inner flame illumines without consuming; discernment and warmth let it transform experience into wisdom and meet the world with clear, steady purpose.
balanced.vignette: Professor Iyer can take a muddled, frightened student and, in ten minutes, make the hard idea clear—his warmth and his sharpness working together, cutting through the confusion without cutting the person. He decides quickly and cleanly, and his enthusiasm lights the room. There is real heat in him, but it warms rather than scorches. Students leave his office both clearer and braver than they arrived.

excess.label: In excess — the element overwhelming the system

facets.overview: Excess Fire creates burning — literal and figurative — a consuming intensity that scorches body, relationships, and peace of mind.
facets.somatic: Inflammation, hyperacidity, skin rashes, fevers, burning sensations, and a digestive fire so sharp (Tīkṣṇa Agni) it begins to consume the body’s own tissues (an aggravated Pitta).
facets.emotional: Irritability, anger, jealousy, impatience, aggression, obsession, and egotism; ambition that consumes relationships and inner peace; an overactive Maṇipūra turned imperious and domineering.
facets.cognitive: An overly critical, judgmental mind that demands perfection and control — a compulsive need to dominate every thought and decision.
facets.psychic: The soul’s fire turns devouring; driven to control, dominate, and burn through all resistance, it consumes its own peace and the bonds around it.

excess.text:
Excess Fire creates burning — literal and figurative — a consuming intensity that scorches body, relationships, and peace of mind.

Somatic: Inflammation, hyperacidity, skin rashes, fevers, burning sensations, and a digestive fire so sharp (Tīkṣṇa Agni) it begins to consume the body’s own tissues (an aggravated Pitta).

Emotional: Irritability, anger, jealousy, impatience, aggression, obsession, and egotism; ambition that consumes relationships and inner peace; an overactive Maṇipūra turned imperious and domineering.

Cognitive: An overly critical, judgmental mind that demands perfection and control — a compulsive need to dominate every thought and decision.

Psychic: The soul’s fire turns devouring; driven to control, dominate, and burn through all resistance, it consumes its own peace and the bonds around it.
excess.vignette: Nina’s brilliance has developed an edge that draws blood. In meetings she is the sharpest mind and the most feared—impatient with the slow, contemptuous of the imperfect, correcting, dominating, and burning through her third assistant this year. Her stomach is a furnace of acid; her sleep is thin and hot. The fire that once made her formidable is now consuming the very things—and people—it was meant to illuminate.

deficient.label: Deficient — the element depleted

facets.overview: Weak Fire dims the inner light — digestion, motivation, and discrimination all falter, and disease finds its opening.
facets.somatic: Poor metabolism, loss of appetite, sluggish digestion (Manda Agni), accumulation of toxins, and poor nutrient absorption — the state most likely, in Āyurveda, to breed disease.
facets.emotional: Low self-esteem, lack of passion and warmth, reluctance, and a directionless deferring to others; an inability to assert one’s needs or values.
facets.cognitive: Confusion, dullness of intellect, and an impaired capacity to discriminate or perceive clearly — since perception itself depends on the fire element.
facets.psychic: The inner light dims; without the flame of discrimination and will, the soul drifts directionless, unable to decide, transform, or assert its own truth.

deficient.text:
Weak Fire dims the inner light — digestion, motivation, and discrimination all falter, and disease finds its opening.

Somatic: Poor metabolism, loss of appetite, sluggish digestion (Manda Agni), accumulation of toxins, and poor nutrient absorption — the state most likely, in Āyurveda, to breed disease.

Emotional: Low self-esteem, lack of passion and warmth, reluctance, and a directionless deferring to others; an inability to assert one’s needs or values.

Cognitive: Confusion, dullness of intellect, and an impaired capacity to discriminate or perceive clearly — since perception itself depends on the fire element.

Psychic: The inner light dims; without the flame of discrimination and will, the soul drifts directionless, unable to decide, transform, or assert its own truth.
deficient.vignette: Sameer has been ‘about to start’ for months. The idea is good, he knows it is, but he cannot seem to strike the match—no appetite, no drive, decisions left to rot on the vine. His digestion is as sluggish as his motivation; food and plans alike sit heavy and unconverted. The inner flame that turns intention into action has guttered low, and the whole world has gone grey and directionless.

attributes.title: Vāyu (Air) — The Breath of Movement
attributes.aspect: Air is the element of motion, force, and kinetic energy — all movement and the forces that produce it. It manifests as breathing, walking, nerve impulses, peristalsis, and all muscular contraction and expansion; its tanmātra is touch (Sparsha), its sense organ the skin, its organ of action the hand; its bīja mantra is ‘Yaṁ,’ and it is seated in the Anāhata (heart) chakra, associated with compassion. Air is the breath of movement — cerebral, mobile, and intimately linked to the mind and the nervous system.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital (movement / vital current) · Ontic mode: Constituent / kinetic principle · Valence: Neutral faculty (balance is the aim) · Guṇa: Rajas-dominant · Scale: Individual (constitutional), with environmental correspondence · Causal status: Kinetic agent (the mover; governs all movement) · Key relation: Cross-dimensional correspondence — Anāhata chakra, Vāta dosha (with Space), bīja Yaṁ, tanmātra touch.
coordinate.causal_status: Kinetic agent (the mover; governs all movement)
coordinate.key_relation: Cross-dimensional correspondence — Anāhata chakra, Vāta dosha (with Space), bīja Yaṁ, tanmātra touch.

balanced.label: Balanced — the element well-proportioned

facets.overview: Balanced Air gives freedom and lightness — movement through life with grace, adaptability, and openness, and a heart that gives and receives love without grasping.
facets.somatic: A smoothly functioning nervous system, deep and steady breath, easy circulation, and supple, coordinated movement.
facets.emotional: Lightness, motivation, and a great capacity for love without attachment — warmth that extends to all beings; an open, balanced Anāhata.
facets.cognitive: Mental agility, innovation, and the free flow of ideas; a mind that is quick, flexible, and able to shift perspective.
facets.psychic: The soul moves with lightness and grace, open to change, giving and receiving freely; the breath that steadies the body becomes the medium of an unattached, flowing love.

balanced.text:
Balanced Air gives freedom and lightness — movement through life with grace, adaptability, and openness, and a heart that gives and receives love without grasping.

Somatic: A smoothly functioning nervous system, deep and steady breath, easy circulation, and supple, coordinated movement.

Emotional: Lightness, motivation, and a great capacity for love without attachment — warmth that extends to all beings; an open, balanced Anāhata.

Cognitive: Mental agility, innovation, and the free flow of ideas; a mind that is quick, flexible, and able to shift perspective.

Psychic: The soul moves with lightness and grace, open to change, giving and receiving freely; the breath that steadies the body becomes the medium of an unattached, flowing love.
balanced.vignette: In the studio, Priya is in flow—ideas arriving quick and light, one sparking the next, her hands keeping pace with a mind that is agile without being scattered. She pivots easily when the plan changes and improvises when the brief shifts, buoyant through all of it. The lightness is a gift, not a symptom. By evening she has made three good things and genuinely enjoyed the making.

excess.label: In excess — the element overwhelming the system

facets.overview: Excess Air produces scattering, instability, and anxiety — the Vāta imbalance whose derangement, governing all movement, disturbs every other system.
facets.somatic: Dryness, tremors, twitches, joint pain and hypermobility, irregular or rapid heartbeat, variable digestion (Viṣamāgni), constipation, and broken sleep; the body grows light, cold, and rough.
facets.emotional: Anxiety, fear, restlessness, and a sense of being scattered and uncontained — as though uprooted and blown about by forces beyond control; an overactive Anāhata can produce a suffocating, obsessive love.
facets.cognitive: Racing thoughts, inability to focus, cognitive fragmentation, and a mind that jumps from idea to idea without completion — mental overload from too much stimulation.
facets.psychic: The soul scatters, uprooted and blown about, unable to land or contain itself; restless movement becomes anxiety, and it loses the still center from which to act.

excess.text:
Excess Air produces scattering, instability, and anxiety — the Vāta imbalance whose derangement, governing all movement, disturbs every other system.

Somatic: Dryness, tremors, twitches, joint pain and hypermobility, irregular or rapid heartbeat, variable digestion (Viṣamāgni), constipation, and broken sleep; the body grows light, cold, and rough.

Emotional: Anxiety, fear, restlessness, and a sense of being scattered and uncontained — as though uprooted and blown about by forces beyond control; an overactive Anāhata can produce a suffocating, obsessive love.

Cognitive: Racing thoughts, inability to focus, cognitive fragmentation, and a mind that jumps from idea to idea without completion — mental overload from too much stimulation.

Psychic: The soul scatters, uprooted and blown about, unable to land or contain itself; restless movement becomes anxiety, and it loses the still center from which to act.
excess.vignette: It is 3 a.m. and Anil’s mind is a wind tunnel—the same worries circling, thoughts leaping from a bill to an email to an imagined argument, none of them ever landing. His hands are cold, his chest tight, his body as restless as his head. He gets up, sits down, checks his phone, cannot settle. The mental quickness that serves him by day has become, by night, a gale he cannot switch off.

deficient.label: Deficient — the element depleted

facets.overview: Depleted Air brings stagnation of movement — the person feels stuck, unable to change, adapt, or open.
facets.somatic: Reduced circulation and range of motion, slowed nerve impulses, shallow mechanical breath, and stalled vitality (with the danger of ischemia from blocked flow).
facets.emotional: Emotional flatness, apathy, coldness, and a rigidity that prevents the heart from opening; an underactive Anāhata leaving the person unfriendly and unable to access genuine connection.
facets.cognitive: Mental sluggishness and an inability to think creatively or shift perspective; the lightness and agility of air are lost.
facets.psychic: The soul stagnates; without movement the heart cannot open and creativity stalls, and a cold rigidity replaces the free flow of life and love.

deficient.text:
Depleted Air brings stagnation of movement — the person feels stuck, unable to change, adapt, or open.

Somatic: Reduced circulation and range of motion, slowed nerve impulses, shallow mechanical breath, and stalled vitality (with the danger of ischemia from blocked flow).

Emotional: Emotional flatness, apathy, coldness, and a rigidity that prevents the heart from opening; an underactive Anāhata leaving the person unfriendly and unable to access genuine connection.

Cognitive: Mental sluggishness and an inability to think creatively or shift perspective; the lightness and agility of air are lost.

Psychic: The soul stagnates; without movement the heart cannot open and creativity stalls, and a cold rigidity replaces the free flow of life and love.
deficient.vignette: George does the same commute, eats the same lunch, thinks the same three thoughts, and cannot remember the last time an idea genuinely surprised him. A change of perspective feels like heavy lifting; his joints and his imagination are equally stiff. When the team needs a fresh angle, his mind simply will not leave the ground. The air has gone still in him—and stillness, past a point, is not calm but stagnation.

attributes.title: Ākāśa (Space/Ether) — The Container of Possibility
attributes.aspect: Space is the subtlest and most pervasive element — not empty space but ether, the container within which all other elements operate. It is present in the body’s voids — nostrils, mouth, abdomen, channels, and pores; its tanmātra is sound (Shabda) and its sense organ the ear; its quality is non-resistance (Apratighatatwa); its bīja mantra is ‘Haṁ,’ and it governs the upper centers — Vishuddhi (throat), Ājñā (third eye), and Sahasrāra (crown). The Brahmavidya names its fivefold human aspects as desire, aversion, shame, fear, and forgetfulness. Space is the field of possibility — the room in which everything else can be.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic (subtle field / higher awareness) · Ontic mode: Constituent / container-field principle · Valence: Neutral faculty (balance is the aim) · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, with cosmic correspondence · Causal status: Field / enabling ground (the room within which the others operate) · Key relation: Cross-dimensional correspondence — Vishuddhi/Ājñā/Sahasrāra chakras, Vāta dosha (with Air), bīja Haṁ, tanmātra sound.
coordinate.causal_status: Field / enabling ground (the room within which the others operate)
coordinate.key_relation: Cross-dimensional correspondence — Vishuddhi/Ājñā/Sahasrāra chakras, Vāta dosha (with Air), bīja Haṁ, tanmātra sound.

balanced.label: Balanced — the element well-proportioned

facets.overview: Balanced Space provides the inner room all other elements need — expansiveness without ungroundedness, openness without diffusion, and the capacity for higher perception.
facets.somatic: Open, unobstructed channels and cavities through which nourishment reaches the cells and waste is eliminated; a sense of physical ease and room.
facets.emotional: Trust, wonder, humility, and receptivity — a beginner’s mind that is spacious and at home in itself.
facets.cognitive: Clear communication and higher cognition; attunement to the silent space between thoughts from which thought itself arises — the ground of meditation, with a free Vishuddhi, an insightful Ājñā, and a transcendent Sahasrāra.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in inner spaciousness, the silent room from which thought, word, and insight arise; expansive yet contained, it touches the transcendent without losing the ground.

balanced.text:
Balanced Space provides the inner room all other elements need — expansiveness without ungroundedness, openness without diffusion, and the capacity for higher perception.

Somatic: Open, unobstructed channels and cavities through which nourishment reaches the cells and waste is eliminated; a sense of physical ease and room.

Emotional: Trust, wonder, humility, and receptivity — a beginner’s mind that is spacious and at home in itself.

Cognitive: Clear communication and higher cognition; attunement to the silent space between thoughts from which thought itself arises — the ground of meditation, with a free Vishuddhi, an insightful Ājñā, and a transcendent Sahasrāra.

Psychic: The soul rests in inner spaciousness, the silent room from which thought, word, and insight arise; expansive yet contained, it touches the transcendent without losing the ground.
balanced.vignette: In the early morning, before the house wakes, Meera sits with her tea and rests in the quiet space between thoughts—spacious, receptive, faintly amused by it all. She has room in her for whatever the day will bring; nothing feels crowded. Out of that inner spaciousness comes her uncanny knack for the right word at the right moment. People say she is restful to be around and cannot quite say why.

excess.label: In excess — the element overwhelming the system

facets.overview: Excess Space creates emptiness, dissociation, and disconnection — the person feels uncontained, lost in their own vastness, unable to locate themselves.
facets.somatic: Hollowness and a feeling of physical insubstantiality — emaciation and weakness as excess space intensifies Vāta.
facets.emotional: Pervasive emptiness, loneliness, and disconnection; an overactive Vishuddhi producing excessive talk and poor listening, while an overstimulated Ājñā drifts into fantasy and, in the extreme, hallucination.
facets.cognitive: Mental absence, spaciness, dissociation, and forgetfulness; the mind loses its tether to practical reality.
facets.psychic: The soul dissolves into its own vastness, unmoored and dispersed; lost in abstraction and emptiness, it cannot locate itself or stay tethered to lived reality.

excess.text:
Excess Space creates emptiness, dissociation, and disconnection — the person feels uncontained, lost in their own vastness, unable to locate themselves.

Somatic: Hollowness and a feeling of physical insubstantiality — emaciation and weakness as excess space intensifies Vāta.

Emotional: Pervasive emptiness, loneliness, and disconnection; an overactive Vishuddhi producing excessive talk and poor listening, while an overstimulated Ājñā drifts into fantasy and, in the extreme, hallucination.

Cognitive: Mental absence, spaciness, dissociation, and forgetfulness; the mind loses its tether to practical reality.

Psychic: The soul dissolves into its own vastness, unmoored and dispersed; lost in abstraction and emptiness, it cannot locate itself or stay tethered to lived reality.
excess.vignette: Jonah is, as his flatmates put it, ‘not quite in the room.’ He drifts off mid-sentence, forgets why he walked into the kitchen, loses whole afternoons to a vague, boundless woolgathering. There is a pleasant vastness to it and also a loneliness—he feels unmoored from his own body, spread too thin across too much sky. The practical world keeps sliding out of his grip while he floats somewhere above it.

deficient.label: Deficient — the element depleted

facets.overview: Depleted Space brings a claustrophobia of the soul — constriction, an inability to express, and a cutting-off from higher awareness.
facets.somatic: Narrowed inner spaces and congestion — blocked channels, impeded circulation, and poor elimination of waste.
facets.emotional: Constriction and a blocked Vishuddhi producing shyness, dishonesty, and an inability to voice one’s truth; reliance on others for direction and susceptibility to confusion.
facets.cognitive: A cluttered, congested mind with no room for new information or insight — and no silence from which wisdom can emerge.
facets.psychic: The soul is constricted, walled in by congested thought, cut off from higher awareness; without inner room there is no silence from which wisdom or true expression can emerge.

deficient.text:
Depleted Space brings a claustrophobia of the soul — constriction, an inability to express, and a cutting-off from higher awareness.

Somatic: Narrowed inner spaces and congestion — blocked channels, impeded circulation, and poor elimination of waste.

Emotional: Constriction and a blocked Vishuddhi producing shyness, dishonesty, and an inability to voice one’s truth; reliance on others for direction and susceptibility to confusion.

Cognitive: A cluttered, congested mind with no room for new information or insight — and no silence from which wisdom can emerge.

Psychic: The soul is constricted, walled in by congested thought, cut off from higher awareness; without inner room there is no silence from which wisdom or true expression can emerge.
deficient.vignette: Rina’s calendar has no white space and neither does her mind—every hour booked, every silence filled with a podcast, every gap between tasks resented as waste. There is no room for a new idea to arrive, no pause from which insight might emerge; the congestion is mental and physical at once. She keeps adding and never subtracting, and wonders why she feels so cramped inside a life she chose. What she has lost is the empty space in which anything can breathe.

attributes.title: Sattva (Purity/Goodness)
attributes.aspect: Sattva is the quality of purity, harmony, knowledge, and virtue. It is characterized by clarity, illumination, and a sense of balance. Sattva represents the highest of the three gunas and is associated with wisdom, virtue, and spiritual enlightenment.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic (clarity / luminosity), pervading all layers · Ontic mode: Constituent mode (a guṇa — one of the three fundamental modes of prakṛti) · Valence: Neutral mode, relatively liberating when cultivated — yet itself a subtle bond to be transcended · Guṇa: Sattva (by definition) · Scale: Individual (mental-spiritual prakṛti), with cosmic ground · Causal status: Conditioning mode (reveals; colors perception toward clarity) · Key relation: One of the guṇa triad — antagonist-complement to Rajas and Tamas.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Constituent mode (a guṇa — one of the three fundamental modes of prakṛti)
coordinate.valence: Neutral mode, relatively liberating when cultivated — yet itself a subtle bond to be transcended
coordinate.causal_status: Conditioning mode (reveals; colors perception toward clarity)
coordinate.key_relation: One of the guṇa triad — antagonist-complement to Rajas and Tamas.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When Sattva is balanced, a person experiences mental clarity, emotional stability, good health, virtuous behavior, wisdom, compassion, and inner peace.
facets.somatic: Good health, stable energy levels, natural sleep patterns, clear complexion, strong immune function, light and energizing food preferences (fruits, vegetables, dairy), absence of disease.
facets.emotional: Emotional stability, inner peace, compassion, contentment, equanimity in face of challenges, natural joy and happiness, absence of mood swings, sense of inner fulfillment.
facets.cognitive: Mental clarity, focused concentration, ability to discern truth from falsehood, wisdom in decision-making, spiritual insight, organized thinking, ability to learn and retain knowledge easily.
facets.psychic: The soul rests most easily in sattva, where clarity and lightness let its inner light shine through; yet it remembers even sattva is a veil to pass beyond.

balanced.text:
When Sattva is balanced, a person experiences mental clarity, emotional stability, good health, virtuous behavior, wisdom, compassion, and inner peace.

Somatic: Good health, stable energy levels, natural sleep patterns, clear complexion, strong immune function, light and energizing food preferences (fruits, vegetables, dairy), absence of disease.

Emotional: Emotional stability, inner peace, compassion, contentment, equanimity in face of challenges, natural joy and happiness, absence of mood swings, sense of inner fulfillment.

Cognitive: Mental clarity, focused concentration, ability to discern truth from falsehood, wisdom in decision-making, spiritual insight, organized thinking, ability to learn and retain knowledge easily.

Psychic: The soul rests most easily in sattva, where clarity and lightness let its inner light shine through; yet it remembers even sattva is a veil to pass beyond.
balanced.vignette: On the hospice ward, Sister Anne is the still point everyone turns to. She moves without hurry, sees each situation plainly—what is needed, what is noise—and speaks with a kindness that has no edge of pity. She sleeps well, eats simply, and somehow has room for everyone’s fear without absorbing it. Being near her, families say, is like stepping into a quiet, well-lit room.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive Sattva can lead to spiritual pride, attachment to virtue, and a sense of superiority. It may cause difficulty in taking action when needed.
facets.somatic: Over-sensitivity to physical environment, tendency to over-regulate diet, potential nutritional deficiencies from excessive restriction, fatigue from lack of stimulation, tendency to isolate from physical world, fragility in facing harsh conditions.
facets.emotional: Spiritual arrogance and pride, judgment of those perceived as less pure, detachment from emotional engagement with others, difficulty experiencing joy from worldly activities, emotional coldness masked as enlightenment, superiority complex.
facets.cognitive: Analysis paralysis due to excessive mental refinement, difficulty with practical problem-solving, over-intellectualization of simple issues, detachment from pragmatic thinking, judgment and criticism of others’ choices, inability to engage with complex worldly situations.
facets.psychic: The soul clings to sattvic states — purity, peace, knowledge — as attainments, attached to the very luminosity meant to be transcended.

excess.text:
Excessive Sattva can lead to spiritual pride, attachment to virtue, and a sense of superiority. It may cause difficulty in taking action when needed.

Somatic: Over-sensitivity to physical environment, tendency to over-regulate diet, potential nutritional deficiencies from excessive restriction, fatigue from lack of stimulation, tendency to isolate from physical world, fragility in facing harsh conditions.

Emotional: Spiritual arrogance and pride, judgment of those perceived as less pure, detachment from emotional engagement with others, difficulty experiencing joy from worldly activities, emotional coldness masked as enlightenment, superiority complex.

Cognitive: Analysis paralysis due to excessive mental refinement, difficulty with practical problem-solving, over-intellectualization of simple issues, detachment from pragmatic thinking, judgment and criticism of others’ choices, inability to engage with complex worldly situations.

Psychic: The soul clings to sattvic states — purity, peace, knowledge — as attainments, attached to the very luminosity meant to be transcended.
excess.vignette: Marcus runs a popular meditation channel and has, without noticing, become insufferable. His diet is immaculate, his home minimalist, his opinions on everyone else’s ‘unconsciousness’ freely given. He can no longer make an ordinary decision—too many refinements to weigh—and recoils from anything messy, loud, or worldly. His peace has curdled into a cool superiority, and the purity he prizes has quietly walled him off from other people.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Deficiency of Sattva results in confusion, mental fog, lack of wisdom, poor health, difficulty distinguishing right from wrong.
facets.somatic: Poor health, chronic disease, low energy, lethargy, weak digestion, heavy or unhealthy food preferences (meat, alcohol, stimulants), susceptibility to infections, poor immune function, unclear skin.
facets.emotional: Emotional confusion and instability, lack of inner peace, difficulty with self-compassion, frustration and irritability, emotional heaviness, depression and melancholy, emotional reactivity without understanding.
facets.cognitive: Mental fog and confusion, difficulty concentrating, poor decision-making ability, inability to distinguish right from wrong, lack of wisdom, living according to false beliefs, poor learning capacity, scattered thinking.
facets.psychic: Sattva is absent; the soul lives in the murk of rajas and tamas, with no clarity through which truth could shine.

deficient.text:
Deficiency of Sattva results in confusion, mental fog, lack of wisdom, poor health, difficulty distinguishing right from wrong.

Somatic: Poor health, chronic disease, low energy, lethargy, weak digestion, heavy or unhealthy food preferences (meat, alcohol, stimulants), susceptibility to infections, poor immune function, unclear skin.

Emotional: Emotional confusion and instability, lack of inner peace, difficulty with self-compassion, frustration and irritability, emotional heaviness, depression and melancholy, emotional reactivity without understanding.

Cognitive: Mental fog and confusion, difficulty concentrating, poor decision-making ability, inability to distinguish right from wrong, lack of wisdom, living according to false beliefs, poor learning capacity, scattered thinking.

Psychic: Sattva is absent; the soul lives in the murk of rajas and tamas, with no clarity through which truth could shine.
deficient.vignette: Lately Priya cannot think straight. A grey fog sits over everything; she rereads the same email three times and still misses the point, and small choices—what to say, what to do—feel murky in a way they never used to. Her body is heavy, her sleep poor, her judgment unreliable. It is not sadness exactly; it is the absence of clarity, as though the light she used to think by had been switched off.

attributes.title: Rajas (Activity/Passion)
attributes.aspect: Rajas is the quality of activity, passion, desire, and motion. It is characterized by dynamism, ambition, attachment, and restlessness. Rajas is the force that drives action, creation, and engagement with the world.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (drive / activity), pervading all layers · Ontic mode: Constituent mode (a guṇa) · Valence: Neutral mode — necessary fuel for action, binding when dominant · Guṇa: Rajas (by definition) · Scale: Individual (prakṛti), with cosmic ground · Causal status: Conditioning mode (projects; the impetus to act) · Key relation: One of the guṇa triad — antagonist-complement to Sattva and Tamas.
coordinate.valence: Neutral mode — necessary fuel for action, binding when dominant
coordinate.causal_status: Conditioning mode (projects; the impetus to act)
coordinate.key_relation: One of the guṇa triad — antagonist-complement to Sattva and Tamas.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When Rajas is balanced, a person is motivated, ambitious, and productive. They take appropriate action, pursue goals with enthusiasm, engage meaningfully with work, and balance activity with rest.
facets.somatic: Good energy and vitality, healthy appetite, varied food preferences, good metabolism, strength and stamina for work, balanced sleep patterns, active lifestyle without exhaustion, resilient immune system.
facets.emotional: Enthusiasm and motivation, healthy passion for life, joy in accomplishment, appropriate ambition, confidence in abilities, ability to feel excitement without becoming manic, genuine interest in relationships and work.
facets.cognitive: Clear goals and purposeful thinking, ability to strategize and execute, creative problem-solving, motivated learning, good memory function, ability to focus on desired outcomes, adaptability to changing situations.
facets.psychic: The soul harnesses rajasic energy as fuel, putting drive and passion into the service of a higher aim.

balanced.text:
When Rajas is balanced, a person is motivated, ambitious, and productive. They take appropriate action, pursue goals with enthusiasm, engage meaningfully with work, and balance activity with rest.

Somatic: Good energy and vitality, healthy appetite, varied food preferences, good metabolism, strength and stamina for work, balanced sleep patterns, active lifestyle without exhaustion, resilient immune system.

Emotional: Enthusiasm and motivation, healthy passion for life, joy in accomplishment, appropriate ambition, confidence in abilities, ability to feel excitement without becoming manic, genuine interest in relationships and work.

Cognitive: Clear goals and purposeful thinking, ability to strategize and execute, creative problem-solving, motivated learning, good memory function, ability to focus on desired outcomes, adaptability to changing situations.

Psychic: The soul harnesses rajasic energy as fuel, putting drive and passion into the service of a higher aim.
balanced.vignette: In the good months of the launch, Devi is a controlled fire—up early, focused, shipping real work, genuinely excited by the problem. She pushes hard and then, sensibly, stops: a run, a dinner, a full night’s sleep. The drive serves the aim rather than consuming her. She is ambitious and also, somehow, at ease.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive Rajas leads to constant restlessness, anxiety, worry, greed, anger, jealousy, and addiction to activity. A person becomes driven by desires and attachment to outcomes.
facets.somatic: Hyperactivity, high blood pressure, insomnia and sleep disturbances, rapid heartbeat and palpitations, muscle tension and tremors, frequent infections due to stress, excessive appetite or eating disorders, overstimulation of nervous system, restlessness in body.
facets.emotional: Anxiety and worry, anger and irritability, jealousy and possessiveness, emotional volatility and mood swings, impatience with others, frustration when goals aren’t met, emotional exhaustion from constant striving, inability to relax emotionally.
facets.cognitive: Racing thoughts, scattered attention, inability to focus deeply, obsessive thinking about goals and outcomes, hypervigilance, excessive planning without execution, attachment to results causing suffering, inability to be present, mental exhaustion.
facets.psychic: The soul is consumed by restless activity and craving, driven without pause and never at peace.

excess.text:
Excessive Rajas leads to constant restlessness, anxiety, worry, greed, anger, jealousy, and addiction to activity. A person becomes driven by desires and attachment to outcomes.

Somatic: Hyperactivity, high blood pressure, insomnia and sleep disturbances, rapid heartbeat and palpitations, muscle tension and tremors, frequent infections due to stress, excessive appetite or eating disorders, overstimulation of nervous system, restlessness in body.

Emotional: Anxiety and worry, anger and irritability, jealousy and possessiveness, emotional volatility and mood swings, impatience with others, frustration when goals aren’t met, emotional exhaustion from constant striving, inability to relax emotionally.

Cognitive: Racing thoughts, scattered attention, inability to focus deeply, obsessive thinking about goals and outcomes, hypervigilance, excessive planning without execution, attachment to results causing suffering, inability to be present, mental exhaustion.

Psychic: The soul is consumed by restless activity and craving, driven without pause and never at peace.
excess.vignette: Sanjay has three side projects, forty browser tabs, and a heart rate that never quite settles. He cannot sit through dinner without his leg jittering; he wakes at 4 a.m. with his mind already sprinting; he snaps at the people he loves for being ‘too slow.’ Every goal reached only reveals the next, so he is never, for a single hour, finished. The engine that once powered him now simply will not idle.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Deficiency of Rajas results in laziness, lack of motivation, inability to take action, apathy, lethargy, procrastination, depression, and stagnation.
facets.somatic: Low energy and fatigue, poor appetite or overeating, weak digestion, sluggish metabolism, poor posture and muscle tone, tendency toward sedentary lifestyle, obesity or weight gain, slow recovery from illness, heaviness in body.
facets.emotional: Apathy and lack of interest, depression and melancholy, emotional flatness, lack of enthusiasm for life, inability to feel joy, low self-worth, hopelessness about future, emotional withdrawal from others.
facets.cognitive: Lack of motivation and drive, poor concentration and focus, difficulty making decisions, procrastination and avoidance, poor learning capacity, inability to plan or strategize, sluggish thinking, mental fog and heaviness.
facets.psychic: Rajas is so deficient the soul cannot rouse itself to act at all, sunk in inertia.

deficient.text:
Deficiency of Rajas results in laziness, lack of motivation, inability to take action, apathy, lethargy, procrastination, depression, and stagnation.

Somatic: Low energy and fatigue, poor appetite or overeating, weak digestion, sluggish metabolism, poor posture and muscle tone, tendency toward sedentary lifestyle, obesity or weight gain, slow recovery from illness, heaviness in body.

Emotional: Apathy and lack of interest, depression and melancholy, emotional flatness, lack of enthusiasm for life, inability to feel joy, low self-worth, hopelessness about future, emotional withdrawal from others.

Cognitive: Lack of motivation and drive, poor concentration and focus, difficulty making decisions, procrastination and avoidance, poor learning capacity, inability to plan or strategize, sluggish thinking, mental fog and heaviness.

Psychic: Rajas is so deficient the soul cannot rouse itself to act at all, sunk in inertia.
deficient.vignette: The projects sit abandoned on Ravi’s desk, and he cannot locate the will to lift even one. Days pass in a low hum of ‘later’; the gym membership goes unused, the emails unanswered, the ambitions he once had reduced to a vague ache. It is not that he is resting—rest implies a return. He is becalmed, and the sails will not fill.

attributes.title: Tamas (Inertia/Darkness)
attributes.aspect: Tamas is the quality of inertia, darkness, ignorance, and heaviness. It is characterized by dullness, confusion, destruction, and dissolution. Tamas represents the lowest of the three gunas and clouds the mind.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical (inertia / density), dulling the mind · Ontic mode: Constituent mode (a guṇa) · Valence: Neutral mode — provides rest and stability, obscuring when dominant · Guṇa: Tamas (by definition) · Scale: Individual (prakṛti), with cosmic ground · Causal status: Conditioning mode (veils; inertia and concealment) · Key relation: One of the guṇa triad — antagonist-complement to Sattva and Rajas.
coordinate.valence: Neutral mode — provides rest and stability, obscuring when dominant
coordinate.key_relation: One of the guṇa triad — antagonist-complement to Sattva and Rajas.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When Tamas is minimized and balanced, it provides necessary rest, sleep, and groundedness. The person has adequate rest and is connected to the earth with stability.
facets.somatic: Healthy sleep patterns, good rest and recovery, grounded and stable physical presence, connection to nature and earth, ability to be still when needed, physical resilience, good digestion and elimination, capacity to relax and recover.
facets.emotional: Emotional stability and groundedness, ability to let go of past hurts, acceptance of what cannot be changed, emotional resilience in face of change, calmness and peace, sense of belonging and connection.
facets.cognitive: Ability to rest the mind when needed, acceptance of limitations of knowledge, wisdom in knowing when to act and when to be still, good memory integration, capacity for deep sleep and restoration of mind.
facets.psychic: The soul accepts tamas in its rightful place — as rest, sleep, and ground of stability — without being ruled by it.

balanced.text:
When Tamas is minimized and balanced, it provides necessary rest, sleep, and groundedness. The person has adequate rest and is connected to the earth with stability.

Somatic: Healthy sleep patterns, good rest and recovery, grounded and stable physical presence, connection to nature and earth, ability to be still when needed, physical resilience, good digestion and elimination, capacity to relax and recover.

Emotional: Emotional stability and groundedness, ability to let go of past hurts, acceptance of what cannot be changed, emotional resilience in face of change, calmness and peace, sense of belonging and connection.

Cognitive: Ability to rest the mind when needed, acceptance of limitations of knowledge, wisdom in knowing when to act and when to be still, good memory integration, capacity for deep sleep and restoration of mind.

Psychic: The soul accepts tamas in its rightful place — as rest, sleep, and ground of stability — without being ruled by it.
balanced.vignette: After a hard week, Lena finally lets Sunday be Sunday. She sleeps late without guilt, moves slowly, sits on the porch doing genuinely nothing, and feels her whole system settle toward the ground. By evening she is restored—not sluggish, but replenished. The stillness did exactly what stillness is for.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive Tamas leads to ignorance, confusion, delusion, depression, lethargy, disease, and self-destructive behavior. A person becomes unmotivated and disorganized.
facets.somatic: Heaviness and sluggishness in body, chronic fatigue and lack of energy, poor health and disease, slow metabolism and weight gain, poor digestion and elimination problems, tendency toward addiction (alcohol, drugs), poor hygiene and appearance, weak immune system.
facets.emotional: Depression and hopelessness, emotional numbness and disconnection, despair and dark thoughts, inability to feel joy or pleasure, emotional heaviness and burden, mood of despair and resignation, self-destructive tendencies.
facets.cognitive: Dullness and mental fog, confusion and inability to understand, poor memory and learning capacity, negative and destructive thinking patterns, delusion and false beliefs, ignorance and inability to discriminate, absent-mindedness and forgetfulness.
facets.psychic: The soul is buried in tamas: dullness, inertia, and ignorance darken everything, even the wish for freedom.

excess.text:
Excessive Tamas leads to ignorance, confusion, delusion, depression, lethargy, disease, and self-destructive behavior. A person becomes unmotivated and disorganized.

Somatic: Heaviness and sluggishness in body, chronic fatigue and lack of energy, poor health and disease, slow metabolism and weight gain, poor digestion and elimination problems, tendency toward addiction (alcohol, drugs), poor hygiene and appearance, weak immune system.

Emotional: Depression and hopelessness, emotional numbness and disconnection, despair and dark thoughts, inability to feel joy or pleasure, emotional heaviness and burden, mood of despair and resignation, self-destructive tendencies.

Cognitive: Dullness and mental fog, confusion and inability to understand, poor memory and learning capacity, negative and destructive thinking patterns, delusion and false beliefs, ignorance and inability to discriminate, absent-mindedness and forgetfulness.

Psychic: The soul is buried in tamas: dullness, inertia, and ignorance darken everything, even the wish for freedom.
excess.vignette: The curtains have been drawn for days. Tom rises only to return to bed; the dishes tower; showering feels like an expedition. A heavy fog has settled over body and mind alike, and even the wish to change it has gone dim. Everything is an effort and nothing seems worth the effort—the particular gravity of too much tamas, which pulls a person down and then hides the rope.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Deficiency of Tamas results in inability to rest or sleep, excessive mental activity, inability to ground oneself, anxiety, and disconnection from the body.
facets.somatic: Insomnia and sleep disturbances, hyperactivity and restlessness, disconnection from physical body, fragility and poor grounding, anxiety-related physical symptoms, tension in muscles, inability to be still, overstimulation of nervous system.
facets.emotional: Anxiety and nervousness, inability to feel calm, emotional instability and reactivity, excessive emotional sensitivity, difficulty with acceptance, racing emotions, disconnection from grounding emotions.
facets.cognitive: Overactive mind, racing thoughts, inability to stop thinking, difficulty focusing on one thing, excessive idealism disconnected from reality, inability to sleep due to mental activity, overthinking and analysis paralysis.
facets.psychic: With too little tamas the soul cannot rest or sleep, ungrounded and unable to stop.

deficient.text:
Deficiency of Tamas results in inability to rest or sleep, excessive mental activity, inability to ground oneself, anxiety, and disconnection from the body.

Somatic: Insomnia and sleep disturbances, hyperactivity and restlessness, disconnection from physical body, fragility and poor grounding, anxiety-related physical symptoms, tension in muscles, inability to be still, overstimulation of nervous system.

Emotional: Anxiety and nervousness, inability to feel calm, emotional instability and reactivity, excessive emotional sensitivity, difficulty with acceptance, racing emotions, disconnection from grounding emotions.

Cognitive: Overactive mind, racing thoughts, inability to stop thinking, difficulty focusing on one thing, excessive idealism disconnected from reality, inability to sleep due to mental activity, overthinking and analysis paralysis.

Psychic: With too little tamas the soul cannot rest or sleep, ungrounded and unable to stop.
deficient.vignette: It is 2 a.m. and Nadia’s body will not power down. She has been ‘about to sleep’ for three hours; the mind races, the muscles will not release, and lying still feels intolerable, like being asked to hold her breath. She is exhausted and wired at once, ungrounded, unable to find the off-switch other people seem to take for granted. Without enough of tamas’s ballast, she cannot come to rest.

attributes.title: Abhibhüya (Inner Battle of Gunas)
attributes.aspect: Abhibhüya is the state of inner conflict between the gunas (sattva, rajas, tamas) where each tries to displace or neutralize the others. It is the perpetual psychological battle within the human system as the qualities compete for dominance.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic (the inner theater of contention) · Ontic mode: Process (the shifting dominance among the guṇas, moment to moment) · Valence: Neutral dynamic — witnessing it is the practice · Guṇa: all three in flux (a rajasic dynamic) · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Mechanism (how one’s dominant mode rises and yields) · Key relation: The guṇa triad in motion — the dynamic relation among the three.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Process (the shifting dominance among the guṇas, moment to moment)
coordinate.valence: Neutral dynamic — witnessing it is the practice
coordinate.guna: all three in flux (a rajasic dynamic)
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (how one’s dominant mode rises and yields)
coordinate.key_relation: The guṇa triad in motion — the dynamic relation among the three.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When understood, this perpetual battle of gunas can be observed without identification. The wise person watches the inner shifts without being a participant, transcending the conflict.
facets.somatic: Awareness of body’s shifting energy states without being controlled, physical adaptability across guna shifts, healthy integration of states.
facets.emotional: Emotional awareness of shifting inner states, ability to observe internal conflict without being swept, emotional witness consciousness, peace amid changes.
facets.cognitive: Clear mental awareness of inner battles between qualities, witness consciousness of the gunas, mental detachment from the war within, wisdom about guna play.
facets.psychic: The soul watches the gunas contend within it, and by awareness gives sattva the upper hand.

balanced.text:
When understood, this perpetual battle of gunas can be observed without identification. The wise person watches the inner shifts without being a participant, transcending the conflict.

Somatic: Awareness of body’s shifting energy states without being controlled, physical adaptability across guna shifts, healthy integration of states.

Emotional: Emotional awareness of shifting inner states, ability to observe internal conflict without being swept, emotional witness consciousness, peace amid changes.

Cognitive: Clear mental awareness of inner battles between qualities, witness consciousness of the gunas, mental detachment from the war within, wisdom about guna play.

Psychic: The soul watches the gunas contend within it, and by awareness gives sattva the upper hand.
balanced.vignette: Aisha has learned, over years, to watch her own inner weather rather than to be it. ‘Ah—rajas is up tonight,’ she notes when the restlessness comes, and does not take the bait; ‘tamas by afternoon,’ and she plans around it. The three forces still contend inside her, as they do in everyone, but she sits a little behind the contest now, and from that seat clarity tends to win. She is not free of the storm; she has simply found the eye of it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: When Abhibhüya is dominant and unconscious, the person is tormented by the constant battle within. They feel pulled in multiple directions, exhausted by inner conflict.
facets.somatic: Physical exhaustion from internal energetic warfare, body torn between drives (rest vs action), inability to find physical equilibrium, somatic stress.
facets.emotional: Constant emotional turmoil from internal conflict, feeling pulled in multiple directions, emotional exhaustion from inner war, inability to feel peace.
facets.cognitive: Mental conflict and indecision, being pulled by competing inner forces, exhausting mental warfare, inability to find consistent direction.
facets.psychic: The soul is torn by the war of the gunas, identified with whichever quality momentarily wins.

excess.text:
When Abhibhüya is dominant and unconscious, the person is tormented by the constant battle within. They feel pulled in multiple directions, exhausted by inner conflict.

Somatic: Physical exhaustion from internal energetic warfare, body torn between drives (rest vs action), inability to find physical equilibrium, somatic stress.

Emotional: Constant emotional turmoil from internal conflict, feeling pulled in multiple directions, emotional exhaustion from inner war, inability to feel peace.

Cognitive: Mental conflict and indecision, being pulled by competing inner forces, exhausting mental warfare, inability to find consistent direction.

Psychic: The soul is torn by the war of the gunas, identified with whichever quality momentarily wins.
excess.vignette: One hour Ben is fired with ambition, the next flattened and hopeless, the next racked with guilt over both—pulled in three directions by forces he cannot name and does not observe. The inner argument never resolves and never quiets; he is exhausted not by any task but by the war itself. By nightfall he feels wrung out, having gone nowhere, torn all day between selves that will not agree.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total dominance of one guna with no shifting represents stagnation. Even pure tamas creates lifeless stagnation; without any shifting, growth becomes impossible.
facets.somatic: Stagnant physical state, no natural shifts in energy, locked into one mode (typically tamasic dullness).
facets.emotional: Emotional stagnation, no shifting between states, locked into one emotional pattern, inability to experience the full range.
facets.cognitive: Mental stagnation in one mode, inability to access different qualities, locked thinking patterns, lack of dynamic mental life.
facets.psychic: The soul never notices the gunas at all, swept unconsciously by whichever force dominates.

deficient.text:
Total dominance of one guna with no shifting represents stagnation. Even pure tamas creates lifeless stagnation; without any shifting, growth becomes impossible.

Somatic: Stagnant physical state, no natural shifts in energy, locked into one mode (typically tamasic dullness).

Emotional: Emotional stagnation, no shifting between states, locked into one emotional pattern, inability to experience the full range.

Cognitive: Mental stagnation in one mode, inability to access different qualities, locked thinking patterns, lack of dynamic mental life.

Psychic: The soul never notices the gunas at all, swept unconsciously by whichever force dominates.
deficient.vignette: For a decade now, nothing in George’s inner life has moved. He is locked in a single grey register—no spark of drive, no clarity, no shift, just the same dull weather every day. It should perhaps be restful; instead it is a kind of death-in-life, because where nothing changes, nothing can grow. Even a storm would be a mercy; the total absence of any inner motion has become its own stagnation.

attributes.title: Three Gunas as Daily Veils
attributes.aspect: The Bhagavata’s teaching: the Divine is hidden behind three gunas which veil. In daily life: develop guna-awareness. Notice moment-to-moment: am I in rajas (restless, ambitious, agitated), tamas (lazy, confused, dull), or sattva (clear, calm, discerning)? Daily food choices, company, activities each strengthen one guna. Conscious choices shift guna-balance over time.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic (the veiling of the Self) · Ontic mode: Condition (the guṇas functioning as veils over awareness) · Valence: Affliction — as veils, to be seen through · Guṇa: all three (each veils in its own way) · Scale: Individual, with cosmic ground · Causal status: Mechanism of concealment · Key relation: Correspondence — the guṇas veil Brahman; complements the reveal-project-veil schema.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Condition (the guṇas functioning as veils over awareness)
coordinate.valence: Affliction — as veils, to be seen through
coordinate.guna: all three (each veils in its own way)
coordinate.key_relation: Correspondence — the guṇas veil Brahman; complements the reveal-project-veil schema.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Active awareness of guna-states. Daily choices consciously shifting toward sattva. Recognition without rigidity.
facets.somatic: Body’s food, sleep, activity chosen to cultivate sattva.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns recognized as guna-driven.
facets.cognitive: Mind aware of guna-dominance moment to moment.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the three gunas as veils over the Divine, and learns to see the light through and beyond them.

balanced.text:
Active awareness of guna-states. Daily choices consciously shifting toward sattva. Recognition without rigidity.

Somatic: Body’s food, sleep, activity chosen to cultivate sattva.

Emotional: Emotional patterns recognized as guna-driven.

Cognitive: Mind aware of guna-dominance moment to moment.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the three gunas as veils over the Divine, and learns to see the light through and beyond them.
balanced.vignette: Maya has quietly made a habit of asking, once or twice a day, ‘what state am I in?’—and then nudging. Wired and scattered, she takes a walk; sunk and dull, she calls a friend; and when she is clear, she uses it well. She holds the practice lightly, without turning it into a rulebook. It is less a discipline than a gentle steering, and over months it has changed the whole weather of her life.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Obsessive guna-monitoring becoming neurotic self-analysis.
facets.somatic: Body restricted by excessive guna-analysis.
facets.emotional: Emotional self-judgment based on guna-states.
facets.cognitive: Mental fixation on guna-categorization.
facets.psychic: The soul mistakes the play of the gunas for reality itself, lost in the veils.

excess.text:
Obsessive guna-monitoring becoming neurotic self-analysis.

Somatic: Body restricted by excessive guna-analysis.

Emotional: Emotional self-judgment based on guna-states.

Cognitive: Mental fixation on guna-categorization.

Psychic: The soul mistakes the play of the gunas for reality itself, lost in the veils.
excess.vignette: Sam has read all the books and now cannot stop diagnosing himself. Every mood is labeled within seconds—‘that’s rajasic,’ ‘very tamasic today’—every meal, song, and conversation audited for its guṇa. The monitoring that was meant to free him has become a cage; he is so busy analyzing his states that he never simply lives one. Self-awareness has tipped over into a neurotic, exhausting self-surveillance.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete unconsciousness of guna-states. Driven by whichever guna is currently dominant.
facets.somatic: Body driven by unconscious guna-patterns.
facets.emotional: Emotional life as unrecognized guna-storms.
facets.cognitive: Mind cycles through gunas without awareness.
facets.psychic: The soul has no sense that anything is veiled, taking the surface play of qualities as the whole.

deficient.text:
Complete unconsciousness of guna-states. Driven by whichever guna is currently dominant.

Somatic: Body driven by unconscious guna-patterns.

Emotional: Emotional life as unrecognized guna-storms.

Cognitive: Mind cycles through gunas without awareness.

Psychic: The soul has no sense that anything is veiled, taking the surface play of qualities as the whole.
deficient.vignette: It has never once occurred to Rob that his inner state is something he could notice, let alone shift. He is simply whatever the day makes him—irritable when tired, manic when caffeinated, flat when it rains—and he reads each mood as plain fact about the world rather than weather passing through him. Ruled entirely by whichever force happens to be up, he lurches from state to state, blaming the circumstances each time.

attributes.title: Two Forms of Brahman (Saguna and Nirguna)
attributes.aspect: Brahman has two forms: gross/embodied (sat) and subtle/disembodied (asat); mortal and immortal; defined and undefined. The gross form is air, sky, sun, moon, fire, water, etc. The subtle form is beyond all this. The description of the indescribable is ‘neti-neti.’

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic (the Absolute with and without qualities) · Ontic mode: Metaphysical distinction (two modes of the one Absolute) · Valence: Neutral — a truth to be realized · Guṇa: spans both — Saguna (with guṇas), Nirguna (beyond guṇas) · Scale: Cosmic / transcendent (Ādhidaivika), realized individually · Causal status: Ultimate ground (Nirguna as substrate; Saguna as its qualified aspect) · Key relation: Complementary pair — the guṇas pertain to Saguna and are transcended in Nirguna.
coordinate.seat: Psychic (the Absolute with and without qualities)
coordinate.ontic_mode: Metaphysical distinction (two modes of the one Absolute)
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a truth to be realized
coordinate.guna: spans both — Saguna (with guṇas), Nirguna (beyond guṇas)
coordinate.causal_status: Ultimate ground (Nirguna as substrate; Saguna as its qualified aspect)
coordinate.key_relation: Complementary pair — the guṇas pertain to Saguna and are transcended in Nirguna.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Recognition of both forms of Brahman—the manifest cosmic forms and the unmanifest absolute. Both are honored.
facets.somatic: Body interacts with the cosmic-saguna forms while knowing the unmanifest.
facets.emotional: Emotional devotion to manifest forms with knowledge of nirguna.
facets.cognitive: Mind holds both: cosmic worship and absolute realization.
facets.psychic: The soul honors both the Absolute with form and without, approaching the formless through the form.

balanced.text:
Recognition of both forms of Brahman—the manifest cosmic forms and the unmanifest absolute. Both are honored.

Somatic: Body interacts with the cosmic-saguna forms while knowing the unmanifest.

Emotional: Emotional devotion to manifest forms with knowledge of nirguna.

Cognitive: Mind holds both: cosmic worship and absolute realization.

Psychic: The soul honors both the Absolute with form and without, approaching the formless through the form.
balanced.vignette: Kamala lights the lamp before the image of Krishna each morning with real love, and she also sits, later, in a silence that holds no image at all—and to her these are not two religions but one. The form gives her heart something to hold; the formless gives it somewhere to dissolve. She honors both without needing to choose, and neither one crowds out the other.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Exclusive focus on one form rejecting the other—either pure nirguna rejecting devotion, or pure saguna missing the absolute.
facets.somatic: Body either entirely in form-worship or rejecting all forms.
facets.emotional: Sectarian emotional fixation.
facets.cognitive: Mental rigidity around one form alone.
facets.psychic: The soul absolutizes one — clinging to form, or to bare formlessness — and loses the fullness of both.

excess.text:
Exclusive focus on one form rejecting the other—either pure nirguna rejecting devotion, or pure saguna missing the absolute.

Somatic: Body either entirely in form-worship or rejecting all forms.

Emotional: Sectarian emotional fixation.

Cognitive: Mental rigidity around one form alone.

Psychic: The soul absolutizes one — clinging to form, or to bare formlessness — and loses the fullness of both.
excess.vignette: Two men argue after the lecture. The first, proud of his philosophy, dismisses all devotion as sentimental crutchwork for simple minds. The second, devoted to his deity, waves away the talk of a formless absolute as cold and empty. Each has seized half of the truth and, clutching it, rejected the other half—and so each is left with something real but incomplete, defending a wall he built himself.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Materialist denial of both Brahman forms—seeing only physical phenomena.
facets.somatic: Body interacts with phenomena without sacred dimension.
