Somatic: Mechanical functioning, no awareness of body across states.

Emotional: Caught in each state’s content without awareness.

Cognitive: No witness consciousness, complete identification with state-contents.

Psychic: The soul takes each state as a separate, disconnected reality, never sensing the continuity beneath.
deficient.vignette: Meena moves through her days, dreams, and nights on autopilot—no memory of what she dreamed, no refreshment from sleep, no one home behind her waking eyes. She is fully identified with each state as it comes, swept from one to the next with never a glimpse of the one to whom they all happen. Life runs her; she does not run it, or even watch it.

attributes.title: Turiya as the Substratum (Krishnananda’s Treatment)
attributes.aspect: Krishnananda emphasizes Turiya is not a fourth state in series but the substratum. The three states (waking, dream, deep sleep) appear and disappear in Turiya; Turiya itself never changes. It is the screen, they are the movie. This radically reorients spiritual practice.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic (the ground of awareness) · Ontic mode: Substratum (the ever-present ground, not a fourth in sequence) · Valence: Neutral — the ground to be realized · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Transcendent (Ādhidaivika), realized individually · Causal status: Substratum (the ground on which the three states appear) · Key relation: Ground — the substratum of the three states.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Substratum (the ever-present ground, not a fourth in sequence)
coordinate.valence: Neutral — the ground to be realized
coordinate.causal_status: Substratum (the ground on which the three states appear)
coordinate.key_relation: Ground — the substratum of the three states.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Turiya recognized as ever-present substratum. All three states seen as appearances in unchanging awareness.
facets.somatic: Body’s states seen as appearances in Turiya.
facets.emotional: Emotional states recognized as movements in unchanging awareness.
facets.cognitive: Mind’s three states all seen against unchanging Turiya-background.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes turīya not as a fourth state in series but as the ever-present ground in which the other three appear.

balanced.text:
Turiya recognized as ever-present substratum. All three states seen as appearances in unchanging awareness.

Somatic: Body’s states seen as appearances in Turiya.

Emotional: Emotional states recognized as movements in unchanging awareness.

Cognitive: Mind’s three states all seen against unchanging Turiya-background.

Psychic: The soul recognizes turīya not as a fourth state in series but as the ever-present ground in which the other three appear.
balanced.vignette: Something shifted for Leela when she stopped waiting for turiya to arrive and noticed it had never left. Waking, dreaming, sleeping—she saw them now as passing weather across an awareness that does not itself come and go. The states still change; the sky in which they change does not. She rests, increasingly, as the sky.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Claiming Turiya-realization while still completely identified with state-contents.
facets.somatic: Body still in state-identification despite claims.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity continues despite claims.
facets.cognitive: Mental claim of Turiya without substratum-realization.
facets.psychic: The soul reifies turīya into a special experience to attain, missing its always-present nature.

excess.text:
Claiming Turiya-realization while still completely identified with state-contents.

Somatic: Body still in state-identification despite claims.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity continues despite claims.

Cognitive: Mental claim of Turiya without substratum-realization.

Psychic: The soul reifies turīya into a special experience to attain, missing its always-present nature.
excess.vignette: Tom quotes the Māṇḍūkya at dinner parties and speaks of himself as ‘pure awareness’—while remaining, by every visible sign, entirely tangled in his moods, his grudges, and his cravings. The doctrine has become a costume worn over an unchanged man. He claims the ground while standing, still, wholly inside the weather.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Turiya treated as a fourth state in sequence—‘I am in waking, then dream, then deep sleep, then maybe Turiya.’ Missing its substratum nature.
facets.somatic: Body cycling through states without substratum-recognition.
facets.emotional: Emotional contents taken as full reality.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t see Turiya as substratum, treats as separate state.
facets.psychic: The soul knows only the three changing states, with no sense of the changeless substratum.

deficient.text:
Turiya treated as a fourth state in sequence—‘I am in waking, then dream, then deep sleep, then maybe Turiya.’ Missing its substratum nature.

Somatic: Body cycling through states without substratum-recognition.

Emotional: Emotional contents taken as full reality.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t see Turiya as substratum, treats as separate state.

Psychic: The soul knows only the three changing states, with no sense of the changeless substratum.
deficient.vignette: For Sam, turiya is a destination at the end of a line: first waking, then dream, then deep sleep, then—if he meditates hard enough—a fourth state he might one day reach. He does not suspect that it is not next in the sequence but underneath the whole of it, the awareness he is already using to look for it. He searches everywhere for the very eyes he is looking with.

attributes.title: Four States: Vaishvanara (Waking)
attributes.aspect: Vaishvanara—the waking state. Consciousness turned outward through seven limbs, nineteen mouths. Experiences gross objects. The first state of consciousness with corresponding body.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical (outward-turned, the gross sensory world) · Ontic mode: State (the first of the four states) · Valence: Neutral — a natural state to inhabit fully · Guṇa: Rajas-dominant · Scale: Individual, with cosmic correlate (Virāṭ) · Causal status: Manifestation (the gross state) · Key relation: Part — first of the four states; corresponds to ‘A’ of AUM.
coordinate.ontic_mode: State (the first of the four states)
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a natural state to inhabit fully
coordinate.key_relation: Part — first of the four states; corresponds to ‘A’ of AUM.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Healthy waking state—senses functioning, world engaged with awareness.
facets.somatic: Body active and healthy in waking.
facets.emotional: Emotional engagement with the world.
facets.cognitive: Mind alert in waking state, conscious participation.
facets.psychic: The soul inhabits the waking state fully and consciously, present to the outer world without being lost in it.

balanced.text:
Healthy waking state—senses functioning, world engaged with awareness.

Somatic: Body active and healthy in waking.

Emotional: Emotional engagement with the world.

Cognitive: Mind alert in waking state, conscious participation.

Psychic: The soul inhabits the waking state fully and consciously, present to the outer world without being lost in it.
balanced.vignette: When Kavya is awake, she is genuinely awake—senses open, attention present, engaged with the actual world in front of her rather than the one on her screen. The soup tastes like soup; the conversation is with the person, not the phone. It is an ordinary competence that has become rare: simply being here, alert, for one’s own waking life.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Workaholic excess of waking-state activity, never resting.
facets.somatic: Body exhausted from over-activity.
facets.emotional: Emotional overload from constant waking-engagement.
facets.cognitive: Mental fatigue from excess waking activity.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly captured by waking experience, the outer world its entire reality.

excess.text:
Workaholic excess of waking-state activity, never resting.

Somatic: Body exhausted from over-activity.

Emotional: Emotional overload from constant waking-engagement.

Cognitive: Mental fatigue from excess waking activity.

Psychic: The soul is wholly captured by waking experience, the outer world its entire reality.
excess.vignette: Mark has not had an unscheduled hour in years. He works through meals, answers email in the bathroom, and treats rest as a moral failing; even his holidays have agendas. The waking state, which is meant to alternate with rest, has swallowed the whole cycle. He is always on, and the always-on is quietly grinding him down.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Half-asleep in waking—drifting, no conscious engagement, life as somnambulism.
facets.somatic: Body mechanical, no presence.
facets.emotional: Emotional flatness in waking life.
facets.cognitive: Mind absent during waking, unconscious living.
facets.psychic: The soul moves through waking life half-present, never fully arriving in the moment it is in.

deficient.text:
Half-asleep in waking—drifting, no conscious engagement, life as somnambulism.

Somatic: Body mechanical, no presence.

Emotional: Emotional flatness in waking life.

Cognitive: Mind absent during waking, unconscious living.

Psychic: The soul moves through waking life half-present, never fully arriving in the moment it is in.
deficient.vignette: Dan gets through the day, but ‘through’ is the word—half-present, running on habit, arriving places with no memory of the journey. He nods in meetings he isn’t really in, eats without tasting, and looks up at six wondering where the day went. His eyes are open; the light behind them is dim. He sleepwalks through his own waking hours.

attributes.title: Four States: Taijasa (Dreaming)
attributes.aspect: Taijasa—the dreaming state. Consciousness turned inward, experiencing subtle objects created by mental impressions. Second state with corresponding subtle body.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental (inward-turned, the subtle dream-world of impressions) · Ontic mode: State (the second of the four states) · Valence: Neutral — a natural state · Guṇa: Rajas–Sattva (subtle activity) · Scale: Individual, with cosmic correlate (Hiraṇyagarbha) · Causal status: Manifestation (the subtle state) · Key relation: Part — second of the four states; corresponds to ‘U’ of AUM.
coordinate.seat: Mental (inward-turned, the subtle dream-world of impressions)
coordinate.ontic_mode: State (the second of the four states)
coordinate.key_relation: Part — second of the four states; corresponds to ‘U’ of AUM.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Healthy dream life—subconscious processing, integration of waking experiences, possible higher dimensions.
facets.somatic: Subtle body active healthily in dream.
facets.emotional: Emotional integration through dreams.
facets.cognitive: Mind processing creatively in dream state.
facets.psychic: The soul observes the dreaming state, recognizing the mind’s power to create whole worlds from its own impressions.

balanced.text:
Healthy dream life—subconscious processing, integration of waking experiences, possible higher dimensions.

Somatic: Subtle body active healthily in dream.

Emotional: Emotional integration through dreams.

Cognitive: Mind processing creatively in dream state.

Psychic: The soul observes the dreaming state, recognizing the mind’s power to create whole worlds from its own impressions.
balanced.vignette: Ria’s nights do quiet work. The day’s tangles sort themselves in dream; a problem she couldn’t crack at her desk is somehow looser by morning; now and then a dream leaves her with an image she can’t explain and doesn’t want to lose. She neither chases her dreams nor fears them. She lets the subconscious do its patient integration, and wakes the better for it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Nightmare-dominated sleep, dream-obsession, escaping waking life into dreams.
facets.somatic: Restless dream-disturbed sleep.
facets.emotional: Emotional turbulence in dreams.
facets.cognitive: Mind escaping into dreams instead of waking engagement.
facets.psychic: The soul is consumed by its inner dream-world of fantasy and projection, even while awake.

excess.text:
Nightmare-dominated sleep, dream-obsession, escaping waking life into dreams.

Somatic: Restless dream-disturbed sleep.

Emotional: Emotional turbulence in dreams.

Cognitive: Mind escaping into dreams instead of waking engagement.

Psychic: The soul is consumed by its inner dream-world of fantasy and projection, even while awake.
excess.vignette: Jon would rather be asleep. His waking life has thinned to a chore he endures between dreams, and he cultivates them—naps, lucid-dream apps, a notebook by the bed—escaping ever deeper into an inner cinema more vivid than his days. The dream-world was meant to serve the waking one; now it has become a place to hide from it.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Dreamless sleep without integration—missing the processing function of dreams.
facets.somatic: Sleep without REM cycles.
facets.emotional: Lack of emotional processing through dreams.
facets.cognitive: Mental rigidity from lack of dream-creativity.
facets.psychic: The soul dismisses the dreaming mind entirely, blind to the impressions that shape it from within.

deficient.text:
Dreamless sleep without integration—missing the processing function of dreams.

Somatic: Sleep without REM cycles.

Emotional: Lack of emotional processing through dreams.

Cognitive: Mental rigidity from lack of dream-creativity.

Psychic: The soul dismisses the dreaming mind entirely, blind to the impressions that shape it from within.
deficient.vignette: Priya crashes hard and wakes unprocessed. Whatever the day stirred up goes down with her untouched and comes back up in the morning exactly as it was—unsorted, unintegrated, still waiting. Her sleep gives rest but does no work; the dreaming mind that should digest her experience seems to have gone offline, and the backlog quietly grows.

attributes.title: Four States: Prajna (Deep Sleep)
attributes.aspect: Prajna—deep dreamless sleep. Where one desires nothing, sees no dream, is unified. Consciousness becomes a mass of bliss. The lord of all, the knower of all, the inner controller, the source of all.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic (the causal, undifferentiated state) · Ontic mode: State (the third of the four states) · Valence: Neutral — the seed of undivided awareness · Guṇa: Tamas-dominant (undifferentiated) · Scale: Individual, with cosmic correlate (Īśvara) · Causal status: Causal state (the seed-condition of manifestation) · Key relation: Part — third of the four states; corresponds to ‘M’ of AUM.
coordinate.ontic_mode: State (the third of the four states)
coordinate.valence: Neutral — the seed of undivided awareness
coordinate.key_relation: Part — third of the four states; corresponds to ‘M’ of AUM.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Deep restorative sleep, contact with the blissful causal level. Refreshment of the whole being.
facets.somatic: Deep restorative sleep, body fully recovered.
facets.emotional: Emotional restoration through deep sleep.
facets.cognitive: Mind refreshed through contact with bliss-state.
facets.psychic: The soul senses, in dreamless sleep, the seed of undivided awareness — the unity it touches each night unknowing.

balanced.text:
Deep restorative sleep, contact with the blissful causal level. Refreshment of the whole being.

Somatic: Deep restorative sleep, body fully recovered.

Emotional: Emotional restoration through deep sleep.

Cognitive: Mind refreshed through contact with bliss-state.

Psychic: The soul senses, in dreamless sleep, the seed of undivided awareness — the unity it touches each night unknowing.
balanced.vignette: Each night Grace drops into a deep, dreamless dark and rises genuinely restored, as though she had gone somewhere nourishing and come back full. She cannot describe the place—there is nothing to describe—but its refreshment is unmistakable in the morning. In deep sleep she touches, without knowing it, a stratum of simple peace the day never reaches.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Oversleeping, depressive sleep as escape, tamasic excess.
facets.somatic: Body weakened by oversleeping.
facets.emotional: Depression manifesting as excessive sleep.
facets.cognitive: Mind escaping into sleep, no engagement.
facets.psychic: The soul mistakes the blankness of deep sleep for the goal, courting oblivion over awakening.

excess.text:
Oversleeping, depressive sleep as escape, tamasic excess.

Somatic: Body weakened by oversleeping.

Emotional: Depression manifesting as excessive sleep.

Cognitive: Mind escaping into sleep, no engagement.

Psychic: The soul mistakes the blankness of deep sleep for the goal, courting oblivion over awakening.
excess.vignette: Ben sleeps ten, eleven, twelve hours and wakes wanting more. Sleep has stopped being rest and become a refuge—a soft dark room he retreats into whenever the world asks too much. The heaviness that pulls him under is not restoration but escape, and the more he sleeps the flatter and further away his waking life becomes.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Chronic insomnia, no deep sleep, exhaustion from never contacting the bliss-state.
facets.somatic: Body exhausted, no recovery.
facets.emotional: Emotional dysregulation from sleep deprivation.
facets.cognitive: Mental dysfunction from chronic sleep lack.
facets.psychic: The soul takes deep sleep as mere unconsciousness, missing the unity that abides within it.

deficient.text:
Chronic insomnia, no deep sleep, exhaustion from never contacting the bliss-state.

Somatic: Body exhausted, no recovery.

Emotional: Emotional dysregulation from sleep deprivation.

Cognitive: Mental dysfunction from chronic sleep lack.

Psychic: The soul takes deep sleep as mere unconsciousness, missing the unity that abides within it.
deficient.vignette: It has been months since Nadia slept properly through. She lies in the dark, surfacing every hour, never sinking to the deep place where body and mind are truly remade. By day she is frayed and brittle, running on a debt no nap can repay—cut off, night after night, from the restorative dark she needs and cannot reach.

attributes.title: Turiya (Fourth State - Pure Awareness)
attributes.aspect: Turiya—not inward-knowing, not outward-knowing, not both, not a mass of consciousness, not knowing, not not-knowing. Unseen, ungraspable, indefinable, unthinkable, indescribable. The essence of the Self. Peaceful, blissful, non-dual.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic (pure awareness) · Ontic mode: State-beyond-states (the ‘fourth,’ the ground of the other three) · Valence: Neutral — the awareness to be realized · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Transcendent (Ādhidaivika), realized individually · Causal status: Ground (the substrate of waking, dream, and sleep) · Key relation: Ground — the fourth; the silence beyond A-U-M.
coordinate.ontic_mode: State-beyond-states (the ‘fourth,’ the ground of the other three)
coordinate.valence: Neutral — the awareness to be realized
coordinate.causal_status: Ground (the substrate of waking, dream, and sleep)
coordinate.key_relation: Ground — the fourth; the silence beyond A-U-M.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Recognition of Turiya as the witnessing presence in all three states—the constant pure awareness underlying everything.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as appearance in Turiya.
facets.emotional: Emotions seen as movements in Turiya.
facets.cognitive: Mental contents witnessed by Turiya.
facets.psychic: The soul rests as turīya, the pure awareness neither inward nor outward, the ground of waking, dream, and sleep.

balanced.text:
Recognition of Turiya as the witnessing presence in all three states—the constant pure awareness underlying everything.

Somatic: Body recognized as appearance in Turiya.

Emotional: Emotions seen as movements in Turiya.

Cognitive: Mental contents witnessed by Turiya.

Psychic: The soul rests as turīya, the pure awareness neither inward nor outward, the ground of waking, dream, and sleep.
balanced.vignette: In the pause between two thoughts, Ella has started to notice what is always already there: a plain, wakeful presence that was watching the last thought and is watching this one, unchanged by either. It witnessed her waking this morning and her dreaming last night. She is learning, slowly, to recognize herself less as the passing content and more as the constant, quiet awareness in which it all appears.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Claiming Turiya without realization—using doctrine as new identity.
facets.somatic: Body neglected with Turiya-claim.
facets.emotional: Bypassing emotions with claim.
facets.cognitive: Concept of Turiya without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul grasps at turīya as a special attainment, the very seeking obscuring the ever-present.

excess.text:
Claiming Turiya without realization—using doctrine as new identity.

Somatic: Body neglected with Turiya-claim.

Emotional: Bypassing emotions with claim.

Cognitive: Concept of Turiya without realization.

Psychic: The soul grasps at turīya as a special attainment, the very seeking obscuring the ever-present.
excess.vignette: Having read that he is ‘the witness,’ Greg now narrates his life in the third person and wears a practiced detachment like a badge. But the detachment is a pose, not a realization; underneath it he is as reactive as ever, merely describing his reactions in spiritual language. He has taken the word for the thing, and made an identity out of a state he has not yet actually found.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete absence of awareness of Turiya—identified entirely with three states.
facets.somatic: Body identified as self.
facets.emotional: Emotions taken as self.
facets.cognitive: Mind taken as ultimate, no fourth dimension known.
facets.psychic: The soul knows only the three ordinary states, the fourth never even suspected.

deficient.text:
Complete absence of awareness of Turiya—identified entirely with three states.

Somatic: Body identified as self.

Emotional: Emotions taken as self.

Cognitive: Mind taken as ultimate, no fourth dimension known.

Psychic: The soul knows only the three ordinary states, the fourth never even suspected.
deficient.vignette: It has genuinely never occurred to Amir that there is anyone home behind his experiences—that the waker, the dreamer, and the sleeper might all be appearing to a single awareness. He is completely his thoughts, completely his moods, completely whatever state he happens to be in. The witnessing presence is not denied by him; it is simply not suspected.

attributes.title: Vaishvanara Vidya (Universal Self Meditation)
attributes.aspect: Six householders go to King Ashvapati to learn Vaishvanara Vidya. Each had a partial conception of Self (as heaven, sun, air, ether, water, earth). The king teaches: the whole cosmos is the Universal Self. The mouth is heaven, eyes are sun, breath is air, body is ether, bladder is water, feet are earth. To meditate on a part is to miss the whole.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic (meditation on the universal Self) · Ontic mode: Practice (an upāsana — meditation on the cosmic person) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, on a cosmic object (Ādhidaivika) · Causal status: Practice (a means to the recognition of the universal Self) · Key relation: Correspondence — takes the waking / cosmic person (Virāṭ) as the object of meditation.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice (an upāsana — meditation on the cosmic person)
coordinate.causal_status: Practice (a means to the recognition of the universal Self)
coordinate.key_relation: Correspondence — takes the waking / cosmic person (Virāṭ) as the object of meditation.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Meditation on cosmic Self as one’s own body—seeing the whole universe as one’s expanded body. Universal Self realized.
facets.somatic: Body experienced as microcosm of the universe.
facets.emotional: Emotional connection with cosmos through bodily meditation.
facets.cognitive: Mind expands to cosmic dimensions through Vaishvanara meditation.
facets.psychic: The soul meditates on the universal Self present in all, recognizing the cosmic person as its own larger being.

balanced.text:
Meditation on cosmic Self as one’s own body—seeing the whole universe as one’s expanded body. Universal Self realized.

Somatic: Body experienced as microcosm of the universe.

Emotional: Emotional connection with cosmos through bodily meditation.

Cognitive: Mind expands to cosmic dimensions through Vaishvanara meditation.

Psychic: The soul meditates on the universal Self present in all, recognizing the cosmic person as its own larger being.
balanced.vignette: In deep meditation, the edges of Sunita’s small self grow porous, and for a while she rests in a sense of the whole—the sun her eye, the wind her breath, the earth her body—not as a fancy but as a felt widening. She returns to her ordinary life gentler, having tasted how large a self can be. The cosmic dimension has quietly re-entered her days.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Inflated identification as ‘I am the universe’ in megalomanic sense.
facets.somatic: Body claimed as cosmic.
facets.emotional: Grandiose emotional inflation.
facets.cognitive: Egotic appropriation of cosmic identity.
facets.psychic: The soul inflates, identifying its ego with the cosmic Self in grandiosity.

excess.text:
Inflated identification as ‘I am the universe’ in megalomanic sense.

Somatic: Body claimed as cosmic.

Emotional: Grandiose emotional inflation.

Cognitive: Egotic appropriation of cosmic identity.

Psychic: The soul inflates, identifying its ego with the cosmic Self in grandiosity.
excess.vignette: After a powerful retreat, Karl comes home convinced, and announces, that he IS the universe—and proceeds to treat everyone as a figment of his cosmic mind, above criticism, beyond the rules that bind lesser beings. What might have been a genuine expansion has inflated into simple megalomania. The ‘universal Self’ has become the biggest small ego in the room.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Body taken as isolated, finite, separate from cosmos. No cosmic dimension recognized.
facets.somatic: Body as small, isolated, finite.
facets.emotional: Emotional isolation from cosmic process.
facets.cognitive: Mind confined to individual body-identity.
facets.psychic: The soul sees only its small separate self, with no sense of the universal being it shares.

deficient.text:
Body taken as isolated, finite, separate from cosmos. No cosmic dimension recognized.

Somatic: Body as small, isolated, finite.

Emotional: Emotional isolation from cosmic process.

Cognitive: Mind confined to individual body-identity.

Psychic: The soul sees only its small separate self, with no sense of the universal being it shares.
deficient.vignette: For Raj, the self ends firmly at his own skin. He is a separate body in a foreign world, joined to the vast cosmos by exactly nothing, and the very idea of an ‘expanded’ identity strikes him as nonsense. He is not wrong about the boundary; he has simply never once felt past it, and so lives his whole life in a single sealed room, unaware there is a horizon at all.

attributes.title: Three States Teaching (Waking, Dream, Deep Sleep)
attributes.aspect: Yajnavalkya teaches Janaka about the three states. In waking, the Self uses the body and external senses. In dream, the Self uses only the subtle world made of its own impressions—‘creates by its own light.’ In deep sleep, the Self enters into itself, beyond desire, beyond fear, beyond all activity. The same Self moves through all three states.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic (tracing the Self through the states) · Ontic mode: Framework (a discriminative teaching — tracing the Self through the three states) · Valence: Neutral — a discriminative teaching · Guṇa: Sattva (a clarifying inquiry) · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Mechanism (an analytic method reaching the witness beyond the states) · Key relation: Method — analyzes the three states to disclose their witnessing ground.
coordinate.seat: Mental–Psychic (tracing the Self through the states)
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework (a discriminative teaching — tracing the Self through the three states)
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (an analytic method reaching the witness beyond the states)
coordinate.key_relation: Method — analyzes the three states to disclose their witnessing ground.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Awareness of one Self moving through all three states—not different selves but one consciousness in different conditions.
facets.somatic: Body recognized in each of its state-functions.
facets.emotional: Emotional content of each state seen against the underlying Self.
facets.cognitive: Mind recognizes the same Self in waking, dream, deep sleep.
facets.psychic: The soul learns, like Janaka under Yājñavalkya, to trace the Self through all three states to the freedom beyond them.

balanced.text:
Awareness of one Self moving through all three states—not different selves but one consciousness in different conditions.

Somatic: Body recognized in each of its state-functions.

Emotional: Emotional content of each state seen against the underlying Self.

Cognitive: Mind recognizes the same Self in waking, dream, deep sleep.

Psychic: The soul learns, like Janaka under Yājñavalkya, to trace the Self through all three states to the freedom beyond them.
balanced.vignette: It came to Hari not as a doctrine but as a quiet recognition: the one who woke this morning, the one who dreamed last night, and the one who slept dreamlessly before that are not three different people but one—himself—in three conditions. The thread does not break at the edge of sleep. Knowing this, he holds the whole of his life more lightly, as a single continuous awareness passing through changing rooms.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Imbalance between states—chronic insomnia, dream-disturbance, oversleeping. Or claiming awareness in deep sleep without realization.
facets.somatic: Dysregulated sleep cycles.
facets.emotional: Emotional content of one state dominating.
facets.cognitive: Mental imbalance from inadequate state-cycling.
facets.psychic: The soul over-intellectualizes the teaching, analyzing the states rather than recognizing the Self.

excess.text:
Imbalance between states—chronic insomnia, dream-disturbance, oversleeping. Or claiming awareness in deep sleep without realization.

Somatic: Dysregulated sleep cycles.

Emotional: Emotional content of one state dominating.

Cognitive: Mental imbalance from inadequate state-cycling.

Psychic: The soul over-intellectualizes the teaching, analyzing the states rather than recognizing the Self.
excess.vignette: Between the insomnia, the disturbed dreams, and the leaden weekend oversleeping, the states of Dylan’s life have fallen badly out of balance—and to make it worse he insists he ‘stays aware even in deep sleep,’ a claim his exhausted body flatly contradicts. The teaching that should have brought harmony among his states has become one more thing to boast about while the states themselves quietly unravel.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Unconscious cycling through three states—no awareness that the same Self moves through all. Each state taken as separate reality.
facets.somatic: Mechanical state-cycling without recognition.
facets.emotional: Different self felt in each state, no continuity.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t recognize Self’s continuity through states.
facets.psychic: The soul never inquires into its states, taking waking life as the whole of what it is.

deficient.text:
Unconscious cycling through three states—no awareness that the same Self moves through all. Each state taken as separate reality.

Somatic: Mechanical state-cycling without recognition.

Emotional: Different self felt in each state, no continuity.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t recognize Self’s continuity through states.

Psychic: The soul never inquires into its states, taking waking life as the whole of what it is.
deficient.vignette: Farah moves from waking to dream to sleep and back with no sense that anything connects them—each a separate little world, none of them clearly hers. There is no felt thread of a single self running through the night; she is simply whoever the current state makes her. The one consciousness moving through all three is, to her, not even a question, let alone an answer.

attributes.title: Annamaya Kośa — The Sheath of Matter

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical · Ontic mode: Sheath — the food-sheath (annamaya kośa) · Valence: Neutral (the outermost layer) · Guṇa: Tamas-predominant (Earth) · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Constituent layer (the physical body) · Key relation: Outermost of the five sheaths; the gross body (sthūla śarīra); Earth-dominant (Dim 1).
coordinate.key_relation: Outermost of the five sheaths; the gross body (sthūla śarīra); Earth-dominant (Dim 1).

attributes.title: Prāṇamaya Kośa — The Sheath of Vital Energy

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Sheath — the vital-sheath (prāṇamaya kośa) · Valence: Neutral · Guṇa: Rajas-predominant (Water / Fire) · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Constituent layer (the vital body) · Key relation: Second sheath; part of the subtle body; the prāṇas (Dim 6).
coordinate.key_relation: Second sheath; part of the subtle body; the prāṇas (Dim 6).

attributes.title: Manomaya Kośa — The Sheath of Mind

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Vital · Ontic mode: Sheath — the mind-sheath (manomaya kośa) · Valence: Neutral · Guṇa: Sattva–Tamas mixed (Fire / Air) · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Constituent layer (the sensory-emotional mind) · Key relation: Third sheath; the emotional-sensory mind; part of the subtle body.
coordinate.key_relation: Third sheath; the emotional-sensory mind; part of the subtle body.

attributes.title: Vijñānamaya Kośa — The Sheath of Discernment

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Sheath — the wisdom-sheath (vijñānamaya kośa) · Valence: Neutral · Guṇa: Sattva–Rajas mixed (Air) · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Constituent layer (the discerning intellect) · Key relation: Fourth sheath; the intellect / buddhi; part of the subtle body; kin to Vijñāna (Dim 28).
coordinate.key_relation: Fourth sheath; the intellect / buddhi; part of the subtle body; kin to Vijñāna (Dim 28).

attributes.title: Ānandamaya Kośa — The Sheath of Bliss

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Sheath — the bliss-sheath (ānandamaya kośa) · Valence: Neutral (the innermost, subtlest veil) · Guṇa: Malina-sattva (near-pure Sattva) · Scale: Individual, toward the transcendent · Causal status: Constituent layer (the causal body) · Key relation: Innermost of the five sheaths; the causal body (kāraṇa śarīra); nearest the Self; kin to Ānanda (Dim 27).
coordinate.key_relation: Innermost of the five sheaths; the causal body (kāraṇa śarīra); nearest the Self; kin to Ānanda (Dim 27).

attributes.title: Four States and Five Sheaths (Indirect Reference)
attributes.aspect: In waking, senses and mind are active. In dream, only mind active without senses. In deep sleep, prana alone remains. The fourth state (Turiya implied) is pure awareness witnessing all.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic (mapping the sheaths across the states of consciousness) · Ontic mode: Framework (a correspondence between the sheaths and the states) · Valence: Neutral — an orienting map · Guṇa: Sattva (a clarifying schema) · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Framework (relates the five sheaths to waking, dream, sleep, and Turīya) · Key relation: Cross-dimensional correspondence — the sheaths (Dim 5) mapped onto the four states of consciousness (Dim 4).
coordinate.seat: Mental–Psychic (mapping the sheaths across the states of consciousness)
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework (a correspondence between the sheaths and the states)
coordinate.causal_status: Framework (relates the five sheaths to waking, dream, sleep, and Turīya)
coordinate.key_relation: Cross-dimensional correspondence — the sheaths (Dim 5) mapped onto the four states of consciousness (Dim 4).

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Awareness through all states—waking activity, dream awareness, deep sleep recognition. The witness present in all.
facets.somatic: Body’s three states clearly recognized.
facets.emotional: Emotional clarity about state-shifts.
facets.cognitive: Mind aware of its own modulations through states.
facets.psychic: The soul maps how the sheaths operate across waking, dream, and sleep, locating itself beyond all of them.

balanced.text:
Awareness through all states—waking activity, dream awareness, deep sleep recognition. The witness present in all.

Somatic: Body’s three states clearly recognized.

Emotional: Emotional clarity about state-shifts.

Cognitive: Mind aware of its own modulations through states.

Psychic: The soul maps how the sheaths operate across waking, dream, and sleep, locating itself beyond all of them.
balanced.vignette: Sofia has begun to feel the same quiet awareness threading through her waking, her dreaming, and even the edge of sleep—one witness, continuous, beneath the changing states. The day’s activity, the night’s images, the deep dark of dreamless rest: all of them now happen to a presence she is learning to rest as. She is less at the mercy of any single state, because she is quietly present to all of them.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Pathological imbalance between states—insomnia, dream-disturbance, oversleeping.
facets.somatic: Sleep dysfunction.
facets.emotional: Emotional dysregulation from state imbalance.
facets.cognitive: Mental dysfunction from inadequate state-cycling.
facets.psychic: The soul over-systematizes the correspondences, lost in the scheme rather than the Self it reveals.

excess.text:
Pathological imbalance between states—insomnia, dream-disturbance, oversleeping.

Somatic: Sleep dysfunction.

Emotional: Emotional dysregulation from state imbalance.

Cognitive: Mental dysfunction from inadequate state-cycling.

Psychic: The soul over-systematizes the correspondences, lost in the scheme rather than the Self it reveals.
excess.vignette: Wired at midnight, groggy at dawn, crashing on Sunday until two—the states of Owen’s life have slipped out of their natural rhythm. His sleep is thin, his dreams are turbulent, and no amount of coffee restores the balance. The states that should flow into one another have jammed, and the whole cycle grinds.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Unconscious cycling through states—no awareness of transitions, no recognition of the witness present in each.
facets.somatic: Mechanical sleep-wake cycles.
facets.emotional: Emotional contents of each state taken as ultimate.
facets.cognitive: Mind without awareness of state-transitions.
facets.psychic: The soul identifies wholly with the active sheath of the moment, never sensing what lies beneath.

deficient.text:
Unconscious cycling through states—no awareness of transitions, no recognition of the witness present in each.

Somatic: Mechanical sleep-wake cycles.

Emotional: Emotional contents of each state taken as ultimate.

Cognitive: Mind without awareness of state-transitions.

Psychic: The soul identifies wholly with the active sheath of the moment, never sensing what lies beneath.
deficient.vignette: Nadia passes from waking to dream to deep sleep and back without ever noticing the seams. There are no remembered dreams, no felt transitions, no sense of a witness that stays while the states change. She is simply carried through the round of day and night, present to none of it—asleep, in a way, even when awake.

attributes.title: Five Koshas (Sheaths of Self)
attributes.aspect: Five sheaths cover the Self: Annamaya (food/body), Pranamaya (vital energy), Manomaya (mind), Vijnanamaya (intellect), Anandamaya (bliss). Each subtler than the last; the Self lies beyond all five.

coordinate.raw: Seat: All layers (the sheaths are the layered coverings of the Self) · Ontic mode: Structure — the five-fold covering (a layered anatomy of the person) · Valence: Neutral — coverings to be recognized and peeled inward · Guṇa: Spans all three (from tamasic Annamaya to sattvic Ānandamaya) · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Structure (the graded coverings over the Ātman) · Key relation: Whole — the aggregate of which the five individual sheaths are the parts.
coordinate.seat: All layers (the sheaths are the layered coverings of the Self)
coordinate.ontic_mode: Structure — the five-fold covering (a layered anatomy of the person)
coordinate.valence: Neutral — coverings to be recognized and peeled inward
coordinate.guna: Spans all three (from tamasic Annamaya to sattvic Ānandamaya)
coordinate.causal_status: Structure (the graded coverings over the Ātman)
coordinate.key_relation: Whole — the aggregate of which the five individual sheaths are the parts.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Recognition of all five sheaths and the Self beyond them. Progressive penetration through each level.
facets.somatic: Annamaya-kosha healthy.
facets.emotional: Pranamaya, manomaya healthy and integrated.
facets.cognitive: Vijnanamaya developed, anandamaya glimpsed, Self recognized as beyond all.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the five sheaths as coverings it wears, peeling inward toward the Self they conceal.

balanced.text:
Recognition of all five sheaths and the Self beyond them. Progressive penetration through each level.

Somatic: Annamaya-kosha healthy.

Emotional: Pranamaya, manomaya healthy and integrated.

Cognitive: Vijnanamaya developed, anandamaya glimpsed, Self recognized as beyond all.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the five sheaths as coverings it wears, peeling inward toward the Self they conceal.
balanced.vignette: Meditating over the years, Ram has come to know himself as a set of nested layers—the body, the breath-energy, the emotions, the discerning mind, the quiet joy beneath—and, faintly, as the awareness in which all five appear. He tends each sheath without mistaking any of them for the whole of who he is. It has made him both more embodied and more free.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Identification with one kosha (commonly body or mind), or claiming to be ‘beyond koshas’ without realization.
facets.somatic: Identifying solely with annamaya.
facets.emotional: Identifying with pranamaya-emotions only.
facets.cognitive: Identifying with manomaya or vijnanamaya as ultimate.
facets.psychic: The soul mistakes a subtler sheath — even the blissful one — for the Self, stopping short of the center.

excess.text:
Identification with one kosha (commonly body or mind), or claiming to be ‘beyond koshas’ without realization.

Somatic: Identifying solely with annamaya.

Emotional: Identifying with pranamaya-emotions only.

Cognitive: Identifying with manomaya or vijnanamaya as ultimate.

Psychic: The soul mistakes a subtler sheath — even the blissful one — for the Self, stopping short of the center.
excess.vignette: For Jake, he simply IS his body—the reflection in the mirror is the verdict on his worth, and a bad gym week is an existential crisis. Others make the same error one layer up, certain they are only their thoughts. Either way, a person has taken a single sheath for the entire self, shrinking a whole identity down to one of its many rooms.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No awareness of the koshas—life lived entirely at one level without recognition of others.
facets.somatic: Body taken as final self, no recognition of subtler levels.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without recognition of subtler dimensions.
facets.cognitive: Mind without map of inner reality.
facets.psychic: The soul is fused to the outermost sheath, the body, with no sense of the depths within.

deficient.text:
No awareness of the koshas—life lived entirely at one level without recognition of others.

Somatic: Body taken as final self, no recognition of subtler levels.

Emotional: Emotional life without recognition of subtler dimensions.

Cognitive: Mind without map of inner reality.

Psychic: The soul is fused to the outermost sheath, the body, with no sense of the depths within.
deficient.vignette: It has never occurred to Dave that he is anything but the obvious surface of himself—a body that eats and works and tires. The idea that there might be layers of vitality, feeling, insight, or joy nested within, let alone an awareness beyond them all, is not something he rejects; it is simply off the map entirely. He lives his whole life on the ground floor, unaware the building has other storeys.

attributes.title: Annamaya Kosha (Food Sheath)
attributes.aspect: Annamaya—the food sheath, the physical body. From food beings are born, by food they live, into food they return. The first sheath, the gross body.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical (the gross, food-built body) · Ontic mode: Constituent — the outermost sheath · Valence: Neutral faculty (the vessel — to be tended, not identified with) · Guṇa: Tamas-predominant (earth-dominant) · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Gross substrate (the densest covering) · Key relation: Part — outermost of the five sheaths; corresponds to Earth and the waking state.
coordinate.valence: Neutral faculty (the vessel — to be tended, not identified with)
coordinate.key_relation: Part — outermost of the five sheaths; corresponds to Earth and the waking state.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Body well-nourished, healthy, recognized as a sheath of Self (not Self itself). Proper relationship with food.
facets.somatic: Healthy body, balanced nutrition.
facets.emotional: Emotional appreciation of body without identification.
facets.cognitive: Mental recognition of body as sheath, not Self.
facets.psychic: The soul tends the food-body well as the outermost vessel, neither despising nor over-identifying with it.

balanced.text:
Body well-nourished, healthy, recognized as a sheath of Self (not Self itself). Proper relationship with food.

Somatic: Healthy body, balanced nutrition.

Emotional: Emotional appreciation of body without identification.

Cognitive: Mental recognition of body as sheath, not Self.

Psychic: The soul tends the food-body well as the outermost vessel, neither despising nor over-identifying with it.
balanced.vignette: Grace feeds herself well and moves her body gladly, treating it as the good and necessary vehicle it is—cared for, respected, enjoyed—without mistaking it for the sum of who she is. Food is nourishment and pleasure, not a battleground; the mirror is not a courtroom. Her body is a home she keeps well, and then lives beyond.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Obsession with food, body, physical perfection—identifying entirely with annamaya.
facets.somatic: Body-obsession, eating disorders, perfectionism.
facets.emotional: Emotional life tied to body-image.
facets.cognitive: Mind dominated by body-concerns.
facets.psychic: The soul takes the physical body as its whole self, all identity invested in form and sensation.

excess.text:
Obsession with food, body, physical perfection—identifying entirely with annamaya.

Somatic: Body-obsession, eating disorders, perfectionism.

Emotional: Emotional life tied to body-image.

Cognitive: Mind dominated by body-concerns.

Psychic: The soul takes the physical body as its whole self, all identity invested in form and sensation.
excess.vignette: The body has become the whole of Tara’s attention—hours before the mirror, every meal a moral event, her mood for the day set by a number or a reflection. The physical form, which is meant to be one sheath among five, has swallowed her entire sense of self. She has forgotten she is anything more than the surface she polices.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Neglect of body, ascetic damage, malnutrition—rejecting the annamaya kosha.
facets.somatic: Damaged body from neglect.
facets.emotional: Emotional rejection of embodiment.
facets.cognitive: Mind dismissive of body’s needs.
facets.psychic: The soul neglects the body, treating the food-sheath with contempt as if it did not matter.

deficient.text:
Neglect of body, ascetic damage, malnutrition—rejecting the annamaya kosha.

Somatic: Damaged body from neglect.

Emotional: Emotional rejection of embodiment.

Cognitive: Mind dismissive of body’s needs.

Psychic: The soul neglects the body, treating the food-sheath with contempt as if it did not matter.
deficient.vignette: In his zeal to be ‘above’ the body, Michael treats it with contempt—neglecting its needs, running it ragged, dismissing its plain signals as weakness to be overcome. He imagines he is being spiritual; he is only being harsh with the one instrument his spirit has to work with. The food-sheath, uncared for, quietly fails the very life it was meant to carry.

attributes.title: Anandamaya (Bliss Sheath/Brahmananda)
attributes.aspect: Anandamaya—the bliss sheath. The most subtle covering of Self. Brahmananda (bliss of Brahman) is described as infinitely greater than all human happiness combined—calculated through ascending blisses.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic (the subtlest covering, a reflection of the Self’s joy) · Ontic mode: Constituent — the innermost sheath · Valence: Neutral — the last covering, easily mistaken for the Self it veils · Guṇa: Sattva-predominant (space-aligned) · Scale: Individual, opening to the transcendent · Causal status: Causal (the causal-body sheath) · Key relation: Part — innermost of the five sheaths; corresponds to the causal body and deep sleep.
coordinate.seat: Psychic (the subtlest covering, a reflection of the Self’s joy)
coordinate.valence: Neutral — the last covering, easily mistaken for the Self it veils
coordinate.key_relation: Part — innermost of the five sheaths; corresponds to the causal body and deep sleep.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Glimpses of anandamaya in meditation and devotion. Recognition that ultimate bliss is one’s nature.
facets.somatic: Body experiences subtle bliss in meditation.
facets.emotional: Emotional bliss as recognition of deeper nature.
facets.cognitive: Mind dwelling in or moving toward anandamaya.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the bliss-sheath as the subtlest covering, a reflection of the Self’s joy, and presses on toward the source.

balanced.text:
Glimpses of anandamaya in meditation and devotion. Recognition that ultimate bliss is one’s nature.

Somatic: Body experiences subtle bliss in meditation.

Emotional: Emotional bliss as recognition of deeper nature.

Cognitive: Mind dwelling in or moving toward anandamaya.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the bliss-sheath as the subtlest covering, a reflection of the Self’s joy, and presses on toward the source.
balanced.vignette: Now and then, in deep prayer or a still morning, Amara is visited by a joy that has no cause—a sweetness welling up from somewhere beneath thought, needing nothing to justify it. She receives these glimpses gratefully and lets them pass, sensing they are hints of what she most deeply is. They do not run her life; they quietly reorient it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Addiction to bliss-states, spiritual hedonism, attachment to anandamaya.
facets.somatic: Body seeking bliss-experiences obsessively.
facets.emotional: Emotional dependence on bliss-states.
facets.cognitive: Mind grasping for ananda, missing the further depth.
facets.psychic: The soul clings to blissful states as the final goal, mistaking the sheath for the Self it veils.

excess.text:
Addiction to bliss-states, spiritual hedonism, attachment to anandamaya.

Somatic: Body seeking bliss-experiences obsessively.

Emotional: Emotional dependence on bliss-states.

Cognitive: Mind grasping for ananda, missing the further depth.

Psychic: The soul clings to blissful states as the final goal, mistaking the sheath for the Self it veils.
excess.vignette: Leo has tasted the bliss in meditation and now wants only that. He chains retreats together, chases the high, judges ordinary contentment as beneath him, and treats his practice like a supply. The very joy meant to point beyond craving has become the object of a subtler craving—a spiritual hedonism, hooked on the sweetness and blind to what it was pointing at.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No glimpse of inner bliss—life of joyless duty or pleasure-seeking. Anandamaya unknown.
facets.somatic: Body without bliss-experience.
facets.emotional: Emotional flatness, no inner joy.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t conceive of inner bliss.
facets.psychic: The soul never touches the bliss-sheath, seeking joy only in outer things and finding none that lasts.

deficient.text:
No glimpse of inner bliss—life of joyless duty or pleasure-seeking. Anandamaya unknown.

Somatic: Body without bliss-experience.

Emotional: Emotional flatness, no inner joy.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t conceive of inner bliss.

Psychic: The soul never touches the bliss-sheath, seeking joy only in outer things and finding none that lasts.
deficient.vignette: For Dana there is no inner sweetness to be found, and she has stopped looking. Life is a ledger of duties to discharge and pleasures to grab, and beneath both runs a flat joylessness she takes to be simply how things are. The bliss-sheath—the wellspring of causeless joy the traditions say is her own nature—is, to her, a rumor from someone else’s life.

attributes.title: Ananda Mimamsa (Inquiry into Bliss Levels)
attributes.aspect: Indra and Virochana go to Prajapati to learn the Self. Prajapati gives progressive teachings, but Virochana stops at ‘body is Self’ and goes happy. Indra realizes the inadequacy of each teaching and returns four times. Body-Self → Dream-Self → Deep-Sleep-Self → finally True Self (pure consciousness). This represents the levels of bliss-realization Indians later mapped onto the five koshas.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic (a graded inquiry into the levels of joy) · Ontic mode: Framework (an analytic ascent through the gradations of bliss) · Valence: Neutral — a discriminative inquiry · Guṇa: Sattva (a clarifying ascent) · Scale: Individual, ascending to the transcendent (Ādhidaivika) · Causal status: Mechanism (a method ascending from sensory pleasure to the bliss of Brahman) · Key relation: Ladder — the graded scale of joy culminating in Brahmānanda.
coordinate.seat: Mental–Psychic (a graded inquiry into the levels of joy)
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework (an analytic ascent through the gradations of bliss)
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (a method ascending from sensory pleasure to the bliss of Brahman)
coordinate.key_relation: Ladder — the graded scale of joy culminating in Brahmānanda.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Indra-like persistence—not settling at any partial realization but inquiring through all levels until the true Self is found.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in continued spiritual labor across long time.
facets.emotional: Emotional readiness to be unsettled, willing to admit each realization is incomplete.
facets.cognitive: Mind continuously checking realization against the deepest truth.
facets.psychic: The soul inquires into the gradations of bliss, ascending from sensory pleasure toward the unconditioned joy of Brahman.

balanced.text:
Indra-like persistence—not settling at any partial realization but inquiring through all levels until the true Self is found.

Somatic: Body engaged in continued spiritual labor across long time.

Emotional: Emotional readiness to be unsettled, willing to admit each realization is incomplete.

Cognitive: Mind continuously checking realization against the deepest truth.

Psychic: The soul inquires into the gradations of bliss, ascending from sensory pleasure toward the unconditioned joy of Brahman.
balanced.vignette: Like Indra in the old story, Vikram will not stop at a comfortable half-answer. Each realization he tests, honors, and then presses past—‘is this it? is there more?’—not out of restlessness but out of an honest, patient hunger for what is truly ultimate. He inquires level by level, refusing to camp on any of them, and the inquiry itself keeps deepening him.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Endless dissatisfaction with all realizations, never settling—the inquiry becomes neurotic.
facets.somatic: Body exhausted by perpetual seeking.
facets.emotional: Chronic spiritual dissatisfaction.
facets.cognitive: Mind always finding fault, never resting in any realization.
facets.psychic: The soul gets lost cataloguing bliss-levels, the inquiry an intellectual exercise.

excess.text:
Endless dissatisfaction with all realizations, never settling—the inquiry becomes neurotic.

Somatic: Body exhausted by perpetual seeking.

Emotional: Chronic spiritual dissatisfaction.

Cognitive: Mind always finding fault, never resting in any realization.

Psychic: The soul gets lost cataloguing bliss-levels, the inquiry an intellectual exercise.
excess.vignette: No answer is ever enough for Ben. Every insight, every teacher, every hard-won peace is discarded within weeks as ‘not quite it,’ and he moves on, forever dissatisfied, forever chasing the next level. The noble refusal to settle for less than the truth has soured into a compulsion that can rest nowhere. His inquiry has become a treadmill dressed as a path.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Virochana-like settling at first level—body taken as Self and going home happy. Maximum loss.
facets.somatic: Body taken as final reality.
facets.emotional: Emotional satisfaction with body-identity.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t probe deeper into Self-nature.
facets.psychic: The soul never inquires into joy at all, settling for the lowest sensory pleasures as if they were the whole.

deficient.text:
Virochana-like settling at first level—body taken as Self and going home happy. Maximum loss.

Somatic: Body taken as final reality.

Emotional: Emotional satisfaction with body-identity.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t probe deeper into Self-nature.

Psychic: The soul never inquires into joy at all, settling for the lowest sensory pleasures as if they were the whole.
deficient.vignette: Like Virochana, who heard ‘the Self’ and cheerfully concluded it meant the body in the mirror, Carl took the first easy answer and went home satisfied. ‘This is all there is—look after number one’—and the inquiry closed before it began. He mistook the ground floor for the whole building and never once climbed the stairs. It is, the tradition says, the maximum possible loss, made in perfect contentment.

attributes.title: Anandamaya Vidya (Bliss Sheath Meditation)
attributes.aspect: Meditation on the Bliss-sheath as the most subtle covering of Self. Recognition that all human happinesses are tiny fractions of Brahmananda. The ascending calculation reveals the magnitude of true bliss.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic (meditation on the sheath of bliss) · Ontic mode: Practice (an upāsana on the bliss-sheath) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, on a transcendent object · Causal status: Practice (a means to recognize all joy as a spark of the Self) · Key relation: Correspondence — takes the Ānandamaya sheath as its object; the practical fruit of the bliss-inquiry.
coordinate.seat: Psychic (meditation on the sheath of bliss)
coordinate.causal_status: Practice (a means to recognize all joy as a spark of the Self)
coordinate.key_relation: Correspondence — takes the Ānandamaya sheath as its object; the practical fruit of the bliss-inquiry.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Glimpses of anandamaya in deep meditation. Recognition that ultimate bliss is one’s nature.
facets.somatic: Body experiences subtle bliss in meditation.
facets.emotional: Emotional bliss as recognition of deeper nature.
facets.cognitive: Mind dwelling in or moving toward anandamaya.
facets.psychic: The soul meditates on the bliss-sheath, recognizing all happiness as a spark of the infinite joy that is the Self.

balanced.text:
Glimpses of anandamaya in deep meditation. Recognition that ultimate bliss is one’s nature.

Somatic: Body experiences subtle bliss in meditation.

Emotional: Emotional bliss as recognition of deeper nature.

Cognitive: Mind dwelling in or moving toward anandamaya.

Psychic: The soul meditates on the bliss-sheath, recognizing all happiness as a spark of the infinite joy that is the Self.
balanced.vignette: In the depths of her evening sit, past the busy mind and the settling emotions, Nadia sometimes arrives at a still layer that is quietly, unaccountably glad—and she rests there, learning its texture, without grasping. The meditation on the bliss-sheath has become, for her, less a technique than a homecoming. She does not chase the gladness; she lets it teach her what she is.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Addiction to bliss-states, spiritual hedonism.
facets.somatic: Body seeking bliss-experiences obsessively.
facets.emotional: Emotional dependence on bliss-states.
facets.cognitive: Mind grasping for ananda, missing the further depth.
facets.psychic: The soul chases blissful meditative states, grasping the experience over the awareness behind it.

excess.text:
Addiction to bliss-states, spiritual hedonism.

Somatic: Body seeking bliss-experiences obsessively.

Emotional: Emotional dependence on bliss-states.

Cognitive: Mind grasping for ananda, missing the further depth.

Psychic: The soul chases blissful meditative states, grasping the experience over the awareness behind it.
excess.vignette: Sam meditates only for the ‘good bit’—the rush of bliss he once learned to reach—and grows irritable and bored when a sit doesn’t deliver it. His practice has become a vending machine for pleasant states, each session judged by the high it produces. The meditation meant to loosen his grasping has become the most refined grasping of all.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No glimpse of inner bliss—life of joyless duty or pleasure-seeking. Anandamaya unknown.
facets.somatic: Body without bliss-experience.
facets.emotional: Emotional flatness, no inner joy.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t conceive of inner bliss.
facets.psychic: The soul never meditates on bliss, its happiness left wholly hostage to circumstance.

deficient.text:
No glimpse of inner bliss—life of joyless duty or pleasure-seeking. Anandamaya unknown.

Somatic: Body without bliss-experience.

Emotional: Emotional flatness, no inner joy.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t conceive of inner bliss.

Psychic: The soul never meditates on bliss, its happiness left wholly hostage to circumstance.
deficient.vignette: Priya sits every morning, dutifully, and feels nothing but the clock. The layers of vitality, feeling, insight, and least of all joy never open for her; her practice is a chore performed at a locked door. She has never once glimpsed the inner gladness the method is meant to reveal, and has half-decided it does not exist—filling the gap, instead, with duties by day and small pleasures by night.

attributes.title: Mitahara (Moderation in Diet)
attributes.aspect: Before pranayama, dietary preparation: ‘Half the stomach with food, one quarter with water, one quarter empty for air.’ Avoid bitter/sour/salty/pungent extremes, fermented food, eating late. Sweet, oily, cooling, easily digestible food preferred.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical (the body the breath-work refines) · Ontic mode: Practice (a dietary discipline) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate (moderation) · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition (a preparatory ground for subtle practice) · Key relation: Enabling condition — prepares the body for prāṇāyāma.
coordinate.causal_status: Condition (a preparatory ground for subtle practice)
coordinate.key_relation: Enabling condition — prepares the body for prāṇāyāma.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Eating to nourish and have room for prana
facets.somatic: Easy digestion, sustained energy
facets.emotional: Food as friend, not coping mechanism
facets.cognitive: Mental clarity after meals
facets.psychic: The soul tends the body it refines through moderate diet, knowing the breath it would still is built from what it eats.

balanced.text:
Eating to nourish and have room for prana

Somatic: Easy digestion, sustained energy

Emotional: Food as friend, not coping mechanism

Cognitive: Mental clarity after meals

Psychic: The soul tends the body it refines through moderate diet, knowing the breath it would still is built from what it eats.
balanced.vignette: Sana eats until she is satisfied and no further, choosing food that leaves her light and clear rather than stuffed. There is room in her, after meals, for energy and attention.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Restrictive food rules creating obsession
facets.somatic: Under-nutrition, weakness
facets.emotional: Anxiety around every meal
facets.cognitive: Constant food-monitoring
facets.psychic: Dietary discipline tips into anxious restriction, the soul fixated on purity of food.

excess.text:
Restrictive food rules creating obsession

Somatic: Under-nutrition, weakness

Emotional: Anxiety around every meal

Cognitive: Constant food-monitoring

Psychic: Dietary discipline tips into anxious restriction, the soul fixated on purity of food.
excess.vignette: Every bite is now a rule for Raj—forbidden lists, permitted lists, guilt at a single lapse—until the discipline meant to free him has become a cage he lives inside. The obsession has quietly replaced the nourishment.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Overeating, eating to numb
facets.somatic: Heaviness, sluggish digestion, weight gain
facets.emotional: Food as emotional regulation
facets.cognitive: Post-meal stupor
facets.psychic: Heedless eating builds a heavy, turbulent body that no breath-work can easily steady.

deficient.text:
Overeating, eating to numb

Somatic: Heaviness, sluggish digestion, weight gain

Emotional: Food as emotional regulation

Cognitive: Post-meal stupor

Psychic: Heedless eating builds a heavy, turbulent body that no breath-work can easily steady.
deficient.vignette: Mark eats past full most nights, not from hunger but to blunt the day—a heaviness he reaches for in order to go numb. The food quiets him for an hour and dulls him for the rest.

attributes.title: Place & Time of Practice
attributes.aspect: Practice not in distant country, forest, capital, or crowd. Choose a good country, hut, well, raised platform. Avoid extreme seasons (winter cold, summer heat, monsoon, autumn). Spring and autumn ideal for beginning practice.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical (the outer conditions of practice) · Ontic mode: Condition (the right setting for sādhana) · Valence: Neutral — a supportive condition · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual, with environmental (ādhibhautika) dimension · Causal status: Enabling condition · Key relation: Support — the setting that lets subtle practice take root.
coordinate.key_relation: Support — the setting that lets subtle practice take root.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Practice has a sacred place and rhythm
facets.somatic: Body knows when and where to settle
facets.emotional: Place becomes psychological anchor
facets.cognitive: Conditioned associations support practice
facets.psychic: The soul chooses a fit place and time for practice, honoring the conditions that let subtle work take root.

balanced.text:
Practice has a sacred place and rhythm

Somatic: Body knows when and where to settle

Emotional: Place becomes psychological anchor

Cognitive: Conditioned associations support practice

Psychic: The soul chooses a fit place and time for practice, honoring the conditions that let subtle work take root.
balanced.vignette: Every dawn, in the same corner by the window, Leela sits—the place and the hour long since worn smooth into a rhythm the body now expects. The regularity carries her on mornings her will alone would not.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Ritual rigidity — only this place, this time, or nothing
facets.somatic: Cannot adapt to travel or change
facets.emotional: Practice anxiety if conditions imperfect
facets.cognitive: Form replaces substance
facets.psychic: The soul fusses endlessly over perfect conditions, the seeking itself an avoidance of practice.

excess.text:
Ritual rigidity — only this place, this time, or nothing

Somatic: Cannot adapt to travel or change

Emotional: Practice anxiety if conditions imperfect

Cognitive: Form replaces substance

Psychic: The soul fusses endlessly over perfect conditions, the seeking itself an avoidance of practice.
excess.vignette: Tom can only practice in his special room, on his special cushion, at 6:04 exactly; a trip, a guest, a change of plan, and the whole thing collapses. The scaffolding has become more important than the building.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Random, scattered, no rhythm
facets.somatic: Body never adapts to consistent practice
facets.emotional: No psychological anchor for return
facets.cognitive: Lack of conditioning sabotages depth
facets.psychic: The soul attends to no conditions at all, practicing haphazardly where focus cannot gather.

deficient.text:
Random, scattered, no rhythm

Somatic: Body never adapts to consistent practice

Emotional: No psychological anchor for return

Cognitive: Lack of conditioning sabotages depth

Psychic: The soul attends to no conditions at all, practicing haphazardly where focus cannot gather.
deficient.vignette: Priya means to meditate, and does—sometimes, somewhere, for however long, whenever it happens to occur to her. With no place and no rhythm to hold it, the practice never quite takes root.

attributes.title: Nadi Shuddhi
attributes.aspect: Before regulated pranayama, channels must be cleansed. Samanu (mental, with bija mantras and visualization of elements) or Nirmanu (physical via shatkarmas). The Vayu cannot enter impure nadis.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (the subtle channels) · Ontic mode: Practice (channel-purification) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate (purification) · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition / mechanism (clears the channels for energy to flow) · Key relation: Enabling condition — prepares the nāḍīs before deeper prāṇāyāma.
coordinate.causal_status: Condition / mechanism (clears the channels for energy to flow)
coordinate.key_relation: Enabling condition — prepares the nāḍīs before deeper prāṇāyāma.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Energetic channels regularly cleaned and balanced
facets.somatic: Both nostrils clear, breathing balanced
facets.emotional: Steady, neither anxious nor torpid
facets.cognitive: Mental clarity, both hemispheres engaged
facets.psychic: The soul cleanses the subtle channels first, knowing a purified system lets the life-force flow unobstructed.

balanced.text:
Energetic channels regularly cleaned and balanced

Somatic: Both nostrils clear, breathing balanced

Emotional: Steady, neither anxious nor torpid

Cognitive: Mental clarity, both hemispheres engaged

Psychic: The soul cleanses the subtle channels first, knowing a purified system lets the life-force flow unobstructed.
balanced.vignette: Nadia keeps her energy channels clear the way one keeps a house—regular, unfussy maintenance—so that when she sits, the current runs clean and the practice goes deep.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Cleansing obsession blocking actual practice
facets.somatic: Over-purified, depleted
facets.emotional: Anxiety about purity
facets.cognitive: Endless preparation, no actual practice
facets.psychic: The soul over-fixates on purification, endlessly preparing and never proceeding.

excess.text:
Cleansing obsession blocking actual practice

Somatic: Over-purified, depleted

Emotional: Anxiety about purity

Cognitive: Endless preparation, no actual practice

Psychic: The soul over-fixates on purification, endlessly preparing and never proceeding.
excess.vignette: Sam has spent three years ‘clearing his channels’ and has not yet begun the actual practice; there is always one more purification to do first. The cleansing has become a way of never arriving.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Practicing on a blocked system
facets.somatic: Forced breath in clogged channels causing harm
facets.emotional: Imbalance never resolved
facets.cognitive: Practice produces no real shift
facets.psychic: The soul skips the cleansing, forcing energy through clogged channels and destabilizing itself.

deficient.text:
Practicing on a blocked system

Somatic: Forced breath in clogged channels causing harm

Emotional: Imbalance never resolved

Cognitive: Practice produces no real shift

Psychic: The soul skips the cleansing, forcing energy through clogged channels and destabilizing itself.
deficient.vignette: Dev forces long sessions through a system that is knotted and congested, and wonders why they feel like pushing water uphill. He is practicing hard on a blocked pipe.

attributes.title: Sahita Kumbhaka
attributes.aspect: Pranayama with retention; can be Sagarbha (with bija mantra repetition and visualization) or Nirgarbha (without). Standard ratio 1:4:2 (inhale:retain:exhale). Three levels: lowest 12 matras, middling 16, best 20.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital (breath retention with in / out breath) · Ontic mode: Practice (a form of breath retention) · Valence: Cultivated discipline · Guṇa: Rajas–Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (gathers and concentrates the life-force) · Key relation: Part — a kumbhaka within the prāṇāyāma family; precedes Kevala Kumbhaka.
coordinate.seat: Physical–Vital (breath retention with in / out breath)
coordinate.key_relation: Part — a kumbhaka within the prāṇāyāma family; precedes Kevala Kumbhaka.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Capacity to hold breath calmly under load
facets.somatic: Increased CO2 tolerance, calm in stress
facets.emotional: Mental composure carries over to life
facets.cognitive: Concentrated single-pointedness developed
facets.psychic: The soul retains the breath consciously, the held pause gathering and concentrating the life-force.

balanced.text:
Capacity to hold breath calmly under load

Somatic: Increased CO2 tolerance, calm in stress

Emotional: Mental composure carries over to life

Cognitive: Concentrated single-pointedness developed

Psychic: The soul retains the breath consciously, the held pause gathering and concentrating the life-force.
balanced.vignette: Under the weight of a hard moment, Mira can hold her breath a beat—steady, calm—and feel the panic lose its grip. The held breath has become a handle she knows how to reach for.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced retention damaging the system
facets.somatic: Headaches, pressure, fainting
facets.emotional: Anxiety from over-doing
facets.cognitive: Bravado-driven practice
facets.psychic: The soul forces long retentions, straining the system in pursuit of power.

excess.text:
Forced retention damaging the system

Somatic: Headaches, pressure, fainting

Emotional: Anxiety from over-doing

Cognitive: Bravado-driven practice

Psychic: The soul forces long retentions, straining the system in pursuit of power.
excess.vignette: Determined to ‘advance,’ Jon holds his breath far past comfort, chasing longer and longer counts, and pays for it in headaches and a jangled nervous system. He is forcing a door that only opens gently.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Never explores breath capacity
facets.somatic: Reactive breath under stress
facets.emotional: Anxiety has no off-switch
facets.cognitive: Cannot focus single-pointedly
facets.psychic: The soul never retains the breath, the subtle pressure that practice builds never gathered.

deficient.text:
Never explores breath capacity

Somatic: Reactive breath under stress

Emotional: Anxiety has no off-switch

Cognitive: Cannot focus single-pointedly

Psychic: The soul never retains the breath, the subtle pressure that practice builds never gathered.
deficient.vignette: Anna breathes shallow and quick all day and has never once explored what a full, consciously held breath could do. A whole capacity sits unused inside her.

attributes.title: Surya Bheda Kumbhaka
attributes.aspect: Right-nostril (solar) inhalation, retention with Jalandhara, left-nostril exhalation. Until perspiration breaks at nail-tips and hair-roots. Destroys decay, awakens Kundalini, increases bodily fire.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital (right-nostril, solar current) · Ontic mode: Practice (a heating breath retention) · Valence: Cultivated discipline · Guṇa: Rajas-dominant (heating) · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (kindles the solar current) · Key relation: Part — a kumbhaka of the prāṇāyāma family; works the Piṅgalā (solar) nāḍī.
coordinate.key_relation: Part — a kumbhaka of the prāṇāyāma family; works the Piṅgalā (solar) nāḍī.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Reliable heating practice for sluggish states
facets.somatic: Warming, increased metabolic activity
facets.emotional: Lifts apathetic states
facets.cognitive: Sharpens dull mind
facets.psychic: The soul kindles the solar, heating current through right-nostril retention, warming and energizing the system.

balanced.text:
Reliable heating practice for sluggish states

Somatic: Warming, increased metabolic activity

Emotional: Lifts apathetic states

Cognitive: Sharpens dull mind

Psychic: The soul kindles the solar, heating current through right-nostril retention, warming and energizing the system.
balanced.vignette: On grey, sluggish mornings Kavi turns to the warming breath and feels the fog lift and the system kindle—a reliable little fire for the days that need one.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Used when already heated; aggravates
facets.somatic: Overheating, sweating, palpitations
facets.emotional: Aggression, restlessness
facets.cognitive: Irritability, anger flares
facets.psychic: The soul over-heats, forcing the solar practice until the system is agitated and dry.

Used when already heated; aggravates

Emotional: Aggression, restlessness

Cognitive: Irritability, anger flares

Psychic: The soul over-heats, forcing the solar practice until the system is agitated and dry.
excess.vignette: Already hot-tempered and overheated, Ravi keeps doing the heating breath because it feels powerful, and grows more irritable, flushed, and volatile by the week. He is pouring fuel on a fire.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Stuck in chronic low-energy default
facets.somatic: Cold hands/feet, weak digestion
facets.emotional: Low motivation, depression-edge
facets.cognitive: Foggy, slow thinking
facets.psychic: The soul never kindles the warming current, its energy left cold and sluggish.

deficient.text:
Stuck in chronic low-energy default
