Emotional: Low motivation, depression-edge

Cognitive: Foggy, slow thinking

Psychic: The soul never kindles the warming current, its energy left cold and sluggish.
deficient.vignette: Dana lives permanently at low ebb—cold, sluggish, unmotivated—and has never learned the one practice that could gently warm her back to life. The ember stays unlit.

attributes.title: Ujjayi Kumbhaka
attributes.aspect: Draw breath through both nostrils with throat constriction; retain with Jalandhara; exhale through left nostril. Cures phlegm disorders, nerve diseases, dysentery, consumption, cough, fever.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital (throat-sounded breath with retention) · Ontic mode: Practice (breath retention) · Valence: Cultivated discipline · Guṇa: Sattva–Rajas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (steadies and deepens the current) · Key relation: Part — a kumbhaka of the prāṇāyāma family.
coordinate.key_relation: Part — a kumbhaka of the prāṇāyāma family.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Portable, all-day breath anchor
facets.somatic: Slowed breath, calmer heart, warmth
facets.emotional: Steady throughout activity
facets.cognitive: Subtle internal anchor everywhere
facets.psychic: The soul breathes the victorious breath, the soft throat-sound steadying and deepening the current.

balanced.text:
Portable, all-day breath anchor

Somatic: Slowed breath, calmer heart, warmth

Emotional: Steady throughout activity

Cognitive: Subtle internal anchor everywhere

Psychic: The soul breathes the victorious breath, the soft throat-sound steadying and deepening the current.
balanced.vignette: Through the whole workday Kira keeps a soft ocean-sound in her throat and a gentle steadiness in her breath—a portable calm she can carry into any meeting. No one even notices she is doing it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Loud, forced throat constriction
facets.somatic: Throat irritation, neck tension
facets.emotional: Performative, showy breathing
facets.cognitive: Technique over substance
facets.psychic: The soul forces the constriction, the strained sound straining the body.

Loud, forced throat constriction

Psychic: The soul forces the constriction, the strained sound straining the body.
excess.vignette: Determined to ‘do it right,’ Ben constricts his throat so hard the breath rasps and strains, loud and effortful, and leaves him more tense than before. He has turned a whisper into a wrestling match.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Misses this universally accessible tool
facets.somatic: Breath remains shallow and reactive
facets.emotional: No portable anchor
facets.cognitive: No internal soundscape
facets.psychic: The soul never gathers the breath this way, its respiration shallow and unsteady.

deficient.text:
Misses this universally accessible tool

Somatic: Breath remains shallow and reactive

Emotional: No portable anchor

Cognitive: No internal soundscape

deficient.vignette: Sara has read about the ocean-breath a dozen times and never once tried it—one of the simplest, most portable tools there is, sitting unused in her pocket.

attributes.title: Bhramari Kumbhaka
attributes.aspect: Practice at past midnight in silence, ears closed. Inner sounds emerge progressively: crickets, lute, thunder, drum, beetle, bells, gongs, trumpets. The last is Anahata nada — the unstruck sound at the heart. Leads to Samadhi.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental (bee-humming breath with retention) · Ontic mode: Practice (a soothing breath retention) · Valence: Cultivated discipline · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant (calming) · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (the inner vibration soothes and gathers the mind) · Key relation: Part — a kumbhaka of the prāṇāyāma family.
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (the inner vibration soothes and gathers the mind)
coordinate.key_relation: Part — a kumbhaka of the prāṇāyāma family.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Subtle daily attunement to inner sound dimension
facets.somatic: Vagal-tone improvement, calmer system
facets.emotional: Tangible peace, inner harmony
facets.cognitive: Mind absorbs in sound, thinking ceases
facets.psychic: The soul hums the bee-breath, the inner vibration soothing the mind and drawing it inward.

balanced.text:
Subtle daily attunement to inner sound dimension

Emotional: Tangible peace, inner harmony

Cognitive: Mind absorbs in sound, thinking ceases

Psychic: The soul hums the bee-breath, the inner vibration soothing the mind and drawing it inward.
balanced.vignette: Each evening Meera hums low and long, and behind the hum a subtle inner ringing opens—a quiet attunement she has come to trust as her own dimension of stillness.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Hallucination mistaken for genuine nada
facets.somatic: Tinnitus confused with inner sound
facets.emotional: Spiritual ego attached to ‘hearing’
facets.cognitive: Magical thinking about experiences
facets.psychic: The soul over-practices, chasing the soothing buzz as an end in itself.

Hallucination mistaken for genuine nada

excess.vignette: Convinced the faint ringing in his ears is a cosmic sound, Karl builds a whole mythology around it and chases ever-stranger ‘signs.’ He has mistaken noise for revelation.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Never accesses inner sound dimension
facets.somatic: Always external sonic environment
facets.emotional: Cannot find inner silence
facets.cognitive: Mind continuously externalized
facets.psychic: The soul never uses the calming hum, its mind left to its unsoothed agitation.

Never accesses inner sound dimension

attributes.title: Pancha Vayu
attributes.aspect: Five primary pranas: Prana (heart, intake), Apana (anus, elimination), Samana (navel, digestion), Udana (throat, expression), Vyana (whole body, circulation). Plus five subsidiary vayus controlling burp, blink, sneeze, yawn, and post-death sound.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (the body’s energetic intelligence) · Ontic mode: Constituent — the five directional currents of prāṇa · Valence: Neutral faculty · Guṇa: Spans all (each vāyu differently) · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (govern the body’s movements) · Key relation: Whole — the aggregate of Prāṇa, Apāna, Samāna, Udāna, and Vyāna.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Constituent — the five directional currents of prāṇa
coordinate.key_relation: Whole — the aggregate of Prāṇa, Apāna, Samāna, Udāna, and Vyāna.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: All five vayus functioning harmoniously
facets.somatic: Even respiration, digestion, circulation, expression
facets.emotional: Balanced inflow and outflow
facets.cognitive: Integrated cognitive function
facets.psychic: The soul learns the five vital airs and how each governs its function, working with the body’s energetic intelligence.

balanced.text:
All five vayus functioning harmoniously

Somatic: Even respiration, digestion, circulation, expression

Emotional: Balanced inflow and outflow

Cognitive: Integrated cognitive function

Psychic: The soul learns the five vital airs and how each governs its function, working with the body’s energetic intelligence.
balanced.vignette: In Ram the five currents run in concert—breath drawing in, waste moving out, food assimilating, energy rising, everything distributing evenly—and the result is simply a body that works, quietly and well.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: One vayu dominating others
facets.somatic: Over-emphasis on one function (e.g., over-eating, over-talking)
facets.emotional: Imbalanced expression
facets.cognitive: Cognitive narrowness
facets.psychic: The soul over-technicalizes the airs, lost in subtle anatomy rather than felt practice.

excess.text:
One vayu dominating others

Somatic: Over-emphasis on one function (e.g., over-eating, over-talking)

Emotional: Imbalanced expression

Cognitive: Cognitive narrowness

Psychic: The soul over-technicalizes the airs, lost in subtle anatomy rather than felt practice.
excess.vignette: One current has taken over in Dev: all upward drive and no grounding, or all elimination and no intake, and the system lists hard to one side. The team of five has a bully on it.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Specific vayu weakened
facets.somatic: Specific organ system weak
facets.emotional: Inability to take in (prana) or release (apana)
facets.cognitive: Specific cognitive deficiency
facets.psychic: The soul is ignorant of the vital airs, its energy uncoordinated and leaking.

deficient.text:
Specific vayu weakened

Emotional: Inability to take in (prana) or release (apana)

Cognitive: Specific cognitive deficiency

Psychic: The soul is ignorant of the vital airs, its energy uncoordinated and leaking.
deficient.vignette: One of Sara’s currents has gone weak—the assimilating fire, say—and though the others carry on, the whole system runs a little short, a little tired, a link in the chain gone slack.

attributes.title: Ajapa Gayatri (So’ham)
attributes.aspect: Every breath naturally says ‘so’ (inhale) and ‘ham’ (exhale) — totaling 21,600 repetitions daily. Every living being unconsciously chants this; the yogi makes it conscious. ‘I am That.’

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic (the breath’s natural mantra) · Ontic mode: Practice (an effortless breath-mantra) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (rides the breath toward its source) · Key relation: Correspondence — unites breath and mantra (so’ham, ‘I am That’).
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (rides the breath toward its source)
coordinate.key_relation: Correspondence — unites breath and mantra (so’ham, ‘I am That’).

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Continuous awareness of natural mantra throughout day
facets.somatic: Each breath becomes mindful
facets.emotional: Continuous remembrance of true nature
facets.cognitive: Mind has constant return point
facets.psychic: The soul follows the breath’s natural mantra — so’ham, ‘I am That’ — riding each breath toward its source.

balanced.text:
Continuous awareness of natural mantra throughout day

Somatic: Each breath becomes mindful

Emotional: Continuous remembrance of true nature

Cognitive: Mind has constant return point

Psychic: The soul follows the breath’s natural mantra — so’ham, ‘I am That’ — riding each breath toward its source.
balanced.vignette: Through her day, in odd quiet moments, Leela hears it—so on the in-breath, ham on the out—the mantra the breath has been chanting all along, and she simply listens. It threads a quiet remembrance through the ordinary hours.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Obsessive monitoring of every breath
facets.somatic: Self-conscious breathing replacing natural
facets.emotional: Spiritual identification as ego-act
facets.cognitive: ’Am I being mindful enough?’ meta-loops
facets.psychic: The soul over-counts and over-manages the repetitions, the effort crowding out the ease.

excess.text:
Obsessive monitoring of every breath

Somatic: Self-conscious breathing replacing natural

Emotional: Spiritual identification as ego-act

Cognitive: ’Am I being mindful enough?’ meta-loops

Psychic: The soul over-counts and over-manages the repetitions, the effort crowding out the ease.
excess.vignette: Sam now monitors every single breath for the mantra, counting, checking, correcting, until the natural thing has become a tense surveillance operation. He is strangling the very ease he meant to notice.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Breath happens unconsciously, life lived absently
facets.somatic: No body-mind connection through breath
facets.emotional: Disconnection from being
facets.cognitive: 21,600 missed opportunities each day
facets.psychic: The soul never hears the breath’s natural prayer, its respiration a mechanical exchange only.

deficient.text:
Breath happens unconsciously, life lived absently

Emotional: Disconnection from being

Cognitive: 21,600 missed opportunities each day

Psychic: The soul never hears the breath’s natural prayer, its respiration a mechanical exchange only.
deficient.vignette: The breath goes in and out of Dan all day, chanting its silent syllables, and he never hears a note of it—present for none of the ten thousand quiet reminders.

attributes.title: Kevala Kumbhaka
attributes.aspect: Spontaneous, effortless breath suspension — neither inhale nor exhale needed. The breath naturally pauses on its own. Considered the highest pranayama; what one cannot accomplish through this is nothing on earth.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic (spontaneous breath-suspension) · Ontic mode: State — the spontaneous cessation of breath in deep absorption · Valence: Neutral — an attained state · Guṇa: Sattva (beyond effort) · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Fruit (the culmination of retention practice) · Key relation: Sequence — the effortless kumbhaka to which Sahita Kumbhaka leads.
coordinate.ontic_mode: State — the spontaneous cessation of breath in deep absorption
coordinate.key_relation: Sequence — the effortless kumbhaka to which Sahita Kumbhaka leads.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Effortless inner stillness during natural breath pauses
facets.somatic: Breath naturally slow, often suspended
facets.emotional: Deep settledness as default
facets.cognitive: Spaciousness between thoughts present
facets.psychic: The soul reaches the spontaneous suspension where breath ceases of itself, the deep stillness of absorption.

balanced.text:
Effortless inner stillness during natural breath pauses

Somatic: Breath naturally slow, often suspended

Emotional: Deep settledness as default

Cognitive: Spaciousness between thoughts present

Psychic: The soul reaches the spontaneous suspension where breath ceases of itself, the deep stillness of absorption.
balanced.vignette: Sometimes, deep in her sit, Nadia notices the breath has simply stopped of its own accord, and she rests in the still gap without reaching to breathe. It is not held; it is suspended, and the suspension is peace.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forcing kevala — defeats its nature
facets.somatic: Strain trying to hold what shouldn’t be forced
facets.emotional: Frustration at lack of ‘experience’
facets.cognitive: Trying to manufacture spontaneity
facets.psychic: The soul forces the suspension, mistaking willed breath-holding for the spontaneous pause.

excess.text:
Forcing kevala — defeats its nature

Somatic: Strain trying to hold what shouldn’t be forced

Emotional: Frustration at lack of ‘experience’

Cognitive: Trying to manufacture spontaneity

Psychic: The soul forces the suspension, mistaking willed breath-holding for the spontaneous pause.
excess.vignette: Ben read about the breathless state and now tries to force it—clamping the breath, straining for stillness—which of course produces only strain. He is chasing a stillness that comes only when he stops chasing.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No experience of natural breath suspension
facets.somatic: Constantly breathing, never pause
facets.emotional: Restlessness as constant state
facets.cognitive: No silence between thoughts ever
facets.psychic: The soul never approaches the still pause, its breath always restless and driven.

deficient.text:
No experience of natural breath suspension

Cognitive: No silence between thoughts ever

Psychic: The soul never approaches the still pause, its breath always restless and driven.
deficient.vignette: For Priya the breath is always moving, always effortful; she has never once tasted the natural pause where it rests by itself. The doorway is there, and she has never stood in it.

attributes.title: Pranayama
attributes.aspect: Conscious regulation of breath. Svatmarama’s central insight (2.2): ‘When the breath moves, the mind moves; when breath is still, the mind is still.’

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital (regulation of the breath) · Ontic mode: Practice (the discipline of breath-regulation) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (steadies the mind by steadying the breath) · Key relation: Genus — the family of which the kumbhakas and breaths are species; the fourth limb of Yoga.
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (steadies the mind by steadying the breath)
coordinate.key_relation: Genus — the family of which the kumbhakas and breaths are species; the fourth limb of Yoga.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Breath as conscious daily ally for state regulation
facets.somatic: Diaphragmatic, slow, even breathing as baseline
facets.emotional: Access to calm anytime via breath
facets.cognitive: Mind-body coordination via breath
facets.psychic: The soul regulates the breath to still the mind, knowing as the breath steadies the thoughts steady too.

balanced.text:
Breath as conscious daily ally for state regulation

Somatic: Diaphragmatic, slow, even breathing as baseline

Emotional: Access to calm anytime via breath

Cognitive: Mind-body coordination via breath

Psychic: The soul regulates the breath to still the mind, knowing as the breath steadies the thoughts steady too.
balanced.vignette: When Kira is anxious she lengthens the exhale; when dull, she brightens the breath—using it, all day, as a conscious dial for her own state. The breath has become a trusted ally.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced, manipulated breathing damaging system
facets.somatic: Hyperventilation, dizziness, panic from over-practice
facets.emotional: Anxiety from too-aggressive techniques
facets.cognitive: Technique obsession losing the purpose
facets.psychic: The soul over-manipulates the breath, forcing techniques that agitate rather than calm.

excess.text:

Cognitive: Technique obsession losing the purpose

excess.vignette: Determined to master it fast, Jon over-manipulates his breath for an hour a day—forcing, straining, ignoring the warning signs—and ends up with the very disturbances the practice was meant to cure.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Breath ignored, allowed to follow stress
facets.somatic: Shallow chest-breathing, chronic tension
facets.emotional: Anxiety has no off-switch
facets.cognitive: Mind trapped in fight-flight loops
facets.psychic: The soul leaves the breath unregulated, the uneven breath keeping the mind forever stirred.

deficient.text:
Breath ignored, allowed to follow stress

Emotional: Anxiety has no off-switch

Cognitive: Mind trapped in fight-flight loops

Psychic: The soul leaves the breath unregulated, the uneven breath keeping the mind forever stirred.
deficient.vignette: Dana’s breath simply follows her stress, shallow and ragged whenever she is tense, and it has never occurred to her that she could take the wheel. The most available tool she owns goes unused.

attributes.title: Nadi Shodhana
attributes.aspect: Alternate nostril breathing—inhale through one nostril, exhale through the other, alternating; purifies the 72,000 nadis (energy channels) within three months of practice.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital (alternate-nostril breathing) · Ontic mode: Practice (a balancing breath) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant (balancing) · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (harmonizes the solar and lunar currents) · Key relation: Part — a prāṇāyāma that balances Iḍā and Piṅgalā.
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (harmonizes the solar and lunar currents)
coordinate.key_relation: Part — a prāṇāyāma that balances Iḍā and Piṅgalā.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Balanced flow between active and receptive modes
facets.somatic: Both nostrils clear, autonomic balance
facets.emotional: Even-keeled, neither anxious nor depressed

attributes.title: Eulogy of Chant on Breath (Prana Stotra)
attributes.aspect: The gods discovered that demons (negative forces) could pierce all sense-functions with evil except Prana. Hence chanting performed in alignment with breath (prana-stotra) carries inherent cosmic protection. This is the basis of all breath-chant practices—rhythmic chanting that follows the breath’s natural cycle.

balanced.vignette: As Sana chants, the words ride the breath—each syllable landing cleanly on an inhale or an exhale—until sound, breath, and body are one resonant thing, and the chanting steadies her whole system.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced breath-chant techniques creating pranic disturbance—rushing past natural rhythm.
facets.somatic: Body strain from forced chant-breath combinations.
facets.emotional: Emotional disturbance from improper practice.
facets.cognitive: Mental disturbance from advanced techniques without preparation.
facets.psychic: The soul over-ritualizes the praise, the form outrunning the recognition.

excess.text:
Forced breath-chant techniques creating pranic disturbance—rushing past natural rhythm.

Somatic: Body strain from forced chant-breath combinations.

Emotional: Emotional disturbance from improper practice.

Cognitive: Mental disturbance from advanced techniques without preparation.

Psychic: The soul over-ritualizes the praise, the form outrunning the recognition.
excess.vignette: Rushing to ‘get through’ the chant, Ben forces the words past the breath’s natural rhythm, gasping and straining, and ends up more agitated than when he began. Speed has broken the resonance.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Chanting disconnected from breath—mechanical recitation without somatic integration.
facets.somatic: Body’s breath ignored during recitation.
facets.emotional: Emotional emptiness in chant practice.
facets.cognitive: Mind chants without integrating breath.
facets.psychic: The soul reveres nothing in the breath, taking respiration as mere mechanics.

deficient.text:
Chanting disconnected from breath—mechanical recitation without somatic integration.

Somatic: Body’s breath ignored during recitation.

Emotional: Emotional emptiness in chant practice.

Cognitive: Mind chants without integrating breath.

Psychic: The soul reveres nothing in the breath, taking respiration as mere mechanics.
deficient.vignette: Dan recites the words correctly and feels nothing—mouth moving, breath elsewhere, the chant a mechanical exercise with no body in it. The sound never reaches him.

attributes.title: Rayi and Prana (Matter and Life Force)
attributes.aspect: Prajapati created the pair Rayi (matter/food) and Prana (life-force) to produce beings. Sun is Prana; Moon is Rayi. All beings result from this fundamental polarity.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital (matter and life-force paired) · Ontic mode: Framework / cosmological principle (the pair whose union produces beings) · Valence: Neutral — a cosmological truth · Guṇa: Rayi tamasic, Prāṇa rajasic–sattvic · Scale: Cosmic (Ādhidaivika), refracted individually · Causal status: Cause (the two principles from which creation proceeds) · Key relation: Complementary pair — matter (Rayi) and life-force (Prāṇa).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework / cosmological principle (the pair whose union produces beings)
coordinate.causal_status: Cause (the two principles from which creation proceeds)
coordinate.key_relation: Complementary pair — matter (Rayi) and life-force (Prāṇa).

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Balanced relationship with both matter (Rayi) and life-force (Prana). Neither materialism nor escapism.
facets.somatic: Body well-fed (Rayi) and well-energized (Prana).
facets.emotional: Emotional groundedness in matter and uplift through prana.
facets.cognitive: Mind understanding both dimensions of existence.
facets.psychic: The soul honors the pair of matter and life-force as the two principles whose union produces all beings.

balanced.text:
Balanced relationship with both matter (Rayi) and life-force (Prana). Neither materialism nor escapism.

Somatic: Body well-fed (Rayi) and well-energized (Prana).

Emotional: Emotional groundedness in matter and uplift through prana.

Cognitive: Mind understanding both dimensions of existence.

Psychic: The soul honors the pair of matter and life-force as the two principles whose union produces all beings.
balanced.vignette: Kira honors both the material and the vital—she eats and rests and earns and builds, and she also breathes and feels and reaches beyond—neither drowning in matter nor fleeing it. The two run in balance.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Imbalance—either over-matter (gluttony, materialism) or over-prana (manic activity).
facets.somatic: Either over-eating or burnt-out energy.
facets.emotional: Either heavy materialism or anxious activity.
facets.cognitive: Mental fixation on one pole only.
facets.psychic: The soul over-emphasizes one pole, exalting spirit and despising matter or the reverse.

excess.text:
Imbalance—either over-matter (gluttony, materialism) or over-prana (manic activity).

Somatic: Either over-eating or burnt-out energy.

Emotional: Either heavy materialism or anxious activity.

Cognitive: Mental fixation on one pole only.

Psychic: The soul over-emphasizes one pole, exalting spirit and despising matter or the reverse.
excess.vignette: Tom lists hard to one side—either gorging on the material (more food, more stuff, more comfort) or manic with a restless energy that never lands. One pole has swallowed the other.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Neither matter nor energy in healthy state—malnourishment and depletion of vital force.
facets.somatic: Body starved and depleted.
facets.emotional: Emotional emptiness, no vital flow.
facets.cognitive: Mind without nourishment or energy.
facets.psychic: The soul sees only inert matter, blind to the life-force that animates it.

deficient.text:
Neither matter nor energy in healthy state—malnourishment and depletion of vital force.

Somatic: Body starved and depleted.

Emotional: Emotional emptiness, no vital flow.

Cognitive: Mind without nourishment or energy.

Psychic: The soul sees only inert matter, blind to the life-force that animates it.
deficient.vignette: Neither well-fed nor well-energized, Sara runs on empty in both registers at once—undernourished in body and depleted in spirit, the matter and the life-force both gone thin.

attributes.attribute: Prana as Ruler of the Body
attributes.title: Prana as Ruler of the Body
attributes.aspect: Prana is supreme among the deities/powers in the body. When Prana withdraws, all others must follow. Prana sustains all senses and the mind itself.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (the chief of the body’s powers) · Ontic mode: Faculty / principle (prāṇa as sovereign of the faculties) · Valence: Neutral faculty · Guṇa: Rajas–Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (the master power on which all faculties depend) · Key relation: Genus — chief among the senses and vital airs; the master key to the rest.
coordinate.seat: Vital (the chief of the body’s powers)
coordinate.ontic_mode: Faculty / principle (prāṇa as sovereign of the faculties)
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (the master power on which all faculties depend)
coordinate.key_relation: Genus — chief among the senses and vital airs; the master key to the rest.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Recognition of Prana as the central power, cultivation through proper breath, awareness, vitality.
facets.somatic: Healthy prana sustaining body, all systems functioning.
facets.emotional: Vital emotions, healthy energy.
facets.cognitive: Mind supported by sufficient prana.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes prāṇa as chief among the body’s powers, tending the breath as the master key to all the rest.

balanced.text:
Recognition of Prana as the central power, cultivation through proper breath, awareness, vitality.

Somatic: Healthy prana sustaining body, all systems functioning.

Emotional: Vital emotions, healthy energy.

Cognitive: Mind supported by sufficient prana.

Psychic: The soul recognizes prāṇa as chief among the body’s powers, tending the breath as the master key to all the rest.
balanced.vignette: Ram tends his prana as the central power it is—through good breath, rest, and attention—and finds that when the life-force is strong, every other system quietly falls into line.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced pranayama or breath-manipulation causing imbalance—prana-disorders, kundalini complications.
facets.somatic: Pranic disorders from forced practices.
facets.emotional: Emotional volatility from prana imbalance.
facets.cognitive: Mental disturbance from forced energy work.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on prāṇa-manipulation, mistaking energetic control for the goal.

excess.text:
Forced pranayama or breath-manipulation causing imbalance—prana-disorders, kundalini complications.

Somatic: Pranic disorders from forced practices.

Emotional: Emotional volatility from prana imbalance.

Cognitive: Mental disturbance from forced energy work.

Psychic: The soul fixates on prāṇa-manipulation, mistaking energetic control for the goal.
excess.vignette: Forcing aggressive breathwork to ‘boost his prana,’ Ben throws the whole system off—disorders, surges, a nervous energy that will not settle. He tried to rule the ruler by force.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Prana depleted—chronic low energy, every system suffering, depression, weakness.
facets.somatic: Chronic fatigue, weak vital functions.
facets.emotional: Emotional depression, no energy for engagement.
facets.cognitive: Mental fog, unable to function.
facets.psychic: The soul ignores the ruling life-force, neglecting the breath on which all its faculties depend.

deficient.text:
Prana depleted—chronic low energy, every system suffering, depression, weakness.

Somatic: Chronic fatigue, weak vital functions.

Emotional: Emotional depression, no energy for engagement.

Cognitive: Mental fog, unable to function.

Psychic: The soul ignores the ruling life-force, neglecting the breath on which all its faculties depend.
deficient.vignette: Dana’s life-force has run down to a trickle, and with it everything sags—low energy, low mood, low immunity—a whole person dimming because the central power is depleted.

attributes.title: Five Pranas (Prana, Apana, Vyana, Udana, Samana)
attributes.aspect: Five life-forces: Prana (inhalation), Apana (elimination), Samana (digestion), Udana (upward movement), Vyana (circulation). All bodily and mental functions depend on these.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (the five functional currents) · Ontic mode: Constituent — the five functional currents of prāṇa · Valence: Neutral faculty · Guṇa: Spans all (each current differently) · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (each governs a sphere of bodily function) · Key relation: Whole — the five currents; the same set as the Pañca Vāyu.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Constituent — the five functional currents of prāṇa
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (each governs a sphere of bodily function)
coordinate.key_relation: Whole — the five currents; the same set as the Pañca Vāyu.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: All five pranas in harmony—breathing, digestion, elimination, energy ascent, circulation all healthy.
facets.somatic: All five pranas functioning optimally.
facets.emotional: Emotional life supported by balanced pranas.
facets.cognitive: Mental clarity from harmonious prana flow.
facets.psychic: The soul works with the five life-forces, each governing its sphere, the body’s energy intelligently coordinated.

balanced.text:
All five pranas in harmony—breathing, digestion, elimination, energy ascent, circulation all healthy.

Somatic: All five pranas functioning optimally.

Emotional: Emotional life supported by balanced pranas.

Cognitive: Mental clarity from harmonious prana flow.

Psychic: The soul works with the five life-forces, each governing its sphere, the body’s energy intelligently coordinated.
balanced.vignette: In Leela the five vital functions all run well—breath, digestion, elimination, the upward lift, the even circulation—and the body simply hums along, each function quietly doing its job.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive focus on prana-manipulation creating imbalances—forced yoga practices disturbing natural flow.
facets.somatic: Pranic imbalances from over-practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional disturbance from forced practices.
facets.cognitive: Mental disturbance from prana-disorders.
facets.psychic: The soul over-technicalizes the five, lost in subtle anatomy rather than living practice.

excess.text:
Excessive focus on prana-manipulation creating imbalances—forced yoga practices disturbing natural flow.

Somatic: Pranic imbalances from over-practice.

Emotional: Emotional disturbance from forced practices.

Cognitive: Mental disturbance from prana-disorders.

Psychic: The soul over-technicalizes the five, lost in subtle anatomy rather than living practice.
excess.vignette: Obsessed with ‘prana work,’ Jon manipulates his breath and energy so aggressively that he disturbs the natural flow, and the finely-tuned system he was trying to optimize begins to misfire.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Disregard for prana—poor breathing, bad digestion, depleted vitality, no awareness of life-force.
facets.somatic: Shallow breathing, weak digestion, low energy.
facets.emotional: Emotional life undernourished by prana.
facets.cognitive: Mental dullness from depleted pranas.
facets.psychic: The soul is ignorant of the vital forces, its energy uncoordinated and dispersed.

deficient.text:
Disregard for prana—poor breathing, bad digestion, depleted vitality, no awareness of life-force.

Somatic: Shallow breathing, weak digestion, low energy.

Emotional: Emotional life undernourished by prana.

Cognitive: Mental dullness from depleted pranas.

Psychic: The soul is ignorant of the vital forces, its energy uncoordinated and dispersed.
deficient.vignette: Sara pays her life-force no mind at all—breathing badly, digesting poorly, running on fumes—with no sense that vitality is even something one could tend. The whole engine runs neglected.

attributes.title: Sixteen Kalas (Parts of the Person)
attributes.aspect: The Purusha within has sixteen kalas (parts): prana, faith, space, air, fire, water, earth, senses, mind, food, virility, austerity, mantras, karma, worlds, name. When realized, all sixteen merge into the Self.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital–Mental (the constituents of the person) · Ontic mode: Constituent — the sixteen parts composing the individual · Valence: Neutral — a structural analysis to trace back to the Self · Guṇa: Spans all three · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Manifestation (the parts constituting the person) · Key relation: Whole — the sixteen kālas traced back to their indivisible ground.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Constituent — the sixteen parts composing the individual
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a structural analysis to trace back to the Self
coordinate.key_relation: Whole — the sixteen kālas traced back to their indivisible ground.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Recognition that all sixteen kalas are parts of Self, ultimately merging into pure consciousness.
facets.somatic: Body’s elements seen as kalas of Self.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns recognized as kalas merging into Self.
facets.cognitive: Mind understands its own components as parts of greater Self.
facets.psychic: The soul contemplates the sixteen parts that constitute the person, tracing them back toward the indivisible Self.

balanced.text:
Recognition that all sixteen kalas are parts of Self, ultimately merging into pure consciousness.

Somatic: Body’s elements seen as kalas of Self.

Emotional: Emotional patterns recognized as kalas merging into Self.

Cognitive: Mind understands its own components as parts of greater Self.

Psychic: The soul contemplates the sixteen parts that constitute the person, tracing them back toward the indivisible Self.
balanced.vignette: Ram can see his many parts—breath, senses, mind, faith, name, and the rest—as facets of one Self, and knows they will all, in the end, resolve back into the single awareness they arose from. The many rest in the one.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Fragmented identification with each kala separately—missing the underlying unity.
facets.somatic: Body identified piecemeal with each element.
facets.emotional: Emotional fragmentation.
facets.cognitive: Mind fragmented across the 16 kalas without unity.
facets.psychic: The soul over-analyzes the parts, lost in enumeration rather than the unity behind them.

excess.text:
Fragmented identification with each kala separately—missing the underlying unity.

Somatic: Body identified piecemeal with each element.

Emotional: Emotional fragmentation.

Cognitive: Mind fragmented across the 16 kalas without unity.

Psychic: The soul over-analyzes the parts, lost in enumeration rather than the unity behind them.
excess.vignette: Karan identifies with each part in turn—now his intellect, now his desire, now his name—fragmenting into a dozen selves and missing the thread that unites them. He is many people and no one.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total identification with the kalas as ultimately real—missing the deeper Purusha they manifest from.
facets.somatic: Body taken as ultimate reality, no underlying.
facets.emotional: Emotional life taken as final reality.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t recognize that all kalas are temporary manifestations.
facets.psychic: The soul sees only a heap of parts, with no sense of the whole person they compose.

deficient.text:
Total identification with the kalas as ultimately real—missing the deeper Purusha they manifest from.

Somatic: Body taken as ultimate reality, no underlying.

Emotional: Emotional life taken as final reality.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t recognize that all kalas are temporary manifestations.

Psychic: The soul sees only a heap of parts, with no sense of the whole person they compose.
deficient.vignette: For Dave the parts are simply what he is, full stop—his body, his job, his opinions, real and final—with no suspicion of the deeper Self they are all expressions of. He takes the facets for the jewel.

attributes.title: Pancha-Agni Vidya (Five Fires Doctrine)
attributes.aspect: Reality is portrayed as a continuous sacrificial fire-cycle through five fires: the heavenly world, the rain cloud, the earth, man, and woman. Through each fire, the previous offering becomes the next form. Soul transmigrates through these five fires. Understanding this cycle reveals the continuity of life beyond apparent death.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental (contemplation of the cosmic fire-cycle) · Ontic mode: Doctrine / practice (a contemplative vidyā) · Valence: Neutral — a contemplative teaching · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Cosmic (Ādhidaivika), contemplated individually · Causal status: Framework (maps the sacrificial round of birth and rebirth) · Key relation: Correspondence — weaves the individual into the cosmic cycle of the five fires.
coordinate.causal_status: Framework (maps the sacrificial round of birth and rebirth)
coordinate.key_relation: Correspondence — weaves the individual into the cosmic cycle of the five fires.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Recognition of the cosmic sacrificial cycle—each death is offering becoming new life. Understanding karma and rebirth through the continuous cycle.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as transient form in the great cycle.
facets.emotional: Emotional peace with mortality through understanding the continuity.
facets.cognitive: Mind grasps the systematic nature of life-death-rebirth.
facets.psychic: The soul contemplates the cosmic fire-cycle, seeing its life woven into the great sacrificial round of existence.

balanced.text:
Recognition of the cosmic sacrificial cycle—each death is offering becoming new life. Understanding karma and rebirth through the continuous cycle.

Somatic: Body recognized as transient form in the great cycle.

Emotional: Emotional peace with mortality through understanding the continuity.

Cognitive: Mind grasps the systematic nature of life-death-rebirth.

Psychic: The soul contemplates the cosmic fire-cycle, seeing its life woven into the great sacrificial round of existence.
balanced.vignette: Meera holds the long view: that death feeds life feeds death in one great cycle, that her actions ripple outward and return, and that this is neither morbid nor frightening but simply the shape of things. It steadies how she lives.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Fatalistic interpretation—‘everything is just the cycle’ as escape from responsibility.
facets.somatic: Body neglected as ‘just part of cycle.’
facets.emotional: Emotional disengagement claiming cosmic perspective.
facets.cognitive: Doctrinal acceptance without genuine understanding.
facets.psychic: The soul over-literalizes the doctrine, lost in cosmological detail rather than its meaning.

excess.text:
Fatalistic interpretation—‘everything is just the cycle’ as escape from responsibility.

Somatic: Body neglected as ‘just part of cycle.’

Emotional: Emotional disengagement claiming cosmic perspective.

Cognitive: Doctrinal acceptance without genuine understanding.

Psychic: The soul over-literalizes the doctrine, lost in cosmological detail rather than its meaning.
excess.vignette: Ravi uses ‘it’s all just the cycle’ to excuse everything—no need to change, to repair, to try; the wheel turns regardless. A profound teaching has become a shrug.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No understanding of cyclic nature—taking this life as isolated event, terror of death, no continuity recognized.
facets.somatic: Body taken as one-time event, no awareness of cycle.
facets.emotional: Maximum death-terror, no understanding of continuity.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees life as isolated phenomenon.
facets.psychic: The soul takes birth and death as isolated facts, blind to the great cycle that holds them.

deficient.text:
No understanding of cyclic nature—taking this life as isolated event, terror of death, no continuity recognized.

Somatic: Body taken as one-time event, no awareness of cycle.

Emotional: Maximum death-terror, no understanding of continuity.

Cognitive: Mind sees life as isolated phenomenon.

Psychic: The soul takes birth and death as isolated facts, blind to the great cycle that holds them.
deficient.vignette: For Sam this life is a sealed, isolated event with nothing before and nothing after, and the thought of death is pure terror because it means simple annihilation. He sees the spark and denies the fire.

attributes.title: Pancha Prana (Five Vital Forces)
attributes.aspect: Five pranas (vital forces) animate the body: Prana (in-breath, head), Apana (down-breath, elimination), Vyana (circulating breath, throughout body), Udana (up-breath, throat/speech), Samana (equalizing breath, digestion). All five are forms of cosmic Prana. When prana is gathered to the heart at death, the body falls.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (the five animating forces) · Ontic mode: Constituent — the five vital forces · Valence: Neutral faculty · Guṇa: Spans all · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (the forces animating the body) · Key relation: Whole — the five forces harmonized through conscious breath (kin to the Pañca Vāyu / Five Prāṇas).
coordinate.key_relation: Whole — the five forces harmonized through conscious breath (kin to the Pañca Vāyu / Five Prāṇas).

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: All five pranas balanced and harmoniously functioning. Body and mind animated by integrated vital force.
facets.somatic: All five pranas in harmony—healthy breathing, digestion, elimination, speech, circulation.
facets.emotional: Emotional vitality from balanced pranic flow.
facets.cognitive: Mental clarity from healthy prana distribution.
facets.psychic: The soul senses the five vital forces animating its body, learning to harmonize them through conscious breath.

balanced.text:
All five pranas balanced and harmoniously functioning. Body and mind animated by integrated vital force.

Somatic: All five pranas in harmony—healthy breathing, digestion, elimination, speech, circulation.

Emotional: Emotional vitality from balanced pranic flow.

Cognitive: Mental clarity from healthy prana distribution.

Psychic: The soul senses the five vital forces animating its body, learning to harmonize them through conscious breath.
balanced.vignette: The five forces move through Kira in concert—drawing in, pushing out, rising, spreading, digesting—and she feels, on a good day, like a well-tuned instrument playing itself.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced manipulation of pranas through advanced techniques without preparation—pranic disorders.
facets.somatic: Pranic disturbances, kundalini complications.
facets.emotional: Emotional volatility from pranic imbalance.
facets.cognitive: Mental disturbance from forced pranayama.
facets.psychic: The soul over-manipulates the forces, straining for energetic effects.

excess.text:
Forced manipulation of pranas through advanced techniques without preparation—pranic disorders.

Somatic: Pranic disturbances, kundalini complications.

Emotional: Emotional volatility from pranic imbalance.

Cognitive: Mental disturbance from forced pranayama.

Psychic: The soul over-manipulates the forces, straining for energetic effects.
excess.vignette: One force runs wild in Ben—all restless upward drive, no settling—and the imbalance shows as a wired, ungrounded energy that cannot come down. The orchestra has one player drowning out the rest.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Depleted pranas—chronic fatigue, poor digestion, sluggish circulation, weak voice, low vitality.
facets.somatic: All systems depleted, low vitality.
facets.emotional: Emotional depression from pranic depletion.
facets.cognitive: Mental fog from inadequate prana.
facets.psychic: The soul lives unaware of the vital forces, its energy leaking through unconscious habit.

deficient.text:
Depleted pranas—chronic fatigue, poor digestion, sluggish circulation, weak voice, low vitality.

Somatic: All systems depleted, low vitality.

Emotional: Emotional depression from pranic depletion.

Cognitive: Mental fog from inadequate prana.

Psychic: The soul lives unaware of the vital forces, its energy leaking through unconscious habit.
deficient.vignette: Sara’s vital forces have all gone faint—shallow breath, weak digestion, no lift—and she moves through her days at half-power, unaware there is a fuller current available to her.

attributes.title: Samvarga Vidya (All-Absorbent Meditation)
attributes.aspect: All-absorbent meditation. Process: harmonising Prana, concentration on unity of psychic beings of individual and universe. Atonement of Prana and Vayu enables reaching Maha-Prana (Hiranyagarbha). Denying food to a guest = denying food to Cosmic Life-Principle. Knower becomes cosmic consumer of food (Hiranyagarbha).

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental (meditation on the all-absorbing principle) · Ontic mode: Practice (an upāsana gathering the powers into unity) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, with cosmic object · Causal status: Practice (gathers the scattered powers into one) · Key relation: Correspondence — meditates on prāṇa as the gatherer of all.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice (an upāsana gathering the powers into unity)
coordinate.causal_status: Practice (gathers the scattered powers into one)
coordinate.key_relation: Correspondence — meditates on prāṇa as the gatherer of all.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Recognition that Prana absorbs all individual functions; Vayu absorbs cosmic. One becomes the all-absorbing Life-Principle through this meditation.
facets.somatic: Body unified through balanced Prana, all sense-functions harmoniously gathered.
facets.emotional: Emotional absorption into Cosmic Life-Principle.
facets.cognitive: Mind absorbs all distinctions through Samvarga, recognizing one Life behind all life.
facets.psychic: The soul meditates on the all-absorbing principle, gathering the scattered powers into a single unity.

balanced.text:
Recognition that Prana absorbs all individual functions; Vayu absorbs cosmic. One becomes the all-absorbing Life-Principle through this meditation.

Somatic: Body unified through balanced Prana, all sense-functions harmoniously gathered.

Emotional: Emotional absorption into Cosmic Life-Principle.

Cognitive: Mind absorbs all distinctions through Samvarga, recognizing one Life behind all life.

Psychic: The soul meditates on the all-absorbing principle, gathering the scattered powers into a single unity.
balanced.vignette: In deep practice Amara rests in the recognition that breath, fire, sun, and moon all gather back into one absorbing principle—the great in-breath of things—and from that vast simplicity she returns quieted and whole.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced absorption practices creating pranic disturbance, attempt to control cosmic forces.
facets.somatic: Pranic disorders from forced Samvarga.
facets.emotional: Emotional grandiosity claiming cosmic Prana-status.
facets.cognitive: Mental claim of being Hiranyagarbha without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on the technique, collecting subtle experiences rather than dissolving into unity.

excess.text:
Forced absorption practices creating pranic disturbance, attempt to control cosmic forces.

Somatic: Pranic disorders from forced Samvarga.

Emotional: Emotional grandiosity claiming cosmic Prana-status.

Cognitive: Mental claim of being Hiranyagarbha without realization.

Psychic: The soul fixates on the technique, collecting subtle experiences rather than dissolving into unity.
excess.vignette: Ben grasps at the ‘all-absorbing’ meditation as a technique to conquer, forcing the vision, straining to make the great absorption happen, and getting only tension for his effort.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No recognition that Prana is the cosmic absorber—life lived as fragmented sense-engagements with no unifying principle.
facets.somatic: Body’s pranas scattered, no integration.
facets.emotional: Emotional fragmentation across senses.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t recognize Prana as the unifying principle.
facets.psychic: The soul never gathers its powers, its energies scattered with nothing to absorb them.

deficient.text:
No recognition that Prana is the cosmic absorber—life lived as fragmented sense-engagements with no unifying principle.

Somatic: Body’s pranas scattered, no integration.

Emotional: Emotional fragmentation across senses.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t recognize Prana as the unifying principle.

Psychic: The soul never gathers its powers, its energies scattered with nothing to absorb them.
deficient.vignette: For Dana the world is a heap of separate objects with nothing gathering them—no absorbing principle, no underlying one—and the meditation that points to it is words with no referent she can find.

attributes.title: Prana Vidya (Vital Life Principle)
attributes.aspect: Meditation on Prana as Brahman itself. The Mukhya-Prana (chief prana) is the starting point of superphysical life. Recognition that all sense-functions exist only because of Prana; without it, no eye sees, no ear hears.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic (meditation on prāṇa as Brahman) · Ontic mode: Practice (an upāsana on the life-force) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, opening to the cosmic · Causal status: Practice (a means to realize prāṇa as the doorway to the absolute) · Key relation: Correspondence — reveres prāṇa as Brahman; the meditative counterpart to ‘Prāṇa as Ruler of the Body.’
coordinate.causal_status: Practice (a means to realize prāṇa as the doorway to the absolute)
coordinate.key_relation: Correspondence — reveres prāṇa as Brahman; the meditative counterpart to ‘Prāṇa as Ruler of the Body.’

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: Prana recognized as the supreme principle in body; all functions seen as Prana’s manifestations. Direct contact with the Life-Principle.
facets.somatic: Body’s prana cultivated and recognized as foundational, healthy vital function.
facets.emotional: Emotional vitality from Prana-recognition.
facets.cognitive: Mind acknowledges Prana as the source of all its functions.
facets.psychic: The soul meditates on prāṇa as Brahman itself, the chief life-force revered as the doorway to the absolute.

balanced.text:
Prana recognized as the supreme principle in body; all functions seen as Prana’s manifestations. Direct contact with the Life-Principle.

Somatic: Body’s prana cultivated and recognized as foundational, healthy vital function.

Emotional: Emotional vitality from Prana-recognition.

Cognitive: Mind acknowledges Prana as the source of all its functions.

Psychic: The soul meditates on prāṇa as Brahman itself, the chief life-force revered as the doorway to the absolute.
balanced.vignette: Kira has come to feel prana as the actual life in her—the thing that leaves in sleep and returns, the current beneath all her functions—and tends it as the precious principle it is. Knowing what carries her, she carries herself well.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced pranayama techniques without preparation, pranic disorders.
facets.somatic: Pranic imbalances from forced practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional volatility from improper Prana work.
facets.cognitive: Mental disturbance from forced prana techniques.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on prāṇa-phenomena, chasing energetic experience over realization.

excess.text:
Forced pranayama techniques without preparation, pranic disorders.

Somatic: Pranic imbalances from forced practice.

Emotional: Emotional volatility from improper Prana work.

Cognitive: Mental disturbance from forced prana techniques.

Psychic: The soul fixates on prāṇa-phenomena, chasing energetic experience over realization.
excess.vignette: Convinced he can supercharge his life-force, Ben forces intense practices to ‘maximize prana’ and instead destabilizes the very thing he was worshipping. Reverence curdled into manipulation.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Prana unrecognized—life lived through senses with no awareness of the unifying vital principle.
facets.somatic: Body’s prana neglected, depleted.
facets.emotional: Emotional dysregulation from poor prana.
facets.cognitive: Mind without recognition of its prana-dependence.
facets.psychic: The soul never meditates on the life-force, taking the breath as mere physiology.

deficient.text:
Prana unrecognized—life lived through senses with no awareness of the unifying vital principle.

Somatic: Body’s prana neglected, depleted.

Emotional: Emotional dysregulation from poor prana.

Cognitive: Mind without recognition of its prana-dependence.

Psychic: The soul never meditates on the life-force, taking the breath as mere physiology.
deficient.vignette: Dana has no felt sense that a life-force exists at all—she is a body and a brain and nothing between—so the one principle that animates her goes entirely unnoticed and untended.

attributes.title: Phenomenological Dimension: Valence (Sukha–Duḥkha Tone of Experience)
attributes.aspect: Below, valence is explored across **three states of being**, followed by **two contrasting vignettes** situated in a single modern scenario.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (the pleasure–pain tone of experience) · Ontic mode: Experiential axis (a dimension along which experience varies) · Valence: Neutral faculty (balance is equanimity, Samatā) · Guṇa: Variable (rajasic in Rāga / Dveṣa dominance) · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Manifestation (the felt tone; governed by attraction and aversion) · Key relation: Bipolar axis (Sukha–Duḥkha) — driven by Rāga and Dveṣa.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Experiential axis (a dimension along which experience varies)
coordinate.causal_status: Manifestation (the felt tone; governed by attraction and aversion)
coordinate.key_relation: Bipolar axis (Sukha–Duḥkha) — driven by Rāga and Dveṣa.

balanced.label: Equilibrium — Balanced Valence (Samatā)

facets.overview: Pleasant and unpleasant are both allowed to appear without commandeering the self.
facets.somatic: The body feels responsive rather than reactive. Pleasant sensations are enjoyed without tightening; unpleasant sensations are registered without collapse. There is a gentle elasticity in the gut and chest—no bracing, no chasing.
facets.emotional: Pleasure does not demand repetition. Discomfort does not demand escape. The emotional field is fluid, with no dominant hunger. There is an underlying tone of *trust*—that experience can be met as it comes.
facets.cognitive: Thought does not immediately label sensations as “good for me” or “bad for me.” The mind stays descriptive rather than evaluative. Meaning is allowed to emerge rather than being imposed.
facets.psychic: At the soul level, there is **samatva**—equanimity. Sukha and duḥkha are known as movements within prakṛti, not verdicts on the self. Valence is seen, not obeyed.
facets.external: Decisions are made without urgency. Work is engaged sincerely but without emotional overinvestment. Relationships have space—affection without clinging, disagreement without aversion. Habits feel clean because they are not compensatory.

balanced.text:
Pleasant and unpleasant are both allowed to appear without commandeering the self.

Somatic: The body feels responsive rather than reactive. Pleasant sensations are enjoyed without tightening; unpleasant sensations are registered without collapse. There is a gentle elasticity in the gut and chest—no bracing, no chasing.

Emotional: Pleasure does not demand repetition. Discomfort does not demand escape. The emotional field is fluid, with no dominant hunger. There is an underlying tone of *trust*—that experience can be met as it comes.

Cognitive: Thought does not immediately label sensations as “good for me” or “bad for me.” The mind stays descriptive rather than evaluative. Meaning is allowed to emerge rather than being imposed.

Psychic: At the soul level, there is **samatva**—equanimity. Sukha and duḥkha are known as movements within prakṛti, not verdicts on the self. Valence is seen, not obeyed.

External: Decisions are made without urgency. Work is engaged sincerely but without emotional overinvestment. Relationships have space—affection without clinging, disagreement without aversion. Habits feel clean because they are not compensatory.
balanced.vignette: He receives a message: a project proposal has been rejected. A brief contraction in the chest, a dull unpleasantness. He notices it while standing at the sink. He exhales, lets the shoulders drop. The unpleasant tone does not become a story about failure. Ten minutes later, he drafts a revised plan. In the evening, he enjoys dinner without replaying the email. The day remains whole.

excess.label: Excess — Hyper-Positive or Hyper-Negative Valence (Rāga / Dveṣa Dominance)

facets.overview: Experience is hijacked by attraction or aversion.
facets.somatic: The body is pulled forward or recoils. In craving, there is a leaning tension—jaw tight, breath shallow. In aversion, there is bracing—gut clenched, shoulders raised. Sensation feels urgent.
facets.emotional: Pleasure carries desperation; discomfort carries threat. There is a constant undertone of *“this must continue”* or *“this must stop.”* Emotional neutrality feels intolerable.
facets.cognitive: The mind rationalizes: *“I need this to feel okay”* or *“I can’t handle this.”* Experience is misinterpreted as a command. Valence masquerades as truth.
facets.psychic: The witnessing self collapses into the wave. The soul forgets its seat and identifies with sensation. Rāga or dveṣa becomes destiny.
facets.external: Work becomes compulsive or avoidant. Relationships become transactional—people are valued for how they regulate one’s internal tone. Habits harden into addictions, distractions, or rigid control patterns.

excess.text:
Experience is hijacked by attraction or aversion.

Somatic: The body is pulled forward or recoils. In craving, there is a leaning tension—jaw tight, breath shallow. In aversion, there is bracing—gut clenched, shoulders raised. Sensation feels urgent.

Emotional: Pleasure carries desperation; discomfort carries threat. There is a constant undertone of *“this must continue”* or *“this must stop.”* Emotional neutrality feels intolerable.

Cognitive: The mind rationalizes: *“I need this to feel okay”* or *“I can’t handle this.”* Experience is misinterpreted as a command. Valence masquerades as truth.

Psychic: The witnessing self collapses into the wave. The soul forgets its seat and identifies with sensation. Rāga or dveṣa becomes destiny.

External: Work becomes compulsive or avoidant. Relationships become transactional—people are valued for how they regulate one’s internal tone. Habits harden into addictions, distractions, or rigid control patterns.
excess.vignette: She wakes already scanning for something pleasant—her phone, a message, a hit of reassurance. When none arrives, irritation blooms. At work, mild feedback feels like an attack. She replays it all day. In the evening, she binge-watches, eats past fullness, scrolls long after exhaustion. Pleasure is pursued frantically, yet relief never arrives.

deficient.label: Deficiency — Flattened Valence (Anhedonia / Numbness)

facets.overview: Pleasant and unpleasant lose their signal value. A wave of unpleasant valence arises—tightness, heaviness. The mind mislabels it: *“I’m stressed. I deserve relief.”* The discomfort is assumed to be an enemy. The nervous system learns: *unpleasant = avoid immediately*. The saṃskāra of rāga–dveṣa deepens. The threshold for discomfort lowers. Tomorrow, the same wave will feel stronger. The same unpleasant wave arises. This time, it is **named honestly**: *“There is heaviness. This is valence, not a command.”* He stays with the sensation—no narration, no resistance. The wave completes itself. Nothing was suppressed or indulged. The old saṃskāra weakens. Valence is experienced as weather, not fate. A quiet dignity returns. Valence is not the problem. **Misidentification with valence is. When pleasantness and unpleasantness are seen clearly: - They guide without ruling - Inform without enslaving - Pass without leaving residue In Vedic terms, liberation does not come from eliminating sukha or duḥkha, but from **refusing to grant them epistemic authority**. To live with phenomenological integrity is to feel fully— without being owned by what is felt.
facets.somatic: The body feels dull or distant. Sensations are muted, as if filtered through cotton. There is little warmth, little sharpness.
facets.emotional: Neither desire nor aversion moves strongly. Life feels gray—not painful enough to flee, not pleasant enough to approach. Motivation is minimal.
facets.cognitive: Thoughts circle around *“What’s the point?”* Meaning feels inaccessible. Even positive events fail to register as rewarding.
facets.psychic: The soul’s signal is faint. Not lost—but veiled. The lesson here is not restraint but **re-contact**.
facets.external: Work is done mechanically. Relationships feel distant, not hostile. Habits drift toward inertia. Life continues, but without intimacy. He reaches for alcohol, junk food, endless scrolling. Action is driven by **escape**, not understanding. He chooses **Karma Yoga** in a small form—takes a walk, prepares a simple meal attentively, breath steady. Action without self-soothing agenda.

Action is driven by **escape**, not understanding. He chooses **Karma Yoga** in a small form—takes a walk, prepares a simple meal attentively, breath steady. Action without self-soothing agenda.
deficient.vignette: He completes his tasks efficiently but without satisfaction. Compliments land flat. Criticism barely stings. In the evening, he sits quietly, unsure what he wants to do. Nothing feels wrong, but nothing feels alive.

attributes.title: Phenomenological Dimension: Arousal (Intensity / Activation)
attributes.aspect: It decides *how intensely* life presses upon the system.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital (nervous activation / prāṇa flow) · Ontic mode: Experiential axis (a dimension of first-person experience) · Valence: Neutral faculty (balance is regulated arousal) · Guṇa: Spans the triad (sattvic balance, rajasic overdrive, tamasic collapse) · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Manifestation (the activation level of body and mind) · Key relation: Bipolar axis (hyperarousal–hypoarousal) — tracks the guṇas and prāṇa.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Experiential axis (a dimension of first-person experience)
coordinate.guna: Spans the triad (sattvic balance, rajasic overdrive, tamasic collapse)
coordinate.causal_status: Manifestation (the activation level of body and mind)
coordinate.key_relation: Bipolar axis (hyperarousal–hypoarousal) — tracks the guṇas and prāṇa.

balanced.label: Equilibrium — Regulated Arousal (Sattvic Prāṇa Flow)

facets.overview: Energy is available without agitation; rest is possible without collapse.
facets.somatic: The body feels alert but not tight. Breath is full and rhythmic. Muscles are engaged only as needed. There is readiness without bracing.
facets.emotional: Emotion has momentum but not urgency. Interest arises without compulsion. Calm is not dullness; excitement is not frenzy. There is a steady inner hum.
facets.cognitive: Thoughts appear at an appropriate pace. The mind can hold complexity without racing or freezing. Attention moves voluntarily rather than being yanked.
facets.psychic: At the soul level, there is **sthira–sukham**—steadiness with ease. Prāṇa serves awareness rather than hijacking it. One feels *capable*.
facets.external: Work unfolds in focused blocks. Breaks are taken before depletion. Relationships feel responsive rather than reactive. Habits support rhythm—sleep, food, movement align naturally.

balanced.text:
Energy is available without agitation; rest is possible without collapse.

Somatic: The body feels alert but not tight. Breath is full and rhythmic. Muscles are engaged only as needed. There is readiness without bracing.

Emotional: Emotion has momentum but not urgency. Interest arises without compulsion. Calm is not dullness; excitement is not frenzy. There is a steady inner hum.

Cognitive: Thoughts appear at an appropriate pace. The mind can hold complexity without racing or freezing. Attention moves voluntarily rather than being yanked.

Psychic: At the soul level, there is **sthira–sukham**—steadiness with ease. Prāṇa serves awareness rather than hijacking it. One feels *capable*.

External: Work unfolds in focused blocks. Breaks are taken before depletion. Relationships feel responsive rather than reactive. Habits support rhythm—sleep, food, movement align naturally.
balanced.vignette: She begins the morning with a clear sense of the day’s demands. During a challenging meeting, her heart rate rises slightly, sharpening attention. When it ends, the activation settles on its own. By evening, energy tapers gently. She sleeps without replaying the day. Nothing feels forced; nothing feels avoided.

excess.label: Excess — Hyperarousal (Rājasic Overdrive)

facets.overview: Energy exceeds the system’s capacity to metabolize it.
facets.somatic: The body is tight, keyed up. Breath is shallow or rapid. Jaw clenched, shoulders lifted. There is a sense of always being “on.”
facets.emotional: Urgency dominates. Even neutral situations feel charged. There is impatience with slowness, silence, or uncertainty. Rest feels unsafe.
facets.cognitive: Thoughts race, fragment, or catastrophize. The mind jumps ahead, scanning for threat or opportunity. Stillness is interpreted as danger or waste.
facets.psychic: The witnessing self is drowned out by momentum. Prāṇa runs the show. The soul experiences itself as **chased by time**.
facets.external: Work becomes frantic or perfectionistic. Multitasking replaces presence. Relationships suffer from irritability or emotional flooding. Habits skew toward stimulants, overwork, or constant distraction.

excess.text:
Energy exceeds the system’s capacity to metabolize it.

Somatic: The body is tight, keyed up. Breath is shallow or rapid. Jaw clenched, shoulders lifted. There is a sense of always being “on.”

Emotional: Urgency dominates. Even neutral situations feel charged. There is impatience with slowness, silence, or uncertainty. Rest feels unsafe.

Cognitive: Thoughts race, fragment, or catastrophize. The mind jumps ahead, scanning for threat or opportunity. Stillness is interpreted as danger or waste.

Psychic: The witnessing self is drowned out by momentum. Prāṇa runs the show. The soul experiences itself as **chased by time**.

External: Work becomes frantic or perfectionistic. Multitasking replaces presence. Relationships suffer from irritability or emotional flooding. Habits skew toward stimulants, overwork, or constant distraction.
excess.vignette: He wakes already late, heart pounding before his feet touch the floor. Emails pile up faster than he can answer them. A minor delay spikes irritation. Even in the evening, his body won’t downshift—scrolling, snacking, pacing. Exhausted but wired, he collapses into shallow sleep.

deficient.label: Deficiency — Hypoarousal (Tāmasic Collapse)

facets.overview: Energy falls below what engagement requires. A surge of arousal hits—heart racing, pressure in the head. The mind mislabels it: *“I’m overwhelmed. I can’t handle this.”* Activation is interpreted as danger. The nervous system learns: *activation = threat*. The tolerance window shrinks. Next time, arousal spikes sooner and lasts longer. Burnout deepens. The same surge arises. This time, it is seen clearly: *“Energy is high. Prāṇa is mobilizing.”* No story of incapacity is added. The arousal peaks and resolves naturally. The system learns: *activation can be metabolized*. Capacity expands. Confidence quietly grows. Arousal is not the enemy. When intensity is misunderstood: - High arousal becomes panic or aggression - Low arousal becomes apathy or withdrawal When intensity is met with clarity: - Energy becomes focus - Rest becomes restoration In Vedic terms, liberation here is not suppression of prāṇa, but its **education**— so that energy moves *through* awareness rather than ruling it. To live with phenomenological integrity at the level of arousal is to know this truth:
facets.somatic: The body feels heavy, slow, or inert. Breath is shallow but sluggish. Limbs feel weighted. Initiation requires effort.
facets.emotional: Emotion is muted. There is little urgency—but also little interest. Desire feels distant; aversion feels blunted. The dominant tone is fatigue.
facets.cognitive: Thoughts move slowly or loop repetitively. Concentration is difficult. Tasks feel overwhelming not because they are intense, but because energy is insufficient.
facets.psychic: The soul feels veiled, not absent. Awareness remains, but prāṇa does not rise to meet life. The lesson here is **re-ignition**, not restraint.
facets.external: Work is postponed or done mechanically. Social contact feels draining. Habits drift toward passivity—sleeping too much, scrolling without engagement, avoiding challenge. He reacts compulsively—snaps at a colleague, opens multiple tabs, works faster without clarity. Or he shuts down completely, avoiding the task. He pauses for three slow breaths (**Yoga**), simplifies the next action (**Karma Yoga**), and brings attention fully to one task (**Jñāna through discernment**).
