facets.psychic: The soul wars against its thoughts directly, the forcing stirring more waves than it stills.

excess.text:
Forced or premature nirodha without proper foundation creates psychological imbalance, dissociation, or catatonic states mistaken for samadhi.

Somatic: Forced suppression of vital functions, body shut down rather than refined, ungrounded physical state.

Emotional: Emotional suppression masquerading as transcendence, dissociation from feelings, false detachment.

Cognitive: Forced thought suppression that creates rebound, dissociative states, false claims of samadhi, mental dullness mistaken for stillness.

Psychic: The soul wars against its thoughts directly, the forcing stirring more waves than it stills.
excess.vignette: Determined to ‘stop his thoughts,’ Jon clamps down hard, wrestling every stirring into silence, and ends each sit more tense than when he began. He has confused suppression with stillness, and the forcing only stirs the water more.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Without any movement toward nirodha, the mind is dominated by endless vrittis, creating suffering and bondage to samsara.
facets.somatic: Body driven by constant mental activity, stress-related illness, nervous system in perpetual agitation.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity dominates life, no peace possible, slavery to feelings as they arise.
facets.cognitive: Constant mental chatter, identification with thoughts, no witness consciousness, life lived in vrittis.
facets.psychic: The soul’s mind churns ceaselessly, never approaching the stillness that is yoga’s aim.

deficient.text:
Without any movement toward nirodha, the mind is dominated by endless vrittis, creating suffering and bondage to samsara.

Somatic: Body driven by constant mental activity, stress-related illness, nervous system in perpetual agitation.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity dominates life, no peace possible, slavery to feelings as they arise.

Cognitive: Constant mental chatter, identification with thoughts, no witness consciousness, life lived in vrittis.

Psychic: The soul’s mind churns ceaselessly, never approaching the stillness that is yoga’s aim.
deficient.vignette: Sara’s mind never stops—a running commentary from waking to sleep, one thought hooking the next, no gap anywhere. She has never once experienced a quiet mind, and cannot imagine that such a thing exists.

attributes.title: Pranava (Om - The Sacred Syllable)
attributes.aspect: Pranava (Om) is the sacred sound-symbol of Ishvara. Patanjali prescribes its repetition (japa) combined with contemplation of its meaning (artha-bhavana) as a primary practice. The sound holds the essence of all sacred sound and unites the practitioner with the Divine.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice (repetition of the sacred syllable) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means (a support for concentration and surrender) · Key relation: Correspondence — the emblem of Īśvara; aid to Īśvara-praṇidhāna.
coordinate.causal_status: Means (a support for concentration and surrender)
coordinate.key_relation: Correspondence — the emblem of Īśvara; aid to Īśvara-praṇidhāna.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When Pranava is practiced with both sound and meaning, it becomes a powerful unifying practice that brings one-pointedness and Self-realization.
facets.somatic: Body resonates with sacred sound, healthy physiological effects of mantra, nervous system attuned, vibrations purifying.
facets.emotional: Emotional unification through sacred sound, devotional warmth from Om, emotional integration.
facets.cognitive: Mental one-pointedness from sound and meaning meditation, contact with deeper reality, mental purification.
facets.psychic: The soul repeats OM as the emblem of the Divine, the sacred sound gathering and lifting the mind.

balanced.text:
When Pranava is practiced with both sound and meaning, it becomes a powerful unifying practice that brings one-pointedness and Self-realization.

Somatic: Body resonates with sacred sound, healthy physiological effects of mantra, nervous system attuned, vibrations purifying.

Emotional: Emotional unification through sacred sound, devotional warmth from Om, emotional integration.

Cognitive: Mental one-pointedness from sound and meaning meditation, contact with deeper reality, mental purification.

Psychic: The soul repeats OM as the emblem of the Divine, the sacred sound gathering and lifting the mind.
balanced.vignette: Amara begins her practice with a few slow rounds of Om, and the sound resonates through her chest and settles her whole system into a devotional stillness. The syllable is a doorway she steps through gladly.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mechanical chanting without meaning becomes empty ritual; obsessive practice can create imbalances if not properly guided.
facets.somatic: Mechanical chanting without engagement, physical exhaustion from forced practice.
facets.emotional: Empty emotional response to mantra, ritualism without devotion.
facets.cognitive: Mechanical mind during chanting, no meaning contemplation, missing the transformative essence.
facets.psychic: The soul repeats the syllable mechanically, the sound emptied of its meaning.

excess.text:
Mechanical chanting without meaning becomes empty ritual; obsessive practice can create imbalances if not properly guided.

Somatic: Mechanical chanting without engagement, physical exhaustion from forced practice.

Emotional: Empty emotional response to mantra, ritualism without devotion.

Cognitive: Mechanical mind during chanting, no meaning contemplation, missing the transformative essence.

Psychic: The soul repeats the syllable mechanically, the sound emptied of its meaning.
excess.vignette: Karl chants Om compulsively, counting rounds like currency and half-believing the sound itself will magically fix his life. The living sacred syllable has become a superstitious tic.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Without any sacred sound practice, the mind lacks one of yoga’s most powerful unifying tools. Practice is harder without mantra support.
facets.somatic: Body without mantra-vibration support, missing somatic benefits of sacred sound.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without devotional sound practice, missing this powerful tool.
facets.cognitive: Mind without japa-anchor, missing efficient concentration technique, harder to fix mind.
facets.psychic: The soul has no sacred sound to gather it, the mind left to its unbroken churn.

deficient.text:
Without any sacred sound practice, the mind lacks one of yoga’s most powerful unifying tools. Practice is harder without mantra support.

Somatic: Body without mantra-vibration support, missing somatic benefits of sacred sound.

Emotional: Emotional life without devotional sound practice, missing this powerful tool.

Cognitive: Mind without japa-anchor, missing efficient concentration technique, harder to fix mind.

Psychic: The soul has no sacred sound to gather it, the mind left to its unbroken churn.
deficient.vignette: For Dan there is no sacred sound in the day at all—no chant, no silence, no resonance, just the flat noise of notifications and traffic. A whole register of the sacred is simply missing from his life.

attributes.title: Asana (Posture/Steady Seat)
attributes.aspect: Asana is the third limb—the steady, comfortable seat for meditation. In Patanjali, asana is not the physical postures of modern yoga but specifically the meditation seat. Mastery means: (1) steadiness, (2) ease, (3) effortlessness, and (4) absorption in the infinite, leading to freedom from dvandvas (pairs of opposites).

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical · Ontic mode: Practice (a steady, comfortable posture) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva–Tamas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition (a steady seat for the mind to settle) · Key relation: The concept underlying Āsana (the 3rd limb).
coordinate.causal_status: Condition (a steady seat for the mind to settle)
coordinate.key_relation: The concept underlying Āsana (the 3rd limb).

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When asana is mastered, the body is no longer a distraction—it sits effortlessly steady and comfortable, allowing deep meditation. The yogi becomes immune to dualities like heat/cold, hunger/thirst.
facets.somatic: Effortless steady seat for long periods, body that doesn’t fatigue, freedom from physical reactivity to extremes, transcendence of dvandvas.
facets.emotional: Emotional steadiness mirrored in physical steadiness, freedom from emotional reactivity to physical conditions.
facets.cognitive: Mind freed from body’s complaints, available for deeper practice, transcendent of dualistic experience.
facets.psychic: The soul finds a steady, comfortable seat, the body quieted so the mind can settle.

balanced.text:
When asana is mastered, the body is no longer a distraction—it sits effortlessly steady and comfortable, allowing deep meditation. The yogi becomes immune to dualities like heat/cold, hunger/thirst.

Somatic: Effortless steady seat for long periods, body that doesn’t fatigue, freedom from physical reactivity to extremes, transcendence of dvandvas.

Emotional: Emotional steadiness mirrored in physical steadiness, freedom from emotional reactivity to physical conditions.

Cognitive: Mind freed from body’s complaints, available for deeper practice, transcendent of dualistic experience.

Psychic: The soul finds a steady, comfortable seat, the body quieted so the mind can settle.
balanced.vignette: Priya can sit, still and comfortable, for a long meditation—the body a stable, forgotten base that lets attention go inward. Her seat is steady and easeful at once, exactly as the sutra asks.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive focus on physical posture without meditation becomes mere gymnastics or postural perfectionism; effort overrides ease.
facets.somatic: Forced posture causing injury, over-effort, rigid mastery without true ease, physical perfectionism.
facets.emotional: Pride in postural achievement, attachment to physical form, missing the meditative purpose.
facets.cognitive: Mind focused on body instead of meditation, attachment to specific postures, mistaking physical for spiritual.
facets.psychic: The soul over-emphasizes physical posture, mistaking athletic accomplishment for the inner steadiness it serves.

excess.text:
Excessive focus on physical posture without meditation becomes mere gymnastics or postural perfectionism; effort overrides ease.

Somatic: Forced posture causing injury, over-effort, rigid mastery without true ease, physical perfectionism.

Emotional: Pride in postural achievement, attachment to physical form, missing the meditative purpose.

Cognitive: Mind focused on body instead of meditation, attachment to specific postures, mistaking physical for spiritual.

Psychic: The soul over-emphasizes physical posture, mistaking athletic accomplishment for the inner steadiness it serves.
excess.vignette: Ben’s yoga is all performance now—deeper backbends, harder poses, the perfect photo—his ego riding on his flexibility. The steady seat meant to free the mind has become one more arena to win.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Inability to sit steadily and comfortably means the body constantly distracts from meditation; physical reactivity to dvandvas dominates.
facets.somatic: Body that cannot sit still, fidgeting, constant discomfort, physical reactivity to slight discomfort.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity to physical discomfort, complaints about conditions, suffering from dvandvas.
facets.cognitive: Mind constantly pulled by body’s complaints, unable to focus, distracted by physical sensations.
facets.psychic: The soul has no steady seat, the restless body keeping the mind unsettled.

deficient.text:
Inability to sit steadily and comfortably means the body constantly distracts from meditation; physical reactivity to dvandvas dominates.

Somatic: Body that cannot sit still, fidgeting, constant discomfort, physical reactivity to slight discomfort.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity to physical discomfort, complaints about conditions, suffering from dvandvas.

Cognitive: Mind constantly pulled by body’s complaints, unable to focus, distracted by physical sensations.

Psychic: The soul has no steady seat, the restless body keeping the mind unsettled.
deficient.vignette: Sara cannot sit still for five minutes without fidgeting, aching, and giving up. Her body has never been made into a stable base for anything, and so the inner work has no seat to sit on.

attributes.title: Pranayama (Breath Control/Life-Force Regulation)
attributes.aspect: Pranayama is the fourth limb—regulation of the breath (prana = life-force, ayama = extension/control). It has three modes: exhalation (rechaka), inhalation (puraka), and retention (kumbhaka). Its purpose is to remove the veil over the inner light (sattva) and prepare the mind for concentration.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Practice (breath-regulation) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (steadies the mind via the breath) · Key relation: The concept underlying Prāṇāyāma (the 4th limb); genus of the breath disciplines (Dim 6).
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (steadies the mind via the breath)
coordinate.key_relation: The concept underlying Prāṇāyāma (the 4th limb); genus of the breath disciplines (Dim 6).

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When pranayama is properly practiced, the breath becomes long, subtle, and refined. The veil over the inner light dissipates and the mind becomes fit for dharana.
facets.somatic: Slow deep breathing, healthy respiratory system, balanced autonomic nervous system, vital energy refined and abundant.
facets.emotional: Emotional calming from breath regulation, balanced nervous system supporting emotional balance, peace from breath.
facets.cognitive: Mind becoming fit for concentration, inner light revealed, mental clarity from sattva uncovered, prepared for dharana.
facets.psychic: The soul regulates the breath to steady the mind, the life-force gathered and refined.

balanced.text:
When pranayama is properly practiced, the breath becomes long, subtle, and refined. The veil over the inner light dissipates and the mind becomes fit for dharana.

Somatic: Slow deep breathing, healthy respiratory system, balanced autonomic nervous system, vital energy refined and abundant.

Emotional: Emotional calming from breath regulation, balanced nervous system supporting emotional balance, peace from breath.

Cognitive: Mind becoming fit for concentration, inner light revealed, mental clarity from sattva uncovered, prepared for dharana.

Psychic: The soul regulates the breath to steady the mind, the life-force gathered and refined.
balanced.vignette: When Kira is frayed, she lengthens her exhale and feels the nervous system downshift; when dull, she brightens the breath. The breath is a conscious dial she has learned to turn.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive forceful pranayama without preparation creates imbalances—prana disorders, anxiety, nervous system damage, mental disturbance.
facets.somatic: Forced breath causing physical harm, hyperventilation, prana disorders, nervous system imbalance, dizziness or fainting.
facets.emotional: Emotional disturbance from forced practice, anxiety from improper pranayama, fear states.
facets.cognitive: Mental imbalance from forced practice, headaches, mental disturbance, kundalini disorders if improperly done.
facets.psychic: The soul over-manipulates the breath, forcing techniques that agitate rather than calm.

excess.text:
Excessive forceful pranayama without preparation creates imbalances—prana disorders, anxiety, nervous system damage, mental disturbance.

Somatic: Forced breath causing physical harm, hyperventilation, prana disorders, nervous system imbalance, dizziness or fainting.

Emotional: Emotional disturbance from forced practice, anxiety from improper pranayama, fear states.

Cognitive: Mental imbalance from forced practice, headaches, mental disturbance, kundalini disorders if improperly done.

Psychic: The soul over-manipulates the breath, forcing techniques that agitate rather than calm.
excess.vignette: Jon does aggressive breathwork for an hour a day, chasing intensity and ignoring the dizziness and jangle it leaves behind. He is forcing the breath and the system is protesting.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Shallow breathing without any pranayama leaves the mind covered by impurities, unable to access deeper practice, and the body lacking vital energy.
facets.somatic: Shallow restricted breathing, poor oxygenation, depleted prana, weak vitality, poor health.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity from disregulated breath, anxiety, no calming through breath.
facets.cognitive: Mind covered by impurities, dullness, no clarity, unable to access deeper practice.
facets.psychic: The soul leaves the breath unregulated, the uneven breath keeping the mind stirred.

deficient.text:
Shallow breathing without any pranayama leaves the mind covered by impurities, unable to access deeper practice, and the body lacking vital energy.

Somatic: Shallow restricted breathing, poor oxygenation, depleted prana, weak vitality, poor health.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity from disregulated breath, anxiety, no calming through breath.

Cognitive: Mind covered by impurities, dullness, no clarity, unable to access deeper practice.

Psychic: The soul leaves the breath unregulated, the uneven breath keeping the mind stirred.
deficient.vignette: Dana’s breath simply follows her stress—shallow when tense, held when afraid—and it has never occurred to her that she could take the wheel. The most available tool she owns sits unused.

attributes.title: Pratyahara (Sense Withdrawal)
attributes.aspect: Pratyahara is the fifth limb—the withdrawal of the senses from their objects. When the mind is withdrawn from sense objects, the senses naturally follow and disconnect from external stimuli. This is the bridge between the external limbs (yamas, niyamas, asana, pranayama) and the internal limbs (dharana, dhyana, samadhi).

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice (sense-withdrawal) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition (gathers the attention inward) · Key relation: The concept underlying Pratyāhāra (the 5th limb).
coordinate.key_relation: The concept underlying Pratyāhāra (the 5th limb).

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When pratyahara is established, the senses follow the mind inward. External stimuli no longer disturb. The yogi can be in the world but the mind is gathered within.
facets.somatic: Senses able to disengage from objects at will, body present but not driven by stimuli, healthy sense functioning when needed.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from sensory triggering, ability to choose engagement, peace from inner gathering.
facets.cognitive: Mind gathered within, senses subjugated, ready for inner practice, freedom from external pull.
facets.psychic: The soul withdraws the senses from their objects, gathering attention for the inner work.

balanced.text:
When pratyahara is established, the senses follow the mind inward. External stimuli no longer disturb. The yogi can be in the world but the mind is gathered within.

Somatic: Senses able to disengage from objects at will, body present but not driven by stimuli, healthy sense functioning when needed.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from sensory triggering, ability to choose engagement, peace from inner gathering.

Cognitive: Mind gathered within, senses subjugated, ready for inner practice, freedom from external pull.

Psychic: The soul withdraws the senses from their objects, gathering attention for the inner work.
balanced.vignette: In a loud café Meera can draw her attention inward at will, the clatter receding to a distant hum while she works. She is not at the mercy of every sound and screen; she can close the doors.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced sensory withdrawal becomes harsh asceticism, denial of senses, harm to sense organs through forced suppression rather than natural withdrawal.
facets.somatic: Atrophied sensory function from forced denial, harm to sense organs, sensory deprivation.
facets.emotional: Emotional rejection of sensory life, harsh asceticism, denial of natural human experience.
facets.cognitive: Mental forcing of senses, suppression rather than transcendence, eventual breakdown of forced control.
facets.psychic: Withdrawal hardens into rigid sensory shutdown, a brittle isolation.

excess.text:
Forced sensory withdrawal becomes harsh asceticism, denial of senses, harm to sense organs through forced suppression rather than natural withdrawal.

Somatic: Atrophied sensory function from forced denial, harm to sense organs, sensory deprivation.

Emotional: Emotional rejection of sensory life, harsh asceticism, denial of natural human experience.

Cognitive: Mental forcing of senses, suppression rather than transcendence, eventual breakdown of forced control.

Psychic: Withdrawal hardens into rigid sensory shutdown, a brittle isolation.
excess.vignette: Overwhelmed by the world, Sam has withdrawn his senses so completely that he now feels numb and unreachable, cut off from ordinary pleasure and connection alike. Withdrawal has become dissociation.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Without any pratyahara, the senses dominate—mind constantly pulled by sensory input, no inner gathering possible, sensory addictions.
facets.somatic: Senses ruling the body, constant sensory stimulation needed, addictions, depleted from sensory pursuit.
facets.emotional: Emotional life dominated by sensory experience, no inner emotional life, sensory cravings.
facets.cognitive: Mind dragged by senses, inability to focus inward, no foundation for inner limbs of yoga.
facets.psychic: The soul cannot withdraw, dragged outward by every sense-object.

deficient.text:
Without any pratyahara, the senses dominate—mind constantly pulled by sensory input, no inner gathering possible, sensory addictions.

Somatic: Senses ruling the body, constant sensory stimulation needed, addictions, depleted from sensory pursuit.

Emotional: Emotional life dominated by sensory experience, no inner emotional life, sensory cravings.

Cognitive: Mind dragged by senses, inability to focus inward, no foundation for inner limbs of yoga.

Psychic: The soul cannot withdraw, dragged outward by every sense-object.
deficient.vignette: Dan is captive to every ping, flavor, and flicker; his attention is dragged wherever the loudest stimulus points, all day, with no ability to look away. The senses run him entirely.

attributes.title: Dharana (Concentration/Fixing the Mind)
attributes.aspect: Dharana is the sixth limb—concentration, the fixing of the mind on one place or object. It is the first of the three internal limbs. The mind is bound to one location (desha-bandha), though the attention may be intermittent. Compared to a series of distinct water droplets.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Practice (fixing attention on one point) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (one-pointed fixing) · Key relation: The concept underlying Dhāraṇā (the 6th limb); first of the three inner limbs.
coordinate.key_relation: The concept underlying Dhāraṇā (the 6th limb); first of the three inner limbs.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When dharana is established, the mind can fix on one object for sustained periods, though attention may still flicker. This is the foundation for dhyana.
facets.somatic: Body steady to support concentration, healthy nervous system, focused physical engagement.
facets.emotional: Emotional focus and dedication to practice, ability to commit, focused devotion.
facets.cognitive: Mind capable of fixing on one object, sustained attention, beginning of one-pointedness, intermittent but returning focus.
facets.psychic: The soul fixes attention on a single point, the scattered mind gathered into steadiness.

balanced.text:
When dharana is established, the mind can fix on one object for sustained periods, though attention may still flicker. This is the foundation for dhyana.

Somatic: Body steady to support concentration, healthy nervous system, focused physical engagement.

Emotional: Emotional focus and dedication to practice, ability to commit, focused devotion.

Cognitive: Mind capable of fixing on one object, sustained attention, beginning of one-pointedness, intermittent but returning focus.

Psychic: The soul fixes attention on a single point, the scattered mind gathered into steadiness.
balanced.vignette: Given one thing to focus on, Priya can hold it—steadily, gently—returning each time the mind wanders without strain. Her concentration is a muscle she has patiently built.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced concentration without proper foundation creates mental strain, frustration, headaches, and inability to relax.
facets.somatic: Tension and strain from forced concentration, headaches, eye strain, physical stress.
facets.emotional: Emotional frustration with concentration difficulties, anxiety about practice, self-criticism.
facets.cognitive: Mental strain, forced effort that prevents natural focus, frustration that makes concentration harder.
facets.psychic: The soul cramps around its focus, the forced concentration straining and snapping.

excess.text:
Forced concentration without proper foundation creates mental strain, frustration, headaches, and inability to relax.

Somatic: Tension and strain from forced concentration, headaches, eye strain, physical stress.

Emotional: Emotional frustration with concentration difficulties, anxiety about practice, self-criticism.

Cognitive: Mental strain, forced effort that prevents natural focus, frustration that makes concentration harder.

Psychic: The soul cramps around its focus, the forced concentration straining and snapping.
excess.vignette: Jon fixes on his object with a white-knuckle intensity, straining so hard to ‘not lose focus’ that he gives himself a headache and misses the point. The grip is too tight to hold anything.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Without dharana, the mind cannot stay on one object even briefly—scattered, distracted, unable to develop further yogic capacities.
facets.somatic: Restless body unable to support concentration, scattered physical energy.
facets.emotional: Emotional scatteredness, lack of focused engagement, surface-level emotional life.
facets.cognitive: Completely scattered mind, monkey-mind, inability to fix attention even briefly, foundation lacking for higher practices.
facets.psychic: The soul’s attention will not hold, scattering the moment it is placed.

deficient.text:
Without dharana, the mind cannot stay on one object even briefly—scattered, distracted, unable to develop further yogic capacities.

Somatic: Restless body unable to support concentration, scattered physical energy.

Emotional: Emotional scatteredness, lack of focused engagement, surface-level emotional life.

Cognitive: Completely scattered mind, monkey-mind, inability to fix attention even briefly, foundation lacking for higher practices.

Psychic: The soul’s attention will not hold, scattering the moment it is placed.
deficient.vignette: Sara cannot hold a single thought for thirty seconds; her attention scatters at the first distraction and never comes back. Focused work of any depth is, for now, beyond her.

attributes.title: Dhyana (Meditation as 7th Limb)
attributes.aspect: Dhyana is the seventh limb—meditation as the continuous uninterrupted flow of attention to one object. Unlike dharana (intermittent droplets), dhyana is a continuous stream. There is still subject-object distinction but absorption is complete.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Stage / practice (sustained meditative flow) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition (unbroken attention) · Key relation: The 7th limb; between Dhāraṇā and Samādhi.
coordinate.key_relation: The 7th limb; between Dhāraṇā and Samādhi.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When dhyana is achieved, attention flows continuously and effortlessly on the object. The mind is fully absorbed without losing the object.
facets.somatic: Body completely settled, physical absorption in meditation, prolonged sitting effortless.
facets.emotional: Profound emotional immersion in object of meditation, devotional absorption, emotional peace.
facets.cognitive: Continuous unbroken flow of attention, deep absorption, mind completely engaged with object, oil-like flow.
facets.psychic: The soul sustains an unbroken flow of attention toward its object, concentration ripening into meditation.

balanced.text:
When dhyana is achieved, attention flows continuously and effortlessly on the object. The mind is fully absorbed without losing the object.

Somatic: Body completely settled, physical absorption in meditation, prolonged sitting effortless.

Emotional: Profound emotional immersion in object of meditation, devotional absorption, emotional peace.

Cognitive: Continuous unbroken flow of attention, deep absorption, mind completely engaged with object, oil-like flow.

Psychic: The soul sustains an unbroken flow of attention toward its object, concentration ripening into meditation.
balanced.vignette: Past the effort of concentration, Amara’s awareness begins to flow toward its object in an unbroken stream, sustained and easeful. Meditation has stopped being something she does and become something that happens.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive attachment to meditation states becomes spiritual materialism, addiction to experiences, attachment to the meditator-meditation duality.
facets.somatic: Physical withdrawal for excessive meditation, neglect of life responsibilities, prolonged sitting damaging body.
facets.emotional: Spiritual materialism, attachment to peaceful states, escape from life into meditation.
facets.cognitive: Mental attachment to dhyana experiences, addiction to meditation states, ego subtly identifying with achievement.
facets.psychic: The soul strains for absorption, the grasping disturbing the very flow it seeks.

excess.text:
Excessive attachment to meditation states becomes spiritual materialism, addiction to experiences, attachment to the meditator-meditation duality.

Somatic: Physical withdrawal for excessive meditation, neglect of life responsibilities, prolonged sitting damaging body.

Emotional: Spiritual materialism, attachment to peaceful states, escape from life into meditation.

Cognitive: Mental attachment to dhyana experiences, addiction to meditation states, ego subtly identifying with achievement.

Psychic: The soul strains for absorption, the grasping disturbing the very flow it seeks.
excess.vignette: Ben strains to ‘get into the meditative state,’ grasping at flow, judging each sit by whether he reached it—and the grasping is exactly what keeps it away. He is chasing a stillness that only comes when he stops.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Without dhyana, dharana cannot deepen into samadhi. Practice remains shallow, no deep absorption possible.
facets.somatic: Body never reaches deep meditative absorption, no settled state.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without deep meditative healing, surface practice only.
facets.cognitive: Mind never sustains uninterrupted attention, no flow state, practice plateaus.
facets.psychic: The soul’s attention is never sustained, meditation collapsing back into distraction.

deficient.text:
Without dhyana, dharana cannot deepen into samadhi. Practice remains shallow, no deep absorption possible.

Somatic: Body never reaches deep meditative absorption, no settled state.

Emotional: Emotional life without deep meditative healing, surface practice only.

Cognitive: Mind never sustains uninterrupted attention, no flow state, practice plateaus.

Psychic: The soul’s attention is never sustained, meditation collapsing back into distraction.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no inner life to speak of—no meditation, no reflection, no still stream of attention, only the outward churn of doing. The whole seventh-limb dimension is uncharted country for him.

attributes.title: Samadhi (Meditative Absorption)
attributes.aspect: Samadhi is the eighth limb—meditative absorption where the mind so identifies with the object that subject-object distinction collapses. Only the object shines forth in pure awareness. There are many types: samprajnata (with seed/cognition), asamprajnata (without seed), and the cloud of virtue (dharma-megha) being the highest.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State (absorption) · Valence: Fruit / attained state · Guṇa: Sattva, beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit (the culmination) · Key relation: The 8th limb; the fruit of Dhyāna.
coordinate.key_relation: The 8th limb; the fruit of Dhyāna.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When samadhi is achieved, consciousness merges with the object of meditation. Subject-object duality dissolves; only essence remains. This is the goal of the eight-limbed path.
facets.somatic: Body in deep state of stillness, vital functions reduced to minimum, somatic peace beyond normal states.
facets.emotional: Beyond emotional states, profound bliss in some samadhis, peace beyond emotion.
facets.cognitive: Subject-object collapse, pure awareness of essence, beyond ordinary cognition, transcendent state.
facets.psychic: The soul absorbs so fully into its object that the sense of a separate observer dissolves.

balanced.text:
When samadhi is achieved, consciousness merges with the object of meditation. Subject-object duality dissolves; only essence remains. This is the goal of the eight-limbed path.

Somatic: Body in deep state of stillness, vital functions reduced to minimum, somatic peace beyond normal states.

Emotional: Beyond emotional states, profound bliss in some samadhis, peace beyond emotion.

Cognitive: Subject-object collapse, pure awareness of essence, beyond ordinary cognition, transcendent state.

Psychic: The soul absorbs so fully into its object that the sense of a separate observer dissolves.
balanced.vignette: Now and then, deep in practice, the sense of a separate ‘meditator’ thins and Meera rests in a spacious, absorbed unity where subject and object soften into one. She holds these moments lightly, as gifts.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Attachment to samadhi states becomes the most subtle bondage—seeking experiences, clinging to bliss, mistaking states for liberation.
facets.somatic: Physical withdrawal for samadhi-seeking, neglect of life, body damaged by extreme practices.
facets.emotional: Attachment to bliss states, spiritual addiction, samadhi-seeking as compensation.
facets.cognitive: Subtle ego claiming samadhi, attachment to experiences, mistaking states for final liberation.
facets.psychic: The soul chases absorption as a peak experience to collect, the seeking obstructing it.

excess.text:
Attachment to samadhi states becomes the most subtle bondage—seeking experiences, clinging to bliss, mistaking states for liberation.

Somatic: Physical withdrawal for samadhi-seeking, neglect of life, body damaged by extreme practices.

Emotional: Attachment to bliss states, spiritual addiction, samadhi-seeking as compensation.

Cognitive: Subtle ego claiming samadhi, attachment to experiences, mistaking states for final liberation.

Psychic: The soul chases absorption as a peak experience to collect, the seeking obstructing it.
excess.vignette: Karl chases absorption like a drug, judging every sit by the high, faking depth in his descriptions, addicted to the state rather than freed by it. He grasps at the very absorption that requires letting go.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Without ever reaching samadhi, the yogic path remains incomplete. The deepest insights and liberation depend on samadhi.
facets.somatic: Body never knows deep samadhi-state benefits.
facets.emotional: Emotional life never knows samadhi-peace.
facets.cognitive: Mind never knows transcendence, remains in subject-object duality.
facets.psychic: The soul never approaches absorption, the divide between knower and known never thinning.

deficient.text:
Without ever reaching samadhi, the yogic path remains incomplete. The deepest insights and liberation depend on samadhi.

Somatic: Body never knows deep samadhi-state benefits.

Emotional: Emotional life never knows samadhi-peace.

Cognitive: Mind never knows transcendence, remains in subject-object duality.

Psychic: The soul never approaches absorption, the divide between knower and known never thinning.
deficient.vignette: Sam’s attention is always divided—half here, half elsewhere—and he has never once known the simple, absorbed unity of being wholly in one thing. His inner life is permanently split.

attributes.title: Samyama (Combined Practice of the Three Inner Limbs)
attributes.aspect: Samyama is the combined application of dharana, dhyana, and samadhi on a single object. It is the technical practice through which the yogi gains direct knowledge (prajna) of whatever object is the focus. Most of Book III’s siddhis (powers) arise from samyama applied to specific objects.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice (the combined application of the three inner limbs) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (penetrates an object’s essence) · Key relation: Whole — Dhāraṇā, Dhyāna, and Samādhi applied together; yields the siddhis.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice (the combined application of the three inner limbs)
coordinate.key_relation: Whole — Dhāraṇā, Dhyāna, and Samādhi applied together; yields the siddhis.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When samyama is mastered, the yogi can apply it to various objects to gain insight, knowledge, and yogic powers. It is the master-key of advanced practice.
facets.somatic: Body able to sustain prolonged samyama, vital functions supporting deep practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional stability enabling sustained samyama, dedication to deep practice.
facets.cognitive: Ability to apply dharana-dhyana-samadhi as one continuous practice, gain direct knowledge of objects, develop prajna.
facets.psychic: The soul applies concentration, meditation, and absorption together on one object, penetrating to its essence.

balanced.text:
When samyama is mastered, the yogi can apply it to various objects to gain insight, knowledge, and yogic powers. It is the master-key of advanced practice.

Somatic: Body able to sustain prolonged samyama, vital functions supporting deep practice.

Emotional: Emotional stability enabling sustained samyama, dedication to deep practice.

Cognitive: Ability to apply dharana-dhyana-samadhi as one continuous practice, gain direct knowledge of objects, develop prajna.

Psychic: The soul applies concentration, meditation, and absorption together on one object, penetrating to its essence.
balanced.vignette: Turning his integrated attention on a single question, Ravi finds it opens—insight arising not by force but by sustained, absorbed inquiry. The deep practice yields understanding he could not have reasoned his way to.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive samyama for siddhis becomes obstacle—Patanjali warns these powers can themselves bind the yogi.
facets.somatic: Body damaged by samyama-seeking for powers, neglect of basic needs.
facets.emotional: Power-seeking emotions, attachment to siddhis, ego inflation.
facets.cognitive: Pursuit of siddhis as goal, distraction from liberation, mental obsession with powers.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on the powers samyama yields, chasing siddhis rather than insight.

excess.text:
Excessive samyama for siddhis becomes obstacle—Patanjali warns these powers can themselves bind the yogi.

Somatic: Body damaged by samyama-seeking for powers, neglect of basic needs.

Emotional: Power-seeking emotions, attachment to siddhis, ego inflation.

Cognitive: Pursuit of siddhis as goal, distraction from liberation, mental obsession with powers.

Psychic: The soul fixates on the powers samyama yields, chasing siddhis rather than insight.
excess.vignette: Karl learned samyama and now hunts for powers—reading minds, bending events—dazzled by siddhis and blind to the liberation they were meant to serve. He has aimed a sacred instrument at his own ego.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Without samyama, advanced yogic insights and powers don’t develop. The yogic path remains incomplete in its potential.
facets.somatic: Body never develops yogic capacities through samyama.
facets.emotional: Emotional life lacks the transformations samyama brings.
facets.cognitive: Mind never gains direct prajna-knowledge of objects, no advanced insights.
facets.psychic: The soul never combines the inner limbs, its practice too scattered to penetrate anything.

deficient.text:
Without samyama, advanced yogic insights and powers don’t develop. The yogic path remains incomplete in its potential.

Somatic: Body never develops yogic capacities through samyama.

Emotional: Emotional life lacks the transformations samyama brings.

Cognitive: Mind never gains direct prajna-knowledge of objects, no advanced insights.

Psychic: The soul never combines the inner limbs, its practice too scattered to penetrate anything.
deficient.vignette: Dev’s practice never reaches the depth where insight opens; he skims the surface of concentration and meditation and never combines them into penetrating vision. The door is there and he never leans on it.

attributes.title: Samprajnata Samadhi (Cognitive/Seeded Absorption)
attributes.aspect: Samprajnata samadhi is samadhi ‘with seed’—absorption that still includes some content (object, idea, bliss, or ego-sense). It has four progressive stages: savitarka (with reasoning), nirvitarka (without reasoning), savichara (with subtle insight), nirvichara (without subtle insight).

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State (seeded absorption — with an object) · Valence: Attained state (a stage toward the seedless) · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit (a graded absorption) · Key relation: Part — the seeded form of Samādhi; precedes Asamprajñāta.
coordinate.valence: Attained state (a stage toward the seedless)
coordinate.key_relation: Part — the seeded form of Samādhi; precedes Asamprajñāta.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When samprajnata is properly attained, the yogi has direct cognition of objects in their essence. Wisdom from samadhi shines forth.
facets.somatic: Body in deep absorption supporting samadhi, vital functions minimized.
facets.emotional: Profound bliss (ananda samadhi), emotional unity with object.
facets.cognitive: Direct cognition of object’s essence, wisdom (prajna) arising, transformed knowing.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in seeded absorption, deeply concentrated while still supported by a subtle object.

balanced.text:
When samprajnata is properly attained, the yogi has direct cognition of objects in their essence. Wisdom from samadhi shines forth.

Somatic: Body in deep absorption supporting samadhi, vital functions minimized.

Emotional: Profound bliss (ananda samadhi), emotional unity with object.

Cognitive: Direct cognition of object’s essence, wisdom (prajna) arising, transformed knowing.

Psychic: The soul rests in seeded absorption, deeply concentrated while still supported by a subtle object.
balanced.vignette: In her deepest sits, Amara rests absorbed in a single subtle object—an idea, a quality, a light—and from that seeded stillness genuine insight arises. The object is a raft she rides toward understanding.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Attachment to samprajnata states with their bliss and insights becomes obstacle to higher asamprajnata samadhi.
facets.somatic: Attachment to physical bliss in samadhi, seeking experiences.
facets.emotional: Attachment to ananda samadhi bliss, spiritual hedonism.
facets.cognitive: Mental attachment to insights and powers gained, identification with samadhi experiences.
facets.psychic: The soul clings to the refined object of absorption, mistaking the seeded state for final freedom.

excess.text:
Attachment to samprajnata states with their bliss and insights becomes obstacle to higher asamprajnata samadhi.

Somatic: Attachment to physical bliss in samadhi, seeking experiences.

Emotional: Attachment to ananda samadhi bliss, spiritual hedonism.

Cognitive: Mental attachment to insights and powers gained, identification with samadhi experiences.

Psychic: The soul clings to the refined object of absorption, mistaking the seeded state for final freedom.
excess.vignette: Ben has grown attached to the blissful insights of seeded absorption, collecting them, recounting them, mistaking the pleasant by-products for the goal. He clings to the raft and refuses to reach the shore.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Without reaching samprajnata, the deeper realizations from yogic practice don’t arise. Subtle insights of prakriti remain hidden.
facets.somatic: Body never knows deep absorption.
facets.emotional: Never experiences ananda-samadhi bliss.
facets.cognitive: Mind never gains direct knowledge through samadhi.
facets.psychic: The soul never reaches even seeded absorption, its mind too restless to settle on anything.

deficient.text:
Without reaching samprajnata, the deeper realizations from yogic practice don’t arise. Subtle insights of prakriti remain hidden.

Somatic: Body never knows deep absorption.

Emotional: Never experiences ananda-samadhi bliss.

Cognitive: Mind never gains direct knowledge through samadhi.

Psychic: The soul never reaches even seeded absorption, its mind too restless to settle on anything.
deficient.vignette: For Sara there is no absorption at all, seeded or otherwise—her mind will not settle on any object long enough to penetrate it. The very first rung of deep practice is out of reach.

attributes.title: Asamprajnata Samadhi (Supraconscious Absorption)
attributes.aspect: Asamprajnata samadhi is samadhi ‘without seed’ or ‘beyond cognition’—the higher samadhi where all mental content has ceased and only latent impressions (samskaras) remain. There is no object, only pure absorption.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State (seedless absorption — objectless) · Valence: Fruit (the higher absorption) · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Transcendent · Causal status: Fruit (objectless absorption) · Key relation: Part — the seedless form of Samādhi; follows Samprajñāta, opening toward Kaivalya.
coordinate.key_relation: Part — the seedless form of Samādhi; follows Samprajñāta, opening toward Kaivalya.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When asamprajnata samadhi is attained, all mental fluctuations are restrained. The yogi rests in pure consciousness beyond all cognition. Liberation is near.
facets.somatic: Body in deepest state of cessation, vital functions at minimum.
facets.emotional: Beyond emotional states entirely, pure stillness.
facets.cognitive: All vrittis stilled, no object of cognition, pure samadhi without seed.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in seedless absorption, all objects dissolved, awareness abiding in its own nature.

balanced.text:
When asamprajnata samadhi is attained, all mental fluctuations are restrained. The yogi rests in pure consciousness beyond all cognition. Liberation is near.

Somatic: Body in deepest state of cessation, vital functions at minimum.

Emotional: Beyond emotional states entirely, pure stillness.

Cognitive: All vrittis stilled, no object of cognition, pure samadhi without seed.

Psychic: The soul rests in seedless absorption, all objects dissolved, awareness abiding in its own nature.
balanced.vignette: Very rarely, past even the subtle object, Meera rests in a stillness with no content at all—pure being, seedless, wanting nothing. She does not grasp at it; she lets it come and go like weather.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Premature claims of asamprajnata samadhi or attachment to formless states create subtle bondage.
facets.somatic: Catatonia mistaken for asamprajnata, harmful states.
facets.emotional: Emotional escape disguised as samadhi.
facets.cognitive: False claims of seedless samadhi, dissociation mistaken for absorption.
facets.psychic: The soul grasps at the seedless state, the very reaching keeping a seed alive.

excess.text:
Premature claims of asamprajnata samadhi or attachment to formless states create subtle bondage.

Somatic: Catatonia mistaken for asamprajnata, harmful states.

Emotional: Emotional escape disguised as samadhi.

Cognitive: False claims of seedless samadhi, dissociation mistaken for absorption.

Psychic: The soul grasps at the seedless state, the very reaching keeping a seed alive.
excess.vignette: Karl announces he has reached the seedless state—while remaining, by every sign, as restless and reactive as ever. He has taken the word for the thing, and made an identity out of a stillness he has not found.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Without ever reaching asamprajnata, the deepest yogic state remains unrealized; samskaras continue to fructify.
facets.somatic: Body never knows this deepest state.
facets.emotional: Never reaches beyond emotional dimensions.
facets.cognitive: Mind never knows cessation, samskaras remain active.
facets.psychic: The soul knows only object-bound consciousness, the seedless ground never approached.

deficient.text:
Without ever reaching asamprajnata, the deepest yogic state remains unrealized; samskaras continue to fructify.

Somatic: Body never knows this deepest state.

Emotional: Never reaches beyond emotional dimensions.

Cognitive: Mind never knows cessation, samskaras remain active.

Psychic: The soul knows only object-bound consciousness, the seedless ground never approached.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s awareness is always bound to some object—a thought, a sound, a worry—and the idea of a contentless, objectless stillness is not something he doubts so much as cannot conceive.

attributes.title: Dharma-Megha Samadhi (Cloud of Virtue Samadhi)
attributes.aspect: Dharma-megha samadhi is the ‘cloud of virtue’ samadhi—the supreme samadhi that comes when even the fruits of yogic accomplishment (including omniscience) hold no interest. It showers dharma (virtue, righteousness) on the yogi, destroying all kleshas and karma. It precedes kaivalya (final liberation).

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State (the supreme absorption — the cloud of virtue) · Valence: The pinnacle / fruit · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Transcendent · Causal status: Fruit (the culminating absorption, raining down liberation) · Key relation: Sequence — the highest Samādhi; the threshold of Kaivalya.
coordinate.ontic_mode: State (the supreme absorption — the cloud of virtue)
coordinate.causal_status: Fruit (the culminating absorption, raining down liberation)
coordinate.key_relation: Sequence — the highest Samādhi; the threshold of Kaivalya.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When dharma-megha samadhi arises, all afflictions and karma are destroyed. The yogi stands at the threshold of kaivalya—final liberation.
facets.somatic: Body completely transformed by ultimate samadhi, fully purified instrument.
facets.emotional: Beyond all emotional bondage, transcendent peace, dharmic spontaneity.
facets.cognitive: All kleshas destroyed, dharma flowing spontaneously, mind at threshold of pure consciousness.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in the cloud-of-virtue absorption, where even the highest discernment is released and freedom rains down.

balanced.text:
When dharma-megha samadhi arises, all afflictions and karma are destroyed. The yogi stands at the threshold of kaivalya—final liberation.

Somatic: Body completely transformed by ultimate samadhi, fully purified instrument.

Emotional: Beyond all emotional bondage, transcendent peace, dharmic spontaneity.

Cognitive: All kleshas destroyed, dharma flowing spontaneously, mind at threshold of pure consciousness.

Psychic: The soul rests in the cloud-of-virtue absorption, where even the highest discernment is released and freedom rains down.
balanced.vignette: After long practice, something has shifted in Leela: virtue seems to rain from her now without effort—patience, kindness, clarity—as bondage quietly loosens. She takes no credit; it simply falls, like weather from a cloud.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Cannot be ‘excessive’ as it is the supreme state—however, false claims of dharma-megha create dangerous spiritual delusion.
facets.somatic: False physical states claimed as dharma-megha.
facets.emotional: False emotional transcendence claims.
facets.cognitive: False claims of supreme realization, dangerous spiritual ego.
facets.psychic: The soul grasps at this supreme state as an attainment, the grasping a last subtle bondage.

excess.text:
Cannot be ‘excessive’ as it is the supreme state—however, false claims of dharma-megha create dangerous spiritual delusion.

Somatic: False physical states claimed as dharma-megha.

Emotional: False emotional transcendence claims.

Cognitive: False claims of supreme realization, dangerous spiritual ego.

Psychic: The soul grasps at this supreme state as an attainment, the grasping a last subtle bondage.
excess.vignette: Convinced he has ‘reached the cloud of virtue,’ Julian wears his supposed attainment as a crown, subtly superior, spiritually proud. The very grasping proves how far the real thing still is.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Without dharma-megha, kaivalya is not yet attained. Kleshas may continue subtly.
facets.somatic: Body still subject to karmic fruition.
facets.emotional: Subtle emotional residue remaining.
facets.cognitive: Mind still has subtle attachment, even to spiritual attainments.
facets.psychic: The soul, far from this height, is still bound by its cravings and confusions.

deficient.text:
Without dharma-megha, kaivalya is not yet attained. Kleshas may continue subtly.

Somatic: Body still subject to karmic fruition.

Emotional: Subtle emotional residue remaining.

Cognitive: Mind still has subtle attachment, even to spiritual attainments.

Psychic: The soul, far from this height, is still bound by its cravings and confusions.
deficient.vignette: For Dana, liberation is not even a rumor; she is thoroughly entangled in craving and aversion, the rain of virtue a poetry with no referent in her life. The cloud has not begun to gather.

attributes.title: Kriya-Yoga (Yoga of Action)
attributes.aspect: Kriya-yoga is the ‘yoga of action’ Patanjali presents in II.1—the integrated practice of tapas (austerity), svadhyaya (study), and Ishvara-pranidhana (devotion to the Lord). It is the action-oriented preparatory path that combines elements of karma, jnana, and bhakti yoga. Its purpose is to attenuate kleshas and bring samadhi.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice (the preliminary yoga of action) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means (the practical engine of transformation) · Key relation: Whole — the triad of Tapas, Svādhyāya, and Īśvara-praṇidhāna (the last three niyamas).
coordinate.key_relation: Whole — the triad of Tapas, Svādhyāya, and Īśvara-praṇidhāna (the last three niyamas).

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When kriya-yoga is properly practiced, the three components synergize: discipline purifies, study illumines, and devotion surrenders. The kleshas weaken, samadhi approaches.
facets.somatic: Body disciplined through tapas, well-cared-for through svadhyaya wisdom, surrendered through devotion.
facets.emotional: Emotional refinement through tapas, devotional warmth through pranidhana, illumined through svadhyaya.
facets.cognitive: Mental purification through discipline, intellectual development through study, surrender through devotion—mind balanced.
facets.psychic: The soul takes up the yoga of action — discipline, self-study, surrender — as the practical engine of transformation.

balanced.text:
When kriya-yoga is properly practiced, the three components synergize: discipline purifies, study illumines, and devotion surrenders. The kleshas weaken, samadhi approaches.

Somatic: Body disciplined through tapas, well-cared-for through svadhyaya wisdom, surrendered through devotion.

Emotional: Emotional refinement through tapas, devotional warmth through pranidhana, illumined through svadhyaya.

Cognitive: Mental purification through discipline, intellectual development through study, surrender through devotion—mind balanced.

Psychic: The soul takes up the yoga of action — discipline, self-study, surrender — as the practical engine of transformation.
balanced.vignette: Ravi’s practice is woven through his ordinary week—a discipline he keeps, a text he studies, a habit of surrendering outcomes he cannot control. The three strands together quietly transform him, without his leaving his life to do it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Imbalanced kriya-yoga (too much of one component) creates distortion—harsh asceticism, dry intellectualism, or blind devotionalism.
facets.somatic: Excessive tapas damaging body, ascetic injury, neglect of other limbs.
facets.emotional: Dry intellectualism without devotion, harsh self-discipline without warmth.
facets.cognitive: Imbalanced practice, missing the integration of three approaches.
facets.psychic: The soul over-emphasizes one strand, harsh discipline or endless study, and the balance is lost.

excess.text:
Imbalanced kriya-yoga (too much of one component) creates distortion—harsh asceticism, dry intellectualism, or blind devotionalism.

Somatic: Excessive tapas damaging body, ascetic injury, neglect of other limbs.

Emotional: Dry intellectualism without devotion, harsh self-discipline without warmth.

Cognitive: Imbalanced practice, missing the integration of three approaches.

Psychic: The soul over-emphasizes one strand, harsh discipline or endless study, and the balance is lost.
excess.vignette: Ben has turned the yoga of action into grim over-effort—punishing discipline, obsessive study, a white-knuckle ‘surrender’ that is really control in disguise. He is trying to force his way to freedom.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Without kriya-yoga, those with rajasic/tamasic minds lack the preparatory practice for samadhi. Without action, vrittis cannot be subdued.
facets.somatic: Undisciplined body, no transformative practice, no preparation.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without devotional dimension, no transformation through practice.
facets.cognitive: Mind unprepared for higher practice, no action-based purification.
facets.psychic: The soul practices none of the three, its aspiration left without a working method.

deficient.text:
Without kriya-yoga, those with rajasic/tamasic minds lack the preparatory practice for samadhi. Without action, vrittis cannot be subdued.

Somatic: Undisciplined body, no transformative practice, no preparation.

Emotional: Emotional life without devotional dimension, no transformation through practice.

Cognitive: Mind unprepared for higher practice, no action-based purification.

Psychic: The soul practices none of the three, its aspiration left without a working method.
deficient.vignette: Sara admires the idea of a transformative practice and does none of it—no discipline, no study, no surrender, just drift. The engine of change sits idle for want of anyone to start it.

attributes.title: Viveka-Khyati (Discriminative Discernment)
attributes.aspect: Viveka-khyati is the supreme discriminative discernment—direct experiential realization of the eternal difference between Purusha (pure consciousness) and Prakriti (matter). When this discrimination becomes unbroken (aviplava), it destroys the root klesha (avidya) and leads to kaivalya. It is the cognitive cure for ignorance.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: State / faculty (unbroken discriminative discernment) · Valence: The near-goal (the realization that liberates) · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism / fruit (discerns Puruṣa from Prakṛti) · Key relation: The direct means to Kaivalya; distinguishes seer from seen.
coordinate.key_relation: The direct means to Kaivalya; distinguishes seer from seen.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When viveka-khyati is established and unbroken, the yogi continuously recognizes the difference between Self and not-Self. This is the destroyer of avidya and the path to liberation.
facets.somatic: Body known as not-Self, healthy non-identification, body used as instrument.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from identification with feelings as Self, witnessing emotions clearly.
facets.cognitive: Continuous direct discrimination between Self and not-Self, mind serves consciousness, wisdom flows.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in unbroken discernment, directly distinguishing the witnessing awareness from the changing show of nature.

balanced.text:
When viveka-khyati is established and unbroken, the yogi continuously recognizes the difference between Self and not-Self. This is the destroyer of avidya and the path to liberation.

Somatic: Body known as not-Self, healthy non-identification, body used as instrument.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from identification with feelings as Self, witnessing emotions clearly.

Cognitive: Continuous direct discrimination between Self and not-Self, mind serves consciousness, wisdom flows.

Psychic: The soul rests in unbroken discernment, directly distinguishing the witnessing awareness from the changing show of nature.
balanced.vignette: In a hard moment Meera can tell the difference between the passing storm of feeling and the awareness watching it—between what she has and what she is. That discernment, grown over years, is quietly setting her free.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Intellectual claims of viveka without direct realization create spiritual arrogance and dangerous self-deception.
facets.somatic: Body neglect from intellectual claims of transcendence.
facets.emotional: Emotional bypassing through intellectual viveka, false detachment.
facets.cognitive: Intellectual conviction without realization, spiritual arrogance, mistaking theory for direct knowing.
facets.psychic: The soul intellectualizes discrimination, the idea standing in for the lived discernment.

excess.text:
Intellectual claims of viveka without direct realization create spiritual arrogance and dangerous self-deception.

Somatic: Body neglect from intellectual claims of transcendence.

Emotional: Emotional bypassing through intellectual viveka, false detachment.

Cognitive: Intellectual conviction without realization, spiritual arrogance, mistaking theory for direct knowing.

Psychic: The soul intellectualizes discrimination, the idea standing in for the lived discernment.
excess.vignette: Julian has turned discrimination into a cold intellectual sport, dissecting self and non-self on the page while remaining, in his actual life, as identified with his moods as anyone. The knowledge never became wisdom.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Without viveka, the fundamental confusion of Self with not-Self continues. All other kleshas flourish on this foundation.
facets.somatic: Complete identification with body as Self, all body-anxieties.
facets.emotional: Complete identification with emotions as Self, all emotional suffering.
facets.cognitive: Complete identification with mind as Self, foundational ignorance, all kleshas active.
facets.psychic: The soul cannot tell seer from seen, wholly merged with the play of nature.

deficient.text:
Without viveka, the fundamental confusion of Self with not-Self continues. All other kleshas flourish on this foundation.

Somatic: Complete identification with body as Self, all body-anxieties.

Emotional: Complete identification with emotions as Self, all emotional suffering.

Cognitive: Complete identification with mind as Self, foundational ignorance, all kleshas active.

Psychic: The soul cannot tell seer from seen, wholly merged with the play of nature.
deficient.vignette: Dan cannot tell his feelings from himself; when anger comes he is anger, when fear comes he is fear, wholly swept into each passing state. The faculty that would distinguish the seer from the seen has never awakened.

attributes.title: Pratipaksha-Bhavana (Cultivating Opposite Thoughts)
attributes.aspect: Pratipaksha-bhavana is the powerful technique of consciously cultivating thoughts opposite to disturbing ones. When thoughts of violence (himsa) arise, cultivate non-violence; when greed arises, cultivate generosity. This active mental practice transforms the citta’s tendencies.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Practice (a corrective mental technique) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (redirects a harmful thought toward its opposite) · Key relation: A method supporting the yamas and niyamas.
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (redirects a harmful thought toward its opposite)
coordinate.key_relation: A method supporting the yamas and niyamas.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When pratipaksha-bhavana is practiced wisely, the yogi transforms harmful tendencies into their opposites through active mental cultivation.
facets.somatic: Body increasingly oriented toward virtuous actions, healthy patterns developing.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns shifting from negative to positive, transformation of feeling-states.
facets.cognitive: Active transformation of thought patterns, conscious replacement of harmful thoughts, mind becomes virtuous.
facets.psychic: The soul meets a harmful thought by deliberately cultivating its opposite, redirecting the mind toward the good.

balanced.text:
When pratipaksha-bhavana is practiced wisely, the yogi transforms harmful tendencies into their opposites through active mental cultivation.

Somatic: Body increasingly oriented toward virtuous actions, healthy patterns developing.

Emotional: Emotional patterns shifting from negative to positive, transformation of feeling-states.

Cognitive: Active transformation of thought patterns, conscious replacement of harmful thoughts, mind becomes virtuous.

Psychic: The soul meets a harmful thought by deliberately cultivating its opposite, redirecting the mind toward the good.
balanced.vignette: When a spiteful thought rises, Kira notices it and deliberately turns her mind toward its opposite—a genuine wish for the person’s good—and feels the spite lose its grip. She is learning to garden her own mind.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mechanical pratipaksha-bhavana without insight becomes mere thought-suppression that doesn’t address root causes.
facets.somatic: Forced behavioral change without inner transformation.
facets.emotional: Suppressing emotions through thought replacement rather than truly transforming.
facets.cognitive: Mental gymnastics without insight, forced opposite thinking that suppresses rather than transforms.
facets.psychic: The soul forces positive thoughts over real feeling, a suppression masquerading as practice.

excess.text:
Mechanical pratipaksha-bhavana without insight becomes mere thought-suppression that doesn’t address root causes.

Somatic: Forced behavioral change without inner transformation.

Emotional: Suppressing emotions through thought replacement rather than truly transforming.

Cognitive: Mental gymnastics without insight, forced opposite thinking that suppresses rather than transforms.

Psychic: The soul forces positive thoughts over real feeling, a suppression masquerading as practice.
excess.vignette: Sam slaps a positive thought over every negative one so fast that the real feeling never gets felt—‘good vibes only,’ a relentless cheerfulness that is really suppression. The technique has become a way to bypass his own heart.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Without pratipaksha-bhavana, harmful thoughts run unchecked, creating samskaras and karma. The mind cannot be reformed.
facets.somatic: Body driven by unchecked harmful impulses.
facets.emotional: Negative emotions run wild without conscious transformation.
facets.cognitive: Mind dominated by harmful thoughts, no active cultivation of virtue, samskaras of vice strengthen.
facets.psychic: The soul lets harmful thoughts run unopposed, swept along by every negative current.

deficient.text:
Without pratipaksha-bhavana, harmful thoughts run unchecked, creating samskaras and karma. The mind cannot be reformed.

Somatic: Body driven by unchecked harmful impulses.

Emotional: Negative emotions run wild without conscious transformation.

Cognitive: Mind dominated by harmful thoughts, no active cultivation of virtue, samskaras of vice strengthen.

Psychic: The soul lets harmful thoughts run unopposed, swept along by every negative current.
deficient.vignette: Dana lets every dark thought run its full course unopposed—the resentment rehearsed, the catastrophe elaborated, the grudge fed—never once reaching for a counter-thought. Her mind is a field where only weeds are watered.

attributes.title: Ahimsa (Non-Violence) [Yoga Sutra Context]
attributes.aspect: In the Yoga Sutras, Ahimsa is the first and foundational Yama. It is non-harming in thought, word, and deed toward all beings. Patanjali states that when Ahimsa is firmly established, all hostility ceases in the presence of the yogi (Sutra 2.35).

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Virtue — a yama (non-harming) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate (the foundational restraint) · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Foundation (the first and root yama) · Key relation: A yama (part of the Yama limb); the ground of all the others.
coordinate.key_relation: A yama (part of the Yama limb); the ground of all the others.

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When Ahimsa is firmly established, the yogi radiates such peaceful presence that hostility cannot exist around them. There is profound non-harming in thoughts, words, and actions.
facets.somatic: Calm and peaceful physical presence, healthy nervous system, balanced energy that doesn’t provoke others, naturally non-aggressive body language, healthy biology from non-stressful living.
facets.emotional: Deep emotional peace and compassion for all beings, freedom from anger and aggression, emotional warmth that disarms hostility, ability to remain peaceful under provocation.
facets.cognitive: Mind free from violent thoughts, mental peace radiates outward, freedom from internal hostility, wisdom about interconnectedness, thoughts that cause no harm.
facets.psychic: The soul establishes non-harming in thought, word, and deed, and a deep harmlessness radiates from it.

balanced.text:
When Ahimsa is firmly established, the yogi radiates such peaceful presence that hostility cannot exist around them. There is profound non-harming in thoughts, words, and actions.

Somatic: Calm and peaceful physical presence, healthy nervous system, balanced energy that doesn’t provoke others, naturally non-aggressive body language, healthy biology from non-stressful living.

Emotional: Deep emotional peace and compassion for all beings, freedom from anger and aggression, emotional warmth that disarms hostility, ability to remain peaceful under provocation.

Cognitive: Mind free from violent thoughts, mental peace radiates outward, freedom from internal hostility, wisdom about interconnectedness, thoughts that cause no harm.

Psychic: The soul establishes non-harming in thought, word, and deed, and a deep harmlessness radiates from it.
balanced.vignette: Leela moves through the world with a habitual gentleness—careful with people, animals, and her own harsh self-talk—without being a pushover. Her non-violence is active kindness, not mere avoidance of harm.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Misunderstood Ahimsa becomes passive submission to harm, inability to set protective boundaries, allowing destruction in name of non-violence.
facets.somatic: Allowing physical harm to oneself or others, inability to take defensive action when needed, weakness disguised as non-violence.
facets.emotional: Emotional passivity in face of injustice, allowing emotional harm, codependent acceptance of toxic relationships.
facets.cognitive: Mental confusion about appropriate response to harm, inability to discriminate when action is needed, false peace masking enabling behavior.
facets.psychic: Non-harming becomes scrupulous paralysis, the soul afraid any act might injure.

excess.text:
Misunderstood Ahimsa becomes passive submission to harm, inability to set protective boundaries, allowing destruction in name of non-violence.

Somatic: Allowing physical harm to oneself or others, inability to take defensive action when needed, weakness disguised as non-violence.

Emotional: Emotional passivity in face of injustice, allowing emotional harm, codependent acceptance of toxic relationships.

Cognitive: Mental confusion about appropriate response to harm, inability to discriminate when action is needed, false peace masking enabling behavior.

Psychic: Non-harming becomes scrupulous paralysis, the soul afraid any act might injure.
excess.vignette: So terrified of causing any harm, Ben cannot swat a mosquito, fire a failing employee, or speak a hard truth that someone needs. His ahimsa has become a paralysis that, in its own way, does harm by omission.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Absence of Ahimsa creates violent thoughts, words, actions—the yogi cannot make progress as their consciousness is contaminated by harm.
facets.somatic: Aggressive physical behavior, violence toward others or self, stress-related illness from internal aggression, harmful actions.
facets.emotional: Anger, hatred, hostility, emotional cruelty, vindictiveness, lack of compassion, emotional violence in relationships.
facets.cognitive: Violent thoughts, mental cruelty, planning harm, mental aggression that contaminates consciousness, obstruction to all yogic progress.
facets.psychic: The soul harms freely in word and deed, the foundational restraint unobserved.

deficient.text:
Absence of Ahimsa creates violent thoughts, words, actions—the yogi cannot make progress as their consciousness is contaminated by harm.

Somatic: Aggressive physical behavior, violence toward others or self, stress-related illness from internal aggression, harmful actions.

Emotional: Anger, hatred, hostility, emotional cruelty, vindictiveness, lack of compassion, emotional violence in relationships.

Cognitive: Violent thoughts, mental cruelty, planning harm, mental aggression that contaminates consciousness, obstruction to all yogic progress.

Psychic: The soul harms freely in word and deed, the foundational restraint unobserved.
deficient.vignette: Dan leaves a trail of small cruelties he never notices—the cutting remark, the careless driving, the contempt for the weak—and would be surprised to hear himself called violent. Harm, for him, is simply background noise.

attributes.title: Satya (Truthfulness) [Yoga Sutra Context]
attributes.aspect: Satya is truthfulness in thought, word, and deed. Patanjali states that when established in truth, action and its fruits become subservient to the yogi (Sutra 2.36)—meaning their words become powerful and creative.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Vital · Ontic mode: Virtue — a yama (truthfulness) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Foundation (a root restraint) · Key relation: A yama (part of the Yama limb); kin to Ārjavam (Dim 10/24).
coordinate.key_relation: A yama (part of the Yama limb); kin to Ārjavam (Dim 10/24).

balanced.label: Balanced expression

facets.overview: When Satya is established, a person’s words and thoughts align with reality. Their pronouncements become true and creative; they can literally manifest what they speak.
facets.somatic: Body language consistent with words, calm nervous system from authenticity, voice that carries truth-power, healthy biology from non-stressful authenticity.
facets.emotional: Emotional authenticity and clarity, freedom from emotional deception, integrity in feelings, peace from authentic living.
facets.cognitive: Clear honest thinking, mind aligned with reality, freedom from self-deception, mental power that comes from truth-alignment.
facets.psychic: The soul establishes truthfulness, its word and reality so aligned that what it says comes to pass.

balanced.text:
When Satya is established, a person’s words and thoughts align with reality. Their pronouncements become true and creative; they can literally manifest what they speak.

Somatic: Body language consistent with words, calm nervous system from authenticity, voice that carries truth-power, healthy biology from non-stressful authenticity.

Emotional: Emotional authenticity and clarity, freedom from emotional deception, integrity in feelings, peace from authentic living.

Cognitive: Clear honest thinking, mind aligned with reality, freedom from self-deception, mental power that comes from truth-alignment.

Psychic: The soul establishes truthfulness, its word and reality so aligned that what it says comes to pass.
balanced.vignette: Meera tells the truth and tells it kindly—honest about the hard thing, but with care for the person hearing it. People trust her precisely because her honesty has no cruelty in it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive Satya without wisdom becomes harmful truth-telling, brutal honesty without compassion, inappropriate disclosure that damages.
facets.somatic: Harsh physical expression of truth, body language that wounds, inappropriate physical disclosure.
facets.emotional: Truth weaponized to hurt others, emotional brutality disguised as honesty, lack of compassion in disclosure.
facets.cognitive: Rigid insistence on stating all truth regardless of impact, mental harshness, inability to recognize when discretion serves higher truth.
facets.psychic: Truth is wielded harshly, honesty becoming a blunt instrument that wounds.

excess.text:
Excessive Satya without wisdom becomes harmful truth-telling, brutal honesty without compassion, inappropriate disclosure that damages.

Somatic: Harsh physical expression of truth, body language that wounds, inappropriate physical disclosure.

Emotional: Truth weaponized to hurt others, emotional brutality disguised as honesty, lack of compassion in disclosure.

Cognitive: Rigid insistence on stating all truth regardless of impact, mental harshness, inability to recognize when discretion serves higher truth.

Psychic: Truth is wielded harshly, honesty becoming a blunt instrument that wounds.
excess.vignette: Ben calls his bluntness ‘just being truthful’ and wields honesty like a blade, announcing every hard fact with no thought for timing or tenderness. His truth wounds where it could heal.

deficient.label: Deficient
