Emotional: Emotional contraction, no spaciousness.

Cognitive: Mind feels solid and bounded.

Psychic: The soul knows only the filled and the solid, with no felt sense of the open space in which everything appears.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s inner world is cramped and crowded—no room, every worry pressed against the next. The contemplation of an accommodating inner space is foreign to him; he has never once felt his awareness as anything but a small, full box, and the congestion is his default weather.

attributes.title: Uddalaka-Aruni Vidya (Self-Brahman Identity)
attributes.aspect: Meditation on the absolute identity of the Self and Brahman—the Inner Controller (Antaryami) in all elements, senses, mind. Meditation on the absolute identity of the Self and Brahman through the Antaryami teaching. He who inhabits all but is not known by any—is your Self, the inner controller, the immortal.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a vidyā · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward identity with the Absolute · Causal status: Means (realizes ‘That thou art’) · Key relation: A highest Advaitic meditation; object = the identity of Self and Brahman (Tat tvam asi, Chāndogya).
coordinate.scale: Individual, toward identity with the Absolute
coordinate.key_relation: A highest Advaitic meditation; object = the identity of Self and Brahman (Tat tvam asi, Chāndogya).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Direct realization that the inner controller in all beings is one’s own Self—and that Self is Brahman.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as instrument of the same Self in all bodies.
facets.emotional: Emotional unity through Antaryami-recognition.
facets.cognitive: Mind realizes the Inner Controller as Self-Brahman.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the one Inner Controller threading through all beings as its very own Self — the antaryāmin who rules from within.

balanced.text:
Direct realization that the inner controller in all beings is one’s own Self—and that Self is Brahman.

Somatic: Body recognized as instrument of the same Self in all bodies.

Emotional: Emotional unity through Antaryami-recognition.

Cognitive: Mind realizes the Inner Controller as Self-Brahman.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the one Inner Controller threading through all beings as its very own Self — the antaryāmin who rules from within.
balanced.vignette: ‘That thou art’—Kavya has sat with it for years, and it has slowly stopped being a doctrine and started being a suspicion she can feel: that the ground of everything and the ground of herself are not two. It doesn’t inflate her; it quiets her, dissolving a little of the wall between her and the world.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Claiming to be the controller of others—ego inflation through doctrine.
facets.somatic: Body in controlling postures toward others.
facets.emotional: Emotional manipulation under guise of Antaryami-realization.
facets.cognitive: Doctrinal misuse to claim power over others.
facets.psychic: The soul intellectualizes the identity, asserting ‘I am the inner ruler of all’ from the ego rather than realizing it.

excess.text:
Claiming to be the controller of others—ego inflation through doctrine.

Somatic: Body in controlling postures toward others.

Emotional: Emotional manipulation under guise of Antaryami-realization.

Cognitive: Doctrinal misuse to claim power over others.

Psychic: The soul intellectualizes the identity, asserting ‘I am the inner ruler of all’ from the ego rather than realizing it.
excess.vignette: Ravi announces to anyone who will listen that he IS Brahman, the Self of all—while behaving like an ordinary, rather arrogant man. He has grabbed the great identity as a trophy for the very ego it was meant to dissolve, and the inflation is precisely the opposite of what ‘that thou art’ points to.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Antaryami unknown—each being seen as separate, no recognition of inner unity.
facets.somatic: Body seen as separate from other bodies.
facets.emotional: Emotional separation from other beings.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees only differences, no inner controller in all.
facets.psychic: The soul sees itself as separate and ruled from outside, never sensing the one indweller it shares with all.

deficient.text:
Antaryami unknown—each being seen as separate, no recognition of inner unity.

Somatic: Body seen as separate from other bodies.

Emotional: Emotional separation from other beings.

Cognitive: Mind sees only differences, no inner controller in all.

Psychic: The soul sees itself as separate and ruled from outside, never sensing the one indweller it shares with all.
deficient.vignette: For Dan the grand identity of self and Absolute is words with no traction—he is his body, his name, his story, full stop, and the suggestion that he might be anything more registers as poetry at best. The teaching’s most radical claim slides off him entirely, unfelt.

attributes.title: Jyotisham Jyotir-Vidya (Light of Lights)
attributes.aspect: He is the Light of Lights. The supreme Self-luminosity by which all other lights are perceived as light. The Vidya of the Light of Lights. Meditation on Brahman as the Self-luminous principle that illumines sun, moon, fire, lightning—the original luminosity behind every perception of light.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a vidyā · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the self-luminous · Causal status: Means (meditation on the Light behind all lights) · Key relation: A highest Advaitic meditation; object = the self-luminous consciousness (Jyotiṣāṁ Jyotiḥ).
coordinate.causal_status: Means (meditation on the Light behind all lights)
coordinate.key_relation: A highest Advaitic meditation; object = the self-luminous consciousness (Jyotiṣāṁ Jyotiḥ).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of one’s own consciousness as the original Light illumining all other lights.
facets.somatic: Body’s perceptions backed by recognition of inner self-luminous Light.
facets.emotional: Emotional perceiving acknowledged as illumined by inner Self.
facets.cognitive: Mind recognizes its illumination by the Self-luminous Self.
facets.psychic: The soul knows itself as the Light of Lights — the self-luminosity by which sun, moon, and mind are themselves made known.

balanced.text:
Recognition of one’s own consciousness as the original Light illumining all other lights.

Somatic: Body’s perceptions backed by recognition of inner self-luminous Light.

Emotional: Emotional perceiving acknowledged as illumined by inner Self.

Cognitive: Mind recognizes its illumination by the Self-luminous Self.

Psychic: The soul knows itself as the Light of Lights — the self-luminosity by which sun, moon, and mind are themselves made known.
balanced.vignette: Ella has begun to notice the light by which she notices everything—the plain awareness that illumines each thought and never itself goes dark. Meditating on this ‘light of lights’ has shifted her sense of who she is, from the passing contents toward the quiet knowing in which they appear.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Claiming to BE the cosmic Light source—megalomanic.
facets.somatic: Body claimed as cosmic illuminator.
facets.emotional: Grandiose inflation as ‘light of world.’
facets.cognitive: Confusing individual experience with cosmic Light.
facets.psychic: The soul chases inner radiances and visions, mistaking lit-up experiences for the awareness that lights them.

excess.text:
Claiming to BE the cosmic Light source—megalomanic.

Somatic: Body claimed as cosmic illuminator.

Emotional: Grandiose inflation as ‘light of world.’

Cognitive: Confusing individual experience with cosmic Light.

Psychic: The soul chases inner radiances and visions, mistaking lit-up experiences for the awareness that lights them.
excess.vignette: Karl is forever chasing inner lights and luminous experiences, judging each meditation by its fireworks, convinced the flashier the vision the closer he is. He has literalized the ‘light of lights’ into a hunt for phenomena, and mistaken the show for the awareness that was watching it all along.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: All illumination believed to come from external sources—self-luminosity unrecognized.
facets.somatic: Body believed lit only by external light.
facets.emotional: Emotional life dependent on external conditions.
facets.cognitive: Mind unaware of self-luminous awareness.
facets.psychic: The soul believes itself lit only from outside, by roles and others, never suspecting it is the light by which all is seen.

deficient.text:
All illumination believed to come from external sources—self-luminosity unrecognized.

Somatic: Body believed lit only by external light.

Emotional: Emotional life dependent on external conditions.

Cognitive: Mind unaware of self-luminous awareness.

Psychic: The soul believes itself lit only from outside, by roles and others, never suspecting it is the light by which all is seen.
deficient.vignette: Dan has never once wondered what it is that is aware. The light by which he sees everything is the one thing he has never turned toward; consciousness itself is invisible to him, taken utterly for granted, and the contemplation of it as the Self of all is not even a question he has thought to ask.

attributes.title: Satya-Jnana-Ananta Vidya (Truth-Knowledge-Infinity)
attributes.aspect: Satyam, Jnanam, Anantam Brahma—Truth, Knowledge, Infinity is Brahman. Whoever knows this hidden in the cave (heart) and the highest heaven obtains all desires. Meditation on Brahman as Satyam (Truth), Jnanam (Knowledge), Anantam (Infinity) simultaneously. The triadic definition that points beyond conceptual limit. Brahman known in the heart-cave equally with the highest heaven.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a vidyā · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the infinite · Causal status: Means (meditation on Brahman as Truth-Knowledge-Infinity) · Key relation: A highest Advaitic meditation; object = Satyam-Jñānam-Anantam (Taittirīya).
coordinate.key_relation: A highest Advaitic meditation; object = Satyam-Jñānam-Anantam (Taittirīya).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of Brahman simultaneously as Truth, Knowledge, Infinity. Triadic realization.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as participant in Truth-Knowledge-Infinity.
facets.emotional: Emotional immersion in triadic Brahman.
facets.cognitive: Mind grasps the triadic nature of ultimate reality.
facets.psychic: The soul contemplates Brahman as Truth, Knowledge, and Infinity together, and finds its own depths answering to each.

balanced.text:
Recognition of Brahman simultaneously as Truth, Knowledge, Infinity. Triadic realization.

Somatic: Body recognized as participant in Truth-Knowledge-Infinity.

Emotional: Emotional immersion in triadic Brahman.

Cognitive: Mind grasps the triadic nature of ultimate reality.

Psychic: The soul contemplates Brahman as Truth, Knowledge, and Infinity together, and finds its own depths answering to each.
balanced.vignette: Meditating on Brahman as Truth-Knowledge-Infinity, Meera has come to sense the real as something boundless, aware, and trustworthy—and it steadies how she meets an uncertain world. The formula has become a felt orientation: reality, at bottom, is not hostile chaos but a limitless, knowing ground.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Intellectual fixation on the triadic formula without realization.
facets.somatic: Body unchanged by formula-recitation.
facets.emotional: Doctrinal devotion without realization.
facets.cognitive: Mental fixation on three terms without depth.
facets.psychic: The soul treats the formula as an object of analysis, parsing the three terms while missing the boundless reality they name.

excess.text:
Intellectual fixation on the triadic formula without realization.

Somatic: Body unchanged by formula-recitation.

Emotional: Doctrinal devotion without realization.

Cognitive: Mental fixation on three terms without depth.

Psychic: The soul treats the formula as an object of analysis, parsing the three terms while missing the boundless reality they name.
excess.vignette: Ravi has turned ‘Truth-Knowledge-Infinity’ into an intellectual system he can debate for hours, defending the definition, refining the concept, and living no differently for any of it. The infinite has become a finite argument he wins, the meditation a doctrine he owns rather than a reality he tastes.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Truth, Knowledge, Infinity unrelated to one’s daily reality—lived experience disconnected from these.
facets.somatic: Body in untruth, ignorance, finitude.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without contact with these dimensions.
facets.cognitive: Mind unaware that Brahman is Truth-Knowledge-Infinity.
facets.psychic: The soul lives as if bounded, false, and inert — the very opposite of its nature — having never contemplated what it truly is.

deficient.text:
Truth, Knowledge, Infinity unrelated to one’s daily reality—lived experience disconnected from these.

Somatic: Body in untruth, ignorance, finitude.

Emotional: Emotional life without contact with these dimensions.

Cognitive: Mind unaware that Brahman is Truth-Knowledge-Infinity.

Psychic: The soul lives as if bounded, false, and inert — the very opposite of its nature — having never contemplated what it truly is.
deficient.vignette: For Dan reality is finite, dumb, and indifferent—matter and chance, no truth beneath the facts, no awareness at its core. The contemplation of a boundless, knowing ground is, to him, wishful poetry, and so he meets the world as a closed and meaningless machine, with nothing infinite to lean on.

attributes.title: Bhūma Vidyā — Meditation on the Infinite
attributes.aspect: Bhūma Vidyā is the meditation on the unconditioned Infinite Plenum. Its core recognition, given by Sanatkumāra to a Nārada already vast in learning yet still sorrowful, is devastatingly simple: in the finite there is no happiness; only in the Infinite — where one sees nothing else, hears nothing else, knows nothing else — is there happiness. The knower of this vidyā becomes svarāt, self-ruled, dependent on nothing. The tradition holds it as the summit of all Upanishadic enquiry.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a vidyā · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Infinite · Causal status: Means (meditation on the Infinite as alone joy) · Key relation: A highest Advaitic meditation; object = Bhūma (the Infinite Plenum, Chāndogya).
coordinate.causal_status: Means (meditation on the Infinite as alone joy)
coordinate.key_relation: A highest Advaitic meditation; object = Bhūma (the Infinite Plenum, Chāndogya).

balanced.label: Balanced — the Infinite recognized as the only satisfaction

facets.overview: Recognition that all finite pursuits leave one wanting; only the Infinite satisfies. Orientation toward Bhuma transforms life.
facets.somatic: Body engaged with finite world while consciousness rests in Infinite.
facets.emotional: Emotional understanding that joy requires the Infinite, finite engagements appropriately contextualized.
facets.cognitive: Mind oriented toward Bhuma as the only source of lasting happiness.
facets.psychic: The soul tastes the Infinite (bhūmā) in which alone there is happiness; resting in the boundless, it stops seeking fullness in the finite.

balanced.text:
Recognition that all finite pursuits leave one wanting; only the Infinite satisfies. Orientation toward Bhuma transforms life.

Somatic: Body engaged with finite world while consciousness rests in Infinite.

Emotional: Emotional understanding that joy requires the Infinite, finite engagements appropriately contextualized.

Cognitive: Mind oriented toward Bhuma as the only source of lasting happiness.

Psychic: The soul tastes the Infinite (bhūmā) in which alone there is happiness; resting in the boundless, it stops seeking fullness in the finite.
balanced.vignette: Chasing satisfactions for decades, Amara finally understood in her body what the Bhūma Vidyā teaches: that only the infinite satisfies, and no finite thing ever will. It did not make her bitter; it made her free—she enjoys the small pleasures without demanding they be more than they are, her deep hunger pointed, at last, in the right direction.

excess.label: In excess — world-rejection, escapist neglect of the finite

facets.overview: Rejection of all finite engagement as ‘mortal’—escapist neglect of legitimate responsibilities.
facets.somatic: Body neglected in the name of Bhuma.
facets.emotional: Emotional escape from finite responsibilities.
facets.cognitive: Premature claim of having reached Bhuma.
facets.psychic: The contemplation of vastness becomes a flight from life; the soul uses ‘the Infinite’ to bypass the finite duties through which it must actually grow.

excess.text:
Rejection of all finite engagement as ‘mortal’—escapist neglect of legitimate responsibilities.

Somatic: Body neglected in the name of Bhuma.

Emotional: Emotional escape from finite responsibilities.

Cognitive: Premature claim of having reached Bhuma.

Psychic: The contemplation of vastness becomes a flight from life; the soul uses ‘the Infinite’ to bypass the finite duties through which it must actually grow.
excess.vignette: Ravi took ‘only the infinite satisfies’ as permission to despise the finite, and now rejects ordinary joys—food, friendship, a sunset—as beneath a seeker of the Infinite. The teaching meant to reorient his hunger has become a world-rejecting escapism, and he is starving amid a feast he refuses to touch.

deficient.label: Deficient — the endless, never-satisfied chase of finite pleasures

facets.overview: Endless pursuit of finite happinesses—addictions, accumulation. The Infinite never recognized as the true source.
facets.somatic: Body in compulsive finite pursuit.
facets.emotional: Emotional addiction to finite satisfactions.
facets.cognitive: Mind never opens to Infinite dimension.
facets.psychic: The soul knows only the small and finite; never having tasted the boundless, it chases one limited happiness after another, never satisfied.

deficient.text:
Endless pursuit of finite happinesses—addictions, accumulation. The Infinite never recognized as the true source.

Somatic: Body in compulsive finite pursuit.

Emotional: Emotional addiction to finite satisfactions.

Cognitive: Mind never opens to Infinite dimension.

Psychic: The soul knows only the small and finite; never having tasted the boundless, it chases one limited happiness after another, never satisfied.
deficient.vignette: Dan is still certain the next acquisition will do it—the next raise, the next purchase, the next win—and each finite prize, reached, evaporates into the hunger for the next. The recognition that no finite thing can ever satisfy has never landed, and so he runs the endless, exhausting chase, forever almost-full.

attributes.title: Sandilya Vidya (Heart-Cave Meditation)
attributes.aspect: All this is Brahman. Tranquil, one should worship it as that from which all comes, into which all dissolves, in which all lives. The Self in the heart is Brahman; into Him I enter on departing. Meditation on Self in the heart-cave conceiving it as simultaneously smaller than an atom and bigger than the universe. Contains all karmas, desires, mind, life, smells, tastes. Unspeaking and unconcerned. Suited to both Saguna and Nirguna meditation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital (the heart) · Ontic mode: Practice — a vidyā · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the indwelling Self · Causal status: Means (meditation on Brahman in the heart) · Key relation: A heart-centered meditation; object = the Self in the heart-cave (Śāṇḍilya).
coordinate.causal_status: Means (meditation on Brahman in the heart)
coordinate.key_relation: A heart-centered meditation; object = the Self in the heart-cave (Śāṇḍilya).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Heart meditation on the paradoxical Self—simultaneously smallest and largest. Tranquil contemplation in the heart-cave.
facets.somatic: Body settled in heart-meditation posture.
facets.emotional: Emotional centering in the heart.
facets.cognitive: Mind absorbed in heart’s paradoxical infinity.
facets.psychic: The soul holds ‘all this is Brahman’ as a living conviction, meeting each moment as the One and resting in the heart’s tranquil Self.

balanced.text:
Heart meditation on the paradoxical Self—simultaneously smallest and largest. Tranquil contemplation in the heart-cave.

Somatic: Body settled in heart-meditation posture.

Emotional: Emotional centering in the heart.

Cognitive: Mind absorbed in heart’s paradoxical infinity.

Psychic: The soul holds ‘all this is Brahman’ as a living conviction, meeting each moment as the One and resting in the heart’s tranquil Self.
balanced.vignette: Each morning Priya rests attention in the space of the heart, contemplating the Self that dwells there—luminous, small as a grain, vast as the sky. Over years it has become a stable inner home she can return to, and she carries a little of its steadiness into the noise of her day.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mechanical visualization without depth, grandiose claims about heart-realization.
facets.somatic: Forced heart-focus without genuine settling.
facets.emotional: Sentimentality without depth.
facets.cognitive: Conceptualization without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul turns the great identity into a slogan, asserting ‘all is Brahman’ while still gripped by preference and fear.

excess.text:
Mechanical visualization without depth, grandiose claims about heart-realization.

Somatic: Forced heart-focus without genuine settling.

Emotional: Sentimentality without depth.

Cognitive: Conceptualization without realization.

Psychic: The soul turns the great identity into a slogan, asserting ‘all is Brahman’ while still gripped by preference and fear.
excess.vignette: Ben has grown so attached to the blissful heart-states of his meditation that he now retreats into them from every difficulty, using the inner cave as a hideout from an outer life he would rather not face. The heart-meditation meant to root him has become a place to escape to.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No heart-practice—mind-dominated spirituality missing the heart-center entirely.
facets.somatic: Body without heart-awareness.
facets.emotional: Emotional heart unrecognized.
facets.cognitive: Pure mental approach missing the heart.
facets.psychic: The soul sees only multiplicity and conflict, with no felt sense that one Reality underlies the many.

deficient.text:
No heart-practice—mind-dominated spirituality missing the heart-center entirely.

Somatic: Body without heart-awareness.

Emotional: Emotional heart unrecognized.

Cognitive: Pure mental approach missing the heart.

Psychic: The soul sees only multiplicity and conflict, with no felt sense that one Reality underlies the many.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no felt inner refuge at all. His identity is scattered entirely outward—into roles, opinions, others’ reactions—with no still center in the heart to come home to. The idea of a luminous Self dwelling in the heart-cave is, for him, an empty room he has never entered.

attributes.title: Akshi Vidya (Eye-Self Meditation)
attributes.aspect: The Person seen in the eye is the Atman, immortal, fearless. This is Brahman. To this Brahman, even when ghee or water is poured, it goes to its source—the eye Purusha. Meditation on the Purusha (Person) seen reflected in another’s eye as the immortal, fearless Atman/Brahman. Recognition of the Self looking through every eye. Knower attains the Saguna Brahman beyond waking state and accrues all sought after.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Physical (the eye) · Ontic mode: Practice — a vidyā · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the indwelling Self · Causal status: Means (the person seen in the eye as the Self) · Key relation: A heart-centered meditation; object = the being in the eye (Chāndogya).
coordinate.causal_status: Means (the person seen in the eye as the Self)
coordinate.key_relation: A heart-centered meditation; object = the being in the eye (Chāndogya).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of the same Self in every eye that looks. Profound inter-subjective discovery.
facets.somatic: Body’s eyes recognized as windows to one Atman.
facets.emotional: Emotional unity with all beings through shared eye-Purusha.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees the one Self looking through every pair of eyes.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the immortal, fearless Person seen in the eye as its own true Self, deathless behind the looking.

balanced.text:
Recognition of the same Self in every eye that looks. Profound inter-subjective discovery.

Somatic: Body’s eyes recognized as windows to one Atman.

Emotional: Emotional unity with all beings through shared eye-Purusha.

Cognitive: Mind sees the one Self looking through every pair of eyes.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the immortal, fearless Person seen in the eye as its own true Self, deathless behind the looking.
balanced.vignette: Contemplating ‘the person in the eye’—the seer behind the seeing—Kavya has begun to sense herself less as the images and more as the silent witness of them. It has loosened her grip on what she sees; she watches her own life a little more freely, from behind her own eyes.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced staring practices claiming Atman-vision without genuine realization.
facets.somatic: Body strain from forced eye-meditation.
facets.emotional: Emotional self-deception about eye-realization.
facets.cognitive: Mental claim of seeing Self in others’ eyes without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on the symbol of the eye and inner reflections, mistaking the image for the deathless awareness.

excess.text:
Forced staring practices claiming Atman-vision without genuine realization.

Somatic: Body strain from forced eye-meditation.

Emotional: Emotional self-deception about eye-realization.

Cognitive: Mental claim of seeing Self in others’ eyes without realization.

Psychic: The soul fixates on the symbol of the eye and inner reflections, mistaking the image for the deathless awareness.
excess.vignette: Ravi has turned the eye-meditation into an obsession with subtle inner perceptions, forever peering for the ‘person in the eye,’ straining after occult sights. He has literalized a pointer to pure witnessing into a search for phenomena, and the straining defeats the very seeing it seeks.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Eyes seen only as biological organs, no recognition of the Purusha looking through them.
facets.somatic: Eyes used mechanically, no sacred dimension.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without eye-meeting recognition.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees only individual eyes, no shared Self.
facets.psychic: The soul identifies wholly with the mortal looker, never suspecting the fearless one who sees through every glance.

deficient.text:
Eyes seen only as biological organs, no recognition of the Purusha looking through them.

Somatic: Eyes used mechanically, no sacred dimension.

Emotional: Emotional life without eye-meeting recognition.

Cognitive: Mind sees only individual eyes, no shared Self.

Psychic: The soul identifies wholly with the mortal looker, never suspecting the fearless one who sees through every glance.
deficient.vignette: Dan is completely identified with what he sees—the object grabs him, the image is him, and the seer behind the seeing has never once been suspected. The contemplation of a witness in the eye is not doubted but simply absent; he is all seen and no seer.

attributes.title: Bhrigu-Varuni Vidya (Progressive Inquiry to Ananda)
attributes.aspect: Bhrigu progressively realizes Brahman as: food, prana, mind, intellect, bliss. ‘That from which beings are born, by which they live, into which they return—desire to know That.’ Final realization: Ananda is Brahman. Aims at the innermost seat of Bliss, on a small part of which other creatures are living. Progressive penetration through five sheaths via tapas (focused practice). Each provisional identification surpassed until ananda (bliss-sheath) is reached.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a vidyā (a progressive inquiry) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, ascending to bliss · Causal status: Means (inquiry ascending through the sheaths to Ānanda) · Key relation: A heart-centered meditation; a ladder through the kośas (Dim 5) to Bliss (Taittirīya).
coordinate.causal_status: Means (inquiry ascending through the sheaths to Ānanda)
coordinate.key_relation: A heart-centered meditation; a ladder through the kośas (Dim 5) to Bliss (Taittirīya).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Systematic penetration through layers until reaching Ananda as innermost seat. Each level recognized provisionally.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as outermost sheath in progressive inquiry.
facets.emotional: Emotional layers progressively transcended toward ananda.
facets.cognitive: Mind systematically penetrates from gross to subtle to bliss.
facets.psychic: The soul, like Bhṛgu, climbs from food to breath to mind to bliss, discovering Brahman at ever-subtler depths until it rests in ānanda.

balanced.text:
Systematic penetration through layers until reaching Ananda as innermost seat. Each level recognized provisionally.

Somatic: Body recognized as outermost sheath in progressive inquiry.

Emotional: Emotional layers progressively transcended toward ananda.

Cognitive: Mind systematically penetrates from gross to subtle to bliss.

Psychic: The soul, like Bhṛgu, climbs from food to breath to mind to bliss, discovering Brahman at ever-subtler depths until it rests in ānanda.
balanced.vignette: Like Bhrigu questioning his father, Meera has inquired her way inward—past body, breath, mind, understanding—and touched, in the depths, an unaccountable bliss beneath it all. The progressive inquiry has become her practice, and each layer she sees through leaves her lighter and closer to the joy at the core.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mechanical going-through levels without genuine tapas.
facets.somatic: Body without genuine practice.
facets.emotional: Mechanical emotional ‘levels’ without depth.
facets.cognitive: Conceptual progression without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul stalls at an intermediate level — taking food, or breath, or mind as the final answer — and stops the ascent too soon.

excess.text:
Mechanical going-through levels without genuine tapas.

Somatic: Body without genuine practice.

Emotional: Mechanical emotional ‘levels’ without depth.

Cognitive: Conceptual progression without realization.

Psychic: The soul stalls at an intermediate level — taking food, or breath, or mind as the final answer — and stops the ascent too soon.
excess.vignette: Ravi treats the inquiry as a bliss-mining operation, pushing for the ananda at the bottom, frustrated when a sit doesn’t deliver it, addicted to the depths. The progressive inquiry meant to reveal his nature has become a grasping descent after a high, and the grasping seals the door.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No inquiry into Brahman—accepting surface reality without question.
facets.somatic: Body in mechanical living without inquiry.
facets.emotional: Emotional acceptance of surface life.
facets.cognitive: Mind without inquiry into fundamental questions.
facets.psychic: The soul never inquires inward at all, taking the outermost sheath as the whole of itself.

deficient.text:
No inquiry into Brahman—accepting surface reality without question.

Somatic: Body in mechanical living without inquiry.

Emotional: Emotional acceptance of surface life.

Cognitive: Mind without inquiry into fundamental questions.

Psychic: The soul never inquires inward at all, taking the outermost sheath as the whole of itself.
deficient.vignette: Dan has never inquired past the surface of himself. That there might be layers—vital, mental, blissful—nested within, culminating in a causeless joy, is a map of a territory he has never entered. He is his outermost sheath and no more, and the depths remain unvisited.

attributes.title: Ushasta-Kahola Vidya (Self as Witness in Senses)
attributes.aspect: The Self is the inner controller, the witness, that which sees through every act of seeing, hears through every act of hearing. It cannot be made an object. Meditation on the Self as the unwitnessable witness—seeing through every act of perception, present in every sense-engagement, but never itself becoming an object.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a vidyā · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the inner witness · Causal status: Means (the Self as the witness within all functions) · Key relation: A heart-centered meditation; object = the immediate inner Self / witness (Bṛhadāraṇyaka).
coordinate.causal_status: Means (the Self as the witness within all functions)
coordinate.key_relation: A heart-centered meditation; object = the immediate inner Self / witness (Bṛhadāraṇyaka).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of the constant witness in all sensory experience. The Self is what perceives, never the perceived.
facets.somatic: Body’s perceptions all witnessed by the same unmoved Seer.
facets.emotional: Emotional witnessing without identification.
facets.cognitive: Mind recognizes that the witness can never become the witnessed.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the witness that sees through every sense and cannot itself be seen — the inner controller that is its own Self.

balanced.text:
Recognition of the constant witness in all sensory experience. The Self is what perceives, never the perceived.

Somatic: Body’s perceptions all witnessed by the same unmoved Seer.

Emotional: Emotional witnessing without identification.

Cognitive: Mind recognizes that the witness can never become the witnessed.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the witness that sees through every sense and cannot itself be seen — the inner controller that is its own Self.
balanced.vignette: Contemplating the Self as the witness within all the senses—the hearer of hearing, the thinker of thought—Ravi has come to rest, faintly, as that unchanging knower. It has quieted his identification with each passing perception; he is, increasingly, the still awareness in which the senses do their work.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced ‘witnessing’ technique creating split between subject and object.
facets.somatic: Body in dissociated witnessing-mode.
facets.emotional: Forced emotional detachment.
facets.cognitive: Mental strain in forced witnessing.
facets.psychic: The soul turns ‘I am the witness’ into a detached pose, watching itself perform detachment.

excess.text:
Forced ‘witnessing’ technique creating split between subject and object.

Somatic: Body in dissociated witnessing-mode.

Emotional: Forced emotional detachment.

Cognitive: Mental strain in forced witnessing.

Psychic: The soul turns ‘I am the witness’ into a detached pose, watching itself perform detachment.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned ‘you cannot see the seer of seeing’ into a party trick of paradoxes, delighting in the conceptual puzzle, debating the witness he has never once actually rested as. The pointer to the deepest subject has become an object he plays with, cleverly, at arm’s length.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete identification with sense-perceptions—‘I see, I hear’ without witness-recognition.
facets.somatic: Senses fully identified with as self.
facets.emotional: Emotional life entirely identified with content.
facets.cognitive: Mind absorbed in objects without witness-recognition.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly absorbed in what is sensed, never noticing the silent seer behind all seeing.

deficient.text:
Complete identification with sense-perceptions—‘I see, I hear’ without witness-recognition.

Somatic: Senses fully identified with as self.

Emotional: Emotional life entirely identified with content.

Cognitive: Mind absorbed in objects without witness-recognition.

Psychic: The soul is wholly absorbed in what is sensed, never noticing the silent seer behind all seeing.
deficient.vignette: For Dan there is only the seen, never the seer; only the thought, never the thinker of it. He is entirely absorbed in the contents of his senses, and the witnessing Self that knows them all has never registered as even a possibility. There is no one home behind the eyes, as far as he can tell.

attributes.title: Dahara Vidyā — The Small Space in the Heart
attributes.aspect: Dahara Vidyā is the meditation on the daharākāśa, the small space within the lotus of the heart — a space the Chāndogya declares to be as vast within as the whole external cosmos, containing earth and sky, sun and stars, and all that one has and has not yet attained. To dwell upon this inner space is to discover that what one had taken to be a tiny interior is, in truth, the dwelling-place of the infinite Self. The heart becomes not a private chamber but the doorway to everything.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital (the heart) · Ontic mode: Practice — a vidyā · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the infinite-in-the-heart · Causal status: Means (the tiny space in the heart holding the infinite) · Key relation: A heart-centered meditation; object = the dahara-ākāśa (small space) in the heart (Chāndogya).
coordinate.causal_status: Means (the tiny space in the heart holding the infinite)
coordinate.key_relation: A heart-centered meditation; object = the dahara-ākāśa (small space) in the heart (Chāndogya).

balanced.label: Balanced — the heart as a stable, spacious inner home

facets.overview: Heart-space meditation reveals infinity within—the heart-cave contains all worlds. Genuine inner-universe identification.
facets.somatic: Body becomes vessel to access inner space, heart-center activated.
facets.emotional: Emotional discovery of vast inner space, deep contentment.
facets.cognitive: Mind enters heart-space and discovers infinity within.
facets.psychic: The soul discovers the vast space within the ‘small’ cave of the heart, where the whole cosmos is found within; outer seeking turns inward and home.

balanced.text:
Heart-space meditation reveals infinity within—the heart-cave contains all worlds. Genuine inner-universe identification.

Somatic: Body becomes vessel to access inner space, heart-center activated.

Emotional: Emotional discovery of vast inner space, deep contentment.

Cognitive: Mind enters heart-space and discovers infinity within.

Psychic: The soul discovers the vast space within the ‘small’ cave of the heart, where the whole cosmos is found within; outer seeking turns inward and home.
balanced.vignette: In the small space within the heart, Amara has found something impossibly large—an inner vastness that, contemplated daily, has become a sanctuary she carries everywhere. However cramped the outer day, she can drop into that spacious inner room, and it holds her.

excess.label: In excess — emotional inwardness that withdraws from the world

facets.overview: Forced visualization claiming heart-space discovery without realization.
facets.somatic: Body strained in forced visualization.
facets.emotional: Sentimental imagination without discovery.
facets.cognitive: Concept-based visualization without realization.
facets.psychic: Absorption in inner space becomes withdrawal; the soul retreats into the heart-cave and abandons its life in the world.

excess.text:
Forced visualization claiming heart-space discovery without realization.

Somatic: Body strained in forced visualization.

Emotional: Sentimental imagination without discovery.

Cognitive: Concept-based visualization without realization.

Psychic: Absorption in inner space becomes withdrawal; the soul retreats into the heart-cave and abandons its life in the world.
excess.vignette: Ben’s inwardness has become a withdrawal. He loves the vast inner space of the Dahara meditation so much that he has retreated into it from the world, neglecting the relationships and duties that feel small beside it. The inner sanctuary has become an exit, and he lives more and more within it and less and less among people.

deficient.label: Deficient — no felt inner refuge; identity scattered entirely outward

facets.overview: No awareness of inner space—heart-cave unknown, all attention turned outward.
facets.somatic: Body never settled enough to discover inner space.
facets.emotional: Emotional heart unrecognized.
facets.cognitive: Mind constantly outward, missing infinite within.
facets.psychic: The heart-cave stays unknown; with all attention turned outward, the soul never suspects that what it seeks abroad waits within.

deficient.text:
No awareness of inner space—heart-cave unknown, all attention turned outward.

Somatic: Body never settled enough to discover inner space.

Emotional: Emotional heart unrecognized.

Cognitive: Mind constantly outward, missing infinite within.

Psychic: The heart-cave stays unknown; with all attention turned outward, the soul never suspects that what it seeks abroad waits within.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no felt inner refuge; his whole identity is flung outward into the world’s demands, with no small inner space to retreat to. The teaching of a vast sanctuary within the heart is, for him, an address that does not exist, and so he faces every storm entirely exposed.

attributes.title: Vaisvanara Vidya (Universal Self/Cosmic Body Meditation)
attributes.aspect: Each seeker had partial conception (heaven, sun, air, ether, water, earth). King teaches: the whole cosmos is the Universal Self. Mouth=heaven, eyes=sun, breath=air, body=ether, bladder=water, feet=earth. Meditation on cosmic Self as one’s expanded body. To meditate on a part is to miss the whole. The realization effected is proportional to the extensiveness or inclusiveness of conception. Body extended to cosmic dimensions in awareness.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Physical (the cosmic body) · Ontic mode: Practice — a vidyā · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the cosmic person (Ādhidaivika) · Causal status: Means (the Self as the universal, cosmic person) · Key relation: A cosmic-identity meditation; object = Vaiśvānara; same as Vaishvanara Vidya (Dim 4).
coordinate.causal_status: Means (the Self as the universal, cosmic person)
coordinate.key_relation: A cosmic-identity meditation; object = Vaiśvānara; same as Vaishvanara Vidya (Dim 4).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Body experienced as microcosm of universe; universe as macrocosm of body. Cosmic Self realized.
facets.somatic: Body felt as universal—heaven in mouth, sun in eyes, air in breath.
facets.emotional: Emotional connection with cosmos through bodily meditation.
facets.cognitive: Mind expanded to cosmic dimensions through Vaisvanara meditation.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the whole cosmos as the body of one Self and its private ‘I’ as a limb of that vastness; the universe is no longer merely ‘out there.’

balanced.text:
Body experienced as microcosm of universe; universe as macrocosm of body. Cosmic Self realized.

Somatic: Body felt as universal—heaven in mouth, sun in eyes, air in breath.

Emotional: Emotional connection with cosmos through bodily meditation.

Cognitive: Mind expanded to cosmic dimensions through Vaisvanara meditation.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the whole cosmos as the body of one Self and its private ‘I’ as a limb of that vastness; the universe is no longer merely ‘out there.’
balanced.vignette: Meditating on the Self as the cosmic body—the sun its eye, the wind its breath—Sunita has felt, at moments, the boundary of her small self grow porous, and returns to her days gentler and larger. The cosmic identity has quietly widened her, without unmooring her from the person she still has to be.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Inflated ‘I am the universe’ in megalomanic sense.
facets.somatic: Body claimed as cosmic in ego-mode.
facets.emotional: Grandiose inflation.
facets.cognitive: Egotic appropriation of cosmic identity.
facets.psychic: The soul inflates, claiming cosmic identity without humility; grandiosity masquerades as realization while the ego quietly persists.

excess.text:
Inflated ‘I am the universe’ in megalomanic sense.

Somatic: Body claimed as cosmic in ego-mode.

Emotional: Grandiose inflation.

Cognitive: Egotic appropriation of cosmic identity.

Psychic: The soul inflates, claiming cosmic identity without humility; grandiosity masquerades as realization while the ego quietly persists.
excess.vignette: After a strong experience of cosmic identity, Karl came home convinced he IS the universe—and now treats other people as figments of his cosmic mind, above the rules that bind lesser beings. A genuine expansion has inflated into simple megalomania; the universal Self has become the biggest small ego in the room.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Body taken as isolated, finite. No cosmic dimension recognized.
facets.somatic: Body as small, isolated, finite.
facets.emotional: Emotional isolation from cosmos.
facets.cognitive: Mind confined to individual body-identity.
facets.psychic: The soul stays trapped in the partial, worshipping each faculty or region piecemeal, never glimpsing the single whole.

deficient.text:
Body taken as isolated, finite. No cosmic dimension recognized.

Somatic: Body as small, isolated, finite.

Emotional: Emotional isolation from cosmos.

Cognitive: Mind confined to individual body-identity.

Psychic: The soul stays trapped in the partial, worshipping each faculty or region piecemeal, never glimpsing the single whole.
deficient.vignette: For Raj the self ends firmly at his skin, connected to the vast cosmos by exactly nothing. The meditation on a universal, cosmic Self strikes him as nonsense; he has never once felt past his own boundary, and lives his whole life in a single sealed room, unaware of the horizon.

attributes.title: Upakosala Vidya (Three Fires Teaching)
attributes.aspect: Prana is Brahman. Happiness is Brahman. Ether is Brahman. Happiness and Ether are one. The Garhapatya, Dakshinagni, and Ahavaniya fires teach. The three sacred fires instruct: they are Purushas in sun, moon, lightning. Collective meditation on these leads through Devayana (path of gods). The Centre of Joy reached through subjective (not objective) conception of these elemental Purushas.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital · Ontic mode: Practice — a vidyā · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the cosmic · Causal status: Means (the sacred fires teach the Self) · Key relation: A cosmic-identity meditation; object = the three fires as revealers of Brahman (Chāndogya).
coordinate.causal_status: Means (the sacred fires teach the Self)
coordinate.key_relation: A cosmic-identity meditation; object = the three fires as revealers of Brahman (Chāndogya).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of cosmic fires as inner forces. Devayana path opened through this practice.
facets.somatic: Body resonates with sacred-fire equivalents within.
facets.emotional: Emotional opening to Devayana path through Centre of Joy.
facets.cognitive: Mind recognizes elemental Purushas as subjective realities.
facets.psychic: The soul learns to find Brahman in the immediate — in breath, in joy, in space itself — so the sacred is met in ordinary life, not only in retreat.

balanced.text:
Recognition of cosmic fires as inner forces. Devayana path opened through this practice.

Somatic: Body resonates with sacred-fire equivalents within.

Emotional: Emotional opening to Devayana path through Centre of Joy.

Cognitive: Mind recognizes elemental Purushas as subjective realities.

Psychic: The soul learns to find Brahman in the immediate — in breath, in joy, in space itself — so the sacred is met in ordinary life, not only in retreat.
balanced.vignette: Like Upakosala tended by the fires, Ravi has learned to let his very circumstances teach him—the hardship, the waiting, the ordinary duties all becoming instructors in disguise. The meditation has changed how he meets his life: nothing is merely an obstacle; everything, rightly seen, is a fire that instructs.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mythologization without inner realization, ritualism around fires.
facets.somatic: Body in mechanical fire-rituals.
facets.emotional: Emotional dependence on ritual external fire.
facets.cognitive: Mental fixation on cosmographic details.
facets.psychic: The soul clings to blissful states as the goal, mistaking pleasant experience for the formless ground beneath it.

excess.text:
Mythologization without inner realization, ritualism around fires.

Somatic: Body in mechanical fire-rituals.

Emotional: Emotional dependence on ritual external fire.

Cognitive: Mental fixation on cosmographic details.

Psychic: The soul clings to blissful states as the goal, mistaking pleasant experience for the formless ground beneath it.
excess.vignette: Sam sees mystical teachings in everything, reading cosmic messages into every coincidence, certain the fires of the universe are speaking to him personally and constantly. The teaching that circumstances can instruct has swollen into a grandiose apophenia, and he is lost in a private cosmos of signs.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Cosmic and inner correspondences unknown, missing the path of devas.
facets.somatic: Body unaware of inner sacred fires.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without sacred-fire dimension.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees only material fires, no spiritual significance.
facets.psychic: The soul finds the sacred nowhere in daily life; breath, joy, and space remain mere facts, never doorways.

deficient.text:
Cosmic and inner correspondences unknown, missing the path of devas.

Somatic: Body unaware of inner sacred fires.

Emotional: Emotional life without sacred-fire dimension.

Cognitive: Mind sees only material fires, no spiritual significance.

Psychic: The soul finds the sacred nowhere in daily life; breath, joy, and space remain mere facts, never doorways.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s circumstances are just circumstances—the hardship a nuisance, the duty a chore, none of it carrying any meaning or teaching. The possibility that his very life might be instructing him has never occurred to him, and so he passes through his lessons learning none of them.

attributes.title: Antaraditya Vidya (Sun-Within Meditation)
attributes.aspect: The Purusha within the sun and the Purusha within the eye are the same. The cosmic representation of inner truth. Counterpart of Akshi Vidya. Cosmic representation through the same insight. Meditation on the Purusha within the Sun as identical with the Purusha within the eye. Comprehensive identification of subjective and objective.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a vidyā · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the cosmic (Ādhidaivika) · Causal status: Means (the golden person within the sun as the Self) · Key relation: A cosmic-identity meditation; object = the being within the sun; kin to Sūrya (Dim 18).
coordinate.causal_status: Means (the golden person within the sun as the Self)
coordinate.key_relation: A cosmic-identity meditation; object = the being within the sun; kin to Sūrya (Dim 18).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of the same Purusha in cosmic Sun and personal eye. The cosmic-individual identity realized.
facets.somatic: Body’s eye-Purusha known as same as cosmic Sun-Purusha.
facets.emotional: Emotional vast-identity recognition.
facets.cognitive: Mind recognizes the one Purusha in both microcosm and macrocosm.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the same light in the sun without and the seeing within; outer and inner are bridged in one Person.

balanced.text:
Recognition of the same Purusha in cosmic Sun and personal eye. The cosmic-individual identity realized.

Somatic: Body’s eye-Purusha known as same as cosmic Sun-Purusha.

Emotional: Emotional vast-identity recognition.

Cognitive: Mind recognizes the one Purusha in both microcosm and macrocosm.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the same light in the sun without and the seeing within; outer and inner are bridged in one Person.
balanced.vignette: Contemplating the golden person within the sun—and within his own eye—Ravi has come to sense a single radiance behind the outer light and the inner seer. The meditation has joined his inner and outer worlds; the light out there and the awareness in here feel, increasingly, like one thing.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Cosmic identification without genuine inner realization.
facets.somatic: Body in ritualistic Sun-worship without realization.
facets.emotional: Inflated cosmic claims.
facets.cognitive: Conceptual cosmic-self without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on inner light-experiences, chasing the symbol of the sun rather than the awareness it points to.

excess.text:
Cosmic identification without genuine inner realization.

Somatic: Body in ritualistic Sun-worship without realization.

Emotional: Inflated cosmic claims.

Cognitive: Conceptual cosmic-self without realization.

Psychic: The soul fixates on inner light-experiences, chasing the symbol of the sun rather than the awareness it points to.
excess.vignette: Karl has literalized the ‘sun within’ into sun-gazing and elaborate light-visualizations, chasing luminous phenomena and neglecting the awareness the imagery was meant to point to. He has mistaken the golden picture for the gold, and the meditation has become a pursuit of inner spectacle.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Sun seen only as physical body, no recognition of cosmic Purusha.
facets.somatic: Body’s relationship to Sun purely physical.
facets.emotional: No cosmic-inner emotional connection.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees only material sun, no Purusha.
facets.psychic: The soul sees only the outer sun and the outer eye, never suspecting the single Person who looks through both.

deficient.text:
Sun seen only as physical body, no recognition of cosmic Purusha.

Somatic: Body’s relationship to Sun purely physical.

Emotional: No cosmic-inner emotional connection.

Cognitive: Mind sees only material sun, no Purusha.

Psychic: The soul sees only the outer sun and the outer eye, never suspecting the single Person who looks through both.
deficient.vignette: For Dan the sun is a ball of gas and his eye a camera, and the notion of a single radiant Self within both is empty metaphor. The contemplation joining outer light and inner seer finds no purchase in him; he sees, and is not moved to wonder what sees.

attributes.title: Aditya Vidya (Sun as Liberator)
attributes.aspect: One who realises the Aditya-Purusha through this Vidya goes above sin and reaches Saguna Brahman. Specific meditation on the Sun as Aditya-Purusha—the personal Purusha within the solar disc. Realization through this Vidya: one transcends sin and reaches Saguna Brahman.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a vidyā · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the cosmic (Ādhidaivika) · Causal status: Means (the sun as the door to liberation) · Key relation: A cosmic-identity meditation; object = Āditya (the sun) as liberator; kin to Sūrya (Dim 18).
coordinate.causal_status: Means (the sun as the door to liberation)
coordinate.key_relation: A cosmic-identity meditation; object = Āditya (the sun) as liberator; kin to Sūrya (Dim 18).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Sun realized as Aditya-Purusha—transcending sin through this realization.
facets.somatic: Body experiences solar Purusha presence.
facets.emotional: Emotional release from sin-burden through Aditya recognition.
facets.cognitive: Mind transcends sin-consciousness through Aditya-Vidya.
facets.psychic: The soul takes the sun as emblem of the self-luminous Self and is carried, through that contemplation, above the reach of sin and sorrow.

balanced.text:
Sun realized as Aditya-Purusha—transcending sin through this realization.

Somatic: Body experiences solar Purusha presence.

Emotional: Emotional release from sin-burden through Aditya recognition.

Cognitive: Mind transcends sin-consciousness through Aditya-Vidya.

Psychic: The soul takes the sun as emblem of the self-luminous Self and is carried, through that contemplation, above the reach of sin and sorrow.
balanced.vignette: Meera contemplates the sun as a symbol of the liberating Self that dispels the darkness of ignorance, and the daily rising has become, for her, a small remembrance—light returns, the dark is not final, and something in her is meant to shine free. It steadies her mornings and lifts her outlook.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Sun-worship without realization, ritualism without transformation.
facets.somatic: Sun-rituals without inner realization.
facets.emotional: Emotional dependence on external Sun-worship.
facets.cognitive: Mental ritualism around Aditya.
facets.psychic: The soul turns the practice into sun-worship for its own sake, literalizing the symbol and losing the Self it stands for.

excess.text:
Sun-worship without realization, ritualism without transformation.

Somatic: Sun-rituals without inner realization.

Emotional: Emotional dependence on external Sun-worship.

Cognitive: Mental ritualism around Aditya.

Psychic: The soul turns the practice into sun-worship for its own sake, literalizing the symbol and losing the Self it stands for.
excess.vignette: Ravi has turned sun-worship into a superstition, convinced that the ritual itself, performed just so, will magically liberate him regardless of any inner change. The sun as symbol of freedom has hardened into a literal transaction, and he waits for the star to do the work only he can do.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Sin-burden carried without recognition of Aditya as liberator.
facets.somatic: Body weighed by stress of sin-consciousness.
facets.emotional: Emotional guilt without resolution.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t see Aditya as the sin-transcender.
facets.psychic: Without this contemplation the soul has no emblem of transcendence, and its faults feel like a weight with no horizon beyond them.

deficient.text:
Sin-burden carried without recognition of Aditya as liberator.

Somatic: Body weighed by stress of sin-consciousness.

Emotional: Emotional guilt without resolution.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t see Aditya as the sin-transcender.

Psychic: Without this contemplation the soul has no emblem of transcendence, and its faults feel like a weight with no horizon beyond them.
deficient.vignette: Dan finds the whole idea of the sun as a spiritual liberator faintly absurd. There is no symbol in the sunrise for him, no remembrance, no light of freedom—only the physics of a bright morning. The meditation’s luminous pointer to liberation is, for him, simply switched off.

attributes.title: Satyakama Vidya (Four-Fold Brahman)
attributes.aspect: Four nature-teachers each give one quarter of Brahman. The meditator becomes shining, endless, dazzling, with an abode/support. Meditation taught by nature itself: bull, fire, swan, diver-bird each reveal one quarter of Brahman. The Vidya makes one ‘shining, endless, dazzling and with an abode or support.’ Recognition that nature itself is teacher when one is ready.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a vidyā · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the cosmic · Causal status: Means (meditation on Brahman’s four quarters) · Key relation: A cosmic-identity meditation; object = the four-fold Brahman (Chāndogya).
coordinate.key_relation: A cosmic-identity meditation; object = the four-fold Brahman (Chāndogya).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Openness to learn from nature itself. Brahman realized in four-fold cosmic teaching.
facets.somatic: Body becomes radiant (‘shining, dazzling’) through this Vidya.
facets.emotional: Emotional openness to nature as teacher.
facets.cognitive: Mind ready to receive teaching from non-human sources.
facets.psychic: The soul learns from the whole world as teacher — directions, fire, sun, breath each giving a quarter of Brahman — and humility before creation becomes the path.

balanced.text:
Openness to learn from nature itself. Brahman realized in four-fold cosmic teaching.

Somatic: Body becomes radiant (‘shining, dazzling’) through this Vidya.

Emotional: Emotional openness to nature as teacher.

Cognitive: Mind ready to receive teaching from non-human sources.

Psychic: The soul learns from the whole world as teacher — directions, fire, sun, breath each giving a quarter of Brahman — and humility before creation becomes the path.
balanced.vignette: Contemplating Brahman as four-fold—present in the shining quarters of the world, of the senses, of the breath—Kavya has come to see the sacred distributed through the whole of her experience. The meditation has made her days feel less flat: divinity is not elsewhere but quartered through the ordinary world.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Romanticism about nature without genuine receptivity, projecting one’s own ideas onto natural phenomena.
facets.somatic: Body postures of nature-worship without realization.
facets.emotional: Sentimental nature-mysticism.
facets.cognitive: Mental projection onto nature.
facets.psychic: The soul over-systematizes, collecting ‘quarters’ as doctrine while missing the living wholeness they together point to.

excess.text:
Romanticism about nature without genuine receptivity, projecting one’s own ideas onto natural phenomena.

Somatic: Body postures of nature-worship without realization.

Emotional: Sentimental nature-mysticism.

Cognitive: Mental projection onto nature.

Psychic: The soul over-systematizes, collecting ‘quarters’ as doctrine while missing the living wholeness they together point to.
excess.vignette: Ravi has turned the four-fold teaching into an elaborate map he loves to draw—quarters and feet and correspondences—mastering the schema while feeling none of the sacred it was meant to reveal. The living presence has become a diagram he is proud of and unmoved by.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Nature seen as dead matter, no recognition that it can teach Brahman.
facets.somatic: Body separated from nature as teacher.
facets.emotional: Emotional disconnect from natural teaching.
facets.cognitive: Mind dismisses nature as source of wisdom.
facets.psychic: The soul takes the world as mute and meaningless, learning nothing from creation, deaf to the teaching all around it.

deficient.text:
Nature seen as dead matter, no recognition that it can teach Brahman.

Somatic: Body separated from nature as teacher.

Emotional: Emotional disconnect from natural teaching.

Cognitive: Mind dismisses nature as source of wisdom.

Psychic: The soul takes the world as mute and meaningless, learning nothing from creation, deaf to the teaching all around it.
deficient.vignette: For Dan the world has no quarters of the sacred in it—no shining presence in the directions, the senses, the breath—only neutral stuff. The meditation’s claim that Brahman is distributed through his very experience finds nothing in him to land on, and the world stays disenchanted.

attributes.title: Madhu Vidyā — The Honey Doctrine of Cosmic Sweetness
attributes.aspect: Madhu Vidyā, the Honey Doctrine, is the meditation on the radiant mutual interdependence of all things. Just as honey is gathered by bees from countless flowers and becomes a single sweetness in which no individual flower can be distinguished, so each being is the honey of all other beings, and all beings are the honey of each — the sun the honey of all creatures, all creatures the honey of the sun. To dwell upon this is to dissolve the felt boundary between self and world into a single, mutually nourishing whole, luminous and sweet.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital · Ontic mode: Practice — a vidyā · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward cosmic interdependence (Ādhidaivika) · Causal status: Means (all beings as honey for one another) · Key relation: A cosmic-identity meditation; object = the mutual sweetness of all beings (Bṛhadāraṇyaka).
coordinate.causal_status: Means (all beings as honey for one another)
coordinate.key_relation: A cosmic-identity meditation; object = the mutual sweetness of all beings (Bṛhadāraṇyaka).

balanced.label: Balanced — the felt interdependence and sweetness of all life

facets.overview: Recognition of cosmic Sweet—Sun as ever-present radiance. Inner experience: ‘it is always day,’ the Light of essential Soul never sets.
facets.somatic: Body energized by cosmic Sun-honey meditation.
facets.emotional: Emotional sweetness from cosmic interconnection.
facets.cognitive: Mind always in inner daylight, the Light of Self never setting.
facets.psychic: The soul tastes the mutual sweetness (madhu) by which each being is honey to every other; the world is seen as one web of reciprocal nourishment.

balanced.text:
Recognition of cosmic Sweet—Sun as ever-present radiance. Inner experience: ‘it is always day,’ the Light of essential Soul never sets.

Somatic: Body energized by cosmic Sun-honey meditation.

Emotional: Emotional sweetness from cosmic interconnection.

Cognitive: Mind always in inner daylight, the Light of Self never setting.

Psychic: The soul tastes the mutual sweetness (madhu) by which each being is honey to every other; the world is seen as one web of reciprocal nourishment.
balanced.vignette: The Honey Doctrine—that all beings are honey to one another, each the sweetness and the source of every other—has become, for Meera, a felt sense of deep interdependence. She moves through her day aware of the web she is woven into, and it makes her both humbler and more grateful.

excess.label: In excess — boundary-loss, the dissolving of healthy distinctions

facets.overview: Sentimental sweetness denying real darkness in life.
facets.somatic: Forced sweetness in body.
facets.emotional: Saccharine emotionality bypassing reality.
facets.cognitive: Doctrinal sweetness without realization.
facets.psychic: The vision of universal sweetness slides into sentimentality; the soul denies real difficulty under a film of forced positivity.

excess.text:
Sentimental sweetness denying real darkness in life.

Somatic: Forced sweetness in body.

Emotional: Saccharine emotionality bypassing reality.

Cognitive: Doctrinal sweetness without realization.

Psychic: The vision of universal sweetness slides into sentimentality; the soul denies real difficulty under a film of forced positivity.
excess.vignette: Karl waxes lyrical about universal interconnection while treating the actual people in front of him carelessly, in love with the cosmic sweetness and blind to the specific person he just interrupted. The Honey Doctrine has become a beautiful abstraction that excuses him from ordinary, particular kindness.

deficient.label: Deficient — isolation, the self walled off from a world it only uses

facets.overview: Perceiving all as struggle, no cosmic sweetness recognized. Sun sets daily in awareness, never inner day.
facets.somatic: Body only knows physical day-night cycle.
facets.emotional: Emotional darkness in absence of cosmic Sun.
facets.cognitive: Mind unaware of inner daylight that never sets.
facets.psychic: The soul perceives only struggle; with no sweetness recognized, life is a grind of separate parts and meaning seems to set each day like the sun.

deficient.text:
Perceiving all as struggle, no cosmic sweetness recognized. Sun sets daily in awareness, never inner day.

Somatic: Body only knows physical day-night cycle.

Emotional: Emotional darkness in absence of cosmic Sun.

Cognitive: Mind unaware of inner daylight that never sets.

Psychic: The soul perceives only struggle; with no sweetness recognized, life is a grind of separate parts and meaning seems to set each day like the sun.
deficient.vignette: Dan experiences himself as an isolated unit in a world of separate units, connected to nothing, owing nothing, sweet to no one. The teaching that all beings are honey to each other, mutually sustaining, is a lovely idea with no felt reality; he tastes no interdependence, only separateness.

attributes.title: Course After Death (Devayana Path)
attributes.aspect: The Devayana (Path of the Gods) is the after-death trajectory for those who have prepared through practice. Each stage represents progressively subtle dimensions: flame→day→bright fortnight→northern course→year→sun→moon→lightning→Amanava Purusha→Brahmaloka. From this path there is no return.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / doctrine — a vidyā (knowledge of the after-death path) · Valence: Cultivated knowledge · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the cosmic journey (Ādhidaivika) · Causal status: Framework (the path of the gods after death) · Key relation: A cosmic-process meditation; the Devayāna (path of light, no return).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / doctrine — a vidyā (knowledge of the after-death path)
coordinate.causal_status: Framework (the path of the gods after death)
coordinate.key_relation: A cosmic-process meditation; the Devayāna (path of light, no return).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Practice oriented toward Devayana path. Life choices made with after-death trajectory in mind.
facets.somatic: Body purified for after-death journey.
facets.emotional: Emotional preparation for the cosmic ascent.
facets.cognitive: Mind oriented toward liberation-path, not return-path.
facets.psychic: The soul orients its living toward the path of light, so that the whole of life becomes a quiet preparation for the journey beyond.

balanced.text:
Practice oriented toward Devayana path. Life choices made with after-death trajectory in mind.

Somatic: Body purified for after-death journey.

Emotional: Emotional preparation for the cosmic ascent.

Cognitive: Mind oriented toward liberation-path, not return-path.

Psychic: The soul orients its living toward the path of light, so that the whole of life becomes a quiet preparation for the journey beyond.
balanced.vignette: Contemplating the bright path the soul is said to travel after death, Ravi finds his relationship to mortality quietly transformed—less a wall than a doorway, less an ending than a passage. It does not make him morbid; it makes him less afraid, and the diminished fear leaves him freer to live.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Obsessive after-death focus crowding out present-life engagement.
facets.somatic: Body abandoned to claimed after-death focus.
facets.emotional: Emotional disengagement from present life.
facets.cognitive: Mental fixation on death-trajectory.
facets.psychic: The soul becomes morbidly preoccupied with what comes after death, neglecting the life it is actually given to live.

excess.text:
Obsessive after-death focus crowding out present-life engagement.

Somatic: Body abandoned to claimed after-death focus.

Emotional: Emotional disengagement from present life.

Cognitive: Mental fixation on death-trajectory.

Psychic: The soul becomes morbidly preoccupied with what comes after death, neglecting the life it is actually given to live.
excess.vignette: Karl is so focused on securing the right after-death path that he neglects the life in front of him, hoarding merit, obsessing over his post-mortem itinerary, escaping the present into a spiritual future. The teaching about the journey beyond has become an avoidance of the journey he is on.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No preparation for the great transition—life lived as if death is the end. Suitable conditions for higher path missed.
facets.somatic: Body lived purely for now.
facets.emotional: Emotional engagement only with this life.
facets.cognitive: Mind never considers post-death possibilities.
facets.psychic: The soul gives no thought to its larger trajectory, living as if this brief span were the whole of its story.

deficient.text:
No preparation for the great transition—life lived as if death is the end. Suitable conditions for higher path missed.

Somatic: Body lived purely for now.

Emotional: Emotional engagement only with this life.

Cognitive: Mind never considers post-death possibilities.

Psychic: The soul gives no thought to its larger trajectory, living as if this brief span were the whole of its story.
deficient.vignette: For Dan death is a full stop and nothing follows it, so the contemplation of any path beyond is empty superstition. The teaching finds no purchase; he faces his mortality as sheer annihilation, with no bright passage to relativize the dark, and the raw fear of it sits, unexamined, underneath his days.

attributes.title: Svetaketu Vidya (Five-Fire/Devayana Knowledge)
attributes.aspect: Through the experiments (salt in water, banyan seed) and ninefold repetition: ‘That subtle essence which is the Self of all this—That you are’ (Tat Tvam Asi). The teaching that culminates in Tat Tvam Asi realization. Through experiments revealing the subtle essence pervading all. Repeated nine times to drive in the identity of individual Self with universal Self.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice / doctrine — a vidyā · Valence: Cultivated knowledge · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the cosmic cycle (Ādhidaivika) · Causal status: Framework (the five-fire teaching of rebirth and return) · Key relation: A cosmic-process meditation; the five-fire / two-path knowledge; kin to Pañcāgni.
coordinate.causal_status: Framework (the five-fire teaching of rebirth and return)
coordinate.key_relation: A cosmic-process meditation; the five-fire / two-path knowledge; kin to Pañcāgni.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Genuine realization ‘I am That’—the subtle essence as one’s true nature.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as expression of That.
facets.emotional: Emotional unity with all through shared essence.
facets.cognitive: Mind realizes Tat Tvam Asi as direct experience.
facets.psychic: Through homely images — salt dissolved, the seed’s invisible essence — the soul tastes ‘That thou art,’ the subtle Being pervading all.

balanced.text:
Genuine realization ‘I am That’—the subtle essence as one’s true nature.

Somatic: Body recognized as expression of That.

Emotional: Emotional unity with all through shared essence.

Cognitive: Mind realizes Tat Tvam Asi as direct experience.

Psychic: Through homely images — salt dissolved, the seed’s invisible essence — the soul tastes ‘That thou art,’ the subtle Being pervading all.
balanced.vignette: Through the five-fire knowledge, Meera has come to hold the great cycle of birth and death with a strange equanimity—seeing her small life as one turning of a vast wheel. It neither frightens nor deflates her; it situates her, and the wider frame makes her daily troubles feel their true size.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Intellectual claim ‘I am That’ as ego-game.
facets.somatic: Body unchanged despite verbal claim.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity continues despite claim.
facets.cognitive: Mind playing with concept, not realizing.
facets.psychic: The soul clings to the analogies as clever ideas, repeating ‘Tat Tvam Asi’ from the head while the heart stays separate.

excess.text:
Intellectual claim ‘I am That’ as ego-game.

Somatic: Body unchanged despite verbal claim.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity continues despite claim.

Cognitive: Mind playing with concept, not realizing.

Psychic: The soul clings to the analogies as clever ideas, repeating ‘Tat Tvam Asi’ from the head while the heart stays separate.
excess.vignette: Ravi uses ‘it’s all just the cosmic cycle’ to excuse everything—no need to change, to repair, to strive, since the wheel turns regardless. A profound cosmology has become a shrug, and the grand knowledge of the cycle has become his reason to do nothing within it.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete separation between ‘I’ and ‘That’—maximum dualistic suffering.
facets.somatic: Body as alien from cosmos.
facets.emotional: Emotional separation from all.
facets.cognitive: Mind cannot conceive of identity with absolute.
facets.psychic: The soul never receives the teaching; the salt, the seed, the saying all pass by, and ‘I’ and ‘That’ remain strangers.

deficient.text:
Complete separation between ‘I’ and ‘That’—maximum dualistic suffering.

Somatic: Body as alien from cosmos.

Emotional: Emotional separation from all.

Cognitive: Mind cannot conceive of identity with absolute.

Psychic: The soul never receives the teaching; the salt, the seed, the saying all pass by, and ‘I’ and ‘That’ remain strangers.
deficient.vignette: For Dan this life is a sealed, isolated event, and the notion of a great cycle of return—soul to moon to rain to grain to birth—is mythology, nothing more. The knowledge finds nothing in him; he sees the single spark and denies the fire, and the isolation makes death a terror.

attributes.title: Shodasakala Vidya (Sixteen-Part Person)
attributes.aspect: The Purusha has sixteen kalas (parts): prana, faith, space, air, fire, water, earth, senses, mind, food, virility, austerity, mantras, karma, worlds, name. When realized, all sixteen merge into the Self. Meditation recognizing the sixteen parts that constitute the personal Purusha. Each is a temporary manifestation; upon realization all sixteen merge back into the Self. Map of human constitution and dissolution.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a vidyā · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Means (dissolving the sixteen parts into the Self) · Key relation: A cosmic-process meditation; object = the sixteen kalās; same as Sixteen Kalas (Dim 6).
coordinate.causal_status: Means (dissolving the sixteen parts into the Self)
coordinate.key_relation: A cosmic-process meditation; object = the sixteen kalās; same as Sixteen Kalas (Dim 6).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: All sixteen kalas recognized as parts of Self, ultimately merging into pure consciousness.
facets.somatic: Body’s elements seen as kalas of Self.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns recognized as kalas, fluid and temporary.
facets.cognitive: Mind understands its components as parts of greater Self.
facets.psychic: The soul sees how its sixteen parts — breath, faith, elements, senses — all return to and rest in the one Person, and is reassured of its indivisible center.

balanced.text:
All sixteen kalas recognized as parts of Self, ultimately merging into pure consciousness.

Somatic: Body’s elements seen as kalas of Self.

Emotional: Emotional patterns recognized as kalas, fluid and temporary.
