Cognitive: Mind understands its components as parts of greater Self.

Psychic: The soul sees how its sixteen parts — breath, faith, elements, senses — all return to and rest in the one Person, and is reassured of its indivisible center.
balanced.vignette: Contemplating the person as sixteen parts that resolve, in the end, into one Self, Kavya can see her many faculties—breath, senses, mind, name—as facets she will one day set down. It has loosened her grip on each; she uses her parts well and knows, quietly, that she is the one in whom they all cohere.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Fragmented identification with each kala separately—missing the underlying unity.
facets.somatic: Body identified piecemeal.
facets.emotional: Emotional fragmentation.
facets.cognitive: Mind fragmented across the 16 kalas.
facets.psychic: The soul over-analyzes its components, dissecting itself endlessly while losing the felt unity that holds them.

excess.text:
Fragmented identification with each kala separately—missing the underlying unity.

Somatic: Body identified piecemeal.

Emotional: Emotional fragmentation.

Cognitive: Mind fragmented across the 16 kalas.

Psychic: The soul over-analyzes its components, dissecting itself endlessly while losing the felt unity that holds them.
excess.vignette: Sam identifies with each of his sixteen ‘parts’ in turn—now his intellect, now his energy, now his name—fragmenting into a dozen selves and missing the one that unites them. The teaching meant to resolve the many into one has, in his hands, only multiplied the many.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total identification with the kalas as ultimately real—missing the deeper Purusha they manifest from.
facets.somatic: Body taken as ultimate reality.
facets.emotional: Emotional life taken as final reality.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t recognize kalas as temporary manifestations of deeper Self.
facets.psychic: The soul experiences itself as a heap of disconnected parts, with no sense of the Person in whom they cohere.

deficient.text:
Total identification with the kalas as ultimately real—missing the deeper Purusha they manifest from.

Somatic: Body taken as ultimate reality.

Emotional: Emotional life taken as final reality.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t recognize kalas as temporary manifestations of deeper Self.

Psychic: The soul experiences itself as a heap of disconnected parts, with no sense of the Person in whom they cohere.
deficient.vignette: For Dan the parts are simply what he is—his body, his job, his opinions, final and real—with no suspicion of a single Self they all express. The teaching that his sixteen faculties resolve into one awareness is, to him, a curiosity; he takes the facets for the whole jewel.

attributes.title: Pañcāgni Vidyā — The Doctrine of the Five Fires
attributes.aspect: Pañcāgni Vidyā contemplates the five cosmic fires through which the offering of life circulates — the way the libation of faith rises to become the moon, descends as rain, becomes food, becomes seed, and is reborn as a being — an endless round of offering and renewal. Knowing it, the meditator distinguishes the two great courses after death: the path of light, the devayāna, that leads on toward Brahman and does not return, and the path of recurrence that comes back again. To dwell on this is to see one’s life as a single moment in a vast, ordered cosmic process.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice / doctrine — a vidyā · Valence: Cultivated knowledge · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Cosmic (Ādhidaivika), contemplated individually · Causal status: Framework (the sacrificial round of birth and rebirth) · Key relation: A cosmic-process meditation; same as Pancha-Agni Vidya (Dim 6).
coordinate.causal_status: Framework (the sacrificial round of birth and rebirth)
coordinate.key_relation: A cosmic-process meditation; same as Pancha-Agni Vidya (Dim 6).

balanced.label: Balanced — a life held within the larger cosmic rhythm

facets.overview: Recognition of cosmic sacrificial cycle—each death is offering becoming new life. Karma-rebirth understood systematically.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as transient form in great cycle.
facets.emotional: Emotional peace with mortality through understanding continuity.
facets.cognitive: Mind grasps systematic nature of life-death-rebirth.
facets.psychic: The soul grasps the great cycle of the five fires through which it passes, and so meets birth and death as movements within a larger order.

balanced.text:
Recognition of cosmic sacrificial cycle—each death is offering becoming new life. Karma-rebirth understood systematically.

Somatic: Body recognized as transient form in great cycle.

Emotional: Emotional peace with mortality through understanding continuity.

Cognitive: Mind grasps systematic nature of life-death-rebirth.

Psychic: The soul grasps the great cycle of the five fires through which it passes, and so meets birth and death as movements within a larger order.
balanced.vignette: Meera holds the long view of the Five Fires—that death feeds life feeds death in one great sacrifice—and it steadies how she lives: her actions ripple out and return, and this is neither morbid nor frightening but simply the shape of things. The cosmic frame quietly dignifies her small days.

excess.label: In excess — morbid preoccupation with death and the afterlife

facets.overview: Fatalistic ‘everything is just the cycle’ as escape from responsibility.
facets.somatic: Body neglected as ‘just part of cycle.’
facets.emotional: Emotional disengagement claiming cosmic perspective.
facets.cognitive: Doctrinal acceptance without understanding.
facets.psychic: Fixation on cosmic cycles becomes morbid; the soul loses this present life in endless speculation about other worlds and rebirths.

excess.text:
Fatalistic ‘everything is just the cycle’ as escape from responsibility.

Somatic: Body neglected as ‘just part of cycle.’

Emotional: Emotional disengagement claiming cosmic perspective.

Cognitive: Doctrinal acceptance without understanding.

Psychic: Fixation on cosmic cycles becomes morbid; the soul loses this present life in endless speculation about other worlds and rebirths.
excess.vignette: Ravi has turned the Five Fires into a fatalistic escape—‘it’s all just the cycle turning’—using the grand doctrine to justify passivity and neglect. The teaching about the sacred circulation of life and death has become his excuse to withdraw from both.

deficient.label: Deficient — the small life lived as if cut off from any larger order

facets.overview: No understanding of cyclic nature—this life taken as isolated, terror of death, no continuity recognized.
facets.somatic: Body taken as one-time event.
facets.emotional: Maximum death-terror, no continuity.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees life as isolated phenomenon.
facets.psychic: Lacking any sense of the cycle, the soul takes this single life as an isolated accident, and the terror of death finds no larger frame to soften it.

deficient.text:
No understanding of cyclic nature—this life taken as isolated, terror of death, no continuity recognized.

Somatic: Body taken as one-time event.

Emotional: Maximum death-terror, no continuity.

Cognitive: Mind sees life as isolated phenomenon.

Psychic: Lacking any sense of the cycle, the soul takes this single life as an isolated accident, and the terror of death finds no larger frame to soften it.
deficient.vignette: For Sam this life is a closed event with nothing before and nothing after, and the thought of death is pure terror because it means simple annihilation. The doctrine of a cosmic cycle feeding life through death is empty to him; he sees the spark and denies the fire.

attributes.title: Udgitha Vidya (Sacred Singing of OM)
attributes.aspect: Speech=Rik, Prana=Saman, OM=Udgitha. The gods conquered demons through prana-as-Udgitha alone—the only one demons couldn’t pierce with evil. Udgitha is singing of OM—the sacred syllable as essence of speech and prana. ‘Ut’=above, ‘gi’=lifted, ‘tha’=established. Both Saguna and Nirguna realizations possible. Sound becomes transformation practice.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic (the chant) · Ontic mode: Practice — a vidyā (meditation on OM as the Udgītha) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the sacred sound · Causal status: Means (the sung OM as the essence of all) · Key relation: A sound / sacrifice meditation; object = the Udgītha (OM); kin to Praṇava (Dim 11).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice — a vidyā (meditation on OM as the Udgītha)
coordinate.causal_status: Means (the sung OM as the essence of all)
coordinate.key_relation: A sound / sacrifice meditation; object = the Udgītha (OM); kin to Praṇava (Dim 11).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Deep Udgitha meditation lifts consciousness through sacred singing—OM as breath of life itself.
facets.somatic: Body resonates with sacred vibration during singing.
facets.emotional: Emotional uplift through Udgitha, devotional warmth.
facets.cognitive: Mind unified through Udgitha practice, lifted upward.
facets.psychic: The soul takes up the sacred chant of OM as the udgītha, and through that vibration gathers its scattered powers toward the divine.

balanced.text:
Deep Udgitha meditation lifts consciousness through sacred singing—OM as breath of life itself.

Somatic: Body resonates with sacred vibration during singing.

Emotional: Emotional uplift through Udgitha, devotional warmth.

Cognitive: Mind unified through Udgitha practice, lifted upward.

Psychic: The soul takes up the sacred chant of OM as the udgītha, and through that vibration gathers its scattered powers toward the divine.
balanced.vignette: When Ravi chants OM, the sound moves through him and settles his whole system into a resonant stillness; the sacred syllable has become a doorway he steps through daily. The Udgitha meditation is not a performance for him but a homecoming, and it tunes the rest of his day.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mechanical chanting without inner participation—empty ritual.
facets.somatic: Vocal strain without inner resonance.
facets.emotional: Empty performance without devotional warmth.
facets.cognitive: Mechanical mind during practice.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on the technique and sound for their own sake, chanting mechanically without the inward turning they serve.

excess.text:
Mechanical chanting without inner participation—empty ritual.

Somatic: Vocal strain without inner resonance.

Emotional: Empty performance without devotional warmth.

Cognitive: Mechanical mind during practice.

Psychic: The soul fixates on the technique and sound for their own sake, chanting mechanically without the inward turning they serve.
excess.vignette: Sam chants OM compulsively, counting rounds like currency, half-believing the sound itself will magically fix his life if he only does enough of it. The living sacred syllable has become a superstitious tic, and the mechanical repetition has drained the very resonance it was meant to open.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No sacred singing practice—missing this profound transformative technique.
facets.somatic: Voice without sacred dimension.
facets.emotional: Missing the unifying power of sacred sound.
facets.cognitive: Mind without the anchor of Udgitha.
facets.psychic: The soul has no sacred sound to gather it, and its energies stay dispersed among the senses with nothing to recall them home.

deficient.text:
No sacred singing practice—missing this profound transformative technique.

Somatic: Voice without sacred dimension.

Emotional: Missing the unifying power of sacred sound.

Cognitive: Mind without the anchor of Udgitha.

Psychic: The soul has no sacred sound to gather it, and its energies stay dispersed among the senses with nothing to recall them home.
deficient.vignette: Dan recites OM correctly and feels precisely nothing—mouth moving, mind elsewhere, the chant a mechanical exercise with no body and no reverence in it. The sacred sound that should open a doorway remains, for him, just a noise he makes because the practice says to.

attributes.title: Purusha Vidya (Life as Sacrifice - 116 Years)
attributes.aspect: The person is a sacrifice. First 24 years = morning oblation. Next 44 years = midday oblation. Next 48 years (till 116) = third oblation. Mahidasa lived 116 years, declaring sickness could not destroy him. Meditation on one’s own life as a continuous sacrifice—the three life stages (study, household, spiritual practice) as three oblations. Knower of this Vidya lives 116 years without sickness. The universalisation of life-conception leads to immediate liberation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital · Ontic mode: Practice — a vidyā (the whole life seen as sacrifice) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, across the lifespan · Causal status: Means (the entire life as a yajña) · Key relation: A sound / sacrifice meditation; object = the person’s life as sacrifice (Chāndogya).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice — a vidyā (the whole life seen as sacrifice)
coordinate.causal_status: Means (the entire life as a yajña)
coordinate.key_relation: A sound / sacrifice meditation; object = the person’s life as sacrifice (Chāndogya).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Life lived as continuous sacrifice through three stages. Body becomes resilient through this orientation; longevity natural result.
facets.somatic: Body strong and resistant to disease through life-as-sacrifice orientation; longevity (up to 116 years).
facets.emotional: Emotional purpose through each stage—learning, householder duties, spiritual completion.
facets.cognitive: Mind organized around three life-sacrifice stages, clear purpose at each.
facets.psychic: The soul re-frames its whole lifespan as a sacrifice offered moment by moment, so that living itself becomes worship.

balanced.text:
Life lived as continuous sacrifice through three stages. Body becomes resilient through this orientation; longevity natural result.

Somatic: Body strong and resistant to disease through life-as-sacrifice orientation; longevity (up to 116 years).

Emotional: Emotional purpose through each stage—learning, householder duties, spiritual completion.

Cognitive: Mind organized around three life-sacrifice stages, clear purpose at each.

Psychic: The soul re-frames its whole lifespan as a sacrifice offered moment by moment, so that living itself becomes worship.
balanced.vignette: Contemplating his whole life as a sacrifice—each stage an offering, the years themselves a rite—Ravi has come to hold his days with a quiet sense of meaning and dedication. Nothing is merely endured; the ordinary business of living has become, for him, an offering, and the framing steadies and dignifies him.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Rigid adherence to stages without flexibility, missing the spirit of self-offering.
facets.somatic: Body forced into stage-appropriate behaviors without authenticity.
facets.emotional: Emotional rigidity around stage-roles.
facets.cognitive: Mental fixation on stage-correctness.
facets.psychic: The soul turns the scheme into rigid ritual accounting, measuring its years mechanically and losing the spirit of offering.

excess.text:
Rigid adherence to stages without flexibility, missing the spirit of self-offering.

Somatic: Body forced into stage-appropriate behaviors without authenticity.

Emotional: Emotional rigidity around stage-roles.

Cognitive: Mental fixation on stage-correctness.

Psychic: The soul turns the scheme into rigid ritual accounting, measuring its years mechanically and losing the spirit of offering.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned ‘life as sacrifice’ into a grim, self-punishing renunciation, treating every pleasure as a failure of dedication and his own enjoyment as something to be immolated. The beautiful framing of life as offering has curdled into a joyless martyrdom he wears as a badge.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Life as random activity, no sense of sacrifice, no stage-awareness. Premature death likely; constant illness possible.
facets.somatic: Body susceptible to chronic illness from absence of sacred orientation.
facets.emotional: Emotional drift without purpose.
facets.cognitive: Mind without organizing principle for life-phases.
facets.psychic: The soul lives unconsecrated, its days a mere sequence of events with no sense of life as an offering to anything.

deficient.text:
Life as random activity, no sense of sacrifice, no stage-awareness. Premature death likely; constant illness possible.

Somatic: Body susceptible to chronic illness from absence of sacred orientation.

Emotional: Emotional drift without purpose.

Cognitive: Mind without organizing principle for life-phases.

Psychic: The soul lives unconsecrated, its days a mere sequence of events with no sense of life as an offering to anything.
deficient.vignette: For Dan life is a series of transactions to be optimized, and the notion that his years might be a sacred offering, a sacrifice with meaning, has never crossed his mind. He spends his days without dedication or reverence, and the possibility that living itself could be a rite passes him entirely by.

attributes.title: Paryanka Vidya (Couch of Brahma)
attributes.aspect: The desireless soul passes beyond moon to lightning, meets Guru, says ‘I am thyself.’ Passes through Agni, Vayu, Varuna, Indra, Prajapati, Brahma. To Brahma says ‘You are everything, and I am yourself.’ Meditation on the soul’s post-mortem journey through cosmic worlds toward Hiranyagarbha’s couch (paryanka). The throne Vichakshana in hall Vibhu-Pramita is of immeasurable splendor. One reaching this gets power of creating/preserving/destroying worlds if in tune with the One on the throne.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / doctrine — a vidyā (the soul’s ascent to Brahman’s couch) · Valence: Cultivated knowledge · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the cosmic goal (Ādhidaivika) · Causal status: Framework (the liberated soul’s approach to Brahman) · Key relation: A journey meditation; the ascent to the couch of Brahmā (Kauṣītaki).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / doctrine — a vidyā (the soul’s ascent to Brahman’s couch)
coordinate.causal_status: Framework (the liberated soul’s approach to Brahman)
coordinate.key_relation: A journey meditation; the ascent to the couch of Brahmā (Kauṣītaki).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Mental preparation for the soul’s cosmic journey, knowing the stages and proper responses. Saguna Brahman realization.
facets.somatic: Body’s relationship with death transformed through journey-awareness.
facets.emotional: Emotional preparation for the cosmic ascent at death.
facets.cognitive: Mind prepared with the journey-map for after-death.
facets.psychic: The soul, grown desireless, is carried beyond the worlds to meet the Real and to declare its own true identity without fear.

balanced.text:
Mental preparation for the soul’s cosmic journey, knowing the stages and proper responses. Saguna Brahman realization.

Somatic: Body’s relationship with death transformed through journey-awareness.

Emotional: Emotional preparation for the cosmic ascent at death.

Cognitive: Mind prepared with the journey-map for after-death.

Psychic: The soul, grown desireless, is carried beyond the worlds to meet the Real and to declare its own true identity without fear.
balanced.vignette: Contemplating the soul’s approach to Brahman—received, welcomed, seated on the couch of the Infinite—Meera has come to feel, faintly, that reality is not indifferent but hospitable, that she is met. It has softened her deepest anxiety; underneath the striving, she senses a welcome waiting.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mythological literalism—getting lost in cosmographic details, missing essence.
facets.somatic: Body obsessed with rituals for journey.
facets.emotional: Emotional dependency on detailed cosmography.
facets.cognitive: Mental fixation on details rather than realization.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on visionary after-death journeys and cosmic geography, mistaking the map of the path for the freedom itself.

excess.text:
Mythological literalism—getting lost in cosmographic details, missing essence.

Somatic: Body obsessed with rituals for journey.

Emotional: Emotional dependency on detailed cosmography.

Cognitive: Mental fixation on details rather than realization.

Psychic: The soul fixates on visionary after-death journeys and cosmic geography, mistaking the map of the path for the freedom itself.
excess.vignette: Ravi has literalized the imagery of thrones and couches and celestial welcomes into a fantasy he inhabits, elaborating the vision, awaiting his cosmic coronation, escaping his ordinary life into a spiritual daydream. The symbolic approach to Brahman has become a place he hides.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No preparation for what happens at death—total uncertainty about post-mortem journey.
facets.somatic: Body without preparation for death-transition.
facets.emotional: Emotional terror of unknown post-death.
facets.cognitive: Mind unprepared for the great journey.
facets.psychic: Still bound by desire, the soul has no passage beyond its small world, and the great journey never begins.

deficient.text:
No preparation for what happens at death—total uncertainty about post-mortem journey.

Somatic: Body without preparation for death-transition.

Emotional: Emotional terror of unknown post-death.

Cognitive: Mind unprepared for the great journey.

Psychic: Still bound by desire, the soul has no passage beyond its small world, and the great journey never begins.
deficient.vignette: For Dan the universe is not hospitable and does not welcome anyone; the imagery of being received by the Infinite is comforting fiction for people who cannot face a cold cosmos. The teaching’s felt sense of being met finds nothing in him, and he stands, as he sees it, alone before an indifferent sky.

attributes.title: Saptanga Yoga (Ghatastha Yoga)
attributes.aspect: Gheranda’s distinctive Seven-Limb Yoga: Purification, Firmness, Steadiness, Calmness, Lightness, Direct Perception, and Liberation — achieved respectively through Shatkarma, Asana, Mudra, Pratyahara, Pranayama, Dhyana, and Samadhi. Don’t skip to advanced practices. The 7 stages each cure a different problem; treat life-development as systematic, not random.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — the sevenfold ladder (saptāṅga) · Valence: Cultivated path · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework (the seven-limbed ladder of Ghaṭastha yoga) · Key relation: Whole — the sevenfold structure of which the purifications, āsanas, and mudrās are the rungs.
coordinate.causal_status: Framework (the seven-limbed ladder of Ghaṭastha yoga)
coordinate.key_relation: Whole — the sevenfold structure of which the purifications, āsanas, and mudrās are the rungs.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Stage-appropriate practice — meets oneself where one actually is
facets.somatic: Body purified before strength built before subtlety attempted
facets.emotional: Calmness arises from prior work, not forced
facets.cognitive: Direct insight grounded in stable body-breath
facets.psychic: The soul follows the sevenfold ascent of the body-vessel, each stage preparing the next toward liberation.

balanced.text:
Stage-appropriate practice — meets oneself where one actually is

Somatic: Body purified before strength built before subtlety attempted

Emotional: Calmness arises from prior work, not forced

Cognitive: Direct insight grounded in stable body-breath

Psychic: The soul follows the sevenfold ascent of the body-vessel, each stage preparing the next toward liberation.
balanced.vignette: Ravi works the seven-limbed path in its proper order—purification before postures, steadiness before subtlety—patient with the ladder and unhurried to reach the top. The systematic progression suits him; each rung, well-established, makes the next one possible.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Rigid stage-obsession; refusing to move forward
facets.somatic: Over-purifying when body is already ready
facets.emotional: Anxiety about ‘which stage am I at?’
facets.cognitive: Theoretical mastery of stages without practice
facets.psychic: The soul rigidly systematizes the stages, treating the sequence as an end rather than a ladder.

excess.text:
Rigid stage-obsession; refusing to move forward

Somatic: Over-purifying when body is already ready

Emotional: Anxiety about ‘which stage am I at?’

Cognitive: Theoretical mastery of stages without practice

Psychic: The soul rigidly systematizes the stages, treating the sequence as an end rather than a ladder.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned the seven limbs into a rigid catechism, obsessing over the ‘correct’ sequence, panicking if a step is done out of order, more devoted to the system than to what it’s for. The ladder has become a cage of rules he climbs anxiously and never enjoys.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Random spirituality — meditation without preparation
facets.somatic: Body issues sabotage advanced practices
facets.emotional: No emotional groundwork; samadhi-chasing
facets.cognitive: Confused, scattered approach with no map
facets.psychic: Without a structured ascent the soul’s practice is haphazard, never building cumulatively toward the goal.

deficient.text:
Random spirituality — meditation without preparation

Somatic: Body issues sabotage advanced practices

Emotional: No emotional groundwork; samadhi-chasing

Cognitive: Confused, scattered approach with no map

Psychic: Without a structured ascent the soul’s practice is haphazard, never building cumulatively toward the goal.
deficient.vignette: Dan dabbles—a bit of breathwork here, a random pose there, a meditation app when he remembers—with no path, no order, no ground built before the roof. Without any systematic progression, his practice never compounds, and he wonders why years of dabbling have left him where he began.

attributes.title: Ghata (The Body-Vessel)
attributes.aspect: The body is a ‘pot’ (ghata) — like an unbaked clay pot in water, it decays unless baked in the fire of yogic training. Karma and rebirth turn like a Persian wheel until the body is purified by sadhana. Your body is not a separate problem from your spiritual life — it is the vessel that holds it. Care for it as you would care for the most sacred object you own.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical · Ontic mode: Constituent — the body as vessel (ghaṭa) · Valence: Neutral (the vessel to be refined) · Guṇa: Tamas–Rajas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Substrate (the body-pot that yoga ripens) · Key relation: The vessel on which all the haṭha disciplines work; kin to Annamaya (Dim 5).
coordinate.key_relation: The vessel on which all the haṭha disciplines work; kin to Annamaya (Dim 5).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Body honored as the vessel of consciousness
facets.somatic: Regular maintenance: clean, strong, well-tuned
facets.emotional: Friendship with the body, not war
facets.cognitive: Recognizes body as foundation, not obstacle
facets.psychic: The soul honors the body as the ghaṭa, the sacred pot in which liberation is cooked — neither despised nor worshipped.

balanced.text:
Body honored as the vessel of consciousness

Somatic: Regular maintenance: clean, strong, well-tuned

Emotional: Friendship with the body, not war

Cognitive: Recognizes body as foundation, not obstacle

Psychic: The soul honors the body as the ghaṭa, the sacred pot in which liberation is cooked — neither despised nor worshipped.
balanced.vignette: Priya treats her body as the vessel it is—cleaned, strengthened, refined so it can hold the deeper work—honoring it without worshipping it. The care she gives the physical form is care for the practice it serves; the vessel is being made fit to carry something.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Body-obsession, vanity, perfectionism
facets.somatic: Over-trained, over-cleansed, brittle
facets.emotional: Disgust at natural bodily realities
facets.cognitive: Identified with the body image
facets.psychic: The soul over-identifies with the vessel, lavishing all attention on the body as if it were the final aim.

excess.text:
Body-obsession, vanity, perfectionism

Somatic: Over-trained, over-cleansed, brittle

Emotional: Disgust at natural bodily realities

Cognitive: Identified with the body image

Psychic: The soul over-identifies with the vessel, lavishing all attention on the body as if it were the final aim.
excess.vignette: Ravi has become obsessed with perfecting the body-vessel—the flexibility, the leanness, the mastery of ever-harder poses—until the means has swallowed the end. He polishes the cup endlessly and forgets it was meant to hold something; the vessel has become the whole point.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Body neglected, treated as merely a vehicle
facets.somatic: Untrained, toxin-laden, weak
facets.emotional: Disconnection from somatic intelligence
facets.cognitive: Spiritual ideas with no embodiment
facets.psychic: The soul neglects or scorns the body, forgetting the vessel must be sound for anything to be held in it.

deficient.text:
Body neglected, treated as merely a vehicle

Somatic: Untrained, toxin-laden, weak

Emotional: Disconnection from somatic intelligence

Cognitive: Spiritual ideas with no embodiment

Psychic: The soul neglects or scorns the body, forgetting the vessel must be sound for anything to be held in it.
deficient.vignette: Dan treats his body as a nuisance carting his mind around, neglecting the rest, the movement, the care it needs to be any kind of vessel at all. The instrument through which every practice must work is left rusting, and he wonders why the deeper work never takes.

attributes.title: Abhyasa (Sustained Practice)
attributes.aspect: Gheranda’s opening: ‘As by learning the alphabets one masters all sciences through practice, so by practising the physical training, one acquires Knowledge of the True.’ Knowledge is alphabet; practice is fluency. Reading about anything—yoga, sourdough, language, instrument—gives only the alphabet. Daily practice is what teaches fluency. Without it, all is theory.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — sustained, devoted repetition (abhyāsa) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Rajas refined to Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (repetition that establishes the ground) · Key relation: With Vairāgya, one of the two wings of yoga (Yoga Sūtra 1.12); the engine of all the practices.
coordinate.key_relation: With Vairāgya, one of the two wings of yoga (Yoga Sūtra 1.12); the engine of all the practices.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Daily practice prioritized as the only real teacher
facets.somatic: Body shaped by repeated patterns
facets.emotional: Confidence grown from earned experience
facets.cognitive: Theory tested in lived practice
facets.psychic: The soul commits to sustained, unbroken practice over time, trusting the slow alchemy of repetition.

balanced.text:
Daily practice prioritized as the only real teacher

Somatic: Body shaped by repeated patterns

Emotional: Confidence grown from earned experience

Cognitive: Theory tested in lived practice

Psychic: The soul commits to sustained, unbroken practice over time, trusting the slow alchemy of repetition.
balanced.vignette: Every morning, without drama, Meera practices—the same steady hour, year after year, through motivation and its absence alike. It is the consistency, not the intensity, that has transformed her; the quiet daily repetition has done what no burst of enthusiasm ever could.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Obsessive practice that becomes rigid identity
facets.somatic: Overtraining, repetitive strain
facets.emotional: Anxiety if practice missed
facets.cognitive: Rules and counting replace presence
facets.psychic: Sustained effort tips into joyless grinding; the soul over-drives, ignoring the rest the body needs.

excess.text:
Obsessive practice that becomes rigid identity

Somatic: Overtraining, repetitive strain

Emotional: Anxiety if practice missed

Cognitive: Rules and counting replace presence

Psychic: Sustained effort tips into joyless grinding; the soul over-drives, ignoring the rest the body needs.
excess.vignette: Ben practices four hours a day and has let his job and relationships thin to make room, driving himself past rest and past reason in pursuit of faster progress. The sustained practice has become a compulsion, and the body and life he’s neglecting are beginning to send the bill.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: All theory, no action — collection of ideas
facets.somatic: Body never adapts because never tested
facets.emotional: Frustration at lack of progress
facets.cognitive: Confuses knowledge-of-X with X
facets.psychic: Practice in fits and starts never accumulates, and the soul’s gains leak away between sessions.

deficient.text:
All theory, no action — collection of ideas

Somatic: Body never adapts because never tested

Emotional: Frustration at lack of progress

Cognitive: Confuses knowledge-of-X with X

Psychic: Practice in fits and starts never accumulates, and the soul’s gains leak away between sessions.
deficient.vignette: Sara starts a practice every January and abandons it by February, the mat gathering dust till the next surge of resolve. Without consistency, nothing accumulates; her considerable potential stays theoretical for want of the one unglamorous thing—showing up, daily, anyway.

attributes.title: Ahimsa
attributes.aspect: Non-injury to living beings in thought, word, and deed; the first of ten yamas listed by Svatmarama. Pause before reactive speech, harsh judgment, or unconscious harm; choose the response that does the least damage.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Virtue — a yama (non-harming) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Foundation (the ethical ground) · Key relation: Same as Ahiṁsā the yama (Dim 11) and the daivī quality (Dim 24); the ethical precondition of haṭha.
coordinate.key_relation: Same as Ahiṁsā the yama (Dim 11) and the daivī quality (Dim 24); the ethical precondition of haṭha.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Compassionate firmness — protects without aggression
facets.somatic: Relaxed shoulders, soft jaw, calm hands
facets.emotional: Steady warmth toward self and others
facets.cognitive: Considers impact before action
facets.psychic: The soul builds its practice on harmlessness; the body refined for yoga is never refined by harming another or oneself.

balanced.text:
Compassionate firmness — protects without aggression

Somatic: Relaxed shoulders, soft jaw, calm hands

Emotional: Steady warmth toward self and others

Cognitive: Considers impact before action

Psychic: The soul builds its practice on harmlessness; the body refined for yoga is never refined by harming another or oneself.
balanced.vignette: Kavya practices with real gentleness—listening to her body, honoring its limits, never forcing a pose past what is wise, and speaking kindly to herself when she can’t. The non-harming extends inward; her practice builds her up rather than grinding her down.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Sentimentalism, enabling, refusing to set limits
facets.somatic: Frozen, unable to push back even when threatened
facets.emotional: Excessive guilt over normal boundary-setting
facets.cognitive: Paralysis through over-analysis of harm
facets.psychic: Non-harming becomes scrupulous paralysis; the soul fears every action might injure and so cannot act.

excess.text:
Sentimentalism, enabling, refusing to set limits

Somatic: Frozen, unable to push back even when threatened

Emotional: Excessive guilt over normal boundary-setting

Cognitive: Paralysis through over-analysis of harm

Psychic: Non-harming becomes scrupulous paralysis; the soul fears every action might injure and so cannot act.
excess.vignette: Ben’s commitment to non-harming has become an anxious scrupulosity—terrified of the slightest strain, he barely moves at all, treats every sensation as an injury, and calls the timidity ‘listening to the body.’ The gentleness has tipped into a fearful inertia that keeps him from practicing at all.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Casual cruelty, careless speech, aggressive ego
facets.somatic: Clenched fists, sharp gestures, tense muscles
facets.emotional: Anger and irritation as default emotional tone
facets.cognitive: Justifies harm as ‘just being honest’
facets.psychic: Without ahiṁsā the soul’s discipline turns cruel — driving body or others violently in the name of progress.

deficient.text:
Casual cruelty, careless speech, aggressive ego

Somatic: Clenched fists, sharp gestures, tense muscles

Emotional: Anger and irritation as default emotional tone

Cognitive: Justifies harm as ‘just being honest’

Psychic: Without ahiṁsā the soul’s discipline turns cruel — driving body or others violently in the name of progress.
deficient.vignette: Dan attacks his body on the mat—forcing the pose past pain, ignoring the sharp warning, treating discomfort as weakness to be conquered. The violence he does himself in the name of progress leaves a trail of small injuries, and the harming, unnoticed, undermines the very practice it’s meant to serve.

attributes.title: Satya
attributes.aspect: Truthfulness in thought, word, and deed; alignment between inner reality and outer expression. Notice the small daily lies—‘I’m fine,’ performative agreement, exaggerations—and replace them with honest, kind speech.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Vital · Ontic mode: Virtue — a yama (truthfulness) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Foundation · Key relation: Same as Satya the yama (Dim 11); an ethical precondition of practice.
coordinate.key_relation: Same as Satya the yama (Dim 11); an ethical precondition of practice.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Honest yet diplomatic, truth in service of welfare
facets.somatic: Steady eye contact, congruent body language
facets.emotional: Inner-outer alignment, freedom from masks
facets.cognitive: Clear thinking unclouded by self-deception
facets.psychic: The soul founds its practice on truth; honesty about one’s real state keeps the path free of self-deception.

balanced.text:
Honest yet diplomatic, truth in service of welfare

Somatic: Steady eye contact, congruent body language

Emotional: Inner-outer alignment, freedom from masks

Cognitive: Clear thinking unclouded by self-deception

Psychic: The soul founds its practice on truth; honesty about one’s real state keeps the path free of self-deception.
balanced.vignette: Meera is honest about where she actually is—she can’t touch her toes, her mind is restless, her practice is ordinary—and the honesty is a relief rather than a wound. Seeing herself clearly, without flattery or flagellation, she can work with what’s real.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Brutal honesty weaponized, oversharing
facets.somatic: Aggressive posture when ‘telling truth’
facets.emotional: Self-righteous pride in being blunt
facets.cognitive: Confuses opinion with truth
facets.psychic: Truth wielded harshly becomes a blunt instrument; the soul confuses brutal self-criticism with honest self-seeing.

excess.text:
Brutal honesty weaponized, oversharing

Somatic: Aggressive posture when ‘telling truth’

Emotional: Self-righteous pride in being blunt

Cognitive: Confuses opinion with truth

Psychic: Truth wielded harshly becomes a blunt instrument; the soul confuses brutal self-criticism with honest self-seeing.
excess.vignette: Ravi’s self-honesty has curdled into harsh self-judgment—every session an audit of his failures, every limit a verdict on his worth. The truth-telling has lost its kindness; he sees himself clearly and mercilessly, and the cruelty of the seeing poisons the practice it was meant to guide.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Habitual deception, performance, image management
facets.somatic: Averted gaze, fidgeting, incongruent gestures
facets.emotional: Chronic anxiety from maintaining false fronts
facets.cognitive: Self-deception layers obscuring reality
facets.psychic: Without satya the soul lies to itself about its practice, mistaking imagined attainment for the real thing.

deficient.text:
Habitual deception, performance, image management

Somatic: Averted gaze, fidgeting, incongruent gestures

Emotional: Chronic anxiety from maintaining false fronts

Cognitive: Self-deception layers obscuring reality

Psychic: Without satya the soul lies to itself about its practice, mistaking imagined attainment for the real thing.
deficient.vignette: Dan tells himself a flattering story—that he’s more advanced than he is, that he’ll start seriously ‘soon,’ that his sporadic effort counts as dedication. The self-deception keeps him comfortable and stuck; unable to see where he actually stands, he cannot take the next real step.

attributes.title: Brahmacharya
attributes.aspect: Conservation and right channeling of vital/sexual energy; literally ‘walking in Brahman.’ Recognize where your life-force leaks—mindless scrolling, fantasy, addictive seeking—and redirect that energy toward what matters.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Virtue — a yama (energy-continence) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (conserves the vital energy haṭha rouses) · Key relation: Same as Brahmacharya the yama (Dim 11); essential where practice awakens kuṇḍalinī.
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (conserves the vital energy haṭha rouses)
coordinate.key_relation: Same as Brahmacharya the yama (Dim 11); essential where practice awakens kuṇḍalinī.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Energy directed by conscious choice, not compulsion
facets.somatic: Vitality, glow, sustained stamina
facets.emotional: Desires felt fully but not enslaving
facets.cognitive: Clear, undistracted focus on chosen aims
facets.psychic: The soul conserves and directs its vital energy wisely, so the force haṭha rouses has somewhere worthy to go.

balanced.text:
Energy directed by conscious choice, not compulsion

Somatic: Vitality, glow, sustained stamina

Emotional: Desires felt fully but not enslaving

Cognitive: Clear, undistracted focus on chosen aims

Psychic: The soul conserves and directs its vital energy wisely, so the force haṭha rouses has somewhere worthy to go.
balanced.vignette: Kavi manages his vital energy wisely—neither repressing his desires nor squandering himself on every impulse—so that his force is available for the practice and the life he cares about. The conservation is not grim; it’s simply the sense of someone who knows what his energy is for.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Rigid repression masking unfaced desire
facets.somatic: Tension, suppressed energy creating symptoms
facets.emotional: Shame and disgust at natural impulses
facets.cognitive: Obsessive purity-monitoring, judgment of others
facets.psychic: Continence hardens into grim repression; the soul wars against its own vitality rather than channeling it.

excess.text:
Rigid repression masking unfaced desire

Somatic: Tension, suppressed energy creating symptoms

Emotional: Shame and disgust at natural impulses

Cognitive: Obsessive purity-monitoring, judgment of others

Psychic: Continence hardens into grim repression; the soul wars against its own vitality rather than channeling it.
excess.vignette: Ben’s continence has hardened into a war on the body—every desire a threat, every pleasure a fall—until his practice has gone rigid and joyless. The discipline meant to conserve his energy is quietly starving it, and the life-denial has drained the vitality it claimed to protect.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Energy bleeding through compulsive indulgence
facets.somatic: Fatigue, depletion, dullness of senses
facets.emotional: Restless craving, never-satisfied seeking
facets.cognitive: Scattered, unable to sustain attention
facets.psychic: Energy squandered leaves the soul depleted, with no reserve for the inner work it hopes to do.

deficient.text:
Energy bleeding through compulsive indulgence

Somatic: Fatigue, depletion, dullness of senses

Emotional: Restless craving, never-satisfied seeking

Cognitive: Scattered, unable to sustain attention

Psychic: Energy squandered leaves the soul depleted, with no reserve for the inner work it hopes to do.
deficient.vignette: Dan spends himself on every appetite the instant it arises—the binge, the fling, the compulsive scroll—with no governor at all, and comes to the mat depleted, wondering where his energy went. Nothing is conserved, so nothing accumulates, and the practice starves for fuel he keeps giving away.

attributes.title: Mitahara
attributes.aspect: Moderate, sattvic eating—filling 3/4 of the stomach with wholesome food offered to the divine; called ‘chief among yamas.’ Stop eating slightly before fullness. Skip the last 25% — that’s where dullness and digestive load begin.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical · Ontic mode: Practice — moderate diet · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition (a preparatory ground) · Key relation: Same as Mitahara (Dim 6); prepares the body for subtle work.
coordinate.key_relation: Same as Mitahara (Dim 6); prepares the body for subtle work.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Eats to nourish, not to numb or perform
facets.somatic: Easy digestion, sustained energy, light body
facets.emotional: Food as ally, not battleground
facets.cognitive: Clarity of thought after meals, not stupor
facets.psychic: The soul tends the body it is refining through moderate, wholesome food — diet as a quiet daily discipline.

balanced.text:
Eats to nourish, not to numb or perform

Somatic: Easy digestion, sustained energy, light body

Emotional: Food as ally, not battleground

Cognitive: Clarity of thought after meals, not stupor

Psychic: The soul tends the body it is refining through moderate, wholesome food — diet as a quiet daily discipline.
balanced.vignette: Sana eats moderately and well—enough to be nourished, light enough to practice—choosing food that leaves her clear rather than heavy. The moderate diet supports the discipline without becoming a discipline of its own; she eats to serve the practice and then forgets about it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Restrictive, food-obsessed asceticism
facets.somatic: Weakness, brittle hair/nails, missed nutrients
facets.emotional: Anxiety around every meal choice
facets.cognitive: Constant calorie/macro accounting
facets.psychic: Eating-discipline tips into anxious restriction; the soul fixates on purity of food as if it were the goal.

excess.text:
Restrictive, food-obsessed asceticism

Somatic: Weakness, brittle hair/nails, missed nutrients

Emotional: Anxiety around every meal choice

Cognitive: Constant calorie/macro accounting

Psychic: Eating-discipline tips into anxious restriction; the soul fixates on purity of food as if it were the goal.
excess.vignette: Every bite is now a rule for Raj—forbidden lists, permitted lists, guilt at a lapse, the whole practice reorganized around the purity of his food. The dietary discipline has become an obsession that crowds out the yoga it was meant to support, and the anxiety around eating is its own affliction.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Overeating, mindless consumption, food as escape
facets.somatic: Heaviness, post-meal fatigue, digestive disorders
facets.emotional: Eating to suppress feelings
facets.cognitive: Mental fog, sluggish post-meal mind
facets.psychic: Heedless eating builds a heavy, turbulent body that cannot sit still, undermining every later rung.

deficient.text:
Overeating, mindless consumption, food as escape

Somatic: Heaviness, post-meal fatigue, digestive disorders

Emotional: Eating to suppress feelings

Cognitive: Mental fog, sluggish post-meal mind

Psychic: Heedless eating builds a heavy, turbulent body that cannot sit still, undermining every later rung.
deficient.vignette: Dan eats whatever, whenever—heavy, late, indiscriminate—and then wonders why the morning practice feels like moving through mud. With no awareness that what he eats shapes what he can do, he keeps undermining, three times a day, the very steadiness he’s trying to build.

attributes.title: Santosha
attributes.aspect: Contentment—the second niyama; satisfaction with what is present, regardless of external circumstances. Practice noticing ‘this is enough’ moments—a meal, breath, conversation—before reaching for the next thing.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Virtue — a niyama (contentment) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: Same as Santoṣa the niyama (Dim 11); steadies the practitioner.
coordinate.key_relation: Same as Santoṣa the niyama (Dim 11); steadies the practitioner.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Settled in present sufficiency while engaged with life
facets.somatic: Relaxed nervous system, slower breath
facets.emotional: Quiet gratitude, freedom from grasping
facets.cognitive: Mind rests in ‘now’ instead of ‘next’
facets.psychic: The soul practices from contentment, not lack; satisfied in itself, it refines the body without grasping for results.

balanced.text:
Settled in present sufficiency while engaged with life

Somatic: Relaxed nervous system, slower breath

Emotional: Quiet gratitude, freedom from grasping

Cognitive: Mind rests in ‘now’ instead of ‘next’

Psychic: The soul practices from contentment, not lack; satisfied in itself, it refines the body without grasping for results.
balanced.vignette: Kira practices without grasping for results—content with today’s session for what it is, unbothered that she isn’t further along, showing up for the process rather than the prize. The contentment frees her; unhooked from the anxious chase of progress, she progresses.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Passivity disguised as acceptance, stagnation
facets.somatic: Inertia, refusal to act on legitimate needs
facets.emotional: Emotional flatness, lack of healthy aspiration
facets.cognitive: Rationalizing away problems worth solving
facets.psychic: Contentment curdles into complacency; the soul uses ‘I am already at peace’ to excuse the discipline it avoids.

excess.text:
Passivity disguised as acceptance, stagnation

Somatic: Inertia, refusal to act on legitimate needs

Emotional: Emotional flatness, lack of healthy aspiration

Cognitive: Rationalizing away problems worth solving

Psychic: Contentment curdles into complacency; the soul uses ‘I am already at peace’ to excuse the discipline it avoids.
excess.vignette: Ben is so ‘content’ with his practice that it never deepens—the same comfortable routine for years, no challenge accepted, no edge approached, complacency dressed as equanimity. His contentment has become a reason to never grow, a spiritual-sounding excuse for staying exactly where he is.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Chronic dissatisfaction, hedonic treadmill
facets.somatic: Tension from constant striving
facets.emotional: Restlessness, envy, perpetual ‘not yet’
facets.cognitive: Comparison loops, future-projecting fantasy
facets.psychic: Without contentment the soul practices from restless dissatisfaction, forever chasing the next state.

deficient.text:
Chronic dissatisfaction, hedonic treadmill

Somatic: Tension from constant striving

Emotional: Restlessness, envy, perpetual ‘not yet’

Cognitive: Comparison loops, future-projecting fantasy

Psychic: Without contentment the soul practices from restless dissatisfaction, forever chasing the next state.
deficient.vignette:
Nothing in Dana’s practice is ever enough—every session judged against where she thinks she should be, every plateau a failure, the constant dissatisfaction poisoning what should nourish her. She reaches each small milestone and feels only the pull toward the next, and the discontent exhausts her.

The tradition then names the conditions that make or break the practice. Six ‘destroyers’ (ṣaḍ-doṣa) ruin yoga — overeating, overexertion, over-talking, rigid rule-following, restless company, and instability — while six ‘enablers’ (ṣaṭ-sampatti) secure it: enthusiasm, perseverance, discrimination, faith, courage, and the avoidance of idle company.

attributes.title: Shad-Dosha (Six Destroyers)
attributes.aspect: Six causes that destroy yogic progress: atyahara (overeating), prayasa (over-exertion), prajalpa (idle talk), niyama-graha (rigid rule-bound-ness), jana-sanga (entanglement with people), laulya (fickleness/restlessness). Audit your day weekly: where am I overeating, overworking, gossiping, rigidly clinging, over-socializing, or flitting? Fix the one biggest leak.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Obstacle-set — the six destroyers of yoga · Valence: Affliction (to avoid) · Guṇa: Rajas–Tamas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Obstruction (six faults that ruin practice — overeating, overexertion, and the rest) · Key relation: Whole — the six destroyers; antagonist-set to the six enablers.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Obstacle-set — the six destroyers of yoga
coordinate.causal_status: Obstruction (six faults that ruin practice — overeating, overexertion, and the rest)
coordinate.key_relation: Whole — the six destroyers; antagonist-set to the six enablers.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Sustainable engagement — full life without these six leaks
facets.somatic: Even rhythm of activity and rest
facets.emotional: Steady, neither frantic nor flat
facets.cognitive: Focused attention on essentials
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the six ruiners of yoga — overeating, overexertion, over-talking, and the rest — and quietly steers clear of them.

balanced.text:
Sustainable engagement — full life without these six leaks

Somatic: Even rhythm of activity and rest

Emotional: Steady, neither frantic nor flat

Cognitive: Focused attention on essentials

Psychic: The soul recognizes the six ruiners of yoga — overeating, overexertion, over-talking, and the rest — and quietly steers clear of them.
balanced.vignette: Meera knows the six things that quietly destroy a practice—overeating, overexertion, idle talk, rigid rules, bad company, restlessness—and steers around them without fuss. The awareness is protective; she keeps the conditions clean and lets the practice grow undisturbed.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Overcorrection — fearful avoidance of all engagement
facets.somatic: Withdrawn, immobile, isolated
facets.emotional: Anxiety about every choice being ‘wrong’
facets.cognitive: Hyper-monitoring oneself for any ‘leak’
facets.psychic: Avoidance of the six becomes rigid scrupulosity; the soul’s fear of every ‘destroyer’ becomes an obstacle in itself.

excess.text:
Overcorrection — fearful avoidance of all engagement

Somatic: Withdrawn, immobile, isolated

Emotional: Anxiety about every choice being ‘wrong’

Cognitive: Hyper-monitoring oneself for any ‘leak’

Psychic: Avoidance of the six becomes rigid scrupulosity; the soul’s fear of every ‘destroyer’ becomes an obstacle in itself.
excess.vignette: Sam hunts obsessively for the six destroyers in himself and everyone else, policing every meal and conversation for faults, so vigilant against what harms the practice that the vigilance itself has become the harm. The watchfulness meant to protect his yoga has become an anxious tyranny.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Life leaks energy through all six channels
facets.somatic: Exhausted, scattered, gut-disturbed
facets.emotional: Drained, irritable, unable to feel deeply
facets.cognitive: Unable to sustain any one practice
facets.psychic: Heedless of the six, the soul lets overindulgence and restlessness quietly dismantle whatever it builds.

deficient.text:
Life leaks energy through all six channels

Somatic: Exhausted, scattered, gut-disturbed

Emotional: Drained, irritable, unable to feel deeply

Cognitive: Unable to sustain any one practice

Psychic: Heedless of the six, the soul lets overindulgence and restlessness quietly dismantle whatever it builds.
deficient.vignette: Dan is heedless of the very things that undo him—he overeats, overtalks, keeps distracting company, and burns himself out—then blames his lack of progress on everything but the six faults he’s committing daily. Oblivious to what sabotages his practice, he keeps sabotaging it.

attributes.title: Shat-Sampatti (Six Enablers)
attributes.aspect: Six qualities that bring success: utsaha (enthusiasm), sahasa (courage/daring), dhairya (perseverance), tattva-jnana (discrimination), nishchaya (resolute conviction), jana-sanga-parityaga (avoiding wrong company). When facing any worthwhile pursuit, check: do I have enthusiasm, courage, perseverance, understanding, resolve, and the right company? If one is missing, start there.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Condition-set — the six enablers of yoga · Valence: Virtue-set to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition (six supports that establish practice — enthusiasm, perseverance, and the rest) · Key relation: Whole — the six enablers; antagonist-set to the six destroyers.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Condition-set — the six enablers of yoga
coordinate.causal_status: Condition (six supports that establish practice — enthusiasm, perseverance, and the rest)
coordinate.key_relation: Whole — the six enablers; antagonist-set to the six destroyers.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: All six qualities mutually reinforce real progress
facets.somatic: Energy mobilized purposefully, body strong
facets.emotional: Confidence rooted in actual capacity
facets.cognitive: Clear direction, sustained focus on goal
facets.psychic: The soul cultivates the six supports — enthusiasm, perseverance, discrimination, faith, courage, right company — and the path secures itself.

balanced.text:
All six qualities mutually reinforce real progress

Somatic: Energy mobilized purposefully, body strong

Emotional: Confidence rooted in actual capacity

Cognitive: Clear direction, sustained focus on goal

Psychic: The soul cultivates the six supports — enthusiasm, perseverance, discrimination, faith, courage, right company — and the path secures itself.
balanced.vignette: Ravi cultivates the enablers—enthusiasm, perseverance, discrimination, faith, courage—and they carry him through the plateaus that would stop others. He’s stacked the conditions in his favor, and the favorable conditions do their quiet work.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Driven striving that becomes its own obstacle
facets.somatic: Overtraining, burnout, sleep deficits
facets.emotional: Grim determination devoid of joy
facets.cognitive: Obsessive monitoring of progress
facets.psychic: The enablers are forced; enthusiasm becomes manic striving and faith becomes credulity, and the supports distort.

excess.text:
Driven striving that becomes its own obstacle

Somatic: Overtraining, burnout, sleep deficits

Emotional: Grim determination devoid of joy

Cognitive: Obsessive monitoring of progress

Psychic: The enablers are forced; enthusiasm becomes manic striving and faith becomes credulity, and the supports distort.
excess.vignette: Ben forces the enablers grimly—manufacturing enthusiasm, white-knuckling perseverance, performing a faith he doesn’t feel—until the qualities that should arise naturally have become one more set of demands. The enabling virtues, forced, have turned into their own kind of strain.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Stalled aspiration, half-hearted attempts
facets.somatic: Listless, easily fatigued, unconditioned
facets.emotional: Doubt, fear, easily discouraged
facets.cognitive: Wavering resolve, unfinished commitments
facets.psychic: Lacking the six, the soul’s practice has no scaffolding and collapses at the first discouragement.

deficient.text:
Stalled aspiration, half-hearted attempts

Somatic: Listless, easily fatigued, unconditioned

Emotional: Doubt, fear, easily discouraged

Cognitive: Wavering resolve, unfinished commitments

Psychic: Lacking the six, the soul’s practice has no scaffolding and collapses at the first discouragement.
deficient.vignette: Dan lacks the enablers entirely—no enthusiasm to start, no perseverance to continue, no faith to sustain him through the dry stretches—and so his practice never gets traction. Without the qualities that make yoga possible, the possibility never quite arrives.

attributes.title: Trataka
attributes.aspect: Steady gaze on a small object until tears flow; cleanses eyes, removes sloth, and develops concentration. Daily, look steadily at one point (candle flame, dot on wall) for 3-5 minutes. This single practice radically strengthens focus.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Mental (the gaze) · Ontic mode: Practice — a ṣaṭkarma (steady gazing) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (cleanses and steadies through fixed gazing) · Key relation: One of the six cleansings; also a concentration aid (Dhāraṇā, Dim 11).
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (cleanses and steadies through fixed gazing)
coordinate.key_relation: One of the six cleansings; also a concentration aid (Dhāraṇā, Dim 11).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Cultivated attention that pierces distraction
facets.somatic: Eyes clear, bright, relaxed
facets.emotional: Calm steadiness in face of stimuli
facets.cognitive: Sustained one-pointed attention
facets.psychic: The soul gathers its scattered attention through steady gazing, cleansing the eyes and concentrating the mind in one act.

balanced.text:
Cultivated attention that pierces distraction

Somatic: Eyes clear, bright, relaxed

Emotional: Calm steadiness in face of stimuli

Cognitive: Sustained one-pointed attention

Psychic: The soul gathers its scattered attention through steady gazing, cleansing the eyes and concentrating the mind in one act.
balanced.vignette: Gazing steadily at the candle flame each evening, Priya feels her scattered attention gather and settle into a single, restful point. The steady gazing has trained her focus; the concentration she builds at the flame she carries into everything else.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Strained gazing, force replacing relaxation
facets.somatic: Eye strain, headaches, blurred vision
facets.emotional: Tension masquerading as focus
facets.cognitive: Effort blocking the very stillness sought
facets.psychic: The soul strains the gaze obsessively, chasing visual phenomena and inner lights as if they were the aim.

excess.text:
Strained gazing, force replacing relaxation

Somatic: Eye strain, headaches, blurred vision

Emotional: Tension masquerading as focus

Cognitive: Effort blocking the very stillness sought

Psychic: The soul strains the gaze obsessively, chasing visual phenomena and inner lights as if they were the aim.
excess.vignette: Ravi strains at the flame for an hour, forcing an unblinking stare past watering, burning eyes, chasing visions and powers he’s read the practice can confer. The gentle concentration has become an eye-straining obsession, and the grasping for phenomena defeats the calm the gazing was meant to build.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Visual attention scatters constantly
facets.somatic: Eyes tired from screen-flicking
facets.emotional: Inability to sit still and notice
facets.cognitive: Attention span fragmented by stimuli
facets.psychic: With no focused gazing, the soul’s attention stays diffuse, unable to settle on a single point.

deficient.text:
Visual attention scatters constantly

Somatic: Eyes tired from screen-flicking

Emotional: Inability to sit still and notice

Cognitive: Attention span fragmented by stimuli

Psychic: With no focused gazing, the soul’s attention stays diffuse, unable to settle on a single point.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s gaze, like his mind, never rests on anything—flitting from screen to screen, thought to thought, never gathered onto a single point for even a minute. The trained, steady attention that concentration practice builds is entirely undeveloped in him, and his focus scatters as fast as it forms.

attributes.title: Jihva Shodhana (Tongue Cleansing)
attributes.aspect: Specific daily cleansing of the tongue — pulling it gently with three fingers, then massaging with butter; said to remove aging, disease, and death. The tongue is the gateway to digestion and prana. Scrape your tongue every morning. The coating that accumulates overnight is removed toxins; not removing it means re-ingesting them. A 30-second daily habit.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical · Ontic mode: Practice — a cleansing (tongue) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva–Tamas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (purifies and frees the tongue, toward Khecharī) · Key relation: A purification; preparatory to Khechari Mudra.
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (purifies and frees the tongue, toward Khecharī)
coordinate.key_relation: A purification; preparatory to Khechari Mudra.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Simple daily ritual of oral hygiene
facets.somatic: Clean tongue, fresh breath, sharper taste
facets.emotional: Sense of starting day clean
facets.cognitive: Small ritual anchoring daily order
facets.psychic: The soul attends even to the smallest purification, cleansing the tongue as part of readying the whole instrument.

balanced.text:
Simple daily ritual of oral hygiene

Somatic: Clean tongue, fresh breath, sharper taste

Emotional: Sense of starting day clean

Cognitive: Small ritual anchoring daily order

Psychic: The soul attends even to the smallest purification, cleansing the tongue as part of readying the whole instrument.
balanced.vignette: As part of her morning care, Leela cleans the tongue and mouth with an unfussy attention—hygiene as a small rite of purification that readies her for practice. The cleansing is simple, thorough, and done; a clean vessel for a clean start.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Compulsive over-scraping causing irritation
facets.somatic: Sore tongue, damaged taste buds
facets.emotional: Anxiety about oral hygiene
facets.cognitive: Ritual obsession
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on minute purity rituals, mistaking fastidiousness for spiritual progress.

excess.text:
Compulsive over-scraping causing irritation

Somatic: Sore tongue, damaged taste buds

Emotional: Anxiety about oral hygiene

Cognitive: Ritual obsession

Psychic: The soul fixates on minute purity rituals, mistaking fastidiousness for spiritual progress.
excess.vignette: Sam’s tongue-cleansing has become an aggressive daily ordeal—scraping raw, stretching the frenum, obsessed with a purity that keeps receding. The gentle hygiene has hardened into a compulsive, sometimes harmful ritual, purity pursued past the point of sense.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Tongue coating accumulated; toxins re-ingested
facets.somatic: Bad breath, dull taste, oral imbalances
facets.emotional: Aversion to morning rituals
facets.cognitive: Skipped foundation habits
facets.psychic: Neglecting such cleansings, the soul lets small impurities accumulate and dull the body’s clarity.

deficient.text:
Tongue coating accumulated; toxins re-ingested

Somatic: Bad breath, dull taste, oral imbalances

Emotional: Aversion to morning rituals

Cognitive: Skipped foundation habits

Psychic: Neglecting such cleansings, the soul lets small impurities accumulate and dull the body’s clarity.
deficient.vignette: Dan neglects the basic purifications entirely—the mouth, the body, the simple cleanliness that readies a person for subtler work—dismissing them as beneath him. The unclean vessel dulls everything he tries to do through it, and he never connects the neglect to the dullness.

attributes.title: Ṣaṭkarma — The Six Cleansings
attributes.aspect: The ṣaṭkarmas are the six purificatory techniques — working on the digestive tract, the nasal passages, the abdomen, the eyes, the breath, and the skull — by which Haṭha removes the gross impurities that obstruct the free flow of prāṇa and keep the body restless. They are recommended especially for those in whom the elements are unbalanced, as a preparation that makes the subtler practices possible. Their spirit is not punishment of the body but its careful, respectful preparation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical · Ontic mode: Practice-set — the six cleansings (ṣaṭkarma) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (purify the body’s systems) · Key relation: Whole — the six cleansings of which Trāṭaka and the rest are members.
coordinate.key_relation: Whole — the six cleansings of which Trāṭaka and the rest are members.

balanced.label: Balanced — a clean, light body that supports stillness

facets.overview: Regular hygiene of body’s systems supports clarity
facets.somatic: Clear sinuses, clean digestion, bright eyes
facets.emotional: Felt sense of being clean and clear
facets.cognitive: Mind reflects body’s clarity
facets.psychic: The soul keeps its vehicle clear; regular purification of the body’s systems supports the clarity in which subtler awareness arises.

balanced.text:
Regular hygiene of body’s systems supports clarity

Somatic: Clear sinuses, clean digestion, bright eyes

Emotional: Felt sense of being clean and clear

Cognitive: Mind reflects body’s clarity

Psychic: The soul keeps its vehicle clear; regular purification of the body’s systems supports the clarity in which subtler awareness arises.
balanced.vignette: When Priya feels congested or heavy, she uses the cleansings judiciously—a neti here, a gentle kriya there—to clear the vessel and then returns to practice. The purifications are tools she reaches for as needed, not ends in themselves; they clear the ground and step aside.

excess.label: In excess — obsessive over-cleansing, harm from forceful practice

facets.overview: Compulsive cleansing, purification obsession
facets.somatic: Damage to mucosa, electrolyte loss
facets.emotional: Disgust with normal bodily processes
facets.cognitive: OCD-like contamination fears
facets.psychic: Cleansing becomes compulsion; the soul’s hunger for purity turns into obsessive ritual that strains rather than steadies.

excess.text:
Compulsive cleansing, purification obsession

Somatic: Damage to mucosa, electrolyte loss

Emotional: Disgust with normal bodily processes

Cognitive: OCD-like contamination fears

Psychic: Cleansing becomes compulsion; the soul’s hunger for purity turns into obsessive ritual that strains rather than steadies.
excess.vignette: Ravi is forever cleansing—the daily neti, the aggressive kriyas, the purification stacked on purification—convinced he must be perfectly clean before he can ‘really’ begin. The cleansing has become a way of never starting, and the over-purification irritates the very body it means to clear.

deficient.label: Deficient — a heavy, impure, restless body that resists practice

facets.overview: Accumulated toxins dull body and mind
facets.somatic: Congestion, sluggish digestion, fatigue
facets.emotional: Heaviness, depression-tinged mood
facets.cognitive: Mental fog and inertia
facets.psychic: Neglect lets the system clog; accumulated heaviness dulls the body and mind through which the soul would work.

deficient.text:
Accumulated toxins dull body and mind

Somatic: Congestion, sluggish digestion, fatigue

Emotional: Heaviness, depression-tinged mood

Cognitive: Mental fog and inertia

Psychic: Neglect lets the system clog; accumulated heaviness dulls the body and mind through which the soul would work.
deficient.vignette: Dan never purifies anything—the congestion, the sluggishness, the accumulated heaviness left to build—and then forces deep practice through a clogged and murky vessel, wondering why it feels like pushing water uphill. The cleansings that would clear his channels are a chapter he’s skipped.

attributes.title: Siddhasana
attributes.aspect: The ‘Adept’s Pose’ — Svatmarama calls it chief among all 84 asanas; left heel at perineum, right above genitals, chin lock, gaze at brow center; cleanses the 72,000 nadis. Choose ONE seated posture you can hold for 30+ minutes, and practice it daily. Stability of body is the door to stability of mind.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical · Ontic mode: Practice — a seated āsana (the adept’s pose) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva–Tamas (stillness) · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition (a firm seat for meditation) · Key relation: An āsana for firmness; the foremost meditative seat.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice — a seated āsana (the adept’s pose)
coordinate.key_relation: An āsana for firmness; the foremost meditative seat.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Single mastered seat as life’s contemplative anchor
facets.somatic: Body sits effortlessly for long meditation
facets.emotional: Seat itself induces calm familiarity
facets.cognitive: Body ‘forgotten,’ mind freed for inner work
facets.psychic: The soul finds in the adept’s steady seat a still foundation; the body locked quietly in place, attention can turn within.

balanced.text:
Single mastered seat as life’s contemplative anchor

Somatic: Body sits effortlessly for long meditation

Emotional: Seat itself induces calm familiarity

Cognitive: Body ‘forgotten,’ mind freed for inner work

Psychic: The soul finds in the adept’s steady seat a still foundation; the body locked quietly in place, attention can turn within.
balanced.vignette: Sitting for meditation, Kavya settles into a stable, comfortable seat and stays—the body a forgotten, steady base that lets attention go fully inward. The pose does exactly its job: it holds her still and easeful so the mind can do its work.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forcing the form before body is ready
facets.somatic: Knee, hip, ankle injuries from rigid practice
facets.emotional: Frustration when body resists ideal posture
facets.cognitive: Posture as ego-trophy, not means
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on perfecting the posture, straining the body into an ideal shape and missing the stillness it serves.

excess.text:
Forcing the form before body is ready

Somatic: Knee, hip, ankle injuries from rigid practice

Emotional: Frustration when body resists ideal posture

Cognitive: Posture as ego-trophy, not means

Psychic: The soul fixates on perfecting the posture, straining the body into an ideal shape and missing the stillness it serves.
excess.vignette: Ben obsesses over the perfect form of the seat—the exact heel placement, the flawless spine—fussing endlessly with the posture while the meditation it was meant to enable never begins. He has mistaken the container for the contents, perfecting the seat and never sitting in it.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No stable seat — meditation never deepens
facets.somatic: Constant fidgeting, fatigue while sitting
facets.emotional: Restlessness becomes obstacle to all practice
facets.cognitive: Mind chases body’s discomfort signals
facets.psychic: Without a steady seat the soul has no stable base; the restless body keeps pulling the mind back to itself.

deficient.text:
No stable seat — meditation never deepens

Somatic: Constant fidgeting, fatigue while sitting

Emotional: Restlessness becomes obstacle to all practice

Cognitive: Mind chases body’s discomfort signals

Psychic: Without a steady seat the soul has no stable base; the restless body keeps pulling the mind back to itself.
deficient.vignette: Dan cannot sit still for five minutes—the back aches, the legs go numb, the fidgeting wins—and he takes this as proof that meditation isn’t for him. The stable seat that every inner practice requires has never been built, and so the inner practice has nowhere to happen.

attributes.title: Savasana
attributes.aspect: Corpse pose—lying flat like a corpse; removes fatigue, gives rest to the mind. Take 5-10 minutes of complete stillness daily (not nap, not phone). This is not laziness—it is the body and mind’s needed reset.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical · Ontic mode: Practice — an āsana (corpse pose, rest) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Tamas (stillness / rest) · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition (deep relaxation and integration) · Key relation: An āsana for firmness; restorative counterpart to the active poses.
coordinate.key_relation: An āsana for firmness; restorative counterpart to the active poses.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Conscious rest that genuinely restores
facets.somatic: Nervous system shifts to parasympathetic
facets.emotional: Release of held emotional charge
facets.cognitive: Mental noise settles like sediment
facets.psychic: The soul learns conscious release; lying as if dead, it practices the letting-go that rehearses the final surrender.

balanced.text:
Conscious rest that genuinely restores

Somatic: Nervous system shifts to parasympathetic

Emotional: Release of held emotional charge

Cognitive: Mental noise settles like sediment

Psychic: The soul learns conscious release; lying as if dead, it practices the letting-go that rehearses the final surrender.
balanced.vignette: At the end of practice, Meera lies in stillness and lets go completely—a conscious, deep relaxation that integrates everything the session stirred. The rest is not laziness; it’s the part where the work settles in, and she gives it the same care she gives the effort.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Habitual lying down used to avoid life
facets.somatic: Inertia, depression-adjacent immobility
facets.emotional: Numbing rather than resting
facets.cognitive: Disengagement masquerading as practice
facets.psychic: Relaxation slides into mere drowsiness; the soul uses śavāsana to escape into dullness rather than alert rest.

excess.text:
Habitual lying down used to avoid life

Somatic: Inertia, depression-adjacent immobility

Emotional: Numbing rather than resting

Cognitive: Disengagement masquerading as practice

Psychic: Relaxation slides into mere drowsiness; the soul uses śavāsana to escape into dullness rather than alert rest.
excess.vignette: Ben’s ‘savasana’ has swallowed his practice—he lies down, drifts off, and calls the nap yoga, using relaxation as an escape from the effort that actual growth requires. The rest meant to complete the work has become a way of avoiding it.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Never truly stops; constant motion or stimulation
facets.somatic: Tension never releases; tired but wired
facets.emotional: Unable to receive rest even when offered
facets.cognitive: Mind cannot tolerate empty time
facets.psychic: Never truly releasing, the soul carries its tension even into rest, and the body never fully lets go.

deficient.text:
Never truly stops; constant motion or stimulation

Somatic: Tension never releases; tired but wired

Emotional: Unable to receive rest even when offered

Cognitive: Mind cannot tolerate empty time
