Psychic: Never truly releasing, the soul carries its tension even into rest, and the body never fully lets go.
deficient.vignette: Dan cannot relax to save his life—even lying still, his mind races and his body stays clenched, the very idea of surrender foreign to him. He powers through practice and never lets it land, and the integration that only deep rest allows never gets its chance.

attributes.title: Asana for Dridhata
attributes.aspect: Of 84 lakh asanas described by Shiva, 32 are useful for humans, 4 are essential. Asanas cultivate Dridhata — firmness/strength — the second limb. Posture builds physical capacity for sustained practice. Strength isn’t optional in spiritual life. A weak body cannot sit for meditation, cannot withstand austerity, cannot hold its center when life applies force. Build it daily.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical · Ontic mode: Practice — āsana for firmness (dṛḍhatā) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva–Tamas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition (steadies and strengthens the body) · Key relation: The firmness stage of the sevenfold ladder.
coordinate.key_relation: The firmness stage of the sevenfold ladder.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Physical strength as servant of contemplative life
facets.somatic: Strong, flexible body that can sit, walk, work
facets.emotional: Confidence rooted in physical capability
facets.cognitive: Strong body supports clear mind
facets.psychic: The soul builds bodily firmness through posture, so the body becomes a steady, unwavering seat for what follows.

balanced.text:
Physical strength as servant of contemplative life

Somatic: Strong, flexible body that can sit, walk, work

Emotional: Confidence rooted in physical capability

Cognitive: Strong body supports clear mind

Psychic: The soul builds bodily firmness through posture, so the body becomes a steady, unwavering seat for what follows.
balanced.vignette: Through steady asana, Ravi has built a real firmness in the body—strong, stable, able to hold a posture and hold the day. The steadiness he’s cultivated on the mat has become a steadiness he carries through his life; the body is a reliable base.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Body-building obsession; asana as gymnastic display
facets.somatic: Over-trained, injured, burnt out
facets.emotional: Vanity, performance-anxiety
facets.cognitive: Form replaces purpose
facets.psychic: The soul over-emphasizes physical firmness, hardening the body and mistaking athletic steadiness for inner steadiness.

excess.text:
Body-building obsession; asana as gymnastic display

Somatic: Over-trained, injured, burnt out

Emotional: Vanity, performance-anxiety

Cognitive: Form replaces purpose

Psychic: The soul over-emphasizes physical firmness, hardening the body and mistaking athletic steadiness for inner steadiness.
excess.vignette: Ben pushes the postures ever harder—deeper, longer, more advanced—chasing the achievement and the photo, and collecting injuries that sideline him for weeks. The pursuit of bodily firmness has become an ego project, and the forcing breaks the very steadiness it seeks.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Body neglected — cannot serve any practice
facets.somatic: Weak, prone to injury, stiff
facets.emotional: Lacks the felt-sense of strength
facets.cognitive: Mental practice undermined by bodily weakness
facets.psychic: Without firmness the body wavers, and the soul has no stable ground from which to still breath and mind.

deficient.text:
Body neglected — cannot serve any practice

Somatic: Weak, prone to injury, stiff

Emotional: Lacks the felt-sense of strength

Cognitive: Mental practice undermined by bodily weakness

Psychic: Without firmness the body wavers, and the soul has no stable ground from which to still breath and mind.
deficient.vignette: Dan does no physical practice at all—stiff, weak, unsteady—and cannot hold a simple posture or, it seems, a simple resolve. Without any cultivated bodily firmness, both his body and his discipline wobble, and the ground everything else stands on was never laid.

attributes.title: Mritasana (Corpse Pose)
attributes.aspect: Lying flat on the ground like a corpse — destroys fatigue and quiets the agitation of the mind. Among the simplest yet most powerful asanas in the entire text. When utterly spent—physically or emotionally—10 minutes lying perfectly still on the floor (not bed) restores more than 30 minutes of distracted rest.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical · Ontic mode: Practice — an āsana (corpse-pose variant) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Tamas (stillness) · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition (stillness and surrender of the body) · Key relation: An āsana for firmness; kin to Śavāsana.
coordinate.causal_status: Condition (stillness and surrender of the body)
coordinate.key_relation: An āsana for firmness; kin to Śavāsana.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Conscious rest practiced as deliberate medicine
facets.somatic: Nervous system shifts to repair mode
facets.emotional: Release of accumulated charge
facets.cognitive: Mental noise settles
facets.psychic: The soul practices dying before death, releasing all holding and tasting the stillness beyond the restless body.

balanced.text:
Conscious rest practiced as deliberate medicine

Somatic: Nervous system shifts to repair mode

Emotional: Release of accumulated charge

Cognitive: Mental noise settles

Psychic: The soul practices dying before death, releasing all holding and tasting the stillness beyond the restless body.
balanced.vignette: In the corpse pose, Priya practices dying a little—letting everything go, surrendering the grip, resting as though the striving self had been set down. The contemplation of release has, paradoxically, made her more alive; she holds her days more lightly for having practiced letting them go.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Used to avoid life and challenge
facets.somatic: Disuse atrophy from too much lying down
facets.emotional: Numbing rather than restoring
facets.cognitive: Disengagement masquerading as practice
facets.psychic: The practice becomes escape into inertia; the soul sinks into dullness rather than alert release.

excess.text:
Used to avoid life and challenge

Somatic: Disuse atrophy from too much lying down

Emotional: Numbing rather than restoring

Cognitive: Disengagement masquerading as practice

Psychic: The practice becomes escape into inertia; the soul sinks into dullness rather than alert release.
excess.vignette: Sam has grown morbidly fixated on the corpse pose’s death-symbolism—brooding on dissolution, romanticizing the end, using the practice to indulge a dark withdrawal from life. The healthy contemplation of letting go has curdled into a morbid escapism.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Never truly stops moving
facets.somatic: Cumulative fatigue, never repaired
facets.emotional: Cannot receive rest even when offered
facets.cognitive: Cannot tolerate empty time
facets.psychic: Never rehearsing release, the soul stays braced against letting go, and even rest is gripped.

deficient.text:
Never truly stops moving

Somatic: Cumulative fatigue, never repaired

Emotional: Cannot receive rest even when offered

Cognitive: Cannot tolerate empty time

Psychic: Never rehearsing release, the soul stays braced against letting go, and even rest is gripped.
deficient.vignette: Dan cannot let go of anything—cannot surrender, cannot release his grip even for the length of a relaxation—and so the corpse pose, for him, is just lying down while everything inside stays clenched. The capacity for release that the practice teaches has never opened in him.

attributes.title: Āsana — The Steady Seat
attributes.aspect: Āsana in the Haṭha sense is the cultivation of a posture that is both steady and comfortable — a body that can be held still long enough, and at ease enough, that it stops clamoring for attention and lets the breath and mind come to rest. Firmness of body is sought not for its own sake but as the foundation on which everything subtler is built; an unsteady, complaining body cannot support meditation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical · Ontic mode: Practice / stage — āsana (the steady seat) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva–Tamas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition (a steady, comfortable seat) · Key relation: Same as Āsana the 3rd limb (Dim 11); the firmness stage of Ghaṭastha yoga.
coordinate.key_relation: Same as Āsana the 3rd limb (Dim 11); the firmness stage of Ghaṭastha yoga.

balanced.label: Balanced — a body steady and at ease, forgotten in stillness

facets.emotional: Sense of dignity and ease together
facets.cognitive: Posture supports presence, not performance
facets.psychic: The soul inhabits the body as embodied awareness; a steady posture is the seat from which stillness becomes possible.

facets.cognitive: Form mistaken for substance of yoga

The steady seat, over-managed, has become a source of strain rather than ease.

attributes.title: Uddiyana Bandha
attributes.aspect: Upward-flying lock—after exhale, drawing belly up and back under ribs; said to lead to youth and immortality. Even gently engaging the lower belly in throughout the day strengthens core, supports digestion, and creates upward energy.

attributes.title: Jalandhara Bandha
attributes.aspect: Throat lock—chin pressed to chest at end of inhalation; seals prana in the head, opens the heart center. Notice forward-head posture from screens. Brief chin-tucks throughout the day restore neck alignment and steady the mind.

attributes.title: Khechari Mudra
attributes.aspect: Tongue rolled back into nasopharynx; sips inner nectar (amrita) said to flow from soma center; brings perfection in samadhi. Even the simple version—tongue tip touching the upper palate during meditation—stills the mind faster than effort to think less.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a mudrā (tongue-lock to the palate) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (accesses the nectar, stills the mind) · Key relation: A seal; the culmination of tongue-purification; tastes the amṛta.

attributes.title: Shambhavi Mudra
attributes.aspect: Inner-aimed, outer-gazing—gaze externally fixed (often at brow point) while attention is wholly internal; called the most secret of mudras. While interacting with the world, keep one strand of attention turned inward to the witness. Engagement and presence both intact.

attributes.title: Nabho Mudra
attributes.aspect: ’Whatever business a yogi may be engaged in, wherever he may be, let him keep his tongue turned upward (toward soft palate) and restrain the breath.’ A continuous, portable mudra integrating practice into all activity. Throughout the day, gently place the tongue tip on the upper palate. This single subtle gesture quiets mental chatter and steadies breath — and you can do it in meetings.

attributes.title: Ashwini Mudra
attributes.aspect: Repeated contraction and relaxation of the anal sphincter (‘Ashwini’ = horse, named for the horse’s reflex). Awakens shakti, destroys rectal disease, gives strength, prevents premature death. A few rounds of conscious pelvic-floor contraction-release throughout the day strengthens core, improves digestion and pelvic health, and creates an upward energetic flow.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Practice — a mudrā (anal contraction) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Rajas–Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (rouses apāna, awakens the root) · Key relation: A seal; kin to Mūla Bandha; works the Apāna vāyu (Dim 6).
coordinate.key_relation: A seal; kin to Mūla Bandha; works the Apāna vāyu (Dim 6).

attributes.title: Pancha Dharana (Five Element Concentrations)
attributes.aspect: Five meditations on the five elements — Parthivi (earth/yellow/stability), Ambhasi (water/white/flow), Agneyi (fire/red/transformation), Vayavi (air/black/movement), Akashi (ether/sea-blue/space). Each held for 5 ghatikas with Pranayama. When unbalanced, you can diagnose by element: Need stability? Earth. Stuck? Water. Cold? Fire. Stagnant? Air. Trapped? Space. Match the practice to the imbalance.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice-set — five elemental concentrations (dhāraṇā) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (mastery over the five elements) · Key relation: Whole — five concentrations on Earth, Water, Fire, Air, and Space; corresponds to the Pañca Mahābhūta (Dim 1).
coordinate.key_relation: Whole — five concentrations on Earth, Water, Fire, Air, and Space; corresponds to the Pañca Mahābhūta (Dim 1).

facets.psychic: The soul concentrates on each element in turn, mastering the subtle ground of earth, water, fire, air, and ether within.

attributes.title: Tadagi Mudra
attributes.aspect: ’Tank’ or ‘pond’ mudra — sitting in Paschimottanasana, hollow the stomach inward and upward, making the belly like a tank. Destroys decay and death. A few rounds of belly-hollowing on the exhale, several times a day, tones digestive organs, lifts energy, and reverses the slumped ‘screen posture’ habit.

excess.vignette: Ben forces the abdominal mudra aggressively, straining the core, holding past comfort, sure that intensity equals progress. The over-forcing strains his system and unsettles the calm the practice was meant to bring.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Slumped, distended belly
facets.somatic: Weak abs, organ prolapse risk, poor digestion
facets.emotional: Despondency, heaviness
facets.cognitive: Mental sluggishness
facets.psychic: Never drawing the energy inward, the soul lets vitality pool and stagnate in the lower body.

deficient.text:
Slumped, distended belly

Somatic: Weak abs, organ prolapse risk, poor digestion

Emotional: Despondency, heaviness

Cognitive: Mental sluggishness

Psychic: Never drawing the energy inward, the soul lets vitality pool and stagnate in the lower body.
deficient.vignette: Dan has never touched the abdominal energetic practices—the subtle work with the belly, the diaphragm, the core energy—unaware they exist. A whole register of technique sits untouched, and he practices only the outermost layer of the body.

attributes.title: Mūla Bandha — The Root Lock
attributes.aspect: Mūla bandha, the root lock, is the contraction and lifting of the pelvic floor by which the downward-moving apāna energy is reversed and drawn upward to meet the prāṇa, kindling the inner fire and rousing the dormant kuṇḍalinī. It is a cornerstone of Haṭha energetics — a small, precise physical action with large subtle consequences. Like all the bandhas, it is to be learned under guidance and applied with care, for it works directly on the vital energies.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Practice — a bandha (root lock) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Rajas–Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (seals and lifts apāna, rousing kuṇḍalinī) · Key relation: One of the three principal bandhas; at the Mūlādhāra; works with Kuṇḍalinī (Dim 6).
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (seals and lifts apāna, rousing kuṇḍalinī)
coordinate.key_relation: One of the three principal bandhas; at the Mūlādhāra; works with Kuṇḍalinī (Dim 6).

balanced.label: Balanced — energy gathered, contained, and rightly directed

facets.overview: Subtle, intelligent activation of base support
facets.somatic: Pelvic floor toned without tension
facets.emotional: Sense of rooted security
facets.cognitive: Grounded, present, undistracted
facets.psychic: The soul gathers and contains its base energy; subtle activation of the root seals and directs the life-force upward.

balanced.text:
Subtle, intelligent activation of base support

Somatic: Pelvic floor toned without tension

Emotional: Sense of rooted security

Cognitive: Grounded, present, undistracted

Psychic: The soul gathers and contains its base energy; subtle activation of the root seals and directs the life-force upward.
balanced.vignette: Ravi engages the root lock lightly and skillfully, grounding his energy at the base and drawing it upward, applied when useful and released when not. The lock steadies him; the base is sealed, the energy gathered, without chronic strain.

excess.label: In excess — forceful energetic manipulation; agitation, imbalance

facets.overview: Gripping clenched pelvic floor habitually
facets.somatic: Pelvic pain, dysfunction, constipation
facets.emotional: Held tension, anxiety in core
facets.cognitive: Hyper-vigilance, can’t relax
facets.psychic: Gripping replaces intelligence; chronic clenching turns a subtle seal into mere tension and strain.

excess.text:

Emotional: Held tension, anxiety in core

Cognitive: Hyper-vigilance, can’t relax

Psychic: Gripping replaces intelligence; chronic clenching turns a subtle seal into mere tension and strain.
excess.vignette: Ben holds the root lock chronically, clenching the base all day in a perpetual tension, convinced more engagement means more power. The constant clenching has become a source of strain and dysfunction, the energy that should be gathered instead locked into knots.

deficient.label: Deficient — vital energy leaking and dissipating, scattered and ungathered

facets.overview: Disconnected from pelvic intelligence
facets.somatic: Weak pelvic floor, incontinence, prolapse
facets.emotional: Ungrounded, anxious, scattered
facets.cognitive: No felt sense of foundation
facets.psychic: Disconnected from its own root, the soul lets vital energy leak away, with no foundation beneath the inner work.

deficient.text:
Disconnected from pelvic intelligence

Somatic: Weak pelvic floor, incontinence, prolapse

Emotional: Ungrounded, anxious, scattered

Cognitive: No felt sense of foundation

Psychic: Disconnected from its own root, the soul lets vital energy leak away, with no foundation beneath the inner work.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no grounding at the base at all—no root, no seal, the energy dissipating out the bottom of a practice with no foundation. Unaware of the root lock entirely, he builds the subtle work on sand, and it keeps washing away.

attributes.title: Pratyahara
attributes.aspect: Withdrawing the mind from sense-attractions: ‘Whenever the mind wanders, draw it back and bring it under control.’ Applied separately to sight, sound, smell, taste — and to praise and censure. When a sense pulls your mind — beautiful sight, irritating sound, tempting smell, sharp criticism — practice the pause. Notice the pull, then choose whether to engage. Don’t react automatically.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Stage / practice — pratyāhāra (sense-withdrawal) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition (gathers the attention inward) · Key relation: Same as Pratyāhāra the 5th limb (Dim 11).
coordinate.key_relation: Same as Pratyāhāra the 5th limb (Dim 11).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Senses engaged consciously, not compulsively
facets.somatic: Reduced reactivity to sensory input
facets.emotional: Equanimity amid praise/criticism
facets.cognitive: Mind under conscious direction
facets.psychic: The soul withdraws the senses from their objects as a tortoise draws in its limbs, gathering attention for inner work.

balanced.text:
Senses engaged consciously, not compulsively

Somatic: Reduced reactivity to sensory input

Emotional: Equanimity amid praise/criticism

Cognitive: Mind under conscious direction

Psychic: The soul withdraws the senses from their objects as a tortoise draws in its limbs, gathering attention for inner work.
balanced.vignette: In a loud café, Meera can draw her attention inward at will, the clatter receding to a distant hum while she rests in her own center. She’s not at the mercy of every sound and screen; she can close the doors of the senses when she chooses, and open them again.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Sensory deprivation, life-avoidance
facets.somatic: Suppressed natural responsiveness
facets.emotional: Forced flatness, emotional bypass
facets.cognitive: Hyper-control as anxiety management
facets.psychic: Withdrawal hardens into rigid sensory shutdown; the soul forces a brittle isolation rather than a supple turning-in.

excess.text:
Sensory deprivation, life-avoidance

Cognitive: Hyper-control as anxiety management

Psychic: Withdrawal hardens into rigid sensory shutdown; the soul forces a brittle isolation rather than a supple turning-in.
excess.vignette: Overwhelmed by the world, Sam has withdrawn his senses so completely that he’s gone numb and unreachable, cut off from ordinary pleasure and connection alike. The withdrawal meant to be a doorway has become a room he can’t leave, and the sensory world has receded out of reach.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Lived entirely from sense-reactivity
facets.somatic: Constant chasing of pleasure, avoiding discomfort
facets.emotional: Swept by praise, devastated by criticism
facets.cognitive: Mind a hostage to sense-input
facets.psychic: Unable to withdraw, the soul is dragged outward by every sense-object, and the mind never collects.

deficient.text:
Lived entirely from sense-reactivity

Emotional: Swept by praise, devastated by criticism

Cognitive: Mind a hostage to sense-input

Psychic: Unable to withdraw, the soul is dragged outward by every sense-object, and the mind never collects.
deficient.vignette: Dan is captive to every ping and flavor and flicker—his attention dragged wherever the loudest stimulus points, all day, with no ability to look away. The senses run him entirely, and the inner focus that deeper practice requires can never gather in the noise.

attributes.title: Pratyahara of Praise & Censure
attributes.aspect: Specifically named: when praise or censure, sweet speech or terrible speech reaches the mind, withdraw the mind and bring it under control of the Self. The social-emotional dimension of sense-restraint. Notice how much energy you spend chasing approval or defending against criticism. Both are sense-objects. The mature response: receive, but don’t be moved.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — pratyāhāra applied to praise and blame · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Mechanism (withdrawing reactivity from others’ opinions) · Key relation: A form of sense-withdrawal; the social application of Pratyāhāra.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice — pratyāhāra applied to praise and blame
coordinate.key_relation: A form of sense-withdrawal; the social application of Pratyāhāra.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Praise neither inflates nor criticism deflates
facets.somatic: Steady physical state through social weather
facets.emotional: Internally sourced worth, not externally dependent
facets.cognitive: Discrimination between feedback and identity
facets.psychic: The soul withdraws from the pull of praise and blame, no longer steered by approval or criticism.

balanced.text:
Praise neither inflates nor criticism deflates

Emotional: Internally sourced worth, not externally dependent

Cognitive: Discrimination between feedback and identity

Psychic: The soul withdraws from the pull of praise and blame, no longer steered by approval or criticism.
balanced.vignette: When they praise Ravi he receives it lightly; when they criticize he considers it honestly and stays steady—his center doesn’t rise or fall with the room’s opinion. He’s withdrawn his sense of worth from the applause and the censure alike, and the freedom in that is unmistakable.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Stoic-performance, false detachment
facets.somatic: Frozen face, controlled affect
facets.emotional: Suppressed responses creating symptoms
facets.cognitive: Pretending not to care while caring deeply
facets.psychic: The soul over-armors against opinion, sliding into a defended indifference that cannot hear useful feedback.

excess.text:
Stoic-performance, false detachment

Somatic: Frozen face, controlled affect

Emotional: Suppressed responses creating symptoms

Cognitive: Pretending not to care while caring deeply

Psychic: The soul over-armors against opinion, sliding into a defended indifference that cannot hear useful feedback.
excess.vignette: Ben has forced himself so hard to ‘not care what people think’ that he’s suppressed all feedback, dismissing genuine criticism and warm praise with the same brittle indifference. The healthy independence has become a wall; in refusing to be moved by others, he’s refused to learn from them.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Approval-seeking and criticism-avoiding rule life
facets.somatic: Tension, defensive posture
facets.emotional: Devastated by criticism, inflated by praise
facets.cognitive: Identity hostage to external opinion
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly governed by praise and censure, its inner state hostage to every passing judgment.

deficient.text:
Approval-seeking and criticism-avoiding rule life

Somatic: Tension, defensive posture

Emotional: Devastated by criticism, inflated by praise

Cognitive: Identity hostage to external opinion

Psychic: The soul is wholly governed by praise and censure, its inner state hostage to every passing judgment.
deficient.vignette: Dan lives entirely inside other people’s opinions—lifted by every compliment, crushed by every slight, his mood and his self-image handed over to the room. With no inner ground of worth, he’s blown about by praise and censure, and the constant weathervaning exhausts him.

attributes.title: Sthula Dhyana (Gross Meditation)
attributes.aspect: Contemplation of a specific form — visualizing a luminous island of jewels with kalpa tree, throne, and the Ishta Devata (chosen deity) or one’s Guru. Detailed sensory imagery as concentration object. Beginners’ meditation works better with form than with formlessness. Visualize a vivid scene daily — your guru, deity, or even a serene place. Detail breeds concentration.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — meditation on a gross form · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means (concentration on a tangible form) · Key relation: The first of three dhyānas (gross); precedes Jyotir and Sūkṣma.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice — meditation on a gross form
coordinate.key_relation: The first of three dhyānas (gross); precedes Jyotir and Sūkṣma.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Concrete visualization develops concentration capacity
facets.somatic: Sustained sitting, relaxed body
facets.emotional: Devotion or refuge in the form
facets.cognitive: Detailed image holds attention well
facets.psychic: The soul meditates on a tangible form — a deity, a guru, a luminous image — giving the mind a worthy place to rest.

balanced.text:
Concrete visualization develops concentration capacity

Somatic: Sustained sitting, relaxed body

Emotional: Devotion or refuge in the form

Cognitive: Detailed image holds attention well

Psychic: The soul meditates on a tangible form — a deity, a guru, a luminous image — giving the mind a worthy place to rest.
balanced.vignette: Meditating on the form of a beloved deity, Kavya finds her scattered mind gathering, steadied and warmed by the image she holds. The form-meditation gives her heart something to rest on; the concentration deepens, and the devotion lifts her.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Image-obsession; clinging to imagined experience
facets.somatic: Strain trying to ‘see’ more vividly
facets.emotional: Disappointment when image is dim
facets.cognitive: Mistakes imagination for realization
facets.psychic: The soul clings to the form as final, mistaking the supportive image for the formless it points toward.

excess.text:
Image-obsession; clinging to imagined experience

Somatic: Strain trying to ‘see’ more vividly

Emotional: Disappointment when image is dim

Cognitive: Mistakes imagination for realization

Psychic: The soul clings to the form as final, mistaking the supportive image for the formless it points toward.
excess.vignette: Ravi has grown attached to the vision itself—the vivid inner form, the pleasant imagery—collecting and clinging to the experiences, mistaking the picture for the presence. The form meant to be a doorway has become an object he grasps, and the grasping stalls him at the threshold.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Cannot hold any image; mind disperses
facets.somatic: Cannot sit still long enough
facets.emotional: No devotional warmth
facets.cognitive: Concentration too weak for formless practice
facets.psychic: With no object to steady it, the soul’s meditation scatters, finding nothing to settle upon.

deficient.text:
Cannot hold any image; mind disperses

Somatic: Cannot sit still long enough

Emotional: No devotional warmth

Cognitive: Concentration too weak for formless practice

Psychic: With no object to steady it, the soul’s meditation scatters, finding nothing to settle upon.
deficient.vignette: Dan cannot hold a single image in the mind for ten seconds before it scatters—no form, no steady object, no concentration at all. The gross meditation that gives beginners a foothold is beyond him, because the mind he’d steady it with won’t stay still.

attributes.title: Jyotir Dhyana (Light Meditation)
attributes.aspect: Contemplation of Brahma as luminous flame — either as Kundalini-flame at Muladhara or as Om-light between the eyebrows. ‘A hundred times superior to gross meditation.’ Once form-meditation is steady, shift to contemplating just light — a candle-flame internalized at the heart or between brows. Light is more subtle than form.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — meditation on light · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means (concentration on inner light) · Key relation: The second of three dhyānas (luminous); between gross and subtle.
coordinate.key_relation: The second of three dhyānas (luminous); between gross and subtle.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Subtler concentration on luminous presence
facets.somatic: Less external imagery, more inner light-sense
facets.emotional: Felt luminosity, joy
facets.cognitive: Mind absorbed in light-quality
facets.psychic: The soul meditates on inner light at brow or heart, letting radiance gather and steady the mind.

balanced.text:
Subtler concentration on luminous presence

Somatic: Less external imagery, more inner light-sense

Emotional: Felt luminosity, joy

Cognitive: Mind absorbed in light-quality

Psychic: The soul meditates on inner light at brow or heart, letting radiance gather and steady the mind.
balanced.vignette: Resting attention on an inner light at the heart or brow, Ravi feels a quiet illumination steady and clarify his mind. The light-meditation gathers and brightens him; the inner radiance he contemplates leaves his ordinary awareness a little clearer.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Chasing visual phenomena
facets.somatic: Eye strain from over-effort to see
facets.emotional: Spiritual materialism — collecting experiences
facets.cognitive: Hallucinatory states mistaken for realization
facets.psychic: The soul chases luminous experiences as attainments, fixating on lights rather than the awareness behind them.

excess.text:
Chasing visual phenomena

Somatic: Eye strain from over-effort to see

Emotional: Spiritual materialism — collecting experiences

Cognitive: Hallucinatory states mistaken for realization

Psychic: The soul chases luminous experiences as attainments, fixating on lights rather than the awareness behind them.
excess.vignette: Sam chases inner lights obsessively, judging each session by the brightness of the phenomena, straining after ever more spectacular visions. He’s literalized the light into a hunt for experiences, and the grasping for luminous shows obscures the quiet awareness they point toward.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Skipped this stage entirely
facets.somatic: Cannot quiet eyes enough to see inner light
facets.emotional: Missing the luminous dimension of practice
facets.cognitive: Only verbal/conceptual meditation
facets.psychic: Never meditating on the inner light, the soul knows only the mind’s darkness and turbulence.

deficient.text:
Skipped this stage entirely

Somatic: Cannot quiet eyes enough to see inner light

Emotional: Missing the luminous dimension of practice

Cognitive: Only verbal/conceptual meditation

Psychic: Never meditating on the inner light, the soul knows only the mind’s darkness and turbulence.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s inner world is dull and dark—no light, no radiance, no luminous quality to his awareness—and the meditation on inner light finds nothing in him to kindle. The whole luminous dimension of practice is, for him, simply switched off.

attributes.title: Sukshma Dhyana (Subtle Meditation)
attributes.aspect: When Kundalini joins Atma and leaves the body through the eyes, walking the ‘royal road’ (astral light). Attained via Shambhavi Mudra — gazing fixedly without winking. ‘A hundred thousand times superior to Jyotir Dhyana.’ The deepest meditation feels like nothing-special — formless, lightless, just open awareness. Don’t chase experiences; rest as awareness itself.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — subtle meditation · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means (meditation on the formless subtle) · Key relation: The third of three dhyānas (subtle); the threshold of samādhi.
coordinate.key_relation: The third of three dhyānas (subtle); the threshold of samādhi.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Formless, contentless awareness as natural state
facets.somatic: Body forgotten, breath nearly suspended
facets.emotional: Beyond ordinary emotion — equanimity-bliss
facets.cognitive: No object, no subject, pure presence
facets.psychic: The soul advances to subtle meditation, dwelling on kuṇḍalinī and the formless Self beyond all image.

balanced.text:
Formless, contentless awareness as natural state

Somatic: Body forgotten, breath nearly suspended

Emotional: Beyond ordinary emotion — equanimity-bliss

Cognitive: No object, no subject, pure presence

Psychic: The soul advances to subtle meditation, dwelling on kuṇḍalinī and the formless Self beyond all image.
balanced.vignette: Past the forms and lights, Meera rests in a subtle, formless awareness—spacious, contentless, quietly aware. The subtle meditation has opened a layer beneath the images; she can let even the sacred forms dissolve and abide in what remains.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Confusing dissociation with subtle meditation
facets.somatic: Numbness, disembodiment
facets.emotional: Flat affect mistaken for equanimity
facets.cognitive: Spiritual bypass disguised as transcendence
facets.psychic: The soul strains prematurely for the subtle, grasping at the formless before the grosser stages are steady.

excess.text:
Confusing dissociation with subtle meditation

Somatic: Numbness, disembodiment

Emotional: Flat affect mistaken for equanimity

Cognitive: Spiritual bypass disguised as transcendence

Psychic: The soul strains prematurely for the subtle, grasping at the formless before the grosser stages are steady.
excess.vignette: Ravi claims to have reached the subtle, formless states—speaks of them, teaches them—while by every sign still tangled in the grossest attachments. He’s grabbed the vocabulary of the subtle without the attainment, and the premature claim is its own kind of self-deception.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Cannot enter formless practice meaningfully
facets.somatic: Concentration insufficient
facets.emotional: Need for content keeps mind busy
facets.cognitive: Always grasping for a ‘thing’ to know
facets.psychic: Without reaching the subtle, the soul’s contemplation stays bound to gross forms and never approaches the formless.

deficient.text:
Cannot enter formless practice meaningfully

Cognitive: Always grasping for a ‘thing’ to know

deficient.vignette: Dan cannot get past the gross at all—if he meditates, it’s on images and sensations, and the subtle, formless layer of awareness is not something he’s ever touched or suspected. The refinement beyond form is a door he’s never even seen.

attributes.title: Manonmani
attributes.aspect: ’Mind-no-mind’ state — when prana enters sushumna, mind stops; equated with samadhi, raja yoga, the highest goal. Brief experiences of true silence (between thoughts, in the breath pause, in awe) are not nothing — they are seeds of manonmani. Cherish them.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State — the ‘no-mind’ condition · Valence: Attained state / fruit · Guṇa: Sattva, beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit (the mind dissolved into pure awareness) · Key relation: The state reached through Śāmbhavī; kin to unmanī (the no-mind).
coordinate.causal_status: Fruit (the mind dissolved into pure awareness)
coordinate.key_relation: The state reached through Śāmbhavī; kin to unmanī (the no-mind).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Glimpses of mind-beyond-mind in daily moments
facets.somatic: Stillness in body during pause states
facets.emotional: Peace beyond ordinary contentment
facets.cognitive: Awareness without object, presence without thought
facets.psychic: The soul touches the state beyond the mind — manonmanī, where thought subsides and pure awareness remains.

balanced.text:
Glimpses of mind-beyond-mind in daily moments

Somatic: Stillness in body during pause states

Emotional: Peace beyond ordinary contentment

Cognitive: Awareness without object, presence without thought

Psychic: The soul touches the state beyond the mind — manonmanī, where thought subsides and pure awareness remains.
balanced.vignette: In deep practice, Ravi sometimes touches a state where the mind’s chatter simply falls silent—a no-mind stillness, spacious and clear, arising on its own. He doesn’t cling to it; he lets it come and go, and it leaves its clarity behind.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Grasping for samadhi as achievement
facets.somatic: Forcing trance states
facets.emotional: Spiritual hunger that pushes the goal away
facets.cognitive: ’Did I get it?’ meta-thinking
facets.psychic: The soul chases no-mind as an experience to acquire, and the very grasping keeps the mind active.

excess.text:
Grasping for samadhi as achievement

Somatic: Forcing trance states

Emotional: Spiritual hunger that pushes the goal away

Cognitive: ’Did I get it?’ meta-thinking

Psychic: The soul chases no-mind as an experience to acquire, and the very grasping keeps the mind active.
excess.vignette: Sam claims the no-mind state constantly—narrates his ‘thoughtlessness,’ performs a serene emptiness—while his actual mind churns as noisily as anyone’s. He’s made an identity of a stillness he hasn’t found, and the claiming is the loudest thought of all.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Continuous mental noise, no silence ever
facets.somatic: Always tense, never still
facets.emotional: No felt sense of inner peace
facets.cognitive: Thoughts unceasing, no gaps recognized
facets.psychic: Never approaching the silence beyond mind, the soul knows only the ceaseless churn of thought.

deficient.text:
Continuous mental noise, no silence ever

Somatic: Always tense, never still

Emotional: No felt sense of inner peace

Cognitive: Thoughts unceasing, no gaps recognized

Psychic: Never approaching the silence beyond mind, the soul knows only the ceaseless churn of thought.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s mind never stops for an instant—no gap, no silence, no glimpse of the stillness beyond thought, from waking to sleep. The no-mind state isn’t something he disbelieves so much as cannot imagine, and the ceaseless inner noise is simply, to him, what a mind is.

attributes.title: Vasana-Prana Connection
attributes.aspect: Two causes of mental activity: vasana (latent desire) and prana (breath); destroying one destroys both. The deepest practical teaching of the text. When triggered, you can work either end: breathe consciously OR examine the underlying desire/aversion. Both work.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Framework — the link between tendency and breath · Valence: Neutral principle · Guṇa: Rajas–Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (mind and prāṇa move together; still one to still the other) · Key relation: Correspondence — vāsanā (Dim 12) and prāṇa (Dim 6) as two ends of one lever.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework — the link between tendency and breath
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (mind and prāṇa move together; still one to still the other)
coordinate.key_relation: Correspondence — vāsanā (Dim 12) and prāṇa (Dim 6) as two ends of one lever.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Skilled use of both breath and desire-awareness
facets.somatic: Breath stays steady through emotional storms
facets.emotional: Desires recognized without enslaving
facets.cognitive: Two handles for inner regulation
facets.psychic: The soul grasps how breath and mental tendency move together, and learns to quiet the mind by quieting the breath.

balanced.text:
Skilled use of both breath and desire-awareness

Somatic: Breath stays steady through emotional storms

Emotional: Desires recognized without enslaving

Cognitive: Two handles for inner regulation

Psychic: The soul grasps how breath and mental tendency move together, and learns to quiet the mind by quieting the breath.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has felt directly how his breath and his deep tendencies are linked—how steadying the breath loosens an old compulsion—and works with the connection skillfully. Through patient breathwork, he’s begun to soften grooves that thinking alone could never reach.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced suppression of both breath and desire
facets.somatic: Breath-holding becomes tension
facets.emotional: Repression manifesting as symptoms
facets.cognitive: Battles with mind that exhaust
facets.psychic: The soul over-technicalizes the link, manipulating breath obsessively to force the mind into stillness.

excess.text:
Forced suppression of both breath and desire

Somatic: Breath-holding becomes tension

Emotional: Repression manifesting as symptoms

Cognitive: Battles with mind that exhaust

Psychic: The soul over-technicalizes the link, manipulating breath obsessively to force the mind into stillness.
excess.vignette: Ben over-manipulates his breath aggressively, convinced he can force his deep patterns to dissolve through sheer intensity of technique, and destabilizes himself in the process. The subtle connection has become a lever he yanks, and the yanking throws the whole system off.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Breath and desires both unconscious
facets.somatic: Reactive breathing patterns ingrained
facets.emotional: Lived by desires, unaware of pattern
facets.cognitive: No leverage on mental states
facets.psychic: Ignorant of the connection, the soul lets an agitated breath keep stirring the tendencies it hopes to calm.

deficient.text:
Breath and desires both unconscious

Somatic: Reactive breathing patterns ingrained

Emotional: Lived by desires, unaware of pattern

Cognitive: No leverage on mental states

Psychic: Ignorant of the connection, the soul lets an agitated breath keep stirring the tendencies it hopes to calm.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no idea that his breath and his mind are connected at all—that how he breathes shapes what he feels and does. The whole subtle link between vitality and tendency is off his map, and the most available handle on his own patterns goes unused.

attributes.title: Sahaja Avastha
attributes.aspect: Spontaneous, natural state of samadhi—not as effortful achievement but as natural condition when mind, prana, and shakti unite without effort. The goal isn’t a special state someday—it’s your most natural state, available now beneath the noise. Practice is removing what obscures, not adding what’s needed.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State — the natural, spontaneous condition · Valence: The fruit / goal (effortless being) · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit (the natural state that remains after practice) · Key relation: The culmination; kin to Sahaj Karma (Dim 16) and Turīya (Dim 4).
coordinate.causal_status: Fruit (the natural state that remains after practice)
coordinate.key_relation: The culmination; kin to Sahaj Karma (Dim 16) and Turīya (Dim 4).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Living from one’s natural awake state
facets.somatic: Spontaneous ease in body
facets.emotional: Joy as natural baseline, not achievement
facets.cognitive: Effortless presence, no spiritual striving
facets.psychic: The soul rests in the effortless natural state where practice has ripened into spontaneous presence.

balanced.text:
Living from one’s natural awake state

Somatic: Spontaneous ease in body

Emotional: Joy as natural baseline, not achievement

Cognitive: Effortless presence, no spiritual striving

Psychic: The soul rests in the effortless natural state where practice has ripened into spontaneous presence.
balanced.vignette: Meera has come, over years, to rest in a natural, spontaneous ease—acting from her own depth without contrivance, neither forcing nor performing. The sahaja state isn’t something she does; it’s what’s left when the doing relaxes, and it’s quietly transformed how she meets each moment.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Spiritual bypass — pretending sahaja exists when forced
facets.somatic: Affected calm hiding tension
facets.emotional: Performed equanimity
facets.cognitive: ’I’m enlightened’ identification
facets.psychic: The soul claims ‘naturalness’ prematurely, using it to bypass the disciplined work that alone makes it natural.

excess.text:
Spiritual bypass — pretending sahaja exists when forced

Somatic: Affected calm hiding tension

Emotional: Performed equanimity

Cognitive: ’I’m enlightened’ identification

Psychic: The soul claims ‘naturalness’ prematurely, using it to bypass the disciplined work that alone makes it natural.
excess.vignette: Ravi performs ‘spontaneity’—the studied casualness, the announced naturalness, the effortful display of effortlessness. He’s turned the natural state into a pose, and the very self-consciousness of the performance proves how far the real thing still is.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Believes natural state requires special technique
facets.somatic: Constantly striving with effort
facets.emotional: Joy seen as result, not given
facets.cognitive: Spiritual goal-chasing, missing the point
facets.psychic: The natural state is unknown; the soul believes ease must be manufactured and never finds the spontaneous ground.

deficient.text:
Believes natural state requires special technique

Somatic: Constantly striving with effort

Emotional: Joy seen as result, not given

Cognitive: Spiritual goal-chasing, missing the point

Psychic: The natural state is unknown; the soul believes ease must be manufactured and never finds the spontaneous ground.
deficient.vignette: Dan is entirely conditioned—every response automatic, every reaction a groove, no spontaneity or natural ease anywhere in him. The free, uncontrived way of being that practice ripens toward is not even a possibility he’s glimpsed; he runs on rails and calls it living.

attributes.title: Six Forms of Samadhi
attributes.aspect: Gheranda’s unique six-fold classification: Dhyana-Samadhi (via Shambhavi), Nada-Samadhi (via Khechari), Rasananda-Samadhi (via Bhramari), Laya-Samadhi (via Yoni mudra), Bhakti-Samadhi (via devotion), Raja-Yoga Samadhi (via Manomurchha). Different temperaments need different paths. Devotional? Bhakti samadhi. Analytical? Dhyana. Musical? Nada. Pleasure-prone? Rasananda. Heart-led? Laya. Use your nature, don’t fight it.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State-set — six modes of absorption · Valence: Fruit / attained states · Guṇa: Sattva, beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit (six doorways to absorption) · Key relation: Whole — six forms of samādhi in the haṭha tradition; kin to the samādhis of Dim 11.
coordinate.key_relation: Whole — six forms of samādhi in the haṭha tradition; kin to the samādhis of Dim 11.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Multiple paths recognized; one’s nature matched to practice
facets.somatic: Body supports the chosen path
facets.emotional: Practice fits temperament
facets.cognitive: Right tool for one’s nature
facets.psychic: The soul finds that absorption can be reached by many doors — sound, devotion, the taste of bliss — and takes the one that fits it.

balanced.text:
Multiple paths recognized; one’s nature matched to practice

Somatic: Body supports the chosen path

Emotional: Practice fits temperament

Cognitive: Right tool for one’s nature

Psychic: The soul finds that absorption can be reached by many doors — sound, devotion, the taste of bliss — and takes the one that fits it.
balanced.vignette: In deep practice, Ravi has tasted genuine absorption—the sense of a separate meditator thinning, a spacious unity opening—and holds these states lightly, as gifts. The samadhis are real to him and not clung to; he visits and returns, changed a little each time.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Switching paths repeatedly, never deepening
facets.somatic: Restless trying different techniques
facets.emotional: Spiritual shopping
facets.cognitive: Comparison and dilettantism
facets.psychic: The soul collects ‘forms’ of samādhi as accomplishments, mistaking method for the absorption itself.

excess.text:

Somatic: Restless trying different techniques

Emotional: Spiritual shopping

Cognitive: Comparison and dilettantism

Psychic: The soul collects ‘forms’ of samādhi as accomplishments, mistaking method for the absorption itself.
excess.vignette: Ben chases samadhi like a drug—judging every session by the high, faking depth in his descriptions, addicted to the states rather than freed by them. He grasps at the very absorption that requires letting go, and the grasping keeps the door shut.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Forces a path unsuited to one’s nature
facets.somatic: Body fights the practice
facets.emotional: Practice never feels home
facets.cognitive: Conformity over fit
facets.psychic: Knowing no doorway to absorption, the soul never crosses the threshold from practice into samādhi.

deficient.text:
Forces a path unsuited to one’s nature

Somatic: Body fights the practice

Emotional: Practice never feels home

Cognitive: Conformity over fit

Psychic: Knowing no doorway to absorption, the soul never crosses the threshold from practice into samādhi.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s attention is always divided, always half-elsewhere, and the absorbed unity of samadhi is something he’s never once approached. The culminating states of the whole path are, for him, not even a rumor; his practice never reaches the depth where they begin.

attributes.title: Bhakti Yoga Samadhi
attributes.aspect: Contemplate the chosen deity within the heart with such devotion that tears of joy flow and ecstasy arises. By this contemplation, Samadhi and Manonmani arise. Distinctive in Gheranda — emotional/devotional path explicitly named. If your nature is loving, lean into that. Daily contemplation of what you love — divine, person, principle — with full feeling can liberate as surely as any austerity.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: State — absorption through devotion · Valence: Fruit / attained state · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Fruit (absorption reached by love) · Key relation: One of the six samādhis; the devotional door; kin to Bhakti Yoga (Dim 10).
coordinate.key_relation: One of the six samādhis; the devotional door; kin to Bhakti Yoga (Dim 10).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.somatic: Tears, warmth, opened chest
facets.emotional: Heart cracked open in love
facets.cognitive: Mind absorbed in beloved
facets.psychic: The soul melts into absorption through love; devotion carries it past the separate self into union.

balanced.text:

balanced.vignette: For Meera, devotion has become a doorway to absorption—the heart so full and self-forgetting in love that the boundary softens and she rests, dissolved, in what she loves. The bhakti carries her where technique alone never could.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Emotional indulgence, hysteria
facets.somatic: Exhaustion from constant emotion
facets.emotional: Drama mistaken for devotion
facets.cognitive: No discrimination remains
facets.psychic: Devotional fervor inflates into emotional excess; the soul mistakes intense feeling for genuine absorption.

excess.vignette: Ben mistakes emotional excess for spiritual absorption—the tears, the highs, the ecstatic display all taken as attainment, the drama of feeling confused with the depth of devotion. The heart’s froth has been mistaken for the heart’s ocean.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Dry, heartless practice
facets.somatic: Closed chest, no felt warmth
facets.emotional: Inability to access devotional energy
facets.cognitive: Heart-blind spirituality
facets.psychic: Without devotion’s warmth the soul’s practice stays dry, and the heart never opens enough to dissolve.

deficient.vignette: Dan’s practice is entirely dry—all technique, no warmth, the heart shut, devotion a language he doesn’t speak. The path of loving absorption is closed to him, and without any warmth to soften it, his practice stays a cold and effortful thing.

attributes.title: Sarvam Brahmamayam (All is Brahman)
attributes.aspect: Final teaching: ‘Vishnu is in water, in earth, on mountains, in fire and flames — the whole universe is full of Vishnu.’ All living beings, trees, oceans, mountains — know all to be Brahman. See all in Atma. The final yogic vision isn’t escape — it’s recognition. The same divine reality is in your morning coffee, your difficult colleague, your own breath. Practice seeing it.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State / realization — ‘all is Brahman’ · Valence: The realization / goal · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit (the seeing of all as Brahman) · Key relation: The realization at the summit; kin to the non-dual vision (Dim 27).
coordinate.causal_status: Fruit (the seeing of all as Brahman)
coordinate.key_relation: The realization at the summit; kin to the non-dual vision (Dim 27).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Sacredness recognized in ordinary daily reality
facets.somatic: Body itself sacred, not opposed to spirit
facets.emotional: Reverence as default mode of being
facets.cognitive: Non-dual seeing through ordinary moments
facets.psychic: The soul comes to perceive everything as saturated with Brahman; the world ceases to be other than the Self.

balanced.text:
Sacredness recognized in ordinary daily reality

Cognitive: Non-dual seeing through ordinary moments

Psychic: The soul comes to perceive everything as saturated with Brahman; the world ceases to be other than the Self.
balanced.vignette: More and more, Ravi sees the one sacred reality shining through everything—the stranger, the chore, the difficult day all somehow Brahman. The recognition doesn’t make him vague; it makes him reverent, and the world has quietly re-enchanted around him.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: ‘All is one’ platitude that bypasses discernment
facets.somatic: Disregard for body’s specific needs
facets.emotional: Spiritual bypass of legitimate emotional concerns
facets.cognitive: Premature non-dualism erasing distinctions
facets.psychic: The soul asserts ‘all is Brahman’ as a slogan, using it to dismiss real distinctions and responsibilities.

excess.text:
‘All is one’ platitude that bypasses discernment

Psychic: The soul asserts ‘all is Brahman’ as a slogan, using it to dismiss real distinctions and responsibilities.
excess.vignette: Karl uses ‘all is Brahman’ to bypass every distinction—if everything’s divine, then nothing needs changing, no wrong needs righting, no discernment applies. The great recognition has become an excuse for a lazy, amoral quietism, holiness invoked to avoid responsibility.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Strict subject-object dualism, sacred/profane split
facets.somatic: Body opposed to spirit
facets.emotional: No reverence in daily life
facets.cognitive: All things flat, only special things sacred
facets.psychic: The soul sees only separate, mundane things, with no felt sense of the one Reality pervading them.

deficient.text:
Strict subject-object dualism, sacred/profane split

deficient.vignette: For Dan the world is just matter—stuff and mechanism, nothing sacred anywhere in it—and the teaching that all is Brahman is empty poetry. He moves through a disenchanted world, and the shining presence others sense in everything is, to him, simply not there.

attributes.title: Manas-Atma Union
attributes.aspect: Samadhi is defined as: ‘Separate the manas (mind) from the body, and unite it with the Paramatma.’ Raja-Yoga Samadhi = Unmani = Sahaja Avastha — all synonyms for this union. The goal isn’t somewhere else. It’s mind dissolving into the awareness that watches it — available in any moment of true stillness.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: State — union of mind and Self · Valence: Fruit / attained union · Guṇa: Sattva, beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit (the mind absorbed in the Self) · Key relation: The consummation of meditation; the mind resolved into ātman.
coordinate.ontic_mode: State — union of mind and Self
coordinate.causal_status: Fruit (the mind absorbed in the Self)
coordinate.key_relation: The consummation of meditation; the mind resolved into ātman.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Mind known as not-separate from awareness
facets.somatic: Body relaxed in the recognition
facets.emotional: Peace that doesn’t depend on conditions
facets.cognitive: Mind merges with its own source
facets.psychic: The soul’s mind dissolves into the Self; the long ascent ends as the instrument merges with what it sought.

balanced.text:
Mind known as not-separate from awareness

Somatic: Body relaxed in the recognition

Emotional: Peace that doesn’t depend on conditions

Cognitive: Mind merges with its own source

Psychic: The soul’s mind dissolves into the Self; the long ascent ends as the instrument merges with what it sought.
balanced.vignette: In his deepest sitting, Ravi feels the restless mind settle back into its source—the little self resting in the larger awareness, the wave remembering the ocean. The union isn’t forced; the mind, quieted, simply finds its home, and he rises steadier for it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Trying to force the union; performing ‘realization’
facets.somatic: Strain to ‘achieve’ samadhi
facets.emotional: Spiritual ego of ‘I am realized’
facets.cognitive: Memorized non-dual concepts replacing direct seeing
facets.psychic: The soul forces or claims the union prematurely, mistaking a merely quiet mind for the final merging.

excess.text:
Trying to force the union; performing ‘realization’

Psychic: The soul forces or claims the union prematurely, mistaking a merely quiet mind for the final merging.
excess.vignette: Sam declares the mind fully merged with the Self—speaks as a realized being, wears the attainment—while his actual mind is as reactive and scattered as ever. He’s claimed the summit from the foothills, and the claim itself keeps him there.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Mind constantly mistaken for self
facets.somatic: Identified entirely with body
facets.emotional: All emotions taken as ‘me’
facets.cognitive: No witness to thoughts ever recognized
facets.psychic: Mind and Self stay divided; the soul remains identified with its restless instrument, the union never glimpsed.

deficient.text:
Mind constantly mistaken for self

Cognitive: No witness to thoughts ever recognized

Psychic: Mind and Self stay divided; the soul remains identified with its restless instrument, the union never glimpsed.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s mind is wholly identified with its own contents—thoughts, roles, reactions—with no felt sense of any deeper Self for it to rest in. The union of mind and Atman isn’t a goal he’s failing to reach; it’s a possibility that has never once occurred to him.

attributes.title: Nāda Anusandhāna — Absorption in Inner Sound
attributes.aspect: Nāda anusandhāna is the practice of listening, with closed ears, to the subtle inner sounds — progressing from gross resonances to ever-finer tones — until the mind, captivated and absorbed by the sound as a deer is by music, dissolves into stillness. The Pradīpikā prizes it as one of the surest gateways to laya, the dissolution of the mind, and to manonmanī, the state beyond mind. It is the gentle culmination toward which the forceful disciplines have been clearing the way.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic (inner sound) · Ontic mode: Practice — absorption in the inner sound (nāda) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means (following the inner sound to its source) · Key relation: The crowning haṭha practice; the sound-current that leads to laya (dissolution).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice — absorption in the inner sound (nāda)
coordinate.causal_status: Means (following the inner sound to its source)
coordinate.key_relation: The crowning haṭha practice; the sound-current that leads to laya (dissolution).

balanced.label: Balanced — the mind absorbed and dissolved into stillness

facets.somatic: Heightened auditory sensitivity
facets.emotional: Deep contentment from inner listening
facets.cognitive: Mind absorbed in sound, naturally still
facets.psychic: The soul attunes to the inner sound (nāda), following the subtle music inward until it dissolves into its source.

balanced.vignette: In the silence of deep practice, Ravi has begun to hear it—a subtle inner sound, a fine ringing beneath the quiet—and rests his attention there, drawn steadily inward. The inner sound has become a thread he follows toward stillness, deeper each time.

excess.label: In excess — chasing dramatic inner phenomena; fascination with experiences

facets.cognitive: Magical thinking about phenomena
facets.psychic: Inner phenomena are mistaken for attainment; the soul chases hallucinated sounds and lights instead of the silence beyond them.

excess.vignette: Sam has convinced himself the ringing in his ears is the cosmic Nada and built a whole mythology around it, chasing ever-stranger inner sounds, mistaking noise for revelation. The subtle absorption has become a grasping hunt for phenomena.

deficient.label: Deficient — no inner absorption; the mind perpetually outward and scattered

facets.cognitive: Mind always identified with thoughts

Never accesses the inner sound dimension

Cognitive: Mind always identified with thoughts

Psychic: The inner soundscape stays unheard; never accessing the subtle dimension, the soul knows only the noise of the outer world.
deficient.vignette: Dan has never sat quietly enough to notice there’s an inner sound at all—the subtle nada beneath the silence is a dimension he passes over entirely. The whole practice of inner-sound absorption is inaccessible to a mind that never grows quiet enough to listen.

attributes.title: Abhyasa (Practice)
attributes.aspect: Practice is the means—‘Whether young or old, sick or weak, success comes to whomever practices.’ Knowledge of texts alone does not bring siddhi. Don’t wait to feel like it. Don’t wait for perfect conditions. Imperfect practice today beats perfect intentions later. Daily, no exceptions.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — sustained practice (abhyāsa), restated at the summit · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Rajas refined to Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (the persistence that carries haṭha into rāja) · Key relation: The same principle as the earlier Abhyāsa; the thread running the whole ladder.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice — sustained practice (abhyāsa), restated at the summit
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (the persistence that carries haṭha into rāja)
coordinate.key_relation: The same principle as the earlier Abhyāsa; the thread running the whole ladder.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Steady, life-long practice through all states

facets.emotional: Practice as foundation through ups and downs
facets.cognitive: Resolve that transcends moods
facets.psychic: The soul gives itself to steady, devoted, long-continued practice, trusting that repetition alone bears fruit.

balanced.vignette: After years of steady practice, Ravi has watched the slow accumulation bear fruit—the deepening calm, the clearer mind, the changed life—and it keeps him showing up. The sustained effort has proven itself; he trusts the daily work because he’s felt what it does.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Compulsive, ego-driven practice

facets.emotional: Anxiety if practice missed
facets.cognitive: Rigid ‘streak’ mentality
facets.psychic: Practice becomes compulsive over-effort; the soul drives itself relentlessly, mistaking strain for progress.

excess.vignette: Ben grasps at the fruits of practice—impatient for results, comparing his progress, over-practicing to force the flowering faster—and the grasping stalls the very growth he’s straining for. He’s trying to yank the plant taller, and the yanking damages the roots.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.emotional: Cycles of enthusiasm and abandonment

Psychic: Without sustained practice the soul’s inspiration evaporates, and nothing takes root for want of repetition.
deficient.vignette: Dan admires the idea of a transformative practice and does almost none of it—no consistency, no accumulation, no fruit. Without the sustained effort that ripens into realization, the whole possibility stays permanently theoretical for him.

attributes.title: Guru-Updesha
attributes.aspect: Practice should be done ‘as instructed by the guru.’ True teacher is rare and essential; without one, deep practice is difficult or dangerous. Find a real teacher of any worthy discipline. Books and apps can take you so far. Sustained transformation needs human transmission.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Condition — the teacher’s instruction · Valence: Neutral / essential support · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Condition (the guidance without which the path is unsafe) · Key relation: The indispensable support; correspondence to Śraddhā (Dim 23).
coordinate.causal_status: Condition (the guidance without which the path is unsafe)
coordinate.key_relation: The indispensable support; correspondence to Śraddhā (Dim 23).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Devoted learning with respect but not blind
facets.somatic: Bodily learning corrected by experienced eye
facets.emotional: Trust grounded in earned confidence
facets.cognitive: Discrimination retained alongside reverence
facets.psychic: The soul receives the living instruction of a teacher — the transmission the texts alone cannot give.

balanced.text:
Devoted learning with respect but not blind

balanced.vignette: Ravi receives his teacher’s guidance with an open, discerning heart—trusting enough to follow, awake enough to understand, taking the instruction in and making it his own. The guidance accelerates him precisely because he neither rejects it nor swallows it whole.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Guru-worship, cultic surrender
facets.somatic: Body abused by unquestioned commands
facets.emotional: Dependency, projection of perfection
facets.cognitive: Loss of own discernment
facets.psychic: The soul surrenders its discernment to the teacher, slipping into dependence and uncritical submission.

excess.text:

Psychic: The soul surrenders its discernment to the teacher, slipping into dependence and uncritical submission.
excess.vignette: Sam has surrendered his judgment entirely to his guru—every decision deferred, every doubt suppressed, the teacher’s word treated as beyond question. The healthy receptivity has become a blind dependence, and in handing over his discernment he’s handed over the very faculty the path was meant to sharpen.
