attributes.title: Haṭha-Rāja Yoga — The Ladder and Its Summit
attributes.aspect: The integration of Haṭha and Rāja is the recognition that the forceful disciplines of the body and breath exist entirely to make possible the effortless absorption of the mind — the ladder and the height it reaches. To hold this rightly is to practice the body-work wholeheartedly while never mistaking it for the goal, so that purification, steadiness, and energetic mastery all flow naturally into meditation rather than becoming ends in themselves.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — the integration of haṭha and rāja · Valence: The whole path · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework (body-work as the ladder to the rāja summit) · Key relation: Whole — haṭha as the ladder, rāja (Dim 11) as its summit.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework — the integration of haṭha and rāja
coordinate.causal_status: Framework (body-work as the ladder to the rāja summit)
coordinate.key_relation: Whole — haṭha as the ladder, rāja (Dim 11) as its summit.

balanced.label: Balanced — body-work flowing naturally into meditative absorption

facets.overview: Body practice and inner work mutually serving
facets.somatic: Strong, flexible body that supports meditation
facets.emotional: Embodied feeling, not just heady spirituality
facets.cognitive: Direct insight grounded in lived experience
facets.psychic: Body-practice and inner work serve each other; the soul climbs the ladder of haṭha toward the kingly stillness of rāja, each wing supporting the other.

balanced.text:
Body practice and inner work mutually serving

Somatic: Strong, flexible body that supports meditation

Emotional: Embodied feeling, not just heady spirituality

Cognitive: Direct insight grounded in lived experience

Psychic: Body-practice and inner work serve each other; the soul climbs the ladder of haṭha toward the kingly stillness of rāja, each wing supporting the other.
balanced.vignette: For Ravi the body-work and the meditation have become one ladder—the āsana steadying the seat, the seat steadying the mind, the whole physical discipline serving the realization at the top. Hatha and Raja are integrated in him; the body has become the servant of the spirit, exactly as intended.

excess.label: In excess — Haṭha as an end in itself; gymnastics, feats, energetic thrill-seeking

facets.overview: Imbalance toward one wing
facets.somatic: Over-emphasis on physical perfection
facets.emotional: Performance yoga, missing inner aim
facets.cognitive: Intellectual spirituality without body
facets.psychic: The soul over-leans on one wing — endless physical practice, or premature inwardness — and the balanced ascent is lost.

excess.text:
Imbalance toward one wing

Somatic: Over-emphasis on physical perfection

Emotional: Performance yoga, missing inner aim

Cognitive: Intellectual spirituality without body

Psychic: The soul over-leans on one wing — endless physical practice, or premature inwardness — and the balanced ascent is lost.
excess.vignette: Ben has spent fifteen years perfecting the physical rung and never climbed off it—the poses, the cleansings, the bandhas mastered as ends in themselves, the meditation and realization they were meant to enable never begun. He’s polished the ladder’s bottom step and forgotten it was a ladder.

deficient.label: Deficient — reaching for meditation with no embodied foundation to support it

facets.overview: Neither well-developed, half-hearted at both
facets.somatic: Body neglected, weak, tense
facets.emotional: No felt-sense of progress
facets.cognitive: Spiritual aspirations remain abstract
facets.psychic: Neither wing develops; half-hearted at both body and meditation, the soul never gains the integrated foundation real progress needs.

deficient.text:
Neither well-developed, half-hearted at both

Somatic: Body neglected, weak, tense

Emotional: No felt-sense of progress

Cognitive: Spiritual aspirations remain abstract

Psychic: Neither wing develops; half-hearted at both body and meditation, the soul never gains the integrated foundation real progress needs.
deficient.vignette: Dan wants the summit without the climb—reaching for the meditative heights while despising the ‘mere’ body-work that steadies them, and wondering why his ungrounded spirituality keeps toppling. He’s tried to skip the physical foundation, and the structure built on nothing keeps falling down.

attributes.attribute: The Lived Experience of Holding the Vision
attributes.title: The Lived Experience of Holding the Vision
attributes.aspect: Before the individual themes, it is essential to examine the three states of the vision itself — for a non-dual understanding can be held in a way that frees, a way that subtly inflates and evades, and it can be wholly absent. This is the single most important practical distinction in the whole dimension.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: State / practice — sustaining the non-dual view in daily life · Valence: Cultivated stance / the fruit lived · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Ground held moment to moment · Key relation: Genus — the lived holding of which all the pointers below are expressions.
coordinate.ontic_mode: State / practice — sustaining the non-dual view in daily life
coordinate.key_relation: Genus — the lived holding of which all the pointers below are expressions.

balanced.label: Balanced — the vision as a lived, grounding recognition

facets.overview: Held in balance, the non-dual vision functions as a quiet, stabilizing ground beneath ordinary life. The person fully engages the relative world — works, loves, grieves, takes responsibility — while some steady part of awareness rests in the recognition that they are, most deeply, the unshaken witness in which all of it appears. The fruit is not detachment from life but a particular fearlessness within it: losses are felt cleanly without becoming identity-collapse, because the one who would be annihilated was never the whole story. The body is at ease, the emotions flow without flooding, and the mind has a ground that does not move.

balanced.text: Held in balance, the non-dual vision functions as a quiet, stabilizing ground beneath ordinary life. The person fully engages the relative world — works, loves, grieves, takes responsibility — while some steady part of awareness rests in the recognition that they are, most deeply, the unshaken witness in which all of it appears. The fruit is not detachment from life but a particular fearlessness within it: losses are felt cleanly without becoming identity-collapse, because the one who would be annihilated was never the whole story. The body is at ease, the emotions flow without flooding, and the mind has a ground that does not move.

excess.label: In excess — the bypass: ‘it’s all One, so nothing matters’

facets.overview: The most dangerous distortion of the non-dual vision is what is sometimes called neo-advaita or spiritual bypassing: the vision is grasped intellectually and used by the ego to evade rather than dissolve. ‘There is no one to suffer; it’s all just appearance; the body and the relationship and the responsibility are unreal’ — the words are technically the tradition’s own, but in this mouth they become a sophisticated avoidance of grief, intimacy, and duty. The vision becomes a hiding-place for an ego that has learned to call its own numbness ‘liberation.’ The texts guard against this precisely: advaita is transcendence, they insist, not negation; harmonization, not the contemptuous dismissal of the relative.

excess.text: The most dangerous distortion of the non-dual vision is what is sometimes called neo-advaita or spiritual bypassing: the vision is grasped intellectually and used by the ego to evade rather than dissolve. ‘There is no one to suffer; it’s all just appearance; the body and the relationship and the responsibility are unreal’ — the words are technically the tradition’s own, but in this mouth they become a sophisticated avoidance of grief, intimacy, and duty. The vision becomes a hiding-place for an ego that has learned to call its own numbness ‘liberation.’ The texts guard against this precisely: advaita is transcendence, they insist, not negation; harmonization, not the contemptuous dismissal of the relative.

deficient.label: Deficient — the contraction: the small self as the whole story

facets.overview: Where the vision is wholly absent, a person lives entirely identified with the separate body-mind. There is no felt ground beneath the small self, no sense of any reality larger than the personal, and so the whole weight of existence rests on a fragile, temporary individual who must be defended, enlarged, and kept safe against a world that will eventually take everything. This is the ordinary condition the tradition diagnoses as suffering at its root: not this or that misfortune, but the structural insecurity of a self that has mistaken a wave for the whole ocean.

deficient.text: Where the vision is wholly absent, a person lives entirely identified with the separate body-mind. There is no felt ground beneath the small self, no sense of any reality larger than the personal, and so the whole weight of existence rests on a fragile, temporary individual who must be defended, enlarged, and kept safe against a world that will eventually take everything. This is the ordinary condition the tradition diagnoses as suffering at its root: not this or that misfortune, but the structural insecurity of a self that has mistaken a wave for the whole ocean.

attributes.title: I Am Shiva (Aham Shivasmi)
attributes.aspect: The final chapter culminates in Janaka’s recognition ‘I am Shiva’—not the deity but the auspicious pure consciousness behind all phenomena. Shiva means the all-pervading auspicious Self that is everyone’s true nature. To say ‘I am Shiva’ is to recognize one’s identity with the ground of all being. This is the destination of Ashtavakra Gita.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Pointer — a non-dual realization · Valence: Neutral — the truth to be seen · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, as the Absolute (transcendent) · Causal status: Ground / realization · Key relation: mahāvākya ‘Aham Shivasmi’ — the Self as pure auspicious awareness.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — the truth to be seen
coordinate.key_relation: mahāvākya ‘Aham Shivasmi’ — the Self as pure auspicious awareness.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Direct living recognition of one’s Shiva-nature. Not pose but actual identity.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as Shiva’s appearance.
facets.emotional: Emotional life as Shiva’s expression.
facets.cognitive: Mind realizes its Shiva-nature directly.
facets.psychic: The soul declares its identity with Śiva, the auspicious pure awareness, and lives from that recognition rather than the small self.

balanced.text:
Direct living recognition of one’s Shiva-nature. Not pose but actual identity.

Somatic: Body recognized as Shiva’s appearance.

Emotional: Emotional life as Shiva’s expression.

Cognitive: Mind realizes its Shiva-nature directly.

Psychic: The soul declares its identity with Śiva, the auspicious pure awareness, and lives from that recognition rather than the small self.
balanced.vignette: In a quiet moment Ravi recognizes, without drama, that what he most deeply is is not the anxious self but the pure awareness in which it appears. The recognition humbles rather than inflates him; ‘I am Shiva’ is not a boast but a homecoming to something that was always here.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Ego-claim of ‘I am Shiva’ as spiritual narcissism.
facets.somatic: Body in pretentious Shiva-postures.
facets.emotional: Emotional grandiosity.
facets.cognitive: Mental claim without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul parrots ‘I am Śiva’ from the ego, inflating the very self the saying is meant to dissolve.

excess.text:
Ego-claim of ‘I am Shiva’ as spiritual narcissism.

Somatic: Body in pretentious Shiva-postures.

Emotional: Emotional grandiosity.

Cognitive: Mental claim without realization.

Psychic: The soul parrots ‘I am Śiva’ from the ego, inflating the very self the saying is meant to dissolve.
excess.vignette: Karl now introduces himself, more or less, as God—‘I am Shiva, the Absolute’—while behaving like an ordinary, rather grandiose man, using the identity to place himself above criticism and rules. He has handed the highest teaching straight to the ego it was meant to dissolve.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total separation from Shiva. ‘Shiva is divine; I am limited human.’ Maximum dualism.
facets.somatic: Body taken as separate from divine.
facets.emotional: Emotional separation from sacred.
facets.cognitive: Mind cannot conceive of identity with Shiva.
facets.psychic: The soul knows itself only as a limited person, never suspecting the divine it already is.

deficient.text:
Total separation from Shiva. ‘Shiva is divine; I am limited human.’ Maximum dualism.

Somatic: Body taken as separate from divine.

Emotional: Emotional separation from sacred.

Cognitive: Mind cannot conceive of identity with Shiva.

Psychic: The soul knows itself only as a limited person, never suspecting the divine it already is.
deficient.vignette: For Dan there is only Dan—his body, his name, his story—and the suggestion that his deepest identity might be pure consciousness registers as pleasant nonsense. The teaching finds no traction; he is wholly the small self and has never suspected anything more.

attributes.title: Prajnana Brahma Lived (Aitareya Mahavakya)
attributes.aspect: Prajnana Brahma is one of the four Mahavakyas, traditionally from Aitareya. Krishnananda emphasizes its lived dimension: the very consciousness in you—right now—is Brahman. Not abstract concept but immediate recognition. The shift from ‘I am conscious of things’ to ‘My consciousness IS Brahman’ transforms experience.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Pointer — a non-dual realization · Valence: Neutral — the truth to be seen · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, as the Absolute (transcendent) · Causal status: Ground / realization · Key relation: the lived Aitareya mahāvākya — consciousness realized as Brahman in experience.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — the truth to be seen
coordinate.key_relation: the lived Aitareya mahāvākya — consciousness realized as Brahman in experience.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Direct living recognition of own consciousness as Brahman. Not abstract but ever-present awareness.
facets.somatic: Body’s consciousness recognized as cosmic Brahman.
facets.emotional: Emotional perceiving acknowledged as Brahman’s perceiving.
facets.cognitive: Mind’s awareness directly known as Brahman.
facets.psychic: The soul lives the truth that consciousness itself is Brahman, meeting each moment as awareness rather than as the things awareness holds.

balanced.text:
Direct living recognition of own consciousness as Brahman. Not abstract but ever-present awareness.

Somatic: Body’s consciousness recognized as cosmic Brahman.

Emotional: Emotional perceiving acknowledged as Brahman’s perceiving.

Cognitive: Mind’s awareness directly known as Brahman.

Psychic: The soul lives the truth that consciousness itself is Brahman, meeting each moment as awareness rather than as the things awareness holds.
balanced.vignette: Meera has come to live from the felt sense that consciousness itself is the ground of everything—so she meets her days as awareness meeting its own display, less gripped, more spacious. The teaching isn’t a belief she holds but a place she increasingly lives from.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Intellectual claim ‘I am consciousness’ without genuine recognition. Doctrinal identity.
facets.somatic: Body unchanged by intellectual claim.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity continues despite claim.
facets.cognitive: Concept without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul turns ‘consciousness is Brahman’ into a mental conclusion, asserting it while still identified with content.

excess.text:
Intellectual claim ‘I am consciousness’ without genuine recognition. Doctrinal identity.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity continues despite claim.

Cognitive: Concept without realization.

Psychic: The soul turns ‘consciousness is Brahman’ into a mental conclusion, asserting it while still identified with content.
excess.vignette: Ravi can lecture on ‘consciousness as Brahman’ for an hour and lives no differently for any of it—the profound recognition kept safely in the head, a doctrine he owns and is never moved by. The lived reality the teaching points to has never touched his Tuesday.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Consciousness taken as biological brain-function only. Brahman dimension never recognized.
facets.somatic: Body taken as producer of consciousness.
facets.emotional: Emotions taken as ultimate reality.
facets.cognitive: Mind reduces consciousness to brain-activity.
facets.psychic: The soul takes itself to be the objects of consciousness, never resting as the knowing in which they appear.

deficient.text:

Cognitive: Mind reduces consciousness to brain-activity.

Psychic: The soul takes itself to be the objects of consciousness, never resting as the knowing in which they appear.
deficient.vignette: Dan takes his own awareness completely for granted—the one thing nearest to him is the one thing he has never once wondered about. That consciousness might be the very ground of the real is not a claim he rejects; it’s a question he’s never thought to ask.

attributes.title: Prajnana Brahma (Consciousness is Brahman)
attributes.aspect: Prajnana Brahma—Consciousness is Brahman. This is one of the four Mahavakyas (great sayings). Pure awareness itself is the absolute reality.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Pointer — a non-dual realization · Valence: Neutral — the truth to be seen · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, as the Absolute (transcendent) · Causal status: Ground / realization · Key relation: mahāvākya ‘Prajñānam Brahma’ — consciousness is Brahman (Aitareya).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — the truth to be seen
coordinate.key_relation: mahāvākya ‘Prajñānam Brahma’ — consciousness is Brahman (Aitareya).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition that consciousness itself, not its contents, is Brahman. Direct realization of pure awareness as ultimate.
facets.somatic: Body’s awareness recognized as Brahman.
facets.emotional: Emotional life seen as movements within consciousness-Brahman.
facets.cognitive: Mind’s awareness recognized as the absolute itself.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes pure consciousness as the absolute, the one knowing that underlies every act of knowing.

balanced.text:
Recognition that consciousness itself, not its contents, is Brahman. Direct realization of pure awareness as ultimate.

Somatic: Body’s awareness recognized as Brahman.

Emotional: Emotional life seen as movements within consciousness-Brahman.

Cognitive: Mind’s awareness recognized as the absolute itself.

Psychic: The soul recognizes pure consciousness as the absolute, the one knowing that underlies every act of knowing.
balanced.vignette: Sitting quietly, Kavya notices that awareness is not a thing she has but the space in which all things appear—and senses, faintly, that this is what the sages meant by the Absolute. The recognition steadies her: reality, at bottom, is aware.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Doctrinal claim ‘I am consciousness’ without realization, using it as new identity-game.
facets.somatic: Body unchanged by claimed realization.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity continues despite claim.
facets.cognitive: Concept of being consciousness without actual recognition.
facets.psychic: The soul intellectualizes the equation, mistaking the idea of consciousness-as-Brahman for the living recognition.

excess.text:

Psychic: The soul intellectualizes the equation, mistaking the idea of consciousness-as-Brahman for the living recognition.
excess.vignette: Sam wields ‘consciousness is Brahman’ as an argument to win, mastering the metaphysics, defeating materialists in debate, and remaining, in his actual life, as reactive as anyone. The living recognition has become a finite position he defends.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Consciousness taken as biological brain-function only—missing the recognition that it IS Brahman.
facets.somatic: Body taken as source of consciousness.
facets.emotional: Emotions taken as ultimate reality.
facets.cognitive: Mind reduces consciousness to brain-activity.
facets.psychic: The soul never glimpses awareness as such, lost wholly in what is perceived.

deficient.text:
Consciousness taken as biological brain-function only—missing the recognition that it IS Brahman.

Somatic: Body taken as source of consciousness.

Cognitive: Mind reduces consciousness to brain-activity.

Psychic: The soul never glimpses awareness as such, lost wholly in what is perceived.
deficient.vignette: For Dan consciousness is just the brain’s electricity, a byproduct of meat, and the claim that awareness is the ground of all is, to him, mysticism for the credulous. The teaching’s central recognition finds nothing in him to land on.

attributes.title: Aham Brahmasmi (I am Brahman)
attributes.aspect: In the beginning, this was Brahman alone. It knew itself: ‘I am Brahman.’ Therefore it became all. One of the four Mahavakyas. Whosoever knows this becomes all.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Pointer — a non-dual realization · Valence: Neutral — the truth to be seen · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, as the Absolute (transcendent) · Causal status: Ground / realization · Key relation: mahāvākya ‘Aham Brahmāsmi’ — I am Brahman (Bṛhadāraṇyaka).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — the truth to be seen
coordinate.key_relation: mahāvākya ‘Aham Brahmāsmi’ — I am Brahman (Bṛhadāraṇyaka).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Authentic realization ‘I am Brahman’—not as ego-claim but as direct recognition of one’s actual nature.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as expression of Brahman.
facets.emotional: Emotional life as manifestation of one Self.
facets.cognitive: Mind genuinely realizes ‘I am Brahman.’
facets.psychic: The soul realizes ‘I am Brahman’ not as a boast but as the dissolving of the boundary between self and the absolute.

balanced.text:
Authentic realization ‘I am Brahman’—not as ego-claim but as direct recognition of one’s actual nature.

Somatic: Body recognized as expression of Brahman.

Emotional: Emotional life as manifestation of one Self.

Cognitive: Mind genuinely realizes ‘I am Brahman.’

Psychic: The soul realizes ‘I am Brahman’ not as a boast but as the dissolving of the boundary between self and the absolute.
balanced.vignette: After long practice, Ravi rests in a quiet certainty that the ground of himself and the ground of everything are not two—‘I am Brahman’ felt as a dissolving of walls, not an inflation of self. It makes him gentler, not grander.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Ego-inflation through Mahavakya—‘I am Brahman’ as spiritual narcissism.
facets.somatic: Body claimed as Brahman in ego-mode.
facets.emotional: Emotional grandiosity.
facets.cognitive: Concept of being Brahman without realization.
facets.psychic: The ego seizes the great saying for itself, swelling into spiritual grandiosity.

excess.text:
Ego-inflation through Mahavakya—‘I am Brahman’ as spiritual narcissism.

Somatic: Body claimed as Brahman in ego-mode.

Emotional: Emotional grandiosity.

Cognitive: Concept of being Brahman without realization.

Psychic: The ego seizes the great saying for itself, swelling into spiritual grandiosity.
excess.vignette: Ben announces that he IS Brahman, the Absolute, the Self of all—and treats waiters and rules as beneath a being of his stature. He has grabbed the great identity as a trophy for the ego, which is precisely backwards from what it means.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete separation from Brahman—‘I am limited self, Brahman is other’ maximum dualistic suffering.
facets.somatic: Body taken as isolated entity.
facets.emotional: Emotional separation from divine.
facets.cognitive: Mind cannot conceive of identity with absolute.
facets.psychic: The soul lives convinced it is only a separate, perishable creature, the great identity unimagined.

deficient.text:
Complete separation from Brahman—‘I am limited self, Brahman is other’ maximum dualistic suffering.

Somatic: Body taken as isolated entity.

Emotional: Emotional separation from divine.

Cognitive: Mind cannot conceive of identity with absolute.

Psychic: The soul lives convinced it is only a separate, perishable creature, the great identity unimagined.
deficient.vignette: For Dan ‘I am Brahman’ is words on a page with no felt reality—he is his body and his name and nothing more, and the grand identity is poetry at best. The most radical claim of the tradition slides off him entirely.

attributes.title: Tat Tvam Asi — Thou Art That
attributes.aspect: Tat tvam asi, ‘thou art That,’ is the instruction-saying given by Uddālaka to his son Śvetaketu nine times over, each time stripping away another layer of what the boy took himself to be. Its claim is the heart of the whole vision: the innermost reality of the individual (tvam, thou) is not different from the ultimate ground of all existence (tat, That). It is not asking the small self to become Brahman; it is pointing out that the small self was never the real subject — that the awareness reading these words is already the one reality, momentarily mistaking itself for a person.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Pointer — a non-dual realization · Valence: Neutral — the truth to be seen · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, as the Absolute (transcendent) · Causal status: Ground / realization · Key relation: mahāvākya ‘Tat tvam asi’ — Thou art That (Chāndogya); same pointer as Uddalaka-Aruni Vidya (Dim 25).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — the truth to be seen
coordinate.key_relation: mahāvākya ‘Tat tvam asi’ — Thou art That (Chāndogya); same pointer as Uddalaka-Aruni Vidya (Dim 25).

balanced.label: Balanced — the identity recognized and lived as quiet ground

facets.overview: Genuine realization of identity with the subtle essence—the Self of all. Not intellectual claim but lived recognition.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as expression of That.
facets.emotional: Emotional connection with all beings through shared Self.
facets.cognitive: Mind realizes Tat Tvam Asi as direct experience.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes its identity with the Absolute; ‘thou art That’ ceases to be a sentence and becomes the felt ground of being.

balanced.text:
Genuine realization of identity with the subtle essence—the Self of all. Not intellectual claim but lived recognition.

Somatic: Body recognized as expression of That.

Emotional: Emotional connection with all beings through shared Self.

Cognitive: Mind realizes Tat Tvam Asi as direct experience.

Psychic: The soul recognizes its identity with the Absolute; ‘thou art That’ ceases to be a sentence and becomes the felt ground of being.
balanced.vignette: ‘That thou art’—Kavya has sat with it until it stopped being a doctrine and became a suspicion she can feel: that the ground of the world and the ground of herself are one. It quiets her, dissolving a little of the wall between her and everything else.

excess.label: In excess — the ego claiming ‘I am the Absolute’ as inflation

facets.overview: Intellectual claim ‘I am That’ becoming ego-game, spiritual narcissism.
facets.somatic: Body unchanged despite verbal claim.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity continues despite claim.
facets.cognitive: Mind playing with concept, not realizing.
facets.psychic: The great saying is reduced to a slogan; the soul asserts ‘I am That’ from the ego while still wholly identified, mistaking the concept for the realization.

excess.text:
Intellectual claim ‘I am That’ becoming ego-game, spiritual narcissism.

Somatic: Body unchanged despite verbal claim.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity continues despite claim.

Cognitive: Mind playing with concept, not realizing.

Psychic: The great saying is reduced to a slogan; the soul asserts ‘I am That’ from the ego while still wholly identified, mistaking the concept for the realization.
excess.vignette: Ravi tells everyone he’s realized ‘Thou art That,’ that he and the Absolute are one—while remaining petty, reactive, and self-absorbed. He’s mistaken understanding the sentence for living it, and the announcement is the loudest evidence he hasn’t.

deficient.label: Deficient — total identification with the separate self; the saying as mere words

facets.overview: Complete separation between ‘I’ and ‘That’—maximum dualistic suffering, no recognition of underlying unity.
facets.somatic: Body as alien from cosmos.
facets.emotional: Emotional separation from all.
facets.cognitive: Mind cannot conceive of identity with absolute.
facets.psychic: Maximum separation reigns; the soul experiences itself as utterly cut off from the divine, an island of ‘I’ in a foreign world.

deficient.text:
Complete separation between ‘I’ and ‘That’—maximum dualistic suffering, no recognition of underlying unity.

Somatic: Body as alien from cosmos.

Emotional: Emotional separation from all.

Cognitive: Mind cannot conceive of identity with absolute.

Psychic: Maximum separation reigns; the soul experiences itself as utterly cut off from the divine, an island of ‘I’ in a foreign world.
deficient.vignette: For Dan the identity of self and Absolute is a fine abstraction with no bearing on his Tuesday—he is his separate self, full stop, and the great equation finds nothing in him to touch. The wall between him and the world stands as thick as ever.

attributes.title: Kutastha (Unmoving Meditation Point)
attributes.aspect: Kutastha is the ‘unmoving’ point—the meditation focal point at the spiritual eye (between eyebrows). It is where individual consciousness meets cosmic consciousness; where inner light is seen; where Pranava is heard. Lahiri Mahasay’s interpretation: Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita IS Kutastha—the silent witnessing core that does not move while everything else does.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the unmoving witness-point (kūṭastha) — the changeless ground behind all change.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the unmoving witness-point (kūṭastha) — the changeless ground behind all change.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Consciousness established at Kutastha. Inner experiences (light, sound, vision) arise here. The witness is stable while phenomena pass through.
facets.somatic: Body relaxed with attention at the spiritual eye.
facets.emotional: Emotional stability through Kutastha-centeredness.
facets.cognitive: Mind anchored at Kutastha, witnessing all activity.
facets.psychic: The soul rests at the unmoving witness-point, the changeless anvil on which all change is struck, untouched by the flux.

balanced.text:

Emotional: Emotional stability through Kutastha-centeredness.

Cognitive: Mind anchored at Kutastha, witnessing all activity.

Psychic: The soul rests at the unmoving witness-point, the changeless anvil on which all change is struck, untouched by the flux.
balanced.vignette: Amid the day’s turbulence, Ravi has learned to rest at the unmoving point in himself—the still witness the changes pass across but never reach. From that changeless center he meets the storms without being swept away.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced concentration on the point creating eye strain and headaches.
facets.somatic: Eye strain from forced focusing.
facets.emotional: Frustration with not seeing expected phenomena.
facets.cognitive: Mental tension preventing genuine settling.
facets.psychic: The soul reifies the point into a special inner location to fixate on, missing its formless nature.

Somatic: Eye strain from forced focusing.

Emotional: Frustration with not seeing expected phenomena.

Cognitive: Mental tension preventing genuine settling.

Psychic: The soul reifies the point into a special inner location to fixate on, missing its formless nature.
excess.vignette: Sam uses ‘I am the unmoving witness’ to excuse a cold detachment—watching his own life and everyone else’s from a lofty distance, untouched, unmoved, uninvolved. The changeless witness has become a reason to stop caring.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No anchor for consciousness—mind scattered without Kutastha-grounding. All inner experiences confused.
facets.somatic: Body’s attention scattered.
facets.emotional: Emotional dispersal without inner reference point.
facets.cognitive: Mind cannot find its center, no Kutastha-experience.
facets.psychic: The soul is swept along with every change, never finding the still point within the movement.

deficient.text:
No anchor for consciousness—mind scattered without Kutastha-grounding. All inner experiences confused.

Somatic: Body’s attention scattered.

Emotional: Emotional dispersal without inner reference point.

Cognitive: Mind cannot find its center, no Kutastha-experience.

Psychic: The soul is swept along with every change, never finding the still point within the movement.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no still center at all—every change sweeps him fully away, every mood is him, every disturbance the whole of reality for as long as it lasts. The unmoving witness within is not something he’s failing to reach; it’s something he’s never glimpsed.

attributes.title: Sarvabhuteshu Atmanam (Self in All Beings)
attributes.aspect: He who perceives all beings in the Self and the Self in all beings entertains no hatred. The realization of one Self in all destroys the basis for hatred, delusion, grief.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the one Self seen in all beings (Gītā 6.29).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the one Self seen in all beings (Gītā 6.29).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of one Self in all beings—each person is a manifestation of the same Self. Hatred becomes impossible; compassion natural.
facets.somatic: Body extends warmth equally to all, no biological aversion to others.
facets.emotional: Emotional unity with all beings, freedom from in-group/out-group dynamics, universal compassion.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees the One in many, dissolution of fundamental otherness.
facets.psychic: The soul sees its own Self in all beings and all beings in the Self, and so meets the world without enmity.

balanced.text:
Recognition of one Self in all beings—each person is a manifestation of the same Self. Hatred becomes impossible; compassion natural.

Somatic: Body extends warmth equally to all, no biological aversion to others.

Emotional: Emotional unity with all beings, freedom from in-group/out-group dynamics, universal compassion.

Cognitive: Mind sees the One in many, dissolution of fundamental otherness.

Psychic: The soul sees its own Self in all beings and all beings in the Self, and so meets the world without enmity.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to see the same Self looking out of every pair of eyes—the stranger, the difficult colleague, the child—and the seeing has made him kinder. Recognizing himself in all beings, he can’t quite treat any of them as merely other.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced universal love without discrimination—accepting harm, inability to set boundaries, losing healthy individual identity.
facets.somatic: Body unable to defend against harm, passive acceptance.
facets.emotional: Emotional doormat, codependent oneness, loss of healthy individuality.
facets.cognitive: Conceptual unity without genuine realization, indiscriminate.
facets.psychic: The soul asserts oneness verbally while still recoiling from particular others, the vision unlived.

excess.text:
Forced universal love without discrimination—accepting harm, inability to set boundaries, losing healthy individual identity.

Somatic: Body unable to defend against harm, passive acceptance.

Emotional: Emotional doormat, codependent oneness, loss of healthy individuality.

Cognitive: Conceptual unity without genuine realization, indiscriminate.

Psychic: The soul asserts oneness verbally while still recoiling from particular others, the vision unlived.
excess.vignette: Karl loves ‘all beings’ in the abstract, waxing lyrical about the one Self in everyone, while treating the actual person in front of him carelessly. The cosmic vision has become a way of skipping the particular, unglamorous work of loving real people.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Perceiving fundamental separation between self and others—maximum capacity for hatred, prejudice, in-group violence.
facets.somatic: Body responses driven by tribal in-group/out-group instincts.
facets.emotional: Easy hatred, prejudice, emotional walls against ‘others.’
facets.cognitive: Mind sees radical separation, perceives others as fundamentally different.
facets.psychic: The soul sees only separate creatures, defended against a world experienced as wholly other.

deficient.text:
Perceiving fundamental separation between self and others—maximum capacity for hatred, prejudice, in-group violence.

Somatic: Body responses driven by tribal in-group/out-group instincts.

Emotional: Easy hatred, prejudice, emotional walls against ‘others.’

Cognitive: Mind sees radical separation, perceives others as fundamentally different.

Psychic: The soul sees only separate creatures, defended against a world experienced as wholly other.
deficient.vignette: For Dan other people are simply other—separate units, means to his ends, opaque surfaces—and the notion of a shared Self looking out through all of them is empty sentiment. He sees no one in anyone, and treats them accordingly.

attributes.title: Atman is Unborn (Na Jayate Na Mriyate)
attributes.aspect: The Self is not born, nor does It die. It did not come from anywhere; nothing came from It. It is unborn, eternal, everlasting, ancient. Not slain when the body is slain.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the Ātman as unborn and undying (Katha) — na jāyate na mriyate.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the Ātman as unborn and undying (Katha) — na jāyate na mriyate.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Realization of the deathless Self. Body’s death no longer terror; the essential is recognized as unborn and undying.
facets.somatic: Body lived freely knowing it is not Self.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from death-terror.
facets.cognitive: Mind grounded in the unborn, free from existential anxiety.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in what was never born and cannot die, and the fear of death loses its grip.

balanced.text:
Realization of the deathless Self. Body’s death no longer terror; the essential is recognized as unborn and undying.

Somatic: Body lived freely knowing it is not Self.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from death-terror.

Cognitive: Mind grounded in the unborn, free from existential anxiety.

Psychic: The soul rests in what was never born and cannot die, and the fear of death loses its grip.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has touched, in deep stillness, something in himself that was never born and will never die—and the touching has quietly loosened his fear of death. He tends his mortal body well and no longer clutches at it in terror.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Intellectual conviction of being unborn without realization—using the doctrine to deny embodied reality.
facets.somatic: Body neglected with ‘I am not body’ claim.
facets.emotional: Emotional bypassing through doctrinal claim.
facets.cognitive: Concept without experience, dangerous if held without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul turns deathlessness into a comforting belief to lean on rather than a realization to live.

excess.text:
Intellectual conviction of being unborn without realization—using the doctrine to deny embodied reality.

Somatic: Body neglected with ‘I am not body’ claim.

Emotional: Emotional bypassing through doctrinal claim.

Cognitive: Concept without experience, dangerous if held without realization.

Psychic: The soul turns deathlessness into a comforting belief to lean on rather than a realization to live.
excess.vignette: Convinced he’s identified with the unborn, deathless Self, Karl neglects his body and his affairs as ‘the perishable, which isn’t really me’—letting his health decay in the name of the deathless. The teaching has become a license for neglect.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete identification with body’s birth and death—maximum existential terror, no recognition of deathless dimension.
facets.somatic: Body anxieties dominate, every aging-sign threatens identity.
facets.emotional: Death-terror as undercurrent of all emotions.
facets.cognitive: Mind cannot conceive of deathless dimension.
facets.psychic: The soul takes itself to be the body that is born and dies, and lives under death’s shadow.

deficient.text:
Complete identification with body’s birth and death—maximum existential terror, no recognition of deathless dimension.

Somatic: Body anxieties dominate, every aging-sign threatens identity.

Emotional: Death-terror as undercurrent of all emotions.

Cognitive: Mind cannot conceive of deathless dimension.

Psychic: The soul takes itself to be the body that is born and dies, and lives under death’s shadow.
deficient.vignette: For Dan there is only the perishable—the aging body, the ticking clock—and the thought of death is raw terror because it means simple annihilation. The unborn ground the tradition points to is, to him, wishful comfort he can’t believe.

attributes.title: Atman is Subtler than Subtle (Anor Aniyan)
attributes.aspect: Subtler than the subtle, greater than the great—the Self dwells in the heart of beings. The desireless beholds it through serenity of senses and mind.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the Ātman as subtler than the subtle and greater than the great (aṇor aṇīyān).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the Ātman as subtler than the subtle and greater than the great (aṇor aṇīyān).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Through serenity, the Self subtler-than-subtle is beheld—simultaneously vast and minute, in the heart-cave.
facets.somatic: Body provides quiet vessel for subtle perception.
facets.emotional: Serene emotions allowing subtle revelation.
facets.cognitive: Mind quiet enough to perceive what is subtler than itself.
facets.psychic: The soul senses the Self as subtler than the subtlest and vaster than the vast, beyond the reach of every measure.

balanced.text:
Through serenity, the Self subtler-than-subtle is beheld—simultaneously vast and minute, in the heart-cave.

Somatic: Body provides quiet vessel for subtle perception.

Emotional: Serene emotions allowing subtle revelation.

Cognitive: Mind quiet enough to perceive what is subtler than itself.

Psychic: The soul senses the Self as subtler than the subtlest and vaster than the vast, beyond the reach of every measure.
balanced.vignette: In the quiet, Ravi senses a presence subtler than any thought—more intimate than the mind, closer than the breath—and rests toward it. The Self ‘subtler than the subtle’ has become, for him, a felt intimacy at the core of things.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Force-seeking the subtle through strained concentration—missing it because of the very effort.
facets.somatic: Strained body trying to perceive subtle.
facets.emotional: Anxious seeking blocks subtle perception.
facets.cognitive: Mind too active, missing the still subtle presence.
facets.psychic: The soul makes the paradox a clever idea to contemplate, missing the immediacy it points to.

excess.text:
Force-seeking the subtle through strained concentration—missing it because of the very effort.

Somatic: Strained body trying to perceive subtle.

Emotional: Anxious seeking blocks subtle perception.

Cognitive: Mind too active, missing the still subtle presence.

Psychic: The soul makes the paradox a clever idea to contemplate, missing the immediacy it points to.
excess.vignette: Sam chases ever-subtler inner experiences, convinced the more refined the phenomenon the closer he is to the Self, forever hunting the next delicate sensation. He’s literalized ‘subtler than subtle’ into a pursuit of experiences the teaching was meant to point beyond.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Caught only in gross perception—the subtle dimension entirely unknown, life lived only at material surface.
facets.somatic: Body senses only gross stimuli.
facets.emotional: Emotional life only gross reactions.
facets.cognitive: Mind only handles gross concepts, no subtlety.
facets.psychic: The soul knows only the gross and measurable, never sensing the subtle ground of itself.

deficient.text:
Caught only in gross perception—the subtle dimension entirely unknown, life lived only at material surface.

Somatic: Body senses only gross stimuli.

Emotional: Emotional life only gross reactions.

Cognitive: Mind only handles gross concepts, no subtlety.

Psychic: The soul knows only the gross and measurable, never sensing the subtle ground of itself.
deficient.vignette: Dan lives entirely at the gross surface—sensation, thought, the obvious—and the notion of a Self subtler than the mind is not something he doubts so much as has never approached. The delicate inner ground is wholly outside his experience.

attributes.title: Atman as Creator
attributes.aspect: In the beginning, Atman alone was. He thought ‘Let me create the worlds.’ From him the worlds emerged. The Self is the source, not a product, of creation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the Ātman as the source from which all proceeds.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the Ātman as the source from which all proceeds.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition that the Self is the source from which all manifestation arises. One’s own awareness is creative source.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as manifestation, not source.
facets.emotional: Emotional life as creative expression of Self.
facets.cognitive: Mind recognizes its participation in creative awareness.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the Self as the source from which its whole experienced world arises.

balanced.text:
Recognition that the Self is the source from which all manifestation arises. One’s own awareness is creative source.

Somatic: Body recognized as manifestation, not source.

Emotional: Emotional life as creative expression of Self.

Cognitive: Mind recognizes its participation in creative awareness.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the Self as the source from which its whole experienced world arises.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to sense the Self not as a passive spectator but as the very source from which his world arises—and the recognition fills him with a quiet, responsible wonder. He is less a victim of his life and more, somehow, its ground.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Megalomanic claim of being the creator—personal ego inflating to cosmic.
facets.somatic: Body claimed as source of all.
facets.emotional: Emotional grandiosity.
facets.cognitive: Confusing personal ego with cosmic Atman.
facets.psychic: The soul inflates this into a grandiose ‘I create reality,’ the ego usurping the Self’s role.

excess.text:
Megalomanic claim of being the creator—personal ego inflating to cosmic.

Somatic: Body claimed as source of all.

Emotional: Emotional grandiosity.

Cognitive: Confusing personal ego with cosmic Atman.

Psychic: The soul inflates this into a grandiose ‘I create reality,’ the ego usurping the Self’s role.
excess.vignette: Karl has decided that since the Self is the creator, HE creates reality—slipping into magical thinking, blaming others for ‘manifesting’ their misfortunes, inflating a metaphysical insight into personal omnipotence. The teaching has fed a grandiose delusion.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Taking oneself as merely created, not creative—passive existence, no recognition of source-nature.
facets.somatic: Body taken as passive product.
facets.emotional: Emotional powerlessness, victim-stance.
facets.cognitive: Mind taken as derivative, no creative source recognized.
facets.psychic: The soul feels itself the passive product of an external world, never the awareness in which that world appears.

deficient.text:
Taking oneself as merely created, not creative—passive existence, no recognition of source-nature.

Somatic: Body taken as passive product.

Emotional: Emotional powerlessness, victim-stance.

Cognitive: Mind taken as derivative, no creative source recognized.

Psychic: The soul feels itself the passive product of an external world, never the awareness in which that world appears.
deficient.vignette: For Dan he is purely an effect—buffeted by circumstance, a leaf on the wind, with no felt sense of being the source of anything. The recognition of the Self as creative ground is entirely absent, and he lives as the world’s object, never its origin.

attributes.attribute: Yajnavalkya-Maitreyi: Atman is What All is Dear
attributes.title: Yajnavalkya-Maitreyi: Atman is What All is Dear
attributes.aspect: Yajnavalkya tells his wife Maitreyi: ‘Not for the sake of the husband is the husband dear, but for the sake of the Self.’ Not for the sake of objects are they dear, but for the sake of the Self. The Self must be seen, heard, reflected upon, meditated upon.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the Self as that for whose sake all is dear; same pointer as Maitreyi Vidya (Dim 25).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the Self as that for whose sake all is dear; same pointer as Maitreyi Vidya (Dim 25).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition that what we love in everything is actually the Self. All love is ultimately Self-love at the deepest sense.
facets.somatic: Body relationships understood as Self-meetings.
facets.emotional: Emotional love deepened through recognition of Self.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees the Self as the ultimate beloved in all loves.
facets.psychic: The soul discovers that everything it loves, it loves for the sake of the Self, and traces all affection home.

balanced.text:
Recognition that what we love in everything is actually the Self. All love is ultimately Self-love at the deepest sense.

Somatic: Body relationships understood as Self-meetings.

Emotional: Emotional love deepened through recognition of Self.

Cognitive: Mind sees the Self as the ultimate beloved in all loves.

Psychic: The soul discovers that everything it loves, it loves for the sake of the Self, and traces all affection home.
balanced.vignette: Loving his wife, Ravi glimpsed what Yajnavalkya taught Maitreyi—that what he loves in her runs back to the Self that makes all things dear. It deepened his love rather than dissolving it; he cherishes her particularly and senses the one source underneath.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Selfish ‘Self-love’ justification—using doctrine to justify self-centered behavior.
facets.somatic: Body in selfish pursuits justified spiritually.
facets.emotional: Emotional manipulation under guise of Self-love.
facets.cognitive: Doctrine misused to justify ego.
facets.psychic: The soul uses ‘all for the Self’ to rationalize self-absorption and withdrawn care.

excess.text:
Selfish ‘Self-love’ justification—using doctrine to justify self-centered behavior.

Somatic: Body in selfish pursuits justified spiritually.

Emotional: Emotional manipulation under guise of Self-love.

Cognitive: Doctrine misused to justify ego.

Psychic: The soul uses ‘all for the Self’ to rationalize self-absorption and withdrawn care.
excess.vignette: Karl took ‘you love others for the Self’s sake’ as license to love no one in particular—holding everyone at a cool, philosophical remove in the name of the universal Self. The teaching meant to deepen love became his excuse to withhold it.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Loving objects/people as objects—no recognition that the Self is what makes them dear.
facets.somatic: Body in possessive relationships.
facets.emotional: Emotional possessiveness, dependency.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t recognize the Self-dimension of love.
facets.psychic: The soul loves only outer objects for themselves, never sensing the Self that makes them dear.

deficient.text:
Loving objects/people as objects—no recognition that the Self is what makes them dear.

Somatic: Body in possessive relationships.

Emotional: Emotional possessiveness, dependency.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t recognize the Self-dimension of love.

Psychic: The soul loves only outer objects for themselves, never sensing the Self that makes them dear.
deficient.vignette: Dana loves people strictly for what they give her, and the idea that her attachments reflect a deeper Self has never crossed her mind. The dearness stays transactional; there’s no felt source beneath her loves, only the getting and the fearing to lose.

attributes.title: Atman as Self-Luminous (Svayamprakasha)
attributes.aspect: Yajnavalkya to King Janaka: ‘When the sun has set, when the moon has set, when fire is out, what light does a person have here?’ ‘The Self alone is one’s light.’ By the light of the Atman alone one acts. The Self is the light of lights, self-luminous beyond all external lights.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the Ātman as self-luminous (svayam-prakāśa) — known by no other light.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the Ātman as self-luminous (svayam-prakāśa) — known by no other light.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of Atman as the self-luminous source of all perception. The light that illumines all other lights.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as illumined by inner light.
facets.emotional: Emotional perception backed by self-luminous awareness.
facets.cognitive: Mind recognizes its illumination by the inner Self.
facets.psychic: The soul knows itself as self-luminous, needing no other light to be aware of its own being.

balanced.text:
Recognition of Atman as the self-luminous source of all perception. The light that illumines all other lights.

Somatic: Body recognized as illumined by inner light.

Emotional: Emotional perception backed by self-luminous awareness.

Cognitive: Mind recognizes its illumination by the inner Self.

Psychic: The soul knows itself as self-luminous, needing no other light to be aware of its own being.
balanced.vignette: Ella has begun to notice that awareness needs nothing else to reveal it—it shines by itself, lighting every thought while needing no light of its own. Resting toward this self-luminous quality has shifted her sense of what she is.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Claiming to be the source of light for everything—megalomanic identification.
facets.somatic: Body strained in claiming cosmic-light identity.
facets.emotional: Grandiose claim of being the universal light.
facets.cognitive: Confusing individual light with cosmic Atman.
facets.psychic: The soul chases inner luminous experiences, mistaking lit-up states for the light that is awareness itself.

excess.text:
Claiming to be the source of light for everything—megalomanic identification.

Somatic: Body strained in claiming cosmic-light identity.

Emotional: Grandiose claim of being the universal light.

Cognitive: Confusing individual light with cosmic Atman.

Psychic: The soul chases inner luminous experiences, mistaking lit-up states for the light that is awareness itself.
excess.vignette: Sam hunts for inner lights and luminous visions, sure the flashier the phenomenon the closer he is, judging each meditation by its fireworks. He’s mistaken the self-luminous awareness for the lit-up experiences it silently reveals.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Believing only external lights illumine—missing the self-luminous Self that is the precondition of all perception.
facets.somatic: Body taken as illumined only by external sources.
facets.emotional: Emotional life believed to depend on external conditions.
facets.cognitive: Mind believes it knows only by external information, missing self-luminosity.
facets.psychic: The soul believes itself known only from outside, never as the self-evident awareness it is.

deficient.text:
Believing only external lights illumine—missing the self-luminous Self that is the precondition of all perception.

Somatic: Body taken as illumined only by external sources.

Emotional: Emotional life believed to depend on external conditions.

Cognitive: Mind believes it knows only by external information, missing self-luminosity.

Psychic: The soul believes itself known only from outside, never as the self-evident awareness it is.
deficient.vignette: Dan has never once wondered what it is that illumines his experience—the awareness by which he knows anything is the one thing he’s never turned toward. That it might shine by its own light is not a question he’s asked.

attributes.title: Akshara (The Imperishable)
attributes.aspect: By the command of the Akshara (Imperishable) sun and moon stand in their places, heaven and earth, moments and hours, rivers flow, etc. It is neither gross nor subtle, neither short nor long, has no smell, taste, sight, hearing, mind. It is unseen seer, unheard hearer, unthought thinker, ununderstood understander. It is the inner ruler.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the Imperishable (akṣara); same object as Akshara Vidya (Dim 25).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the Imperishable (akṣara); same object as Akshara Vidya (Dim 25).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of the Imperishable as the foundation behind all phenomena. Neither this nor that, yet ruler of all.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as functioning by command of Akshara.
facets.emotional: Emotional reverence for the unseen seer.
facets.cognitive: Mind acknowledges the unthought thinker behind thought.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in the imperishable that no quality touches, approached by clearing away all that it is not.

balanced.text:
Recognition of the Imperishable as the foundation behind all phenomena. Neither this nor that, yet ruler of all.

Somatic: Body recognized as functioning by command of Akshara.

Emotional: Emotional reverence for the unseen seer.

Cognitive: Mind acknowledges the unthought thinker behind thought.

Psychic: The soul rests in the imperishable that no quality touches, approached by clearing away all that it is not.
balanced.vignette: Through loss and change, Meera has found something in herself that does not change—an imperishable ground beneath the perishing surface—and grieves without being destroyed. The changeless gives her a place to stand while everything else moves.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Abstract intellectual claim about Akshara without realization—doctrine as substitute.
facets.somatic: Body unchanged by abstract claim.
facets.emotional: No genuine reverence, just concept.
facets.cognitive: Doctrinal grasp without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul makes negation a barren denial, arriving at blankness rather than the fullness beyond attributes.

excess.text:
Abstract intellectual claim about Akshara without realization—doctrine as substitute.

Somatic: Body unchanged by abstract claim.

Emotional: No genuine reverence, just concept.

Cognitive: Doctrinal grasp without realization.

Psychic: The soul makes negation a barren denial, arriving at blankness rather than the fullness beyond attributes.
excess.vignette: Convinced he’s the imperishable, Ravi treats his perishable body and affairs with contempt, letting them decay because ‘they’ll pass anyway.’ The meditation on the changeless has become a license to neglect the changing life he still has to live.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Phenomena taken as self-existing—no recognition of the Imperishable behind them.
facets.somatic: Body experiences taken as independent.
facets.emotional: Emotional life experienced as self-generated.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t recognize the unmoved mover behind movement.
facets.psychic: The soul clutches every perishable form as final, never reaching the changeless ground.

deficient.text:
Phenomena taken as self-existing—no recognition of the Imperishable behind them.

Somatic: Body experiences taken as independent.

Emotional: Emotional life experienced as self-generated.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t recognize the unmoved mover behind movement.

Psychic: The soul clutches every perishable form as final, never reaching the changeless ground.
deficient.vignette: Everything Dan clings to perishes, and he clings anyway, devastated by each loss as if it were the first. An imperishable ground beneath the flux is not something he doubts so much as has never glimpsed, and so he rides the wheel with nothing steady to hold.

attributes.title: The Self-Luminous Atman — the Light of Lights
attributes.aspect: The Self is svayam-prakāśa, self-luminous — it is not illumined by anything else, but is itself the light by which everything else is known. The sun, the moon, the fire, the mind: all are known by this awareness, but this awareness is known by nothing other than itself. It is, as the Upaniṣad says, the light of lights. To recognize this is to stop searching for the Self as an object — a special experience, a state to be attained — and to notice instead the ever-present knowing in which every experience, ordinary or exalted, is already appearing.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the Light of Lights — the self-luminous Ātman; kin to Jyotisham Jyotir-Vidya (Dim 25).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
