balanced.label: Balanced — resting as the aware light in which experience appears

facets.overview: Recognition that one’s own consciousness is the original light by which everything is known. Self-luminosity acknowledged.
facets.somatic: Body’s perception backed by recognition of inner light.
facets.emotional: Emotional perceiving acknowledged as illumined by inner Self.
facets.cognitive: Mind recognizes its illumination by the self-luminous Self.
facets.psychic: The soul knows itself as the self-luminous awareness by which all else is known — the light that needs no other light to be seen.

balanced.text:
Recognition that one’s own consciousness is the original light by which everything is known. Self-luminosity acknowledged.

Somatic: Body’s perception backed by recognition of inner light.

Emotional: Emotional perceiving acknowledged as illumined by inner Self.

Cognitive: Mind recognizes its illumination by the self-luminous Self.

Psychic: The soul knows itself as the self-luminous awareness by which all else is known — the light that needs no other light to be seen.
balanced.vignette: In the gap between two thoughts, Ravi notices the plain awareness that lit the last one and lights this one, itself never dark. Resting as this ‘light of lights’ has loosened his grip on the passing contents and steadied him in the constant.

excess.label: In excess — seeking the Self as a special object or peak experience

facets.overview: Claim of being the cosmic light source—megalomanic identification.
facets.somatic: Body claimed as cosmic illuminator.
facets.emotional: Grandiose emotional inflation as ‘light of the world.’
facets.cognitive: Confusing individual experience with cosmic Light.
facets.psychic: The teaching becomes an experience to chase; the soul hunts for inner lights rather than resting as the awareness that witnesses them.

excess.text:
Claim of being the cosmic light source—megalomanic identification.

Somatic: Body claimed as cosmic illuminator.

Emotional: Grandiose emotional inflation as ‘light of the world.’

Cognitive: Confusing individual experience with cosmic Light.

Psychic: The teaching becomes an experience to chase; the soul hunts for inner lights rather than resting as the awareness that witnesses them.
excess.vignette: Karl chases luminous experiences relentlessly, convinced the brighter the inner light the higher his attainment, straining after ever more spectacular visions. He’s mistaken the phenomena for the awareness that was quietly watching them all along.

deficient.label: Deficient — the Self never noticed; identity lost entirely in objects

facets.overview: Believing all illumination comes from external sources—missing the inner Light that is the precondition of all perception.
facets.somatic: Body believed to be lit only by external light.
facets.emotional: Emotional life experienced as dependent on external conditions.
facets.cognitive: Mind unaware of self-luminous awareness.
facets.psychic: The soul takes itself to be lit from outside — defined by roles, others, circumstances — never suspecting it is itself the light.

deficient.text:
Believing all illumination comes from external sources—missing the inner Light that is the precondition of all perception.

Somatic: Body believed to be lit only by external light.

Emotional: Emotional life experienced as dependent on external conditions.

Cognitive: Mind unaware of self-luminous awareness.

Psychic: The soul takes itself to be lit from outside — defined by roles, others, circumstances — never suspecting it is itself the light.
deficient.vignette: For Dan the awareness by which he sees everything is invisible, taken utterly for granted—the ‘light of lights’ is a phrase with no referent in his experience. He sees, and is never once moved to wonder what sees.

attributes.attribute: Awake in Sleep, Dreaming Without Dreaming
attributes.title: Awake in Sleep, Dreaming Without Dreaming
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra’s profound description of the Jivanmukta’s state across all three states of consciousness. In sleep, he’s not really asleep (witness remains). In dream, he’s not identified with the dreamer. In waking, he’s not entangled with objects like ordinary people. This is the Turiya-state operative through Jagrat, Svapna, Sushupti—the fourth that is the substratum.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State — an already-present condition · Valence: The fruit / goal · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit / ground · Key relation: Turīya lived — awake within sleep, dreaming without dreaming; the fourth (Dim 4).
coordinate.key_relation: Turīya lived — awake within sleep, dreaming without dreaming; the fourth (Dim 4).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Witness-awareness operative through all three states. The substratum recognized as one’s nature.
facets.somatic: Body’s three states cycled through naturally; awareness unchanged.
facets.emotional: Emotional states arising in each state but not binding.
facets.cognitive: Mind operates in waking, dreams in dream, rests in deep sleep—Self witnesses all.
facets.psychic: The soul senses the continuous awareness present even in dreamless sleep — the witness that never goes out.

balanced.text:
Witness-awareness operative through all three states. The substratum recognized as one’s nature.

Somatic: Body’s three states cycled through naturally; awareness unchanged.

Emotional: Emotional states arising in each state but not binding.

Cognitive: Mind operates in waking, dreams in dream, rests in deep sleep—Self witnesses all.

Psychic: The soul senses the continuous awareness present even in dreamless sleep — the witness that never goes out.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to sense a thread of awareness that runs through waking, dream, and even the edge of deep sleep—one witness, continuous, beneath the changing states. It leaves him less identified with any single state and quietly more free.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Claiming witness-state in all three while showing identification markers.
facets.somatic: Body still reactive to states despite claims.
facets.emotional: Emotional content of states still binding.
facets.cognitive: Mental claim without actual witness-stability.
facets.psychic: The soul chases special sleep-states and lucidity as attainments rather than recognizing the ever-present witness.

excess.text:
Claiming witness-state in all three while showing identification markers.

Somatic: Body still reactive to states despite claims.

Emotional: Emotional content of states still binding.

Cognitive: Mental claim without actual witness-stability.

Psychic: The soul chases special sleep-states and lucidity as attainments rather than recognizing the ever-present witness.
excess.vignette: Sam claims to remain fully aware even in dreamless sleep, and says so often—a claim his exhausted, ordinary mornings quietly contradict. He’s grabbed the language of continuous awareness without the attainment, and the claiming is its own delusion.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete identification with content of each state. No witness recognized.
facets.somatic: Body identified with each state successively.
facets.emotional: Emotional state-shifts taken as identity-shifts.
facets.cognitive: Mind cycles through states without witnessing presence.
facets.psychic: The soul takes its nightly oblivion as proof of its own discontinuity, never noticing what abides through it.

deficient.text:
Complete identification with content of each state. No witness recognized.

Somatic: Body identified with each state successively.

Emotional: Emotional state-shifts taken as identity-shifts.

Cognitive: Mind cycles through states without witnessing presence.

Psychic: The soul takes its nightly oblivion as proof of its own discontinuity, never noticing what abides through it.
deficient.vignette: Dan passes from waking to dream to sleep with no sense that anything connects them—each a separate world, none of them clearly his, no witness that stays. The one awareness threading the states is not even a question he’s considered.

attributes.title: Quietude (Shanti) — Already-Here State
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra’s distinctive teaching on Quietude: it is not produced, not earned, not achieved through effort. It is the natural state of consciousness; we only disturb it through false identification. When false identification with body-mind drops, quietude is recognized as one’s nature. Not a goal but a return.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State — an already-present condition · Valence: The fruit / goal · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit / ground · Key relation: the already-here peace (śānti); kin to Śama (Dim 8) and Śānti (Dim 17).
coordinate.key_relation: the already-here peace (śānti); kin to Śama (Dim 8) and Śānti (Dim 17).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Quietude recognized as natural state. Allowing rather than producing peace.
facets.somatic: Body at rest in its natural state.
facets.emotional: Emotional life as movements in quietude.
facets.cognitive: Mind quiet because we no longer disturb it through false claims.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in a peace that is not produced but discovered, already here beneath the noise.

balanced.text:
Quietude recognized as natural state. Allowing rather than producing peace.

Somatic: Body at rest in its natural state.

Emotional: Emotional life as movements in quietude.

Cognitive: Mind quiet because we no longer disturb it through false claims.

Psychic: The soul rests in a peace that is not produced but discovered, already here beneath the noise.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has stopped chasing peace and started noticing it was already here—a quiet that was present all along beneath the noise, needing only to be recognized. Resting in the already-present quietude, he’s less at war with his own mind.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced quietude becoming flat emptiness or suppression.
facets.somatic: Body forced into stillness.
facets.emotional: Emotional life suppressed in name of quietude.
facets.cognitive: Mind forced quiet through pressure.
facets.psychic: The soul clings to calm as a state to defend, mistaking a pleasant stillness for the unconditioned peace.

excess.text:
Forced quietude becoming flat emptiness or suppression.

Somatic: Body forced into stillness.

Emotional: Emotional life suppressed in name of quietude.

Cognitive: Mind forced quiet through pressure.

Psychic: The soul clings to calm as a state to defend, mistaking a pleasant stillness for the unconditioned peace.
excess.vignette: Sam performs serenity—the soft voice, the studied calm—while suppressing the agitation underneath, mistaking a managed surface for genuine peace. The already-present quiet he speaks of is buried beneath the effort of seeming to have found it.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Constant inner noise from false identification. Quietude believed impossible.
facets.somatic: Body in constant tension.
facets.emotional: Emotional turbulence continuous.
facets.cognitive: Mind in chronic agitation.
facets.psychic: The soul believes peace must be manufactured by arranging conditions, and so never finds the quiet already present.

deficient.text:
Constant inner noise from false identification. Quietude believed impossible.

Somatic: Body in constant tension.

Emotional: Emotional turbulence continuous.

Cognitive: Mind in chronic agitation.

Psychic: The soul believes peace must be manufactured by arranging conditions, and so never finds the quiet already present.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no access to any inner peace—the mind churns from waking to sleep, and stillness of any kind is a country he’s never visited. That a quietude might be already present beneath the noise is, to him, not even imaginable.

attributes.title: Sahaja Samādhi — the Natural, Already-Here State
attributes.aspect: Sahaja samādhi is the natural, spontaneous absorption — not a special trance entered and exited, but the recognition of the awareness that is always already present, in which one can rest in the midst of any activity. It is turīya lived: the discovery that the peace one was seeking through effort is the very ground from which the seeking arose, a quietude (śānti) that is not produced but uncovered, already here beneath the noise. Its mark is that it does not depend on conditions — it is as available in the marketplace as on the cushion.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State — an already-present condition · Valence: The fruit / goal · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit / ground · Key relation: the natural, already-here absorption; kin to Sahaja Avastha (Dim 26) and Sahaj Karma (Dim 16).
coordinate.key_relation: the natural, already-here absorption; kin to Sahaja Avastha (Dim 26) and Sahaj Karma (Dim 16).

balanced.label: Balanced — resting in the ever-present awareness amid activity

facets.overview: Natural samadhi-state through all activities. Sustained without effort because effortlessness IS the state.
facets.somatic: Body in continuous ease through all activities.
facets.emotional: Emotional life flows without strain.
facets.cognitive: Mind in sustained witness-state naturally.
facets.psychic: The soul abides in the natural state — absorption that needs no special posture or effort, the sacred and the ordinary become one.

balanced.text:
Natural samadhi-state through all activities. Sustained without effort because effortlessness IS the state.

Somatic: Body in continuous ease through all activities.

Emotional: Emotional life flows without strain.

Cognitive: Mind in sustained witness-state naturally.

Psychic: The soul abides in the natural state — absorption that needs no special posture or effort, the sacred and the ordinary become one.
balanced.vignette: Meera has come, over years, to rest in a natural ease—acting from her own depth without contrivance, neither forcing nor performing. The already-here state isn’t something she achieves; it’s what remains when the effort of being someone relaxes.

excess.label: In excess — mistaking a pleasant state for the stateless ground; clinging to calm

facets.overview: Forcing ‘sahaja’ through performed effortlessness. Fake naturalness.
facets.somatic: Body forcing relaxation.
facets.emotional: Emotional performance of peace.
facets.cognitive: Mental claim of sahaja without genuine recognition.
facets.psychic: ‘Naturalness’ is claimed prematurely; the soul uses ‘everything is already perfect’ to excuse the discipline it has not yet done.

excess.text:
Forcing ‘sahaja’ through performed effortlessness. Fake naturalness.

Somatic: Body forcing relaxation.

Emotional: Emotional performance of peace.

Cognitive: Mental claim of sahaja without genuine recognition.

Psychic: ‘Naturalness’ is claimed prematurely; the soul uses ‘everything is already perfect’ to excuse the discipline it has not yet done.
excess.vignette: Ravi performs ‘naturalness’—the studied spontaneity, the announced effortlessness—turning the natural state into a pose. The self-consciousness of the performance is the very proof of how far the real thing still is.

deficient.label: Deficient — awareness wholly captured by its contents; no felt ground at all

facets.overview: Every activity requires effort. Strain in every action. Never resting in natural state.
facets.somatic: Body in continuous strain.
facets.emotional: Emotional life of perpetual exertion.
facets.cognitive: Mind never knows effortless functioning.
facets.psychic: The natural state is unknown; the soul believes peace requires special conditions and never finds it in the midst of ordinary life.

deficient.text:
Every activity requires effort. Strain in every action. Never resting in natural state.

Somatic: Body in continuous strain.

Emotional: Emotional life of perpetual exertion.

Cognitive: Mind never knows effortless functioning.

Psychic: The natural state is unknown; the soul believes peace requires special conditions and never finds it in the midst of ordinary life.
deficient.vignette: Dan is entirely conditioned—every reaction a groove, no spontaneity anywhere—and the free, uncontrived way of being the teaching points to isn’t even a possibility he’s glimpsed. He runs on rails and calls it living.

attributes.attribute: World Exists On Account of You
attributes.title: World Exists On Account of You
attributes.aspect: Atmananda’s radical commentary on Ashtavakra: the world exists because YOU witness it. Without consciousness, no world is. The world is in consciousness; consciousness is not in the world. This reverses the ordinary view that places the observer in the observed-world. The observer (you) is the foundation; the world is appearance.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the world as depending on the witnessing Self.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the world as depending on the witnessing Self.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition that the world depends on consciousness, not the reverse. Profound shift in perspective.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as appearance in consciousness.
facets.emotional: Emotional world acknowledged as appearance.
facets.cognitive: Mind realizes itself as foundation, not derivative.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes that the whole experienced world hangs upon its own awareness, and stops treating the world as an alien given.

balanced.text:
Recognition that the world depends on consciousness, not the reverse. Profound shift in perspective.

Somatic: Body recognized as appearance in consciousness.

Emotional: Emotional world acknowledged as appearance.

Cognitive: Mind realizes itself as foundation, not derivative.

Psychic: The soul recognizes that the whole experienced world hangs upon its own awareness, and stops treating the world as an alien given.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to see that his whole world—the sights, the sounds, the very sense of ‘out there’—appears within his awareness, and the recognition has made him strangely responsible for how he meets it. The world exists, for him, on account of the awareness he is.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Solipsism or magical thinking—‘I create the world,’ demanding world conform to wishes.
facets.somatic: Body in magical-thinking mode.
facets.emotional: Emotional grandiosity.
facets.cognitive: Confusing individual mind with cosmic consciousness.
facets.psychic: The soul inflates this into solipsistic grandiosity, the ego imagining itself the maker of all.

excess.text:
Solipsism or magical thinking—‘I create the world,’ demanding world conform to wishes.

Somatic: Body in magical-thinking mode.

Emotional: Emotional grandiosity.

Cognitive: Confusing individual mind with cosmic consciousness.

Psychic: The soul inflates this into solipsistic grandiosity, the ego imagining itself the maker of all.
excess.vignette: Karl has slid into solipsism—‘the world exists on account of me, so others aren’t really real’—using the teaching to dismiss other people’s reality and his obligations to them. A pointer to the primacy of awareness has become a license for cruelty.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: World taken as independently real, consciousness as derivative product of brain.
facets.somatic: Body taken as fundamental, consciousness as secondary.
facets.emotional: Emotional life dependent on external reality.
facets.cognitive: Mind taken as brain-function, no recognition of fundamental consciousness.
facets.psychic: The soul feels itself a tiny object lost in a vast indifferent world, never the awareness on which the world depends.

deficient.text:
World taken as independently real, consciousness as derivative product of brain.

Somatic: Body taken as fundamental, consciousness as secondary.

Emotional: Emotional life dependent on external reality.

Cognitive: Mind taken as brain-function, no recognition of fundamental consciousness.

Psychic: The soul feels itself a tiny object lost in a vast indifferent world, never the awareness on which the world depends.
deficient.vignette: For Dan he is one small object among countless others in a world that was here before him and won’t notice when he’s gone. That his experienced world arises within his own awareness is a thought that’s never occurred to him; he is wholly the world’s object.

attributes.title: Advaita as Harmonization (Not Negation)
attributes.aspect: Krishnananda’s deep teaching from Sankara: Advaita is misunderstood as ‘plurality is unreal, only One is real.’ Actually, Advaita is HARMONIZATION—unity does not abolish plurality but integrates it. The senses, reason, intuition are not negated but integrated, each in its proper place. Higher transcends lower without rejecting it.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: non-duality as harmonization, not negation, of the many.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: non-duality as harmonization, not negation, of the many.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Authentic Advaita as integration of all levels. Senses honored, reason honored, intuition honored—each in proper place. Unity that includes diversity.
facets.somatic: Body honored even in highest practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional life honored, not dismissed.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees both unity and diversity simultaneously.
facets.psychic: The soul holds non-duality as the harmonizing of all apparent opposites, not the erasure of the world.

balanced.text:
Authentic Advaita as integration of all levels. Senses honored, reason honored, intuition honored—each in proper place. Unity that includes diversity.

Somatic: Body honored even in highest practice.

Emotional: Emotional life honored, not dismissed.

Cognitive: Mind sees both unity and diversity simultaneously.

Psychic: The soul holds non-duality as the harmonizing of all apparent opposites, not the erasure of the world.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has stopped pitting the One against the many—he sees the unity shining through the diversity, holding both without collapsing either. Advaita, for him, harmonizes his world rather than erasing it; the many are how the One appears.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Negativistic ‘Advaita’ that dismisses all plurality. World-denial that creates psychological pathology.
facets.somatic: Body neglected through ‘unity’ claim.
facets.emotional: Emotional rejection of legitimate experience.
facets.cognitive: Mental dismissal of plurality as ‘maya only.’
facets.psychic: The soul over-reconciles, using ‘all is one’ to paper over real distinctions and avoid discernment.

excess.text:
Negativistic ‘Advaita’ that dismisses all plurality. World-denial that creates psychological pathology.

Somatic: Body neglected through ‘unity’ claim.

Emotional: Emotional rejection of legitimate experience.

Cognitive: Mental dismissal of plurality as ‘maya only.’

Psychic: The soul over-reconciles, using ‘all is one’ to paper over real distinctions and avoid discernment.
excess.vignette: Sam wields non-duality as a hammer against the relative—dismissing distinctions, ethics, and ordinary reality as ‘illusion to be negated,’ harsh and world-denying. He’s mistaken harmonizing unity for a negation that leaves him cold and disconnected.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Pure pluralism with no recognition of underlying unity. Maximum fragmentation.
facets.somatic: Body experiences only fragmentation.
facets.emotional: Emotional life of pure differentiation.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees only multiplicity, no unity.
facets.psychic: The soul lives in unreconciled opposition, seeing only conflict and duality everywhere.

deficient.text:
Pure pluralism with no recognition of underlying unity. Maximum fragmentation.

Somatic: Body experiences only fragmentation.

Emotional: Emotional life of pure differentiation.

Cognitive: Mind sees only multiplicity, no unity.

Psychic: The soul lives in unreconciled opposition, seeing only conflict and duality everywhere.
deficient.vignette: For Dan there is only the many—separate things, separate people, no underlying unity anywhere. The recognition that harmonizes the one and the many finds nothing in him; his world stays fragmented, and the unity is not even a rumor.

attributes.title: Transcendence Not Negation
attributes.aspect: Krishnananda’s profound teaching: ‘There is no such thing as criticism or condemnation of values, but only a living touch given to existent conditions for the purpose of a growth into a higher state of being.’ We never negate any stage of education or practice—we transcend. The concept of negation is unknown to a real vision of life.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: transcending the world by seeing through it, not denying it.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: transcending the world by seeing through it, not denying it.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: All stages of life and practice honored as legitimate. Movement is transcendence, never negation. Growth through inclusion.
facets.somatic: Body’s various stages all honored.
facets.emotional: Emotional development honors each stage.
facets.cognitive: Mind transcends without negating earlier understandings.
facets.psychic: The soul transcends the world by seeing through it, not by denying it — engaging fully while resting in the beyond.

balanced.text:
All stages of life and practice honored as legitimate. Movement is transcendence, never negation. Growth through inclusion.

Somatic: Body’s various stages all honored.

Emotional: Emotional development honors each stage.

Cognitive: Mind transcends without negating earlier understandings.

Psychic: The soul transcends the world by seeing through it, not by denying it — engaging fully while resting in the beyond.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has found a way to rise above his circumstances without denying them—transcending the grip of his moods and roles while still fully meeting them. The transcendence doesn’t negate his life; it frees him within it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Refusing to discriminate at all—‘everything is equally valid’—missing the actual hierarchical movement.
facets.somatic: Body without progressive discipline.
facets.emotional: Emotional acceptance without discrimination.
facets.cognitive: Mental relativism missing real growth.
facets.psychic: The soul mistakes transcendence for rejection, withdrawing from life as ‘unreal.’

excess.text:
Refusing to discriminate at all—‘everything is equally valid’—missing the actual hierarchical movement.

Somatic: Body without progressive discipline.

Emotional: Emotional acceptance without discrimination.

Cognitive: Mental relativism missing real growth.

Psychic: The soul mistakes transcendence for rejection, withdrawing from life as ‘unreal.’
excess.vignette: Karl ‘transcends’ by denying—rejecting the body, the world, the relationships as beneath him, floating above an ordinary life he finds distasteful. He’s confused transcendence with escapism, and the flight upward is really a flight away.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Constant negation/criticism of previous stages. Spiritual life as rejection. Toxic spirituality.
facets.somatic: Body rejects earlier disciplines.
facets.emotional: Emotional contempt for earlier engagements.
facets.cognitive: Mental criticism of previous understandings.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly immersed in the world as final, with no sense of anything beyond it.

deficient.text:
Constant negation/criticism of previous stages. Spiritual life as rejection. Toxic spirituality.

Somatic: Body rejects earlier disciplines.

Emotional: Emotional contempt for earlier engagements.

Cognitive: Mental criticism of previous understandings.

Psychic: The soul is wholly immersed in the world as final, with no sense of anything beyond it.
deficient.vignette: Dan is wholly trapped in the immanent—his moods, his circumstances, his conditioning are the whole of reality for him, with no capacity to rise above any of it. The transcendent dimension isn’t denied by him; it’s simply never appeared.

attributes.title: Brahma-Satyam Jagan-Mithyā — the Real and the Apparent
attributes.aspect: This formula — Brahman is real, the world is apparent — is the precise statement of the non-dual relationship between the Absolute and the world. The world is not denied; it is correctly located, as a dependent appearance arising in and inseparable from the one reality, like images in a mirror or waves in the ocean. To hold this rightly is to engage the world fully while no longer mistaking it for the final ground — to invest in the wave while knowing oneself the water. The error to avoid is the contempt that calls the world ‘mere illusion’ and uses the doctrine to dismiss life’s genuine claims.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: Brahman as real, the world as apparent (mithyā); the classic Advaita formula.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: Brahman as real, the world as apparent (mithyā); the classic Advaita formula.

balanced.label: Balanced — full engagement with the world, held as dependent appearance

facets.overview: Living with awareness of three ontological levels. World engaged as mithya, Brahman recognized as Satyam, individual self known as Brahman.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in mithya world without taking it as ultimately real.
facets.emotional: Emotional life acknowledged as mithya while deeper Self is known.
facets.cognitive: Mind operates with full awareness of ontological levels.
facets.psychic: The soul holds the world lightly as appearance while resting in the one Reality; it engages fully without being deceived into taking the show as final.

balanced.text:
Living with awareness of three ontological levels. World engaged as mithya, Brahman recognized as Satyam, individual self known as Brahman.

Somatic: Body engaged in mithya world without taking it as ultimately real.

Emotional: Emotional life acknowledged as mithya while deeper Self is known.

Cognitive: Mind operates with full awareness of ontological levels.

Psychic: The soul holds the world lightly as appearance while resting in the one Reality; it engages fully without being deceived into taking the show as final.
balanced.vignette: Ravi holds it rightly: the ground is real, the passing show is real-ish—dependent, fleeting, not to be clung to but not to be despised. ‘The world is apparent’ has taught him to hold his life lightly, not to abandon it.

excess.label: In excess — world-denial; using ‘it’s all illusion’ to dodge real claims

facets.overview: Claiming world-illusion while still acting as if it’s ultimately real. Or denying functional reality entirely.
facets.somatic: Body neglected with ‘it’s all mithya’ rationalization.
facets.emotional: Emotional dismissal of legitimate experience.
facets.cognitive: Doctrinal claim without integrated understanding.
facets.psychic: The teaching becomes an excuse for disengagement; calling the world ‘unreal,’ the soul neglects its responsibilities and people as ‘mere illusion.’

excess.text:
Claiming world-illusion while still acting as if it’s ultimately real. Or denying functional reality entirely.

Somatic: Body neglected with ‘it’s all mithya’ rationalization.

Emotional: Emotional dismissal of legitimate experience.

Cognitive: Doctrinal claim without integrated understanding.

Psychic: The teaching becomes an excuse for disengagement; calling the world ‘unreal,’ the soul neglects its responsibilities and people as ‘mere illusion.’
excess.vignette: Karl uses ‘the world is unreal’ to avoid it—dodging responsibilities, dismissing others’ suffering as ‘mere appearance,’ hiding from life behind a metaphysical shrug. The teaching about the apparent has become his excuse to disengage from the actual.

deficient.label: Deficient — the apparent taken as absolute; the wave mistaken for the whole sea

facets.overview: World taken as ultimate reality. Brahman entirely abstract or denied. Self taken as separate.
facets.somatic: Body taken as ultimate self.
facets.emotional: Emotional life as final reality.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees only objects, no underlying Brahman.
facets.psychic: The soul takes the changing world as the whole of reality; with no sense of the abiding ground, it is wholly at the mercy of appearances.

deficient.text:
World taken as ultimate reality. Brahman entirely abstract or denied. Self taken as separate.

Somatic: Body taken as ultimate self.

Emotional: Emotional life as final reality.

Cognitive: Mind sees only objects, no underlying Brahman.

Psychic: The soul takes the changing world as the whole of reality; with no sense of the abiding ground, it is wholly at the mercy of appearances.
deficient.vignette: For Dan the passing world is utterly, finally real—the only real there is—and he clings to each fleeting thing as though it were permanent. The recognition that the apparent is dependent and the ground alone abiding finds no purchase; he’s devastated by every change.

attributes.title: Boundless Ocean (Cosmic Identity)
attributes.aspect: The boundless ocean is Ashtavakra’s metaphor: phenomena (thoughts, emotions, body, world) are waves that rise and fall. The Self is the ocean itself—the depth that does not change as waves come and go. To realize oneself as the ocean rather than a wave is to find true rest. The wave-identity is the source of all suffering; the ocean-identity is liberation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the Self as the boundless ocean of which beings are waves — cosmic identity.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the Self as the boundless ocean of which beings are waves — cosmic identity.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Self recognized as boundless ocean. All experiences are waves; the ocean is unchanged.
facets.somatic: Body experienced as a wave appearing in oceanic awareness.
facets.emotional: Emotions as waves in the unchanging ocean.
facets.cognitive: Thoughts as waves; the ocean is one’s nature.
facets.psychic: The soul knows itself as the boundless ocean of being, the individual waves rising and falling within it.

balanced.text:

Cognitive: Thoughts as waves; the ocean is one’s nature.

Psychic: The soul knows itself as the boundless ocean of being, the individual waves rising and falling within it.
balanced.vignette: In deep stillness, Ravi has felt the boundary of his small self grow porous, and rested, for a while, as a boundless ocean in which his little wave appears. He returns to his days gentler and larger, no longer quite believing he ends at his skin.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Grandiose ‘I am the ocean’ identification with cosmic overlay.
facets.somatic: Body claimed as cosmic.
facets.emotional: Emotional inflation.
facets.cognitive: Confusing personal expansion with cosmic ocean.
facets.psychic: The soul inflates into a grandiose cosmic self-image, the ego swelling to the size of the universe.

excess.text:
Grandiose ‘I am the ocean’ identification with cosmic overlay.

Somatic: Body claimed as cosmic.

Emotional: Emotional inflation.

Cognitive: Confusing personal expansion with cosmic ocean.

Psychic: The soul inflates into a grandiose cosmic self-image, the ego swelling to the size of the universe.
excess.vignette: After an experience of vastness, Karl came home convinced he IS the cosmic ocean—and now treats others as mere ripples of his own being, above them all. A genuine glimpse of oneness has inflated into simple grandiosity.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total identification as a tiny wave. No recognition of the ocean.
facets.somatic: Body experienced as small isolated entity.
facets.emotional: Emotional life as bounded wave-experience.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees only wave, never the ocean.
facets.psychic: The soul takes itself to be a single isolated wave, never sensing the ocean that bears it.

deficient.vignette: Dan experiences himself as a single isolated drop—sealed, separate, connected to nothing larger—and the boundless ocean of a shared identity is, to him, a poetic figure with no felt reality. He lives his whole life inside the drop.

attributes.title: Antarayami (Universal Self Within Heart)
attributes.aspect: The Universal Self resides in the heart-cave of every being. When realized in oneself, the same Self is seen as residing in all other beings. This recognition of one Antaryami in all bodies dissolves the fundamental separation that creates suffering.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the universal Self dwelling within the heart.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of one Self in all beings—the Antaryami. Profound interconnection without loss of healthy differentiation.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as one of many vehicles for one Self.
facets.emotional: Emotional connection with all beings through shared Self.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees the same Self looking through every being.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the universal Self dwelling in the heart as its own innermost being.

balanced.text:

Somatic: Body recognized as one of many vehicles for one Self.

Emotional: Emotional connection with all beings through shared Self.

Cognitive: Mind sees the same Self looking through every being.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the universal Self dwelling in the heart as its own innermost being.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to sense a presence within the cave of his own heart—an indwelling Self, quiet and steady, closer than his thoughts. Turning toward it has given him an inner companion that was there all along.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced merging with all beings—losing healthy boundaries in name of unity.
facets.somatic: Body loses individual integrity.
facets.emotional: Codependent fusion mistaken for unity.
facets.cognitive: Conceptual oneness without functional differentiation.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on inner heart-experiences, chasing the symbol rather than resting as the indweller.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.emotional: Emotional isolation despite physical presence of others.

facets.psychic: The soul feels empty at its own center, never suspecting the universal Self that lives within.

attributes.title: Isavasyam Idam Sarvam (All is Pervaded by the Lord)
attributes.aspect: All this universe must be enveloped by the Lord. Seeing all things in God and God in all things is the fundamental orientation. By such envelopment, one is protected; one does not covet what belongs to others.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: all this is pervaded by the Lord (Īśāvāsya 1).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When all is seen as pervaded by the Divine, possession-greed dissolves naturally. There is reverence in every encounter, sacredness in ordinary perception.
facets.somatic: Body interacts gently with environment, no exploitation of resources, reverent touch.
facets.emotional: Emotional warmth toward all beings, reverence as default emotional tone, freedom from possessive longing.
facets.cognitive: Mind perceives sacredness in all phenomena, sees Divine in/as everything, no fundamental separation.
facets.psychic: The soul perceives the whole world as enveloped by the Divine, and moves through it with reverence and non-grasping.

facets.emotional: False reverence as escape from real engagement, sentimental piety.
facets.cognitive: Mental dissolution of necessary distinctions, magical thinking.
facets.psychic: The soul turns the vision into pious sentiment, asserting it while still grasping and possessing.

attributes.title: Indweller in All Beings
attributes.aspect: The Self entered into the bodies through the crown of the head. This Indweller (antaryamin) is the same in all beings, looking out through every eye, hearing through every ear.

attributes.title: Antaryami (Inner Controller)
attributes.aspect: The Self residing in the heart is the inner controller (antaryami) of all functions—heart-beat, breath, mind-activity. Smaller than a grain of rice yet vaster than all worlds, this Self silently directs everything from within.

facets.cognitive: Mind acknowledges the deeper director behind all activities.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the inner controller that governs all from within as its very own Self, the antaryāmin.

attributes.title: Antaryami (Inner Controller of the Universe)
attributes.aspect: Uddalaka asks Yajnavalkya about the inner controller. Yajnavalkya: ‘He who inhabits the earth but is within it, whom the earth does not know, whose body the earth is, who controls the earth from within—is your Self, the inner controller, the immortal.’ This is repeated for all elements, senses, mind. The Antaryami is in all but unknown by all.

coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized

facets.cognitive: Mind acknowledges the deeper controller behind all functions.
facets.psychic: The soul senses the single inner ruler threading through sun, earth, and every being, holding the cosmos from within.

attributes.title: Two Birds on One Tree
attributes.aspect: The Muṇḍaka and Śvetāśvatara image: two birds, close companions, perch on the same tree. One eats the sweet and bitter fruits; the other looks on without eating. The first is the jīva, the personal self caught in enjoyment and suffering; the second is the witnessing Self, the unaffected awareness. The vision’s liberating turn is the recognition that one has always been, in truth, the watching bird — that the eating, suffering self is something witnessed, not the witness. To live from the watching bird is to be in the midst of life’s sweet and bitter fruits without being consumed by them.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the two birds — the eating jīva and the watching Self on one tree (Muṇḍaka); kin to the witness.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the two birds — the eating jīva and the watching Self on one tree (Muṇḍaka); kin to the witness.

balanced.label: Balanced — living as the witness while fully present to experience

facets.overview: Recognition of the witnessing bird (true Self) alongside the consuming bird (ego-self). Witness becomes prominent; suffering ceases.
facets.somatic: Body experiences sensations; witness-self observes them.
facets.emotional: Emotional experiences continue; witness remains free.
facets.cognitive: Mind’s processes witnessed by the unmoved second bird.
facets.psychic: The soul begins to notice the second bird — the witness that watches, untouched, while the first eats the fruits of action; identification starts to shift toward the watcher.

balanced.text:

balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to identify less with the bird that anxiously eats the fruits of life and more with the one that simply watches, serene, from the same branch. The shift of identity—from the doer to the witness—has quietly freed him from the frantic pecking of his own desires.

excess.label: In excess — a detached ‘witnessing’ that becomes dissociation or coldness

facets.somatic: Body’s experiences denied claiming witnessing.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactions present but denied through claimed witnessing.
facets.cognitive: Pseudo-witness, intellectual claim without realization.
facets.psychic: The witness is turned into a pose; the soul performs ‘detachment,’ watching itself watching, rather than genuinely resting as awareness.

excess.text:

attributes.title: Quantum Flight to the Absolute (Instantaneous Liberation)
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra’s revolutionary claim: liberation is not a gradual achievement but an instantaneous quantum flight. The seeker is here in the phenomenal world; in the next moment, established in the Absolute. Both the world and the ‘I’ dissolve into nothingness. This contradicts all gradualist paths—instantaneous awakening is the central claim.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State — an already-present condition · Valence: The fruit / goal · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit / ground · Key relation: instantaneous liberation — the immediate leap to the Absolute (no gradual path).
coordinate.key_relation: instantaneous liberation — the immediate leap to the Absolute (no gradual path).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition that liberation is available NOW, in this moment, through direct understanding. Not delayed by sadhana but immediate recognition.
facets.somatic: Body lives ordinary life while consciousness has taken the flight.
facets.emotional: Emotional life continues but no longer binds.
facets.cognitive: Mind has shifted from time-bound to timeless orientation.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes that liberation can be instantaneous — a sudden, total shift of identity needing no gradual ladder.

balanced.text:
Recognition that liberation is available NOW, in this moment, through direct understanding. Not delayed by sadhana but immediate recognition.

Somatic: Body lives ordinary life while consciousness has taken the flight.

Emotional: Emotional life continues but no longer binds.

Cognitive: Mind has shifted from time-bound to timeless orientation.

Psychic: The soul recognizes that liberation can be instantaneous — a sudden, total shift of identity needing no gradual ladder.
balanced.vignette: Ravi holds his practice with an openness to sudden grace—knowing realization isn’t only earned inch by inch but can arrive, whole, in a single clear recognition. He works steadily and stays open to the flash, neither forcing nor foreclosing it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Casual claim of having taken the quantum flight while still showing every marker of unawakened state.
facets.somatic: Body still reactive despite claims.
facets.emotional: Emotional bondage continues underneath.
facets.cognitive: Mental claim without genuine recognition.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on the dream of a single dramatic leap, neglecting the ripening that makes the leap possible.

excess.text:
Casual claim of having taken the quantum flight while still showing every marker of unawakened state.

Somatic: Body still reactive despite claims.

Emotional: Emotional bondage continues underneath.

Cognitive: Mental claim without genuine recognition.

Psychic: The soul fixates on the dream of a single dramatic leap, neglecting the ripening that makes the leap possible.
excess.vignette: Ben wants enlightenment by Friday—skipping the patient work, chasing the ‘instant awakening’ he’s read about, hopping teacher to teacher for the shortcut. He’s turned the possibility of sudden grace into a consumer’s impatience, and the grasping bars the door.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Belief that liberation requires lifetimes of sadhana. Endless postponement.
facets.somatic: Body in eternal preparation mode.
facets.emotional: Emotional life of postponed freedom.
facets.cognitive: Mind cannot conceive of immediate awakening.
facets.psychic: The soul believes freedom is impossibly far, reachable only by endless effort, and never opens to the immediate possibility.

deficient.text:
Belief that liberation requires lifetimes of sadhana. Endless postponement.

Somatic: Body in eternal preparation mode.

Emotional: Emotional life of postponed freedom.

Cognitive: Mind cannot conceive of immediate awakening.

Psychic: The soul believes freedom is impossibly far, reachable only by endless effort, and never opens to the immediate possibility.
deficient.vignette: For Dan liberation, if it exists at all, is infinitely far off—lifetimes away, impossible for someone like him—and so he never even begins. The teaching that the Absolute can be reached in a single leap finds no faith in him; he’s ruled out the summit before the first step.

attributes.title: Repose in Self (Atma-Vishrama)
attributes.aspect: Atma-vishrama is repose in one’s own Self—the final cessation of seeking. The seeker has come home; the wanderer has reached the destination. This is not metaphorical rest but actual cessation of all spiritual ambition, all becoming, all striving. The Self is its own resting place. Whoever rests in Self rests in everything.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State — an already-present condition · Valence: The fruit / goal · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit / ground · Key relation: resting in the Self (ātma-viśrāma) — liberation as effortless repose.
coordinate.key_relation: resting in the Self (ātma-viśrāma) — liberation as effortless repose.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Genuine repose—all seeking has ceased because the seeker IS the sought. Profound rest.
facets.somatic: Body in unforced ease.
facets.emotional: Emotional life rests in its source.
facets.cognitive: Mind has come home; no further wandering.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in its own being as in a home, the restless seeking finally laid down.

balanced.text:
Genuine repose—all seeking has ceased because the seeker IS the sought. Profound rest.

Somatic: Body in unforced ease.

Emotional: Emotional life rests in its source.

Cognitive: Mind has come home; no further wandering.

Psychic: The soul rests in its own being as in a home, the restless seeking finally laid down.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has learned to rest in the Self—to drop, in the middle of the day, into a quiet inner ease that asks nothing and lacks nothing. The repose isn’t withdrawal; it’s a place he returns to that leaves him steadier when he re-engages.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Sleepy ‘repose’ that’s actually spiritual laziness disguised as resting in Self.
facets.somatic: Body in actual sloth.
facets.emotional: Emotional flatness called peace.
facets.cognitive: Mental dullness disguised as rest.
facets.psychic: The soul turns repose into passive withdrawal, using ‘resting in the Self’ to avoid life.

excess.text:
Sleepy ‘repose’ that’s actually spiritual laziness disguised as resting in Self.

Somatic: Body in actual sloth.

Emotional: Emotional flatness called peace.

Cognitive: Mental dullness disguised as rest.

Psychic: The soul turns repose into passive withdrawal, using ‘resting in the Self’ to avoid life.
excess.vignette: Sam uses ‘resting in the Self’ to justify doing nothing—the dishes, the duties, the hard conversations all left undone in the name of ‘abiding.’ The genuine repose has become a spiritual-sounding cover for plain avoidance.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Constant seeking, never resting. The seeker continues forever without arriving.
facets.somatic: Body in eternal pursuit.
facets.emotional: Emotional life never at rest.
facets.cognitive: Mind always seeking, never resting.
facets.psychic: The soul knows no inner rest, driven ceaselessly outward in search of a ground it already carries within.

deficient.text:
Constant seeking, never resting. The seeker continues forever without arriving.

Somatic: Body in eternal pursuit.

Emotional: Emotional life never at rest.

Cognitive: Mind always seeking, never resting.

Psychic: The soul knows no inner rest, driven ceaselessly outward in search of a ground it already carries within.
deficient.vignette: Dan never rests—inwardly always striving, grasping, on to the next thing, with no capacity to simply abide in himself. The repose in the Self the teaching describes is a country he’s never entered; even his stillness is restless.

attributes.title: Separation from Body-Mind (Direct Method)
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra’s radical instruction: just separate yourself from body and mind and rest in consciousness. Immediately you are happy, peaceful, free. No process, no gradual purification—immediate liberation through correct cognitive shift. The body-mind continues; you simply don’t take it as Self.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: State — an already-present condition · Valence: The fruit / goal · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit / ground · Key relation: the direct method — disidentifying the Self from body and mind.
coordinate.key_relation: the direct method — disidentifying the Self from body and mind.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Immediate dis-identification with body-mind. Resting in consciousness while body-mind continues to function.
facets.somatic: Body functions without being taken as self.
facets.emotional: Emotions arise without being claimed as ‘mine.’
facets.cognitive: Mind operates while consciousness rests as itself.
facets.psychic: The soul practices discerning itself as the awareness distinct from body and mind, loosening the knot of identification.

balanced.text:
Immediate dis-identification with body-mind. Resting in consciousness while body-mind continues to function.

Somatic: Body functions without being taken as self.

Emotional: Emotions arise without being claimed as ‘mine.’

Cognitive: Mind operates while consciousness rests as itself.

Psychic: The soul practices discerning itself as the awareness distinct from body and mind, loosening the knot of identification.
balanced.vignette: Practicing the direct discernment—‘I am not this body, not this mind, but the awareness of them’—Ravi has loosened his identification with the very things that used to define and imprison him. The separation frees him: he has a body and a mind now, rather than being them.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Verbally claiming separation while still emotionally identified. Spiritual bypassing.
facets.somatic: Body still treated as self despite words.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity continues unchanged.
facets.cognitive: Mental claim of separation without actual shift.
facets.psychic: The soul turns separation into cold dissociation, splitting from its own embodiment rather than witnessing it.

excess.text:
Verbally claiming separation while still emotionally identified. Spiritual bypassing.

Somatic: Body still treated as self despite words.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity continues unchanged.

Cognitive: Mental claim of separation without actual shift.

Psychic: The soul turns separation into cold dissociation, splitting from its own embodiment rather than witnessing it.
excess.vignette: Karl has ‘separated from the body-mind’ into a cold dissociation—neglecting his health, numb to his feelings, floating above an embodied life he now disdains. The discernment meant to free him has become an exile from his own aliveness.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total fusion with body-mind. No separation conceivable. Maximum bondage.
facets.somatic: Body as ultimate self-identity.
facets.emotional: Emotions as final reality.
facets.cognitive: Mind as ultimate self.
facets.psychic: The soul is fused without remainder to body and mind, unable even to conceive of itself apart from them.

deficient.text:
Total fusion with body-mind. No separation conceivable. Maximum bondage.

Somatic: Body as ultimate self-identity.

Emotional: Emotions as final reality.

Cognitive: Mind as ultimate self.

Psychic: The soul is fused without remainder to body and mind, unable even to conceive of itself apart from them.
deficient.vignette: Dan is wholly welded to his body and mind—their sensations are him, their thoughts are him, their moods are the whole of reality—with no capacity to step back and witness any of it. The freeing discernment isn’t failed; it’s never been attempted.

attributes.title: Jīvanmukti — Liberation as Already-Realized
attributes.aspect: Jīvanmukti is liberation while still embodied — and in its most radical Upanishadic and Aṣṭāvakra form, it is presented not as a distant goal but as one’s already-existing nature, merely unrecognized. The seeker is not building freedom but discovering that the awareness they already are was never bound. This is the vision’s most liberating and most easily counterfeited claim: rightly held, it ends the endless postponement of peace; wrongly held, it lets the ego declare itself already free and excuse the work that recognition actually requires.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State — an already-present condition · Valence: The fruit / goal · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit / ground · Key relation: liberation-while-living — freedom already realized in this body.
coordinate.key_relation: liberation-while-living — freedom already realized in this body.

balanced.label: Balanced — freedom recognized as present nature, expressed in lived equanimity

facets.overview: Living recognition of one’s eternal liberation. Activities continue without identification with doership.
facets.somatic: Body lives out its karmic momentum.
facets.emotional: Emotional life unfolds without binding the Self.
facets.cognitive: Mind operates while consciousness rests in its always-free nature.
facets.psychic: The soul knows freedom as already its nature, here and now; liberation is not postponed to after death but lived in this very body.

balanced.text:
Living recognition of one’s eternal liberation. Activities continue without identification with doership.

Somatic: Body lives out its karmic momentum.

Emotional: Emotional life unfolds without binding the Self.

Cognitive: Mind operates while consciousness rests in its always-free nature.

Psychic: The soul knows freedom as already its nature, here and now; liberation is not postponed to after death but lived in this very body.
balanced.vignette: Meera lives an ordinary life—job, family, dishes—and something in her is quietly, already free, unhooked from the deep anxiety that drives most striving. Her liberation isn’t a future event; it’s a present ease she brings to the most mundane afternoon.

excess.label: In excess — premature claim of enlightenment; bypass dressed as already-free

facets.overview: Premature claim of jivanmukti while still bound. Doctrine without realization.
facets.somatic: Body still reactive despite claims.
facets.emotional: Emotional bondage continues underneath claim.
facets.cognitive: Mental performance of liberation.
facets.psychic: ‘I am already free’ becomes a bypass; the soul declares liberation to avoid the work, mistaking the idea of freedom for the fact of it.

excess.text:
Premature claim of jivanmukti while still bound. Doctrine without realization.

Somatic: Body still reactive despite claims.

Emotional: Emotional bondage continues underneath claim.

Cognitive: Mental performance of liberation.

Psychic: ‘I am already free’ becomes a bypass; the soul declares liberation to avoid the work, mistaking the idea of freedom for the fact of it.
excess.vignette: Sam announces he’s a jivanmukta—liberated while living—and expects to be treated as one, while reacting to slights and cravings like anyone. He’s claimed the crown from the foothills, and the announcement is the surest sign he hasn’t earned it.

deficient.label: Deficient — liberation imagined as forever distant; peace endlessly postponed

facets.overview: Believing liberation requires death (videhamukti only). Endless waiting for some future event.
facets.somatic: Body experienced as ultimately limiting.
facets.emotional: Emotional life of postponed liberation.
facets.cognitive: Mind never conceives liberation in living.
facets.psychic: The soul believes freedom is forever distant — only after death, or only for others — and so never looks for what is already present.

deficient.text:
Believing liberation requires death (videhamukti only). Endless waiting for some future event.

Somatic: Body experienced as ultimately limiting.

Emotional: Emotional life of postponed liberation.

Cognitive: Mind never conceives liberation in living.

Psychic: The soul believes freedom is forever distant — only after death, or only for others — and so never looks for what is already present.
deficient.vignette: For Dan freedom, if real at all, comes only after death, in some other realm—never here, never now, never in this ordinary life. That liberation could be a present reality, lived on an ordinary Tuesday, isn’t a hope he’s failed to reach; it’s one he’s never held.

attributes.title: Vac (Speech as Creative Power)
attributes.aspect: Vac is the cosmic creative speech-principle. Beyond mere human language, it is the Word-vibration that creates worlds. The Vedas are crystallisations of Vac; human speech a tiny fraction. Recognizing Vac transforms one’s relationship with words: each word becomes a creative act, each speech an exercise of cosmic power.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a cosmogonic principle · Valence: Neutral — a creative truth · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Cosmic (Ādhidaivika), realized individually · Causal status: Cause / ground · Key relation: speech (Vāc) as creative power — the word that brings forth worlds.
coordinate.key_relation: speech (Vāc) as creative power — the word that brings forth worlds.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Speech used with awareness of its cosmic creative nature. Each word becomes deliberate, sacred.
facets.somatic: Voice used reverently, recognized as cosmic instrument.
facets.emotional: Emotional dimension of speech respected, no careless words.
facets.cognitive: Mind orchestrates speech with awareness of its creative power.
facets.psychic: The soul reveres the creative power of the word, recognizing speech as a force that shapes worlds.

balanced.text:
Speech used with awareness of its cosmic creative nature. Each word becomes deliberate, sacred.

Somatic: Voice used reverently, recognized as cosmic instrument.

Emotional: Emotional dimension of speech respected, no careless words.

Cognitive: Mind orchestrates speech with awareness of its creative power.

Psychic: The soul reveres the creative power of the word, recognizing speech as a force that shapes worlds.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to feel the creative weight of his speech—that his words shape worlds, bless or wound, build or dissolve—and he uses them with care and truth. Language, for him, is a sacred power, and he wields it as one.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Verbal manipulation believing one can create reality through mere words—magical thinking.
facets.somatic: Voice strained by attempted ‘power-speech.’
facets.emotional: Emotional manipulation through claimed verbal magic.
facets.cognitive: Mental claim that one’s words have cosmic power.
facets.psychic: The soul inflates the power of its own words, mistaking verbal facility for creative truth.

excess.text:
Verbal manipulation believing one can create reality through mere words—magical thinking.

Somatic: Voice strained by attempted ‘power-speech.’

Emotional: Emotional manipulation through claimed verbal magic.

Cognitive: Mental claim that one’s words have cosmic power.

Psychic: The soul inflates the power of its own words, mistaking verbal facility for creative truth.
excess.vignette: Karl believes his words literally manifest reality—that saying it makes it so—and slips into magical thinking, blaming others for ‘speaking’ their misfortunes into being. The truth that speech is creative has swollen into a superstitious omnipotence.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Words used carelessly without recognition of their power. Casual speech damaging self and others.
facets.somatic: Voice misused in gossip, complaint, untruth.
facets.emotional: Emotional hurt caused by careless words.
facets.cognitive: Mind unaware of speech as creative power.
facets.psychic: The soul treats speech as empty noise, deaf to the creative and sacred dimension of the word.

deficient.text:
Words used carelessly without recognition of their power. Casual speech damaging self and others.

Somatic: Voice misused in gossip, complaint, untruth.

Emotional: Emotional hurt caused by careless words.

Cognitive: Mind unaware of speech as creative power.

Psychic: The soul treats speech as empty noise, deaf to the creative and sacred dimension of the word.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s words fall carelessly—thoughtless, cruel, or empty—with no felt sense that they carry any power at all. The creative, sacred dimension of speech is invisible to him, and he scatters words like litter, oblivious to the worlds they make and unmake.

attributes.title: OM as Everything (Sarvam Aum)
attributes.aspect: OM—this syllable is all this. Whatever was, is, will be—all is OM. What is beyond past, present, future—that too is OM. Everything verily is Brahman; this Self is Brahman.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a cosmogonic principle · Valence: Neutral — a creative truth · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Cosmic (Ādhidaivika), realized individually · Causal status: Cause / ground · Key relation: OM (Praṇava) as all that is; kin to Udgitha Vidya (Dim 25) and Praṇava (Dim 11).
coordinate.key_relation: OM (Praṇava) as all that is; kin to Udgitha Vidya (Dim 25) and Praṇava (Dim 11).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Realization that OM encompasses all of reality—past, present, future, and beyond. The Self is OM is Brahman.
facets.somatic: Body resonates with universal Om.
facets.emotional: Emotional reverence for the all-encompassing nature.
facets.cognitive: Mind recognizes Om as the totality.
facets.psychic: The soul takes OM as the sonic emblem of the whole — past, present, future, and what is beyond — gathering all into one syllable.

balanced.text:
Realization that OM encompasses all of reality—past, present, future, and beyond. The Self is OM is Brahman.

Somatic: Body resonates with universal Om.

Emotional: Emotional reverence for the all-encompassing nature.

Cognitive: Mind recognizes Om as the totality.

Psychic: The soul takes OM as the sonic emblem of the whole — past, present, future, and what is beyond — gathering all into one syllable.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to sense the whole of things as a single vibration—every sound, every form a note of one cosmic hum he names OM. It’s given the world a sacred resonance; the ordinary clatter of his day is, faintly, music.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Intellectual identification of everything with Om without realization—doctrine without experience.
facets.somatic: Mechanical Om practice without depth.
facets.emotional: Conceptual reverence without genuine devotion.
facets.cognitive: Doctrinal claim without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on the sound for its own sake, chanting mechanically without the recognition it carries.

excess.text:
Intellectual identification of everything with Om without realization—doctrine without experience.

Cognitive: Doctrinal claim without realization.

Psychic: The soul fixates on the sound for its own sake, chanting mechanically without the recognition it carries.
excess.vignette: Sam chants OM compulsively, counting rounds like currency, half-believing the sound itself will magically fix his life. The living recognition of all-as-vibration has hardened into a superstitious tic, and the mechanical repetition drains the very resonance it seeks.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No recognition of Om’s universal significance—seeing all as separate fragments.
facets.somatic: Body without sacred-sound dimension.
facets.emotional: Missing the unifying recognition.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees only fragmentation.
facets.psychic: The soul has no sacred sound to gather the whole, and reality stays scattered into disconnected parts.

deficient.text:
No recognition of Om’s universal significance—seeing all as separate fragments.

Emotional: Missing the unifying recognition.

Cognitive: Mind sees only fragmentation.

Psychic: The soul has no sacred sound to gather the whole, and reality stays scattered into disconnected parts.
deficient.vignette: For Dan sound is just sound—vibrating air, nothing sacred in it—and the notion that all reality is one cosmic syllable is empty mysticism. The sacred resonance others sense in everything is, to him, simply noise.

attributes.title: Mrityu (Death as Creative Principle)
attributes.aspect: In Brihadaranyaka’s cosmology, creation begins with Mrityu (Death) or hunger—the cosmic principle of consumption-desire. This ‘hunger’ creates more and more objects to consume. The intensity of desire equals the intensity of separation. Recognizing this principle in oneself transforms one’s relationship with desire and death.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a cosmogonic principle · Valence: Neutral — a creative truth · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Cosmic (Ādhidaivika), realized individually · Causal status: Cause / ground · Key relation: death (Mṛtyu) as a creative principle — the hunger from which creation proceeds.
coordinate.key_relation: death (Mṛtyu) as a creative principle — the hunger from which creation proceeds.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition that desire/hunger is the cosmic creative principle. Living with this awareness transforms desire from bondage to creative force.
facets.somatic: Body’s hungers recognized as cosmic creative impulse.
facets.emotional: Emotional hungers seen as expressions of creative principle.
facets.cognitive: Mind understands desire as creative cosmic force, not personal flaw.
facets.psychic: The soul contemplates how death itself is woven into creation, the great recycler through which life renews.

balanced.text:
Recognition that desire/hunger is the cosmic creative principle. Living with this awareness transforms desire from bondage to creative force.

Somatic: Body’s hungers recognized as cosmic creative impulse.

Emotional: Emotional hungers seen as expressions of creative principle.

Cognitive: Mind understands desire as creative cosmic force, not personal flaw.

Psychic: The soul contemplates how death itself is woven into creation, the great recycler through which life renews.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to hold death as part of the creative turning of things—not an enemy but a principle, the counter-stroke that makes room for the new. The framing has quieted his fear; he sees his own ending as one beat in a vast rhythm.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Glorification of hunger/desire as ‘creative’—using doctrine to justify endless consumption.
facets.somatic: Body in endless creative-consumption.
facets.emotional: Emotional gluttony justified spiritually.
facets.cognitive: Mental rationalizing of desire-driven life.
facets.psychic: The soul becomes morbidly fixated on death, losing life to preoccupation with its end.

excess.text:
Glorification of hunger/desire as ‘creative’—using doctrine to justify endless consumption.

Somatic: Body in endless creative-consumption.

Emotional: Emotional gluttony justified spiritually.

Cognitive: Mental rationalizing of desire-driven life.

Psychic: The soul becomes morbidly fixated on death, losing life to preoccupation with its end.
excess.vignette: Sam has grown morbidly fascinated with death—romanticizing it, brooding on dissolution, using its ‘creative’ meaning to indulge a dark withdrawal from life. The teaching’s equanimity has curdled into a fixation that pulls him under.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Hunger seen as enemy to be defeated—missing that it IS the creative cosmic force.
facets.somatic: Body forced into anti-desire suppression.
facets.emotional: Emotional war with natural desires.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees only personal failure in desire, no cosmic perspective.
facets.psychic: The soul denies death entirely, and so cannot grasp the rhythm of renewal it drives.

deficient.text:
Hunger seen as enemy to be defeated—missing that it IS the creative cosmic force.

Somatic: Body forced into anti-desire suppression.

Emotional: Emotional war with natural desires.

Cognitive: Mind sees only personal failure in desire, no cosmic perspective.

Psychic: The soul denies death entirely, and so cannot grasp the rhythm of renewal it drives.
deficient.vignette: For Dan death is sheer annihilation, an enemy to be denied and never contemplated—and the terror of it, unexamined, sits under everything he does. That death might be a creative principle in a larger cycle is a thought he cannot let himself entertain.

attributes.title: Prajapati’s Hunger Creating Food (Annada)
attributes.aspect: The cosmic eating-eaten cycle: Prajapati creates beings to be His food, and Himself becomes the food for these beings. Everything that eats is being eaten; everything that is eaten is also eating. This cosmic vision transforms the act of eating into participation in cosmic sacrament.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Framework — a cosmogonic principle · Valence: Neutral — a creative truth · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Cosmic (Ādhidaivika), realized individually · Causal status: Cause / ground · Key relation: Prajāpati’s hunger creating food (annada) — desire as the engine of manifestation.
coordinate.key_relation: Prajāpati’s hunger creating food (annada) — desire as the engine of manifestation.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Eating recognized as cosmic sacrament—participating in Prajapati’s eating Himself. Reverence in every meal.
facets.somatic: Body eats with awareness of cosmic eating-eaten cycle.
facets.emotional: Emotional reverence in food preparation and consumption.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees every meal as cosmic ritual.
facets.psychic: The soul sees in the cosmic hunger that creates food the deep truth that creation feeds upon itself in sacred reciprocity.

balanced.text:
Eating recognized as cosmic sacrament—participating in Prajapati’s eating Himself. Reverence in every meal.

Cognitive: Mind sees every meal as cosmic ritual.

balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to feel the sacred interdependence of eating and being eaten—the whole living web in which each is food and feeder—and eats now with a quiet gratitude. The ordinary meal has become, for him, a participation in a vast reciprocal giving.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Spiritualizing eating into compulsive ritualism—missing actual nourishment for sacred theater.
facets.somatic: Body forced into ritualistic eating that becomes neurotic.
facets.emotional: Emotional over-investment in food rituals.
facets.cognitive: Mind obsessed with sacred-eating technique.
facets.psychic: The soul over-literalizes the myth, lost in cosmological detail rather than the truth it figures.

Psychic: The soul over-literalizes the myth, lost in cosmological detail rather than the truth it figures.
excess.vignette: Karl has turned ‘creation arises from hunger’ into a justification for his own greed—consuming without limit as though appetite were a cosmic virtue. A teaching about sacred interdependence has become his license for endless taking.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Eating as mere biological consumption with no cosmic dimension. Disconnect from sacred eating-eaten cycle.
facets.somatic: Body in mechanical eating without awareness.
facets.emotional: Emotional disconnect from food.
facets.cognitive: Mind treats food as fuel only.
facets.psychic: The soul takes eating and being eaten as mere biology, blind to the sacrificial web of nourishment.

deficient.text:
Eating as mere biological consumption with no cosmic dimension. Disconnect from sacred eating-eaten cycle.

Somatic: Body in mechanical eating without awareness.

Emotional: Emotional disconnect from food.

Cognitive: Mind treats food as fuel only.

Psychic: The soul takes eating and being eaten as mere biology, blind to the sacrificial web of nourishment.
deficient.vignette: For Dan food is just fuel and eating just a transaction—no web, no reciprocity, no sacred cycle of nourishment anywhere in it. The living interdependence the teaching reveals is invisible to him; he consumes, and senses no giving in it at all.

attributes.title: Madhu Vidya (Honey Doctrine)
attributes.aspect: The sun yonder is the honey of the gods. Sky is its hive; rays are its bees. Eastern rays are the eastern honey-cells; Rig verses are the bees; Brahman is the flower. Through this honey, all worlds nourish each other. This is the doctrine of interconnected sweetness—all beings are honey for each other.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the honey doctrine of cosmic sweetness; same object as Madhu Vidyā (Dim 25).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the honey doctrine of cosmic sweetness; same object as Madhu Vidyā (Dim 25).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of cosmic interconnection—all beings are ‘honey’ (nourishment) for each other. Sweet web of mutual sustaining.
facets.somatic: Body participates in the honey-exchange of nourishment.
facets.emotional: Emotional sweetness, recognition of mutual giving.
facets.cognitive: Mind perceives the great web of mutual nourishment.
facets.psychic: The soul tastes the mutual sweetness by which each being is honey to every other, the world one web of reciprocal nourishment.

balanced.text:
Recognition of cosmic interconnection—all beings are ‘honey’ (nourishment) for each other. Sweet web of mutual sustaining.

Somatic: Body participates in the honey-exchange of nourishment.

Emotional: Emotional sweetness, recognition of mutual giving.

Cognitive: Mind perceives the great web of mutual nourishment.

Psychic: The soul tastes the mutual sweetness by which each being is honey to every other, the world one web of reciprocal nourishment.
