balanced.vignette: The Honey Doctrine—that all beings are honey to one another, each the sweetness and source of every other—has become, for Meera, a felt sense of deep interdependence. She moves through her day aware of the web she’s woven into, and it makes her humbler and more grateful.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Sentimental sweetness denying real darkness—claiming everything is honey while ignoring pain.
facets.somatic: Body in false sweetness, ignoring real conditions.
facets.emotional: Saccharine emotionality denying reality.
facets.cognitive: Bypass of difficulty through honey-doctrine.
facets.psychic: The vision slides into sentimentality, denying real difficulty under forced positivity.

excess.text:
Sentimental sweetness denying real darkness—claiming everything is honey while ignoring pain.

Somatic: Body in false sweetness, ignoring real conditions.

Emotional: Saccharine emotionality denying reality.

Cognitive: Bypass of difficulty through honey-doctrine.

Psychic: The vision slides into sentimentality, denying real difficulty under forced positivity.
excess.vignette: Karl waxes lyrical about universal interconnection while treating the actual people in front of him carelessly, in love with the cosmic sweetness and blind to the person he just interrupted. The Honey Doctrine has become a beautiful abstraction that excuses him from ordinary kindness.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Perceiving all as struggle, predation, takings—no recognition of the honey-exchange.
facets.somatic: Body experiences only depletion, never the sweetness.
facets.emotional: Emotional isolation, no sense of mutual sweetness.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees only taking, never the mutual nourishment.
facets.psychic: The soul perceives only struggle, with no sweetness recognized in the web of life.

deficient.text:
Perceiving all as struggle, predation, takings—no recognition of the honey-exchange.

Somatic: Body experiences only depletion, never the sweetness.

Emotional: Emotional isolation, no sense of mutual sweetness.

Cognitive: Mind sees only taking, never the mutual nourishment.

Psychic: The soul perceives only struggle, with no sweetness recognized in the web of life.
deficient.vignette: Dan experiences himself as an isolated unit among separate units—connected to nothing, sweet to no one—and the teaching that all beings are mutually sustaining honey is a lovely idea with no felt reality. He tastes only separateness.

attributes.title: Madhu Vidya (Honey Doctrine in Brihadaranyaka)
attributes.aspect: This earth is honey for all beings, and all beings are honey for this earth. The waters are honey for all beings, and all beings are honey for the waters. Fire, air, sun, quarters, moon, lightning, thunder, ether, dharma, truth, mankind, Self—each is honey for all others. The infinite, immutable, all-pervading Atman is the honey of all.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the honey doctrine (Bṛhadāraṇyaka) — all beings mutually honey; cosmic reciprocity.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the honey doctrine (Bṛhadāraṇyaka) — all beings mutually honey; cosmic reciprocity.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of universal interconnection—each being nourishing every other being. The cosmic mutuality of sweetness.
facets.somatic: Body participates in cosmic exchange of sustaining.
facets.emotional: Emotional sweetness toward all beings, recognition of mutual giving.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees the cosmic web of mutual nourishment.
facets.psychic: The soul contemplates how each element is honey to all others, self and cosmos bound in radiant mutual dependence.

balanced.text:
Recognition of universal interconnection—each being nourishing every other being. The cosmic mutuality of sweetness.

Somatic: Body participates in cosmic exchange of sustaining.

Emotional: Emotional sweetness toward all beings, recognition of mutual giving.

Cognitive: Mind sees the cosmic web of mutual nourishment.

Psychic: The soul contemplates how each element is honey to all others, self and cosmos bound in radiant mutual dependence.
balanced.vignette: Reading the great dialogue, Ravi felt it land: that he is honey to all beings and all beings are honey to him, each sustaining and sustained. The mutual sweetness has become a lived sense of belonging to the whole, and it has softened how he meets everyone.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Sentimental denial of difficulty—claiming everything is honey while ignoring suffering and conflict.
facets.somatic: False sweetness in body interactions.
facets.emotional: Saccharine emotionality bypassing real darkness.
facets.cognitive: Doctrinal sweetness without genuine realization.
facets.psychic: The soul collects the correspondences as doctrine, missing the living interdependence they point to.

excess.text:
Sentimental denial of difficulty—claiming everything is honey while ignoring suffering and conflict.

Somatic: False sweetness in body interactions.

Emotional: Saccharine emotionality bypassing real darkness.

Cognitive: Doctrinal sweetness without genuine realization.

Psychic: The soul collects the correspondences as doctrine, missing the living interdependence they point to.
excess.vignette: Sam quotes the Honey Doctrine at length, marveling aloud at cosmic reciprocity, and cannot manage ordinary reciprocity with his own family. The grand vision of mutual sweetness has become a way of feeling connected to everything while connecting with no one in particular.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Perceiving all as struggle, predation, exploitation—no recognition of the cosmic honey-exchange.
facets.somatic: Body experiences only depletion.
facets.emotional: Emotional life of isolation and struggle.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees only taking, never the cosmic giving.
facets.psychic: The soul feels itself isolated and self-sufficient, blind to the mutual nourishing of all things.

deficient.text:
Perceiving all as struggle, predation, exploitation—no recognition of the cosmic honey-exchange.

Somatic: Body experiences only depletion.

Emotional: Emotional life of isolation and struggle.

Cognitive: Mind sees only taking, never the cosmic giving.

Psychic: The soul feels itself isolated and self-sufficient, blind to the mutual nourishing of all things.
deficient.vignette: For Dan the notion that he is honey to all and all to him is sentimental nonsense—he owes nothing, is owed nothing, and the great web of mutual nourishment is invisible to him. He stands, as he sees it, alone, sweet to no one and needing no one.

attributes.title: Awareness as Heart (Hridaya-Bodha)
attributes.aspect: Byrom’s poetic framing of Ashtavakra Gita: awareness is the single mystery that enfolds all other mysteries, including the secret of the separate self. As a child of seven, Byrom became aware of awareness itself by the sea. The Gita answers: this awareness you noticed is YOU. The heart of everything is awareness recognizing itself.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: awareness as the Heart (hṛdaya-bodha) — the seat of the Self.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: awareness as the Heart (hṛdaya-bodha) — the seat of the Self.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of awareness as one’s heart-nature. All experience appearing in this awareness.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as appearance in awareness-heart.
facets.emotional: Emotional life as movements in awareness.
facets.cognitive: Mind’s contents appearing in awareness-heart.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes awareness itself as the true heart, the seat from which ‘I’ arises and to which it returns.

balanced.text:
Recognition of awareness as one’s heart-nature. All experience appearing in this awareness.

Somatic: Body recognized as appearance in awareness-heart.

Emotional: Emotional life as movements in awareness.

Cognitive: Mind’s contents appearing in awareness-heart.

Psychic: The soul recognizes awareness itself as the true heart, the seat from which ‘I’ arises and to which it returns.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to rest awareness in the heart—sensing consciousness not as a thing in the head but as a warm, spacious knowing centered in the chest. The heart-awareness has made him at once more grounded and more open; he thinks less and senses more.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Romantic idealization of ‘awareness’ without genuine recognition.
facets.somatic: Body’s relationship with awareness still external.
facets.emotional: Sentimental approach to awareness.
facets.cognitive: Conceptual love of ‘awareness’ without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on the physical heart-center and its sensations, chasing the symbol over the awareness.

excess.text:
Romantic idealization of ‘awareness’ without genuine recognition.

Somatic: Body’s relationship with awareness still external.

Emotional: Sentimental approach to awareness.

Cognitive: Conceptual love of ‘awareness’ without realization.

Psychic: The soul fixates on the physical heart-center and its sensations, chasing the symbol over the awareness.
excess.vignette: Sam has sentimentalized the heart-teaching into a performance of feeling—the theatrical openness, the announced tenderness—mistaking emotional display for the quiet awareness the teaching means. The heart he speaks of so much is buried under the show of it.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Awareness taken as one capacity among others. No recognition of its primacy.
facets.somatic: Body taken as more fundamental than awareness.
facets.emotional: Emotional life taken as more real.
facets.cognitive: Mind taken as more primary than awareness.
facets.psychic: The soul locates itself only in the head’s thinking, never finding the deeper seat of awareness.

deficient.text:
Awareness taken as one capacity among others. No recognition of its primacy.

Somatic: Body taken as more fundamental than awareness.

Emotional: Emotional life taken as more real.

Cognitive: Mind taken as more primary than awareness.

Psychic: The soul locates itself only in the head’s thinking, never finding the deeper seat of awareness.
deficient.vignette: Dan lives entirely in his head—analyzing, calculating, disconnected from any felt center in the heart. The awareness centered in the heart is a dimension he’s never entered, and he meets his life as a problem to be solved rather than a presence to be felt.

attributes.title: My Own Splendor (Sva-Mahima-Bodha)
attributes.aspect: Janaka’s culminating recognition: the splendor he sought outside was always his own. The Self is not lacking—it is the very splendor that all things shine with. To recognize ‘my own splendor’ is to stop seeking what is already present as one’s nature. This is the ultimate beauty—being radiantly oneself.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the world as one’s own splendor (sva-mahimā) — all as the Self’s glory.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the world as one’s own splendor (sva-mahimā) — all as the Self’s glory.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of one’s own splendor as the source of all beauty. Glorious self-being.
facets.somatic: Body radiates the inner splendor.
facets.emotional: Emotional life expressing inner glory.
facets.cognitive: Mind acknowledges its own splendor as Self.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the whole magnificent display as its own splendor, neither vain nor diminished.

balanced.text:
Recognition of one’s own splendor as the source of all beauty. Glorious self-being.

Somatic: Body radiates the inner splendor.

Emotional: Emotional life expressing inner glory.

Cognitive: Mind acknowledges its own splendor as Self.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the whole magnificent display as its own splendor, neither vain nor diminished.
balanced.vignette: In a rare clear moment, Ravi glimpsed the sheer splendor of the awareness he is—vast, luminous, untouched—and the glimpse humbled rather than inflated him. ‘My own glory’ turned out to belong not to his ego but to the Self that his ego had been obscuring.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Egoic claim of personal greatness as ‘splendor.’ Spiritual narcissism.
facets.somatic: Body in self-aggrandizing display.
facets.emotional: Emotional grandiosity.
facets.cognitive: Mental claim of greatness without realization.
facets.psychic: The ego appropriates the splendor, swelling into self-aggrandizement.

excess.text:
Egoic claim of personal greatness as ‘splendor.’ Spiritual narcissism.

Somatic: Body in self-aggrandizing display.

Emotional: Emotional grandiosity.

Cognitive: Mental claim of greatness without realization.

Psychic: The ego appropriates the splendor, swelling into self-aggrandizement.
excess.vignette: Karl has grabbed ‘my own splendor’ for the ego—strutting his supposed magnificence, above the ordinary, specially glorious. He’s mistaken the impersonal splendor of the Self for a personal distinction, and the inflation is precisely what the recognition should dissolve.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Self perceived as poor, inadequate, lacking. Endless seeking of splendor outside.
facets.somatic: Body in chronic inadequacy.
facets.emotional: Emotional poverty mindset.
facets.cognitive: Mind never recognizes its own splendor.
facets.psychic: The soul feels small and unremarkable, blind to the magnificence that is its own nature.

deficient.text:
Self perceived as poor, inadequate, lacking. Endless seeking of splendor outside.

Somatic: Body in chronic inadequacy.

Emotional: Emotional poverty mindset.

Cognitive: Mind never recognizes its own splendor.

Psychic: The soul feels small and unremarkable, blind to the magnificence that is its own nature.
deficient.vignette: Dan feels no splendor anywhere in himself—only smallness, inadequacy, a low sense that he doesn’t much matter. The recognition of an inherent glory at the core of his being is not something he doubts so much as has never once tasted; the light is on, and he’s never looked.

attributes.title: Bhakti Washes Karma (Daily Confidence)
attributes.aspect: One of the Bhagavata’s central teachings: bhakti washes away karma. This contradicts views that karma is iron-clad fate. Sincere daily devotion progressively cleanses karmic accumulation. In daily life this is enormously freeing: past mistakes, regrets, perceived bad karma are not iron destiny — current devotional practice transforms them. There is always a way forward.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the daily confidence that devotion dissolves karma; kin to Bhakti (Dim 10).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the daily confidence that devotion dissolves karma; kin to Bhakti (Dim 10).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Trust in transformative power of bhakti. Past mistakes do not define future; present devotion transforms karmic patterns.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in present devotion regardless of past.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from regret through present practice.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t despair about past — bhakti now cleanses.
facets.psychic: The soul trusts that sincere devotion can dissolve the residue of past action, and so moves forward unburdened.

balanced.text:
Trust in transformative power of bhakti. Past mistakes do not define future; present devotion transforms karmic patterns.

Somatic: Body engaged in present devotion regardless of past.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from regret through present practice.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t despair about past — bhakti now cleanses.

Psychic: The soul trusts that sincere devotion can dissolve the residue of past action, and so moves forward unburdened.
balanced.vignette: Ravi carries a quiet confidence that sincere devotion lightens the weight of his past—not as a loophole but as a real turning of the heart toward the good. The faith frees him from paralyzing guilt, so he can make amends and move forward rather than drown.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Cheap-grace mentality — ‘I can do anything because bhakti will wash it.’ Spiritual license.
facets.somatic: Body engaging in harmful patterns expecting bhakti-clean.
facets.emotional: Emotional irresponsibility rationalized.
facets.cognitive: Mental abuse of doctrine.
facets.psychic: The soul uses ‘devotion washes all’ as license, presuming grace while neglecting effort and amends.

excess.text:
Cheap-grace mentality — ‘I can do anything because bhakti will wash it.’ Spiritual license.

Somatic: Body engaging in harmful patterns expecting bhakti-clean.

Emotional: Emotional irresponsibility rationalized.

Cognitive: Mental abuse of doctrine.

Psychic: The soul uses ‘devotion washes all’ as license, presuming grace while neglecting effort and amends.
excess.vignette: Sam uses ‘devotion washes all karma’ as a cheap grace—harming people, then chanting it away, treating his practice as a laundering service for a life he never actually changes. The teaching of transformative devotion has become his excuse for un-transformation.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Paralyzed by past karma. Believing ‘I’m too damaged.’ No trust in transformative practice. Maximum despair.
facets.somatic: Body lives under weight of perceived karma.
facets.emotional: Emotional life crushed by regret.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees past as immutable destiny.
facets.psychic: The soul feels crushed under the weight of its past, with no confidence that anything can lighten it.

deficient.text:
Paralyzed by past karma. Believing ‘I’m too damaged.’ No trust in transformative practice. Maximum despair.

Somatic: Body lives under weight of perceived karma.

Emotional: Emotional life crushed by regret.

Cognitive: Mind sees past as immutable destiny.

Psychic: The soul feels crushed under the weight of its past, with no confidence that anything can lighten it.
deficient.vignette: Dan is crushed under the weight of his past—every mistake a permanent stain, no possibility of grace or renewal, only the grinding ledger of guilt. That sincere devotion might lighten and turn the heart is a mercy he can’t believe in, and so he carries it all alone.

attributes.title: Mandukya as Sole Necessary Upanishad
attributes.aspect: Mandukya is uniquely positioned as the COMPLETE teaching in 12 verses. Per Muktika Upanishad: if Kaivalya is sought, Mandukya alone is sufficient. The 10 principal, 32 important, and 108 minor Upanishads are progressively for those who don’t get it from Mandukya. This places Mandukya as the apex.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the Māṇḍūkya as the one Upaniṣad sufficient for liberation; the four states (Dim 4).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the Māṇḍūkya as the one Upaniṣad sufficient for liberation; the four states (Dim 4).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of Mandukya’s exceptional position. Devoting extra study to these 12 verses with full attention.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in deep Mandukya practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional reverence for this concise apex teaching.
facets.cognitive: Mind devoted to penetrating the 12 verses fully.
facets.psychic: The soul finds in the Māṇḍūkya’s map of the four states a complete and sufficient guide to its own depths.

balanced.text:
Recognition of Mandukya’s exceptional position. Devoting extra study to these 12 verses with full attention.

Somatic: Body engaged in deep Mandukya practice.

Emotional: Emotional reverence for this concise apex teaching.

Cognitive: Mind devoted to penetrating the 12 verses fully.

Psychic: The soul finds in the Māṇḍūkya’s map of the four states a complete and sufficient guide to its own depths.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has taken the essential teaching to heart—the four states, the Self as their ground—and finds in that one compact map everything he needs, working it deeply rather than skimming a hundred texts. The focus has borne fruit that his old scattering never did.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Exclusivism—dismissing all other Upanishads in favor of Mandukya only. Missing the broader context.
facets.somatic: Body only engaged with one text.
facets.emotional: Emotional fixation on Mandukya alone.
facets.cognitive: Mental rejection of other texts.
facets.psychic: The soul over-venerates the text as a fetish, clinging to the map rather than traveling the territory.

excess.text:
Exclusivism—dismissing all other Upanishads in favor of Mandukya only. Missing the broader context.

Somatic: Body only engaged with one text.

Emotional: Emotional fixation on Mandukya alone.

Cognitive: Mental rejection of other texts.

Psychic: The soul over-venerates the text as a fetish, clinging to the map rather than traveling the territory.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned ‘the Mandukya is the only Upanishad you need’ into a dogmatic minimalism—dismissing all other teachings, all other paths, with a proud narrowness. The rightful emphasis on essentials has become a closed, contemptuous certainty.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Treating Mandukya as just one short Upanishad among many. Missing its apex significance.
facets.somatic: Body gives Mandukya same casual study as others.
facets.emotional: Emotional indifference to its uniqueness.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t recognize its special status.
facets.psychic: The soul has no orienting map of consciousness, and wanders its own states without understanding.

deficient.text:
Treating Mandukya as just one short Upanishad among many. Missing its apex significance.

Somatic: Body gives Mandukya same casual study as others.

Emotional: Emotional indifference to its uniqueness.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t recognize its special status.

Psychic: The soul has no orienting map of consciousness, and wanders its own states without understanding.
deficient.vignette: Dan skims everything and lands on nothing—a hundred teachings sampled, none taken to heart, the essential map never studied deeply enough to use. Scattered across the whole spiritual marketplace, he’s grasped no single thing firmly enough to be changed by it.

attributes.title: Kena Ishitam (Who Directs the Senses?)
attributes.aspect: By whom willed and directed does the mind fall on its objects? By whom commanded does prana move? This opening question reverses ordinary perception—asking what lies BEHIND the senses, the mind, the vital force.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: ‘by whom directed?’ (Kena) — the Self as the power behind the senses.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: ‘by whom directed?’ (Kena) — the Self as the power behind the senses.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Inquiry into the source of mental activity awakens awareness of the deeper consciousness behind sensory experience.
facets.somatic: Body’s functions traced back to their source, not taken as self-acting.
facets.emotional: Emotional life seen as movement, with awareness of the moved-by behind it.
facets.cognitive: Mind investigates its own source, opens to the consciousness behind.
facets.psychic: The soul turns the great question back upon itself — who is the awareness behind eye and ear? — and inquiry begins.

balanced.text:
Inquiry into the source of mental activity awakens awareness of the deeper consciousness behind sensory experience.

Somatic: Body’s functions traced back to their source, not taken as self-acting.

Emotional: Emotional life seen as movement, with awareness of the moved-by behind it.

Cognitive: Mind investigates its own source, opens to the consciousness behind.

Psychic: The soul turns the great question back upon itself — who is the awareness behind eye and ear? — and inquiry begins.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to ask the deep question—what is it that moves the senses, that hears behind the hearing, that knows behind the mind?—and the inquiry has drawn him toward the awareness that was never itself an object. The question has become a doorway inward.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive analytical inquiry that prevents direct experience—endless asking ‘who/what’ without realization.
facets.somatic: Body neglected in obsessive inquiry.
facets.emotional: Emotional disconnection through over-analysis.
facets.cognitive: Mind trapped in inquiry-loops, never resting in realization.
facets.psychic: The soul makes the question a clever puzzle to debate, never letting it pierce to the felt seer.

excess.text:
Excessive analytical inquiry that prevents direct experience—endless asking ‘who/what’ without realization.

Somatic: Body neglected in obsessive inquiry.

Emotional: Emotional disconnection through over-analysis.

Cognitive: Mind trapped in inquiry-loops, never resting in realization.

Psychic: The soul makes the question a clever puzzle to debate, never letting it pierce to the felt seer.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned ‘who directs the senses?’ into an intellectual parlor game—delighting in the paradox, debating the answer, and never once actually resting as the awareness the question points to. He plays with the pointer and never follows it.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Living entirely on the surface—senses, thoughts, emotions all taken as self-acting, no inquiry into their source.
facets.somatic: Body’s activities taken as self-existing.
facets.emotional: Emotions appearing/disappearing without curiosity about their source.
facets.cognitive: Mind never asks the foundational question of who is conscious.
facets.psychic: The soul never asks who is aware, taking its faculties as self-operating and itself as merely their sum.

deficient.text:
Living entirely on the surface—senses, thoughts, emotions all taken as self-acting, no inquiry into their source.

Somatic: Body’s activities taken as self-existing.

Emotional: Emotions appearing/disappearing without curiosity about their source.

Cognitive: Mind never asks the foundational question of who is conscious.

Psychic: The soul never asks who is aware, taking its faculties as self-operating and itself as merely their sum.
deficient.vignette: Dan has never wondered what lies behind his own perceiving—the seer, the hearer, the knower are utterly taken for granted, and he is wholly absorbed in the seen. The great question of what animates the senses has simply never occurred to him.

attributes.title: Tadvanam (Adorable to All Beings)
attributes.aspect: Brahman is known as Tadvanam—the one to whom all beings pray (consciously or unconsciously). When one knows Brahman as Tadvanam, all beings yearn toward one.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: Tadvanam — That which is adorable to all beings (Kena).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: Tadvanam — That which is adorable to all beings (Kena).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition that all beings unconsciously yearn for the same Brahman one is realizing. This creates universal connection.
facets.somatic: Body radiates the realized presence, attracts beings naturally.
facets.emotional: Recognition that all hearts yearn for the same source.
facets.cognitive: Mind recognizes the universal yearning behind all activities.
facets.psychic: The soul senses the one Reality that all beings adore beneath their countless particular longings.

balanced.text:
Recognition that all beings unconsciously yearn for the same Brahman one is realizing. This creates universal connection.

Somatic: Body radiates the realized presence, attracts beings naturally.

Emotional: Recognition that all hearts yearn for the same source.

Cognitive: Mind recognizes the universal yearning behind all activities.

Psychic: The soul senses the one Reality that all beings adore beneath their countless particular longings.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to sense that what he truly loves in everything he loves is a single adorable core—the Self that is dear in all things because it is the source of all dearness. It has deepened his loves rather than dissolving them; he adores the particular and senses the one.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Ego-claim of being ‘beloved by all beings’—spiritual narcissism, demanding adoration.
facets.somatic: Body postures demanding worship.
facets.emotional: Inflated sense of being adored.
facets.cognitive: Confusing personal magnetism for the universal Brahman.
facets.psychic: The soul intellectualizes the teaching, missing the love it both names and awakens.

excess.text:
Ego-claim of being ‘beloved by all beings’—spiritual narcissism, demanding adoration.

Somatic: Body postures demanding worship.

Emotional: Inflated sense of being adored.

Cognitive: Confusing personal magnetism for the universal Brahman.

Psychic: The soul intellectualizes the teaching, missing the love it both names and awakens.
excess.vignette: Karl craves adoration for his ego—positioning himself as the one to be revered, mistaking the impersonal lovable Self the teaching describes for his own personal specialness. He wants to be Tadvanam, the adored, rather than to recognize it as the Self of all.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No recognition of universal yearning—each being seen as isolated, hostile, or indifferent. No unity of purpose.
facets.somatic: Body in defensive postures against others.
facets.emotional: Sense of isolation, no shared yearning recognized.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees only individual desires, no underlying universal yearning.
facets.psychic: The soul knows only scattered cravings, with no sense of the single Adorable they all secretly seek.

deficient.text:
No recognition of universal yearning—each being seen as isolated, hostile, or indifferent. No unity of purpose.

Somatic: Body in defensive postures against others.

Emotional: Sense of isolation, no shared yearning recognized.

Cognitive: Mind sees only individual desires, no underlying universal yearning.

Psychic: The soul knows only scattered cravings, with no sense of the single Adorable they all secretly seek.
deficient.vignette: For Dan there is no adorable core to anything—things are just things, useful or not, and the notion of a lovable Self at the heart of all is empty sentiment. He loves nothing deeply, senses no dearness at the center of the world, and the adorable ground stays invisible.

attributes.title: Two Selves in Heart-Cave (Jiva and Ishvara)
attributes.aspect: Two Selves dwell in the same heart-cave—Ishvara (universal Self, all-knowing, all-powerful) and Jiva (individual self, limited in knowledge and power). They are like original and reflection, light and shadow. The Jiva is enslaved by avidya; Ishvara controls maya. Yet both inhabit the same place.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the two selves in the heart-cave — jīva and Īśvara (Katha); kin to the two birds.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the two selves in the heart-cave — jīva and Īśvara (Katha); kin to the two birds.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition that both selves coexist in the heart-cave. The individual self can recognize its source in the universal Self.
facets.somatic: Body provides the cave where both selves dwell.
facets.emotional: Emotional awareness of both limited self and universal Self within.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees the relationship between individual and universal—light and shadow of one source.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the two birds in the heart-cave — the individual self and the Lord — and turns toward the silent witness.

balanced.text:
Recognition that both selves coexist in the heart-cave. The individual self can recognize its source in the universal Self.

Somatic: Body provides the cave where both selves dwell.

Emotional: Emotional awareness of both limited self and universal Self within.

Cognitive: Mind sees the relationship between individual and universal—light and shadow of one source.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the two birds in the heart-cave — the individual self and the Lord — and turns toward the silent witness.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to sense two presences in the cave of his heart—his own small self and, quietly beside it, the larger divine Self—in a relationship of trust and gradual surrender. The inner companionship has changed his solitude; he is, he feels, accompanied from within.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Claiming identity with Ishvara prematurely while still functioning as ego—megalomanic confusion.
facets.somatic: Body claimed as cosmic in ego-mode.
facets.emotional: Emotional grandiosity confusing the two selves.
facets.cognitive: Conceptual identification with Ishvara without genuine realization.
facets.psychic: The soul turns the image into a rigid dualistic doctrine, fixing the two as permanently separate.

excess.text:
Claiming identity with Ishvara prematurely while still functioning as ego—megalomanic confusion.

Somatic: Body claimed as cosmic in ego-mode.

Emotional: Emotional grandiosity confusing the two selves.

Cognitive: Conceptual identification with Ishvara without genuine realization.

Psychic: The soul turns the image into a rigid dualistic doctrine, fixing the two as permanently separate.
excess.vignette: Karl has collapsed the two selves into one—declaring his individual self simply IS the Lord, inflating the jiva into Ishvara, ego crowned as God. The subtle inner relationship the teaching describes has become a grandiose identification.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Recognition only of Jiva—the limited self taken as final reality. No glimpse of the universal Self within.
facets.somatic: Body as isolated individual entity.
facets.emotional: Emotional life confined to individual concerns.
facets.cognitive: Mind unaware of the universal Self residing in the same cave.
facets.psychic: The soul knows only the eating bird of personal experience, blind to its silent companion.

deficient.text:
Recognition only of Jiva—the limited self taken as final reality. No glimpse of the universal Self within.

Somatic: Body as isolated individual entity.

Emotional: Emotional life confined to individual concerns.

Cognitive: Mind unaware of the universal Self residing in the same cave.

Psychic: The soul knows only the eating bird of personal experience, blind to its silent companion.
deficient.vignette: For Dan there’s only his individual self in the heart—no divine presence beside it, no larger Self to relate to, just the familiar churn of his own mind. The two-birds companionship of jiva and Ishvara is, to him, an empty poetic image.

attributes.title: Inverted Tree of Life (Urdhva-Mula Ashvattha)
attributes.aspect: Eternal is the Ashvattha tree with roots above (in Brahman) and branches below (manifestation). ‘Ashvattha’ means ‘not the same tomorrow’—ever-changing. The tree appears solid but is in constant transformation. All worlds are in this tree; none can go beyond. The roots (Brahman) is the source; the branches (manifest world) are the expression.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the inverted aśvattha — roots above, branches below (Gītā 15); the cosmos rooted in Brahman.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the inverted aśvattha — roots above, branches below (Gītā 15); the cosmos rooted in Brahman.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of life as inverted tree with roots in transcendent source. Understanding that all manifestation hangs from a higher source.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as branch of the inverted tree, source above.
facets.emotional: Emotional life seen as branch-phenomenon, source elsewhere.
facets.cognitive: Mind grasps the tree-structure of reality, source above, manifestation below.
facets.psychic: The soul perceives existence as a tree rooted above in the Absolute and branching below, and so seeks its source upward.

balanced.text:
Recognition of life as inverted tree with roots in transcendent source. Understanding that all manifestation hangs from a higher source.

Somatic: Body recognized as branch of the inverted tree, source above.

Emotional: Emotional life seen as branch-phenomenon, source elsewhere.

Cognitive: Mind grasps the tree-structure of reality, source above, manifestation below.

Psychic: The soul perceives existence as a tree rooted above in the Absolute and branching below, and so seeks its source upward.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to see his life as a tree rooted not in the world below but in the transcendent above—the visible branches dependent on an invisible source. The vision loosens his grip on the branches; he tends his worldly life while sensing its true root elsewhere.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Rejection of the tree (manifestation) entirely—wanting only roots, denying branches. Escapist spirituality.
facets.somatic: Body neglected as ‘branches to escape.’
facets.emotional: Emotional life dismissed in pursuit of ‘roots only.’
facets.cognitive: Mental rejection of all manifestation.
facets.psychic: The soul gets lost in elaborating the cosmic symbol, mistaking the diagram for the reality.

excess.text:
Rejection of the tree (manifestation) entirely—wanting only roots, denying branches. Escapist spirituality.

Somatic: Body neglected as ‘branches to escape.’

Emotional: Emotional life dismissed in pursuit of ‘roots only.’

Cognitive: Mental rejection of all manifestation.

Psychic: The soul gets lost in elaborating the cosmic symbol, mistaking the diagram for the reality.
excess.vignette: Karl has taken ‘cut down the tree of worldly life’ as license for a harsh renunciation—slashing at his relationships, duties, and pleasures as entanglements to be severed. The teaching about the tree’s transcendent root has become his rationale for a cold rejection of life.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Seeing only the tree (manifestation), no awareness of inverted root above. The tree taken as self-existing.
facets.somatic: Body taken as self-existing branch.
facets.emotional: Emotional life as ungrounded.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees only manifestation, no source above.
facets.psychic: The soul sees only the branches — the visible world — with no sense of the hidden root that sustains them.

deficient.text:
Seeing only the tree (manifestation), no awareness of inverted root above. The tree taken as self-existing.

Somatic: Body taken as self-existing branch.

Emotional: Emotional life as ungrounded.

Cognitive: Mind sees only manifestation, no source above.

Psychic: The soul sees only the branches — the visible world — with no sense of the hidden root that sustains them.
deficient.vignette: For Dan life is rooted entirely in the world—in matter, status, survival—with no transcendent source above and no branches that lead anywhere beyond. The inverted tree, rooted in the Absolute, is an image with no purchase; he’s planted wholly in the soil of the finite.

attributes.title: Bhuma Vidya (The Infinite)
attributes.aspect: Narada is taught: in the finite is no happiness; only in the Infinite (Bhuma) is happiness. ‘That in which one sees nothing else, hears nothing else, knows nothing else—That is Infinite.’ Bhuma is below, above, behind, before, right, left—it is all this.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the Infinite (Bhūma) as alone joy; same object as Bhūma Vidyā (Dim 25).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the Infinite (Bhūma) as alone joy; same object as Bhūma Vidyā (Dim 25).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition that finite pursuits cannot satisfy—only the Infinite brings true happiness. Orientation toward Bhuma.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in finite world while recognizing limits.
facets.emotional: Emotional understanding that happiness requires Bhuma.
facets.cognitive: Mind oriented toward the Infinite, finite engagements wisely contextualized.
facets.psychic: The soul tastes the Infinite in which alone there is happiness, and ceases seeking fullness in the finite.

balanced.text:
Recognition that finite pursuits cannot satisfy—only the Infinite brings true happiness. Orientation toward Bhuma.

Somatic: Body engaged in finite world while recognizing limits.

Emotional: Emotional understanding that happiness requires Bhuma.

Cognitive: Mind oriented toward the Infinite, finite engagements wisely contextualized.

Psychic: The soul tastes the Infinite in which alone there is happiness, and ceases seeking fullness in the finite.
balanced.vignette: Chasing satisfactions for decades, Amara finally understood in her body that only the infinite satisfies, and no finite thing ever will. It didn’t embitter her; it freed her—she enjoys the small pleasures without demanding they be more than they are, her deep hunger pointed rightly at last.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Rejection of all finite engagement, demanding immediate infinite—escapist spirituality.
facets.somatic: Body neglected in name of Infinite.
facets.emotional: Emotional escape from finite responsibilities.
facets.cognitive: Premature claim of having Bhuma.
facets.psychic: The contemplation of vastness becomes flight from finite duty, the Infinite used as a bypass.

excess.text:
Rejection of all finite engagement, demanding immediate infinite—escapist spirituality.

Somatic: Body neglected in name of Infinite.

Emotional: Emotional escape from finite responsibilities.

Cognitive: Premature claim of having Bhuma.

Psychic: The contemplation of vastness becomes flight from finite duty, the Infinite used as a bypass.
excess.vignette: Ravi took ‘only the infinite satisfies’ as permission to despise the finite—rejecting ordinary joys as beneath a seeker of the Infinite, world-rejecting and starved amid a feast he won’t touch. The teaching that reorients hunger has become an escapist contempt for life.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Endless pursuit of finite happinesses—addiction, accumulation, never recognizing Bhuma as the true source.
facets.somatic: Body in compulsive pursuit of finite pleasures.
facets.emotional: Emotional addiction to finite satisfactions.
facets.cognitive: Mind never opens to the Infinite dimension.
facets.psychic: The soul chases one limited happiness after another, never having tasted the boundless.

deficient.text:
Endless pursuit of finite happinesses—addiction, accumulation, never recognizing Bhuma as the true source.

Somatic: Body in compulsive pursuit of finite pleasures.

Emotional: Emotional addiction to finite satisfactions.

Cognitive: Mind never opens to the Infinite dimension.

Psychic: The soul chases one limited happiness after another, never having tasted the boundless.
deficient.vignette: Dan is certain the next finite thing will finally do it—the next raise, purchase, win—and each, attained, evaporates into hunger for the next. The recognition that no finite thing can ever satisfy has never landed, and he runs the endless, exhausting chase.

attributes.title: Sat Vidya (Pure Being Inquiry)
attributes.aspect: Uddalaka teaches: ‘In the beginning, this was Sat alone, one only without a second. Some say it was Asat (non-existence). But how can existence come from non-existence? Sat alone was; it desired to become many. From Sat, fire was emitted; from fire, water; from water, food. All this has Sat as its root, support, and abode.’

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a non-dual vision · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: pure Being inquiry; same object as Sat-Vidya (Dim 25).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: pure Being inquiry; same object as Sat-Vidya (Dim 25).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of pure Being (Sat) as the foundation of all manifestation. Direct realization of one’s existence-nature.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as expression of Sat.
facets.emotional: Emotional grounding in pure being.
facets.cognitive: Mind realizes Sat as the foundation of all that exists.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in pure Being, one without a second, the same ‘is’ underlying every form.

balanced.text:
Recognition of pure Being (Sat) as the foundation of all manifestation. Direct realization of one’s existence-nature.

Somatic: Body recognized as expression of Sat.

Emotional: Emotional grounding in pure being.

Cognitive: Mind realizes Sat as the foundation of all that exists.

Psychic: The soul rests in pure Being, one without a second, the same ‘is’ underlying every form.
balanced.vignette: On an ordinary day, Sofia notices it: beneath the chair, the coffee, the passing worry, the same simple Being quietly is. The inquiry into pure Being has become a steadying background hum—everything changes, that it is does not—and resting there has made her unshakeable in small ways.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Static identification with ‘I am’ as fixed eternal entity—missing the dynamic creative aspect.
facets.somatic: Body taken as eternal in wrong sense.
facets.emotional: Rigid emotional claim of eternal being.
facets.cognitive: Mental fixation on ‘I exist’ as identity-game.
facets.psychic: The soul abstracts Existence into a concept to ponder, losing the immediacy of the being it already is.

excess.text:
Static identification with ‘I am’ as fixed eternal entity—missing the dynamic creative aspect.

Somatic: Body taken as eternal in wrong sense.

Emotional: Rigid emotional claim of eternal being.

Cognitive: Mental fixation on ‘I exist’ as identity-game.

Psychic: The soul abstracts Existence into a concept to ponder, losing the immediacy of the being it already is.
excess.vignette: Ravi has decided that since ‘only Being is real,’ the details don’t matter—neglecting his job, his bills, his relationships as ‘mere appearance.’ The inquiry into Being has become an escape from actually existing, a spiritual excuse to disengage from the life he still has to live.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Existence taken for granted, never inquired into. The miracle of being is missed.
facets.somatic: Body taken as obvious, no wonder at existing.
facets.emotional: Emotional flatness about the fact of being.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t wonder at the fact of existence itself.
facets.psychic: The soul takes only forms and names as real, never noticing the bare Existence in which they appear.

deficient.text:
Existence taken for granted, never inquired into. The miracle of being is missed.

Somatic: Body taken as obvious, no wonder at existing.

Emotional: Emotional flatness about the fact of being.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t wonder at the fact of existence itself.

Psychic: The soul takes only forms and names as real, never noticing the bare Existence in which they appear.
deficient.vignette: For Dan ‘pure Being’ is a phrase in a book that has never once touched his Tuesday—recited, never felt, changing nothing. The inquiry is dry words with no lived reality behind them, and the ground it points to remains, for him, a concept he owns and cannot use.

attributes.title: Three Rare Blessings (Manushyatva-Mumukshutva-Mahapurusha-Samshraya)
attributes.aspect: Three exceedingly rare attainments mark the seeker’s situation: (1) Manushyatva - human birth itself, (2) Mumukshutva - the burning desire for liberation, (3) Mahapurusha-samshraya - association with a realized teacher. Each is rare in isolation; together they constitute extraordinary good fortune. Wasting them is the supreme tragedy.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Framework — the three rare attainments · Valence: Neutral — preconditions to prize · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition (human birth, longing for freedom, a great teacher) · Key relation: Whole — the three rarities; Mumukṣutva (Dim 23) is one of them.
coordinate.causal_status: Condition (human birth, longing for freedom, a great teacher)
coordinate.key_relation: Whole — the three rarities; Mumukṣutva (Dim 23) is one of them.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of the rare blessing of having all three. Sustained urgency without anxiety. Devoted use of these blessings.
facets.somatic: Body honored as the rare vehicle of potential liberation.
facets.emotional: Emotional gratitude for the three rare gifts; sustained burning longing.
facets.cognitive: Mind alert to the preciousness of present opportunity for liberation.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the rare fortune of human birth, the longing for freedom, and the company of the wise, and uses it well.

balanced.text:
Recognition of the rare blessing of having all three. Sustained urgency without anxiety. Devoted use of these blessings.

Somatic: Body honored as the rare vehicle of potential liberation.

Emotional: Emotional gratitude for the three rare gifts; sustained burning longing.

Cognitive: Mind alert to the preciousness of present opportunity for liberation.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the rare fortune of human birth, the longing for freedom, and the company of the wise, and uses it well.
balanced.vignette: Ravi holds it as a genuine wonder—that he has a human birth, a real desire for freedom, and access to wisdom, three rare things aligned—and lets the sense of rare opportunity fuel his practice. Grateful for the chance, he uses it rather than squandering it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Anxiety-driven striving from sense of urgency. Rushing practice without integration.
facets.somatic: Body burnt out by anxiety-driven practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional panic about wasting opportunity.
facets.cognitive: Mental fixation on time-pressure preventing deeper engagement.
facets.psychic: The soul turns the recognition into spiritual pride, congratulating itself on its rare blessings.

excess.text:
Anxiety-driven striving from sense of urgency. Rushing practice without integration.

Somatic: Body burnt out by anxiety-driven practice.

Emotional: Emotional panic about wasting opportunity.

Cognitive: Mental fixation on time-pressure preventing deeper engagement.

Psychic: The soul turns the recognition into spiritual pride, congratulating itself on its rare blessings.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned ‘you are among the rare blessed ones’ into spiritual pride—specially chosen, ahead of the sleeping masses, marked out for greatness. The recognition of a rare opportunity has become a badge of superiority he wears over an unchanged life.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Casual treatment of human birth as just any existence. Spiritual opportunity squandered in trivia.
facets.somatic: Body used for sense-pursuits only.
facets.emotional: Emotional indifference to spiritual opportunity.
facets.cognitive: Mind unaware of the rare configuration available.
facets.psychic: The soul squanders its rare opportunity, taking human life and the longing for truth for granted.

deficient.text:
Casual treatment of human birth as just any existence. Spiritual opportunity squandered in trivia.

Somatic: Body used for sense-pursuits only.

Emotional: Emotional indifference to spiritual opportunity.

Cognitive: Mind unaware of the rare configuration available.

Psychic: The soul squanders its rare opportunity, taking human life and the longing for truth for granted.
deficient.vignette: Dan has the human birth, the vague longing, the available wisdom—and squanders all three, distracted, delaying, treating the rare alignment as though it were nothing and would keep. The preciousness of the opportunity is invisible to him, and it drains away unused.

attributes.title: Citta (Mind-Stuff/Consciousness Vehicle)
attributes.aspect: Citta is the totality of the inner cognitive apparatus—the mind-stuff comprising intelligence (buddhi), ego (ahamkara), and mind proper (manas). It is the field on which all mental activity occurs and the instrument that must be stilled for liberation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Faculty / constituent — the mind-stuff, vehicle of consciousness · Valence: Neutral faculty · Guṇa: Spans all three · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Instrument (the field on which vṛttis and saṁskāras play) · Key relation: The mind-stuff; kin to the Antaḥkaraṇa (Dim 3); its vṛttis (Dim 20) are to be stilled.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Faculty / constituent — the mind-stuff, vehicle of consciousness
coordinate.causal_status: Instrument (the field on which vṛttis and saṁskāras play)
coordinate.key_relation: The mind-stuff; kin to the Antaḥkaraṇa (Dim 3); its vṛttis (Dim 20) are to be stilled.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When the citta is purified and stilled, it becomes a clear translucent medium that reflects the Self perfectly. It can be the ideal instrument for self-realization.
facets.somatic: Healthy nervous system reflecting purified mind, integrated body-mind, energy flowing harmoniously through the citta-system.
facets.emotional: Emotional clarity from purified citta, healing of emotional patterns, emotional intelligence developed.
facets.cognitive: Clear mind functioning optimally, all three aspects (buddhi, ahamkara, manas) in harmony, mind serves consciousness.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the mind-stuff as a vehicle it has, not the self it is, and so is no longer ruled by its movements.

balanced.text:
When the citta is purified and stilled, it becomes a clear translucent medium that reflects the Self perfectly. It can be the ideal instrument for self-realization.

Somatic: Healthy nervous system reflecting purified mind, integrated body-mind, energy flowing harmoniously through the citta-system.

Emotional: Emotional clarity from purified citta, healing of emotional patterns, emotional intelligence developed.

Cognitive: Clear mind functioning optimally, all three aspects (buddhi, ahamkara, manas) in harmony, mind serves consciousness.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the mind-stuff as a vehicle it has, not the self it is, and so is no longer ruled by its movements.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has learned to work with his mind as the instrument it is—training it, steadying it, using it without being used by it. The mind-stuff, understood as a tool rather than a master, has become something he can quiet and direct rather than merely suffer.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Over-identification with citta as Self creates confusion between mind and consciousness, leading to mental arrogance or premature dissolution claims.
facets.somatic: Body identified with mental activity, stress from over-mentation, neglect of body for mental development.
facets.emotional: Emotional rigidity around mental states, identification with thoughts as self, emotional suffering from misidentification.
facets.cognitive: Mistaking the citta for the Self, mental pride about psychological development, identification with mental contents.
facets.psychic: The soul over-identifies with its rich inner mental life, mistaking the vehicle for the driver.

excess.text:
Over-identification with citta as Self creates confusion between mind and consciousness, leading to mental arrogance or premature dissolution claims.

Somatic: Body identified with mental activity, stress from over-mentation, neglect of body for mental development.

Emotional: Emotional rigidity around mental states, identification with thoughts as self, emotional suffering from misidentification.

Cognitive: Mistaking the citta for the Self, mental pride about psychological development, identification with mental contents.

Psychic: The soul over-identifies with its rich inner mental life, mistaking the vehicle for the driver.
excess.vignette: Sam is wholly absorbed in his own mental activity—every thought gripping, every mood total, identified so completely with the churn of the mind that there’s no space to see it as merely an instrument. The vehicle has swallowed the traveler.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Damaged or unhealthy citta (through trauma, disease, or affliction) prevents proper yogic functioning. The instrument itself is compromised.
facets.somatic: Physical symptoms from disturbed citta, somatic distress from mental dysfunction.
facets.emotional: Emotional dysregulation from impaired citta, inability to feel coherently, trauma effects.
facets.cognitive: Citta dominated by kleshas, scattered mental functioning, inability to think clearly, mental dysfunction.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly fused with the citta, taking every thought and mood as simply itself.

deficient.text:
Damaged or unhealthy citta (through trauma, disease, or affliction) prevents proper yogic functioning. The instrument itself is compromised.

Somatic: Physical symptoms from disturbed citta, somatic distress from mental dysfunction.

Emotional: Emotional dysregulation from impaired citta, inability to feel coherently, trauma effects.

Cognitive: Citta dominated by kleshas, scattered mental functioning, inability to think clearly, mental dysfunction.

Psychic: The soul is wholly fused with the citta, taking every thought and mood as simply itself.
deficient.vignette: Dan has never once considered his mind as a thing he could observe or train—it simply is him, its every movement taken as reality, no distance, no instrument. That consciousness has a trainable vehicle he might learn to steer is not a possibility he’s glimpsed.

attributes.title: Samskaras (Latent Mental Impressions)
attributes.aspect: Samskaras are subliminal imprints—mental and karmic seeds left in the citta by every experience and action. They are stored dormant and activated when similar circumstances arise, creating habit patterns, personality traits, and karmic destiny. Yoga aims to burn these seeds.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Process / latent residue · Valence: Neutral tending to bondage · Guṇa: Takes the action’s coloring · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism / residue · Key relation: Same as the Saṁskāras (Dim 12); the grooves that condition response.
coordinate.key_relation: Same as the Saṁskāras (Dim 12); the grooves that condition response.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When samskaras are recognized and purified through practice, they lose their compulsive power. The yogi acts freely without being driven by latent patterns. New samskaras from yogic practice support liberation.
facets.somatic: Body free from compulsive habit patterns, healthy physical conditioning from yogic samskaras, no driven physical patterns.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from old patterns, healing of emotional samskaras, ability to choose responses consciously.
facets.cognitive: Mental freedom from compulsive thinking patterns, awareness of when samskaras activate, ability to not feed them.
facets.psychic: The soul understands its grooves of habit as conditioned impressions, and so can work to soften rather than obey them.

balanced.text:
When samskaras are recognized and purified through practice, they lose their compulsive power. The yogi acts freely without being driven by latent patterns. New samskaras from yogic practice support liberation.

Somatic: Body free from compulsive habit patterns, healthy physical conditioning from yogic samskaras, no driven physical patterns.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from old patterns, healing of emotional samskaras, ability to choose responses consciously.

Cognitive: Mental freedom from compulsive thinking patterns, awareness of when samskaras activate, ability to not feed them.

Psychic: The soul understands its grooves of habit as conditioned impressions, and so can work to soften rather than obey them.
balanced.vignette: Ravi can feel the grooves his past has worn in him—the reflexes, the ruts—and works with them patiently, softening the ones that bind and deepening the ones that serve. The awareness of his own conditioning has become the beginning of freedom from it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive focus on samskaras becomes obsessive psychoanalysis, endless processing without liberation, getting lost in patterns.
facets.somatic: Body trapped in trauma analysis, somatic re-experiencing without resolution.
facets.emotional: Emotional regression into old patterns through over-analysis, getting stuck in past, trauma re-living.
facets.cognitive: Mental obsession with patterns, endless analysis, intellectual understanding without transformation.
facets.psychic: The soul becomes fatalistic about its conditioning, treating samskāras as an unchangeable identity.

excess.text:
Excessive focus on samskaras becomes obsessive psychoanalysis, endless processing without liberation, getting lost in patterns.

Somatic: Body trapped in trauma analysis, somatic re-experiencing without resolution.

Emotional: Emotional regression into old patterns through over-analysis, getting stuck in past, trauma re-living.

Cognitive: Mental obsession with patterns, endless analysis, intellectual understanding without transformation.

Psychic: The soul becomes fatalistic about its conditioning, treating samskāras as an unchangeable identity.
excess.vignette: Sam analyzes his conditioning endlessly—naming every groove, tracing every pattern to its root, so busy studying his samskaras that he never actually lives past them. The self-examination meant to free him has become an absorbing, paralyzing hobby.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: When samskaras are dominant and unconscious, the person lives entirely controlled by hidden conditioning—compulsive behavior, addictions, personality patterns.
facets.somatic: Body completely controlled by samskara-driven habits, physical compulsions, addictions, automatic patterns.
facets.emotional: Emotions arising compulsively from samskaras without awareness, repetitive emotional patterns, slavery to conditioning.
facets.cognitive: Mind dominated by unconscious patterns, repetitive thinking, no awareness of why one thinks/feels as one does, life on autopilot.
facets.psychic: The soul is run by its grooves unawares, mistaking deep habit for free choice.

deficient.text:
When samskaras are dominant and unconscious, the person lives entirely controlled by hidden conditioning—compulsive behavior, addictions, personality patterns.

Somatic: Body completely controlled by samskara-driven habits, physical compulsions, addictions, automatic patterns.

Emotional: Emotions arising compulsively from samskaras without awareness, repetitive emotional patterns, slavery to conditioning.

Cognitive: Mind dominated by unconscious patterns, repetitive thinking, no awareness of why one thinks/feels as one does, life on autopilot.

Psychic: The soul is run by its grooves unawares, mistaking deep habit for free choice.
deficient.vignette: Dan is run entirely by grooves he can’t see—the same reactions firing for the same triggers, the same ruts walked daily, mistaken each time for free choice. That his behavior is largely conditioned impression, and could be worked with, has never occurred to him.

attributes.title: Vasanas (Karmic Tendencies)
attributes.aspect: Vasanas are deeper, accumulated currents of karmic tendency built up over many lifetimes. They are clusters of samskaras forming subtle character orientations and propensities. Distinct from individual samskaras, they represent the deeper karmic flow of an individual.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Faculty / latent tendency · Valence: Faculty tending to affliction · Guṇa: Rajas–Tamas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Latent cause · Key relation: Same as the Vāsanās (Dim 12); the tendencies born of saṁskāra.
coordinate.key_relation: Same as the Vāsanās (Dim 12); the tendencies born of saṁskāra.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When vasanas are understood and gradually purified through practice, the yogi gains insight into karmic patterns spanning lifetimes. There is wisdom about deep tendencies.
facets.somatic: Body that has accumulated yogic vasanas supporting practice, healthy long-term physical orientation toward liberation.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns oriented toward spiritual progress, accumulated wisdom-vasanas guiding feelings.
facets.cognitive: Mind oriented toward liberation by accumulated yogic vasanas, deeper tendencies toward Self-realization.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes its driving tendencies as vāsanās to be gradually exhausted, not commands to be obeyed.

balanced.text:
When vasanas are understood and gradually purified through practice, the yogi gains insight into karmic patterns spanning lifetimes. There is wisdom about deep tendencies.

Somatic: Body that has accumulated yogic vasanas supporting practice, healthy long-term physical orientation toward liberation.

Emotional: Emotional patterns oriented toward spiritual progress, accumulated wisdom-vasanas guiding feelings.

Cognitive: Mind oriented toward liberation by accumulated yogic vasanas, deeper tendencies toward Self-realization.

Psychic: The soul recognizes its driving tendencies as vāsanās to be gradually exhausted, not commands to be obeyed.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to sense the deep tendencies that drive him—the old cravings and aversions running beneath his choices—and, seeing them, has begun gently to loosen their grip. The awareness doesn’t free him instantly, but it ends their invisibility, and what’s seen can change.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Strong unbroken vasanas of any kind (even spiritual) can become limiting—identification with personality traits, refusal to evolve beyond accumulated patterns.
facets.somatic: Body locked into vasana-driven patterns, inability to change physical habits, limited by deep conditioning.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns running on auto-pilot, deep tendencies that resist transformation.
facets.cognitive: Mind locked into accumulated patterns, personality as prison, inability to transcend deep conditioning.
facets.psychic: The soul indulges its tendencies in the name of authenticity, deepening the very grooves that bind it.

excess.text:
Strong unbroken vasanas of any kind (even spiritual) can become limiting—identification with personality traits, refusal to evolve beyond accumulated patterns.

Somatic: Body locked into vasana-driven patterns, inability to change physical habits, limited by deep conditioning.

Emotional: Emotional patterns running on auto-pilot, deep tendencies that resist transformation.

Cognitive: Mind locked into accumulated patterns, personality as prison, inability to transcend deep conditioning.

Psychic: The soul indulges its tendencies in the name of authenticity, deepening the very grooves that bind it.
excess.vignette: Sam uses his tendencies as an excuse—‘that’s just my nature, my vasanas, nothing to be done’—a fatalism dressed in Sanskrit that justifies never changing anything. The teaching about deep tendencies has become his permission to be run by them forever.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Negative vasanas dominating create persistent obstacles to liberation—deep currents pulling toward worldly bondage despite spiritual intentions.
facets.somatic: Body pulled toward physical samsara by deep vasanas, addictions rooted in deep tendency.
facets.emotional: Deep emotional currents toward suffering, persistent patterns despite practice, vasanas of klesha types.
facets.cognitive: Deep mental currents toward bondage, persistent karmic patterns, obstacles arising from deep conditioning.
facets.psychic: The soul is steered by unseen tendencies it never questions, calling their pull its own will.

deficient.text:
Negative vasanas dominating create persistent obstacles to liberation—deep currents pulling toward worldly bondage despite spiritual intentions.

Somatic: Body pulled toward physical samsara by deep vasanas, addictions rooted in deep tendency.

Emotional: Deep emotional currents toward suffering, persistent patterns despite practice, vasanas of klesha types.

Cognitive: Deep mental currents toward bondage, persistent karmic patterns, obstacles arising from deep conditioning.

Psychic: The soul is steered by unseen tendencies it never questions, calling their pull its own will.
deficient.vignette: Dan is driven blindly by his deep tendencies—the compulsions and aversions steering his life while he believes himself the free author of every choice. That latent karmic tendencies shape him at all is invisible to him, and so they run him entirely, unopposed.

attributes.title: Ishvara (The Lord/Personal God)
attributes.aspect: Ishvara is the special Purusha—a unique eternally free consciousness, the supreme being—distinct from individual liberated souls. Patanjali presents devotion to Ishvara as one path to samadhi (an alternative to discrimination-based path), and identifies the symbol of Ishvara as Pranava (Om).

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework / principle — the personal God (saguṇa Brahman) · Valence: Neutral / object of devotion · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant (pure saguṇa) · Scale: Cosmic (Ādhidaivika), approached individually · Causal status: Ground (the Absolute with attributes, the Lord) · Key relation: Saguṇa Brahman (Dim 2’s Two Forms); the inner controller and object of Bhakti.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework / principle — the personal God (saguṇa Brahman)
coordinate.causal_status: Ground (the Absolute with attributes, the Lord)
coordinate.key_relation: Saguṇa Brahman (Dim 2’s Two Forms); the inner controller and object of Bhakti.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Ishvara is wisely engaged as object of devotion and meditation, profound spiritual progress occurs. Bhakti-yoga path through Ishvara-pranidhana brings samadhi.
facets.somatic: Body surrendered to higher reality, healthy effects of devotional practice, body as instrument of divine.
facets.emotional: Devotional love, emotional warmth and connection, peace from surrender, divine relationship.
facets.cognitive: Mind united with Ishvara through Pranava and meditation, surrendered intellect, devotional intelligence.
facets.psychic: The soul relates to the Lord as the personal face of the absolute, a focus for devotion and surrender.

balanced.text:
When Ishvara is wisely engaged as object of devotion and meditation, profound spiritual progress occurs. Bhakti-yoga path through Ishvara-pranidhana brings samadhi.

Somatic: Body surrendered to higher reality, healthy effects of devotional practice, body as instrument of divine.

Emotional: Devotional love, emotional warmth and connection, peace from surrender, divine relationship.

Cognitive: Mind united with Ishvara through Pranava and meditation, surrendered intellect, devotional intelligence.

Psychic: The soul relates to the Lord as the personal face of the absolute, a focus for devotion and surrender.
balanced.vignette: Ravi holds a warm, sane relationship with the personal divine—devotion that doesn’t switch off his reason, trust that doesn’t excuse his effort. The Lord, for him, is both beloved and real, and the relationship steadies and softens him without making him credulous.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive devotion without wisdom becomes blind dependency, anthropomorphic projection, or sectarian fundamentalism.
facets.somatic: Physical exhaustion from extreme devotional practices, body neglect, ritualism over realization.
facets.emotional: Emotional dependency on divine figure as crutch, codependent spirituality, emotional manipulation.
facets.cognitive: Mental dependency on external authority, fundamentalist thinking, projection rather than direct realization.
facets.psychic: The soul anthropomorphizes and bargains, reducing Ishvara to a cosmic functionary for its wishes.

excess.text:
Excessive devotion without wisdom becomes blind dependency, anthropomorphic projection, or sectarian fundamentalism.

Somatic: Physical exhaustion from extreme devotional practices, body neglect, ritualism over realization.

Emotional: Emotional dependency on divine figure as crutch, codependent spirituality, emotional manipulation.

Cognitive: Mental dependency on external authority, fundamentalist thinking, projection rather than direct realization.

Psychic: The soul anthropomorphizes and bargains, reducing Ishvara to a cosmic functionary for its wishes.
excess.vignette: Karl’s God is superstition and projection at once—a cosmic vending machine to be bargained with, or his own ego magnified and enthroned in the sky. The living relationship with the divine has become either a fearful magic or a mirror for his own grandiosity.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Without any orientation toward Ishvara (or higher reality), the path of yoga is harder—lacks devotional support, requires harder discrimination path alone.
facets.somatic: Body lacks devotional supports for practice, harder physical discipline without bhakti.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without devotional dimension, drier practice, lack of heart-warmth.
facets.cognitive: Pure intellectual approach without bhakti support, harder progress, lacks devotional aid.
facets.psychic: The soul has no felt relationship to a higher reality, and faces existence as if utterly alone.

deficient.text:
Without any orientation toward Ishvara (or higher reality), the path of yoga is harder—lacks devotional support, requires harder discrimination path alone.

Somatic: Body lacks devotional supports for practice, harder physical discipline without bhakti.

Emotional: Emotional life without devotional dimension, drier practice, lack of heart-warmth.

Cognitive: Pure intellectual approach without bhakti support, harder progress, lacks devotional aid.

Psychic: The soul has no felt relationship to a higher reality, and faces existence as if utterly alone.
deficient.vignette: For Dan there is no divine of any kind—no Lord, no personal God, no sacred presence to relate to—only matter and mechanism, and the whole dimension of devotion is closed to him. The relationship the teaching offers finds nothing in him, and the sky is, to him, empty.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: State / practice — knowing as lived · Valence: Neutral · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Ground · Key relation: Genus — the lived act of knowing of which the distinctions below are refinements.
coordinate.key_relation: Genus — the lived act of knowing of which the distinctions below are refinements.

balanced.label: Balanced — discriminating knowledge held with humility

facets.overview: Held in balance, the way of knowing combines sharp discrimination with deep humility. The person can tell the difference between an idea they hold and a truth they have realized, and is honest about which is which. They use the intellect fully — to inquire, to discern, to navigate — while knowing its limits before the ultimate. They tend to choose the good over the merely pleasant at the small daily forks, and they hold even their best understanding lightly, as a finger pointing at the moon rather than the moon itself. The fruit is a kind of clear-eyed groundedness: confident where confidence is warranted, humble where it is not.

balanced.text: Held in balance, the way of knowing combines sharp discrimination with deep humility. The person can tell the difference between an idea they hold and a truth they have realized, and is honest about which is which. They use the intellect fully — to inquire, to discern, to navigate — while knowing its limits before the ultimate. They tend to choose the good over the merely pleasant at the small daily forks, and they hold even their best understanding lightly, as a finger pointing at the moon rather than the moon itself. The fruit is a kind of clear-eyed groundedness: confident where confidence is warranted, humble where it is not.

excess.label: In excess — the pride of ‘knowing’; intellectualism as bypass

facets.overview: The characteristic distortion here is intellectual spiritual pride: a head richly furnished with concepts, mistaken for actual transformation. The person can discourse brilliantly on non-duality and remains entirely run by their reactions; they have collected realizations as information and confused the menu for the meal. The Kena’s warning lands precisely on them — the one who thinks he knows, knows not. There is a subtler form too: using analysis itself as an evasion, retreating into the safety of conceptualizing whenever direct feeling or honest not-knowing threatens.

excess.text: The characteristic distortion here is intellectual spiritual pride: a head richly furnished with concepts, mistaken for actual transformation. The person can discourse brilliantly on non-duality and remains entirely run by their reactions; they have collected realizations as information and confused the menu for the meal. The Kena’s warning lands precisely on them — the one who thinks he knows, knows not. There is a subtler form too: using analysis itself as an evasion, retreating into the safety of conceptualizing whenever direct feeling or honest not-knowing threatens.

deficient.label: Deficient — credulity, or no discernment at all
