facets.overview: At the other pole is the absence of discrimination: a person who cannot tell the pleasant from the good, the sound source from the flattering one, the realized teacher from the charlatan. They believe too easily or chase whatever gratifies, swept along by preyas at every fork, with no inner compass of viveka to steer by. Where the excess pole knows too proudly, the deficient pole does not really know at all — and both, in their different ways, keep a person bound.

deficient.text: At the other pole is the absence of discrimination: a person who cannot tell the pleasant from the good, the sound source from the flattering one, the realized teacher from the charlatan. They believe too easily or chase whatever gratifies, swept along by preyas at every fork, with no inner compass of viveka to steer by. Where the excess pole knows too proudly, the deficient pole does not really know at all — and both, in their different ways, keep a person bound.

attributes.title: Knowledge vs Knowing (Information vs Realization)
attributes.aspect: The crucial distinction between accumulated information (knowledge) and direct realization (knowing). Knowledge is parasitic, can grow infinitely without transformation. Knowing requires personal alchemical change—you must become what you would know.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — an epistemic distinction · Valence: Neutral — a discernment to make · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Framework (clarifies how one knows) · Key relation: information (about) vs realization (being) — the core distinction of this dimension.
coordinate.key_relation: information (about) vs realization (being) — the core distinction of this dimension.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Both used wisely—knowledge for practical life and social function, knowing for spiritual realization. Not confusing one for the other.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in both worldly knowledge and embodied knowing.
facets.emotional: Heart open to knowing while head functions for knowledge.
facets.cognitive: Mind clear that knowledge and knowing are different dimensions, both honored.
facets.psychic: The soul values realization over information, knowing that data about truth and the living taste of it are different orders.

balanced.text:
Both used wisely—knowledge for practical life and social function, knowing for spiritual realization. Not confusing one for the other.

Somatic: Body engaged in both worldly knowledge and embodied knowing.

Emotional: Heart open to knowing while head functions for knowledge.

Cognitive: Mind clear that knowledge and knowing are different dimensions, both honored.

Psychic: The soul values realization over information, knowing that data about truth and the living taste of it are different orders.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has read a hundred books on presence and, lately, actually become a little present—the difference between knowing about the thing and knowing it landing at last. What he understands now, he understands in his body; the information has ripened into realization.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Knowledge accumulated as ego-asset—becoming ‘man of knowledge’ while being empty inside. Or rejecting knowledge claiming only knowing matters.
facets.somatic: Either body burdened with study or rejecting all learning.
facets.emotional: Pride in knowledge or contempt for learning, both ego-states.
facets.cognitive: Confusing knowledge for knowing or vice versa.
facets.psychic: The soul mistakes accumulated knowledge for attainment, a library of concepts standing in for the missing realization.

excess.text:
Knowledge accumulated as ego-asset—becoming ‘man of knowledge’ while being empty inside. Or rejecting knowledge claiming only knowing matters.

Somatic: Either body burdened with study or rejecting all learning.

Emotional: Pride in knowledge or contempt for learning, both ego-states.

Cognitive: Confusing knowledge for knowing or vice versa.

Psychic: The soul mistakes accumulated knowledge for attainment, a library of concepts standing in for the missing realization.
excess.vignette: Sam has accumulated spiritual information like a collector—every text, every technique, every teacher catalogued—and mistakes the size of his library for the depth of his realization. He knows everything and has realized nothing, and can’t tell the difference.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Mistaking knowledge for knowing—reading scriptures and believing one knows. Or having neither knowledge nor knowing.
facets.somatic: Body merely accumulating words.
facets.emotional: Emotional flatness despite knowledge accumulation.
facets.cognitive: Mind crowded with information, no realization.
facets.psychic: The soul knows neither, neither informed nor awakened, drifting without orientation.

deficient.text:
Mistaking knowledge for knowing—reading scriptures and believing one knows. Or having neither knowledge nor knowing.

Somatic: Body merely accumulating words.

Emotional: Emotional flatness despite knowledge accumulation.

Cognitive: Mind crowded with information, no realization.

Psychic: The soul knows neither, neither informed nor awakened, drifting without orientation.
deficient.vignette: Dan neither knows about the deeper things nor knows them—incurious, unread, unreflective, with no information and no realization. The whole domain of inner knowing is simply off his map; he has never wondered what he doesn’t know.

attributes.title: Veda vs Vedanta (Knowledge for Ignorant vs Knower)
attributes.aspect: Two layers of teaching—one provisional for those still identified, one ultimate for the realized. The ignorant cannot hear the ultimate teaching directly; the provisional teaching breaks identification. When identification breaks, the ultimate teaching is naturally realized.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — an epistemic distinction · Valence: Neutral — a discernment to make · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Framework (clarifies how one knows) · Key relation: ritual knowledge for the ignorant vs the knowledge of the knower.
coordinate.key_relation: ritual knowledge for the ignorant vs the knowledge of the knower.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Understanding which teaching applies to which state. Provisional teachings used skillfully to break identification; ultimate teachings recognized when consciousness has matured.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in appropriate practices for current state.
facets.emotional: Emotional engagement matched to capacity.
facets.cognitive: Mind receiving teaching appropriate to its current level.
facets.psychic: The soul honors ritual knowledge as preparation while pressing on toward the knowledge that liberates.

balanced.text:
Understanding which teaching applies to which state. Provisional teachings used skillfully to break identification; ultimate teachings recognized when consciousness has matured.

Somatic: Body engaged in appropriate practices for current state.

Emotional: Emotional engagement matched to capacity.

Cognitive: Mind receiving teaching appropriate to its current level.

Psychic: The soul honors ritual knowledge as preparation while pressing on toward the knowledge that liberates.
balanced.vignette: Ravi honored the preparatory work—the disciplines, the rituals, the ethical ground—and then moved on to the Self-knowledge they were preparing him for. He values the ladder and has climbed it; the earlier rungs were not despised but used.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Premature claiming of ultimate teaching—saying ‘world is illusion’ while still identified with body, money, status.
facets.somatic: Body still identified while mouth speaks Vedanta.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity hidden behind ultimate teaching.
facets.cognitive: Mental theft of ultimate teaching without authentic level.
facets.psychic: The soul absolutizes the preliminary, mistaking the means — ritual, technique — for the end of realization.

excess.text:
Premature claiming of ultimate teaching—saying ‘world is illusion’ while still identified with body, money, status.

Somatic: Body still identified while mouth speaks Vedanta.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity hidden behind ultimate teaching.

Cognitive: Mental theft of ultimate teaching without authentic level.

Psychic: The soul absolutizes the preliminary, mistaking the means — ritual, technique — for the end of realization.
excess.vignette: Sam dismisses all preparatory practice—ritual, discipline, devotion—as ‘for the ignorant,’ leaping straight to lofty Self-talk from a foundation he never built. He’s scorned the ladder and wonders why he keeps falling off the wall.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Stuck only at provisional level—body is real, world is real, never approaching the ultimate teaching about consciousness.
facets.somatic: Body taken as ultimate self.
facets.emotional: Emotional life confined to objects.
facets.cognitive: Mind limited to first-layer understanding.
facets.psychic: The soul has neither the discipline of the path nor its culmination, ungrounded at both ends.

deficient.text:
Stuck only at provisional level—body is real, world is real, never approaching the ultimate teaching about consciousness.

Somatic: Body taken as ultimate self.

Emotional: Emotional life confined to objects.

Cognitive: Mind limited to first-layer understanding.

Psychic: The soul has neither the discipline of the path nor its culmination, ungrounded at both ends.
deficient.vignette: Dan is stuck at the level of mere information and ritual—performing the forms, collecting the facts—with no sense that any of it points beyond itself to a knowing that transforms. The higher knowledge the whole edifice exists for is invisible to him.

attributes.title: Vijnana (Understanding Beyond Information)
attributes.aspect: Vijnana is the discriminating intelligence that distinguishes between what is real and what is apparent. Beyond mere knowledge (jnana), it is the applied wisdom that knows what matters and what doesn’t, what is eternal and what is passing. Higher Vijnana directly perceives the Self.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Faculty — higher, realized understanding (vijñāna) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (realized, not merely informational, knowing) · Key relation: Higher knowledge; kin to the Vijñānamaya kośa (Dim 5) and Jñāna (Dim 10).
coordinate.key_relation: Higher knowledge; kin to the Vijñānamaya kośa (Dim 5) and Jñāna (Dim 10).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Active discriminating wisdom in every encounter. Seeing through appearances to essences.
facets.somatic: Body’s choices guided by discrimination.
facets.emotional: Emotional discrimination—real love vs. attachment, real joy vs. excitement.
facets.cognitive: Mind continuously discriminates real from apparent.
facets.psychic: The soul reaches understanding that has ripened past mere information into lived comprehension.

balanced.text:
Active discriminating wisdom in every encounter. Seeing through appearances to essences.

Somatic: Body’s choices guided by discrimination.

Emotional: Emotional discrimination—real love vs. attachment, real joy vs. excitement.

Cognitive: Mind continuously discriminates real from apparent.

Psychic: The soul reaches understanding that has ripened past mere information into lived comprehension.
balanced.vignette: When Meera finally understood impermanence, it wasn’t a fact she added but a lens that changed everything—the understanding reorganizing how she held her losses. Vijnana, for her, is knowledge that transforms the knower, not just fills her in.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Endless analysis without commitment—using discrimination to avoid action and engagement.
facets.somatic: Body paralyzed by over-analysis.
facets.emotional: Emotional disengagement disguised as discrimination.
facets.cognitive: Mental analysis as escape.
facets.psychic: The soul prides itself on subtle understanding, the intellect inflating where humility was the lesson.

excess.text:
Endless analysis without commitment—using discrimination to avoid action and engagement.

Somatic: Body paralyzed by over-analysis.

Emotional: Emotional disengagement disguised as discrimination.

Cognitive: Mental analysis as escape.

Psychic: The soul prides itself on subtle understanding, the intellect inflating where humility was the lesson.
excess.vignette: Ravi prides himself on ‘deep understanding’—dissecting every teaching to its subtlest implication—while remaining, in his actual life, entirely unchanged by any of it. The understanding has become a trophy admired from a distance, not a fire he’s walked through.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No discrimination—taking every appearance as reality, every passing emotion as truth.
facets.somatic: Body responds to every stimulus indiscriminately.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity without discrimination.
facets.cognitive: Mind without discriminating power.
facets.psychic: The soul stays at surface information, never letting knowledge deepen into understanding.

deficient.text:
No discrimination—taking every appearance as reality, every passing emotion as truth.

Somatic: Body responds to every stimulus indiscriminately.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity without discrimination.

Cognitive: Mind without discriminating power.

Psychic: The soul stays at surface information, never letting knowledge deepen into understanding.
deficient.vignette: Dan skims the surface of everything and understands the depth of nothing—collecting headlines, missing meanings, never once penetrating to the transforming insight beneath the information. He mistakes being informed for understanding, and is neither.

attributes.title: Para-Apara Vidya Distinction (Krishnananda’s Deep Treatment)
attributes.aspect: Krishnananda emphasizes that even Vedas, sacred texts, philosophy, sciences are ‘apara’ (lower) because they remain object-knowledge. ‘Para’ (higher) is only the knowledge BY WHICH the Imperishable is realized—which is direct knower-knowledge, not knower-knowing-objects.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — an epistemic distinction · Valence: Neutral — a discernment to make · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Framework (clarifies how one knows) · Key relation: the deep treatment of higher (parā) vs lower (aparā) knowledge.
coordinate.key_relation: the deep treatment of higher (parā) vs lower (aparā) knowledge.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Both apara and para vidya cultivated appropriately. Apara as preparation; para as goal. Each in proper place.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in both worldly skill and spiritual practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional balance between scholarship and devotion.
facets.cognitive: Mind cultivating both dimensions without confusing them.
facets.psychic: The soul holds clearly the difference between knowledge that informs and knowledge that transforms, seeking the higher without despising the lower.

balanced.text:
Both apara and para vidya cultivated appropriately. Apara as preparation; para as goal. Each in proper place.

Somatic: Body engaged in both worldly skill and spiritual practice.

Emotional: Emotional balance between scholarship and devotion.

Cognitive: Mind cultivating both dimensions without confusing them.

Psychic: The soul holds clearly the difference between knowledge that informs and knowledge that transforms, seeking the higher without despising the lower.
balanced.vignette: Ravi values both the worldly knowledge that runs his life and the higher knowledge that frees it—the map of how things work and the recognition of what he is—each in its place. He neither despises the practical nor stops at it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Accumulation of vast apara without ever opening to para—the learned man who remains in suffering.
facets.somatic: Body burdened with scholarly identity.
facets.emotional: Emotional dryness from intellectualism without para.
facets.cognitive: Mind crowded with apara-knowledge, no para-realization.
facets.psychic: The soul over-systematizes the distinction, debating categories while neglecting the transformation they point to.

excess.text:
Accumulation of vast apara without ever opening to para—the learned man who remains in suffering.

Somatic: Body burdened with scholarly identity.

Emotional: Emotional dryness from intellectualism without para.

Cognitive: Mind crowded with apara-knowledge, no para-realization.

Psychic: The soul over-systematizes the distinction, debating categories while neglecting the transformation they point to.
excess.vignette: Sam dismisses all ‘lower,’ worldly knowledge—science, skill, practical competence—as beneath the seeker of the Absolute, and can’t manage his own affairs. He’s exalted the higher knowledge into a contempt for the lower that leaves him impractical and dependent.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Premature claim of para-vidya without foundation in apara—undisciplined ‘mysticism’ without grounding.
facets.somatic: Body lacking disciplinary foundation.
facets.emotional: Emotional spirituality without intellectual grounding.
facets.cognitive: Mental claim of higher knowledge without discipline.
facets.psychic: The soul conflates the two, taking competence in worldly knowledge as if it were spiritual realization.

deficient.text:
Premature claim of para-vidya without foundation in apara—undisciplined ‘mysticism’ without grounding.

Somatic: Body lacking disciplinary foundation.

Emotional: Emotional spirituality without intellectual grounding.

Cognitive: Mental claim of higher knowledge without discipline.

Psychic: The soul conflates the two, taking competence in worldly knowledge as if it were spiritual realization.
deficient.vignette: For Dan there’s only the lower knowledge—facts, skills, information about the world—and the higher knowledge that liberates isn’t something he disbelieves so much as has never heard of. He’s a competent manager of a life he’s never asked the meaning of.

attributes.title: Vidya and Avidya (Knowledge and Action Combined)
attributes.aspect: Those who worship only action (Avidya/karma) go to dark worlds; those who worship only knowledge (Vidya) go to greater darkness. He who knows both together transcends mortality through action and reaches immortality through knowledge.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — knowledge and action to be known together (Īśāvāsya) · Valence: Neutral · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework (the balance of knowledge and works) · Key relation: The Īśāvāsya teaching that vidyā and avidyā are complementary.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework — knowledge and action to be known together (Īśāvāsya)
coordinate.causal_status: Framework (the balance of knowledge and works)
coordinate.key_relation: The Īśāvāsya teaching that vidyā and avidyā are complementary.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Both action and knowledge integrated. Worldly action handled wisely; transcendent knowledge cultivated simultaneously. Neither path alone is sufficient.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in both worldly action and contemplative practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional balance between engagement and transcendence.
facets.cognitive: Mind active in world while rooted in higher knowledge.
facets.psychic: The soul combines knowledge and action wisely, crossing death by the one and reaching immortality by the other, as the Īśā enjoins.

balanced.text:
Both action and knowledge integrated. Worldly action handled wisely; transcendent knowledge cultivated simultaneously. Neither path alone is sufficient.

Somatic: Body engaged in both worldly action and contemplative practice.

Emotional: Emotional balance between engagement and transcendence.

Cognitive: Mind active in world while rooted in higher knowledge.

Psychic: The soul combines knowledge and action wisely, crossing death by the one and reaching immortality by the other, as the Īśā enjoins.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has learned to weave knowledge and action together—understanding informing his doing, his doing testing his understanding—neither a paralyzed knower nor a blind actor. The two hands work together, and his life is the more whole for it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Extreme renunciate denying all action, claiming pure knowledge while life dissolves. Or compulsive ritualist denying any inner knowledge.
facets.somatic: Either extreme withdrawal or compulsive ritualism.
facets.emotional: Either pride in knowledge or pride in ritual action.
facets.cognitive: Either escapist contemplation or mechanical ritual.
facets.psychic: The soul over-invests in one alone — pure action or pure knowledge — and the prescribed balance is lost.

excess.text:
Extreme renunciate denying all action, claiming pure knowledge while life dissolves. Or compulsive ritualist denying any inner knowledge.

Somatic: Either extreme withdrawal or compulsive ritualism.

Emotional: Either pride in knowledge or pride in ritual action.

Cognitive: Either escapist contemplation or mechanical ritual.

Psychic: The soul over-invests in one alone — pure action or pure knowledge — and the prescribed balance is lost.
excess.vignette: Sam is all knowledge and no action—endless study, endless insight, and not one changed habit—or, on his off days, all frantic action and no wisdom. The integration the teaching urges eludes him; he keeps splitting the knowing from the doing.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Pure materialist—neither inner knowledge nor sacred action. Life lived entirely on surface, missing both dimensions.
facets.somatic: Body without ritual nor practice, mere biological function.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without sacred dimension.
facets.cognitive: Neither contemplation nor sacred action—life as mere existing.
facets.psychic: The soul has neither skill nor insight, blundering through life without competence or understanding.

deficient.text:
Pure materialist—neither inner knowledge nor sacred action. Life lived entirely on surface, missing both dimensions.

Somatic: Body without ritual nor practice, mere biological function.

Emotional: Emotional life without sacred dimension.

Cognitive: Neither contemplation nor sacred action—life as mere existing.

Psychic: The soul has neither skill nor insight, blundering through life without competence or understanding.
deficient.vignette: Dan has neither real knowledge nor skillful action—drifting through his days uninformed and ineffective, the wisdom that would guide him and the competence that would enact it both undeveloped. Neither hand works well, and they never work together.

attributes.title: Parā and Aparā Vidyā — The Two Knowledges
attributes.aspect: The two knowledges are the higher and the lower: aparā, the knowledge of everything that can be learned, named, and stored — including scripture-as-information; and parā, the knowledge by which the imperishable Self is directly realized. The crucial teaching is that no quantity of the lower ever adds up to the higher — they are different in kind, not degree. A library of spiritual books is aparā; a single moment of direct recognition is parā. The whole danger of the educated seeker is to mistake an abundance of the first for the second.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — an epistemic distinction · Valence: Neutral — a discernment to make · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Framework (clarifies how one knows) · Key relation: the two knowledges — the higher (of Brahman) and the lower (of the world and texts).
coordinate.key_relation: the two knowledges — the higher (of Brahman) and the lower (of the world and texts).

balanced.label: Balanced — using lower knowledge in service of the higher realization

facets.overview: Mastery of both lower knowledge (worldly) and higher knowledge (spiritual). Each in its domain, with proper priority.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in both worldly skill and spiritual practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional balance between worldly engagement and transcendent realization.
facets.cognitive: Mind cultivating both dimensions of knowledge wisely.
facets.psychic: The soul honors both knowledges in their place — worldly learning for the world, higher knowing for the Self — and never confuses the map for the territory.

balanced.text:
Mastery of both lower knowledge (worldly) and higher knowledge (spiritual). Each in its domain, with proper priority.

Somatic: Body engaged in both worldly skill and spiritual practice.

Emotional: Emotional balance between worldly engagement and transcendent realization.

Cognitive: Mind cultivating both dimensions of knowledge wisely.

Psychic: The soul honors both knowledges in their place — worldly learning for the world, higher knowing for the Self — and never confuses the map for the territory.
balanced.vignette: Ravi holds the two knowledges in right relation—the perishable knowledge of the world and the imperishable knowledge of the Self—using the first to live and the second to be free. He’s at home in both, and mistakes neither for the other.

excess.label: In excess — vast information mistaken for realization; the learned-but-unfree

facets.overview: Excessive lower knowledge as ego-asset, or premature claim to higher knowledge without preparation.
facets.somatic: Body burdened with skill-display.
facets.emotional: Pride in scholarship or spiritual pretension.
facets.cognitive: Confusion of lower for higher, or claiming higher without grounding.
facets.psychic: Lower knowledge is mistaken for the highest; the soul accumulates information about Brahman and takes erudition for realization.

excess.text:
Excessive lower knowledge as ego-asset, or premature claim to higher knowledge without preparation.

Somatic: Body burdened with skill-display.

Emotional: Pride in scholarship or spiritual pretension.

Cognitive: Confusion of lower for higher, or claiming higher without grounding.

Psychic: Lower knowledge is mistaken for the highest; the soul accumulates information about Brahman and takes erudition for realization.
excess.vignette: Karl clutches only the ‘higher’ knowledge and lets his worldly life decay—escapist, impractical, above the mere lower knowledge that pays the rent. The exaltation of the imperishable has become a neglect of the perishable he still has to live in.

deficient.label: Deficient — neither real learning nor realization; ungrounded in either

facets.overview: Neither lower nor higher knowledge—life of ignorance in both dimensions.
facets.somatic: Body without skill or practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without depth.
facets.cognitive: Mind in ignorance of both worldly and spiritual.
facets.psychic: The soul has neither competent worldly knowledge nor the higher kind, and lives in a doubled ignorance.

deficient.text:
Neither lower nor higher knowledge—life of ignorance in both dimensions.

Somatic: Body without skill or practice.

Emotional: Emotional life without depth.

Cognitive: Mind in ignorance of both worldly and spiritual.

Psychic: The soul has neither competent worldly knowledge nor the higher kind, and lives in a doubled ignorance.
deficient.vignette: For Dan there’s only the lower, perishable knowledge—the facts and skills of the world—and the imperishable knowledge that liberates is a rumor from another life. He knows a great deal about how things work and nothing about what he is.

attributes.title: Impersonal Divine (TAT vs THOU)
attributes.aspect: The divine is not a person—personhood is itself illusion. Therefore no I-Thou relationship is possible. The divine is ‘That’—pure existence without personality. One cannot be related TO the divine; one can only become ONE with it.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — an epistemic distinction · Valence: Neutral — a discernment to make · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Framework (clarifies how one knows) · Key relation: the Absolute as TAT (That, impersonal) vs THOU (personal) — two modes of knowing the Divine.
coordinate.key_relation: the Absolute as TAT (That, impersonal) vs THOU (personal) — two modes of knowing the Divine.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Understanding the divine as ‘That’—the impersonal source. Relationship becomes impossible; merger becomes the only way.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as appearance in impersonal field.
facets.emotional: Emotional movement from personal devotion to impersonal merging.
facets.cognitive: Mind drops personal-divine concept, opens to impersonal That-ness.
facets.psychic: The soul holds both faces of the Absolute — the impersonal That and the intimate Thou — without forcing a choice between them.

balanced.text:
Understanding the divine as ‘That’—the impersonal source. Relationship becomes impossible; merger becomes the only way.

Somatic: Body recognized as appearance in impersonal field.

Emotional: Emotional movement from personal devotion to impersonal merging.

Cognitive: Mind drops personal-divine concept, opens to impersonal That-ness.

Psychic: The soul holds both faces of the Absolute — the impersonal That and the intimate Thou — without forcing a choice between them.
balanced.vignette: Ravi can hold the divine both ways—as the intimate ‘Thou’ he can love and the impersonal ‘That’ he can dissolve into—without needing to choose. The personal gives his heart something to hold; the impersonal gives it somewhere to disappear.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Cold philosophical impersonalism that loses devotional warmth—dry metaphysics without heart.
facets.somatic: Body without devotional engagement.
facets.emotional: Loss of devotional warmth, cold spirituality.
facets.cognitive: Pure intellectual approach missing the heart dimension.
facets.psychic: The soul absolutizes the impersonal, using ‘it’s all formless’ to avoid the warmth and demand of relationship.

excess.text:
Cold philosophical impersonalism that loses devotional warmth—dry metaphysics without heart.

Somatic: Body without devotional engagement.

Emotional: Loss of devotional warmth, cold spirituality.

Cognitive: Pure intellectual approach missing the heart dimension.

Psychic: The soul absolutizes the impersonal, using ‘it’s all formless’ to avoid the warmth and demand of relationship.
excess.vignette: Sam has embraced only the impersonal Absolute—‘That,’ the formless ground—and dismisses all devotion to a personal ‘Thou’ as primitive sentiment. In choosing the impersonal so exclusively, he’s gone cold, and half the truth has hardened into a proud abstraction.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Pure anthropomorphism—God as super-person, sectarian devotion to specific form, never reaching impersonal Source.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in personal-deity rituals without transcendence.
facets.emotional: Emotional dependence on personal-form deity.
facets.cognitive: Mind stuck in personal-divine concept, unable to transcend to impersonal.
facets.psychic: The soul knows only a personal deity to bargain with, never sensing the formless ground beyond all names.

deficient.text:
Pure anthropomorphism—God as super-person, sectarian devotion to specific form, never reaching impersonal Source.

Somatic: Body engaged in personal-deity rituals without transcendence.

Emotional: Emotional dependence on personal-form deity.

Cognitive: Mind stuck in personal-divine concept, unable to transcend to impersonal.

Psychic: The soul knows only a personal deity to bargain with, never sensing the formless ground beyond all names.
deficient.vignette: For Dan the divine, if it exists, can only be a person—a big someone in the sky—and the notion of an impersonal Absolute, a formless ground, is unthinkable to him. He can grasp only the ‘Thou,’ and the vast impersonal ‘That’ finds no purchase.

attributes.title: Naming the Divine (Indication, not Definition)
attributes.aspect: Naming the divine is necessary for relating, but every name is artificial. The Upanishads use negative theology (neti-neti) to avoid creating positive limitations. To say ‘He is one’ creates the conflict ‘we say He is all.’ To say ‘He is not two’ includes both possibilities.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — indication, not definition · Valence: Neutral · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework (names point to, cannot define, Brahman) · Key relation: The Absolute indicated (lakṣaṇā), never defined.
coordinate.causal_status: Framework (names point to, cannot define, Brahman)
coordinate.key_relation: The Absolute indicated (lakṣaṇā), never defined.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Names used as devices, not as truths. Free to use any name to call to the divine, knowing all names point beyond themselves to the same reality.
facets.somatic: Body free to participate in any ritual without dogmatic attachment, healthy religious flexibility.
facets.emotional: Emotional openness to all names of divine, freedom from sectarian passion.
facets.cognitive: Mind understands names as fingers pointing to the moon—not the moon itself.
facets.psychic: The soul uses words for the Divine as indications, not definitions — fingers pointing, never the moon itself.

balanced.text:
Names used as devices, not as truths. Free to use any name to call to the divine, knowing all names point beyond themselves to the same reality.

Somatic: Body free to participate in any ritual without dogmatic attachment, healthy religious flexibility.

Emotional: Emotional openness to all names of divine, freedom from sectarian passion.

Cognitive: Mind understands names as fingers pointing to the moon—not the moon itself.

Psychic: The soul uses words for the Divine as indications, not definitions — fingers pointing, never the moon itself.
balanced.vignette: Ravi holds his words for the divine lightly—knowing that every name only points, never captures, that ‘God’ and ‘Brahman’ are fingers indicating a moon no word can hold. The names serve his reaching without imprisoning it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Attachment to one name as ‘the only name’—creating sectarian religion, conflict with those who use different names, religious violence.
facets.somatic: Aggressive religious display, body tense in defending name/form.
facets.emotional: Sectarian passion, hatred for those of other names, religious zealotry.
facets.cognitive: Mental fundamentalism, mistaking word for reality, debating name superiority.
facets.psychic: The soul mistakes its names and concepts for the reality, worshipping the description and missing the described.

excess.text:
Attachment to one name as ‘the only name’—creating sectarian religion, conflict with those who use different names, religious violence.

Somatic: Aggressive religious display, body tense in defending name/form.

Emotional: Sectarian passion, hatred for those of other names, religious zealotry.

Cognitive: Mental fundamentalism, mistaking word for reality, debating name superiority.

Psychic: The soul mistakes its names and concepts for the reality, worshipping the description and missing the described.
excess.vignette: Sam mistakes the name for the thing—clinging to his particular word for the sacred, warring over vocabulary, certain his label captures the reality. He’s frozen a pointer into a possession, and defends the finger while forgetting the moon.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Rejection of all names creates dry intellectualism—unable to relate emotionally to the divine, philosophy without warmth.
facets.somatic: Body without devotional engagement, no participation in religious life.
facets.emotional: Emotional coldness, no devotional warmth, dry philosophizing.
facets.cognitive: Pure intellectual approach, missing the relational dimension that names enable.
facets.psychic: The soul, finding no name adequate, abandons the search and loses the pointer along with the pointed-to.

deficient.text:
Rejection of all names creates dry intellectualism—unable to relate emotionally to the divine, philosophy without warmth.

Somatic: Body without devotional engagement, no participation in religious life.

Emotional: Emotional coldness, no devotional warmth, dry philosophizing.

Cognitive: Pure intellectual approach, missing the relational dimension that names enable.

Psychic: The soul, finding no name adequate, abandons the search and loses the pointer along with the pointed-to.
deficient.vignette: For Dan the names of the divine are just noises—‘God,’ ‘Brahman,’ ‘the Self’—pointing to nothing he can find. The reality the names are meant to indicate is absent from his experience, so the words hover, referring to a moon he’s never seen.

attributes.title: Symptoms vs Disease (Sankara’s Diagnostic)
attributes.aspect: Critical diagnostic insight: anxiety, depression, addiction, restlessness are SYMPTOMS, not the disease. The actual disease is mis-identification (taking non-Self as Self). Treating symptoms relieves temporarily; treating mis-identification cures. Therapy stabilizes personality; Vedanta dissolves mis-identity. They operate at different depths.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — an epistemic distinction · Valence: Neutral — a discernment to make · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Framework (clarifies how one knows) · Key relation: Śaṅkara’s diagnostic — treating symptoms vs the root disease of ignorance.
coordinate.key_relation: Śaṅkara’s diagnostic — treating symptoms vs the root disease of ignorance.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Both levels acknowledged. Symptoms addressed through psychology where appropriate; root through Vedanta. Different tools for different depths.
facets.somatic: Body symptoms treated competently while deeper work proceeds.
facets.emotional: Emotional issues addressed at appropriate level.
facets.cognitive: Mind understands distinction between symptom-level and disease-level work.
facets.psychic: The soul learns to treat the root, not the symptom — addressing ignorance itself rather than its endless surface effects.

balanced.text:
Both levels acknowledged. Symptoms addressed through psychology where appropriate; root through Vedanta. Different tools for different depths.

Somatic: Body symptoms treated competently while deeper work proceeds.

Emotional: Emotional issues addressed at appropriate level.

Cognitive: Mind understands distinction between symptom-level and disease-level work.

Psychic: The soul learns to treat the root, not the symptom — addressing ignorance itself rather than its endless surface effects.
balanced.vignette: When his anxiety flares, Ravi doesn’t just quiet the symptom—he asks what’s underneath, treating the root rather than chasing the surface. Sankara’s diagnostic has become his habit: he looks past the presenting complaint to the disease that generates it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Either Vedantic dismissal of psychological work (‘it’s all maya’) OR psychological dismissal of Vedanta. Either-or fallacy.
facets.somatic: Body symptoms ignored while claiming spiritual transcendence.
facets.emotional: Emotional issues bypassed in name of Vedanta.
facets.cognitive: Either-or thinking missing both-and reality.
facets.psychic: The soul becomes obsessed with diagnosing causes, analyzing the disease while never taking the cure.

excess.text:
Either Vedantic dismissal of psychological work (‘it’s all maya’) OR psychological dismissal of Vedanta. Either-or fallacy.

Somatic: Body symptoms ignored while claiming spiritual transcendence.

Emotional: Emotional issues bypassed in name of Vedanta.

Cognitive: Either-or thinking missing both-and reality.

Psychic: The soul becomes obsessed with diagnosing causes, analyzing the disease while never taking the cure.
excess.vignette: Sam is forever diagnosing the root—analyzing the underlying cause of everything, tracing each surface trouble to its deepest source—and never actually treating anything. The wise focus on the root has become an endless analysis that never reaches the cure.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Only symptom-treatment, never root work. Endless therapy without realization. OR endless inquiry without addressing functional symptoms.
facets.somatic: Body symptoms cycling forever.
facets.emotional: Emotional issues endlessly processed without resolution.
facets.cognitive: Mind never reaches root cause.
facets.psychic: The soul chases symptom after symptom, never suspecting the single ignorance that generates them all.

deficient.text:
Only symptom-treatment, never root work. Endless therapy without realization. OR endless inquiry without addressing functional symptoms.

Somatic: Body symptoms cycling forever.

Emotional: Emotional issues endlessly processed without resolution.

Cognitive: Mind never reaches root cause.

Psychic: The soul chases symptom after symptom, never suspecting the single ignorance that generates them all.
deficient.vignette: Dan treats only symptoms—medicating the anxiety, distracting from the emptiness, patching each surface trouble as it arises—with no sense that they share a root. He fights the same recurring symptoms forever, never once asking what disease keeps producing them.

attributes.title: Neti-Neti — Knowing by Negation
attributes.aspect: Neti-neti, ‘not this, not this,’ is the via negativa of the Upaniṣads — the method of approaching the Self by systematically negating everything it is not. Not the body, not the senses, not the breath, not the mind, not any object that can be known — for all of these are things the Self knows, and the knower can never be among the known. What remains when every ‘this’ has been set aside is not a final object but the objectless awareness itself, recognized as one’s own nature. It is knowing by subtraction, the only knowing adequate to what cannot be made an object.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — knowing by negation (‘not this, not this’) · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (negates all objects to reveal the Self) · Key relation: The via negativa of the Upaniṣads; kin to Viveka (Dim 23).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — knowing by negation (‘not this, not this’)
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (negates all objects to reveal the Self)
coordinate.key_relation: The via negativa of the Upaniṣads; kin to Viveka (Dim 23).

balanced.label: Balanced — the mind humbly pointing beyond itself, resting in not-knowing

facets.overview: Through systematic negation, false identifications drop. What remains after all negations cannot be negated—that is the Self. Then both positive and negative dissolve into Para-Brahman.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as not-Self, used freely without identification.
facets.emotional: Emotions seen as not-Self, witnessing freedom from emotional identification.
facets.cognitive: Mind seen as not-Self, what remains as witness cannot be negated—Self realized.
facets.psychic: The soul approaches the Absolute by negation — ‘not this, not this’ — clearing away every limited concept until only the unsayable remains.

balanced.text:
Through systematic negation, false identifications drop. What remains after all negations cannot be negated—that is the Self. Then both positive and negative dissolve into Para-Brahman.

Somatic: Body recognized as not-Self, used freely without identification.

Emotional: Emotions seen as not-Self, witnessing freedom from emotional identification.

Cognitive: Mind seen as not-Self, what remains as witness cannot be negated—Self realized.

Psychic: The soul approaches the Absolute by negation — ‘not this, not this’ — clearing away every limited concept until only the unsayable remains.
balanced.vignette: When Ravi asks ‘who am I?’, he sets aside each answer—not the body, not the emotions, not the thoughts—until what remains is the awareness that was doing the setting-aside. The negation is a doorway; by clearing away what he isn’t, he arrives at what he is.

excess.label: In excess — negation as nihilism or world-denial; stripping that becomes coldness

facets.overview: Endless negation that becomes nihilism—‘I am nothing, life is nothing’—missing that the positive flowers spontaneously after total negation.
facets.somatic: Body neglect through nihilistic denial.
facets.emotional: Emotional flatness from negating everything without realizing positive.
facets.cognitive: Mental nihilism, life as meaningless, missing the breakthrough to fullness.
facets.psychic: Negation becomes nihilism; the soul uses ‘not this’ to deny everything, including the reality the method was meant to disclose.

excess.text:
Endless negation that becomes nihilism—‘I am nothing, life is nothing’—missing that the positive flowers spontaneously after total negation.

Somatic: Body neglect through nihilistic denial.

Emotional: Emotional flatness from negating everything without realizing positive.

Cognitive: Mental nihilism, life as meaningless, missing the breakthrough to fullness.

Psychic: Negation becomes nihilism; the soul uses ‘not this’ to deny everything, including the reality the method was meant to disclose.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned ‘not this, not this’ into a nihilism—negating everything, valuing nothing, using the method to dismiss the whole world as unreal and himself along with it. The tool for finding the Self has become a corrosive that dissolves meaning wholesale.

deficient.label: Deficient — grasping at the Absolute as one more object to be defined and possessed

facets.overview: Premature positive affirmation without negation—‘I am Brahman’ as mantra without realization. Imagination mistaken for realization.
facets.somatic: Body remains identified despite positive claims.
facets.emotional: Same emotional patterns despite affirmations.
facets.cognitive: Mind playing with positive concepts, auto-hypnosis, false realization.
facets.psychic: The soul clutches every concept as final; without the cleansing ‘no,’ it mistakes its images of the divine for the divine itself.

deficient.text:
Premature positive affirmation without negation—‘I am Brahman’ as mantra without realization. Imagination mistaken for realization.

Somatic: Body remains identified despite positive claims.

Emotional: Same emotional patterns despite affirmations.

Cognitive: Mind playing with positive concepts, auto-hypnosis, false realization.

Psychic: The soul clutches every concept as final; without the cleansing ‘no,’ it mistakes its images of the divine for the divine itself.
deficient.vignette: Dan can’t set anything aside—he is his body, his moods, his thoughts, each identification total, no distance anywhere. The negating inquiry that would reveal the witness beneath is not something he’s failed at; it’s a move he’s never made.

attributes.title: Choicelessness (Mind as Choice-Maker)
attributes.aspect: Mind functions through choice—creating dichotomies that don’t exist in reality. Choosing one side means choosing the other (love implies hate). Either embrace both, or remain choiceless—both dissolve the mind.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: State / stance — awareness prior to the choosing mind · Valence: The fruit / cultivated stance · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Fruit (awareness before choice) · Key relation: Choiceless awareness; the mind seen as the choice-maker to transcend.
coordinate.ontic_mode: State / stance — awareness prior to the choosing mind
coordinate.key_relation: Choiceless awareness; the mind seen as the choice-maker to transcend.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Choiceless awareness sees all as one continuum. Death is not against life; hate is not against love—they are poles. Mind dissolves when not choosing.
facets.somatic: Body relaxed without internal conflict, no fighting one half of life against the other.
facets.emotional: Emotional acceptance of all polarities, no internal civil war.
facets.cognitive: Mind beyond duality, choiceless witness, peace beyond preference.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in choiceless awareness, watching the mind’s preferences arise and pass without being driven by them.

balanced.text:
Choiceless awareness sees all as one continuum. Death is not against life; hate is not against love—they are poles. Mind dissolves when not choosing.

Somatic: Body relaxed without internal conflict, no fighting one half of life against the other.

Emotional: Emotional acceptance of all polarities, no internal civil war.

Cognitive: Mind beyond duality, choiceless witness, peace beyond preference.

Psychic: The soul rests in choiceless awareness, watching the mind’s preferences arise and pass without being driven by them.
balanced.vignette: In stillness, Ravi has tasted a choiceless awareness—a resting in what is, prior to the mind’s endless picking and rejecting. It hasn’t made him passive; it’s freed him from the exhausting compulsion to control, so he can act cleanly when action is called for.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced choicelessness—pretending to have no preferences while still divided inside. Or claiming acceptance of all while suppressing real choices.
facets.somatic: Body’s natural preferences suppressed in name of choicelessness.
facets.emotional: False neutrality covering real feelings, suppression masquerading as wisdom.
facets.cognitive: Mind pretending unity while still divided, intellectual concept of choicelessness.
facets.psychic: The soul makes ‘choicelessness’ an excuse for passivity, refusing necessary decisions in the name of non-doing.

excess.text:
Forced choicelessness—pretending to have no preferences while still divided inside. Or claiming acceptance of all while suppressing real choices.

Somatic: Body’s natural preferences suppressed in name of choicelessness.

Emotional: False neutrality covering real feelings, suppression masquerading as wisdom.

Cognitive: Mind pretending unity while still divided, intellectual concept of choicelessness.

Psychic: The soul makes ‘choicelessness’ an excuse for passivity, refusing necessary decisions in the name of non-doing.
excess.vignette: Sam invokes ‘choiceless awareness’ to dodge decisions—refusing to choose a direction, commit to a path, or take responsibility, all dressed up as spiritual non-doership. The teaching about resting prior to the divided mind has become an excuse for drift.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Constant choosing creates constant suffering—when chosen pleasant turns to unpleasant, when chosen friend becomes enemy. Whole life as choice-suffering.
facets.somatic: Body in constant tension of preferring and rejecting.
facets.emotional: Emotional turmoil from constant choosing, suffering when chosen turns.
facets.cognitive: Mind exhausted by perpetual choice-making, mental civil war.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly identified with the choosing mind, swept by every preference and aversion.

deficient.text:
Constant choosing creates constant suffering—when chosen pleasant turns to unpleasant, when chosen friend becomes enemy. Whole life as choice-suffering.

Somatic: Body in constant tension of preferring and rejecting.

Emotional: Emotional turmoil from constant choosing, suffering when chosen turns.

Cognitive: Mind exhausted by perpetual choice-making, mental civil war.

Psychic: The soul is wholly identified with the choosing mind, swept by every preference and aversion.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s mind is a churning factory of choice—weighing, picking, rejecting, second-guessing, never resting for an instant in simple awareness. The choiceless ground beneath the choosing mind is a stillness he’s never touched; even his rest is a deciding.

attributes.title: Subjectivity as Spirituality
attributes.aspect: The divine cannot be objectified—it is pure subjectivity. To know it, one must become it. Religion is not knowledge ABOUT the divine but living AS the divine. Subjectivity is spirituality; objectivity is science.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — an epistemic distinction · Valence: Neutral — a discernment to make · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Framework (clarifies how one knows) · Key relation: the turn from object to subject — spirituality as the deepening of subjectivity.
coordinate.key_relation: the turn from object to subject — spirituality as the deepening of subjectivity.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When the divine is approached subjectively—lived rather than known—transformation happens. One becomes what one seeks.
facets.somatic: Body becomes vehicle of subjective realization, alive with presence.
facets.emotional: Emotions infused with the felt presence of the divine.
facets.cognitive: Mind drops object-orientation, becomes pure subject—the divine itself.
facets.psychic: The soul turns toward the subject — the awareness that knows — recognizing the spiritual quest as inward, not a hunt among objects.

balanced.text:
When the divine is approached subjectively—lived rather than known—transformation happens. One becomes what one seeks.

Somatic: Body becomes vehicle of subjective realization, alive with presence.

Emotional: Emotions infused with the felt presence of the divine.

Cognitive: Mind drops object-orientation, becomes pure subject—the divine itself.

Psychic: The soul turns toward the subject — the awareness that knows — recognizing the spiritual quest as inward, not a hunt among objects.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to honor his inner life as the real ground—the place where meaning, awareness, and value actually live—rather than chasing reality only in the outer world. Turning toward subjectivity has deepened him; the within, he’s found, is where the real is nearest.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Pure subjectivism cut off from any external reference—solipsism, dismissing all objective dimensions of life.
facets.somatic: Neglect of objective physical realities.
facets.emotional: Emotional life cut off from relational dimension.
facets.cognitive: Mental subjectivism, dismissal of any shared reality.
facets.psychic: The soul collapses into self-absorption, mistaking preoccupation with its own states for genuine inwardness.

excess.text:
Pure subjectivism cut off from any external reference—solipsism, dismissing all objective dimensions of life.

Somatic: Neglect of objective physical realities.

Emotional: Emotional life cut off from relational dimension.

Cognitive: Mental subjectivism, dismissal of any shared reality.

Psychic: The soul collapses into self-absorption, mistaking preoccupation with its own states for genuine inwardness.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned ‘subjectivity is spirituality’ into pure self-absorption—endlessly examining his own states, his own depths, his own specialness, with no window left onto anyone else. The honoring of inner life has curdled into a solipsistic hall of mirrors.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Pure objectivism—trying to know the divine as one knows a chemical, missing it entirely. Religion approached scientifically.
facets.somatic: Body never knows the felt presence of the divine.
facets.emotional: No emotional contact with the sacred.
facets.cognitive: Mind treats divine as object, accumulates knowledge ABOUT, never realizes.
facets.psychic: The soul looks only outward, seeking itself among objects, never turning to face the one who looks.

deficient.text:
Pure objectivism—trying to know the divine as one knows a chemical, missing it entirely. Religion approached scientifically.

Somatic: Body never knows the felt presence of the divine.

Emotional: No emotional contact with the sacred.

Cognitive: Mind treats divine as object, accumulates knowledge ABOUT, never realizes.

Psychic: The soul looks only outward, seeking itself among objects, never turning to face the one who looks.
deficient.vignette: Dan lives entirely in the outer—facts, objects, achievements, the measurable world—with no felt inner life to speak of and no sense that the within is where reality is nearest. The subjective ground the teaching honors is, to him, unreal; only the outside counts.

attributes.title: Outward Vision vs Inward Vision
attributes.aspect: Lahiri makes a crucial distinction: outward vision (scholarly understanding of scripture) and inward vision (direct experiential revelation through Kriya). Scholarly study comes first—necessary preparation. But it does not produce inward vision automatically. Inward vision is revealed only through Guru-initiation in the lineage. Both are necessary; outward without inward is barren scholarship.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — an epistemic distinction · Valence: Neutral — a discernment to make · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Framework (clarifies how one knows) · Key relation: the senses turned outward vs the rare turn inward (Katha).
coordinate.key_relation: the senses turned outward vs the rare turn inward (Katha).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Outward vision (scholarly study) supplemented by inward vision (initiated practice). Both honored in their place.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in both study and practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional satisfaction from both dimensions.
facets.cognitive: Mind cultivating both scholarly understanding and direct revelation.
facets.psychic: The soul learns, like the Kaṭha’s rare one, to turn the gaze inward toward the immortal Self rather than always outward.

balanced.text:
Outward vision (scholarly study) supplemented by inward vision (initiated practice). Both honored in their place.

Somatic: Body engaged in both study and practice.

Emotional: Emotional satisfaction from both dimensions.

Cognitive: Mind cultivating both scholarly understanding and direct revelation.

Psychic: The soul learns, like the Kaṭha’s rare one, to turn the gaze inward toward the immortal Self rather than always outward.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has learned to turn his gaze both ways—out to meet the world skillfully and in to know himself—shifting between the two as life requires. Neither the outward vision nor the inward has swallowed the other; he sees where he’s going and who is going.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Either pure scholarship dismissing practice, or pure practice dismissing scholarship.
facets.somatic: Body either in endless study or in mystical-only practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional imbalance toward one pole.
facets.cognitive: Mind either over-intellectualized or anti-intellectual.
facets.psychic: The soul over-rejects the outer world, forcing an inwardness that despises ordinary perception.

excess.text:
Either pure scholarship dismissing practice, or pure practice dismissing scholarship.

Somatic: Body either in endless study or in mystical-only practice.

Emotional: Emotional imbalance toward one pole.

Cognitive: Mind either over-intellectualized or anti-intellectual.

Psychic: The soul over-rejects the outer world, forcing an inwardness that despises ordinary perception.
excess.vignette: Karl gazes only inward now—so absorbed in his own depths that the outer world and the people in it have gone dim, neglected, unmet. The inward vision the teaching prizes has become a withdrawal, and he’s turned his back on a life still happening around him.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Neither outward nor inward vision. Religious life without serious study or practice.
facets.somatic: Body without genuine engagement in either.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without spiritual development.
facets.cognitive: Mind without cultivation in either direction.
facets.psychic: The soul’s eyes are fixed entirely outward, drawn by sense-objects, the inward turn never made.

deficient.text:
Neither outward nor inward vision. Religious life without serious study or practice.

Somatic: Body without genuine engagement in either.

Emotional: Emotional life without spiritual development.

Cognitive: Mind without cultivation in either direction.

Psychic: The soul’s eyes are fixed entirely outward, drawn by sense-objects, the inward turn never made.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s gaze goes only outward—forever fixed on the world, the tasks, the screen—and never once turns back on the one who’s looking. The inward vision that would let him know himself is a direction he’s never faced; he sees everything but the seer.

attributes.title: Preyas and Śreyas — The Pleasant and the Good
attributes.aspect: At every choice point, the Kaṭha Upaniṣad teaches, two paths present themselves: the preyas, the pleasant, which gratifies the senses and the ego in the moment, and the śreyas, the good, which genuinely serves one’s deepest welfare though it may cost something now. They rarely coincide. The whole quality of a life is set by which one is habitually chosen — and the discriminating intelligence that can tell them apart, and the strength to choose the good over the merely pleasant, is the very faculty the path is built to develop.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — an epistemic distinction · Valence: Neutral — a discernment to make · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Framework (clarifies how one knows) · Key relation: the pleasant (preyas) vs the good (śreyas) — the fork the Katha names.
coordinate.key_relation: the pleasant (preyas) vs the good (śreyas) — the fork the Katha names.

balanced.label: Balanced — the good discerned and chosen over the merely pleasant

facets.overview: Wisdom to discriminate between immediate pleasure and ultimate good. Choosing shreyas consistently transforms life.
facets.somatic: Body chooses long-term wellbeing over short-term indulgence.
facets.emotional: Emotional choices favor depth over surface pleasure.
facets.cognitive: Mind discriminates wisely between preyas and shreyas.
facets.psychic: At each fork the soul can tell the merely pleasant (preyas) from the truly good (śreyas), and has the strength to choose the good.

balanced.text:
Wisdom to discriminate between immediate pleasure and ultimate good. Choosing shreyas consistently transforms life.

Somatic: Body chooses long-term wellbeing over short-term indulgence.

Emotional: Emotional choices favor depth over surface pleasure.

Cognitive: Mind discriminates wisely between preyas and shreyas.

Psychic: At each fork the soul can tell the merely pleasant (preyas) from the truly good (śreyas), and has the strength to choose the good.
balanced.vignette: Offered the pleasant and the good, Meera can tell them apart and, when it matters, chooses the good—the harder, truer thing over the sweeter, easier one. It doesn’t make her joyless; she still enjoys the pleasant when it’s harmless, but the good has her allegiance.

excess.label: In excess — grim duty-for-its-own-sake; rejecting all pleasure as suspect

facets.overview: Rigid pursuit of shreyas rejecting all pleasure—joyless asceticism, missing the play of life.
facets.somatic: Body deprived of healthy pleasure.
facets.emotional: Joyless emotional life, harsh choice-making.
facets.cognitive: Mind rigid in rejecting all preyas.
facets.psychic: Discrimination hardens into joyless severity; the soul rejects every pleasant thing on principle, mistaking grimness for wisdom.

excess.text:
Rigid pursuit of shreyas rejecting all pleasure—joyless asceticism, missing the play of life.

Somatic: Body deprived of healthy pleasure.

Emotional: Joyless emotional life, harsh choice-making.

Cognitive: Mind rigid in rejecting all preyas.

Psychic: Discrimination hardens into joyless severity; the soul rejects every pleasant thing on principle, mistaking grimness for wisdom.
excess.vignette: Ravi has turned choosing the good into a grim war on the pleasant—refusing every enjoyment, suspecting every ease, treating all sweetness as a temptation to be conquered. The wise preference for sreyas has hardened into a joyless asceticism that mistakes misery for virtue.

deficient.label: Deficient — the pleasant chosen at every fork; no compass for the good

facets.overview: Always choosing preyas—immediate pleasure—falling away from ultimate good. Like Nachiketa’s father who chose ephemeral things.
facets.somatic: Body driven by immediate pleasure, harmful indulgences.
facets.emotional: Emotional life ruled by gratification-seeking.
facets.cognitive: Mind always chooses momentary over enduring.
facets.psychic: The soul reaches always for the pleasant, never the good; ruled by immediate gratification, it forfeits the lasting for the nice.

deficient.text:
Always choosing preyas—immediate pleasure—falling away from ultimate good. Like Nachiketa’s father who chose ephemeral things.

Somatic: Body driven by immediate pleasure, harmful indulgences.

Emotional: Emotional life ruled by gratification-seeking.

Cognitive: Mind always chooses momentary over enduring.

Psychic: The soul reaches always for the pleasant, never the good; ruled by immediate gratification, it forfeits the lasting for the nice.
deficient.vignette: Dan reaches, every time, for the pleasant—the easy, the sweet, the immediately gratifying—and the good, the harder thing that would actually serve him, is never chosen. He can’t tell preyas from sreyas because he’s never once picked the second over the first.

attributes.title: Sky/Space Metaphor (Inner Suchness)
attributes.aspect: Sky/space is the perfect metaphor for the Self. Sky contains everything, is touched by nothing. The inner self has the same quality—all experiences happen IN it but never touch it. Recognition of this inner sky-nature is jivanmukti.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / pointer · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: space / sky as the image of inner suchness; kin to Ākāśa (Dim 1 / 25).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: space / sky as the image of inner suchness; kin to Ākāśa (Dim 1 / 25).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Self known as inner sky—all experiences seen as clouds passing through unchanging space. Suchness, eternal virginity recognized.
facets.somatic: Body experienced as activity within space-like self.
facets.emotional: Emotions seen as clouds in sky-like consciousness.
facets.cognitive: Mind activities seen as movements in unchanging space.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes its awareness as sky-like — spacious, untouched by the clouds of thought and feeling passing through it.

balanced.text:
Self known as inner sky—all experiences seen as clouds passing through unchanging space. Suchness, eternal virginity recognized.

Somatic: Body experienced as activity within space-like self.

Emotional: Emotions seen as clouds in sky-like consciousness.

Cognitive: Mind activities seen as movements in unchanging space.

Psychic: The soul recognizes its awareness as sky-like — spacious, untouched by the clouds of thought and feeling passing through it.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to rest in a sky-like awareness—spacious, open, letting thoughts and feelings pass like weather without being disturbed by any of them. The inner spaciousness has made him less reactive; there’s room in him now for whatever arises.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Claiming sky-nature intellectually while still emotionally reactive—pretense of detachment.
facets.somatic: Body shows reactivity while claiming space-nature.
facets.emotional: Suppressed emotions disguised as space-like detachment.
facets.cognitive: Mental claim of sky-status without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul clings to the image of spaciousness as a state to maintain, gripping the very openness it describes.

excess.text:
Claiming sky-nature intellectually while still emotionally reactive—pretense of detachment.

Somatic: Body shows reactivity while claiming space-nature.

Emotional: Suppressed emotions disguised as space-like detachment.

Cognitive: Mental claim of sky-status without realization.

Psychic: The soul clings to the image of spaciousness as a state to maintain, gripping the very openness it describes.
excess.vignette: Sam has ‘become the vast sky’ so thoroughly that he floats through his days ungrounded and vague, missing appointments, forgetting the concrete, too spacious to bother with the actual. He’s mistaken the spaced-out for the spacious.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Identification with every passing experience as if self IS the cloud, not the sky. No space-recognition.
facets.somatic: Body experiences claimed as self.
facets.emotional: Every emotion is ‘me,’ total identification.
facets.cognitive: Mind contents fully identified with as self.
facets.psychic: The soul identifies with every passing cloud, never sensing the sky in which they move.

deficient.text:
Identification with every passing experience as if self IS the cloud, not the sky. No space-recognition.

Somatic: Body experiences claimed as self.

Emotional: Every emotion is ‘me,’ total identification.

Cognitive: Mind contents fully identified with as self.

Psychic: The soul identifies with every passing cloud, never sensing the sky in which they move.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s inner world is cramped and crowded—no room, every worry pressed against the next, no sky-like openness anywhere. The spacious awareness the metaphor points to is foreign to him; his mind is a small, full box, and always has been.

attributes.title: Shrotrasya Shrotram (Ear of the Ear)
attributes.aspect: Brahman is the ear of the ear, the mind of the mind, the breath of the breath, the eye of the eye. The senses are organs—but what enables them to function as senses is the deeper consciousness.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / pointer · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the ‘Ear of the ear’ — the awareness behind the senses (Kena).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the ‘Ear of the ear’ — the awareness behind the senses (Kena).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition that consciousness is what makes senses work, not the senses making consciousness. The ‘mind of the mind’ is realized as the witnessing presence.
facets.somatic: Senses function but consciousness behind them is known.
facets.emotional: Emotional perception backed by witness-awareness.
facets.cognitive: The ‘mind of the mind’ realized—the witnessing presence behind thought.
facets.psychic: The soul turns toward the awareness behind the senses — the ear of the ear — that the senses themselves cannot grasp.

balanced.text:
Recognition that consciousness is what makes senses work, not the senses making consciousness. The ‘mind of the mind’ is realized as the witnessing presence.

Somatic: Senses function but consciousness behind them is known.

Emotional: Emotional perception backed by witness-awareness.

Cognitive: The ‘mind of the mind’ realized—the witnessing presence behind thought.

Psychic: The soul turns toward the awareness behind the senses — the ear of the ear — that the senses themselves cannot grasp.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to sense the awareness behind his senses—the hearing behind the hearing, the seeing behind the seeing—the knower that no sense can turn around and grasp. Resting toward this ‘ear of the ear’ has shifted his sense of what he is.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Intellectual conviction without realization—claiming to know the ‘mind of the mind’ as concept only.
facets.somatic: Body unchanged by intellectual claim.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity continues despite intellectual claim.
facets.cognitive: Concept of witness held intellectually without genuine inhering.
facets.psychic: The soul makes the teaching a clever paradox to admire, never letting it pierce to the seer behind seeing.

excess.text:
Intellectual conviction without realization—claiming to know the ‘mind of the mind’ as concept only.

Somatic: Body unchanged by intellectual claim.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity continues despite intellectual claim.

Cognitive: Concept of witness held intellectually without genuine inhering.

Psychic: The soul makes the teaching a clever paradox to admire, never letting it pierce to the seer behind seeing.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned ‘the ear of the ear’ into an intellectual puzzle he loves to pose—delighting in the paradox, debating the answer—and never once actually rests as the awareness it points to. He plays with the riddle and never follows it home.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete identification with the senses—‘I see, I hear’ without awareness of what enables these. Mind taken as ultimate.
facets.somatic: Senses fully identified with as self.
facets.emotional: Emotions taken as ultimate self-expression.
facets.cognitive: Mind taken as final reality, no awareness of consciousness behind.
facets.psychic: The soul takes the senses as self-operating, never asking who hears behind the hearing.

deficient.text:
Complete identification with the senses—‘I see, I hear’ without awareness of what enables these. Mind taken as ultimate.

Somatic: Senses fully identified with as self.

Emotional: Emotions taken as ultimate self-expression.

Cognitive: Mind taken as final reality, no awareness of consciousness behind.

Psychic: The soul takes the senses as self-operating, never asking who hears behind the hearing.
deficient.vignette: Dan is wholly absorbed in what he perceives—the sound, the sight, the sensation—with no sense of any awareness standing behind the senses. The knower prior to the known is not doubted by him; it’s simply never been noticed.

attributes.title: Gargi’s Inquiry (Beyond the Beyond)
attributes.aspect: Gargi Vachaknavi asks Yajnavalkya progressive questions: ‘On what is the earth woven, warp and woof?’ Each answer leads to another question ‘on what is THAT woven?’ Through worlds of water, air, sky, sun, moon, stars, gods, Indra-world, Prajapati-world, Brahma-world. Finally Yajnavalkya: ‘Don’t ask too much, Gargi, lest your head fall off.’ She finds the Akshara (Imperishable) on which all is woven.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / pointer · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: Gārgī’s questioning ‘beyond the beyond’ to the imperishable ground (Bṛhadāraṇyaka).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: Gārgī’s questioning ‘beyond the beyond’ to the imperishable ground (Bṛhadāraṇyaka).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Fierce intellectual inquiry that doesn’t stop at any answer until the ultimate is reached. Gargi’s persistence reveals the foundation of foundations.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in extended inquiry without distraction.
facets.emotional: Emotional courage to push beyond conventional answers.
facets.cognitive: Mind continues asking until it reaches the unaskable.
facets.psychic: The soul, like Gārgī, presses its questioning past every support to the unsupported ground on which all rests.

balanced.text:
Fierce intellectual inquiry that doesn’t stop at any answer until the ultimate is reached. Gargi’s persistence reveals the foundation of foundations.

Somatic: Body engaged in extended inquiry without distraction.

Emotional: Emotional courage to push beyond conventional answers.

Cognitive: Mind continues asking until it reaches the unaskable.

Psychic: The soul, like Gārgī, presses its questioning past every support to the unsupported ground on which all rests.
balanced.vignette: Like Gargi pressing Yajnavalkya, Ravi won’t stop at any comfortable answer—inquiring past each resting place toward what lies beyond the beyond. The relentless questioning isn’t restlessness; it’s an honest refusal to settle for less than the ultimate.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Endless intellectual probing without arrival—using questions as escape from realization.
facets.somatic: Body exhausted by perpetual inquiry.
facets.emotional: Emotional dissatisfaction without resolution.
facets.cognitive: Mind addicted to questioning, no synthesis.
facets.psychic: The soul turns relentless inquiry into intellectual one-upmanship, questioning to win rather than to know.

excess.text:
Endless intellectual probing without arrival—using questions as escape from realization.

Somatic: Body exhausted by perpetual inquiry.

Emotional: Emotional dissatisfaction without resolution.

Cognitive: Mind addicted to questioning, no synthesis.

Psychic: The soul turns relentless inquiry into intellectual one-upmanship, questioning to win rather than to know.
excess.vignette: Sam questions endlessly and rests nowhere—no answer ever enough, every insight discarded for the next, the inquiry become a treadmill he can’t step off. Gargi’s noble refusal to settle has curdled, in him, into an inability to ever arrive.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Accepting first answers without probing deeper. Settled understanding becomes barrier to truth.
facets.somatic: Body content with surface engagement.
facets.emotional: Emotional satisfaction at superficial level.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t push beyond conventional answers.
facets.psychic: The soul stops asking too soon, content with a penultimate answer, never reaching the final ground.

deficient.text:
Accepting first answers without probing deeper. Settled understanding becomes barrier to truth.

Somatic: Body content with surface engagement.

Emotional: Emotional satisfaction at superficial level.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t push beyond conventional answers.

Psychic: The soul stops asking too soon, content with a penultimate answer, never reaching the final ground.
deficient.vignette: Dan takes the first easy answer and stops—incurious, unquestioning, content with a shallow certainty he never presses. The relentless inquiry toward the ultimate is foreign to him; he’s camped on the ground floor and never wondered what’s above.

attributes.title: Yo Manyate Na Veda — He Who Thinks He Knows, Knows Not
attributes.aspect: The Kena’s great paradox: the one who claims to comprehend the Absolute thereby reveals that he has reduced it to an object and so missed it; the one who realizes that it cannot be comprehended as an object has come nearer the truth. ‘It is unknown to those who know and known to those who do not know.’ This is the epistemic humility at the heart of the tradition — not the false modesty of ‘I might be wrong,’ but the structural recognition that the knower can never be made into the known, and that the deepest knowing wears the face of a luminous not-knowing.
