coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — the paradox of knowing · Valence: Neutral · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework (he who thinks he knows, knows not) · Key relation: The Kena paradox — Brahman is unknown to the one who claims to know it.
coordinate.causal_status: Framework (he who thinks he knows, knows not)
coordinate.key_relation: The Kena paradox — Brahman is unknown to the one who claims to know it.

balanced.label: Balanced — luminous humility; deep knowing held as not-knowing

facets.overview: Held in the productive paradox—knowing that one doesn’t know in the ordinary sense, yet knowing through being. Humble certainty.
facets.somatic: Body holds the paradox without strain, presence in not-knowing.
facets.emotional: Emotional comfort with not-knowing, sacred mystery.
facets.cognitive: Mind holds the paradox, neither claiming nor denying knowledge.
facets.psychic: The soul holds its knowing in humility; sensing that the Absolute exceeds every thought, it stays open and unfinished before the mystery.

balanced.text:
Held in the productive paradox—knowing that one doesn’t know in the ordinary sense, yet knowing through being. Humble certainty.

Somatic: Body holds the paradox without strain, presence in not-knowing.

Emotional: Emotional comfort with not-knowing, sacred mystery.

Cognitive: Mind holds the paradox, neither claiming nor denying knowledge.

Psychic: The soul holds its knowing in humility; sensing that the Absolute exceeds every thought, it stays open and unfinished before the mystery.
balanced.vignette: The more Ravi realizes, the less he claims to know—holding his deepest insights with an open, humble not-knowing, aware that the moment he’s sure he’s grasped it, he’s lost it. His humility isn’t false modesty; it’s the very shape of real understanding.

excess.label: In excess — the pride of comprehension; ‘I have understood it’ as ego-claim

facets.overview: Spiritual pride in ‘not knowing’—claiming superiority through paradox, false humility.
facets.somatic: Body postures of pretended humility.
facets.emotional: Pride in claimed humility, spiritual one-upmanship.
facets.cognitive: Using paradox as new identity, not actual realization.
facets.psychic: The soul is certain it has grasped Brahman; intellectual conceit closes the very openness through which realization could come.

excess.text:
Spiritual pride in ‘not knowing’—claiming superiority through paradox, false humility.

Somatic: Body postures of pretended humility.

Emotional: Pride in claimed humility, spiritual one-upmanship.

Cognitive: Using paradox as new identity, not actual realization.

Psychic: The soul is certain it has grasped Brahman; intellectual conceit closes the very openness through which realization could come.
excess.vignette: Sam is certain he knows—announces his realization, corrects his teachers, wears his understanding as a settled possession. And by the teaching’s own measure, the certainty is the surest sign he hasn’t understood: he who thinks he knows, knows not.

deficient.label: Deficient — no inquiry at all; neither knowing nor the humility of not-knowing

facets.overview: Either claiming definite knowledge of Brahman (intellectual conceit) or denying it can be known (skeptical rejection). Both miss the paradox.
facets.somatic: Body confident in scriptural knowledge or dismissive.
facets.emotional: Either pride or rejection, no genuine humility.
facets.cognitive: Mind takes definite positions, missing the productive paradox.
facets.psychic: The soul never inquires at all; incurious, it neither claims nor seeks, and the question that could free it is never asked.

deficient.text:
Either claiming definite knowledge of Brahman (intellectual conceit) or denying it can be known (skeptical rejection). Both miss the paradox.

Somatic: Body confident in scriptural knowledge or dismissive.

Emotional: Either pride or rejection, no genuine humility.

Cognitive: Mind takes definite positions, missing the productive paradox.

Psychic: The soul never inquires at all; incurious, it neither claims nor seeks, and the question that could free it is never asked.
deficient.vignette: Dan neither knows nor is troubled by not knowing—no humble not-knowing, but a flat incuriosity that never approaches the question at all. The teaching’s subtle humility is beyond someone who has never once reached for the knowledge he’d then have to hold humbly.

attributes.title: Satchidananda (Existence-Consciousness-Bliss)
attributes.aspect: The three inseparable attributes of ultimate reality. To realize any one is to realize all three—they are not three separate things but one reality named from three angles. Existence is conscious, consciousness is blissful, bliss is existence.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — Being-Consciousness-Bliss (sat-cit-ānanda) · Valence: Neutral — the nature of Brahman · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Transcendent · Causal status: Ground · Key relation: The threefold nature of Brahman; kin to Satya-Jnana-Ananta (Dim 25).
coordinate.key_relation: The threefold nature of Brahman; kin to Satya-Jnana-Ananta (Dim 25).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Realization that the changeless ‘I am’ through all life-changes is the doorway. The eternal existence-consciousness-bliss is recognized as one’s actual nature.
facets.somatic: Body experiences itself as ever-changing while the ‘I am’ remains constant—a profound dis-identification.
facets.emotional: Emotional sense of permanence beneath constant change.
facets.cognitive: Mind discovers the unchanging witness through changing states.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes its own nature as existence-consciousness-bliss, and rests in the fullness it had sought outside.

balanced.text:
Realization that the changeless ‘I am’ through all life-changes is the doorway. The eternal existence-consciousness-bliss is recognized as one’s actual nature.

Somatic: Body experiences itself as ever-changing while the ‘I am’ remains constant—a profound dis-identification.

Emotional: Emotional sense of permanence beneath constant change.

Cognitive: Mind discovers the unchanging witness through changing states.

Psychic: The soul recognizes its own nature as existence-consciousness-bliss, and rests in the fullness it had sought outside.
balanced.vignette: In deep stillness, Ravi has touched something that simply is, is aware, and is quietly glad—being, consciousness, and bliss not as three things but as the single texture of what he most deeply is. The recognition has become a ground he returns to beneath the noise.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Intellectual grasp of Satchidananda without realization—endlessly quoting the formula without inner contact.
facets.somatic: Body unchanged by intellectual concept.
facets.emotional: No emotional contact with the bliss aspect.
facets.cognitive: Mind playing with words, missing reality.
facets.psychic: The soul turns the formula into a mantra of identity, asserting ‘I am bliss’ while suffering, the words outrunning realization.

excess.text:
Intellectual grasp of Satchidananda without realization—endlessly quoting the formula without inner contact.

Somatic: Body unchanged by intellectual concept.

Emotional: No emotional contact with the bliss aspect.

Cognitive: Mind playing with words, missing reality.

Psychic: The soul turns the formula into a mantra of identity, asserting ‘I am bliss’ while suffering, the words outrunning realization.
excess.vignette: Sam chases the ‘ananda’—meditating for the bliss, judging each sit by the high, addicted to the sweetness he’s tasted. He’s grabbed one third of Satchidananda and made it a drug, and the grasping seals off the very depth it came from.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Identification only with changing states—‘I am child/young/old.’ No awareness of the unchanging ‘I am’ beneath all changes.
facets.somatic: Body identified with as self through all changes—each change is identity crisis.
facets.emotional: Emotions fully identified with—I am angry/happy/sad.
facets.cognitive: Mind identified with all its contents, no witness of unchanging existence.
facets.psychic: The soul knows itself only as a needy, transient creature, blind to the being-awareness-joy at its core.

deficient.text:
Identification only with changing states—‘I am child/young/old.’ No awareness of the unchanging ‘I am’ beneath all changes.

Somatic: Body identified with as self through all changes—each change is identity crisis.

Emotional: Emotions fully identified with—I am angry/happy/sad.

Cognitive: Mind identified with all its contents, no witness of unchanging existence.

Psychic: The soul knows itself only as a needy, transient creature, blind to the being-awareness-joy at its core.
deficient.vignette: For Dan there is only matter and mechanism—no being-consciousness-bliss at the core of anything, least of all himself. The recognition that his own nature is existence, awareness, and joy is, to him, mystical poetry; he feels only a self that exists, worries, and is rarely glad.

attributes.title: Three Layers: Matter, Energy, Consciousness
attributes.aspect: Three layers of reality—matter (gross), energy (subtle), consciousness (causal). Science is moving from matter to energy; Vedanta knows the deeper layer of consciousness. In your body, these three layers correspond. Penetrating to consciousness reveals the whole world as conscious.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / pointer · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the graded map of reality as matter, energy, and consciousness.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the graded map of reality as matter, energy, and consciousness.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Penetration through all three layers—matter to energy to consciousness. The world recognized as conscious manifestation, not dead matter.
facets.somatic: Body known as conscious manifestation, not just matter.
facets.emotional: Emotions known as energy-movements within consciousness.
facets.cognitive: Mind known as consciousness in motion, with the unmoved witness behind.
facets.psychic: The soul understands itself as more than matter and energy — as the consciousness in which both appear — and lives from that depth.

balanced.text:
Penetration through all three layers—matter to energy to consciousness. The world recognized as conscious manifestation, not dead matter.

Somatic: Body known as conscious manifestation, not just matter.

Emotional: Emotions known as energy-movements within consciousness.

Cognitive: Mind known as consciousness in motion, with the unmoved witness behind.

Psychic: The soul understands itself as more than matter and energy — as the consciousness in which both appear — and lives from that depth.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to sense the layered depth of things—the matter, the energy animating it, the consciousness knowing it all—and it’s given his world dimension. He meets a person or a problem aware there’s more there than the surface layer shows.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Spiritualist denial of physical reality—claiming all is consciousness while ignoring practical material realities.
facets.somatic: Body neglected as ‘just consciousness manifestation.’
facets.emotional: Practical emotions dismissed as ‘just energy.’
facets.cognitive: Theory without integration.
facets.psychic: The soul over-systematizes the layers into a rigid map, mistaking the diagram for the lived depth.

excess.text:
Spiritualist denial of physical reality—claiming all is consciousness while ignoring practical material realities.

Somatic: Body neglected as ‘just consciousness manifestation.’

Emotional: Practical emotions dismissed as ‘just energy.’

Cognitive: Theory without integration.

Psychic: The soul over-systematizes the layers into a rigid map, mistaking the diagram for the lived depth.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned the three layers into an elaborate system he loves to diagram—matter, energy, consciousness mapped and sub-mapped—mastering the schema while feeling none of the depth it describes. The living dimensionality has become a chart he’s proud of.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Materialism stuck at first layer—matter as ultimate reality, energy as derivative, consciousness denied entirely.
facets.somatic: Body as ultimate identity, only material reality.
facets.emotional: Emotions reduced to chemicals.
facets.cognitive: Mind reduced to brain matter, consciousness denied.
facets.psychic: The soul takes itself to be only matter and energy, with no sense of the consciousness that knows them.

deficient.text:
Materialism stuck at first layer—matter as ultimate reality, energy as derivative, consciousness denied entirely.

Somatic: Body as ultimate identity, only material reality.

Emotional: Emotions reduced to chemicals.

Cognitive: Mind reduced to brain matter, consciousness denied.

Psychic: The soul takes itself to be only matter and energy, with no sense of the consciousness that knows them.
deficient.vignette: For Dan there’s only the surface layer—matter, stuff, mechanism—with no energy animating it and no consciousness knowing it, just things. The layered reality the teaching describes is flattened, in him, to a single dead dimension.

attributes.title: Purusha (Witness Consciousness)
attributes.aspect: Purusha is the experience of being an objective, unattached observer of your own thoughts, emotions, and physical actions. It is the witness consciousness that remains unchanged while all phenomena arise and pass.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework / principle — witness consciousness (puruṣa) · Valence: Neutral · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Transcendent · Causal status: Ground (the seer distinct from the seen) · Key relation: The witness; the Sāṁkhya puruṣa, distinguished from prakṛti by Viveka-Khyāti (Dim 11).
coordinate.causal_status: Ground (the seer distinct from the seen)
coordinate.key_relation: The witness; the Sāṁkhya puruṣa, distinguished from prakṛti by Viveka-Khyāti (Dim 11).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Purusha consciousness is accessed, a person observes thoughts and feelings without identification. There is freedom from emotional reactivity and profound inner peace.
facets.somatic: Physical ease and relaxation from non-identification with body, freedom from physical reactivity, light and free physical presence, body functions naturally.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from identification with feelings, ability to observe emotions without being swept away, emotional peace and non-reactivity, emotional freedom.
facets.cognitive: Mental clarity from observing thoughts, freedom from mental identification, mental peace and stillness, clarity about what is real vs. mental construct.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the silent witness, distinct from the moving show of nature, and rests as the seer rather than the seen.

balanced.text:
When Purusha consciousness is accessed, a person observes thoughts and feelings without identification. There is freedom from emotional reactivity and profound inner peace.

Somatic: Physical ease and relaxation from non-identification with body, freedom from physical reactivity, light and free physical presence, body functions naturally.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from identification with feelings, ability to observe emotions without being swept away, emotional peace and non-reactivity, emotional freedom.

Cognitive: Mental clarity from observing thoughts, freedom from mental identification, mental peace and stillness, clarity about what is real vs. mental construct.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the silent witness, distinct from the moving show of nature, and rests as the seer rather than the seen.
balanced.vignette: Amid the day’s activity, Ravi rests increasingly as the witness—the still consciousness that watches the body act and the mind think without being either. The witnessing isn’t detachment; it’s a spacious presence from which he engages his life more freely.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Over-identification with witness consciousness can lead to dissociation, disconnection from body and emotion, and inability to engage meaningfully with life.
facets.somatic: Physical disconnection and numbness, difficulty functioning in physical world, disembodiment, inability to feel body.
facets.emotional: Emotional detachment and numbness, inability to feel appropriately, disconnection from emotional life, spiritual bypass of emotional work.
facets.cognitive: Mental dissociation, inability to engage with practical concerns, spiritual escape from necessary growth, detachment from meaningful engagement.
facets.psychic: The soul turns ‘I am the witness’ into a detached posture, watching itself watching.

excess.text:
Over-identification with witness consciousness can lead to dissociation, disconnection from body and emotion, and inability to engage meaningfully with life.

Somatic: Physical disconnection and numbness, difficulty functioning in physical world, disembodiment, inability to feel body.

Emotional: Emotional detachment and numbness, inability to feel appropriately, disconnection from emotional life, spiritual bypass of emotional work.

Cognitive: Mental dissociation, inability to engage with practical concerns, spiritual escape from necessary growth, detachment from meaningful engagement.

Psychic: The soul turns ‘I am the witness’ into a detached posture, watching itself watching.
excess.vignette: Sam uses ‘I am the witness’ to excuse a cold detachment—watching his own life and everyone else’s from a lofty remove, uninvolved, untouched, above the fray. The witness consciousness has become his reason to stop caring.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Inability to access witness consciousness results in complete identification with thoughts and emotions. There is no freedom, only reactivity and suffering.
facets.somatic: Physical reactivity and tension, identification with bodily sensations, poor physical awareness, reactive physical behavior.
facets.emotional: Complete emotional identification and reactivity, emotional suffering from identification, inability to step back from feelings, emotional slavery.
facets.cognitive: Complete mental identification, inability to observe thoughts, mental reactivity and suffering, mental slavery to thought patterns.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly absorbed in the play of nature, never noticing the unmoving witness behind it.

deficient.text:
Inability to access witness consciousness results in complete identification with thoughts and emotions. There is no freedom, only reactivity and suffering.

Somatic: Physical reactivity and tension, identification with bodily sensations, poor physical awareness, reactive physical behavior.

Emotional: Complete emotional identification and reactivity, emotional suffering from identification, inability to step back from feelings, emotional slavery.

Cognitive: Complete mental identification, inability to observe thoughts, mental reactivity and suffering, mental slavery to thought patterns.

Psychic: The soul is wholly absorbed in the play of nature, never noticing the unmoving witness behind it.
deficient.vignette: Dan is wholly the doer and the sufferer—every action his, every feeling total, no witnessing presence anywhere behind it. The Purusha that watches, serene, while nature acts is not something he’s failed to find; it’s something he’s never suspected.

attributes.title: Order of Progression (10 Levels of Reality)
attributes.aspect: Beyond the senses are objects, beyond them is mind, beyond mind is intellect, beyond intellect is the Cosmic Mind (Mahat), beyond Mahat is the Unmanifest (Mulaprakriti), beyond which is Purusha (Spirit). Beyond Purusha there is nothing. This is the gradient of reality from gross to subtle to ultimate.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / pointer · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the ten graded levels of reality — a map of ascent.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the ten graded levels of reality — a map of ascent.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Understanding the systematic levels allows progressive practice. One ascends through senses, mind, intellect to cosmic levels.
facets.somatic: Body engaged with senses while aware of higher levels.
facets.emotional: Emotional life understood as movements at one level, contextualized in larger map.
facets.cognitive: Mind has clear map of reality’s levels, knows where it stands and where it’s going.
facets.psychic: The soul grasps that reality unfolds in graded levels, and meets its own development with patience and a sense of where it stands.

balanced.text:
Understanding the systematic levels allows progressive practice. One ascends through senses, mind, intellect to cosmic levels.

Somatic: Body engaged with senses while aware of higher levels.

Emotional: Emotional life understood as movements at one level, contextualized in larger map.

Cognitive: Mind has clear map of reality’s levels, knows where it stands and where it’s going.

Psychic: The soul grasps that reality unfolds in graded levels, and meets its own development with patience and a sense of where it stands.
balanced.vignette: Ravi respects the gradual order of the path—that realization unfolds through levels, each preparing the next, and there’s no skipping the middle rungs. He works his current level patiently, trusting the progression rather than grasping for the top.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Obsessive mapping of levels as intellectual exercise, missing the direct experience at each.
facets.somatic: Body abandoned in pursuit of higher levels.
facets.emotional: Emotional engagement dismissed as ‘lower.’
facets.cognitive: Mental theorizing about levels without genuine ascent.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on the map of levels, measuring and ranking its progress rather than living it.

excess.text:
Obsessive mapping of levels as intellectual exercise, missing the direct experience at each.

Somatic: Body abandoned in pursuit of higher levels.

Emotional: Emotional engagement dismissed as ‘lower.’

Cognitive: Mental theorizing about levels without genuine ascent.

Psychic: The soul fixates on the map of levels, measuring and ranking its progress rather than living it.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned the levels of reality into a rigid ladder he obsesses over—forever locating himself on it, ranking others, anxious about his rung. The map of progression has become a status hierarchy that feeds his ego rather than his growth.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Confined to one level (usually senses or mind) with no awareness of higher dimensions of reality.
facets.somatic: Body taken as the only reality.
facets.emotional: Emotional life seen as final dimension.
facets.cognitive: Mind unable to conceive higher levels of reality.
facets.psychic: The soul has no sense of any graded order, and its development proceeds blind and haphazard.

deficient.text:
Confined to one level (usually senses or mind) with no awareness of higher dimensions of reality.

Somatic: Body taken as the only reality.

Emotional: Emotional life seen as final dimension.

Cognitive: Mind unable to conceive higher levels of reality.

Psychic: The soul has no sense of any graded order, and its development proceeds blind and haphazard.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no sense of any order or progression—his spiritual life, such as it is, is a random grab-bag with no map, no sequence, no sense that some things must come before others. Without any felt structure to the path, he wanders it in circles.

attributes.title: Gravitation vs Grace (Two Cosmic Laws)
attributes.aspect: Two fundamental laws govern existence: gravitation (pull toward objects, downward and outward) and grace (pull toward Source, inward and upward). When body-attraction breaks through awareness, grace becomes operative. The fundamental orientation reverses.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — an epistemic distinction · Valence: Neutral — a discernment to make · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Framework (clarifies how one knows) · Key relation: two cosmic laws — the downward pull of matter vs the upward lift of grace.
coordinate.key_relation: two cosmic laws — the downward pull of matter vs the upward lift of grace.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Available to grace—pulled inward and upward naturally. Not fighting gravitation but transcending its limited domain. Both laws known; one is freely chosen.
facets.somatic: Body responds to subtle grace-pull, sense of being uplifted, lightness.
facets.emotional: Emotional life lifted by grace, joy not dependent on sense-objects.
facets.cognitive: Mind oriented toward inner sky, available to higher pulls.
facets.psychic: The soul senses two forces — the downward pull of conditioning and the upward lift of grace — and consciously aligns with the lift.

balanced.text:
Available to grace—pulled inward and upward naturally. Not fighting gravitation but transcending its limited domain. Both laws known; one is freely chosen.

Somatic: Body responds to subtle grace-pull, sense of being uplifted, lightness.

Emotional: Emotional life lifted by grace, joy not dependent on sense-objects.

Cognitive: Mind oriented toward inner sky, available to higher pulls.

Psychic: The soul senses two forces — the downward pull of conditioning and the upward lift of grace — and consciously aligns with the lift.
balanced.vignette: Ravi does the work—the practice, the effort, the discipline—and stays open to the grace that no effort can command, the unearned opening that sometimes arrives. He rows hard and also raises the sail; neither the striving nor the surrender is neglected.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced spirituality trying to push grace—efforts that themselves block the law of grace which requires receptivity.
facets.somatic: Body strained in spiritual effort.
facets.emotional: Anxious longing for grace, demanding spiritual experience.
facets.cognitive: Mental striving that itself blocks the natural inflow.
facets.psychic: The soul presumes upon grace, expecting to be lifted while doing nothing to cooperate.

excess.text:
Forced spirituality trying to push grace—efforts that themselves block the law of grace which requires receptivity.

Somatic: Body strained in spiritual effort.

Emotional: Anxious longing for grace, demanding spiritual experience.

Cognitive: Mental striving that itself blocks the natural inflow.

Psychic: The soul presumes upon grace, expecting to be lifted while doing nothing to cooperate.
excess.vignette: Sam has decided it’s ‘all grace’—so he waits, doing nothing, expecting the opening to descend on him unearned while his practice gathers dust. The truth that grace can’t be forced has become an excuse to make no effort at all.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total gravity-bondage—drawn outward to bodies, hormones, objects. Maximum slavery to biological/chemical pulls.
facets.somatic: Body driven by chemical attractions, addiction, depletion.
facets.emotional: Emotional life ruled by sensual pulls.
facets.cognitive: Mind constantly seeking outer objects, slavery to gravitation.
facets.psychic: The soul knows only gravity, the downward pull of habit, with no sense of any force that could raise it.

deficient.text:
Total gravity-bondage—drawn outward to bodies, hormones, objects. Maximum slavery to biological/chemical pulls.

Somatic: Body driven by chemical attractions, addiction, depletion.

Emotional: Emotional life ruled by sensual pulls.

Cognitive: Mind constantly seeking outer objects, slavery to gravitation.

Psychic: The soul knows only gravity, the downward pull of habit, with no sense of any force that could raise it.
deficient.vignette: Dan believes it’s all effort—grind hard enough and enlightenment is a wage he’ll collect—with no sense that anything might arrive as gift. Closed to grace, he strains and strains at a door that, the tradition says, partly opens from the other side.

attributes.title: Prarbdha Karma (Past Momentum)
attributes.aspect: Prarabdha is the karma that has begun to fructify—it must run its course even after realization. The realized one doesn’t generate new karma but the past momentum continues. This explains why even Buddha experienced physical illness, aging, death.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Psychic · Ontic mode: Process / given portion — past momentum (prārabdha) · Valence: Neutral (fixed conditions) · Guṇa: Mixed · Scale: Individual, this lifetime · Causal status: Effect-in-force · Key relation: Same as Prārabdha Karma (Dim 12); the momentum a knower still lives out.
coordinate.key_relation: Same as Prārabdha Karma (Dim 12); the momentum a knower still lives out.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Realization complete but bodily life continues until prarabdha exhausts. The witness watches the unfolding momentum without identification.
facets.somatic: Body continues karmic completion while self remains free.
facets.emotional: Emotional consequences of past completing without new generation.
facets.cognitive: Mind witnesses karmic unfolding without ownership.
facets.psychic: The soul accepts the momentum of past action as the given conditions of this life, meeting them without resentment or fatalism.

balanced.text:
Realization complete but bodily life continues until prarabdha exhausts. The witness watches the unfolding momentum without identification.

Somatic: Body continues karmic completion while self remains free.

Emotional: Emotional consequences of past completing without new generation.

Cognitive: Mind witnesses karmic unfolding without ownership.

Psychic: The soul accepts the momentum of past action as the given conditions of this life, meeting them without resentment or fatalism.
balanced.vignette: The hand Ravi was dealt—his temperament, his circumstances, the momentum of his past—he accepts and plays well, neither raging at the givens nor using them as an excuse. He works the field he actually has, and the acceptance is the ground of his freedom.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Using prarabdha as excuse for not changing—‘My past karma made me do this.’ Fatalism disguised as wisdom.
facets.somatic: Body neglected as ‘just karma.’
facets.emotional: Emotional irresponsibility hiding behind karma-talk.
facets.cognitive: Mental excuse-making with karma-doctrine.
facets.psychic: The soul over-uses ‘it is my prārabdha’ as a fatalistic excuse, surrendering all responsibility to destiny.

excess.text:
Using prarabdha as excuse for not changing—‘My past karma made me do this.’ Fatalism disguised as wisdom.

Somatic: Body neglected as ‘just karma.’

Emotional: Emotional irresponsibility hiding behind karma-talk.

Cognitive: Mental excuse-making with karma-doctrine.

Psychic: The soul over-uses ‘it is my prārabdha’ as a fatalistic excuse, surrendering all responsibility to destiny.
excess.vignette: Sam explains everything by ‘prarabdha’—the unhappy marriage, the stalled career, his own passivity all just ‘past momentum working itself out,’ nothing to be done. The teaching about inherited momentum has become a fatalism that excuses every failure to try.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No awareness of karmic momentum—every effect creates new clinging, identification, more karma. Endless wheel.
facets.somatic: Body fully engaged in karma-creation.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactions creating more karma.
facets.cognitive: Mental engagement with everything creating new bondage.
facets.psychic: The soul rages against its given conditions as if they should not be, exhausting itself fighting what cannot change.

deficient.text:
No awareness of karmic momentum—every effect creates new clinging, identification, more karma. Endless wheel.

Somatic: Body fully engaged in karma-creation.

Emotional: Emotional reactions creating more karma.

Cognitive: Mental engagement with everything creating new bondage.

Psychic: The soul rages against its given conditions as if they should not be, exhausting itself fighting what cannot change.
deficient.vignette: Dan denies he inherited anything—no past, no momentum, no given terrain, just a self starting fresh each morning by pure will. Blind to the deep momentum shaping him, he keeps crashing into the same conditioned walls, baffled each time.

attributes.title: Adhikari-Bheda (Four Types of Seekers / Four Horses)
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra acknowledges different categories of seekers (adhikari-bheda). Some realize through a single phrase; others need elaborate instruction; others never realize despite a lifetime. The path is not one-size-fits-all. The Quantum Leap path works for the highest-caliber seeker; others may need preparatory work. Recognizing one’s category is itself important wisdom.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — the four types of seekers (the four horses) · Valence: Neutral (a taxonomy of readiness) · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework (teaching fitted to fitness) · Key relation: The gradation of seekers; kin to the readiness of Sādhana Chatuṣṭaya (Dim 23).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework — the four types of seekers (the four horses)
coordinate.key_relation: The gradation of seekers; kin to the readiness of Sādhana Chatuṣṭaya (Dim 23).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Honest self-assessment of category without false modesty or pride. Appropriate path chosen accordingly.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in path appropriate to one’s stage.
facets.emotional: Emotional acceptance of one’s actual capacity.
facets.cognitive: Mind chooses appropriate practices for one’s level.
facets.psychic: The soul honors that different seekers need different paths, and chooses the practice fitted to its own readiness.

balanced.text:
Honest self-assessment of category without false modesty or pride. Appropriate path chosen accordingly.

Somatic: Body engaged in path appropriate to one’s stage.

Emotional: Emotional acceptance of one’s actual capacity.

Cognitive: Mind chooses appropriate practices for one’s level.

Psychic: The soul honors that different seekers need different paths, and chooses the practice fitted to its own readiness.
balanced.vignette: Ravi knows what kind of seeker he is—his temperament, his readiness, his stage—and practices accordingly, choosing the path that fits rather than forcing one that doesn’t. The honest self-assessment has saved him years of pushing on the wrong door.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Claiming high adhikari status without justification. Skipping necessary preparation.
facets.somatic: Body unprepared but claiming readiness.
facets.emotional: Emotional pride in supposed advancement.
facets.cognitive: Mind takes Quantum Leap path without genuine capacity.
facets.psychic: The soul ranks itself proudly among the ‘higher’ types, using the typology for spiritual vanity.

excess.text:
Claiming high adhikari status without justification. Skipping necessary preparation.

Somatic: Body unprepared but claiming readiness.

Emotional: Emotional pride in supposed advancement.

Cognitive: Mind takes Quantum Leap path without genuine capacity.

Psychic: The soul ranks itself proudly among the ‘higher’ types, using the typology for spiritual vanity.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned the four types of seekers into a hierarchy he ranks everyone on—himself near the top, of course—competing over spiritual readiness as though it were a leaderboard. The teaching about fitness for the path has become fuel for his pride.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Falsely modest about one’s actual capacity. Or treating self as the dullest, never trying direct path.
facets.somatic: Body in elementary practices for years without progressing.
facets.emotional: Emotional self-deprecation preventing higher engagement.
facets.cognitive: Mind underestimates own readiness.
facets.psychic: The soul forces itself onto a path unsuited to it, ignoring its actual readiness and stalling.

deficient.text:
Falsely modest about one’s actual capacity. Or treating self as the dullest, never trying direct path.

Somatic: Body in elementary practices for years without progressing.

Emotional: Emotional self-deprecation preventing higher engagement.

Cognitive: Mind underestimates own readiness.

Psychic: The soul forces itself onto a path unsuited to it, ignoring its actual readiness and stalling.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no idea what kind of seeker he is—forcing practices wildly unsuited to his stage, or drifting with no practice at all, blind to his own readiness. Without any self-knowledge of where he stands, he keeps mismatching the medicine to the patient.

attributes.title: Sanga (Attachment/Association)
attributes.aspect: Sanga is the foundational psychological trigger where repeated mental focus on an object breeds attachment. This progressive process leads from interest to attachment to desire to anger. It is the mechanism by which external objects become internally anchored through the mind’s habitual association.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Faculty tending to affliction — attachment / association (saṅga) · Valence: Affliction (to loosen) · Guṇa: Rajas · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Mechanism (the clinging that binds) · Key relation: Kin to Rāga (Dim 19); loosened by Vairāgya (Dim 23).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Faculty tending to affliction — attachment / association (saṅga)
coordinate.key_relation: Kin to Rāga (Dim 19); loosened by Vairāgya (Dim 23).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Sanga is appropriately balanced, a person can engage with objects and activities without becoming psychologically bound to them. There is healthy interaction without compulsive attachment.
facets.somatic: Free and natural physical engagement with activities, healthy body language in interactions, flexibility in physical response, good energy management without depletion.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom in engagement, ability to enjoy without grasping, emotional flexibility and spontaneity, healthy emotional connections without codependence.
facets.cognitive: Clear mental awareness of the process of attachment forming, ability to observe associations without being consumed by them, mental freedom and fluidity, mindful awareness of mental patterns.
facets.psychic: The soul chooses its company and attachments wisely, knowing that whom and what one keeps near shapes the inner life.

balanced.text:
When Sanga is appropriately balanced, a person can engage with objects and activities without becoming psychologically bound to them. There is healthy interaction without compulsive attachment.

Somatic: Free and natural physical engagement with activities, healthy body language in interactions, flexibility in physical response, good energy management without depletion.

Emotional: Emotional freedom in engagement, ability to enjoy without grasping, emotional flexibility and spontaneity, healthy emotional connections without codependence.

Cognitive: Clear mental awareness of the process of attachment forming, ability to observe associations without being consumed by them, mental freedom and fluidity, mindful awareness of mental patterns.

Psychic: The soul chooses its company and attachments wisely, knowing that whom and what one keeps near shapes the inner life.
balanced.vignette: Ravi keeps good company—people who lift him toward what’s true—and holds even them lightly, nourished by association without being enslaved by it. The right company shapes him; the non-attachment keeps him free within it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive Sanga creates deep psychological bonds to objects, relationships, outcomes, and desires. This leads to compulsive behavior, obsession, and the inevita descent into craving and anger.
facets.somatic: Physical tension from holding onto attachments, compulsive physical behaviors, seeking physical contact or objects repeatedly, nervous habits, physical depletion from excessive engagement.
facets.emotional: Obsessive emotional attachment, emotional vulnerability to loss, emotional reactivity when attachments are threatened, emotional dependence and neediness, anxiety about maintaining connections.
facets.cognitive: Obsessive thinking about attached objects or people, inability to think of anything else, mental preoccupation and rumination, loss of mental freedom and autonomy, mental suffering from anticipated loss.
facets.psychic: The soul clings to associations and identities, bound by the very attachments it should hold lightly.

excess.text:
Excessive Sanga creates deep psychological bonds to objects, relationships, outcomes, and desires. This leads to compulsive behavior, obsession, and the inevita descent into craving and anger.

Somatic: Physical tension from holding onto attachments, compulsive physical behaviors, seeking physical contact or objects repeatedly, nervous habits, physical depletion from excessive engagement.

Emotional: Obsessive emotional attachment, emotional vulnerability to loss, emotional reactivity when attachments are threatened, emotional dependence and neediness, anxiety about maintaining connections.

Cognitive: Obsessive thinking about attached objects or people, inability to think of anything else, mental preoccupation and rumination, loss of mental freedom and autonomy, mental suffering from anticipated loss.

Psychic: The soul clings to associations and identities, bound by the very attachments it should hold lightly.
excess.vignette: Sam clings to his spiritual community like a limpet—unable to think a thought his group hasn’t approved, terrified of leaving the fold, his identity wholly borrowed from the association. Good company has become a dependency that’s replaced his own discernment.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Deficiency of Sanga results in inability to engage meaningfully or form healthy connections. There is emotional detachment and indifference that prevents normal human bonding.
facets.somatic: Withdrawn physical presence, lack of physical engagement or interaction, poor body language in relationships, physical numbness or dissociation, inability to engage in physical activities.
facets.emotional: Emotional coldness and detachment, inability to feel connected to others, emotional indifference, difficulty experiencing closeness or intimacy, emotional numbness.
facets.cognitive: Mental disinterest and apathy, inability to focus on meaningful pursuits, lack of motivation to engage, mental distance and dissociation, inability to develop deep understanding through study.
facets.psychic: The soul drifts without discernment into any company, shaped helplessly by whatever surrounds it.

deficient.text:
Deficiency of Sanga results in inability to engage meaningfully or form healthy connections. There is emotional detachment and indifference that prevents normal human bonding.

Somatic: Withdrawn physical presence, lack of physical engagement or interaction, poor body language in relationships, physical numbness or dissociation, inability to engage in physical activities.

Emotional: Emotional coldness and detachment, inability to feel connected to others, emotional indifference, difficulty experiencing closeness or intimacy, emotional numbness.

Cognitive: Mental disinterest and apathy, inability to focus on meaningful pursuits, lack of motivation to engage, mental distance and dissociation, inability to develop deep understanding through study.

Psychic: The soul drifts without discernment into any company, shaped helplessly by whatever surrounds it.
deficient.vignette: Dan keeps company with no one who lifts him—isolated, or surrounded by influences that drag him down, with no association to shape him toward the good. The nourishing power of right company is something he’s never sought or felt.

attributes.title: Buddhi (Intellect/Discernment)
attributes.aspect: Buddhi is the faculty of logical determination and discernment. The Gita categorizes it into three states (sattvic, rajasic, tamasic), determining how clearly a human can distinguish right from wrong, eternal from temporary, and truth from illusion.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Faculty — the intellect / discerning function · Valence: Neutral faculty · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Instrument (the faculty of discernment) · Key relation: The charioteer of the psyche (Dim 3); the seat of Viveka.
coordinate.key_relation: The charioteer of the psyche (Dim 3); the seat of Viveka.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Buddhi is sattvic and balanced, a person possesses clear discriminative intelligence that guides right action. This faculty enables distinction between dharma and adharma, benefit and harm.
facets.somatic: Clear eyes and alert posture, confident physical bearing, steady and purposeful movement, healthy nervous system function, integrated body-mind connection.
facets.emotional: Emotional wisdom and clarity, emotional discernment about true vs. false feelings, emotional maturity, emotional confidence in decisions, peaceful emotional acceptance of wisdom.
facets.cognitive: Crystal clear thinking, ability to distinguish right from wrong effortlessly, penetrating insight, excellent problem-solving ability, memory and retention of learning, ability to see root causes.
facets.psychic: The soul’s discerning intellect functions clearly, the charioteer steering the senses toward the good.

balanced.text:
When Buddhi is sattvic and balanced, a person possesses clear discriminative intelligence that guides right action. This faculty enables distinction between dharma and adharma, benefit and harm.

Somatic: Clear eyes and alert posture, confident physical bearing, steady and purposeful movement, healthy nervous system function, integrated body-mind connection.

Emotional: Emotional wisdom and clarity, emotional discernment about true vs. false feelings, emotional maturity, emotional confidence in decisions, peaceful emotional acceptance of wisdom.

Cognitive: Crystal clear thinking, ability to distinguish right from wrong effortlessly, penetrating insight, excellent problem-solving ability, memory and retention of learning, ability to see root causes.

Psychic: The soul’s discerning intellect functions clearly, the charioteer steering the senses toward the good.
balanced.vignette: When the clever pitch comes, Ravi’s discernment quietly works—separating the sound from the plausible, the essential from the noise—and lands him on a judgment he can stand behind. The intellect, in him, is a clear blade that serves rather than dazzles.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive Buddhi manifests as intellectual arrogance, over-analysis, and a rigid application of logic that lacks compassion or contextual understanding. The intellect becomes a weapon.
facets.somatic: Physical tension in head and neck, restless physical energy, harsh physical gestures, poor sleep from mental over-stimulation, physical rigidity.
facets.emotional: Emotional coldness and detachment, emotional arrogance and superiority, dismissal of others’ feelings, lack of emotional wisdom, emotional judgment and criticism.
facets.cognitive: Analysis paralysis and over-intellectualization, inability to make decisions, overthinking simple matters, excessive mental activity, inability to trust intuition, intellectual rigidity.
facets.psychic: The soul over-trusts the intellect, mistaking sharp analysis for wisdom and arguing itself away from the heart.

excess.text:
Excessive Buddhi manifests as intellectual arrogance, over-analysis, and a rigid application of logic that lacks compassion or contextual understanding. The intellect becomes a weapon.

Somatic: Physical tension in head and neck, restless physical energy, harsh physical gestures, poor sleep from mental over-stimulation, physical rigidity.

Emotional: Emotional coldness and detachment, emotional arrogance and superiority, dismissal of others’ feelings, lack of emotional wisdom, emotional judgment and criticism.

Cognitive: Analysis paralysis and over-intellectualization, inability to make decisions, overthinking simple matters, excessive mental activity, inability to trust intuition, intellectual rigidity.

Psychic: The soul over-trusts the intellect, mistaking sharp analysis for wisdom and arguing itself away from the heart.
excess.vignette: Sam’s intellect never stops cutting—dissecting every question into further questions, so subtle and analytical that he can’t cross a street without weighing options, dazzling and paralyzed. The discerning faculty has hypertrophied into an analysis that never lets him act.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Deficient Buddhi results in poor discernment, inability to distinguish right from wrong, foolish decision-making, and mental delusion. The person lacks clarity to navigate life wisely.
facets.somatic: Confused and uncoordinated physical presence, poor posture and uncertain movement, poor physical judgment, susceptibility to physical harm, weak nervous system function.
facets.emotional: Emotional gullibility and confusion, inability to discern true vs. false emotions, emotional mistakes in relationships, emotional instability from poor judgment.
facets.cognitive: Mental confusion and fog, inability to think clearly, poor decision-making, vulnerability to delusion and false beliefs, inability to learn from experience, scattered thinking.
facets.psychic: The soul’s discernment is dull or overridden, the chariot driven by the runaway horses of the senses.

deficient.text:
Deficient Buddhi results in poor discernment, inability to distinguish right from wrong, foolish decision-making, and mental delusion. The person lacks clarity to navigate life wisely.

Somatic: Confused and uncoordinated physical presence, poor posture and uncertain movement, poor physical judgment, susceptibility to physical harm, weak nervous system function.

Emotional: Emotional gullibility and confusion, inability to discern true vs. false emotions, emotional mistakes in relationships, emotional instability from poor judgment.

Cognitive: Mental confusion and fog, inability to think clearly, poor decision-making, vulnerability to delusion and false beliefs, inability to learn from experience, scattered thinking.

Psychic: The soul’s discernment is dull or overridden, the chariot driven by the runaway horses of the senses.
deficient.vignette: Dan takes the first framing offered and never tests it—the confident claim, the loud argument, the plausible story swallowed whole. The discerning intellect that would sort the true from the merely certain has simply never engaged, and he’s fooled again and again.

attributes.title: Asuri Sampat (Demoniac Qualities)
attributes.aspect: Asuri Sampat is a detailed psychological profile of toxic human traits including pride, arrogance, harshness, anger, insatiable lust, and cruelty. These qualities bind the mind to suffering and create karmic consequences.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Affliction-set — the demoniac qualities · Valence: Affliction (to transmute) · Guṇa: Rajas–Tamas · Scale: Individual / interpersonal · Causal status: Distortion · Key relation: Same as the Āsurī Sampat (Dim 24); the binding endowment.
coordinate.key_relation: Same as the Āsurī Sampat (Dim 24); the binding endowment.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When asuri qualities are minimized, a person exhibits opposing divine qualities: humility, gentleness, compassion, restraint, and respect for others.
facets.somatic: Gentle and non-aggressive body, open and relaxed posture, kind physical presence, healthy respect for others’ space, non-violent physical expression.
facets.emotional: Humility and absence of arrogance, compassion and caring for others, emotional gentleness, emotional restraint without suppression, emotional maturity.
facets.cognitive: Humble and respectful thinking, absence of pride, lack of harsh judgment, mental compassion, recognition of limitations and others’ worth.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the demoniac tendencies in itself — arrogance, greed, deceit — and works to transform them.

balanced.text:
When asuri qualities are minimized, a person exhibits opposing divine qualities: humility, gentleness, compassion, restraint, and respect for others.

Somatic: Gentle and non-aggressive body, open and relaxed posture, kind physical presence, healthy respect for others’ space, non-violent physical expression.

Emotional: Humility and absence of arrogance, compassion and caring for others, emotional gentleness, emotional restraint without suppression, emotional maturity.

Cognitive: Humble and respectful thinking, absence of pride, lack of harsh judgment, mental compassion, recognition of limitations and others’ worth.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the demoniac tendencies in itself — arrogance, greed, deceit — and works to transform them.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has learned to see his own shadow clearly—the pride, the anger, the grasping—naming his demoniac tendencies without either indulging or denying them. The honest recognition is what lets him work with them; what’s seen can, slowly, be transformed.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: When asuri qualities are dominant, there is a constellation of toxic traits: pride, arrogance, harshness, cruelty, lust, anger, and insatiable desire. These create suffering for self and others.
facets.somatic: Harsh and aggressive physical presence, violent or cruel physical behaviors, poor self-control in physical expression, physical harm to others, neglect of physical ethics.
facets.emotional: Arrogance and superiority, lack of compassion, emotional cruelty, inability to empathize, emotional lust and possessiveness, emotional rage and anger, emotional insatiability.
facets.cognitive: Proud and arrogant thinking, disrespect for others, harsh and cruel mental patterns, obsessive and controlling thoughts, inability to consider others’ perspectives, insatiable mental desires.
facets.psychic: The soul gives these qualities free rein, mistaking ruthlessness and pride for strength.

excess.text:
When asuri qualities are dominant, there is a constellation of toxic traits: pride, arrogance, harshness, cruelty, lust, anger, and insatiable desire. These create suffering for self and others.

Somatic: Harsh and aggressive physical presence, violent or cruel physical behaviors, poor self-control in physical expression, physical harm to others, neglect of physical ethics.

Emotional: Arrogance and superiority, lack of compassion, emotional cruelty, inability to empathize, emotional lust and possessiveness, emotional rage and anger, emotional insatiability.

Cognitive: Proud and arrogant thinking, disrespect for others, harsh and cruel mental patterns, obsessive and controlling thoughts, inability to consider others’ perspectives, insatiable mental desires.

Psychic: The soul gives these qualities free rein, mistaking ruthlessness and pride for strength.
excess.vignette: Sam is obsessed with rooting out evil—forever hunting the demoniac in himself and everyone else, a grim inquisitor of shadows, so fixated on the darkness that the fixation has become its own affliction. The healthy self-knowledge has curdled into a witch-hunt.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Deficiency of asuri qualities (which is desirable) means the absence of these harmful traits. This is identical to having divine qualities.
facets.somatic: Gentle and peaceful physical presence, non-violent expression, respectful physical boundaries.
facets.emotional: Compassionate and humble emotions, absence of arrogance and cruelty, gentle emotional expression.
facets.cognitive: Respectful and humble thinking, compassionate mental patterns, ethical decision-making.
facets.psychic: The soul denies it has any such tendencies at all, and so can never guard against them.

deficient.text:
Deficiency of asuri qualities (which is desirable) means the absence of these harmful traits. This is identical to having divine qualities.

Somatic: Gentle and peaceful physical presence, non-violent expression, respectful physical boundaries.

Emotional: Compassionate and humble emotions, absence of arrogance and cruelty, gentle emotional expression.

Cognitive: Respectful and humble thinking, compassionate mental patterns, ethical decision-making.

Psychic: The soul denies it has any such tendencies at all, and so can never guard against them.
deficient.vignette: Dan is blind to his own darkness—the arrogance, the cruelty, the greed invisible to him, always someone else’s fault. The demoniac tendencies the teaching names are alive in him and unseen, and the unseen runs freely; you can’t work with a shadow you won’t look at.

attributes.title: Daivi Sampat (Divine Qualities)
attributes.aspect: Daivi Sampat is a checklist of twenty-six psychological virtues including fearlessness, purification of mind, charity, absence of anger and malice, truthfulness, and self-control. These qualities elevate human consciousness and create positive karma.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Virtue-set — the divine qualities · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual / interpersonal · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: Same as the Daivī Sampat (Dim 24); the liberating endowment.
coordinate.key_relation: Same as the Daivī Sampat (Dim 24); the liberating endowment.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When divine qualities are cultivated and present, a person exhibits fearlessness, purity, generosity, truthfulness, self-control, compassion, and wisdom. Life becomes an expression of these elevating qualities.
facets.somatic: Radiant and healthy appearance, confident and upright posture, non-aggressive physical presence, disciplined physical habits, generosity in physical actions.
facets.emotional: Fearlessness and courage, purity of heart, generosity and compassion, truthfulness in feelings, emotional self-control, absence of anger and malice, emotional elevation.
facets.cognitive: Fearless thinking and ideas, pure and clear mental patterns, generous and expansive thinking, truthful mental expression, disciplined and focused mind, wisdom and spiritual insight.
facets.psychic: The soul cultivates the divine qualities — fearlessness, purity, compassion — as the natural fruit of right knowing.

balanced.text:
When divine qualities are cultivated and present, a person exhibits fearlessness, purity, generosity, truthfulness, self-control, compassion, and wisdom. Life becomes an expression of these elevating qualities.

Somatic: Radiant and healthy appearance, confident and upright posture, non-aggressive physical presence, disciplined physical habits, generosity in physical actions.

Emotional: Fearlessness and courage, purity of heart, generosity and compassion, truthfulness in feelings, emotional self-control, absence of anger and malice, emotional elevation.

Cognitive: Fearless thinking and ideas, pure and clear mental patterns, generous and expansive thinking, truthful mental expression, disciplined and focused mind, wisdom and spiritual insight.

Psychic: The soul cultivates the divine qualities — fearlessness, purity, compassion — as the natural fruit of right knowing.
balanced.vignette: Ravi quietly cultivates the divine qualities—the fearlessness, the compassion, the truthfulness—working at them daily without fanfare, letting them slowly become his character. The cultivation is real and humble; he’s building the good in himself, brick by brick.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: While divine qualities are generally beneficial, excessive manifestation can lead to spiritual pride, self-righteousness, judging others for not having these qualities, and disconnection.
facets.somatic: Over-discipline and rigidity, perfectionism in physical habits, harsh physical discipline, judgment of others’ physical habits.
facets.emotional: Spiritual pride and arrogance, judgment of others’ lack of qualities, emotional coldness, difficulty with ordinary human emotions, spiritual superiority.
facets.cognitive: Mental rigidity about virtue, judgment and criticism of others, spiritual intellectualism, inability to accept human flaws in self or others.
facets.psychic: The soul performs virtue for show, the divine qualities becoming a costume for the ego.

excess.text:
While divine qualities are generally beneficial, excessive manifestation can lead to spiritual pride, self-righteousness, judging others for not having these qualities, and disconnection.

Somatic: Over-discipline and rigidity, perfectionism in physical habits, harsh physical discipline, judgment of others’ physical habits.

Emotional: Spiritual pride and arrogance, judgment of others’ lack of qualities, emotional coldness, difficulty with ordinary human emotions, spiritual superiority.

Cognitive: Mental rigidity about virtue, judgment and criticism of others, spiritual intellectualism, inability to accept human flaws in self or others.

Psychic: The soul performs virtue for show, the divine qualities becoming a costume for the ego.
excess.vignette: Sam is proud of his virtues—his compassion, his honesty, his discipline—wearing them as badges, subtly superior to the less evolved. The divine qualities have become, in him, an ego project; he’s virtuous and insufferable, and can’t see the second part.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Absence of divine qualities results in the presence of demoniac qualities: fear, impurity, selfishness, deception, lack of self-control, anger, and cruelty.
facets.somatic: Poor health and appearance, cowardly or aggressive posture, selfish and harming physical actions, lack of discipline.
facets.emotional: Fear and cowardice, impurity and corruption, selfishness and greed, anger and malice, lack of compassion, emotional degradation.
facets.cognitive: Fearful and negative thinking, impure and corrupted mind, selfish and cruel thoughts, deceptive thinking, undisciplined mind, spiritual blindness.
facets.psychic: The soul neglects these qualities entirely, and its knowledge stays barren of character.

deficient.text:
Absence of divine qualities results in the presence of demoniac qualities: fear, impurity, selfishness, deception, lack of self-control, anger, and cruelty.

Somatic: Poor health and appearance, cowardly or aggressive posture, selfish and harming physical actions, lack of discipline.

Emotional: Fear and cowardice, impurity and corruption, selfishness and greed, anger and malice, lack of compassion, emotional degradation.

Cognitive: Fearful and negative thinking, impure and corrupted mind, selfish and cruel thoughts, deceptive thinking, undisciplined mind, spiritual blindness.

Psychic: The soul neglects these qualities entirely, and its knowledge stays barren of character.
deficient.vignette: Dan cultivates nothing—no fearlessness, no compassion, no discipline deliberately grown—leaving his character to whatever the day makes of it. The divine qualities the teaching describes aren’t rejected by him; it’s simply never occurred to him that virtue could be practiced.

attributes.title: Yajna (Sacrifice/Reciprocity)
attributes.aspect: Yajna is the law of cosmic interdependency, stating that human survival and well-being rely on continuous, selfless contributions back to nature and society. It is the principle of reciprocity and sacred exchange.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / principle — sacrifice, reciprocity (yajña) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Interpersonal / cosmic · Causal status: Mechanism (the giving that sustains the world) · Key relation: Sacrifice as the law of reciprocity; kin to Karma Yoga (Dim 10) and Purusha Vidya (Dim 25).
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (the giving that sustains the world)
coordinate.key_relation: Sacrifice as the law of reciprocity; kin to Karma Yoga (Dim 10) and Purusha Vidya (Dim 25).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Yajna is practiced appropriately, a person contributes selflessly to the world while allowing themselves to receive. There is balanced giving and taking, gratitude and generosity.
facets.somatic: Physical health from balanced engagement with world, good energy circulation, physical vitality from meaningful contribution, healthy physical sustainability.
facets.emotional: Emotional satisfaction from giving, emotional peace from reciprocal exchange, emotional connection to community and world, emotional fulfillment from contribution.
facets.cognitive: Mental clarity about interdependency, wisdom about responsibility to world, mental peace from right action, mental connection to larger systems.
facets.psychic: The soul lives in the spirit of sacrifice, giving and receiving in the great reciprocity that sustains the world.

balanced.text:
When Yajna is practiced appropriately, a person contributes selflessly to the world while allowing themselves to receive. There is balanced giving and taking, gratitude and generosity.

Somatic: Physical health from balanced engagement with world, good energy circulation, physical vitality from meaningful contribution, healthy physical sustainability.

Emotional: Emotional satisfaction from giving, emotional peace from reciprocal exchange, emotional connection to community and world, emotional fulfillment from contribution.

Cognitive: Mental clarity about interdependency, wisdom about responsibility to world, mental peace from right action, mental connection to larger systems.

Psychic: The soul lives in the spirit of sacrifice, giving and receiving in the great reciprocity that sustains the world.
balanced.vignette: Ravi lives reciprocally—giving as freely as he receives, treating his work and relationships as an exchange of mutual nourishment rather than a taking. His life has the quality of a sacrifice in the old sense: an offering, freely made, that comes back as abundance.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive Yajna or self-sacrifice can lead to burnout, resentment, enabling others’ dependency, and loss of personal well-being.
facets.somatic: Physical exhaustion and burnout from giving, poor self-care, depletion and fatigue, vulnerability to illness.
facets.emotional: Emotional exhaustion and resentment, emotional self-sacrifice without healthy boundaries, emotional emptiness beneath giving, emotional depletion.
facets.cognitive: Mental exhaustion, mental resentment about obligations, loss of personal identity in contribution, mental depletion.
facets.psychic: The soul turns sacrifice into transactional bargaining, offering only in order to get.

excess.text:
Excessive Yajna or self-sacrifice can lead to burnout, resentment, enabling others’ dependency, and loss of personal well-being.

Somatic: Physical exhaustion and burnout from giving, poor self-care, depletion and fatigue, vulnerability to illness.

Emotional: Emotional exhaustion and resentment, emotional self-sacrifice without healthy boundaries, emotional emptiness beneath giving, emotional depletion.

Cognitive: Mental exhaustion, mental resentment about obligations, loss of personal identity in contribution, mental depletion.

Psychic: The soul turns sacrifice into transactional bargaining, offering only in order to get.
excess.vignette: Sam performs sacrifice—the ostentatious giving, the publicized generosity, the offerings made for the credit they earn. The reciprocity has become theater; he gives to be seen giving, and the sacred exchange has curdled into a transaction with his own image.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Lack of Yajna results in taking without giving, living off others’ contributions without reciprocal engagement, and karmic debt accumulation.
facets.somatic: Poor long-term physical sustainability, health problems from karmic consequences, physical dependence on others, eventual physical decline.
facets.emotional: Emotional emptiness from lack of meaningful contribution, emotional dependence, emotional guilt from not contributing, emotional isolation.
facets.cognitive: Mental awareness of debt and obligation, mental guilt and shame, mental isolation from community, eventual mental suffering.
facets.psychic: The soul takes without giving, breaking the cycle of reciprocity and starving its own larger life.

deficient.text:
Lack of Yajna results in taking without giving, living off others’ contributions without reciprocal engagement, and karmic debt accumulation.

Somatic: Poor long-term physical sustainability, health problems from karmic consequences, physical dependence on others, eventual physical decline.

Emotional: Emotional emptiness from lack of meaningful contribution, emotional dependence, emotional guilt from not contributing, emotional isolation.

Cognitive: Mental awareness of debt and obligation, mental guilt and shame, mental isolation from community, eventual mental suffering.

Psychic: The soul takes without giving, breaking the cycle of reciprocity and starving its own larger life.
deficient.vignette: Dan only takes—from his relationships, his work, his world—with no felt sense of any reciprocal giving owed in return. The web of mutual offering that the teaching of yajna describes is invisible to him; he consumes, and senses no debt.

attributes.title: Shraddha (Faith/Core Conviction)
attributes.aspect: Shraddha is the fundamental psychological orientation of a human being. The Gita states that a person is entirely shaped by the nature of their deep-seated beliefs and convictions. It determines thoughts, emotions, and actions.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Virtue — faith, core conviction (śraddhā) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition (the conviction that sustains knowing) · Key relation: Same as Śraddhā the treasure (Dim 23); antidote to Saṁśaya (Dim 21).
coordinate.key_relation: Same as Śraddhā the treasure (Dim 23); antidote to Saṁśaya (Dim 21).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: With balanced Shraddha, a person has sincere, tested faith that is neither blind nor overly skeptical. Beliefs are examined, questioned, and deeply held. This faith shapes positive action.
facets.somatic: Physical health from belief in possibility, good energy and vitality, physical resilience from faith, strength to overcome challenges.
facets.emotional: Emotional confidence and peace from deep conviction, emotional resilience through difficulties, emotional strength from faith, emotional security.
facets.cognitive: Mental clarity from solid beliefs, focus and determination from conviction, mental resilience, ability to persist through challenges.
facets.psychic: The soul holds a functional faith that lets it act before proof, the conviction that carries it across what it cannot yet see.

balanced.text:
With balanced Shraddha, a person has sincere, tested faith that is neither blind nor overly skeptical. Beliefs are examined, questioned, and deeply held. This faith shapes positive action.

Somatic: Physical health from belief in possibility, good energy and vitality, physical resilience from faith, strength to overcome challenges.

Emotional: Emotional confidence and peace from deep conviction, emotional resilience through difficulties, emotional strength from faith, emotional security.

Cognitive: Mental clarity from solid beliefs, focus and determination from conviction, mental resilience, ability to persist through challenges.

Psychic: The soul holds a functional faith that lets it act before proof, the conviction that carries it across what it cannot yet see.
balanced.vignette: Ravi holds a grounded faith—a core conviction that lets him commit and act before every question is answered, without surrendering his judgment. The faith isn’t credulity; it’s the reasoned trust that makes beginning anything real possible.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive or blind Shraddha leads to dogmatism, inability to question or adapt, vulnerability to manipulation, and resistance to new understanding.
facets.somatic: Physical rigidity from dogmatic beliefs, poor adaptation to necessary changes, physical vulnerability from false beliefs.
facets.emotional: Emotional rigidity and defensiveness about beliefs, emotional vulnerability to manipulation, inability to question beliefs emotionally.
facets.cognitive: Mental dogmatism and rigidity, refusal to question or examine, vulnerability to false teachings, mental inability to evolve.
facets.psychic: The soul’s faith hardens into credulity, swallowing whatever it is told without discernment.

excess.text:
Excessive or blind Shraddha leads to dogmatism, inability to question or adapt, vulnerability to manipulation, and resistance to new understanding.

Somatic: Physical rigidity from dogmatic beliefs, poor adaptation to necessary changes, physical vulnerability from false beliefs.

Emotional: Emotional rigidity and defensiveness about beliefs, emotional vulnerability to manipulation, inability to question beliefs emotionally.

Cognitive: Mental dogmatism and rigidity, refusal to question or examine, vulnerability to false teachings, mental inability to evolve.

Psychic: The soul’s faith hardens into credulity, swallowing whatever it is told without discernment.
excess.vignette: Sam believes whatever he’s told with conviction—the teacher, the doctrine, the confident claim swallowed whole, discernment abandoned at the door. His ‘faith’ is blind credulity, and it’s made him a follower of whoever speaks most certainly, however wrong.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Lack of Shraddha results in chronic doubt, inability to commit to anything, constant questioning without foundation, and weakness in facing challenges.
facets.somatic: Poor physical health from lack of conviction, weakness in face of challenges, inability to sustain physical practices, lack of resilience.
facets.emotional: Emotional instability and doubt, emotional weakness, inability to feel secure or grounded, emotional vulnerability.
facets.cognitive: Mental doubt and uncertainty, inability to commit to direction, constant questioning without foundation, mental instability, mental weakness.
facets.psychic: The soul’s corrosive doubt undermines every step, unable to commit to a path long enough for it to work.

deficient.text:
Lack of Shraddha results in chronic doubt, inability to commit to anything, constant questioning without foundation, and weakness in facing challenges.

Somatic: Poor physical health from lack of conviction, weakness in face of challenges, inability to sustain physical practices, lack of resilience.

Emotional: Emotional instability and doubt, emotional weakness, inability to feel secure or grounded, emotional vulnerability.

Cognitive: Mental doubt and uncertainty, inability to commit to direction, constant questioning without foundation, mental instability, mental weakness.

Psychic: The soul’s corrosive doubt undermines every step, unable to commit to a path long enough for it to work.
deficient.vignette: Dan can’t commit to anything he can’t first prove—every path met with a corrosive doubt, every beginning deferred until a certainty that never comes. The faith that lets a person act before proof has never developed, and so he stands forever at the trailhead.

attributes.title: Maya (Illusion/Misperception)
attributes.aspect: Maya is the cognitive overlay that distorts human perception, making the temporary, changing world appear permanent and ultimate. It is the fundamental illusion that clouds reality perception.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Framework / principle — illusion, misperception (māyā) · Valence: Affliction-as-veil (to see through) · Guṇa: Tamas–Rajas · Scale: Cosmic (Ādhidaivika), lived individually · Causal status: Mechanism of appearance (the power by which the one appears as many) · Key relation: The veiling-projecting power; kin to the guṇas-as-veils (Dim 2) and Avidyā (Dim 19).
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism of appearance (the power by which the one appears as many)
coordinate.key_relation: The veiling-projecting power; kin to the guṇas-as-veils (Dim 2) and Avidyā (Dim 19).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Maya’s nature is understood, a person sees through illusions without being deceived. There is clear perception of what is temporary vs. eternal, and wisdom guides action.
facets.somatic: Clear perception of physical reality, freedom from physical delusions, wise physical action based on accurate perception, good judgment about material world.
facets.emotional: Emotional clarity and freedom from delusions, emotional wisdom about temporary nature of feelings, emotional non-attachment from understanding impermanence.
facets.cognitive: Mental clarity and freedom from illusions, ability to perceive reality accurately, wisdom about nature of existence, mental discrimination between real and illusory.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes māyā as the creative-veiling power, neither denying the world nor being fully deceived by it.

balanced.text:
When Maya’s nature is understood, a person sees through illusions without being deceived. There is clear perception of what is temporary vs. eternal, and wisdom guides action.

Somatic: Clear perception of physical reality, freedom from physical delusions, wise physical action based on accurate perception, good judgment about material world.

Emotional: Emotional clarity and freedom from delusions, emotional wisdom about temporary nature of feelings, emotional non-attachment from understanding impermanence.

Cognitive: Mental clarity and freedom from illusions, ability to perceive reality accurately, wisdom about nature of existence, mental discrimination between real and illusory.

Psychic: The soul recognizes māyā as the creative-veiling power, neither denying the world nor being fully deceived by it.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has learned to see through appearances without dismissing them—recognizing the show as a show, the surface as surface, while still engaging it skillfully. He’s not fooled by the glamour, and not so ‘above’ it that he ignores the real work it demands.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Over-focus on illusion and unreality can lead to nihilism, cynicism, and loss of meaning. Reality becomes meaningless.
facets.somatic: Physical apathy from sense of futility, poor physical engagement, lack of motivation to care for body or world.
facets.emotional: Emotional cynicism and meaninglessness, emotional emptiness, inability to feel significance of anything.
facets.cognitive: Mental nihilism, sense that nothing is real or meaningful, inability to commit to anything, mental despair.
facets.psychic: The soul over-uses ‘it is all māyā’ to dismiss real life and avoid responsibility.

excess.text:
