Over-focus on illusion and unreality can lead to nihilism, cynicism, and loss of meaning. Reality becomes meaningless.

Somatic: Physical apathy from sense of futility, poor physical engagement, lack of motivation to care for body or world.

Emotional: Emotional cynicism and meaninglessness, emotional emptiness, inability to feel significance of anything.

Cognitive: Mental nihilism, sense that nothing is real or meaningful, inability to commit to anything, mental despair.

Psychic: The soul over-uses ‘it is all māyā’ to dismiss real life and avoid responsibility.
excess.vignette: Sam dismisses everything as ‘maya’—his responsibilities, others’ suffering, the whole practical world waved away as illusion. The teaching about misperception has become a bypass; calling the world unreal has become his excuse to disengage from it.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Inability to see through illusions results in complete delusion, mistaking the temporary for the permanent, and attaching to what is false.
facets.somatic: Complete physical delusion about reality, poor judgment and vulnerability to deception, physical attachment to illusions.
facets.emotional: Emotional delusion and attachment, emotional suffering from mistaking illusions for reality, emotional bondage to false appearances.
facets.cognitive: Complete mental delusion, inability to see through appearances, mental attachment to illusions, mental bondage and suffering.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly taken in by appearances, with no suspicion that the obvious might be a veiling.

deficient.text:
Inability to see through illusions results in complete delusion, mistaking the temporary for the permanent, and attaching to what is false.

Somatic: Complete physical delusion about reality, poor judgment and vulnerability to deception, physical attachment to illusions.

Emotional: Emotional delusion and attachment, emotional suffering from mistaking illusions for reality, emotional bondage to false appearances.

Cognitive: Complete mental delusion, inability to see through appearances, mental attachment to illusions, mental bondage and suffering.

Psychic: The soul is wholly taken in by appearances, with no suspicion that the obvious might be a veiling.
deficient.vignette: Dan is wholly taken in by appearances—the glamour, the surface, the persuasive show—with no capacity to see through any of it to what’s actually there. The discerning vision that penetrates maya is undeveloped; he believes the world exactly as it presents itself.

attributes.title: Niscreyasa (The Ultimate Good)
attributes.aspect: Niscreyasa is the pursuit of long-term spiritual well-being and liberation, contrasted sharply with Preyas (immediate, short-term sensory pleasure). It represents the choice of the wise.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State / goal — the ultimate good (niḥśreyasa) · Valence: The goal · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Transcendent · Causal status: Fruit (the highest good, liberation) · Key relation: The summum bonum; the fruit of right knowing.
coordinate.key_relation: The summum bonum; the fruit of right knowing.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When pursuing Niscreyasa, a person makes wise long-term choices even when requiring short-term sacrifice. This leads to lasting peace, liberation, and profound fulfillment.
facets.somatic: Physical health from wise choices, body serves spiritual purpose, good vitality from aligned actions, physical sustainability.
facets.emotional: Emotional peace from alignment with higher purpose, emotional fulfillment that lasts, emotional strength from values, emotional freedom from addiction to quick fixes.
facets.cognitive: Mental clarity about true well-being, mental strength to choose wisely, mental peace from alignment, mental wisdom and discernment.
facets.psychic: The soul orients its whole life toward the highest good, the lasting freedom beyond all transient gains.

balanced.text:
When pursuing Niscreyasa, a person makes wise long-term choices even when requiring short-term sacrifice. This leads to lasting peace, liberation, and profound fulfillment.

Somatic: Physical health from wise choices, body serves spiritual purpose, good vitality from aligned actions, physical sustainability.

Emotional: Emotional peace from alignment with higher purpose, emotional fulfillment that lasts, emotional strength from values, emotional freedom from addiction to quick fixes.

Cognitive: Mental clarity about true well-being, mental strength to choose wisely, mental peace from alignment, mental wisdom and discernment.

Psychic: The soul orients its whole life toward the highest good, the lasting freedom beyond all transient gains.
balanced.vignette: Beneath his ordinary aims, Ravi is oriented toward the highest good—the ultimate freedom that relativizes every smaller prize—and it quietly orders his priorities. The orientation doesn’t make him neglect his life; it gives it a north.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Over-pursuit of the ultimate good without compassion can lead to harshness, rejection of legitimate pleasures, and spiritual pride.
facets.somatic: Over-discipline and physical rigidity, rejection of healthy pleasures, harsh physical practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional judgment of those seeking pleasure, emotional coldness, difficulty with human warmth.
facets.cognitive: Mental rigidity about what is good, spiritual pride, inability to accept that pleasure has legitimate role.
facets.psychic: The soul becomes grimly single-minded, sacrificing all legitimate human goods in a forced pursuit of the ultimate.

excess.text:
Over-pursuit of the ultimate good without compassion can lead to harshness, rejection of legitimate pleasures, and spiritual pride.

Somatic: Over-discipline and physical rigidity, rejection of healthy pleasures, harsh physical practice.

Emotional: Emotional judgment of those seeking pleasure, emotional coldness, difficulty with human warmth.

Cognitive: Mental rigidity about what is good, spiritual pride, inability to accept that pleasure has legitimate role.

Psychic: The soul becomes grimly single-minded, sacrificing all legitimate human goods in a forced pursuit of the ultimate.
excess.vignette: Sam grasps at the ultimate good impatiently—demanding enlightenment now, resentful of the slow path, treating the highest freedom as one more thing to acquire and display. The very grasping for the supreme good has become an obstacle to it.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Choosing only immediate Preyas over long-term Niscreyasa leads to short-term satisfaction followed by long-term suffering, addiction, and spiritual emptiness.
facets.somatic: Poor long-term physical health, addiction and indulgence, physical deterioration, lack of lasting vitality.
facets.emotional: Emotional emptiness beneath momentary pleasures, emotional addiction and dependency, emotional suffering and dissatisfaction, emotional instability.
facets.cognitive: Mental focus on immediate gratification, inability to see long-term consequences, mental restlessness and dissatisfaction, mental slavery to impulses.
facets.psychic: The soul never lifts its eyes to any ultimate good, settling for one passing aim after another.

deficient.text:
Choosing only immediate Preyas over long-term Niscreyasa leads to short-term satisfaction followed by long-term suffering, addiction, and spiritual emptiness.

Somatic: Poor long-term physical health, addiction and indulgence, physical deterioration, lack of lasting vitality.

Emotional: Emotional emptiness beneath momentary pleasures, emotional addiction and dependency, emotional suffering and dissatisfaction, emotional instability.

Cognitive: Mental focus on immediate gratification, inability to see long-term consequences, mental restlessness and dissatisfaction, mental slavery to impulses.

Psychic: The soul never lifts its eyes to any ultimate good, settling for one passing aim after another.
deficient.vignette: Dan is oriented toward nothing beyond the immediate—the next comfort, the next win, the next distraction—with no sense of any ultimate good that might order his desires. The highest end the teaching names is off his map; he lives entirely among the nearer things.

attributes.title: Svadharma (Individual Nature-Driven Duty)
attributes.aspect: Svadharma is the realization that one’s true obligations must align with one’s inherent psychological makeup (Svabhava) rather than artificial social expectations. It is duty aligned with natural inclination and capacity.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Faculty / calling — one’s own duty (svadharma) · Valence: Virtue to align with · Guṇa: Sattva when aligned · Scale: Individual, engaging the world · Causal status: Means / calling · Key relation: Same as Svadharma (Dim 16); nature-driven duty.
coordinate.key_relation: Same as Svadharma (Dim 16); nature-driven duty.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When living according to Svadharma, a person’s actions align with their nature, gifts, and values. There is authenticity, fulfillment, and natural excellence.
facets.somatic: Physical health from work aligned with capacity, good energy from meaningful engagement, natural physical excellence from aligned action.
facets.emotional: Emotional fulfillment from authentic work, emotional peace from alignment with values, emotional strength from living authentically, emotional joy in expression.
facets.cognitive: Mental clarity about natural gifts, mental focus and excellence, mental peace from authenticity, mental fulfillment from aligned action.
facets.psychic: The soul follows its own nature-given duty, finding freedom in the work that fits its constitution.

balanced.text:
When living according to Svadharma, a person’s actions align with their nature, gifts, and values. There is authenticity, fulfillment, and natural excellence.

Somatic: Physical health from work aligned with capacity, good energy from meaningful engagement, natural physical excellence from aligned action.

Emotional: Emotional fulfillment from authentic work, emotional peace from alignment with values, emotional strength from living authentically, emotional joy in expression.

Cognitive: Mental clarity about natural gifts, mental focus and excellence, mental peace from authenticity, mental fulfillment from aligned action.

Psychic: The soul follows its own nature-given duty, finding freedom in the work that fits its constitution.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has found the work that’s genuinely his—suited to his nature, expressive of his gifts—and does it well and gladly, rather than living out someone else’s script. Following his own dharma, imperfect as it is, has made him more whole than any borrowed perfection could.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Over-emphasis on personal nature without considering social responsibility can lead to selfishness, narcissism, and harm to others.
facets.somatic: Physical indulgence in personal pursuits without care for others, potentially harmful physical actions.
facets.emotional: Emotional selfishness and narcissism, lack of emotional consideration for others, emotional entitlement.
facets.cognitive: Mental selfishness and lack of social consideration, mental entitlement, inability to consider others’ needs.
facets.psychic: The soul rigidly absolutizes its role, mistaking a fixed identity for its living dharma.

excess.text:
Over-emphasis on personal nature without considering social responsibility can lead to selfishness, narcissism, and harm to others.

Somatic: Physical indulgence in personal pursuits without care for others, potentially harmful physical actions.

Emotional: Emotional selfishness and narcissism, lack of emotional consideration for others, emotional entitlement.

Cognitive: Mental selfishness and lack of social consideration, mental entitlement, inability to consider others’ needs.

Psychic: The soul rigidly absolutizes its role, mistaking a fixed identity for its living dharma.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned his ‘dharma’ into a rigid cage—forcing himself into a role he’s decided is his duty, grimly, joylessly, past all evidence that it doesn’t fit. The teaching about one’s own nature has hardened into a self-imposed sentence he serves without question.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Rejection of Svadharma to follow others’ expectations leads to inauthenticity, lack of fulfillment, poor performance, and internal conflict.
facets.somatic: Poor physical health from work misaligned with nature, lack of physical vitality, physical mediocrity despite effort.
facets.emotional: Emotional emptiness from inauthenticity, emotional resentment about imposed duties, emotional frustration despite effort.
facets.cognitive: Mental confusion about true purpose, mental frustration and poor performance, mental resentment about imposed paths, mental unfulfillment.
facets.psychic: The soul abandons its own path to imitate another’s, and lives in the chronic friction of a borrowed life.

deficient.text:
Rejection of Svadharma to follow others’ expectations leads to inauthenticity, lack of fulfillment, poor performance, and internal conflict.

Somatic: Poor physical health from work misaligned with nature, lack of physical vitality, physical mediocrity despite effort.

Emotional: Emotional emptiness from inauthenticity, emotional resentment about imposed duties, emotional frustration despite effort.

Cognitive: Mental confusion about true purpose, mental frustration and poor performance, mental resentment about imposed paths, mental unfulfillment.

Psychic: The soul abandons its own path to imitate another’s, and lives in the chronic friction of a borrowed life.
deficient.vignette: Dan lives someone else’s life—the career his father chose, the path his peers took, a borrowed script he never questioned—with no sense of what’s actually his to do. His own nature-driven duty is undiscovered, buried under other people’s expectations.

attributes.title: Bhakti-Jnana-Vairagya Cycle
attributes.aspect: The Bhagavatam’s distinctive cycle: Bhakti (devotion) opens the heart; experienced ananda (joy) is the result; from ananda arises Jnana (awareness/insight); from jnana arises Vairagya (dispassion). In daily life: cultivating any one strengthens the others. Devotional listening produces joy, which produces clarity, which produces detachment. Not a linear path but a self-reinforcing cycle.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework / process — a mutually-reinforcing cycle · Valence: Virtue-cycle to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (each deepens the others) · Key relation: The reinforcing loop of Bhakti and Jñāna (Dim 10) and Vairāgya (Dim 23).
coordinate.key_relation: The reinforcing loop of Bhakti and Jñāna (Dim 10) and Vairāgya (Dim 23).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Daily cultivation of any one (usually bhakti) producing all three. The cycle becomes self-sustaining.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in any of three; the others follow.
facets.emotional: Emotional life cycles through devotion-joy-clarity-detachment.
facets.cognitive: Mind in continuous cycle of devotional development.
facets.psychic: The soul lets devotion, knowledge, and dispassion feed one another in a rising spiral, each strengthening the next.

balanced.text:
Daily cultivation of any one (usually bhakti) producing all three. The cycle becomes self-sustaining.

Somatic: Body engaged in any of three; the others follow.

Emotional: Emotional life cycles through devotion-joy-clarity-detachment.

Cognitive: Mind in continuous cycle of devotional development.

Psychic: The soul lets devotion, knowledge, and dispassion feed one another in a rising spiral, each strengthening the next.
balanced.vignette: In Ravi the three reinforce each other—his devotion feeds his knowledge, his knowledge deepens his detachment, his detachment purifies his devotion—turning as one wheel. No single strand dominates; the cycle carries him, and he’s the more balanced for it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mechanical pursuit of all three as separate practices. Missing the organic interconnection.
facets.somatic: Body in multiple disjointed practices.
facets.emotional: Emotional life fragmented across practices.
facets.cognitive: Mental compartmentalization.
facets.psychic: The soul over-emphasizes one strand — all feeling, or all analysis, or all renunciation — and the balance is lost.

excess.text:
Mechanical pursuit of all three as separate practices. Missing the organic interconnection.

Somatic: Body in multiple disjointed practices.

Emotional: Emotional life fragmented across practices.

Cognitive: Mental compartmentalization.

Psychic: The soul over-emphasizes one strand — all feeling, or all analysis, or all renunciation — and the balance is lost.
excess.vignette: Sam has seized one strand at the others’ expense—all dry knowledge and no devotion, or all emotional bhakti and no discernment—and the imbalance has stalled him. The cycle that should turn as a whole grinds, one gear driving the rest into the ground.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: None of three cultivated. Daily life without bhakti, without joy, without clarity, without detachment.
facets.somatic: Body without spiritual exercise.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without sacred dimension.
facets.cognitive: Mind without development in any of three.
facets.psychic: The soul cultivates none of the three, and its inner life has neither warmth, light, nor freedom.

deficient.text:
None of three cultivated. Daily life without bhakti, without joy, without clarity, without detachment.

Somatic: Body without spiritual exercise.

Emotional: Emotional life without sacred dimension.

Cognitive: Mind without development in any of three.

Psychic: The soul cultivates none of the three, and its inner life has neither warmth, light, nor freedom.
deficient.vignette: Dan has developed none of the three—no devotion, no real knowledge, no detachment—and so the transforming cycle never begins to turn in him. The mutually reinforcing wheel of the spiritual life sits, in his case, entirely still.

attributes.title: Four Types of Dhyana (Mahavira’s Classification)
attributes.aspect: Mahavira’s revolutionary framework: meditation is not a special activity—everyone meditates. The angry person meditates on anger; the suffering on suffering; the religious on dharma; the highest on pure consciousness. The quality of life depends on the object of meditation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a fourfold classification of meditation · Valence: Neutral (a taxonomy) · Guṇa: Spans (from tamasic to sattvic dhyāna) · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: Mahāvīra’s taxonomy of meditative states, from harmful to liberating.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework — a fourfold classification of meditation
coordinate.key_relation: Mahāvīra’s taxonomy of meditative states, from harmful to liberating.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Awareness that one is always meditating allows conscious choice. Dharmadhyan and Shukladhyan—dwelling on truth and pure consciousness—transform life.
facets.somatic: Body shaped by dharmic dwelling—health, vitality, peace.
facets.emotional: Emotions colored by chosen meditation—peace, joy, compassion.
facets.cognitive: Mind elevated by sattvic meditation, ultimately moving toward formless awareness.
facets.psychic: The soul discerns wholesome meditation from unwholesome, cultivating the elevating forms and abandoning the brooding ones.

balanced.text:
Awareness that one is always meditating allows conscious choice. Dharmadhyan and Shukladhyan—dwelling on truth and pure consciousness—transform life.

Somatic: Body shaped by dharmic dwelling—health, vitality, peace.

Emotional: Emotions colored by chosen meditation—peace, joy, compassion.

Cognitive: Mind elevated by sattvic meditation, ultimately moving toward formless awareness.

Psychic: The soul discerns wholesome meditation from unwholesome, cultivating the elevating forms and abandoning the brooding ones.
balanced.vignette: Ravi’s meditation is of the wholesome kind—the mind gathered on the true and the good, the practice clarifying and steadying him. He’s learned to tell the meditations that liberate from those that bind, and to dwell in the ones that lift.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Pride in higher meditation while still unconsciously engaging in raudradhyan or artadhyan—claiming spirituality while being secretly angry or self-pitying.
facets.somatic: Body shows the lower meditations even when one claims higher practice.
facets.emotional: Hidden anger or suffering beneath claimed spirituality.
facets.cognitive: Self-deception about what one is actually meditating on.
facets.psychic: The soul over-classifies meditative states, analyzing types rather than meditating.

excess.text:
Pride in higher meditation while still unconsciously engaging in raudradhyan or artadhyan—claiming spirituality while being secretly angry or self-pitying.

Somatic: Body shows the lower meditations even when one claims higher practice.

Emotional: Hidden anger or suffering beneath claimed spirituality.

Cognitive: Self-deception about what one is actually meditating on.

Psychic: The soul over-classifies meditative states, analyzing types rather than meditating.
excess.vignette: Sam ‘meditates’ on his grievances and his cravings—rehearsing the wrong done to him, dwelling on the object of his desire, a dark concentration that deepens the very bondage it feeds. Not all sustained attention frees; his is a meditation that binds, and he can’t tell the difference.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Unconscious life—not realizing that anger, lust, jealousy are meditations producing their effects. Helpless to be otherwise.
facets.somatic: Body damaged by unconscious negative meditations—illness, stress-related disease.
facets.emotional: Caught in raudradhyan or artadhyan without awareness, repeating patterns.
facets.cognitive: Unconscious mental dwelling shaping life negatively, no awareness of the mechanism.
facets.psychic: The soul’s mind broods in the dark forms of meditation — sorrowful and cruel rumination — mistaking them for thought.

deficient.text:
Unconscious life—not realizing that anger, lust, jealousy are meditations producing their effects. Helpless to be otherwise.

Somatic: Body damaged by unconscious negative meditations—illness, stress-related disease.

Emotional: Caught in raudradhyan or artadhyan without awareness, repeating patterns.

Cognitive: Unconscious mental dwelling shaping life negatively, no awareness of the mechanism.

Psychic: The soul’s mind broods in the dark forms of meditation — sorrowful and cruel rumination — mistaking them for thought.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s mind never gathers on anything—no meditation of any kind, wholesome or otherwise, just the scattered churn of the untrained mind. The whole disciplined practice of directed attention, in any of its forms, is foreign to him.

attributes.title: Universal Destiny (Beyond Individual Ego)
attributes.aspect: Individual destiny is the ego’s illusion. We are part of the universal continuity—every atom, every event in cosmic history has contributed to our existence. When this is realized, individual concerns dissolve into participation in cosmic destiny.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / pointer · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: the movement of all toward the one goal, beyond the individual ego.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the movement of all toward the one goal, beyond the individual ego.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When universal destiny is realized, one no longer suffers as a separate individual. The body dies as a part; the universe continues. There is freedom beyond birth and death.
facets.somatic: Body relaxed in trust of cosmic flow, no individual desperate clinging.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from personal destiny anxiety, deep peace.
facets.cognitive: Mind aware of universal interconnection, freedom from ego-bound concerns.
facets.psychic: The soul senses its life as part of a universal movement, and its small concerns relax within that larger story.

balanced.text:
When universal destiny is realized, one no longer suffers as a separate individual. The body dies as a part; the universe continues. There is freedom beyond birth and death.

Somatic: Body relaxed in trust of cosmic flow, no individual desperate clinging.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from personal destiny anxiety, deep peace.

Cognitive: Mind aware of universal interconnection, freedom from ego-bound concerns.

Psychic: The soul senses its life as part of a universal movement, and its small concerns relax within that larger story.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to sense a destiny larger than his own ego’s story—a movement of the whole that his small life participates in—and it’s loosened his self-importance. He plays his part with care and holds it lightly, part of something vaster than himself.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Premature identification with universe as escape from personal responsibility—‘I am the universe so nothing matters.’ Spiritual bypassing of individual ethics.
facets.somatic: Neglect of body using cosmic excuse, lack of practical care.
facets.emotional: Emotional disconnection from human relationships, false transcendence.
facets.cognitive: Bypassing personal work claiming cosmic identity.
facets.psychic: The soul inflates, identifying its ego with cosmic destiny and grandiose mission.

excess.text:
Premature identification with universe as escape from personal responsibility—‘I am the universe so nothing matters.’ Spiritual bypassing of individual ethics.

Somatic: Neglect of body using cosmic excuse, lack of practical care.

Emotional: Emotional disconnection from human relationships, false transcendence.

Cognitive: Bypassing personal work claiming cosmic identity.

Psychic: The soul inflates, identifying its ego with cosmic destiny and grandiose mission.
excess.vignette: Sam invokes ‘universal destiny’ to dodge his own—no personal responsibility, no individual effort, since ‘it’s all the cosmic movement anyway.’ The vision of a larger destiny has become an excuse to abdicate the particular one that’s his to live.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total identification with individual destiny—‘I’ as separate, isolated, with personal birth and personal death. Maximum suffering, maximum fear.
facets.somatic: Body experiencing isolation, no sense of cosmic support, chronic stress.
facets.emotional: Anxiety about individual destiny, fear of death, isolation suffering.
facets.cognitive: Mind trapped in ‘I am separate’ delusion, all the suffering of individuality.
facets.psychic: The soul sees only its private fate, cut off from any larger meaning, and so feels small and forsaken.

deficient.text:
Total identification with individual destiny—‘I’ as separate, isolated, with personal birth and personal death. Maximum suffering, maximum fear.

Somatic: Body experiencing isolation, no sense of cosmic support, chronic stress.

Emotional: Anxiety about individual destiny, fear of death, isolation suffering.

Cognitive: Mind trapped in ‘I am separate’ delusion, all the suffering of individuality.

Psychic: The soul sees only its private fate, cut off from any larger meaning, and so feels small and forsaken.
deficient.vignette: Dan is trapped entirely in his own ego’s story—his gains, his losses, his little arc the whole of reality—with no sense of any larger destiny it belongs to. The vaster movement the teaching points to is invisible; he is, to himself, the center and the sum.

attributes.title: Complementariness of Sin and Virtue
attributes.aspect: The Hindu view sees good and evil not as warring opposites but as complementary balance. The lotus needs dirty mud to flower. Sin gives meaning to virtue; they balance the cosmic process. God is the neutral source from which both arise.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — an epistemic distinction · Valence: Neutral — a discernment to make · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Framework (clarifies how one knows) · Key relation: sin and virtue as complementary within the larger movement, transcended in liberation.
coordinate.key_relation: sin and virtue as complementary within the larger movement, transcended in liberation.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Seeing sin and virtue as complementary creates inner peace—no civil war between halves of self. The whole is accepted, transcending good/evil dichotomy.
facets.somatic: Body without internal conflict, peace replacing internal Crucifixion-pattern.
facets.emotional: Emotional acceptance of all aspects of self, no rejected shadow.
facets.cognitive: Mind transcending moral dualism, peace beyond good and evil.
facets.psychic: The soul holds good and evil as a complementary pair within the larger whole, neither naive about wrong nor self-righteous.

balanced.text:
Seeing sin and virtue as complementary creates inner peace—no civil war between halves of self. The whole is accepted, transcending good/evil dichotomy.

Somatic: Body without internal conflict, peace replacing internal Crucifixion-pattern.

Emotional: Emotional acceptance of all aspects of self, no rejected shadow.

Cognitive: Mind transcending moral dualism, peace beyond good and evil.

Psychic: The soul holds good and evil as a complementary pair within the larger whole, neither naive about wrong nor self-righteous.
balanced.vignette: Ravi holds moral life with a hard-won nuance—knowing that virtue and vice are more entangled than the simple stories say, that his own good acts have shadows and his failings have lessons. The complexity hasn’t made him amoral; it’s made him humble and merciful.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Misunderstanding leading to amoralism—‘since good and evil are one, anything goes.’ Tantric license without tantric discipline.
facets.somatic: Body abused as ‘beyond morality,’ destructive behavior.
facets.emotional: Emotional license, no compass.
facets.cognitive: False non-dualism justifying anything.
facets.psychic: The soul over-relativizes morality, using ‘beyond good and evil’ to excuse genuine harm.

excess.text:
Misunderstanding leading to amoralism—‘since good and evil are one, anything goes.’ Tantric license without tantric discipline.

Somatic: Body abused as ‘beyond morality,’ destructive behavior.

Emotional: Emotional license, no compass.

Cognitive: False non-dualism justifying anything.

Psychic: The soul over-relativizes morality, using ‘beyond good and evil’ to excuse genuine harm.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned ‘beyond good and evil’ into a license—since virtue and vice are ‘complementary,’ nothing he does is really wrong, and ordinary morality is for the unenlightened. The subtle teaching about moral complexity has become his bypass around accountability.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Christian-style absolute dualism—good/evil at war creates inner war. Constant guilt, suppression of shadow, sad spirituality. Suffering from internal civil war.
facets.somatic: Body as battleground, antibody self-attack, illness.
facets.emotional: Constant guilt, shadow-suppression, sad religious life.
facets.cognitive: Mental civil war, fragmentation, depression from rejecting half of life.
facets.psychic: The soul splits the world rigidly into pure good and pure evil, unable to see the whole that holds both.

deficient.text:
Christian-style absolute dualism—good/evil at war creates inner war. Constant guilt, suppression of shadow, sad spirituality. Suffering from internal civil war.

Somatic: Body as battleground, antibody self-attack, illness.

Emotional: Constant guilt, shadow-suppression, sad religious life.

Cognitive: Mental civil war, fragmentation, depression from rejecting half of life.

Psychic: The soul splits the world rigidly into pure good and pure evil, unable to see the whole that holds both.
deficient.vignette: Dan sees the moral world in stark black and white—the good wholly good, the bad wholly bad, no complexity, no mercy, no shadow in the virtue or lesson in the vice. The entanglement of sin and virtue the teaching names is beyond his rigid, brittle moralism.

attributes.title: Nama (Name as First Reality)
attributes.aspect: Name (Nama) is the most basic reality we engage with—all knowledge is fundamentally name-knowledge. The Vedas, sciences, arts—all are systems of names. Recognizing this is the first teaching: meditate on Name to understand its scope, and discover that beyond it lies something greater.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework / principle — name as the first reality (nāma) · Valence: Neutral · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Cosmic (Ādhidaivika) · Causal status: Cause (name and form as the first differentiation) · Key relation: Nāma as the primal reality; kin to Vāc (Dim 27) and nāma-rūpa.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework / principle — name as the first reality (nāma)
coordinate.causal_status: Cause (name and form as the first differentiation)
coordinate.key_relation: Nāma as the primal reality; kin to Vāc (Dim 27) and nāma-rūpa.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Awareness of how thoroughly we live in named-reality. Recognition that names point to but don’t capture reality.
facets.somatic: Body’s experience recognized as filtered through naming.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactions seen as name-based.
facets.cognitive: Mind aware of its name-constructed world.
facets.psychic: The soul senses the creative power of name — how naming carves a world out of the undifferentiated — and uses words with care.

balanced.text:
Awareness of how thoroughly we live in named-reality. Recognition that names point to but don’t capture reality.

Somatic: Body’s experience recognized as filtered through naming.

Emotional: Emotional reactions seen as name-based.

Cognitive: Mind aware of its name-constructed world.

Psychic: The soul senses the creative power of name — how naming carves a world out of the undifferentiated — and uses words with care.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to feel the creative weight of names—that to name a thing is partly to call it into being, to shape reality by how it’s spoken. He names carefully, aware that his words don’t just describe his world but help make it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Obsession with naming as ultimate reality—mistaking words for things.
facets.somatic: Body engagement reduced to labeling.
facets.emotional: Emotional life through pre-named filters.
facets.cognitive: Mind fixated on names as final reality.
facets.psychic: The soul mistakes the name for the named, lost in labels and definitions.

excess.text:
Obsession with naming as ultimate reality—mistaking words for things.

Somatic: Body engagement reduced to labeling.

Emotional: Emotional life through pre-named filters.

Cognitive: Mind fixated on names as final reality.

Psychic: The soul mistakes the name for the named, lost in labels and definitions.
excess.vignette: Sam has literalized the power of naming into magic—convinced the right words, chanted just so, will manifest what he wants, blaming others for ‘naming’ their troubles into being. The teaching that naming is creative has become a superstition.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Living entirely through unconscious naming—no awareness of the name-constructed nature of experience.
facets.somatic: Body responses driven by unconscious naming.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactions to names rather than realities.
facets.cognitive: Mind takes its names as reality without question.
facets.psychic: The soul takes names as empty noise, deaf to how language shapes the reality it perceives.

deficient.text:
Living entirely through unconscious naming—no awareness of the name-constructed nature of experience.

Somatic: Body responses driven by unconscious naming.

Emotional: Emotional reactions to names rather than realities.

Cognitive: Mind takes its names as reality without question.

Psychic: The soul takes names as empty noise, deaf to how language shapes the reality it perceives.
deficient.vignette: For Dan a name is just a label—arbitrary, inert, describing a reality it has no hand in shaping. That naming might be a first reality, a creative act that calls forth what it names, is a notion with no purchase; words, to him, only point at a world already fixed.

attributes.title: Allegorical Mahabharata (Inner Characters)
attributes.aspect: Lahiri Mahasay’s profound allegorical interpretation: every character of the Mahabharata represents an inner psychological force in the Kriyavan. Duryodhana represents the mind that refuses to do Kriya. Krishna represents Kutastha consciousness. The Pandavas are the pure tendencies; the Kauravas the impure. The battlefield Kurukshetra is the body. The Gita is Krishna (Kutastha) instructing Arjuna (soul) during the inner war.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — the epic read as inner characters · Valence: Neutral (an interpretive key) · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: The Mahābhārata’s figures as the inner faculties and forces.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework — the epic read as inner characters
coordinate.key_relation: The Mahābhārata’s figures as the inner faculties and forces.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of all characters as inner psychological forces. Scripture read as instruction for inner work.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as Kurukshetra—the field of inner battle.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns identified as Kaurava-tendencies to be transformed.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees scripture as map of inner psychology.
facets.psychic: The soul reads the great epic inwardly, recognizing its warring characters as forces within its own psyche.

balanced.text:
Recognition of all characters as inner psychological forces. Scripture read as instruction for inner work.

Somatic: Body recognized as Kurukshetra—the field of inner battle.

Emotional: Emotional patterns identified as Kaurava-tendencies to be transformed.

Cognitive: Mind sees scripture as map of inner psychology.

Psychic: The soul reads the great epic inwardly, recognizing its warring characters as forces within its own psyche.
balanced.vignette: Ravi reads the great war as happening inside him—the noble impulses and the grasping ones, the wise counsel and the blind attachment, all inner characters in his own field. The allegory has become a mirror; the epic’s battle is the one he fights each day.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forcing allegorical interpretation onto every detail, missing historical/literary value.
facets.somatic: Body neglected in over-allegorization.
facets.emotional: Emotional richness of stories dismissed.
facets.cognitive: Mind locked in single interpretation.
facets.psychic: The soul over-allegorizes, reducing every story to a scheme and missing its living force.

excess.text:
Forcing allegorical interpretation onto every detail, missing historical/literary value.

Somatic: Body neglected in over-allegorization.

Emotional: Emotional richness of stories dismissed.

Cognitive: Mind locked in single interpretation.

Psychic: The soul over-allegorizes, reducing every story to a scheme and missing its living force.
excess.vignette: Sam allegorizes everything—every story, every event, every dream forced into elaborate symbolic meaning until nothing is allowed to be simply itself. The fruitful reading of the epic as inner drama has metastasized into a compulsion that buries the literal under endless interpretation.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Reading scripture only literally/historically. Missing inner psychological message.
facets.somatic: Body not engaged as Kurukshetra.
facets.emotional: Emotional content treated only as story.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees only external narrative.
facets.psychic: The soul reads only the surface tale, never suspecting that the battlefield is its own heart.

deficient.text:
Reading scripture only literally/historically. Missing inner psychological message.

Somatic: Body not engaged as Kurukshetra.

Emotional: Emotional content treated only as story.

Cognitive: Mind sees only external narrative.

Psychic: The soul reads only the surface tale, never suspecting that the battlefield is its own heart.
deficient.vignette: Dan reads only the surface—the epic a war story, his own life a series of literal events, with no sense of any inner meaning beneath the plot. The allegorical dimension, where the outer tale mirrors the inner battle, is invisible to him; everything is exactly and only what it appears.

attributes.title: Birth and Death as Correlatives (Sasyam Iva)
attributes.aspect: Like corn growing and falling, growing again from fallen seed—human life follows the same pattern. Birth contains death; death contains rebirth. They are not opposites but two phases of one organic process. Understanding this dissolves the terror of death.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Physical · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / pointer · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, toward the Absolute · Causal status: Framework / realization · Key relation: birth and death as correlative like the grass (sasyam iva) — the cycle of coming and going.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: birth and death as correlative like the grass (sasyam iva) — the cycle of coming and going.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of birth-death as one organic process. Neither attached to birth-celebration nor terrified by death-fear.
facets.somatic: Body lived as natural seed-cycle, no terror of decay.
facets.emotional: Emotional acceptance of both birth and death as natural correlatives.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees the organic unity of life and death.
facets.psychic: The soul accepts birth and death as a single correlated rhythm — like crops that ripen, fall, and rise again — and meets mortality without terror.

balanced.text:
Recognition of birth-death as one organic process. Neither attached to birth-celebration nor terrified by death-fear.

Somatic: Body lived as natural seed-cycle, no terror of decay.

Emotional: Emotional acceptance of both birth and death as natural correlatives.

Cognitive: Mind sees the organic unity of life and death.

Psychic: The soul accepts birth and death as a single correlated rhythm — like crops that ripen, fall, and rise again — and meets mortality without terror.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to hold birth and death as two ends of one thing—like a plant that rises and falls in a single motion—and the framing has quieted his fear. He sees his own coming and going as one natural gesture, and meets his mortality with a strange equanimity.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Fatalistic acceptance—using the cycle as excuse for not living fully. ‘Why bother, it all dies anyway.’
facets.somatic: Body neglected as ‘just part of cycle.’
facets.emotional: Emotional disengagement justified by cycle-awareness.
facets.cognitive: Nihilistic interpretation of the cycle teaching.
facets.psychic: The soul becomes morbidly preoccupied with the cycle, losing the present to brooding on its turns.

excess.text:
Fatalistic acceptance—using the cycle as excuse for not living fully. ‘Why bother, it all dies anyway.’

Somatic: Body neglected as ‘just part of cycle.’

Emotional: Emotional disengagement justified by cycle-awareness.

Cognitive: Nihilistic interpretation of the cycle teaching.

Psychic: The soul becomes morbidly preoccupied with the cycle, losing the present to brooding on its turns.
excess.vignette: Sam has grown morbidly fixated on the death end of the pair—brooding on impermanence, romanticizing dissolution, using the correlation of birth and death to justify a dark withdrawal from living. The equanimous teaching has curdled into a fixation.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total separation of birth and death—birth celebrated, death feared. Maximum terror, no recognition of underlying unity.
facets.somatic: Body terrified by aging and decay.
facets.emotional: Death-terror coloring all life.
facets.cognitive: Mind unable to see life and death as one process.
facets.psychic: The soul takes death as a final, isolated catastrophe, with no sense of the rhythm that holds it.

deficient.text:
Total separation of birth and death—birth celebrated, death feared. Maximum terror, no recognition of underlying unity.

Somatic: Body terrified by aging and decay.

Emotional: Death-terror coloring all life.

Cognitive: Mind unable to see life and death as one process.

Psychic: The soul takes death as a final, isolated catastrophe, with no sense of the rhythm that holds it.
deficient.vignette: Dan clings to the birth end and denies the death—grasping at life, refusing to contemplate its ending, treating death as an outrage rather than the natural correlate of being born. That the two belong together, one gesture, is a truth he can’t let himself hold.

attributes.title: Three Desires (Wealth, Worlds, Sons)
attributes.aspect: Knowing the Self, sages give up the three desires: for offspring, for wealth, for worlds (heaven). These three eshanas (cravings) bind one to samsara. When transcended, one becomes a wandering monk.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Framework — the three desires (wealth, worlds, sons) · Valence: Affliction-set (to renounce) · Guṇa: Rajas · Scale: Individual / interpersonal · Causal status: Root cravings · Key relation: The three eṣaṇās to renounce for freedom; same as the Eśanas (Dim 12).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework — the three desires (wealth, worlds, sons)
coordinate.key_relation: The three eṣaṇās to renounce for freedom; same as the Eśanas (Dim 12).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Wise relationship with desires for family, prosperity, status—engaged but not bound. Eventually transcended.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in life without slavery to three desires.
facets.emotional: Emotional engagement with family/wealth/world without compulsive grasping.
facets.cognitive: Mind oriented beyond the three desires.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the three great desires that drive worldly life, and gradually sees through them toward the freedom beyond.

balanced.text:
Wise relationship with desires for family, prosperity, status—engaged but not bound. Eventually transcended.

Somatic: Body engaged in life without slavery to three desires.

Emotional: Emotional engagement with family/wealth/world without compulsive grasping.

Cognitive: Mind oriented beyond the three desires.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the three great desires that drive worldly life, and gradually sees through them toward the freedom beyond.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has quietly loosened the three great cravings—for wealth, for status and ‘worlds,’ for progeny and legacy—wanting them still, but no longer ruled by them. Free of the grip of the three, he pursues what he values without being enslaved to any of it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Premature renunciation of three desires without genuine transcendence—forced asceticism.
facets.somatic: Forced rejection of legitimate engagements.
facets.emotional: Suppressed desires creating distortion.
facets.cognitive: Pseudo-renunciation without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly consumed by these desires, mistaking their satisfaction for the goal of life.

excess.text:
Premature renunciation of three desires without genuine transcendence—forced asceticism.

Somatic: Forced rejection of legitimate engagements.

Emotional: Suppressed desires creating distortion.

Cognitive: Pseudo-renunciation without realization.

Psychic: The soul is wholly consumed by these desires, mistaking their satisfaction for the goal of life.
excess.vignette: Sam has renounced the three desires harshly—forcing down every wish for security, recognition, or legacy, treating each as a sin to be crushed. The freeing loosening the teaching describes has become a grim, life-denying suppression that leaves him clenched.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total bondage to three desires—life entirely about offspring, wealth, status. Maximum samsara.
facets.somatic: Body driven by desire for progeny, wealth, status.
facets.emotional: Emotional life dominated by these three pursuits.
facets.cognitive: Mind entirely captured by three desires.
facets.psychic: The soul forcibly represses these natural drives without understanding, and the denied energy curdles into frustration.

deficient.text:
Total bondage to three desires—life entirely about offspring, wealth, status. Maximum samsara.

Somatic: Body driven by desire for progeny, wealth, status.

Emotional: Emotional life dominated by these three pursuits.

Cognitive: Mind entirely captured by three desires.

Psychic: The soul forcibly represses these natural drives without understanding, and the denied energy curdles into frustration.
deficient.vignette: Dan is ruled entirely by the three—the endless hunger for wealth, the craving for status and standing, the drive for legacy—chasing all three without pause and never satisfied. The great cravings the teaching would loosen own him completely, and he calls the enslavement ambition.

attributes.title: Death and the Two Paths (Devayana and Pitryana)
attributes.aspect: Two paths after death: Devayana (path of the gods) for those who know Brahman—they pass through flame, day, bright fortnight, six months of sun’s northern course, year, sun, moon, lightning—never to return to samsara. Pitryana (path of the fathers) for those of good works—they pass through smoke, night, dark fortnight, six months of sun’s southern course—and return to be reborn.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework / doctrine — the two after-death paths · Valence: Neutral (a cosmological map) · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Cosmic (Ādhidaivika) · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: Devayāna (path of the gods, no return) vs Pitṛyāna (path of the fathers, return); kin to Course After Death (Dim 25).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework / doctrine — the two after-death paths
coordinate.key_relation: Devayāna (path of the gods, no return) vs Pitṛyāna (path of the fathers, return); kin to Course After Death (Dim 25).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Understanding of the two paths motivates choice—pursuing knowledge for liberation (Devayana) rather than just good karma (Pitryana).
facets.somatic: Body engaged in spiritual practice for Devayana.
facets.emotional: Emotional commitment to liberation, not just heavens.
facets.cognitive: Mind oriented toward knowledge for ultimate freedom.
facets.psychic: The soul orients its living toward the path of light, so its whole life becomes a preparation for the journey that does not return.

balanced.text:
Understanding of the two paths motivates choice—pursuing knowledge for liberation (Devayana) rather than just good karma (Pitryana).

Somatic: Body engaged in spiritual practice for Devayana.

Emotional: Emotional commitment to liberation, not just heavens.

Cognitive: Mind oriented toward knowledge for ultimate freedom.

Psychic: The soul orients its living toward the path of light, so its whole life becomes a preparation for the journey that does not return.
balanced.vignette: Contemplating the two paths the soul is said to take after death, Ravi finds his relation to his own end quietly reoriented—toward the bright path of return and freedom, and away from the dim path of mere recurrence. It doesn’t make him morbid; it makes him aim his life higher.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Fearful preoccupation with path after death—missing the point of present transformation.
facets.somatic: Body driven by death-anxiety.
facets.emotional: Emotional life dominated by afterlife concerns.
facets.cognitive: Mental obsession with post-mortem destiny.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on after-death geography, neglecting the present life that actually shapes the path.

excess.text:
Fearful preoccupation with path after death—missing the point of present transformation.

Somatic: Body driven by death-anxiety.

Emotional: Emotional life dominated by afterlife concerns.

Cognitive: Mental obsession with post-mortem destiny.

Psychic: The soul fixates on after-death geography, neglecting the present life that actually shapes the path.
excess.vignette: Sam obsesses over securing the bright path—hoarding merit, calculating his post-mortem itinerary, so focused on the journey beyond that he neglects the one he’s on. The teaching about the two paths has become an anxious spiritual accounting that steals him from the present.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete denial of afterlife continuity—living as if this life is everything, no spiritual urgency.
facets.somatic: Body without spiritual orientation toward liberation.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without depth from death-awareness.
facets.cognitive: Mind never considers post-mortem path.
facets.psychic: The soul gives no thought to its trajectory, living as if this span were the whole and final story.

deficient.text:
Complete denial of afterlife continuity—living as if this life is everything, no spiritual urgency.

Somatic: Body without spiritual orientation toward liberation.

Emotional: Emotional life without depth from death-awareness.

Cognitive: Mind never considers post-mortem path.

Psychic: The soul gives no thought to its trajectory, living as if this span were the whole and final story.
deficient.vignette: For Dan death is a full stop—no bright path, no dim path, no journey of any kind beyond it—and the whole teaching of the two ways is empty superstition. He faces his mortality as sheer annihilation, with no path to orient toward, and the fear of it sits unexamined underneath.

attributes.title: Encompassing the Body (Reversing Identification)
attributes.aspect: Most identify the self as inside the body. The reversal—seeing body as inside the encompassing self—immediately transforms experience. The body becomes light because identification has dropped. Self expands beyond physical boundaries.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / corrective — reversing the superimposition · Valence: Cultivated corrective · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (undoes identification) · Key relation: the antidote to Identification with Body — reversing the body-superimposition.
coordinate.key_relation: the antidote to Identification with Body — reversing the body-superimposition.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When the encompassing experience is realized, the body floats in vast consciousness. Identification dissolves; the body becomes a small center in unlimited awareness.
facets.somatic: Body experiences lightness, no chronic heaviness, sense of expansiveness.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from body-bondage, vastness of feeling.
facets.cognitive: Mind no longer cramped in body-identity, sense of cosmic dimensions.
facets.psychic: The soul reverses the usual error, holding the body within awareness rather than awareness within the body.

balanced.text:
When the encompassing experience is realized, the body floats in vast consciousness. Identification dissolves; the body becomes a small center in unlimited awareness.

Somatic: Body experiences lightness, no chronic heaviness, sense of expansiveness.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from body-bondage, vastness of feeling.

Cognitive: Mind no longer cramped in body-identity, sense of cosmic dimensions.

Psychic: The soul reverses the usual error, holding the body within awareness rather than awareness within the body.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to feel it the other way around—not a tiny self trapped inside a body, but a spacious awareness within which the body appears, like a hand held up in an open sky. The reversal has loosened his claustrophobia; he holds his body now, rather than being held hostage by it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Disembodied dissociation pretending to be encompassing—neglect of body’s signals, ungrounded existence.
facets.somatic: Body neglected, signals ignored, accidents from inattention.
facets.emotional: Emotional disconnection from embodied life.
facets.cognitive: Floating in concept of expansion without genuine realization.
facets.psychic: The soul over-abstracts, treating the body as nothing and neglecting its real needs.

excess.text:
Disembodied dissociation pretending to be encompassing—neglect of body’s signals, ungrounded existence.

Somatic: Body neglected, signals ignored, accidents from inattention.

Emotional: Emotional disconnection from embodied life.

Cognitive: Floating in concept of expansion without genuine realization.

Psychic: The soul over-abstracts, treating the body as nothing and neglecting its real needs.
excess.vignette: Karl has taken ‘I contain the body, it doesn’t contain me’ as license to neglect it—ignoring its hunger, its pain, its need for care, as though the vessel were beneath the awareness that holds it. The freeing reversal has become an excuse for abandonment.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total cramped identification within body—identifying with specific center (head or belly), feeling bounded by skin. Maximum sense of limitation.
facets.somatic: Heavy bodily presence, constant body-anxiety, every sensation magnified.
facets.emotional: Emotional life confined to body-experiences, narrow.
facets.cognitive: Mind cramped in body-identity, suffocating sense of limitation.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly enclosed in the body, taking the flesh as the boundary of itself.

deficient.text:
Total cramped identification within body—identifying with specific center (head or belly), feeling bounded by skin. Maximum sense of limitation.

Somatic: Heavy bodily presence, constant body-anxiety, every sensation magnified.

Emotional: Emotional life confined to body-experiences, narrow.

Cognitive: Mind cramped in body-identity, suffocating sense of limitation.

Psychic: The soul is wholly enclosed in the body, taking the flesh as the boundary of itself.
deficient.vignette: Dan feels himself squarely inside his body—a small self peering out through the eyes, trapped in the flesh, ending firmly at the skin. That awareness might contain the body rather than the reverse is a reversal he’s never once felt; the box, to him, holds him.

attributes.title: Personality as Mask (Persona)
attributes.aspect: Personality is not what we are but the various masks we wear. The original face—the actual nature—exists before personality, beneath it. We have not one personality but many, switching constantly. The original face is faceless.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Framework — a mechanism of bondage (superimposition) · Valence: Affliction-as-veil (to see through) · Guṇa: Tamas–Rajas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism of concealment · Key relation: the persona superimposed on the Self; kin to Asmitā (Dim 19).
coordinate.key_relation: the persona superimposed on the Self; kin to Asmitā (Dim 19).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Awareness of masks while wearing them—not getting trapped in personality. Functions socially with appropriate faces while knowing the faceless nature.
facets.somatic: Body wears expressions appropriately without identification with them.
facets.emotional: Emotional masks worn consciously, freedom from being any one mask.
facets.cognitive: Recognition of the original face beneath all personalities.
facets.psychic: The soul wears its personality as a mask it can set down, knowing the face beneath is not the mask.

balanced.text:
Awareness of masks while wearing them—not getting trapped in personality. Functions socially with appropriate faces while knowing the faceless nature.

Somatic: Body wears expressions appropriately without identification with them.

Emotional: Emotional masks worn consciously, freedom from being any one mask.

Cognitive: Recognition of the original face beneath all personalities.

Psychic: The soul wears its personality as a mask it can set down, knowing the face beneath is not the mask.
balanced.vignette: Meera plays her roles—the professional, the mother, the friend—well and knowingly, aware that each is a useful mask and none is the whole of her. She wears the persona lightly, able to set it down; the face she shows the world isn’t mistaken, by her, for her face.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Total identification with one mask as ‘real me’—rigid persona that resists all other expression. Or pretending to have no masks while still using them.
facets.somatic: Face frozen in one expression, body in single persona.
facets.emotional: One emotional mask becomes prison.
facets.cognitive: Identity completely tied to particular persona.
facets.psychic: The soul polishes and defends the mask obsessively, the persona hardening into a prison.

excess.text:
Total identification with one mask as ‘real me’—rigid persona that resists all other expression. Or pretending to have no masks while still using them.

Somatic: Face frozen in one expression, body in single persona.

Emotional: One emotional mask becomes prison.

Cognitive: Identity completely tied to particular persona.

Psychic: The soul polishes and defends the mask obsessively, the persona hardening into a prison.
excess.vignette: Ben has vanished behind his masks—so identified with his professional persona, his curated image, his roles, that he no longer knows who’s behind them. The functional mask has become a prison; there’s no one home but the performance.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete unconsciousness of being multiple personae—every mask believed as ‘real me’ in that moment. Identity chaos.
facets.somatic: Body’s expressions changing without awareness.
facets.emotional: Emotional identity shifting without recognition.
facets.cognitive: Mind completely identified with whatever mask is current.
facets.psychic: The soul is fused to its mask without remainder, unable to imagine a self beneath the role.

deficient.text:
Complete unconsciousness of being multiple personae—every mask believed as ‘real me’ in that moment. Identity chaos.

Somatic: Body’s expressions changing without awareness.

Emotional: Emotional identity shifting without recognition.

Cognitive: Mind completely identified with whatever mask is current.

Psychic: The soul is fused to its mask without remainder, unable to imagine a self beneath the role.
deficient.vignette: Dan can’t hold any social face at all—no functional persona, blurting whatever, unable to meet the ordinary conventions that let people connect. The healthy mask that makes social life workable never formed, and his rawness leaves everyone, including him, adrift.

attributes.title: World as Dream (Reality of Projection)
attributes.aspect: The world appears real only because we are not present to ourselves. In dream, we are absent; therefore dream becomes real. In life, similarly absent, the world becomes solid. Self-remembering—the practice of ‘I am’—dissolves both world-and-dream illusion.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a mechanism of bondage (superimposition) · Valence: Affliction-as-veil (to see through) · Guṇa: Tamas–Rajas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism of concealment · Key relation: the projected world taken as independently real; kin to Māyā (Dim 28) and mithyā (Dim 27).
coordinate.key_relation: the projected world taken as independently real; kin to Māyā (Dim 28) and mithyā (Dim 27).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Self-remembering active—‘I am’ present in all activities. The world is recognized as dream-like, not real-versus-illusion but witnessed transparency.
facets.somatic: Body present in self-remembering, vibrant aliveness.
facets.emotional: Emotional life seen as dream-movements while self remembers.
facets.cognitive: Mind always rooted in ‘I am’ while perceiving the world-show.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the experienced world as a kind of waking dream projected by mind, and so holds it more lightly.

balanced.text:
Self-remembering active—‘I am’ present in all activities. The world is recognized as dream-like, not real-versus-illusion but witnessed transparency.

Somatic: Body present in self-remembering, vibrant aliveness.

Emotional: Emotional life seen as dream-movements while self remembers.

Cognitive: Mind always rooted in ‘I am’ while perceiving the world-show.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the experienced world as a kind of waking dream projected by mind, and so holds it more lightly.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to hold the world a little like a vivid dream—real enough to engage fully, insubstantial enough not to be devastated by. The dream-like seeing doesn’t make him careless; it makes him free, gripping his circumstances less and meeting them more.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Nihilistic denial of world reality—‘all is illusion, nothing matters.’ Spiritual escapism mistaken for realization.
facets.somatic: Body neglected as ‘illusion.’
facets.emotional: Emotional engagement avoided as ‘unreal.’
facets.cognitive: World-denial without authentic self-remembering.
facets.psychic: The soul uses ‘it’s all a dream’ to derealize life and dodge its claims.

excess.text:
Nihilistic denial of world reality—‘all is illusion, nothing matters.’ Spiritual escapism mistaken for realization.

Somatic: Body neglected as ‘illusion.’

Emotional: Emotional engagement avoided as ‘unreal.’

Cognitive: World-denial without authentic self-remembering.

Psychic: The soul uses ‘it’s all a dream’ to derealize life and dodge its claims.
excess.vignette: Karl dismisses everything as ‘just a projection, not real’—his responsibilities, others’ pain, the consequences of his acts all waved away as dream-stuff. The insight into the world’s dream-like nature has become a derealizing escape from a life still very much happening.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete self-forgetfulness—world taken as solidly real because we are absent to ourselves. Maximum bondage.
facets.somatic: Body experiences taken as undeniable reality.
facets.emotional: Emotions taken as ultimate facts.
facets.cognitive: Mind contents taken as solid reality, no ‘I am’ presence.
facets.psychic: The soul takes the projected world as solid, final fact, never suspecting its dream-like nature.

deficient.text:
Complete self-forgetfulness—world taken as solidly real because we are absent to ourselves. Maximum bondage.

Somatic: Body experiences taken as undeniable reality.

Emotional: Emotions taken as ultimate facts.

Cognitive: Mind contents taken as solid reality, no ‘I am’ presence.

Psychic: The soul takes the projected world as solid, final fact, never suspecting its dream-like nature.
deficient.vignette: For Dan the world is utterly, crushingly solid—every event final, every loss permanent, no give in it anywhere. The dream-like, projected quality the teaching describes is foreign to him; he takes the whole heavy show as bedrock, and suffers accordingly.

attributes.title: How Senses Fool Us (Indriya-Bhrama)
attributes.aspect: The senses systematically deceive—presenting partial appearances as complete realities, ephemeral attractions as enduring goods, separate entities where there is connection. Understanding this deception is the prerequisite for liberation from sense-bondage.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Mental · Ontic mode: Framework — a mechanism of bondage (superimposition) · Valence: Affliction-as-veil (to see through) · Guṇa: Tamas–Rajas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism of concealment · Key relation: the senses’ misreporting (indriya-bhrama); kin to Viparyaya (Dim 20).
coordinate.key_relation: the senses’ misreporting (indriya-bhrama); kin to Viparyaya (Dim 20).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Aware participation with senses—using them while recognizing their inherent limitations and deceptions.
facets.somatic: Body’s senses used with awareness of their partial nature.
facets.emotional: Emotional life informed by sense-data but not enslaved to it.
facets.cognitive: Mind recognizes sense-deception, uses senses without complete trust.
facets.psychic: The soul knows the senses report a partial, distorted world, and trusts them without being ruled by them.

balanced.text:
Aware participation with senses—using them while recognizing their inherent limitations and deceptions.

Somatic: Body’s senses used with awareness of their partial nature.

Emotional: Emotional life informed by sense-data but not enslaved to it.

Cognitive: Mind recognizes sense-deception, uses senses without complete trust.

Psychic: The soul knows the senses report a partial, distorted world, and trusts them without being ruled by them.
balanced.vignette: Ravi knows his senses can fool him—the stick bent in water, the fear that isn’t there, the assumption that misreads a face—and holds his perceptions with a light, checking hand. Aware the instruments distort, he double-checks, and is fooled less.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Total sense-rejection—refusing all sensory engagement, ascetic damage.
facets.somatic: Body’s senses atrophied through forced denial.
facets.emotional: Emotional life cut off from sensory richness.
facets.cognitive: Mental rejection of all sensory information.
facets.psychic: The soul grows paranoid about all perception, paralyzed by distrust of every appearance.

excess.text:
Total sense-rejection—refusing all sensory engagement, ascetic damage.

Somatic: Body’s senses atrophied through forced denial.

Emotional: Emotional life cut off from sensory richness.

Cognitive: Mental rejection of all sensory information.

Psychic: The soul grows paranoid about all perception, paralyzed by distrust of every appearance.
excess.vignette: Sam has grown paranoid about perception—trusting nothing his senses report, doubting the plainest facts, so convinced everything’s an illusion that he can’t act on anything. The healthy awareness that senses can deceive has swollen into a corrosive distrust of all experience.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete trust in senses—sense-impressions taken as ultimate reality. Maximum delusion.
facets.somatic: Body driven by every sense-stimulation.
facets.emotional: Emotions ruled by sensory inputs.
facets.cognitive: Mind takes all sense-presentations as true reality.
facets.psychic: The soul takes sense-reports as simple truth, deceived by every illusion they present.

deficient.text:
Complete trust in senses—sense-impressions taken as ultimate reality. Maximum delusion.

Somatic: Body driven by every sense-stimulation.

Emotional: Emotions ruled by sensory inputs.

Cognitive: Mind takes all sense-presentations as true reality.

Psychic: The soul takes sense-reports as simple truth, deceived by every illusion they present.
deficient.vignette: Dan trusts his senses absolutely—if he saw it, felt it, heard it, it’s exactly so—with no suspicion that perception distorts. He’s fooled constantly by the obvious tricks the mind plays, and never once suspects the instrument might be lying.

attributes.title: Disentanglement of Personality (Vyutthana)
attributes.aspect: What we call ‘personality’ is a complex tangle of identifications, roles, conditioned patterns accumulated over a lifetime (and previous lives). Liberation is the patient disentanglement of these threads—not destruction but unweaving. Each thread released frees the underlying Self.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice / corrective — reversing the superimposition · Valence: Cultivated corrective · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (undoes identification) · Key relation: the un-knotting of identification (vyutthāna) — a liberating move.
coordinate.key_relation: the un-knotting of identification (vyutthāna) — a liberating move.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Patient ongoing disentanglement of false identifications. Each strand released brings more spaciousness.
facets.somatic: Body progressively disentangled from identity-postures.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns recognized and untangled one by one.
facets.cognitive: Mental identifications systematically loosened.
facets.psychic: The soul steps back from its conditioned personality, disentangling awareness from the character it had taken itself to be.

balanced.text:
Patient ongoing disentanglement of false identifications. Each strand released brings more spaciousness.

Somatic: Body progressively disentangled from identity-postures.

Emotional: Emotional patterns recognized and untangled one by one.

Cognitive: Mental identifications systematically loosened.

Psychic: The soul steps back from its conditioned personality, disentangling awareness from the character it had taken itself to be.
balanced.vignette: When his moods and roles threaten to swallow him, Ravi can step back—disentangling the awareness from the personality it was fused with—and watch the storm rather than being it. The capacity to un-stick from his own character has become his quiet freedom.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Violent attempt to destroy personality—creating psychological harm through forced dissolution.
facets.somatic: Body damaged by forced identity-rejection.
facets.emotional: Emotional breakdown from forced disentanglement.
facets.cognitive: Mental dissolution without integration.
facets.psychic: The soul forces the disentanglement coldly, dissociating from its humanity rather than freeing itself within it.

excess.text:
Violent attempt to destroy personality—creating psychological harm through forced dissolution.

Somatic: Body damaged by forced identity-rejection.

Emotional: Emotional breakdown from forced disentanglement.

Cognitive: Mental dissolution without integration.

Psychic: The soul forces the disentanglement coldly, dissociating from its humanity rather than freeing itself within it.
excess.vignette: Sam disentangles from his personality so habitually that he’s gone dissociated—floating above his own feelings and relationships, detached to the point of numbness, calling the estrangement freedom. The healthy stepping-back has become a permanent, cold exile from himself.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total identification with personality as final self. No disentanglement attempted.
facets.somatic: Body completely fused with role-identity.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns taken as ‘who I am.’
facets.cognitive: Mind identified completely with personality-constructs.
facets.psychic: The soul stays wholly tangled in its personality, every conditioned trait felt as bedrock identity.

deficient.text:
Total identification with personality as final self. No disentanglement attempted.

Somatic: Body completely fused with role-identity.

Emotional: Emotional patterns taken as ‘who I am.’

Cognitive: Mind identified completely with personality-constructs.

Psychic: The soul stays wholly tangled in its personality, every conditioned trait felt as bedrock identity.
deficient.vignette: Dan is wholly fused with his personality—every mood total, every reaction him, no distance anywhere from which to observe himself. The capacity to disentangle awareness from character is one he’s never exercised; he is his conditioning, seamlessly.

attributes.title: Identification with Body
attributes.aspect: Identification with the body is the fundamental confusion. We feel embodied; really, the body is contained in consciousness. This reversal of perspective destroys the bondage of body-identification.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Framework — a mechanism of bondage (superimposition) · Valence: Affliction-as-veil (to see through) · Guṇa: Tamas–Rajas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism of concealment · Key relation: the primal misidentification (dehātma-buddhi); the root superimposition; kin to Deha-vāsanā (Dim 12).
coordinate.key_relation: the primal misidentification (dehātma-buddhi); the root superimposition; kin to Deha-vāsanā (Dim 12).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When body is known as instrument within consciousness (not consciousness within body), it becomes light. One is the encompassing space; body is just a minor center.
facets.somatic: Body felt as light, weightless, instrument-like, healthy and free.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from body-anxieties, no constant body-concern, peace.
facets.cognitive: Mind no longer identified with body changes, witness of body, freedom.
facets.psychic: The soul holds the body as instrument and home without mistaking it for the self that uses it.

balanced.text:
When body is known as instrument within consciousness (not consciousness within body), it becomes light. One is the encompassing space; body is just a minor center.

Somatic: Body felt as light, weightless, instrument-like, healthy and free.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from body-anxieties, no constant body-concern, peace.

Cognitive: Mind no longer identified with body changes, witness of body, freedom.

Psychic: The soul holds the body as instrument and home without mistaking it for the self that uses it.
balanced.vignette: Kira inhabits her body gladly and isn’t imprisoned by it—caring for it, enjoying it, and sensing that she is more than the sum of its sensations. She’s neither fused with the flesh nor fleeing it; the body is a good home she lives in without mistaking it for the whole of herself.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Total identification with body—‘I am this face, this age, this gender, this health.’ Every body-change is felt as identity-change, causing constant anxiety.
facets.somatic: Body felt as heavy, every sensation overwhelming, hypochondria, chronic stress.
facets.emotional: Emotional crisis at every body-change (aging, illness, weight), narcissism or self-hatred.
facets.cognitive: Mental life dominated by body-concerns, identity tied to physical appearance and condition.
facets.psychic: The soul over-attends to the body, its whole identity invested in form, appearance, and sensation.

excess.text:
Total identification with body—‘I am this face, this age, this gender, this health.’ Every body-change is felt as identity-change, causing constant anxiety.

Somatic: Body felt as heavy, every sensation overwhelming, hypochondria, chronic stress.

Emotional: Emotional crisis at every body-change (aging, illness, weight), narcissism or self-hatred.

Cognitive: Mental life dominated by body-concerns, identity tied to physical appearance and condition.

Psychic: The soul over-attends to the body, its whole identity invested in form, appearance, and sensation.
