excess.vignette: For Tara the body is the whole self—its looks, its aches, its aging her total identity—so that a wrinkle is an existential threat and the mirror rules her mood. Wholly identified with the flesh, she rides its every fluctuation as if her very being were at stake.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Forced dis-identification from body becomes denial—neglect of bodily needs, ascetic damage, dissociation that is not real transcendence.
facets.somatic: Body neglect, malnourishment, ascetic damage, dissociative symptoms.
facets.emotional: Emotional disconnection from embodied life, denial of legitimate physical pleasures.
facets.cognitive: Forced detachment from body without realization, spiritual bypassing of physical reality.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly identified with the body, and every bodily change feels like a change in its very being.

deficient.text:
Forced dis-identification from body becomes denial—neglect of bodily needs, ascetic damage, dissociation that is not real transcendence.

Somatic: Body neglect, malnourishment, ascetic damage, dissociative symptoms.

Emotional: Emotional disconnection from embodied life, denial of legitimate physical pleasures.

Cognitive: Forced detachment from body without realization, spiritual bypassing of physical reality.

Psychic: The soul is wholly identified with the body, and every bodily change feels like a change in its very being.
deficient.vignette: Michael treats his body as a nuisance carting the important part around, dissociated from its signals, numb to its needs. Fled from rather than fused with, the neglected body breaks down unheard, and he’s genuinely surprised each time, having forgotten he lives in it.

attributes.title: Hypnotic Projection (Maya as Auto-Hypnosis)
attributes.aspect: Maya is not external illusion but our own hypnotic projection. We project beauty, ugliness, value, meaning—then take our projections as reality. The world we perceive is largely our own creation, and we suffer or enjoy according to our projections.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a mechanism of bondage (superimposition) · Valence: Affliction-as-veil (to see through) · Guṇa: Tamas–Rajas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism of concealment · Key relation: māyā as auto-hypnosis (bahir-mukha projection); kin to Māyā (Dim 28).
coordinate.key_relation: māyā as auto-hypnosis (bahir-mukha projection); kin to Māyā (Dim 28).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When projection is recognized, reality is seen as it is, free of overlay. The world is not denied but seen without the auto-hypnotic distortion.
facets.somatic: Body responds to actual reality, not projections.
facets.emotional: Emotional life based on what is, not on hypnotic overlays.
facets.cognitive: Mind clear of projection, seeing things in their nature without imposed meaning.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes that it has, in effect, hypnotized itself into a limited identity, and begins the counter-suggestion of truth.

balanced.text:
When projection is recognized, reality is seen as it is, free of overlay. The world is not denied but seen without the auto-hypnotic distortion.

Somatic: Body responds to actual reality, not projections.

Emotional: Emotional life based on what is, not on hypnotic overlays.

Cognitive: Mind clear of projection, seeing things in their nature without imposed meaning.

Psychic: The soul recognizes that it has, in effect, hypnotized itself into a limited identity, and begins the counter-suggestion of truth.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to notice the trance—the way his habitual perceptions and assumptions run like a self-hypnosis he’d never questioned—and, noticing, to wake a little from it. The seeing itself loosens the spell; he catches the projection mid-air more often now.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Hypnosis to the opposite extreme—Hindu hypnotizing as ‘all is spiritual,’ Western as ‘all is material.’ Just trading one projection for another, not freedom.
facets.somatic: Body still in hypnotic patterns of new conditioning.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactions to new projections (now spiritual ones).
facets.cognitive: Trading one mental conditioning for another, no real freedom.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on ‘it’s all hypnosis,’ using the idea to evade rather than to wake.

excess.text:
Hypnosis to the opposite extreme—Hindu hypnotizing as ‘all is spiritual,’ Western as ‘all is material.’ Just trading one projection for another, not freedom.

Somatic: Body still in hypnotic patterns of new conditioning.

Emotional: Emotional reactions to new projections (now spiritual ones).

Cognitive: Trading one mental conditioning for another, no real freedom.

Psychic: The soul fixates on ‘it’s all hypnosis,’ using the idea to evade rather than to wake.
excess.vignette: Karl uses ‘it’s all self-hypnosis, all Maya’ to dismiss everything real—deepening a detached derealization where nothing matters because nothing’s ‘really’ happening. The insight into the trance has become a trance of its own, a spell of unreality he’s cast on his whole life.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete unconsciousness of projection—everything experienced is taken as objective reality. Maximum suffering from believing one’s own projections.
facets.somatic: Body shaped by unconscious projections, somatic effects.
facets.emotional: Suffering from love-projections, hate-projections, value-projections.
facets.cognitive: Mind completely identified with its projections, no awareness of the mechanism.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly entranced, taking the self-induced spell of separateness as plain reality.

deficient.text:
Complete unconsciousness of projection—everything experienced is taken as objective reality. Maximum suffering from believing one’s own projections.

Somatic: Body shaped by unconscious projections, somatic effects.

Emotional: Suffering from love-projections, hate-projections, value-projections.

Cognitive: Mind completely identified with its projections, no awareness of the mechanism.

Psychic: The soul is wholly entranced, taking the self-induced spell of separateness as plain reality.
deficient.vignette: Dan is wholly inside the trance—his projections taken as bare reality, his assumptions as facts, his habitual seeing never once suspected. That perception might be a kind of self-hypnosis, and could be woken from, is a possibility that’s never dawned on him.

attributes.title: Externalization of Consciousness (Bahir-Mukha)
attributes.aspect: Consciousness has a tendency to externalise—to gaze outward, to become object. As this externalisation deepens, consciousness becomes more dense, more ‘material.’ What we call matter is just consciousness extremely externalised. Liberation is internalisation—the reverse current.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Framework — a mechanism of bondage (superimposition) · Valence: Affliction-as-veil (to see through) · Guṇa: Tamas–Rajas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism of concealment · Key relation: consciousness turned outward (bahir-mukha); antagonist to the inward turn (Dim 28).
coordinate.key_relation: consciousness turned outward (bahir-mukha); antagonist to the inward turn (Dim 28).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Awareness of externalisation as the universal mechanism. Conscious reversal of the current through interiorization.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as densest externalisation, its ‘solidity’ seen through.
facets.emotional: Emotional outward-flow recognized; deliberately reversed.
facets.cognitive: Mind aware of its outward tendency; cultivates inward turn.
facets.psychic: The soul notices its habit of pouring awareness outward, and learns to turn the current back toward its source.

balanced.text:
Awareness of externalisation as the universal mechanism. Conscious reversal of the current through interiorization.

Somatic: Body recognized as densest externalisation, its ‘solidity’ seen through.

Emotional: Emotional outward-flow recognized; deliberately reversed.

Cognitive: Mind aware of its outward tendency; cultivates inward turn.

Psychic: The soul notices its habit of pouring awareness outward, and learns to turn the current back toward its source.
balanced.vignette: Ravi can turn his attention inward at will—back from the endless outward pull of objects and tasks toward the awareness that’s aware of them. Not lost in the outer show, he balances the outward and the inward, and returns to the source when he chooses.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced interiorization rejecting all outward engagement—becoming dissociated from material reality.
facets.somatic: Body neglected through forced interiorization.
facets.emotional: Emotional withdrawal mistaken for transcendence.
facets.cognitive: Mental dissociation from outward reality.
facets.psychic: The soul over-corrects into a forced inwardness that withdraws from necessary engagement.

excess.text:
Forced interiorization rejecting all outward engagement—becoming dissociated from material reality.

Somatic: Body neglected through forced interiorization.

Emotional: Emotional withdrawal mistaken for transcendence.

Cognitive: Mental dissociation from outward reality.

Psychic: The soul over-corrects into a forced inwardness that withdraws from necessary engagement.
excess.vignette: Ben’s consciousness lives entirely outside itself—flung into objects, achievements, the next stimulus, never once turning back on the awareness doing the looking. Wholly externalized, he’s a searchlight that has forgotten it’s a light, chasing everything and finding no rest.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete unconscious externalisation—consciousness entirely lost in objects, no return current.
facets.somatic: Body fully captured in material engagement.
facets.emotional: Emotional life entirely externalised.
facets.cognitive: Mind locked in outward-looking, no return possible.
facets.psychic: The soul’s awareness streams entirely outward, never once turning to face itself.

deficient.text:
Complete unconscious externalisation—consciousness entirely lost in objects, no return current.

Somatic: Body fully captured in material engagement.

Emotional: Emotional life entirely externalised.

Cognitive: Mind locked in outward-looking, no return possible.

Psychic: The soul’s awareness streams entirely outward, never once turning to face itself.
deficient.vignette: Sam has turned so far inward that the outer world has gone dim and unmet—withdrawn into his own interior, neglecting the life and people outside him. Over-internalized, he’s mistaken a dissociative retreat for depth, and the balance the teaching urges tips the wrong way.

attributes.title: Adhyāsa — Superimposition, the Root of Bondage
attributes.aspect: Adhyāsa is the superimposition of the not-self upon the Self — the foundational confusion by which the limitless witnessing awareness comes to experience itself as a limited body-mind. Śaṅkara opens his great commentary with it, because it is the single error from which all bondage flows: like mistaking a rope for a snake in dim light, the mind lays the attributes of the perishable onto the imperishable and then lives in the fear and craving appropriate to a perishable thing. Liberation is, precisely, the un-doing of this superimposition.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Framework — the root mechanism (adhyāsa) · Valence: Affliction-as-veil (the root to see through) · Guṇa: Tamas-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Root mechanism of concealment · Key relation: Whole / root — superimposition itself, ground of all the bondage mechanisms; kin to Avidyā (Dim 19).
coordinate.key_relation: Whole / root — superimposition itself, ground of all the bondage mechanisms; kin to Avidyā (Dim 19).

balanced.label: Balanced — the superimposition recognized and seen through

facets.overview: Recognition of adhyasa-mechanism. The rope-snake confusion is seen through; Self is recognized as Self.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as not-Self though still functioning.
facets.emotional: Emotions recognized as not-Self though still felt.
facets.cognitive: Mind’s contents recognized as not-Self though still operating.
facets.psychic: The soul begins to catch the basic error in the act — the superimposing of self onto not-self — and each catching loosens the knot at the root.

balanced.text:
Recognition of adhyasa-mechanism. The rope-snake confusion is seen through; Self is recognized as Self.

Somatic: Body recognized as not-Self though still functioning.

Emotional: Emotions recognized as not-Self though still felt.

Cognitive: Mind’s contents recognized as not-Self though still operating.

Psychic: The soul begins to catch the basic error in the act — the superimposing of self onto not-self — and each catching loosens the knot at the root.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to catch the superimposition in the act—the way he lays ‘I’ and ‘mine’ over the body, the roles, the passing states that aren’t the Self at all. Seeing the root error clearly is what loosens it; he still says ‘I,’ but with a lighter, truer grip.

excess.label: In excess — a merely intellectual ‘seeing through’ used to bypass real life

facets.overview: Intellectual claim of seeing through adhyasa without actual realization. Verbal Advaita.
facets.somatic: Body unchanged despite intellectual claims.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity continues despite doctrine.
facets.cognitive: Concept of non-superimposition without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul over-intellectualizes the doctrine, analyzing ‘superimposition’ endlessly while still living entirely inside it.

excess.text:
Intellectual claim of seeing through adhyasa without actual realization. Verbal Advaita.

Somatic: Body unchanged despite intellectual claims.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity continues despite doctrine.

Cognitive: Concept of non-superimposition without realization.

Psychic: The soul over-intellectualizes the doctrine, analyzing ‘superimposition’ endlessly while still living entirely inside it.
excess.vignette: For Sam the superimposition is total and unquestioned—he IS his body, his story, his possessions, his moods, the false ‘I’ pasted seamlessly over all of it. The root of bondage runs at full strength in him, invisible, and every suffering it generates lands as simple reality.

deficient.label: Deficient — total, unconscious identification; the rope wholly taken for a snake

facets.overview: Total superimposition unrecognized. All identification taken as absolute. Maximum bondage.
facets.somatic: Body taken as ultimate self.
facets.emotional: Emotions taken as final reality.
facets.cognitive: Mind taken as ultimate, no awareness of error.
facets.psychic: Superimposition is total and unrecognized; the soul takes every identification — I am this body, this role, this story — as simply given, and bondage is complete.

deficient.text:
Total superimposition unrecognized. All identification taken as absolute. Maximum bondage.

Somatic: Body taken as ultimate self.

Emotional: Emotions taken as final reality.

Cognitive: Mind taken as ultimate, no awareness of error.

Psychic: Superimposition is total and unrecognized; the soul takes every identification — I am this body, this role, this story — as simply given, and bondage is complete.
deficient.vignette: So intent on ‘I am not the body, not the mind,’ Karl has over-negated into neglect—refusing even the legitimate use of the instruments he still, inconveniently, depends on. The discernment meant to free him has tipped into a denial of the very equipment his life requires.

attributes.title: Vasanas as Dream-Stuff (No Need to Remove)
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra’s radical teaching on vasanas: the entire process of removing vasanas (latent tendencies) is unnecessary. Why? Because the vasanas, like the body-mind, are themselves part of the dream. No vasanas ever existed in Reality—why beat a snake that was always just a rope? Most traditions emphasize vasana-kshaya; Ashtavakra says: skip it through direct knowledge.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Framework — a structure of bondage · Valence: Affliction (the binding structure) · Guṇa: Tamas–Rajas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Structure of bondage · Key relation: the vāsanās (Dim 12) seen as insubstantial dream-stuff — to see through, not fight.
coordinate.key_relation: the vāsanās (Dim 12) seen as insubstantial dream-stuff — to see through, not fight.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Direct recognition that vasanas are themselves dream-stuff. No effort to remove what was never there.
facets.somatic: Body lived without anti-vasana striving.
facets.emotional: Emotional life accepted as appearance.
facets.cognitive: Mind released from endless vasana-management.
facets.psychic: The soul sees its tendencies as insubstantial dream-stuff that loses its grip the moment it is seen as such.

balanced.text:
Direct recognition that vasanas are themselves dream-stuff. No effort to remove what was never there.

Somatic: Body lived without anti-vasana striving.

Emotional: Emotional life accepted as appearance.

Cognitive: Mind released from endless vasana-management.

Psychic: The soul sees its tendencies as insubstantial dream-stuff that loses its grip the moment it is seen as such.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has stopped waging war on his tendencies and started seeing them as dream-stuff—arising in awareness, insubstantial, needing to be seen through rather than fought. The shift has eased his struggle; what he once battled, he now simply watches dissolve.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Using doctrine to ignore real psychological work. ‘It’s all maya’ as bypass.
facets.somatic: Body driven by unprocessed patterns.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity continuing under doctrinal cover.
facets.cognitive: Mental dismissal of legitimate psychological work.
facets.psychic: The soul obsesses over ‘removing’ vāsanās, fighting shadows and deepening the very fixation.

excess.text:
Using doctrine to ignore real psychological work. ‘It’s all maya’ as bypass.

Somatic: Body driven by unprocessed patterns.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity continuing under doctrinal cover.

Cognitive: Mental dismissal of legitimate psychological work.

Psychic: The soul obsesses over ‘removing’ vāsanās, fighting shadows and deepening the very fixation.
excess.vignette: Sam has taken ‘no need to remove the vasanas, they’re just dream-stuff’ as license to indulge every one—the craving, the compulsion, all excused as ‘insubstantial appearances’ he needn’t resist. The subtle teaching has become a permission slip for his every impulse.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Endless lifetime spent removing vasanas one by one. Never reaching direct recognition.
facets.somatic: Body in perpetual purification practices.
facets.emotional: Emotional life as constant vasana-management.
facets.cognitive: Mind treating every tendency as something to fight.
facets.psychic: The soul takes its tendencies as solid, permanent traits, and is bound by what it could simply see through.

deficient.text:
Endless lifetime spent removing vasanas one by one. Never reaching direct recognition.

Somatic: Body in perpetual purification practices.

Emotional: Emotional life as constant vasana-management.

Cognitive: Mind treating every tendency as something to fight.

Psychic: The soul takes its tendencies as solid, permanent traits, and is bound by what it could simply see through.
deficient.vignette: Dan grimly battles his tendencies as solid enemies—wrestling each craving, fighting each pattern head-on, exhausted by a war he keeps losing. That the vasanas might be dream-stuff, dissolved by seeing rather than fighting, is an insight he’s never touched; he only knows the struggle.

attributes.title: Vasana (Tendencies/Impressions)
attributes.aspect: Vasana refers to deep mental tendencies, conditioned patterns, and karmic impressions in the unconscious mind. They are the subtle drives that motivate behavior even when not consciously recognized.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Faculty / latent tendency (vāsanā) · Valence: Faculty tending to affliction · Guṇa: Rajas–Tamas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Latent cause / structure of bondage · Key relation: Same as the Vāsanās (Dim 12).
coordinate.key_relation: Same as the Vāsanās (Dim 12).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Vasanas are purified and understood, they lose their unconscious power. A person acts with awareness rather than automaticity and can transform conditioned patterns.
facets.somatic: Physical freedom and lack of compulsion, free movement and natural behavior, lack of nervous habits and compulsions, body expresses awareness and choice.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom and spontaneity, conscious choice in emotional response, freedom from compulsive emotional patterns, emotional maturity and wisdom, ability to respond rather than react.
facets.cognitive: Conscious awareness of patterns, ability to observe tendencies without being controlled, mental freedom and autonomy, wisdom about one’s conditioning, ability to choose new patterns.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes its driving impressions as conditioned grooves it can gradually soften, not commands to obey.

balanced.text:
When Vasanas are purified and understood, they lose their unconscious power. A person acts with awareness rather than automaticity and can transform conditioned patterns.

Somatic: Physical freedom and lack of compulsion, free movement and natural behavior, lack of nervous habits and compulsions, body expresses awareness and choice.

Emotional: Emotional freedom and spontaneity, conscious choice in emotional response, freedom from compulsive emotional patterns, emotional maturity and wisdom, ability to respond rather than react.

Cognitive: Conscious awareness of patterns, ability to observe tendencies without being controlled, mental freedom and autonomy, wisdom about one’s conditioning, ability to choose new patterns.

Psychic: The soul recognizes its driving impressions as conditioned grooves it can gradually soften, not commands to obey.
balanced.vignette: Ravi can feel the old tendencies stirring—the reflex to grab, to flee, to prove—and, sensing them, chooses not to be carried. Aware of his impressions as impressions, he’s begun to loosen grooves that once ran him without his consent.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: When Vasanas are strong and unconscious, they create compulsive behavior, addiction, repetitive patterns, and a sense of being controlled by unseen forces.
facets.somatic: Compulsive physical habits and behaviors, nervous tics and repetitive movements, addictive behaviors and substance use, physical automaticity without consciousness.
facets.emotional: Compulsive emotional patterns and reactivity, emotional overwhelm from unconscious triggers, cycles of emotional patterns, feeling like victim of emotions, inability to break emotional patterns.
facets.cognitive: Compulsive thinking patterns and obsessions, feeling driven by unseen forces, repetitive thought cycles, inability to break mental patterns, unconscious motivation and behavior.
facets.psychic: The soul indulges its tendencies as ‘just who I am,’ deepening the bondage.

excess.text:
When Vasanas are strong and unconscious, they create compulsive behavior, addiction, repetitive patterns, and a sense of being controlled by unseen forces.

Somatic: Compulsive physical habits and behaviors, nervous tics and repetitive movements, addictive behaviors and substance use, physical automaticity without consciousness.

Emotional: Compulsive emotional patterns and reactivity, emotional overwhelm from unconscious triggers, cycles of emotional patterns, feeling like victim of emotions, inability to break emotional patterns.

Cognitive: Compulsive thinking patterns and obsessions, feeling driven by unseen forces, repetitive thought cycles, inability to break mental patterns, unconscious motivation and behavior.

Psychic: The soul indulges its tendencies as ‘just who I am,’ deepening the bondage.
excess.vignette: Sam has made a project of his tendencies—cataloguing every impression, analyzing every latent groove, so absorbed in mapping his conditioning that the mapping has become one more compulsion. The awareness meant to free him has become an anxious inventory.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Suppressing awareness of Vasanas leads to unconscious projection and loss of self-knowledge. Some tendency is natural while alive.
facets.somatic: Suppressed physical tendencies seeking outlet, nervous system dysregulation, physical symptoms from repression, potential for explosion or breakdown.
facets.emotional: Suppressed emotions and tendencies creating psychological pressure, projection of tendencies onto others, inability to understand self, emotional numbness combined with eruptions.
facets.cognitive: Unconscious tendencies controlling behavior unbeknownst to person, lack of self-knowledge, unconscious projection, inability to grow or change without awareness.
facets.psychic: The soul is run by unseen impressions it never questions, calling their pull its own will.

deficient.text:
Suppressing awareness of Vasanas leads to unconscious projection and loss of self-knowledge. Some tendency is natural while alive.

Somatic: Suppressed physical tendencies seeking outlet, nervous system dysregulation, physical symptoms from repression, potential for explosion or breakdown.

Emotional: Suppressed emotions and tendencies creating psychological pressure, projection of tendencies onto others, inability to understand self, emotional numbness combined with eruptions.

Cognitive: Unconscious tendencies controlling behavior unbeknownst to person, lack of self-knowledge, unconscious projection, inability to grow or change without awareness.

Psychic: The soul is run by unseen impressions it never questions, calling their pull its own will.
deficient.vignette: Dan is driven entirely by tendencies he can’t see—the same reflexes firing unbidden, the same grooves walked daily, each mistaken for a free choice. That latent impressions shape him at all is invisible to him, and so they steer him, unopposed and unsuspected.

attributes.title: Graha-Atigraha (Grasper and Grasped Bondage)
attributes.aspect: Bondage is the mutual capture between senses (grahas) and objects (atigrahas). The eye grasps form; form also grasps the eye. Mind grasps thought; thought also grasps the mind. Eight pairs of grasper-grasped: prana-apana (smell), speech-name, eye-form, ear-sound, tongue-taste, mind-desire, hand-action, skin-touch. Liberation comes from breaking this mutual capture.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Framework — a structure of bondage · Valence: Affliction (the binding structure) · Guṇa: Tamas–Rajas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Structure of bondage · Key relation: the grasper / grasped bondage — subject gripping object; kin to Rāga and Dveṣa (Dim 19).
coordinate.key_relation: the grasper / grasped bondage — subject gripping object; kin to Rāga and Dveṣa (Dim 19).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Awareness of the mutual capture mechanism. Senses engaged consciously without being captured; objects encountered without grasping.
facets.somatic: Senses functioning without being captured by their objects.
facets.emotional: Emotional encounters with objects without bondage.
facets.cognitive: Mind aware of grasper-grasped dynamic, not enslaved to it.
facets.psychic: The soul sees how grasper and grasped bind each other, and loosens its grip on the objects that grip it back.

balanced.text:
Awareness of the mutual capture mechanism. Senses engaged consciously without being captured; objects encountered without grasping.

Somatic: Senses functioning without being captured by their objects.

Emotional: Emotional encounters with objects without bondage.

Cognitive: Mind aware of grasper-grasped dynamic, not enslaved to it.

Psychic: The soul sees how grasper and grasped bind each other, and loosens its grip on the objects that grip it back.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to notice the whole grasping machinery—the grasper reaching, the grasped resisting, the endless clutch and counter-clutch that is bondage itself—and, noticing, to relax his grip a little. Seeing the grasping is the beginning of releasing it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced sense-withdrawal as the only solution—missing that awareness, not avoidance, liberates.
facets.somatic: Body in forced ascetic withdrawal from all sense-objects.
facets.emotional: Emotional rejection of natural sense engagement.
facets.cognitive: Mental violence against grasping rather than understanding.
facets.psychic: The soul grasps ever harder, multiplying the very attachments that hold it.

excess.text:
Forced sense-withdrawal as the only solution—missing that awareness, not avoidance, liberates.

Somatic: Body in forced ascetic withdrawal from all sense-objects.

Emotional: Emotional rejection of natural sense engagement.

Cognitive: Mental violence against grasping rather than understanding.

Psychic: The soul grasps ever harder, multiplying the very attachments that hold it.
excess.vignette: Sam’s whole life is grasping—clutching at pleasures, at people, at outcomes, at his own spiritual progress—the grasper and the grasped locked in a grip that tightens the harder he pulls. He grasps even at non-grasping, and the machinery runs him ragged.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete unconsciousness of grasper-grasped capture. Maximum bondage through both directions.
facets.somatic: Body captured by every object it encounters.
facets.emotional: Emotional life as constant capture by objects.
facets.cognitive: Mind in eight-fold simultaneous bondage.
facets.psychic: The soul does not even see the grasping, taking its bondage to objects as natural living.

deficient.text:
Complete unconsciousness of grasper-grasped capture. Maximum bondage through both directions.

Somatic: Body captured by every object it encounters.

Emotional: Emotional life as constant capture by objects.

Cognitive: Mind in eight-fold simultaneous bondage.

Psychic: The soul does not even see the grasping, taking its bondage to objects as natural living.
deficient.vignette: Dan has never noticed that he grasps at all—the reaching, the clutching, the anxious holding all invisible to him, taken as simply how one lives. The grasper-grasped dynamic the teaching names as bondage is, to him, not a trap but the unquestioned water he swims in.

attributes.title: Tree of Samsara (Anatomy of Bondage)
attributes.aspect: The complete anatomy of samsara as a living tree: rooted in tamas (unconscious ignorance), sprouting into body-identification, leafing into desires, watered by action-loops, bearing the fruit of sorrow. Each stage feeds the next. Liberation requires uprooting at the seed (ignorance) level.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Framework — a structure of bondage · Valence: Affliction (the binding structure) · Guṇa: Tamas–Rajas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Structure of bondage · Key relation: the whole structure of bondage as a tree; kin to the inverted aśvattha (Dim 27).
coordinate.key_relation: the whole structure of bondage as a tree; kin to the inverted aśvattha (Dim 27).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of the complete tree-structure. Working at the root (ignorance) rather than just trimming leaves (managing desires).
facets.somatic: Body’s role in the tree understood; action-loops broken.
facets.emotional: Emotional craving-leaves recognized as symptoms.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees the whole tree; works at root.
facets.psychic: The soul perceives the whole tree of worldly existence and reaches to cut it at the root.

balanced.text:
Recognition of the complete tree-structure. Working at the root (ignorance) rather than just trimming leaves (managing desires).

Somatic: Body’s role in the tree understood; action-loops broken.

Emotional: Emotional craving-leaves recognized as symptoms.

Cognitive: Mind sees the whole tree; works at root.

Psychic: The soul perceives the whole tree of worldly existence and reaches to cut it at the root.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to see the whole tree of his bondage—how one craving branches into action, action into impression, impression into fresh craving—and, seeing the pattern, to stop watering it. The map of samsara has become, for him, the beginning of the way out.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Obsessive analysis of the tree without uprooting. Endless mapping.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in surface-level practices that don’t reach root.
facets.emotional: Emotional management without root-cutting.
facets.cognitive: Intellectual mapping without genuine inquiry.
facets.psychic: The soul gets lost mapping the tree’s anatomy, analyzing bondage rather than cutting it.

excess.text:
Obsessive analysis of the tree without uprooting. Endless mapping.

Somatic: Body engaged in surface-level practices that don’t reach root.

Emotional: Emotional management without root-cutting.

Cognitive: Intellectual mapping without genuine inquiry.

Psychic: The soul gets lost mapping the tree’s anatomy, analyzing bondage rather than cutting it.
excess.vignette: Sam knows the anatomy of bondage in exquisite detail—every root, branch, and leaf of samsara diagrammed—and remains as bound as ever, mistaking the study of the tree for cutting it. He’s an expert on the trap he’s still sitting in.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Unconscious participation in entire tree. Only sorrow-fruits noticed, not their structural cause.
facets.somatic: Body in action-loops without awareness.
facets.emotional: Emotional cravings unrecognized as tree-leaves.
facets.cognitive: Mind never sees the systematic structure of own bondage.
facets.psychic: The soul sees only scattered branches of its suffering, never the single tree they grow from.

deficient.text:
Unconscious participation in entire tree. Only sorrow-fruits noticed, not their structural cause.

Somatic: Body in action-loops without awareness.

Emotional: Emotional cravings unrecognized as tree-leaves.

Cognitive: Mind never sees the systematic structure of own bondage.

Psychic: The soul sees only scattered branches of its suffering, never the single tree they grow from.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no map of his own bondage at all—no sense of how his cravings, acts, and impressions feed each other into the endless cycle. Blind to the tree he’s tangled in, he keeps watering its roots and wondering why the same fruit keeps falling on his head.

attributes.title: Vasana (Latent Tendency)
attributes.aspect: Vasana is the latent karmic seed—unconscious tendency formed by past actions stored in chitta. Modern psychology calls it conditioning. Vasanas drive action even against clear knowing. The cognition-affect split: buddhi knows it’s harmful, manas desires it anyway, vasana provides momentum. Liberation requires vasana-kshaya (withering of tendencies).

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Faculty / latent tendency (vāsanā, dormant form) · Valence: Faculty tending to affliction · Guṇa: Tamas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Latent cause · Key relation: The dormant vāsanā; same root as Dim 12.
coordinate.key_relation: The dormant vāsanā; same root as Dim 12.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Vasanas being progressively recognized and dissolved through nididhyasana. Awareness of being driven without being driven.
facets.somatic: Body’s habit patterns observed and gradually released.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns seen as vasanas, not self.
facets.cognitive: Mind aware of vasana-momentum and works with it.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes its latent tendencies as seeds that awareness can roast so they no longer sprout.

balanced.text:
Vasanas being progressively recognized and dissolved through nididhyasana. Awareness of being driven without being driven.

Somatic: Body’s habit patterns observed and gradually released.

Emotional: Emotional patterns seen as vasanas, not self.

Cognitive: Mind aware of vasana-momentum and works with it.

Psychic: The soul recognizes its latent tendencies as seeds that awareness can roast so they no longer sprout.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to sense the dormant tendencies sleeping in him—the seeds not yet sprouted, the reactions waiting for their trigger—and tends the soil so the binding ones aren’t watered. Aware of what lies latent, he’s careful which seeds he feeds and which he lets quietly die.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Obsessive analysis of vasanas without dissolving them. Theoretical understanding.
facets.somatic: Body still driven despite analytical awareness.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns analyzed but not transformed.
facets.cognitive: Mental mapping of vasanas without uprooting.
facets.psychic: The soul waters its tendencies through repeated indulgence, deepening the grooves.

excess.text:
Obsessive analysis of vasanas without dissolving them. Theoretical understanding.

Somatic: Body still driven despite analytical awareness.

Emotional: Emotional patterns analyzed but not transformed.

Cognitive: Mental mapping of vasanas without uprooting.

Psychic: The soul waters its tendencies through repeated indulgence, deepening the grooves.
excess.vignette: Sam has become fascinated by his latent tendencies—forever probing for buried patterns, half-hoping to find dramatic hidden depths, so absorbed in excavating dormant seeds that the digging is its own compulsion. The awareness meant to free him has become an endless, self-absorbed excavation.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Vasanas operating entirely unconsciously. Repetition-compulsion driving life.
facets.somatic: Body in unconscious habit-loops.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns running on auto-pilot.
facets.cognitive: Mind unaware of being driven by vasanas.
facets.psychic: The soul is steered by latent seeds it never notices, surprised again and again by its own reactions.

deficient.text:
Vasanas operating entirely unconsciously. Repetition-compulsion driving life.

Somatic: Body in unconscious habit-loops.

Emotional: Emotional patterns running on auto-pilot.

Cognitive: Mind unaware of being driven by vasanas.

Psychic: The soul is steered by latent seeds it never notices, surprised again and again by its own reactions.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no idea anything lies latent in him—no sense of dormant tendencies waiting to sprout, so that when an old pattern suddenly fires he’s blindsided every time. That seeds long buried might one day bear fruit, and could be tended now, is a possibility he’s never considered.

attributes.title: Hṛdaya Granthi — The Knot of the Heart
attributes.aspect: The knot of the heart is the tight identification by which the Self is bound to the body-mind — the felt cinch of ‘I am this person, this body, this story.’ The Upaniṣads describe its undoing in dramatic terms: when the Self is seen, the knot of the heart is cut, all doubts are dissolved, and the bondage of action falls away. As lived experience, the knot is the constant, low-grade contraction of self-protection — the bracing of a self that feels its existence to be at stake — and its loosening is felt as a profound and growing ease, as though a fist held tight for a lifetime were finally beginning to open.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital (the heart) · Ontic mode: Framework — the heart-knot (hṛdaya-granthi) · Valence: Affliction (the knot to cut) · Guṇa: Tamas-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Core structure of bondage · Key relation: The knot binding Self to not-Self, whose cutting is liberation (Muṇḍaka); kin to Adhyāsa.
coordinate.key_relation: The knot binding Self to not-Self, whose cutting is liberation (Muṇḍaka); kin to Adhyāsa.

balanced.label: Balanced — the knot loosening; growing ease and non-contraction

facets.overview: Heart-knots progressively dissolved through practice and realization. As bindings release, immortality is realized here-and-now.
facets.somatic: Heart center opening, physical bondage patterns releasing.
facets.emotional: Emotional knots untying, freedom from compulsive heart-binding.
facets.cognitive: Mind released from heart-knots, true freedom recognized.
facets.psychic: The knot binding spirit to matter begins to loosen; as it does, doubts fall away and the soul feels the first real freedom in the chest.

balanced.text:
Heart-knots progressively dissolved through practice and realization. As bindings release, immortality is realized here-and-now.

Somatic: Heart center opening, physical bondage patterns releasing.

Emotional: Emotional knots untying, freedom from compulsive heart-binding.

Cognitive: Mind released from heart-knots, true freedom recognized.

Psychic: The knot binding spirit to matter begins to loosen; as it does, doubts fall away and the soul feels the first real freedom in the chest.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has felt the knot at the heart begin to loosen—the tight fusion of self and world, spirit and matter, that binds most people—and something in his chest has opened. The heart-knot untying isn’t a metaphor to him; it’s a felt release he’s tasted.

excess.label: In excess — forcing or claiming the knot ‘cut’ prematurely; spiritual pretense

facets.overview: Forced cutting of heart-knots without preparation—spiritual surgery without skill.
facets.somatic: Body in forced practices to cut knots.
facets.emotional: Emotional severing without genuine release.
facets.cognitive: Forced detachment without true understanding.
facets.psychic: The soul forces the opening, straining for breakthrough, and tightens the very knot it means to release.

excess.text:
Forced cutting of heart-knots without preparation—spiritual surgery without skill.

Somatic: Body in forced practices to cut knots.

Emotional: Emotional severing without genuine release.

Cognitive: Forced detachment without true understanding.

Psychic: The soul forces the opening, straining for breakthrough, and tightens the very knot it means to release.
excess.vignette: Sam obsesses over ‘cutting the heart-knot’—straining, forcing, treating the subtle release as a task to be muscled through—and the very effort tightens the knot he’s trying to undo. He grips at the ungripping, and the tension is the bondage.

deficient.label: Deficient — the knot pulled tight; chronic self-protective contraction

facets.overview: All heart-knots tight—maximum bondage, no recognition that immortality requires their release.
facets.somatic: Body tightly bound by heart-patterns.
facets.emotional: Emotional knots dominating life.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t see the heart-knots as obstacles.
facets.psychic: The knot stays tightly tied; Self and body fused without remainder, the soul cannot even conceive of itself as free.

deficient.text:
All heart-knots tight—maximum bondage, no recognition that immortality requires their release.

Somatic: Body tightly bound by heart-patterns.

Emotional: Emotional knots dominating life.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t see the heart-knots as obstacles.

Psychic: The knot stays tightly tied; Self and body fused without remainder, the soul cannot even conceive of itself as free.
deficient.vignette: Dan has never felt the knot, let alone its loosening—the tight fusion of self and world simply is his reality, unquestioned, with no sense that anything in the heart could untie. The granthi binds him invisibly, and he’s never once suspected it was a knot at all.

attributes.attribute: Last Barrier: Fear of Liberation Itself
attributes.title: Last Barrier: Fear of Liberation Itself (Mokshat Vibhishika)
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra identifies the most subtle obstacle: fear of liberation itself. At the very threshold, the seeker realizes that moksha means dissolution of ego-individuality. Fear arises: ‘But what about me? I’ll cease!’ This terror is the last barrier. Gaudapada’s Karika confirms: ‘The yogis fear from that (Brahman) which is fearless, perceiving fear in fearlessness itself.’

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital · Ontic mode: State — the fear of liberation itself · Valence: Affliction (the final resistance) · Guṇa: Tamas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Final barrier · Key relation: The ego’s fear of its own dissolution; kin to Abhiniveśa (Dim 19).
coordinate.ontic_mode: State — the fear of liberation itself
coordinate.key_relation: The ego’s fear of its own dissolution; kin to Abhiniveśa (Dim 19).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of the fear when it arises at the threshold; willingness to dissolve despite fear.
facets.somatic: Body willing to be ‘lost’ in the merger.
facets.emotional: Emotional courage at the threshold.
facets.cognitive: Mind willing to dissolve its own identity.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes its own subtle fear of the freedom it seeks, and gently moves through the final resistance.

balanced.text:
Recognition of the fear when it arises at the threshold; willingness to dissolve despite fear.

Somatic: Body willing to be ‘lost’ in the merger.

Emotional: Emotional courage at the threshold.

Cognitive: Mind willing to dissolve its own identity.

Psychic: The soul recognizes its own subtle fear of the freedom it seeks, and gently moves through the final resistance.
balanced.vignette: Nearing a real letting-go, Ravi felt it—the strange fear of freedom itself, the ego’s terror of its own dissolution—and, recognizing it as the last barrier, walked through rather than fleeing. He met the fear of liberation and did not let it turn him back.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Self-conscious ‘fearless leap’ that’s actually performative ego-self-destruction.
facets.somatic: Body in dramatic self-rejection.
facets.emotional: Emotional spectacle of ego-death.
facets.cognitive: Mind performing surrender rather than surrendering.
facets.psychic: The soul magnifies the fear, clinging to bondage as safety at the very threshold of release.

excess.text:
Self-conscious ‘fearless leap’ that’s actually performative ego-self-destruction.

Somatic: Body in dramatic self-rejection.

Emotional: Emotional spectacle of ego-death.

Cognitive: Mind performing surrender rather than surrendering.

Psychic: The soul magnifies the fear, clinging to bondage as safety at the very threshold of release.
excess.vignette: Sam has made the fear of liberation into a permanent excuse—forever ‘not ready,’ ‘needing more preparation,’ dressing the ego’s terror of dissolving in the language of prudence. The last barrier has become a wall he keeps rebuilding, and calls it wisdom.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: At the threshold, recoiling in terror. Pulling back to safe bondage. Never crossing.
facets.somatic: Body retreating to familiar identifications.
facets.emotional: Emotional terror of merger overwhelming.
facets.cognitive: Mind refusing to dissolve, clinging to ‘me.’
facets.psychic: The soul does not even sense this last barrier, mistaking its avoidance of freedom for lack of interest.

deficient.text:
At the threshold, recoiling in terror. Pulling back to safe bondage. Never crossing.

Somatic: Body retreating to familiar identifications.

Emotional: Emotional terror of merger overwhelming.

Cognitive: Mind refusing to dissolve, clinging to ‘me.’

Psychic: The soul does not even sense this last barrier, mistaking its avoidance of freedom for lack of interest.
deficient.vignette: Dan has never come near enough to freedom to fear it—the dissolution of the ego isn’t a terror for him because it’s not even a possibility he’s approached. The last barrier the teaching describes is one he’ll only meet if he ever nears the door, and he never has.

attributes.title: Gajendra Moksha (Call in Crisis)
attributes.aspect: Gajendra Moksha is one of the most beloved Bhagavatam stories. The elephant-king fought a crocodile for 1000 years using his own strength. Only when he surrendered completely and cried out to the Lord did Vishnu appear and save him. The lesson: prolonged self-effort eventually exhausts; sincere surrender brings immediate response. In daily crises: don’t struggle alone forever. Call.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: State / turning — the surrendered cry in extremity · Valence: The redemptive turn · Guṇa: Sattva emerging from Tamas · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Turning point (the cry that draws grace) · Key relation: The crisis-call that liberates; kin to Prasāda / grace.
coordinate.ontic_mode: State / turning — the surrendered cry in extremity
coordinate.causal_status: Turning point (the cry that draws grace)
coordinate.key_relation: The crisis-call that liberates; kin to Prasāda / grace.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Daily struggles met with appropriate self-effort PLUS sincere prayer when needed. Not waiting until exhaustion to call.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in effort while heart also calls.
facets.emotional: Emotional balance between effort and surrender.
facets.cognitive: Mind both works and prays.
facets.psychic: The soul, like the elephant-king seized by the crocodile, cries out for help when its own strength fails, and grace answers.

balanced.text:
Daily struggles met with appropriate self-effort PLUS sincere prayer when needed. Not waiting until exhaustion to call.

Somatic: Body engaged in effort while heart also calls.

Emotional: Emotional balance between effort and surrender.

Cognitive: Mind both works and prays.

Psychic: The soul, like the elephant-king seized by the crocodile, cries out for help when its own strength fails, and grace answers.
balanced.vignette: When the crisis came and his own strength failed, Ravi finally, genuinely called out—surrendering the pretense of self-sufficiency, opening to a help beyond himself. Like the elephant-king seized by the crocodile, he learned that the sincere cry from the depths is itself the turning.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Passive waiting for divine rescue without doing one’s part. Magical thinking.
facets.somatic: Body inactive expecting miracles.
facets.emotional: Emotional dependency.
facets.cognitive: Mental abdication of effort.
facets.psychic: The soul cries out endlessly without ever exhausting its self-reliance, performing helplessness.

excess.text:
Passive waiting for divine rescue without doing one’s part. Magical thinking.

Somatic: Body inactive expecting miracles.

Emotional: Emotional dependency.

Cognitive: Mental abdication of effort.

Psychic: The soul cries out endlessly without ever exhausting its self-reliance, performing helplessness.
excess.vignette: Sam treats the ‘call in crisis’ as a formula to game—performing surrender, mouthing the cry, expecting rescue as a transaction while never actually letting go of his own grip. The desperate, sincere opening the story describes has become, for him, a technique he applies and a control he never relinquishes.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Pure self-effort to the point of exhaustion. Pride in self-reliance preventing the call. Suffering needlessly.
facets.somatic: Body destroyed by endless solo struggle.
facets.emotional: Emotional isolation in difficulty.
facets.cognitive: Mind refuses to admit need for help.
facets.psychic: The soul never calls for help, drowning in its crisis while insisting it can save itself.

deficient.text:
Pure self-effort to the point of exhaustion. Pride in self-reliance preventing the call. Suffering needlessly.

Somatic: Body destroyed by endless solo struggle.

Emotional: Emotional isolation in difficulty.

Cognitive: Mind refuses to admit need for help.

Psychic: The soul never calls for help, drowning in its crisis while insisting it can save itself.
deficient.vignette: Dan never calls out, however deep the crisis—too proud, too self-reliant, too certain he must save himself, gripping harder as he sinks. The sincere cry from the depths that opens the door to grace is one he cannot bring himself to make, and so the door stays shut.

attributes.title: Seeing Through Delusion of Desire
attributes.aspect: Desire is delusive—it promises an end (fulfillment) while actually being a self-perpetuating mechanism. The fulfillment of desire produces more desire, not contentment. Seeing through this delusion (not suppressing desire) liberates one from its operation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice / method — a means to freedom · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the seeing that disillusions desire and loosens Kāma (Dim 24) and Rāga (Dim 19).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — a means to freedom
coordinate.key_relation: the seeing that disillusions desire and loosens Kāma (Dim 24) and Rāga (Dim 19).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Awareness of desire’s nature without suppression. Each desire seen for what it is—a promise that won’t be kept. This very seeing transforms one’s relationship with desires.
facets.somatic: Body free from compulsive desire-driven behavior.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom—desires arise but lose their commanding force.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees desire’s mechanism clearly, no longer enthralled.
facets.psychic: The soul sees through the promise each desire makes, recognizing the fullness it seeks cannot be found in the object.

balanced.text:
Awareness of desire’s nature without suppression. Each desire seen for what it is—a promise that won’t be kept. This very seeing transforms one’s relationship with desires.

Somatic: Body free from compulsive desire-driven behavior.

Emotional: Emotional freedom—desires arise but lose their commanding force.

Cognitive: Mind sees desire’s mechanism clearly, no longer enthralled.

Psychic: The soul sees through the promise each desire makes, recognizing the fullness it seeks cannot be found in the object.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to see through his desires as he has them—watching the craving promise total fulfillment and knowing, even mid-reach, that the fulfillment will evaporate. The seeing-through doesn’t kill his wanting; it loosens its spell, so he chases a little less and rests a little more.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced desire-suppression that strengthens what is denied. Pathology of repression.
facets.somatic: Body in war with suppressed desires.
facets.emotional: Suppressed desires creating distortion.
facets.cognitive: Mind obsessed with what it is denying.
facets.psychic: The soul turns disillusionment into bitterness, cynically devaluing all desire and joy.

excess.text:
Forced desire-suppression that strengthens what is denied. Pathology of repression.

Somatic: Body in war with suppressed desires.

Emotional: Suppressed desires creating distortion.

Cognitive: Mind obsessed with what it is denying.

Psychic: The soul turns disillusionment into bitterness, cynically devaluing all desire and joy.
excess.vignette: Sam has weaponized ‘desire is delusion’ into a war on all wanting—suppressing every wish, suspecting every pleasure, mistaking a grim anhedonia for freedom. Seeing through desire has curdled into refusing to feel it, and the refusal binds him as tightly as the craving did.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total enslavement to desire—every wish chased, no recognition of the delusion-mechanism.
facets.somatic: Body driven by endless desires.
facets.emotional: Emotional life as constant wanting and reaction.
facets.cognitive: Mind never sees the desire-delusion, just follows.
facets.psychic: The soul believes each desire’s promise afresh, forever chasing the satisfaction just beyond reach.

deficient.text:
Total enslavement to desire—every wish chased, no recognition of the delusion-mechanism.

Somatic: Body driven by endless desires.

Emotional: Emotional life as constant wanting and reaction.

Cognitive: Mind never sees the desire-delusion, just follows.

Psychic: The soul believes each desire’s promise afresh, forever chasing the satisfaction just beyond reach.
deficient.vignette: Dan is wholly taken in by every desire—each craving believed absolutely, each object promising the happiness that never comes, the delusion never once pierced. He chases fulfillment down the same evaporating road forever, never suspecting the road is a mirage.

attributes.title: Removal of Sorrow (Duhkha-Nivritti)
attributes.aspect: Sorrow has its root cause in avidya (ignorance) about one’s true nature. No external acquisition can remove it because the cause is internal. Wisdom—seeing through ignorance—is the only genuine removal. Therapy addresses symptoms; jnana removes the root.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: State — a mark of freedom · Valence: The fruit · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: duḥkha-nivṛtti — the ceasing of sorrow; the negative face of liberation.
coordinate.key_relation: duḥkha-nivṛtti — the ceasing of sorrow; the negative face of liberation.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Wisdom-based freedom from sorrow—not happy-pretense but genuine release through realizing the deeper truth.
facets.somatic: Body relieved of sorrow’s somatic burdens.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from grief-roots through wisdom.
facets.cognitive: Mind seeing through ignorance, sorrow at its root dissolved.
facets.psychic: The soul orients toward the cessation of suffering at its root, addressing the ignorance rather than merely managing the pain.

balanced.text:
Wisdom-based freedom from sorrow—not happy-pretense but genuine release through realizing the deeper truth.

Somatic: Body relieved of sorrow’s somatic burdens.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from grief-roots through wisdom.

Cognitive: Mind seeing through ignorance, sorrow at its root dissolved.

Psychic: The soul orients toward the cessation of suffering at its root, addressing the ignorance rather than merely managing the pain.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has found that sorrow can be met and moved through rather than merely endured or fled—that facing the pain honestly is itself the road to its removal. He neither wallows nor represses; he lets the grief do its work, and it passes, leaving him clearer.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced positivity claiming ‘sorrow removed’ while suppressing real grief.
facets.somatic: Body holds suppressed grief while face shows positivity.
facets.emotional: Emotional suppression mistaken for transcendence.
facets.cognitive: Doctrinal claim without genuine root-cutting.
facets.psychic: The soul becomes obsessed with eliminating all discomfort, fleeing even the productive pain that teaches.

excess.text:
Forced positivity claiming ‘sorrow removed’ while suppressing real grief.

Somatic: Body holds suppressed grief while face shows positivity.

Emotional: Emotional suppression mistaken for transcendence.

Cognitive: Doctrinal claim without genuine root-cutting.

Psychic: The soul becomes obsessed with eliminating all discomfort, fleeing even the productive pain that teaches.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned ‘removal of sorrow’ into a frantic avoidance—numbing, distracting, spiritually bypassing every ache before it can be felt, so determined to be free of suffering that he never lets any of it complete. The unfaced sorrow pools underground and runs him from there.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Sorrow taken as permanent feature, no orientation toward wisdom that could remove it.
facets.somatic: Body chronically marked by sorrow.
facets.emotional: Sorrow as baseline emotional state.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees no exit from sorrow.
facets.psychic: The soul resigns itself to sorrow as inevitable, never suspecting it has a removable cause.

deficient.text:
Sorrow taken as permanent feature, no orientation toward wisdom that could remove it.

Somatic: Body chronically marked by sorrow.

Emotional: Sorrow as baseline emotional state.

Cognitive: Mind sees no exit from sorrow.

Psychic: The soul resigns itself to sorrow as inevitable, never suspecting it has a removable cause.
deficient.vignette: Dan is drowning in a sorrow he treats as permanent—no sense that grief can be moved through, no path to its removal, only the endless present of the pain. That suffering might be met and released is a possibility he’s lost, and so he carries it all, indefinitely.

attributes.attribute: Intensity of Desire = Intensity of Separation
attributes.title: Intensity of Desire = Intensity of Separation
attributes.aspect: Desire’s intensity reveals the felt separation. Strong desire = strong felt-separation. The mechanism: as consciousness materialises and objects become more solid, the separation between subject and object becomes more intense, generating proportionally intense desire. Reducing felt-separation reduces desire naturally.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Framework — desire as the measure of separation · Valence: Affliction-diagnostic · Guṇa: Rajas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (craving marks the distance from wholeness) · Key relation: Desire as the felt index of separation; kin to Autsukya (Dim 9).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework — desire as the measure of separation
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (craving marks the distance from wholeness)
coordinate.key_relation: Desire as the felt index of separation; kin to Autsukya (Dim 9).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Awareness that desire-intensity measures separation. Working on the separation rather than fighting the desire.
facets.somatic: Body’s intense desires recognized as symptoms of felt-separation.
facets.emotional: Emotional cravings understood as separation-pain.
facets.cognitive: Mind addresses root (separation) rather than symptom (desire).
facets.psychic: The soul reads the heat of its craving as the exact measure of its felt separation, and lets longing point it home.

balanced.text:
Awareness that desire-intensity measures separation. Working on the separation rather than fighting the desire.

Somatic: Body’s intense desires recognized as symptoms of felt-separation.

Emotional: Emotional cravings understood as separation-pain.

Cognitive: Mind addresses root (separation) rather than symptom (desire).

Psychic: The soul reads the heat of its craving as the exact measure of its felt separation, and lets longing point it home.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has noticed the strange law—that the fiercer his craving for a thing, the more separate from it he feels—and used the insight to loosen his grip. Recognizing that intensity of desire measures the depth of the split, he’s learned that wanting less is, oddly, being closer.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Intellectual claim ‘I am one with all’ without actually working on felt separation.
facets.somatic: Body claims unity while still acting from separation.
facets.emotional: Emotional pretense of unity covering real separation.
facets.cognitive: Doctrinal claim without felt experience of unity.
facets.psychic: The soul feeds the intensity, mistaking the fever of wanting for aliveness.

excess.text:
Intellectual claim ‘I am one with all’ without actually working on felt separation.

Somatic: Body claims unity while still acting from separation.

Emotional: Emotional pretense of unity covering real separation.

Cognitive: Doctrinal claim without felt experience of unity.

Psychic: The soul feeds the intensity, mistaking the fever of wanting for aliveness.
excess.vignette: Sam mistakes the intensity of his longing for the depth of his love or his devotion—the fiercer he craves, the holier he feels—never seeing that the very intensity marks how separate he is. He’s proud of a hunger that is precisely his distance from what he hungers for.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Maximum felt-separation generating maximum desires. Endless craving without understanding the root.
facets.somatic: Body in constant intense craving.
facets.emotional: Emotional life ruled by intense longings.
facets.cognitive: Mind never understands desire-separation correlation.
facets.psychic: The soul never connects its restless craving to the underlying separation that drives it.

deficient.text:
Maximum felt-separation generating maximum desires. Endless craving without understanding the root.

Somatic: Body in constant intense craving.

Emotional: Emotional life ruled by intense longings.

Cognitive: Mind never understands desire-separation correlation.

Psychic: The soul never connects its restless craving to the underlying separation that drives it.
deficient.vignette: Dan feels the pull of his desires without any insight into what they reveal—craving intensely, suffering the separation, never once connecting the two. The teaching that the fire of wanting measures the gulf of estrangement is one he’s never glimpsed; he only knows the burn.

attributes.title: Narada’s Crisis (Ego Despite Vast Learning)
attributes.aspect: Narada represents the modern intellectual—mastering vast knowledge yet remaining in inner suffering. He had mastered the four Vedas, history, mathematics, astronomy, all sciences—still suffered. His crisis: ‘Knowledge has not freed me; only the Self can.’ The starting point of the great Bhuyo-Bhuya ascending teaching.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: State — learned despair (knowledge without peace) · Valence: Affliction (the crisis that opens inquiry) · Guṇa: Rajas–Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Turning point · Key relation: The crisis of the erudite; kin to Nirveda (Dim 9).
coordinate.key_relation: The crisis of the erudite; kin to Nirveda (Dim 9).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Honest recognition that intellectual mastery without Self-realization brings no real freedom. The crisis becomes turning-point.
facets.somatic: Body acknowledges that no amount of skill brings inner peace.
facets.emotional: Emotional courage to admit despite achievements, suffering remains.
facets.cognitive: Mind acknowledges that knowledge of objects cannot solve the self-problem.
facets.psychic: The soul, like Nārada, recognizes that all its learning has not touched the sorrowing ego, and turns toward the knowledge that liberates.

balanced.text:
Honest recognition that intellectual mastery without Self-realization brings no real freedom. The crisis becomes turning-point.

Somatic: Body acknowledges that no amount of skill brings inner peace.

Emotional: Emotional courage to admit despite achievements, suffering remains.

Cognitive: Mind acknowledges that knowledge of objects cannot solve the self-problem.

Psychic: The soul, like Nārada, recognizes that all its learning has not touched the sorrowing ego, and turns toward the knowledge that liberates.
balanced.vignette: Ravi, learned and accomplished, hit the wall Narada hit—all his vast knowledge leaving the ego and the ache untouched—and had the honesty to feel it. The crisis of realizing that information hadn’t freed him became, for him, the true beginning of the search.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Spiritual show of ‘suffering’ to gain attention or sympathy from teachers.
facets.somatic: Body performing distress.
facets.emotional: Emotional manipulation through pretended crisis.
facets.cognitive: Mental performance of seeker-identity.
facets.psychic: The soul accumulates ever more learning to plaster over the crisis, deepening the very pride that sorrows.

excess.text:
Spiritual show of ‘suffering’ to gain attention or sympathy from teachers.

Somatic: Body performing distress.

Emotional: Emotional manipulation through pretended crisis.

Cognitive: Mental performance of seeker-identity.

Psychic: The soul accumulates ever more learning to plaster over the crisis, deepening the very pride that sorrows.
excess.vignette: Sam has amassed enormous learning and wears it as attainment—quoting scriptures, correcting teachers—the very erudition inflating the ego it was meant to dissolve. Like Narada before his crisis, he mistakes the size of his knowledge for the depth of his freedom, and the mistake keeps him bound.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Continuing to accumulate knowledge without ever acknowledging the inadequacy of mere learning.
facets.somatic: Body endlessly engaged in learning without transformation.
facets.emotional: Emotional satisfaction with intellectual achievement only.
facets.cognitive: Mind believes more knowledge will eventually solve the problem.
facets.psychic: The soul never feels the crisis beneath its competence, content with knowledge that leaves it untouched.

deficient.text:
Continuing to accumulate knowledge without ever acknowledging the inadequacy of mere learning.

Somatic: Body endlessly engaged in learning without transformation.

Emotional: Emotional satisfaction with intellectual achievement only.

Cognitive: Mind believes more knowledge will eventually solve the problem.

Psychic: The soul never feels the crisis beneath its competence, content with knowledge that leaves it untouched.
deficient.vignette: Dan has neither the vast learning nor the crisis it can precipitate—incurious, unread, never having pursued knowledge far enough to discover its limits. The humbling recognition that information doesn’t liberate is one he’ll never reach, because he’s never gathered enough to be disillusioned by it.

attributes.title: Becoming vs Being (Restlessness as Symptom)
attributes.aspect: All restlessness, all activity, all becoming—reveals that we have not yet achieved our end. The world is in motion; things pass rather than exist. This perpetual becoming indicates an urge of being toward something. The end is Being, where becoming ceases—like the river reaching the ocean.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Framework — becoming vs being · Valence: Affliction-diagnostic · Guṇa: Rajas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Symptom (restlessness as the mark of bondage) · Key relation: The restlessness of becoming vs the rest of being.
coordinate.causal_status: Symptom (restlessness as the mark of bondage)
coordinate.key_relation: The restlessness of becoming vs the rest of being.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition that all becoming aims at Being—using the restlessness as motivation for reaching the place beyond motion.
facets.somatic: Body’s activity directed toward Being, not just more becoming.
facets.emotional: Emotional engagement with becoming while aiming at Being.
facets.cognitive: Mind understands the urge of becoming as pointing to Being.
facets.psychic: The soul reads its chronic restlessness as the symptom of becoming, and turns toward the being it always already is.

balanced.text:
Recognition that all becoming aims at Being—using the restlessness as motivation for reaching the place beyond motion.

Somatic: Body’s activity directed toward Being, not just more becoming.

Emotional: Emotional engagement with becoming while aiming at Being.

Cognitive: Mind understands the urge of becoming as pointing to Being.

Psychic: The soul reads its chronic restlessness as the symptom of becoming, and turns toward the being it always already is.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to rest in being rather than forever becoming—noticing the restless drive to be someone, somewhere, something else as the symptom of estrangement it is. Increasingly at home in what already is, he strives without the frantic ache of never-arriving.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Premature claim of ‘Being’ while actually inactive—stagnation called ‘non-doing.’
facets.somatic: Body stagnant claimed as ‘being.’
facets.emotional: Emotional flatness called ‘transcendence.’
facets.cognitive: Mental stagnation labeled as ‘non-becoming.’
facets.psychic: The soul drives the becoming harder, chasing the next state and calling the restlessness ambition.

excess.text:
Premature claim of ‘Being’ while actually inactive—stagnation called ‘non-doing.’

Somatic: Body stagnant claimed as ‘being.’

Emotional: Emotional flatness called ‘transcendence.’

Cognitive: Mental stagnation labeled as ‘non-becoming.’

Psychic: The soul drives the becoming harder, chasing the next state and calling the restlessness ambition.
excess.vignette: Sam is pure becoming—forever improving, achieving, arriving at the next version of himself, the restlessness never resting because there’s always a further self to reach. He mistakes the ceaseless striving for ambition, never seeing it as the symptom of a self ill at ease with simply being.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Pure becoming with no recognition that it aims at Being. Endless activity, perpetual restlessness, no destination.
facets.somatic: Body in chronic activity, never resting in being.
facets.emotional: Emotional life as endless becoming, no being.
facets.cognitive: Mind never recognizes the destination of all becoming.
facets.psychic: The soul takes its perpetual restlessness as simply normal, never sensing the stillness of being beneath it.

deficient.text:
Pure becoming with no recognition that it aims at Being. Endless activity, perpetual restlessness, no destination.

Somatic: Body in chronic activity, never resting in being.

Emotional: Emotional life as endless becoming, no being.

Cognitive: Mind never recognizes the destination of all becoming.

Psychic: The soul takes its perpetual restlessness as simply normal, never sensing the stillness of being beneath it.
deficient.vignette: Dan is restless without even the drive to become—no striving, no direction, just a formless agitation that never settles into either being or becoming. The peace of resting in what is eludes him, but so does the fire of genuine growth; he’s stuck between, at ease with neither.

attributes.title: The Great Fear (Mahad-Bhayam)
attributes.aspect: All cosmic forces operate under the Great Fear of the Supreme Power. Fire burns, sun shines, wind blows, Indra acts, Death runs—all from fear of the Lord. Only by knowing the Self does one transcend this Great Fear. Until then, all of life is shot through with subtle terror.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: State — the great existential fear (mahad-bhayam) · Valence: Affliction · Guṇa: Tamas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Felt core of bondage · Key relation: The great fear born of duality (Bṛhadāraṇyaka); kin to Bhaya (Dim 8).
coordinate.key_relation: The great fear born of duality (Bṛhadāraṇyaka); kin to Bhaya (Dim 8).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition that even cosmic phenomena operate under Great Fear. Reverence for the awesome Power that makes universe function.
facets.somatic: Body recognizes its operation under cosmic order.
facets.emotional: Emotional reverence rather than just fear—awe of the Great.
facets.cognitive: Mind understands the awesome power behind cosmic functioning.
facets.psychic: The soul meets the great background fear of existence consciously, tracing it to the root-ignorance from which it springs.

balanced.text:
Recognition that even cosmic phenomena operate under Great Fear. Reverence for the awesome Power that makes universe function.

Somatic: Body recognizes its operation under cosmic order.

Emotional: Emotional reverence rather than just fear—awe of the Great.

Cognitive: Mind understands the awesome power behind cosmic functioning.

Psychic: The soul meets the great background fear of existence consciously, tracing it to the root-ignorance from which it springs.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to sense the ‘great fear’—the deep, nameless dread underlying the separate self’s existence—and, rather than fleeing it, to face it as the doorway it is. Meeting the primal fear at the root of his ego has, paradoxically, begun to free him from it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Paralyzing terror of cosmic forces—no engagement possible, frozen by Great Fear.
facets.somatic: Body paralyzed by cosmic terror.
facets.emotional: Emotional paralysis from sense of Great Fear.
facets.cognitive: Mind frozen by cosmic dread.
facets.psychic: The soul is overwhelmed by the dread, identifying with it until it colors everything.

excess.text:
Paralyzing terror of cosmic forces—no engagement possible, frozen by Great Fear.

Somatic: Body paralyzed by cosmic terror.

Emotional: Emotional paralysis from sense of Great Fear.

Cognitive: Mind frozen by cosmic dread.

Psychic: The soul is overwhelmed by the dread, identifying with it until it colors everything.
excess.vignette: Sam’s whole life is organized around fleeing the great fear—the frantic activity, the constant distraction, the accumulation all unconscious flight from a dread he won’t turn to face. The unmet fear runs him from underground, and the more he flees, the more it rules.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total denial of Great Fear—life lived as if no awesome Power exists. Ego claims it can do anything without consequence.
facets.somatic: Body in arrogant defiance of cosmic order.
facets.emotional: Emotional invulnerability claimed against cosmic forces.
facets.cognitive: Mind unaware of the Great Power behind all functioning.
facets.psychic: The soul numbs the great fear entirely, and the unfelt dread drives its compulsions from below.

deficient.text:
Total denial of Great Fear—life lived as if no awesome Power exists. Ego claims it can do anything without consequence.

Somatic: Body in arrogant defiance of cosmic order.

Emotional: Emotional invulnerability claimed against cosmic forces.

Cognitive: Mind unaware of the Great Power behind all functioning.

Psychic: The soul numbs the great fear entirely, and the unfelt dread drives its compulsions from below.
deficient.vignette: Dan has so numbed himself that he no longer feels the great fear at all—not because he’s faced it, but because he’s buried it under a thick, managed surface. The primal dread that could, if faced, become a doorway is sealed away, and with it the door.

attributes.title: Vikshepa (Distractions of Mind)
attributes.aspect: Vikshepa is the scattering/distraction of the mind, accompanied by five symptoms: duhkha (suffering/pain), daurmanasya (depression/depression of mind), anga-mejayatva (trembling of limbs), and shvasa-prashvasa (irregular inhalation and exhalation). These are the diagnostic markers of an obstructed mind.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Mechanism — the mind’s scattering (vikṣepa) · Valence: Affliction (to still) · Guṇa: Rajas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (the projecting / scattering power) · Key relation: The distracting power (with āvaraṇa); kin to the vṛttis (Dim 20).
coordinate.key_relation: The distracting power (with āvaraṇa); kin to the vṛttis (Dim 20).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When vikshepa is overcome, the mind becomes one-pointed (ekagra) and ready for yoga. Body, breath, and mind are coherent.
facets.somatic: Steady body without tremors, regular smooth breath, healthy nervous system.
facets.emotional: Emotional stability, no depression, peace.
facets.cognitive: Focused mind, no scattering, ready for concentration.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the scattering power of mind, and gathers its attention back again and again.

balanced.text:
