When vikshepa is overcome, the mind becomes one-pointed (ekagra) and ready for yoga. Body, breath, and mind are coherent.

Somatic: Steady body without tremors, regular smooth breath, healthy nervous system.

Emotional: Emotional stability, no depression, peace.

Cognitive: Focused mind, no scattering, ready for concentration.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the scattering power of mind, and gathers its attention back again and again.
balanced.vignette: When distractions pull at Ravi—the ping, the worry, the passing urge—he notices the scattering and gently gathers his attention back. Aware of vikshepa as vikshepa, he isn’t at its mercy; the mind wanders, and he returns it, again and again, without drama.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Severe vikshepa with all five symptoms manifesting creates a mind incapable of yoga—body, breath, emotion all dysregulated.
facets.somatic: Trembling body, irregular breath, somatic distress, nervous system breakdown.
facets.emotional: Severe suffering and depression, despair.
facets.cognitive: Completely scattered mind, unable to focus, all obstacles active.
facets.psychic: The soul fights the distractions with a tension that scatters it further.

excess.text:
Severe vikshepa with all five symptoms manifesting creates a mind incapable of yoga—body, breath, emotion all dysregulated.

Somatic: Trembling body, irregular breath, somatic distress, nervous system breakdown.

Emotional: Severe suffering and depression, despair.

Cognitive: Completely scattered mind, unable to focus, all obstacles active.

Psychic: The soul fights the distractions with a tension that scatters it further.
excess.vignette: Sam is so distractible that he’s built a whole restless life around it—never finishing, never settling, the mind flung in a hundred directions and mistaking the frenzy for engagement. The distractedness has become his default, and he can’t tell the scattering from a full life.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Absence of vikshepa is the desired yogic state—a focused integrated mind-body-breath system.
facets.somatic: Steady body, regular breath, healthy integration.
facets.emotional: Stable emotions, freedom from depression.
facets.cognitive: Focused mind, ready for samadhi.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly given to distraction, its attention flung outward at every passing thing.

deficient.text:
Absence of vikshepa is the desired yogic state—a focused integrated mind-body-breath system.

Somatic: Steady body, regular breath, healthy integration.

Emotional: Stable emotions, freedom from depression.

Cognitive: Focused mind, ready for samadhi.

Psychic: The soul is wholly given to distraction, its attention flung outward at every passing thing.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s attention is pure scatter—no capacity to gather it, no awareness that it’s scattered, the mind flung wherever the loudest stimulus points, all day. That the distraction could even be noticed, let alone gathered, is not a possibility he’s met; the confetti is simply his mind.

attributes.title: Viṣāda — Dejection as the Doorway
attributes.aspect: Viṣāda is dejection, the existential crisis — the collapse of meaning, the sense that one’s whole way of living has reached a wall. It is the condition in which the Bhagavad Gītā opens: Arjuna, capable and accomplished, sits down in despair on the battlefield, unable to go on. The tradition’s profound insight is that viṣāda, though it feels like an ending, is often the doorway — the necessary crisis in which the old, bondage-built self exhausts itself and a genuine question can finally be asked. It is suffering that has become honest enough to turn a person around.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: State / wave — dejection (viṣāda) as the doorway · Valence: Faculty tending to affliction — but the doorway to inquiry · Guṇa: Tamas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Turning point · Key relation: Same as Viṣāda (Dim 9); Arjuna’s dejection that opens the Gītā.
coordinate.ontic_mode: State / wave — dejection (viṣāda) as the doorway
coordinate.valence: Faculty tending to affliction — but the doorway to inquiry
coordinate.key_relation: Same as Viṣāda (Dim 9); Arjuna’s dejection that opens the Gītā.

balanced.label: Balanced — dejection met honestly, becoming the doorway to inquiry

facets.overview: When Vishada is properly understood and transcended, a person uses moments of crisis as gateways to deeper wisdom. The crisis becomes catalyst for transformation rather than collapse.
facets.somatic: Restored physical vitality after crisis, body that has integrated the lessons of difficulty, healthy resilience built through transformation.
facets.emotional: Emotional depth and wisdom from having faced crisis, healthy capacity to face difficulty, emotional resilience and transformation, deep peace.
facets.cognitive: Mental clarity emerging from crisis, wisdom gained through difficulty, ability to see beyond immediate appearances, transformed perspective.
facets.psychic: Dejection is met as a doorway; like Arjuna’s collapse, the soul’s crisis becomes the very opening through which a higher teaching can enter.

balanced.text:
When Vishada is properly understood and transcended, a person uses moments of crisis as gateways to deeper wisdom. The crisis becomes catalyst for transformation rather than collapse.

Somatic: Restored physical vitality after crisis, body that has integrated the lessons of difficulty, healthy resilience built through transformation.

Emotional: Emotional depth and wisdom from having faced crisis, healthy capacity to face difficulty, emotional resilience and transformation, deep peace.

Cognitive: Mental clarity emerging from crisis, wisdom gained through difficulty, ability to see beyond immediate appearances, transformed perspective.

Psychic: Dejection is met as a doorway; like Arjuna’s collapse, the soul’s crisis becomes the very opening through which a higher teaching can enter.
balanced.vignette: When dejection came, Ravi didn’t merely suffer it or flee it—he let it hollow him out, and in the honest emptiness something turned. Like Arjuna’s despair on the battlefield, his lowest point became the doorway; the visada, fully felt, opened onto the real question.

excess.label: In excess — despair indulged or spiritualized; crisis without inquiry

facets.overview: Severe Vishada is the dominant state—chronic depression, existential collapse, paralysis, and inability to find way forward. The crisis becomes prolonged without transformation.
facets.somatic: Physical collapse, severe depression with somatic symptoms, body weighed down, inability to function physically, profound fatigue.
facets.emotional: Profound despair and depression, emotional collapse, hopelessness, emotional paralysis, dark emotional states, inability to feel hope.
facets.cognitive: Mental paralysis and confusion, inability to see way forward, dark thoughts, severe cognitive impairment from depression, mental despair.
facets.psychic: The soul sinks into and identifies with its despair; what could have been a threshold becomes a pit it furnishes and inhabits.

excess.text:
Severe Vishada is the dominant state—chronic depression, existential collapse, paralysis, and inability to find way forward. The crisis becomes prolonged without transformation.

Somatic: Physical collapse, severe depression with somatic symptoms, body weighed down, inability to function physically, profound fatigue.

Emotional: Profound despair and depression, emotional collapse, hopelessness, emotional paralysis, dark emotional states, inability to feel hope.

Cognitive: Mental paralysis and confusion, inability to see way forward, dark thoughts, severe cognitive impairment from depression, mental despair.

Psychic: The soul sinks into and identifies with its despair; what could have been a threshold becomes a pit it furnishes and inhabits.
excess.vignette: Sam romanticizes his dejection—wallowing in the darkness, cultivating the despair, mistaking the wound for depth and refusing the turn the visada is meant to make possible. The doorway has become a room he’s furnished and moved into, and calls the stagnation profundity.

deficient.label: Deficient — the crisis numbed or fled; the doorway passed by, bondage resumed

facets.overview: Inability to feel any dejection means inability to recognize when life’s structures are inadequate. The person never has the necessary crisis that prompts deeper inquiry.
facets.somatic: Forced physical positivity, ignoring body’s signals of distress, manic activity to avoid feeling.
facets.emotional: Emotional bypassing of necessary difficulty, forced positivity, inability to feel appropriate concern, emotional shallowness.
facets.cognitive: Mental denial of life’s serious questions, refusal to face hard truths, superficial thinking, inability to engage with depth.
facets.psychic: The soul numbs or flees the crisis; unable to feel the productive dejection, it forecloses the breakdown that might have become a breakthrough.

deficient.text:
Inability to feel any dejection means inability to recognize when life’s structures are inadequate. The person never has the necessary crisis that prompts deeper inquiry.

Somatic: Forced physical positivity, ignoring body’s signals of distress, manic activity to avoid feeling.

Emotional: Emotional bypassing of necessary difficulty, forced positivity, inability to feel appropriate concern, emotional shallowness.

Cognitive: Mental denial of life’s serious questions, refusal to face hard truths, superficial thinking, inability to engage with depth.

Psychic: The soul numbs or flees the crisis; unable to feel the productive dejection, it forecloses the breakdown that might have become a breakthrough.
deficient.vignette: Dan papers over every dejection the instant it appears—numbing, distracting, refusing to feel the low that might, if faced, open a door. The visada that could become a threshold is never allowed to land, and so he keeps skimming the surface of a life that never deepens.

attributes.title: Stage 1: Subtle Disharmony (Early Bondage)
attributes.aspect: Stage 1 of degradation: subtle disharmony. Buddhi still functions but is not fully commanding. Manas grows reactive. Ego identification becomes intermittent. The seeker notices something is wrong but cannot yet name it. Modern markers: anxiety without clear cause, over-identification with success/failure, periodic existential discomfort.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Stage — a stage of bondage · Valence: Affliction (a degree of descent) · Guṇa: Rajas rising · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Stage / cycle of descent · Key relation: First stage — subtle early disharmony, still beneath notice.
coordinate.key_relation: First stage — subtle early disharmony, still beneath notice.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of early warning signs. Acting on them through preliminary practice (karma without attachment, ethical stabilization, preliminary viveka).
facets.somatic: Body’s early signals heeded.
facets.emotional: Emotional disquiet noticed and addressed.
facets.cognitive: Mind alert to early markers, begins practice.
facets.psychic: The soul catches bondage early, sensing the first subtle disharmony before it hardens, and corrects course.

balanced.text:
Recognition of early warning signs. Acting on them through preliminary practice (karma without attachment, ethical stabilization, preliminary viveka).

Somatic: Body’s early signals heeded.

Emotional: Emotional disquiet noticed and addressed.

Cognitive: Mind alert to early markers, begins practice.

Psychic: The soul catches bondage early, sensing the first subtle disharmony before it hardens, and corrects course.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has learned to catch the subtle disharmony early—the faint unease, the small split between what he does and what he values—before it hardens. Sensitive to the first tremor of inner conflict, he adjusts while adjustment is still easy, and the early bondage never sets.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Hyperawareness of every minor symptom; constant self-diagnosis.
facets.somatic: Body anxiously monitored.
facets.emotional: Emotional life under constant scrutiny.
facets.cognitive: Mind obsessed with stage-1 self-assessment.
facets.psychic: The soul over-monitors itself for the faintest disharmony, anxious vigilance becoming its own knot.

excess.text:
Hyperawareness of every minor symptom; constant self-diagnosis.

Somatic: Body anxiously monitored.

Emotional: Emotional life under constant scrutiny.

Cognitive: Mind obsessed with stage-1 self-assessment.

Psychic: The soul over-monitors itself for the faintest disharmony, anxious vigilance becoming its own knot.
excess.vignette: Sam is hyper-vigilant for the faintest inner disharmony—monitoring every flicker of unease, pathologizing every small tension, so anxiously attuned to early bondage that the vigilance itself has become the disharmony. He can’t let a minor discomfort simply be minor.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Ignoring early signals. Rationalizing restlessness as ambition. Missing the window for easy intervention.
facets.somatic: Body’s signals ignored.
facets.emotional: Emotional warnings dismissed.
facets.cognitive: Mind rationalizes the deepening problem.
facets.psychic: The soul misses the early signs entirely, letting subtle disharmony deepen unchecked.

deficient.text:
Ignoring early signals. Rationalizing restlessness as ambition. Missing the window for easy intervention.

Somatic: Body’s signals ignored.

Emotional: Emotional warnings dismissed.

Cognitive: Mind rationalizes the deepening problem.

Psychic: The soul misses the early signs entirely, letting subtle disharmony deepen unchecked.
deficient.vignette: Dan misses the early warnings entirely—the subtle unease, the small betrayals of his own values accumulating unnoticed until they’ve hardened into real conflict. Deaf to the first tremors, he only feels the bondage once it’s deep, and wonders how it got so far.

attributes.title: Stage 2: Active Inner Conflict (Intermediate Bondage)
attributes.aspect: Stage 2: active inner conflict. Rajas dominates; sattva weakens; tamas grows. Buddhi is overridden by emotional/desire forces. The ego strengthens through repeated identification. Modern markers: compulsive striving, addictive behaviors, workaholism, emotional volatility, chronic rumination, therapy-resistant patterns.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Stage — a stage of bondage · Valence: Affliction (a degree of descent) · Guṇa: Rajas-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Stage / cycle of descent · Key relation: Second stage — open, active inner conflict.
coordinate.key_relation: Second stage — open, active inner conflict.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of being in stage 2. Strong vairagya cultivation, structured self-inquiry, withdrawal from excessive stimulation, sattva cultivation.
facets.somatic: Body’s pattern of compulsion addressed.
facets.emotional: Emotional storm recognized; vairagya applied.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees the rajas-domination; works to restore buddhi.
facets.psychic: The soul faces its active inner conflict honestly, using the friction between higher and lower as the engine of growth.

balanced.text:
Recognition of being in stage 2. Strong vairagya cultivation, structured self-inquiry, withdrawal from excessive stimulation, sattva cultivation.

Somatic: Body’s pattern of compulsion addressed.

Emotional: Emotional storm recognized; vairagya applied.

Cognitive: Mind sees the rajas-domination; works to restore buddhi.

Psychic: The soul faces its active inner conflict honestly, using the friction between higher and lower as the engine of growth.
balanced.vignette: In the thick of active inner conflict—the war between what he wants and what he knows—Ravi can hold both sides without being torn apart, using the tension as information rather than merely suffering it. The intermediate bondage becomes, for him, a workable crossroads rather than a trap.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forcing intense ascetic practices without preparation, creating breakdown.
facets.somatic: Body broken by forced renunciation.
facets.emotional: Emotional collapse from forced practice.
facets.cognitive: Mental fragmentation from over-zealous practice.
facets.psychic: The soul becomes consumed by the inner war, identifying with the conflict itself.

excess.text:
Forcing intense ascetic practices without preparation, creating breakdown.

Somatic: Body broken by forced renunciation.

Emotional: Emotional collapse from forced practice.

Cognitive: Mental fragmentation from over-zealous practice.

Psychic: The soul becomes consumed by the inner war, identifying with the conflict itself.
excess.vignette: Sam is consumed by his inner conflicts—the warring desires, the endless self-division amplified and rehearsed until the battle is his whole identity. He’s so identified with the struggle that resolving it would feel like a loss, and so the intermediate bondage becomes a permanent home.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Reinforcement of vasanas, identity hardening, loss of inner observer.
facets.somatic: Body deeper in habitual loops.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns hardening.
facets.cognitive: Mind further from witness, deeper in identification.
facets.psychic: The soul represses the conflict, and the unfaced war runs it from below.

deficient.text:
Reinforcement of vasanas, identity hardening, loss of inner observer.

Somatic: Body deeper in habitual loops.

Emotional: Emotional patterns hardening.

Cognitive: Mind further from witness, deeper in identification.

Psychic: The soul represses the conflict, and the unfaced war runs it from below.
deficient.vignette: Dan doesn’t feel his inner conflicts at all—the war between his values and his impulses suppressed so thoroughly that he experiences a false, brittle peace. The active conflict that could, if faced, be worked through runs underground instead, and leaks out sideways in ways he never connects to the buried war.

attributes.title: Stage 3: Late Bondage (Tamas Dominance)
attributes.aspect: Stage 3: late bondage. Tamas overwhelms. Rajas is exhausted. Sattva nearly absent. The veiling power has fully covered discrimination. Modern markers: clinical depression, existential nihilism, addictions or withdrawal, spiritual cynicism, learned helplessness. Even learned scholars cannot see the Self at this stage.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Stage — a stage of bondage · Valence: Affliction (a degree of descent) · Guṇa: Tamas-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Stage / cycle of descent · Key relation: Third stage — entrenched, tamasic late bondage.
coordinate.key_relation: Third stage — entrenched, tamasic late bondage.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recovery requires: gentle sattva restoration, association with sages/stable guides, faith before philosophy, gradual re-awakening of discrimination.
facets.somatic: Body gently restored through nourishing practices.
facets.emotional: Emotional life carefully tended; gentle re-engagement.
facets.cognitive: Mind slowly cleared through faith before deep philosophy.
facets.psychic: Even in deep bondage the soul recognizes its condition — and recognition is the first crack of light in the dark.

balanced.text:
Recovery requires: gentle sattva restoration, association with sages/stable guides, faith before philosophy, gradual re-awakening of discrimination.

Somatic: Body gently restored through nourishing practices.

Emotional: Emotional life carefully tended; gentle re-engagement.

Cognitive: Mind slowly cleared through faith before deep philosophy.

Psychic: Even in deep bondage the soul recognizes its condition — and recognition is the first crack of light in the dark.
balanced.vignette: Even in the heavy grip of late bondage—the tamas, the inertia, the near-total identification—Ravi has found the one small crack of awareness that can begin the turn. Recognizing how deep the dullness runs is itself the first light; he’s begun, slowly, to stir from the heaviest sleep.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Pushing too hard for sattva from tamas state. Forcing what cannot yet be forced.
facets.somatic: Body forced beyond its capacity.
facets.emotional: Emotional shutdown from forced practice.
facets.cognitive: Mental forcing creating deeper collapse.
facets.psychic: The soul sinks fully into inertia and dullness, the dark so complete that even the wish for freedom is gone.

excess.text:
Pushing too hard for sattva from tamas state. Forcing what cannot yet be forced.

Somatic: Body forced beyond its capacity.

Emotional: Emotional shutdown from forced practice.

Cognitive: Mental forcing creating deeper collapse.

Psychic: The soul sinks fully into inertia and dullness, the dark so complete that even the wish for freedom is gone.
excess.vignette: Sam mistakes the numbness of late bondage for peace—the tamas so complete that he calls his inertia serenity, his dullness detachment, his near-total identification ‘acceptance.’ The deepest bondage wears, in him, the mask of the highest freedom, and he can’t tell them apart.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete loss of aspiration (mumukshutva). Identification with suffering itself.
facets.somatic: Body abandoned to tamas state.
facets.emotional: Emotional identification with despair.
facets.cognitive: Mind without any aspiration for change.
facets.psychic:
The soul mistakes its heavy dullness for peace, the deepest bondage wearing the mask of contentment. | **Binding state** | **Its character** |
| --- | --- |
| Kāma–Krodha–Lobha | Lust, anger, greed — the threefold gate of ruin |
| Moha | Delusion, the obfuscation of clear seeing |
| Mada | Arrogance, the intoxication of success |
| Mātsarya / Asūyā | Jealousy and chronic, envious fault-finding |
| Karpaṇya-doṣa | The flaw of self-pity and weakness of resolve |
| Dvandva | Being thrown by the pairs of opposites |

deficient.text:
Complete loss of aspiration (mumukshutva). Identification with suffering itself.

Somatic: Body abandoned to tamas state.

Emotional: Emotional identification with despair.

Cognitive: Mind without any aspiration for change.

Psychic: The soul mistakes its heavy dullness for peace, the deepest bondage wearing the mask of contentment. | **Binding state** | **Its character** |
| --- | --- |
| Kāma–Krodha–Lobha | Lust, anger, greed — the threefold gate of ruin |
| Moha | Delusion, the obfuscation of clear seeing |
| Mada | Arrogance, the intoxication of success |
| Mātsarya / Asūyā | Jealousy and chronic, envious fault-finding |
| Karpaṇya-doṣa | The flaw of self-pity and weakness of resolve |
| Dvandva | Being thrown by the pairs of opposites |
deficient.vignette: Dan is sunk so far into the inertia of late bondage that no awareness of it remains—the dullness total, the identification seamless, no crack of light anywhere. That his heaviness is a stage of bondage, and not simply reality, is a thought that can no longer reach him.

attributes.title: Ajamila’s Redemption (Even at Death, Calling Saves)
attributes.aspect: Ajamila lived a deeply sinful life but happened to name his youngest son Narayana. When death approached and Yama’s messengers came, Ajamila cried for his son ‘Narayana!’ — unconsciously calling the Divine. Vishnu’s messengers came and rescued him. The lesson: even unintentional connection to the Divine has saving power; consciously cultivated devotion has incalculable power. Don’t postpone practice.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: State / turning — redemption even at death · Valence: The redemptive turn · Guṇa: Sattva emerging · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Turning point (grace at the last moment) · Key relation: The saving power of the Name; kin to Gajendra Moksha.
coordinate.ontic_mode: State / turning — redemption even at death
coordinate.causal_status: Turning point (grace at the last moment)
coordinate.key_relation: The saving power of the Name; kin to Gajendra Moksha.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Conscious practice of divine remembrance now, building deep connection before crisis or death.
facets.somatic: Body cultivating connection daily.
facets.emotional: Emotional connection consciously built.
facets.cognitive: Mind weaving divine name into daily life.
facets.psychic: The soul learns that even a single sincere turning toward the Divine, even at the last hour, can redeem a wasted life.

balanced.text:
Conscious practice of divine remembrance now, building deep connection before crisis or death.

Somatic: Body cultivating connection daily.

Emotional: Emotional connection consciously built.

Cognitive: Mind weaving divine name into daily life.

Psychic: The soul learns that even a single sincere turning toward the Divine, even at the last hour, can redeem a wasted life.
balanced.vignette: At the very end, when everything else had failed, Ravi found that a single sincere turning toward the good could redeem a lifetime of drift—like Ajamila, saved at death by one true call. He holds this not as a loophole but as evidence that grace reaches even the last hour.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Magical thinking — ‘just chant at death and I’m fine.’ Postponing real practice expecting last-minute redemption.
facets.somatic: Body lives unrestrained expecting deathbed salvation.
facets.emotional: Emotional license rationalized by salvation-doctrine.
facets.cognitive: Mental gambling on last-moment grace.
facets.psychic: The soul presumes upon last-minute grace, postponing all change in the confidence of rescue.

excess.text:
Magical thinking — ‘just chant at death and I’m fine.’ Postponing real practice expecting last-minute redemption.

Somatic: Body lives unrestrained expecting deathbed salvation.

Emotional: Emotional license rationalized by salvation-doctrine.

Cognitive: Mental gambling on last-moment grace.

Psychic: The soul presumes upon last-minute grace, postponing all change in the confidence of rescue.
excess.vignette: Sam treats Ajamila’s redemption as a license to sin freely now and ‘call out at the end’—deferring all repentance to a deathbed rescue he’s sure he can summon. The story of last-moment grace has become his excuse for a lifetime of un-repentance, gaming a mercy he presumes upon.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No divine connection at all. Death approaches without any preparation. Maximum spiritual unpreparedness.
facets.somatic: Body never engaged in any practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without divine connection.
facets.cognitive: Mind has no divine recall available.
facets.psychic: The soul despairs of redemption, believing its failings have placed it beyond all reach of grace.

deficient.text:
No divine connection at all. Death approaches without any preparation. Maximum spiritual unpreparedness.

Somatic: Body never engaged in any practice.

Emotional: Emotional life without divine connection.

Cognitive: Mind has no divine recall available.

Psychic: The soul despairs of redemption, believing its failings have placed it beyond all reach of grace.
deficient.vignette: Dan believes his errors have damned him beyond any redemption—no possibility that a sincere turning, even late, could save him, only the grinding certainty of the ledger. The mercy the story offers, that grace reaches even the last hour, is a hope he can’t believe in, and so he carries his past as a sentence.

attributes.title: Encountering Death (Death as Social Phenomenon)
attributes.aspect: Death is the unresolved problem at the root of all human anxiety. Yet death is not what we think—it is just the absence of life from this body, like darkness is absence of light. Don’t fight death; search for life. Find the deathless flame within.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Psychic · Ontic mode: State / turning — the encounter with death · Valence: The awakening shock · Guṇa: Tamas opening to Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Turning point (death as teacher) · Key relation: Death as the great teacher; kin to Maraṇa (Dim 9) and Śani (Dim 18).
coordinate.ontic_mode: State / turning — the encounter with death
coordinate.key_relation: Death as the great teacher; kin to Maraṇa (Dim 9) and Śani (Dim 18).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When the deathless within is realized, death loses its sting. One knows death never happens to oneself—only to others’ perception. The flame of life continues.
facets.somatic: Body relaxed in face of mortality, no chronic death-anxiety in cells, healthy aging.
facets.emotional: Peace with mortality, no terror of death, emotional freedom to live fully.
facets.cognitive: Clear understanding that what is essential is deathless, freedom from fear of ending.
facets.psychic: The soul lets the deaths it witnesses awaken it, using mortality as the great teacher it is.

balanced.text:
When the deathless within is realized, death loses its sting. One knows death never happens to oneself—only to others’ perception. The flame of life continues.

Somatic: Body relaxed in face of mortality, no chronic death-anxiety in cells, healthy aging.

Emotional: Peace with mortality, no terror of death, emotional freedom to live fully.

Cognitive: Clear understanding that what is essential is deathless, freedom from fear of ending.

Psychic: The soul lets the deaths it witnesses awaken it, using mortality as the great teacher it is.
balanced.vignette: When death entered his circle, Ravi met it fully—present at the bedside, unflinching before the fact of it, letting the encounter deepen rather than flee him. He treats death not as an obscenity to be hidden but as a teacher to be faced, and the facing has made him more alive.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Obsession with avoiding death—building endless securities, medical phobias, escape into pleasure to forget mortality. All life becomes a fight with death.
facets.somatic: Health anxiety, hypochondria, body in chronic fear-tension.
facets.emotional: Death-terror coloring all experience, inability to enjoy life because of death-fear.
facets.cognitive: Mind constantly calculating how to avoid death, life wasted in defense.
facets.psychic: The soul becomes morbidly fixated on death, hollowing out the life still to be lived.

excess.text:
Obsession with avoiding death—building endless securities, medical phobias, escape into pleasure to forget mortality. All life becomes a fight with death.

Somatic: Health anxiety, hypochondria, body in chronic fear-tension.

Emotional: Death-terror coloring all experience, inability to enjoy life because of death-fear.

Cognitive: Mind constantly calculating how to avoid death, life wasted in defense.

Psychic: The soul becomes morbidly fixated on death, hollowing out the life still to be lived.
excess.vignette: Sam has grown morbidly preoccupied with death—drawn to it, brooding on it, romanticizing the encounter until the fascination has become its own dark withdrawal from life. What could be a sobering teacher has become, for him, a fixation that pulls him under.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Denying mortality completely, living as if death doesn’t exist—shallow living, no urgency to find the deathless within.
facets.somatic: Reckless body, addiction, no care for vital signals.
facets.emotional: Emotional shallowness, no depth that mortality brings.
facets.cognitive: Surface living, no contemplation of fundamental questions, denial.
facets.psychic: The soul keeps death at arm’s length as something that happens to others, refusing its lesson.

deficient.text:
Denying mortality completely, living as if death doesn’t exist—shallow living, no urgency to find the deathless within.

Somatic: Reckless body, addiction, no care for vital signals.

Emotional: Emotional shallowness, no depth that mortality brings.

Cognitive: Surface living, no contemplation of fundamental questions, denial.

Psychic: The soul keeps death at arm’s length as something that happens to others, refusing its lesson.
deficient.vignette: Dan avoids death entirely—looks away from the dying, changes the subject, keeps the whole fact of mortality at a managed distance he never lets close. The encounter that could sober and deepen him is one he’s arranged never to have, and so he lives as though he won’t die.

attributes.title: Bondage at Death (Anta-Prashna)
attributes.aspect: Death is the moment when all patterns established in life come to fruition. The senses that grasped objects in life continue grasping at death. Liberation requires breaking these patterns BEFORE death. The whole spiritual life is preparation for the final moment when consciousness must navigate its accumulated patterns.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — the last thought shaping the next birth · Valence: Neutral — a decisive law · Guṇa: Mixed · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism (the state at death, anta-praśna) · Key relation: The final thought (Gītā 8.6); kin to Maraṇa (Dim 9).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework — the last thought shaping the next birth
coordinate.key_relation: The final thought (Gītā 8.6); kin to Maraṇa (Dim 9).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Life lived as preparation for death—not morbidly but consciously dis-entangling from grasper-grasped patterns.
facets.somatic: Body’s habit-patterns systematically loosened.
facets.emotional: Emotional attachments worked through consciously.
facets.cognitive: Mind progressively freed from clinging patterns.
facets.psychic: The soul prepares its whole life for the final passage, knowing the mind at death gathers up the life that led to it.

balanced.text:
Life lived as preparation for death—not morbidly but consciously dis-entangling from grasper-grasped patterns.

Somatic: Body’s habit-patterns systematically loosened.

Emotional: Emotional attachments worked through consciously.

Cognitive: Mind progressively freed from clinging patterns.

Psychic: The soul prepares its whole life for the final passage, knowing the mind at death gathers up the life that led to it.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to loosen, while he still can, the attachments that would bind him at the end—the grasping, the unfinished grievances, the frantic clutch at life. Working now on the knots that tighten at death, he faces his own ending less bound, more free.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Morbid death-preparation that crowds out actual living.
facets.somatic: Body neglected in obsessive death-focus.
facets.emotional: Emotional life dominated by mortality.
facets.cognitive: Mind in constant death-anticipation.
facets.psychic: The soul becomes anxiously preoccupied with dying well, straining over the end.

excess.text:
Morbid death-preparation that crowds out actual living.

Somatic: Body neglected in obsessive death-focus.

Emotional: Emotional life dominated by mortality.

Cognitive: Mind in constant death-anticipation.

Psychic: The soul becomes anxiously preoccupied with dying well, straining over the end.
excess.vignette: Sam obsesses over ‘dying well’—so anxiously preoccupied with the state of his mind at death, so busy managing his future ending, that he forgets to live the present. The wise concern with bondage at death has become a morbid fixation that steals the life he’s still in.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No death-awareness at all. Life lived without thought for the moment of departure.
facets.somatic: Body habits strengthened without thought of departure.
facets.emotional: Emotional attachments deepened without freedom-work.
facets.cognitive: Mind unprepared for the great transition.
facets.psychic: The soul gives no thought to the passage, and arrives at death wholly unprepared and afraid.

deficient.text:
No death-awareness at all. Life lived without thought for the moment of departure.

Somatic: Body habits strengthened without thought of departure.

Emotional: Emotional attachments deepened without freedom-work.

Cognitive: Mind unprepared for the great transition.

Psychic: The soul gives no thought to the passage, and arrives at death wholly unprepared and afraid.
deficient.vignette: Dan gives no thought to the end—the attachments that will bind him at death left entirely untended, the whole question of how he’ll meet it never once raised. That the grip he lives with will tighten unbearably at the last is a warning he’s never heard, and so he lets the knots grow.

attributes.title: Self-Inquiry into Body’s Fate (Bhasmantam Idam Shariram)
attributes.aspect: Let vital air merge with the immortal Air; let this body be reduced to ashes. The body’s destiny is ash—knowing this transforms how one lives. The mind is asked to remember all that has been done.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice / method — a means to freedom · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the memento-mori inquiry (‘this body ends in ashes’) that spurs detachment.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — a means to freedom
coordinate.key_relation: the memento-mori inquiry (‘this body ends in ashes’) that spurs detachment.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Holding the body’s mortality as a constant background awareness without morbidity. This awareness gives life its sacred urgency.
facets.somatic: Body cared for knowing it returns to ash—proper care without clinging.
facets.emotional: Emotional depth from mortality-awareness, no surface frivolity.
facets.cognitive: Mind illumined by death-awareness, prioritizes wisely.
facets.psychic: The soul contemplates that this body ends in ash, and loosens its grip on the form it must release.

balanced.text:
Holding the body’s mortality as a constant background awareness without morbidity. This awareness gives life its sacred urgency.

Somatic: Body cared for knowing it returns to ash—proper care without clinging.

Emotional: Emotional depth from mortality-awareness, no surface frivolity.

Cognitive: Mind illumined by death-awareness, prioritizes wisely.

Psychic: The soul contemplates that this body ends in ash, and loosens its grip on the form it must release.
balanced.vignette: Contemplating that his own body will one day be ash, Ravi finds the reflection clarifying rather than morbid—it sorts the essential from the trivial and loosens his grip on the perishable. ‘This body ends in ashes’ has become, for him, a sober lens that makes the days matter more.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Morbid death-obsession that paralyzes life. Or harsh body-rejection because it will become ash.
facets.somatic: Body neglected since it ‘will be ash anyway.’
facets.emotional: Depressive death-focus, paralyzing morbidity.
facets.cognitive: Mind dominated by death-thoughts, life-energy drained.
facets.psychic: The soul moralizes the body’s mortality into a grim contempt for embodied life.

excess.text:
Morbid death-obsession that paralyzes life. Or harsh body-rejection because it will become ash.

Somatic: Body neglected since it ‘will be ash anyway.’

Emotional: Depressive death-focus, paralyzing morbidity.

Cognitive: Mind dominated by death-thoughts, life-energy drained.

Psychic: The soul moralizes the body’s mortality into a grim contempt for embodied life.
excess.vignette: Sam broods on ‘this body ends in ash’ until it’s darkened into nihilism—if it all burns, nothing matters, effort is pointless, and the contemplation meant to clarify has become an excuse for despair and neglect. The sober reflection has curdled into a morbid futility.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total denial of body’s mortality—living as if it’s permanent. Endless plans, no urgency, surface engagement.
facets.somatic: Body abused as if invincible.
facets.emotional: Emotional shallowness from death-denial.
facets.cognitive: Mind in endless future-planning without death-awareness, no depth.
facets.psychic: The soul ignores the body’s certain end, living as if the form were permanent.

deficient.text:
Total denial of body’s mortality—living as if it’s permanent. Endless plans, no urgency, surface engagement.

Somatic: Body abused as if invincible.

Emotional: Emotional shallowness from death-denial.

Cognitive: Mind in endless future-planning without death-awareness, no depth.

Psychic: The soul ignores the body’s certain end, living as if the form were permanent.
deficient.vignette: Dan never once reflects on his body’s fate—the fact that it will end simply not entertained, the sobering inquiry never made. Without the clarifying pressure of his own mortality, he can’t sort the essential from the trivial, and spends his one perishable life as though it were endless.

attributes.title: Phase 1: Karma-Yoga Purification (Ethical Stabilization)
attributes.aspect: First phase of transformation: karma-yoga (selfless action) and yama-niyama (ethical living) reduce the influence of rajas (restlessness) and tamas (inertia). This creates psychological stabilization—a precondition for deeper work. Without this foundation, deeper inquiry produces only conceptual results.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Stage — a phase of liberation · Valence: Cultivated stage · Guṇa: Rajas refined to Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Stage in the liberation sequence · Key relation: First phase — karma-yoga purification (ethical stabilization); kin to Karma Yoga (Dim 10).
coordinate.key_relation: First phase — karma-yoga purification (ethical stabilization); kin to Karma Yoga (Dim 10).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Disciplined ethical living, selfless action, gradual reduction of rajas-tamas. Stable psychological foundation forming.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in dharmic action without compulsion.
facets.emotional: Emotional stability from ethical alignment.
facets.cognitive: Mind progressively clearer through right action.
facets.psychic: The soul stabilizes its ethical ground through selfless action, the first phase that makes all later freedom possible.

balanced.text:
Disciplined ethical living, selfless action, gradual reduction of rajas-tamas. Stable psychological foundation forming.

Somatic: Body engaged in dharmic action without compulsion.

Emotional: Emotional stability from ethical alignment.

Cognitive: Mind progressively clearer through right action.

Psychic: The soul stabilizes its ethical ground through selfless action, the first phase that makes all later freedom possible.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has done the ground-floor work—stabilizing his life ethically through selfless action, cleaning up his conduct so the deeper work has a foundation. The karma-yoga purification isn’t glamorous, but it’s steadied him; the house is being built on level ground.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Believing karma-yoga alone produces liberation. Stuck in this phase indefinitely.
facets.somatic: Body endlessly busy in selfless action without progressing.
facets.emotional: Emotional pride in service.
facets.cognitive: Mental confusion that action = liberation.
facets.psychic: The soul over-invests in good works as identity, the doer-ego inflating through service.

excess.text:
Believing karma-yoga alone produces liberation. Stuck in this phase indefinitely.

Somatic: Body endlessly busy in selfless action without progressing.

Emotional: Emotional pride in service.

Cognitive: Mental confusion that action = liberation.

Psychic: The soul over-invests in good works as identity, the doer-ego inflating through service.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned ethical purification into scrupulous perfectionism—obsessing over the purity of every act, paralyzed by the fear of impure motive, so fixated on cleaning up his conduct that he never moves past the cleaning. The foundation-work has become the whole building.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Skipping ethical foundation, jumping to inquiry without base. Pseudo-spirituality.
facets.somatic: Body undisciplined.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity continuing despite spiritual claims.
facets.cognitive: Mind discussing Brahman without foundation.
facets.psychic: The soul skips the ethical foundation, and its higher aspirations have no stable ground to stand on.

deficient.text:
Skipping ethical foundation, jumping to inquiry without base. Pseudo-spirituality.

Somatic: Body undisciplined.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity continuing despite spiritual claims.

Cognitive: Mind discussing Brahman without foundation.

Psychic: The soul skips the ethical foundation, and its higher aspirations have no stable ground to stand on.
deficient.vignette: Dan wants the heights without the ground-floor work—reaching for insight and bliss while his actual conduct stays selfish and unstabilized. Skipping the ethical purification that every deeper practice rests on, he keeps building on sand, and the structure keeps sliding.

attributes.title: Phase 2: Buddhi Restoration (Cognitive Re-education)
attributes.aspect: Second phase: cognitive re-education through receptive hearing and sustained contemplation. The buddhi (executive cognition) is progressively restored to its rightful role of commanding manas (emotional appraisal). Without this, the strongest emotions and impulses continue to override clear thinking.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Stage — a phase of liberation · Valence: Cultivated stage · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Stage in the liberation sequence · Key relation: Second phase — buddhi restoration (cognitive re-education); restores Buddhi (Dim 3 / 28).
coordinate.key_relation: Second phase — buddhi restoration (cognitive re-education); restores Buddhi (Dim 3 / 28).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Buddhi progressively reclaiming authority. Clear thinking commands emotional appraisal. Wise responses replace blind reactions.
facets.somatic: Body’s responses guided by buddhi.
facets.emotional: Emotions still felt but no longer overriding wisdom.
facets.cognitive: Mind discriminating, restored to executive function.
facets.psychic: The soul re-educates its discernment, the intellect restored to its rightful place as charioteer.

balanced.text:
Buddhi progressively reclaiming authority. Clear thinking commands emotional appraisal. Wise responses replace blind reactions.

Somatic: Body’s responses guided by buddhi.

Emotional: Emotions still felt but no longer overriding wisdom.

Cognitive: Mind discriminating, restored to executive function.

Psychic: The soul re-educates its discernment, the intellect restored to its rightful place as charioteer.
balanced.vignette: Through patient study and reflection, Ravi has re-educated his own understanding—replacing the confused convictions that ran his life with clearer ones. The restoration of the intellect isn’t dry to him; it’s the slow correction of the lens through which he sees everything.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Over-intellectualization. Buddhi dominates and dismisses manas entirely. Cold rationality.
facets.somatic: Body disconnected from feeling.
facets.emotional: Emotional life suppressed by buddhi.
facets.cognitive: Mental dominance creating affective deadness.
facets.psychic: The soul over-relies on cognitive re-education, mistaking clarified concepts for transformation.

excess.text:
Over-intellectualization. Buddhi dominates and dismisses manas entirely. Cold rationality.

Somatic: Body disconnected from feeling.

Emotional: Emotional life suppressed by buddhi.

Cognitive: Mental dominance creating affective deadness.

Psychic: The soul over-relies on cognitive re-education, mistaking clarified concepts for transformation.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned cognitive re-education into endless intellectualizing—forever refining his concepts, correcting his understanding, so absorbed in getting the ideas right that the ideas never change his life. The buddhi is beautifully restored and entirely unapplied.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Manas continues to override buddhi. Emotions and impulses drive behavior; intellect rationalizes after the fact.
facets.somatic: Body driven by emotional impulse.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity continues dominantly.
facets.cognitive: Mind serves emotions rather than commanding them.
facets.psychic: The soul never repairs its discernment, and the runaway senses keep the chariot off the road.

deficient.text:
Manas continues to override buddhi. Emotions and impulses drive behavior; intellect rationalizes after the fact.

Somatic: Body driven by emotional impulse.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity continues dominantly.

Cognitive: Mind serves emotions rather than commanding them.

Psychic: The soul never repairs its discernment, and the runaway senses keep the chariot off the road.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s understanding runs on the same confused convictions it always has—untested, un-reeducated, the distorted lens never corrected. The patient cognitive work of replacing false ideas with true ones is one he’s never undertaken, and so he keeps seeing, and suffering, through the old distortions.

attributes.title: Phase 4: Ego Release (Identity Deconstruction)
attributes.aspect: Fourth phase: progressive dis-identification through neti-neti (not this, not this). The ego (ahamkara) is recognized as superimposition, not essence. Roles can be performed without identity-fusion. This is existential liberation—the false self dissolves while functional self continues.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Stage — a phase of liberation · Valence: Cultivated stage · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Stage in the liberation sequence · Key relation: Fourth phase — ego release (identity deconstruction); dissolves Asmitā (Dim 19).
coordinate.key_relation: Fourth phase — ego release (identity deconstruction); dissolves Asmitā (Dim 19).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Ego seen as function not essence. Roles played without fusion. Existential liberation while continuing to function.
facets.somatic: Body functions without identification.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without ego-defense.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees ego as appearance, not reality.
facets.psychic: The soul releases its grip on a fixed identity, the constructed self gently deconstructed.

balanced.text:
Ego seen as function not essence. Roles played without fusion. Existential liberation while continuing to function.

Somatic: Body functions without identification.

Emotional: Emotional life without ego-defense.

Cognitive: Mind sees ego as appearance, not reality.

Psychic: The soul releases its grip on a fixed identity, the constructed self gently deconstructed.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun the hardest work—loosening his grip on the ego-identity itself, letting the ‘I’ he’s defended his whole life quietly deconstruct. It’s not a collapse but a release; as the false center dissolves, he finds not less of himself but more, and freer.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Premature ego-destruction creating psychological harm. Confusing dissociation with realization.
facets.somatic: Body without coherent function.
facets.emotional: Emotional dissolution rather than liberation.
facets.cognitive: Mental fragmentation called transcendence.
facets.psychic: The soul forces ego-dissolution prematurely, dismantling a self it still needs to function.

excess.text:
Premature ego-destruction creating psychological harm. Confusing dissociation with realization.

Somatic: Body without coherent function.

Emotional: Emotional dissolution rather than liberation.

Cognitive: Mental fragmentation called transcendence.

Psychic: The soul forces ego-dissolution prematurely, dismantling a self it still needs to function.
excess.vignette: Sam performs ego-death—announcing his ‘dissolved identity,’ theatrically self-deprecating, making a new and subtler ego out of having no ego. The genuine deconstruction has become a costume, and the ‘I’ he claims to have released is running the whole performance.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Ego strongly entrenched. Total identification with role and personality.
facets.somatic: Body completely fused with role identity.
facets.emotional: Emotional life serving ego-defense.
facets.cognitive: Mind entirely organized around ‘I am the doer.’
facets.psychic: The soul clings to its ego intact, and the final freedom stays barred by the one who would be free.

deficient.text:
Ego strongly entrenched. Total identification with role and personality.

Somatic: Body completely fused with role identity.

Emotional: Emotional life serving ego-defense.

Cognitive: Mind entirely organized around ‘I am the doer.’

Psychic: The soul clings to its ego intact, and the final freedom stays barred by the one who would be free.
deficient.vignette: Dan clutches his ego-identity with both hands—every threat to his self-image an existential attack, the ‘I’ defended at all costs, no loosening imaginable. The identity-deconstruction the path asks for is not something he’s failing at; it’s something he’d sooner die than attempt.

attributes.title: Phase 5: Abidance (Stable Harmony)
attributes.aspect: Final phase: stable abidance in realization. All four functions of antahkarana harmoniously integrated. Cognition is clear; affect is peaceful; will is non-compulsive; identity is witness-awareness. The vasanas (latent tendencies) progressively wither. This is jivanmukti—liberation while living.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Stage — a phase of liberation · Valence: Cultivated stage · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Stage in the liberation sequence · Key relation: Fifth phase — abidance (stable harmony); kin to Sahaja Avastha (Dim 26).
coordinate.key_relation: Fifth phase — abidance (stable harmony); kin to Sahaja Avastha (Dim 26).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: All faculties harmonized in the witnessing awareness. Vasanas progressively withering. Effortless abidance.
facets.somatic: Body functions in spontaneous flow.
facets.emotional: Emotional life as movement in awareness, not bondage.
facets.cognitive: Mind crystal clear, witnessing presence stable.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in stable abidance, the realized freedom no longer a peak experience but the ordinary ground.

balanced.text:
All faculties harmonized in the witnessing awareness. Vasanas progressively withering. Effortless abidance.

Somatic: Body functions in spontaneous flow.

Emotional: Emotional life as movement in awareness, not bondage.

Cognitive: Mind crystal clear, witnessing presence stable.

Psychic: The soul rests in stable abidance, the realized freedom no longer a peak experience but the ordinary ground.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come, after the long work, to a stable harmony—an abidance in his own depth that no longer needs defending or achieving, simply lived. The striving has quieted into a steady, unforced rest; he abides, and the abiding is the fruit of everything before it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Claiming abidance while still showing all the markers of earlier stages. Spiritual delusion.
facets.somatic: Body shows signs of compulsion despite claims.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity continues underneath claim.
facets.cognitive: Mental claim without genuine stable witnessing.
facets.psychic: The soul clings to its abidance as a special attainment, the very gripping disturbing the rest.

excess.text:
Claiming abidance while still showing all the markers of earlier stages. Spiritual delusion.

Somatic: Body shows signs of compulsion despite claims.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity continues underneath claim.

Cognitive: Mental claim without genuine stable witnessing.

Psychic: The soul clings to its abidance as a special attainment, the very gripping disturbing the rest.
excess.vignette: Sam claims to have ‘arrived’ at stable abidance—announcing his permanent peace—while his actual life still churns with the old reactivity. He’s mistaken a good week for the summit, and the premature claim of abidance is itself the unrest it denies.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Never reaching abidance. Spiritual life remains as effort, never effortless.
facets.somatic: Body in continued strain of practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional life still battleground.
facets.cognitive: Mind never knows effortless witnessing.
facets.psychic: The soul cannot stabilize, its glimpses of freedom flickering and lost for want of abidance.

deficient.text:
Never reaching abidance. Spiritual life remains as effort, never effortless.

Somatic: Body in continued strain of practice.

Emotional: Emotional life still battleground.

Cognitive: Mind never knows effortless witnessing.

Psychic: The soul cannot stabilize, its glimpses of freedom flickering and lost for want of abidance.
deficient.vignette:
Dan has no stable ground anywhere—no abidance, no settled harmony, only the endless oscillation of a self that’s done none of the work that leads to rest. The steady dwelling in one’s own depth that crowns the path is, for him, not even a rumor; he’s never left the churn.

Phase 3: Manas Re-orientation. **The affective integration in which the feeling-mind is gradually re-oriented from its old cravings and aversions toward the new center — the heart re-educated to find its rest in being rather than in becoming.

The arc is coherent: first the conduct is stabilized (karma-yoga), then the intellect is re-educated to see correctly (buddhi), then the emotions are integrated around the new seeing (manas), then the ego-identity itself is released, and finally there is stable abidance in the natural state — the freedom that no longer needs to be maintained by effort.

attributes.title: Ending of Desire as Freedom (Jivan Mukti)
attributes.aspect: Jivan Mukti is freedom in life itself—not after death. It is the ending of desire through understanding, not suppression. Understood desire releases its energy for spiritual upward movement. This is the difference between repression and transcendence.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State — a mark of freedom · Valence: The fruit · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: desirelessness as freedom — jīvanmukti; the extinguishing of craving.
coordinate.key_relation: desirelessness as freedom — jīvanmukti; the extinguishing of craving.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Desires understood and naturally dropped. Energy conserved, available for higher movement. Freedom in living—Jivan Mukti.
facets.somatic: Body free from compulsive pursuit, energy retained.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from desire-suffering, contentment.
facets.cognitive: Mind free from future-projection, present-centered, available for higher dimensions.
facets.psychic: The soul finds freedom not in getting what it wants but in the ending of the wanting itself, full where it once was hungry.

balanced.text:
Desires understood and naturally dropped. Energy conserved, available for higher movement. Freedom in living—Jivan Mukti.

Somatic: Body free from compulsive pursuit, energy retained.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from desire-suffering, contentment.

Cognitive: Mind free from future-projection, present-centered, available for higher dimensions.

Psychic: The soul finds freedom not in getting what it wants but in the ending of the wanting itself, full where it once was hungry.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has watched his compulsive desires quietly thin—not suppressed but outgrown, as the fulfillment he chased in objects he increasingly finds in being itself. The ending of desire isn’t a loss to him; it’s the freedom of no longer being dragged, and it feels like coming home.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced desire-suppression—body and mind in war with natural urges. Energy goes underground, distorts.
facets.somatic: Body in conflict with suppressed desires, somatic symptoms.
facets.emotional: Suppressed desires creating anxiety, eventual eruption.
facets.cognitive: Mind obsessed with what it suppresses, false ascetic identity.
facets.psychic: The soul forces desirelessness, suppressing natural wants and calling the deadness freedom.

excess.text:
Forced desire-suppression—body and mind in war with natural urges. Energy goes underground, distorts.

Somatic: Body in conflict with suppressed desires, somatic symptoms.

Emotional: Suppressed desires creating anxiety, eventual eruption.

Cognitive: Mind obsessed with what it suppresses, false ascetic identity.

Psychic: The soul forces desirelessness, suppressing natural wants and calling the deadness freedom.
excess.vignette: Sam wages a violent war on desire—forcing every wish down, suspecting every pleasure, mistaking a grim, white-knuckled suppression for the natural ending the sages describe. He’s crushing the wanting rather than outgrowing it, and the war binds him as tightly as the desire ever did.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total slavery to endless desire—every fulfilled desire creates new desires. Constant becoming, never being. Energy completely dispersed.
facets.somatic: Body exhausted by endless pursuit, depleted.
facets.emotional: Chronic dissatisfaction, suffering from wanting.
facets.cognitive: Mind perpetually planning, no present-being possible.
facets.psychic: The soul remains ruled by desire, mistaking each new craving for life itself.

deficient.text:
Total slavery to endless desire—every fulfilled desire creates new desires. Constant becoming, never being. Energy completely dispersed.

Somatic: Body exhausted by endless pursuit, depleted.

Emotional: Chronic dissatisfaction, suffering from wanting.

Cognitive: Mind perpetually planning, no present-being possible.

Psychic: The soul remains ruled by desire, mistaking each new craving for life itself.
deficient.vignette: Dan is ruled entirely by his desires—each craving obeyed, each appetite chased, the very notion of their ending unimaginable. That freedom might lie in the quieting of compulsive desire, rather than its endless feeding, is a possibility he’s never once entertained.

attributes.title: Spontaneity (Dharma as Natural Being)
attributes.aspect: True spontaneity is impossible while mind interferes—mind always brings the past. Spontaneous action happens only in the present, fresh, never repeating. Through samadhi, one comes back to dharma—the natural, spontaneous nature without distortion by mind.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: State — a mark of freedom · Valence: The fruit · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: spontaneous right action — dharma as natural being; kin to Sahaj Karma (Dim 16).
coordinate.key_relation: spontaneous right action — dharma as natural being; kin to Sahaj Karma (Dim 16).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Natural responses arising freshly in each moment—not reactions from past memory but live engagement with present. Dharma flowing.
facets.somatic: Body acts freshly, not from habit—each moment new.
facets.emotional: Emotional responses fresh, not preprogrammed reactions.
facets.cognitive: Mind serves rather than dominates—responses flow from present awareness, not past patterns.
facets.psychic: The soul acts spontaneously from its true nature, right action flowing without calculation.

balanced.text:
Natural responses arising freshly in each moment—not reactions from past memory but live engagement with present. Dharma flowing.

Somatic: Body acts freshly, not from habit—each moment new.

Emotional: Emotional responses fresh, not preprogrammed reactions.

Cognitive: Mind serves rather than dominates—responses flow from present awareness, not past patterns.

Psychic: The soul acts spontaneously from its true nature, right action flowing without calculation.
balanced.vignette: Meera acts, more and more, from her own depth without contrivance—her rightness arising naturally, her dharma expressed as spontaneous being rather than effortful rule-following. The good, in her, has stopped being a should and become a flow; she does right because it’s simply what she is.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Pseudo-spontaneity—claiming acting from present while actually reactive. Or random impulsivity mistaken for spontaneity.
facets.somatic: Body in impulsive movements claimed as spontaneity.
facets.emotional: Reactive emotions claimed as authentic responses.
facets.cognitive: Confusion between reactive and responsive.
facets.psychic: The soul mistakes mere impulsiveness for spontaneity, license dressed as freedom.

excess.text:
Pseudo-spontaneity—claiming acting from present while actually reactive. Or random impulsivity mistaken for spontaneity.

Somatic: Body in impulsive movements claimed as spontaneity.

Emotional: Reactive emotions claimed as authentic responses.

Cognitive: Confusion between reactive and responsive.

Psychic: The soul mistakes mere impulsiveness for spontaneity, license dressed as freedom.
excess.vignette: Sam performs spontaneity—the studied naturalness, the announced effortlessness, the ‘authentic self’ carefully curated. He’s turned natural being into a pose, and the self-consciousness of the performance is the very proof that the spontaneity is manufactured.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete dominance by past-patterns—every situation triggers automatic reaction from memory. No present-meeting possible.
facets.somatic: Body locked in habitual patterns.
facets.emotional: Emotional life entirely reactive from past.
facets.cognitive: Mind functioning as repetitive program, no fresh response.
facets.psychic: The soul acts only from rule and fear, never trusting the natural rightness of its own depths.

deficient.text:
Complete dominance by past-patterns—every situation triggers automatic reaction from memory. No present-meeting possible.

Somatic: Body locked in habitual patterns.

Emotional: Emotional life entirely reactive from past.

Cognitive: Mind functioning as repetitive program, no fresh response.

Psychic: The soul acts only from rule and fear, never trusting the natural rightness of its own depths.
deficient.vignette: Dan is entirely rule-bound and reactive—every action either a grinding should or an automatic reflex, no natural spontaneity anywhere. The effortless right-action that flows from realized being is foreign to him; he white-knuckles the good when he does it at all.

attributes.title: Egolessness as Jivanmukti
attributes.aspect: Jivanmukti is not adding something but the dropping of ego. Ego can take any form—even spiritual ego is dangerous. Watching for ego’s arising is the key practice. When ‘I’ doesn’t arise, only ‘am-ness’ remains—vast, infinite Brahman.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State — a mark of freedom · Valence: The fruit · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: the egoless state; kin to Naiṣkarmya (Dim 16).
coordinate.key_relation: the egoless state; kin to Naiṣkarmya (Dim 16).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Continuous observation prevents ego-formation. Even spiritual achievements don’t create ego. ‘I am’ becomes simply ‘amness’—Jivanmukta.
facets.somatic: Body functions without ego-pride, healthy non-identification.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from ego-driven feelings, peace.
facets.cognitive: Mind without ‘I’ as center, vast amness instead.
facets.psychic: The soul lives free of the ego-knot, present and effective without a separate self at the center.

balanced.text:
Continuous observation prevents ego-formation. Even spiritual achievements don’t create ego. ‘I am’ becomes simply ‘amness’—Jivanmukta.

Somatic: Body functions without ego-pride, healthy non-identification.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from ego-driven feelings, peace.

Cognitive: Mind without ‘I’ as center, vast amness instead.

Psychic: The soul lives free of the ego-knot, present and effective without a separate self at the center.
balanced.vignette: Meera has grown remarkably transparent—her ego so thinned that she meets people and situations without the constant static of self-defense and self-promotion. The egolessness isn’t a void; it’s a spaciousness, and living from it, she’s both more useful to others and more free.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Spiritual ego—‘I am egoless,’ ‘I have achieved’ becoming new ego-trip. Claims of jivanmukti as ultimate ego-achievement.
facets.somatic: Body with subtle spiritual pride, pretentious humility.
facets.emotional: Hidden ego in claiming egolessness.
facets.cognitive: Mind subtly building ego on supposed achievements.
facets.psychic: The soul performs egolessness, a subtle spiritual ego congratulating itself on having none.

excess.text:
Spiritual ego—‘I am egoless,’ ‘I have achieved’ becoming new ego-trip. Claims of jivanmukti as ultimate ego-achievement.

Somatic: Body with subtle spiritual pride, pretentious humility.

Emotional: Hidden ego in claiming egolessness.

Cognitive: Mind subtly building ego on supposed achievements.

Psychic: The soul performs egolessness, a subtle spiritual ego congratulating itself on having none.
excess.vignette: Sam has built a magnificent ego out of egolessness—forever announcing his humility, performing his selflessness, subtly superior in his lack of superiority. The ‘egoless’ identity is the biggest ego in the room, and he cannot see that the one claiming no self is the self, inflated.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Strong ego on everything—every achievement, possession, identity strengthens ‘I.’ Maximum bondage.
facets.somatic: Body as ego-extension—identifying with appearance.
facets.emotional: Emotional life serving ego-defense.
facets.cognitive: Mind entirely organized around ‘I’-construction.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly centered in ego, every experience referred back to ‘me.’

deficient.text:
Strong ego on everything—every achievement, possession, identity strengthens ‘I.’ Maximum bondage.

Somatic: Body as ego-extension—identifying with appearance.

Emotional: Emotional life serving ego-defense.

Cognitive: Mind entirely organized around ‘I’-construction.

Psychic: The soul is wholly centered in ego, every experience referred back to ‘me.’
deficient.vignette: Dan’s ego is total and unthinned—every interaction filtered through self-interest, self-image, self-defense, with no capacity to set the ‘I’ aside even for a moment. The egolessness that marks the free is not something he’s failing to reach; it’s a state he can’t even conceive.

attributes.title: Disciple’s Transformation (Total Receptivity)
attributes.aspect: The whole Adhyatma Upanishad has only one disciple-speaking moment—at the end, after total transformation. Throughout, complete silent receptivity. The transformation isn’t through arguing or asking but through total receptive listening to the master’s communion.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: State — a mark of freedom · Valence: The fruit · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: the ripened, total receptivity of the disciple; kin to Śraddhā (Dim 23).
coordinate.key_relation: the ripened, total receptivity of the disciple; kin to Śraddhā (Dim 23).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Total receptivity to teaching, ready to take the jump into unknown. Transformation through openness rather than analysis.
facets.somatic: Body open and receptive, ready for transformation.
facets.emotional: Emotional openness to whatever the teaching brings.
facets.cognitive: Mind open, not defending current view, ready to be changed.
facets.psychic: The soul becomes wholly receptive, an empty vessel into which the teaching can finally pour.

balanced.text:
Total receptivity to teaching, ready to take the jump into unknown. Transformation through openness rather than analysis.

Somatic: Body open and receptive, ready for transformation.

Emotional: Emotional openness to whatever the teaching brings.

Cognitive: Mind open, not defending current view, ready to be changed.

Psychic: The soul becomes wholly receptive, an empty vessel into which the teaching can finally pour.
balanced.vignette: When Ravi found a genuine teacher, he made himself genuinely receptive—open, humble, willing to be changed, without surrendering his discernment. The total receptivity accelerated him; he could receive what was offered because he neither resisted it nor swallowed it whole.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Blind acceptance without genuine receptivity—pretending openness while protecting identity. Or surrendering to wrong teachers.
facets.somatic: Body forced into passive postures.
facets.emotional: False emotional surrender hiding resistance.
facets.cognitive: Pseudo-receptivity, mental performance of openness.
facets.psychic: The soul’s receptivity slides into dependence, surrendering its discernment along with its resistance.

excess.text:
Blind acceptance without genuine receptivity—pretending openness while protecting identity. Or surrendering to wrong teachers.

Somatic: Body forced into passive postures.

Emotional: False emotional surrender hiding resistance.

Cognitive: Pseudo-receptivity, mental performance of openness.

Psychic: The soul’s receptivity slides into dependence, surrendering its discernment along with its resistance.
excess.vignette: Sam has surrendered so totally to his teacher that he’s dissolved—no judgment of his own, no thought unapproved, his whole self handed over in a receptivity that’s become mere self-erasure. The openness that should transmit wisdom has become a dependence that’s abolished his discernment.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Constant defensive listening—every teaching met with argument, comparison, dismissal. Heart never engaged.
facets.somatic: Body tensed against teaching.
facets.emotional: Heart closed, no emotional opening.
facets.cognitive: Mind constantly arguing, never receiving, blocked transformation.
facets.psychic: The soul stays defended and full of itself, with no room for the teaching to enter.

deficient.text:
Constant defensive listening—every teaching met with argument, comparison, dismissal. Heart never engaged.

Somatic: Body tensed against teaching.

Emotional: Heart closed, no emotional opening.

Cognitive: Mind constantly arguing, never receiving, blocked transformation.

Psychic: The soul stays defended and full of itself, with no room for the teaching to enter.
deficient.vignette: Dan can receive nothing from anyone—too proud to be a student, too defended to be changed, meeting every teaching with resistance and every teacher with suspicion. The receptivity that lets transmission happen is closed in him, and so, however good the teaching, none of it gets in.

attributes.title: Effortless Inaction (Master Idler)
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra’s radical depiction of the liberated being: the alasya-dhurinasya (chief among the lazy). The Jivanmukta is the master idler—even blinking is too much exertion. Yet this is not pathological laziness; it is the cessation of compulsive doership. Actions happen as destiny brings them; the person no longer ‘does’ anything as separate doer.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: State — a mark of freedom · Valence: The fruit · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: action-in-inaction — the ‘master idler’; kin to Naiṣkarmya (Dim 16).
coordinate.key_relation: action-in-inaction — the ‘master idler’; kin to Naiṣkarmya (Dim 16).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Effortless action arising from destiny without ego-doership. Like a child acting from spontaneity, not strategy.
facets.somatic: Body’s actions arise without compulsive force.
facets.emotional: Emotional life flowing without ego-strain.
facets.cognitive: Mind not generating endless plans and projects.
facets.psychic: The soul acts from stillness, accomplishing without strain, doing-by-non-doing in the manner of the sage.

balanced.text:
Effortless action arising from destiny without ego-doership. Like a child acting from spontaneity, not strategy.

Somatic: Body’s actions arise without compulsive force.

Emotional: Emotional life flowing without ego-strain.

Cognitive: Mind not generating endless plans and projects.

Psychic: The soul acts from stillness, accomplishing without strain, doing-by-non-doing in the manner of the sage.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has learned the art of effortless inaction—doing what needs doing without the frantic doer, resting deeply when rest is right, unhurried and yet somehow more effective than the strivers around him. His ‘idleness’ is the ease of someone acting from stillness rather than compulsion.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Pathological laziness disguised as ‘effortless inaction.’ Avoidance of duties claiming liberation.
facets.somatic: Body in actual sloth and neglect.
facets.emotional: Emotional shutdown.
facets.cognitive: Mental laziness justified spiritually.
facets.psychic: The soul mistakes idleness for the effortless action of the free, using ‘non-doing’ to excuse sloth.

excess.text:
Pathological laziness disguised as ‘effortless inaction.’ Avoidance of duties claiming liberation.

Somatic: Body in actual sloth and neglect.

Emotional: Emotional shutdown.

Cognitive: Mental laziness justified spiritually.

Psychic: The soul mistakes idleness for the effortless action of the free, using ‘non-doing’ to excuse sloth.
excess.vignette: Sam invokes ‘effortless inaction’ to justify plain laziness—the dishes, the duties, the responsibilities all left undone in the name of ‘non-doing,’ spiritual language draped over ordinary sloth. The masterful inaction of the sage has become his excuse for doing nothing.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Compulsive doership in everything. Anxious activity, planning, striving.
facets.somatic: Body in constant strained action.
facets.emotional: Emotional life of perpetual ambition.
facets.cognitive: Mind generating endless projects, never resting.
facets.psychic: The soul drives everything by effort, unable to trust the action that arises from rest.

deficient.text:
Compulsive doership in everything. Anxious activity, planning, striving.

Somatic: Body in constant strained action.

Emotional: Emotional life of perpetual ambition.

Cognitive: Mind generating endless projects, never resting.

Psychic: The soul drives everything by effort, unable to trust the action that arises from rest.
deficient.vignette: Dan cannot stop doing—compulsively busy, unable to rest, every silence filled with activity, driven by an inability to simply be. The effortless inaction of the free eludes him entirely; even his leisure is a kind of frantic work, and stillness feels to him like failure.

attributes.title: Householder Jivanmukta (Liberation in Family Life)
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra explicitly affirms: Jivanmukti is possible in family life. The householder Jivanmukta lives without attachment to wife and children, unperturbed by family criticism. King Janaka himself is the model—ruling a kingdom, with wife and family, yet fully liberated. This contradicts the renunciate-only model. Sannyas is not required for liberation.
