coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: State — a mark of freedom · Valence: The fruit · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: liberation lived in family life; kin to Jīvanmukti (Dim 27).
coordinate.key_relation: liberation lived in family life; kin to Jīvanmukti (Dim 27).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Living family life fully while inner liberation is recognized. Householder duties performed without binding.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in family responsibilities without identification.
facets.emotional: Emotional connection with family without attachment.
facets.cognitive: Mind free while body operates in worldly context.
facets.psychic: The soul lives its freedom in the midst of family and work, needing no cave or robe to be free.

balanced.text:
Living family life fully while inner liberation is recognized. Householder duties performed without binding.

Somatic: Body engaged in family responsibilities without identification.

Emotional: Emotional connection with family without attachment.

Cognitive: Mind free while body operates in worldly context.

Psychic: The soul lives its freedom in the midst of family and work, needing no cave or robe to be free.
balanced.vignette: Meera lives an ordinary family life—kids, mortgage, in-laws, the daily grind—and something in her is quietly, already free, the liberation lived right there in the school run and the shared kitchen. She’s proof that freedom needn’t flee the world; it can be found, fully, in the middle of it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Using ‘householder Jivanmukti’ to justify worldly attachment. Doctrine cover for spiritual laziness.
facets.somatic: Body in attachment-driven family pursuits.
facets.emotional: Emotional bondage continues underneath claim.
facets.cognitive: Mental cover for worldly engagement.
facets.psychic: The soul performs spiritual specialness within ordinary life, the householder-sage role a subtle vanity.

excess.text:
Using ‘householder Jivanmukti’ to justify worldly attachment. Doctrine cover for spiritual laziness.

Somatic: Body in attachment-driven family pursuits.

Emotional: Emotional bondage continues underneath claim.

Cognitive: Mental cover for worldly engagement.

Psychic: The soul performs spiritual specialness within ordinary life, the householder-sage role a subtle vanity.
excess.vignette: Sam claims to be a liberated householder—announcing his freedom amid family life—while reacting to his kids and his bills exactly as bound as anyone. He’s grabbed the flattering label of jivanmukta-in-the-world and lives none of it, and the claim itself is the bondage.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Belief that liberation requires monastic renunciation. Family seen as obstacle.
facets.somatic: Body forced into renunciate pose.
facets.emotional: Emotional rejection of family.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees only sannyas as path to freedom.
facets.psychic: The soul believes freedom requires escape from ordinary life, and so never finds it where it stands.

deficient.text:
Belief that liberation requires monastic renunciation. Family seen as obstacle.

Somatic: Body forced into renunciate pose.

Emotional: Emotional rejection of family.

Cognitive: Mind sees only sannyas as path to freedom.

Psychic: The soul believes freedom requires escape from ordinary life, and so never finds it where it stands.
deficient.vignette: Dan believes freedom is impossible in family life—that liberation requires renouncing the world, so his householder existence is, by definition, a spiritual dead end. That freedom might be lived fully in the midst of ordinary domestic life is a possibility he’s ruled out, and so he never seeks it where he actually is.

attributes.title: Disciple’s Joy at Self-Realization
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra Gita Chapter 2 records Janaka’s spontaneous joy after instruction. He bursts forth with verses like ‘Oh wonderful! I am the pure consciousness, beyond all this. How long was I deluded by mistaking myself for the body!’ This natural overflowing recognition is the marker of genuine realization—not learned reverence but spontaneous wonder.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: State — a mark of freedom · Valence: The fruit · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: the joy of self-realization; Harṣa (Dim 9) refined into abiding gladness.
coordinate.key_relation: the joy of self-realization; Harṣa (Dim 9) refined into abiding gladness.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Natural spontaneous wonder upon glimpse of true nature. Joy that is recognition, not learned response.
facets.somatic: Body radiates the joy of recognition.
facets.emotional: Emotional explosion of wonder, gratitude, freedom.
facets.cognitive: Mind exclaims at what was always so.
facets.psychic: The soul knows the incomparable joy of recognizing its own true nature, a gladness springing from being rather than circumstance.

balanced.text:
Natural spontaneous wonder upon glimpse of true nature. Joy that is recognition, not learned response.

Somatic: Body radiates the joy of recognition.

Emotional: Emotional explosion of wonder, gratitude, freedom.

Cognitive: Mind exclaims at what was always so.

Psychic: The soul knows the incomparable joy of recognizing its own true nature, a gladness springing from being rather than circumstance.
balanced.vignette: When the recognition finally dawned, Ravi’s response was a quiet, overflowing joy—not the ego’s triumph but a gladness at what was always already the case. The joy at Self-realization, in him, is unmistakable and humble; it lights him without inflating him.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Performing exuberant joy to convince self or others of realization. Fake spiritual ecstasy.
facets.somatic: Body in forced ecstatic display.
facets.emotional: Emotional performance of bliss.
facets.cognitive: Mental insistence on having realized.
facets.psychic: The soul clings to the joy as an experience to repeat, chasing the high rather than abiding as the source.

excess.text:
Performing exuberant joy to convince self or others of realization. Fake spiritual ecstasy.

Somatic: Body in forced ecstatic display.

Emotional: Emotional performance of bliss.

Cognitive: Mental insistence on having realized.

Psychic: The soul clings to the joy as an experience to repeat, chasing the high rather than abiding as the source.
excess.vignette: Sam performs the ecstasy of realization—the theatrical bliss, the announced awakening, the joy displayed for its effect on his audience. The genuine gladness of recognition has become a show, and the louder he proclaims his joy, the clearer it is that it’s manufactured.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No wonder despite extensive teachings. Concepts accumulated without recognition.
facets.somatic: Body unchanged by teachings.
facets.emotional: Emotional flatness despite spiritual study.
facets.cognitive: Mind has heard but never recognized.
facets.psychic: The soul knows only the qualified pleasures of circumstance, never the unconditioned joy of being.

deficient.text:
No wonder despite extensive teachings. Concepts accumulated without recognition.

Somatic: Body unchanged by teachings.

Emotional: Emotional flatness despite spiritual study.

Cognitive: Mind has heard but never recognized.

Psychic: The soul knows only the qualified pleasures of circumstance, never the unconditioned joy of being.
deficient.vignette: Dan has never known the joy of self-recognition because he’s never come near it—the whole possibility of a gladness rooted in what one already is remains outside his experience. His happiness, such as it is, depends entirely on circumstance, and the deeper joy is one he’s never tasted.

attributes.title: Test of the Disciple’s Realization
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra’s chapter 3 is the master’s test of the disciple. Markers of genuine realization: no need for samadhi-states (already realized); no liberation-seeking (already free); no entanglement in reasoning (transcends categories); spontaneous response. The unrealized still asks; the realized rests.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a test of realization · Valence: Neutral (a criterion) · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: The criterion by which realization is verified.
coordinate.key_relation: The criterion by which realization is verified.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Marks of genuine realization—no further seeking, no anxiety about samadhi, natural resting in being.
facets.somatic: Body lives without spiritual striving.
facets.emotional: Emotional peace not dependent on practices.
facets.cognitive: Mind no longer seeks; rests.
facets.psychic: The soul welcomes the testing of its realization in real life, knowing that only what holds under pressure is real.

balanced.text:
Marks of genuine realization—no further seeking, no anxiety about samadhi, natural resting in being.

Somatic: Body lives without spiritual striving.

Emotional: Emotional peace not dependent on practices.

Cognitive: Mind no longer seeks; rests.

Psychic: The soul welcomes the testing of its realization in real life, knowing that only what holds under pressure is real.
balanced.vignette: When life tested whether Ravi’s realization was real—the sudden loss, the provocation, the crisis—his equanimity held, and he knew the recognition had gone deeper than words. He welcomes such tests rather than fearing them; they show him what’s actually been realized and what’s still just idea.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Claiming realization while still showing markers of seeking. Self-deception.
facets.somatic: Body still in spiritual-seeking mode.
facets.emotional: Emotional anxiety about practice continues.
facets.cognitive: Mind still asks fundamental questions despite claims.
facets.psychic: The soul anxiously tests and re-tests itself, the self-examination becoming its own preoccupation.

excess.text:
Claiming realization while still showing markers of seeking. Self-deception.

Somatic: Body still in spiritual-seeking mode.

Emotional: Emotional anxiety about practice continues.

Cognitive: Mind still asks fundamental questions despite claims.

Psychic: The soul anxiously tests and re-tests itself, the self-examination becoming its own preoccupation.
excess.vignette: Sam avoids every test of his supposed realization—arranging his life to never be provoked, mistaking untested calm for stable freedom, fleeing the situations that would reveal how shallow the realization actually is. He guards his ‘attainment’ precisely because, on some level, he knows it wouldn’t survive examination.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Continuous seeking, samadhi-hunting, liberation-anxiety. The unrealized state in full operation.
facets.somatic: Body in eternal pursuit.
facets.emotional: Emotional life consumed by spiritual ambition.
facets.cognitive: Mind always seeking, never resting.
facets.psychic: The soul never lets its ‘realization’ be tested, mistaking untried ideas for attainment.

deficient.text:
Continuous seeking, samadhi-hunting, liberation-anxiety. The unrealized state in full operation.

Somatic: Body in eternal pursuit.

Emotional: Emotional life consumed by spiritual ambition.

Cognitive: Mind always seeking, never resting.

Psychic: The soul never lets its ‘realization’ be tested, mistaking untried ideas for attainment.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no realization to test—no recognition, deep or shallow, that life’s provocations could confirm or expose. The whole question of whether one’s freedom holds under pressure is moot for him, because there’s nothing yet to hold; he’s still entirely at the mercy of circumstance.

attributes.title: Sukha (Happiness as Inner State)
attributes.aspect: Sukha is the inner experience of happiness/joy—the actual felt-state for which all spiritual practice exists. But ordinary sukha from objects is fleeting; true sukha comes from Bhuma alone. The recognition that happiness can be cultivated as inner state independent of circumstances is liberating.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: State — a mark of freedom · Valence: The fruit · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: inner, causeless happiness (sukha); kin to Ānanda (Dim 5).
coordinate.key_relation: inner, causeless happiness (sukha); kin to Ānanda (Dim 5).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Cultivation of inner sukha independent of circumstances. Happiness as a state, not a response.
facets.somatic: Body’s natural happiness chemistry cultivated through practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional happiness as baseline, not occasional event.
facets.cognitive: Mind oriented toward inner sources of happiness.
facets.psychic: The soul discovers happiness as an inner state independent of conditions, a sukha no loss can finally remove.

balanced.text:
Cultivation of inner sukha independent of circumstances. Happiness as a state, not a response.

Somatic: Body’s natural happiness chemistry cultivated through practice.

Emotional: Emotional happiness as baseline, not occasional event.

Cognitive: Mind oriented toward inner sources of happiness.

Psychic: The soul discovers happiness as an inner state independent of conditions, a sukha no loss can finally remove.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has discovered that happiness is finally an inner state—not delivered by circumstances but resting beneath them, available even on an ordinary grey Tuesday. Having found the source inside, he enjoys good fortune without depending on it, and meets misfortune without being destroyed.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced happiness becoming spiritual bypassing of legitimate sorrow.
facets.somatic: Body performing happiness while exhausted.
facets.emotional: Emotional suppression masquerading as inner sukha.
facets.cognitive: Mental positivity bypassing real engagement.
facets.psychic: The soul grasps at the pleasant state, the clinging disturbing the very happiness it seeks.

excess.text:
Forced happiness becoming spiritual bypassing of legitimate sorrow.

Somatic: Body performing happiness while exhausted.

Emotional: Emotional suppression masquerading as inner sukha.

Cognitive: Mental positivity bypassing real engagement.

Psychic: The soul grasps at the pleasant state, the clinging disturbing the very happiness it seeks.
excess.vignette: Sam chases inner happiness the way others chase money—grasping at bliss states, comparing his contentment, treating ‘inner peace’ as one more prize to acquire and display. The very grasping for the inner sukha keeps it away; he pursues, and the pursuit is the unrest.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Happiness sought entirely externally—every external loss = loss of happiness.
facets.somatic: Body’s happiness depends on external conditions.
facets.emotional: Emotional life rises and falls with circumstances.
facets.cognitive: Mind cannot conceive of inner happiness.
facets.psychic: The soul makes its happiness wholly hostage to circumstance, rising and falling with every event.

deficient.text:
Happiness sought entirely externally—every external loss = loss of happiness.

Somatic: Body’s happiness depends on external conditions.

Emotional: Emotional life rises and falls with circumstances.

Cognitive: Mind cannot conceive of inner happiness.

Psychic: The soul makes its happiness wholly hostage to circumstance, rising and falling with every event.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s happiness is entirely at the mercy of circumstance—up when things go well, crushed when they don’t, no inner source to draw on when the outer supply fails. That happiness could be an inner state, available regardless of conditions, is a discovery he’s never made; he waits, always, for the world to deliver it.

attributes.title: Four Selves Realization (Indra-Prajapati)
attributes.aspect: The four-stage realization of Self through Prajapati-Indra dialogue: (1) Body as Self (most stop here), (2) Dream-self (subtle body), (3) Deep-sleep self (causal), (4) True Self as Spirit (Turiya). Indra’s four-fold returning reveals the importance of not settling at partial realizations. The whole journey takes 101 years.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — the fourfold progressive realization of the Self · Valence: The graded fruit · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Sequence · Key relation: The Indra-Prajāpati dialogue (Chāndogya) — the four selves.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework — the fourfold progressive realization of the Self
coordinate.key_relation: The Indra-Prajāpati dialogue (Chāndogya) — the four selves.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Indra-like persistence—returning each time the realization proves inadequate. Willingness for long journey.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in continuous return to teaching.
facets.emotional: Emotional readiness to admit each realization is incomplete.
facets.cognitive: Mind continuously checking realization against deepest truth.
facets.psychic: The soul, like Indra under Prajāpati, progresses past the bodily and dreaming selves to the true Self beyond them all.

balanced.text:
Indra-like persistence—returning each time the realization proves inadequate. Willingness for long journey.

Somatic: Body engaged in continuous return to teaching.

Emotional: Emotional readiness to admit each realization is incomplete.

Cognitive: Mind continuously checking realization against deepest truth.

Psychic: The soul, like Indra under Prajāpati, progresses past the bodily and dreaming selves to the true Self beyond them all.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has worked his way, like Indra under Prajapati, through the tempting lower answers—I am the body, the dream-self, the sleeper—each held and then seen past, toward the awareness that outlasts them all. The patient refusal to settle for a lower self has carried him steadily toward the true one.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Endless dissatisfaction with all realizations—neurotic seeking that never settles.
facets.somatic: Body exhausted by perpetual seeking.
facets.emotional: Chronic spiritual dissatisfaction.
facets.cognitive: Mind always finding fault, never resting.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on an intermediate self, mistaking a partial realization for the final one.

excess.text:
Endless dissatisfaction with all realizations—neurotic seeking that never settles.

Somatic: Body exhausted by perpetual seeking.

Emotional: Chronic spiritual dissatisfaction.

Cognitive: Mind always finding fault, never resting.

Psychic: The soul fixates on an intermediate self, mistaking a partial realization for the final one.
excess.vignette: Sam has settled prematurely on a lower self and crowned it the ultimate—mistaking a pleasant meditative blankness for the final realization, camped on a lower rung and calling it the summit. Like Indra tempted to stop early, he’s taken a way-station for the destination.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Virochana-like settling at first level—body taken as Self and going home satisfied. Maximum loss.
facets.somatic: Body taken as final reality.
facets.emotional: Emotional satisfaction with body-identity.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t probe deeper into Self-nature.
facets.psychic: The soul takes the first, bodily self as the whole of itself, the higher selves unimagined.

deficient.text:
Virochana-like settling at first level—body taken as Self and going home satisfied. Maximum loss.

Somatic: Body taken as final reality.

Emotional: Emotional satisfaction with body-identity.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t probe deeper into Self-nature.

Psychic: The soul takes the first, bodily self as the whole of itself, the higher selves unimagined.
deficient.vignette: Dan is wholly identified with the bodily self and has never once inquired past it—the dream-self, the deep-sleep-self, the true Self all unexplored, unquestioned. The progressive realization the story maps out has never begun in him; he is the body, full stop, and asks no further.

attributes.attribute: Saint as One Beyond Likes and Dislikes
attributes.title: Saint as One Beyond Likes and Dislikes (Svarajya)
attributes.aspect: He who knows the Self moves at will in all worlds. Beyond grief, beyond fear—he is free, his desires fulfilled. Self-rule (svarajya), self-sovereignty.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State — a mark of freedom · Valence: The fruit · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: self-sovereignty (svarājya) beyond rāga-dveṣa (Dim 19).
coordinate.key_relation: self-sovereignty (svarājya) beyond rāga-dveṣa (Dim 19).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Genuine self-sovereignty—free from compulsive likes and dislikes, moving freely through life.
facets.somatic: Body moves freely without compulsions.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from likes/dislikes.
facets.cognitive: Mind self-ruled, not driven by preferences.
facets.psychic: The soul attains self-rule, no longer pushed and pulled by every like and dislike, sovereign in its own being.

balanced.text:
Genuine self-sovereignty—free from compulsive likes and dislikes, moving freely through life.

Somatic: Body moves freely without compulsions.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from likes/dislikes.

Cognitive: Mind self-ruled, not driven by preferences.

Psychic: The soul attains self-rule, no longer pushed and pulled by every like and dislike, sovereign in its own being.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has grown remarkably free of the tyranny of his own likes and dislikes—able to meet what comes without being yanked toward the pleasant and away from the unpleasant. This self-rule is a quiet sovereignty; his peace no longer depends on the world arranging itself to his preferences.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Claiming sovereignty while still ruled—pretended freedom while deeply compulsive.
facets.somatic: Body still driven by hidden compulsions.
facets.emotional: Suppressed likes/dislikes continuing operation.
facets.cognitive: Mental claim of freedom hiding bondage.
facets.psychic: The soul forces a flat indifference, mistaking the suppression of preference for freedom from it.

excess.text:
Claiming sovereignty while still ruled—pretended freedom while deeply compulsive.

Somatic: Body still driven by hidden compulsions.

Emotional: Suppressed likes/dislikes continuing operation.

Cognitive: Mental claim of freedom hiding bondage.

Psychic: The soul forces a flat indifference, mistaking the suppression of preference for freedom from it.
excess.vignette: Sam forces a grim indifference—suppressing every preference, denying every like and dislike, mistaking a flattened numbness for freedom from them. The saint’s ease beyond preference has become, in him, an effortful war on his own responses, and the war is its own bondage.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete slavery to likes and dislikes—every choice driven by compulsion, no self-rule.
facets.somatic: Body completely driven by attractions/aversions.
facets.emotional: Emotional slavery to preferences.
facets.cognitive: Mind ruled by compulsions, no inner sovereignty.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly governed by its likes and dislikes, a subject in its own house.

deficient.text:
Complete slavery to likes and dislikes—every choice driven by compulsion, no self-rule.

Somatic: Body completely driven by attractions/aversions.

Emotional: Emotional slavery to preferences.

Cognitive: Mind ruled by compulsions, no inner sovereignty.

Psychic: The soul is wholly governed by its likes and dislikes, a subject in its own house.
deficient.vignette: Dan is ruled entirely by his likes and dislikes—lurching toward every pleasure, recoiling from every discomfort, his whole day dictated by the tug of preference. The self-sovereignty that meets the pairs of opposites unmoved is a freedom he’s never tasted; his preferences are his masters.

attributes.title: Sthitaprajña — The One of Stable Wisdom
attributes.aspect: The sthitaprajña, described in the second chapter of the Gītā, is the person of settled wisdom — undisturbed in sorrow and unelated in pleasure, free from craving, fear, and anger, drawing the senses in like a tortoise drawing in its limbs. This is not the coldness of suppression but the stability of one who has found a ground that the pairs of opposites no longer move. As a mark of freedom, it is the most testable: not special experiences or knowledge, but the simple, observable steadiness of a person no longer at the mercy of their own reactions.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: State — the one of stable wisdom (sthitaprajña) · Valence: The fruit / the very portrait of freedom · Guṇa: Sattva, beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: The steady-in-wisdom (Gītā 2.55); kin to Dhṛti and Mati (Dim 9).
coordinate.ontic_mode: State — the one of stable wisdom (sthitaprajña)
coordinate.valence: The fruit / the very portrait of freedom
coordinate.key_relation: The steady-in-wisdom (Gītā 2.55); kin to Dhṛti and Mati (Dim 9).

balanced.label: Balanced — genuine, lived equanimity amid pleasure and pain

facets.overview: When Sthitaprajña is achieved, a person lives from stable wisdom regardless of external circumstances. Intellect is firmly anchored in Self, immune to disturbance by pleasure or pain.
facets.somatic: Stable physical presence in all conditions, body that follows wisdom rather than impulse, healthy steady nervous system, vitality from stable consciousness.
facets.emotional: Profound emotional stability and peace, freedom from emotional reactivity, equanimity in all circumstances, unshakeable inner peace, emotional liberation.
facets.cognitive: Mind stabilized on Self, freedom from mental oscillation, perfect mental clarity, wisdom that doesn’t waver, established inner knowing.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in steady wisdom — undisturbed in gain or loss, the senses gathered like a tortoise’s limbs — established in being rather than tossed by circumstance.

balanced.text:
When Sthitaprajña is achieved, a person lives from stable wisdom regardless of external circumstances. Intellect is firmly anchored in Self, immune to disturbance by pleasure or pain.

Somatic: Stable physical presence in all conditions, body that follows wisdom rather than impulse, healthy steady nervous system, vitality from stable consciousness.

Emotional: Profound emotional stability and peace, freedom from emotional reactivity, equanimity in all circumstances, unshakeable inner peace, emotional liberation.

Cognitive: Mind stabilized on Self, freedom from mental oscillation, perfect mental clarity, wisdom that doesn’t waver, established inner knowing.

Psychic: The soul rests in steady wisdom — undisturbed in gain or loss, the senses gathered like a tortoise’s limbs — established in being rather than tossed by circumstance.
balanced.vignette: When fortune swings—the windfall, the loss, the praise, the blame—something in Meera stays level, a stable wisdom that neither inflates in the good nor collapses in the bad. Her steadiness isn’t coldness; it’s a depth the surface weather no longer reaches.

excess.label: In excess — counterfeit calm; suppression or detachment posing as stability

facets.overview: Apparent Sthitaprajña that’s actually emotional flatness becomes detachment without compassion, inability to engage warmly, false stability masking dissociation.
facets.somatic: Physical disconnection, body that doesn’t respond to legitimate needs, mechanical physical presence.
facets.emotional: Emotional coldness mistaken for stability, inability to feel warmly, false equanimity that’s actually numbness, lack of warm engagement.
facets.cognitive: Mental rigidity disguised as stability, dissociation from feelings, intellectual detachment from human reality, false superiority.
facets.psychic: Steadiness is counterfeited as suppression; the soul forces a flat composure, mistaking numbness for the living equanimity of the sage.

excess.text:
Apparent Sthitaprajña that’s actually emotional flatness becomes detachment without compassion, inability to engage warmly, false stability masking dissociation.

Somatic: Physical disconnection, body that doesn’t respond to legitimate needs, mechanical physical presence.

Emotional: Emotional coldness mistaken for stability, inability to feel warmly, false equanimity that’s actually numbness, lack of warm engagement.

Cognitive: Mental rigidity disguised as stability, dissociation from feelings, intellectual detachment from human reality, false superiority.

Psychic: Steadiness is counterfeited as suppression; the soul forces a flat composure, mistaking numbness for the living equanimity of the sage.
excess.vignette: Sam performs stable wisdom—the measured voice, the studied calm, the announced equanimity—while inwardly as rattled by fortune as anyone. He’s mistaken the demeanor of the sthitaprajna for the state, and the performance cracks the moment life presses hard.

deficient.label: Deficient — wholly at the mercy of reaction; thrown by every fortune and slight

facets.overview: Absence of Sthitaprajña manifests as being swept by every circumstance, no inner foundation, constant reactivity, and instability in face of life’s changes.
facets.somatic: Physical instability based on circumstances, body reactive to every input, nervous system constantly dysregulated.
facets.emotional: Constant emotional reactivity, no inner stability, being tossed by every event, emotional suffering from instability.
facets.cognitive: Mental instability and reactivity, no inner foundation, constant mental fluctuation, inability to maintain clear thinking under pressure.
facets.psychic: The soul is swept by every circumstance; with no stable center, it rises and falls with each pleasure and pain.

deficient.text:
Absence of Sthitaprajña manifests as being swept by every circumstance, no inner foundation, constant reactivity, and instability in face of life’s changes.

Somatic: Physical instability based on circumstances, body reactive to every input, nervous system constantly dysregulated.

Emotional: Constant emotional reactivity, no inner stability, being tossed by every event, emotional suffering from instability.

Cognitive: Mental instability and reactivity, no inner foundation, constant mental fluctuation, inability to maintain clear thinking under pressure.

Psychic: The soul is swept by every circumstance; with no stable center, it rises and falls with each pleasure and pain.
deficient.vignette: Dan is swept fully by every fluctuation—elated by the win, devastated by the loss, his inner state a mirror of his outer luck with no stable depth beneath. The steady wisdom unmoved by fortune is not something he’s failing at; it’s a stability he’s never once felt.

attributes.title: Prasada (Tranquility/Grace)
attributes.aspect: Prasada is a state of profound mental peace, clarity, and grace achieved when the senses are fully controlled and freed from likes and dislikes. It is a state of divine blessing and inner luminosity.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital · Ontic mode: State / grace — tranquil clarity (prasāda) · Valence: The fruit / grace · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual, receiving the Divine · Causal status: Fruit / grace · Key relation: The tranquility of grace (Gītā 2.64–65).
coordinate.key_relation: The tranquility of grace (Gītā 2.64–65).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Prasada is present, there is a natural state of inner grace, peace, and clarity that seems to flow without effort. The mind is luminous and free.
facets.somatic: Radiant physical appearance, light and free physical presence, healthy glow and vitality, sense of ease and grace in movement, natural beauty.
facets.emotional: Profound inner peace and tranquility, emotional grace and beauty, sense of being blessed, emotional lightness and joy, absence of emotional turbulence.
facets.cognitive: Mental clarity and luminosity, freedom from mental confusion, deep peace and stillness, spiritual insight and wisdom, mind functioning at its highest clarity.
facets.psychic: The soul receives the tranquil clarity of grace, a settling that comes as gift rather than achievement.

balanced.text:
When Prasada is present, there is a natural state of inner grace, peace, and clarity that seems to flow without effort. The mind is luminous and free.

Somatic: Radiant physical appearance, light and free physical presence, healthy glow and vitality, sense of ease and grace in movement, natural beauty.

Emotional: Profound inner peace and tranquility, emotional grace and beauty, sense of being blessed, emotional lightness and joy, absence of emotional turbulence.

Cognitive: Mental clarity and luminosity, freedom from mental confusion, deep peace and stillness, spiritual insight and wisdom, mind functioning at its highest clarity.

Psychic: The soul receives the tranquil clarity of grace, a settling that comes as gift rather than achievement.
balanced.vignette: After the striving quieted, Ravi found a settled tranquility he hadn’t manufactured—a grace-like peace that seemed given rather than achieved, and he received it gratefully. The prasada isn’t his accomplishment; it’s what arrived when he stopped grasping, and he holds it with open hands.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive focus on maintaining Prasada can lead to spiritual attachment, inability to engage with normal life challenges, and disconnection from reality.
facets.somatic: Fragility in the face of physical challenges, inability to handle harsh conditions, physical disconnection from reality.
facets.emotional: Emotional detachment from life’s challenges, inability to engage appropriately, emotional escape from necessary action.
facets.cognitive: Mental escapism from reality, inability to handle practical concerns, spiritual bypassing of necessary growth.
facets.psychic: The soul tries to manufacture or possess grace, grasping at what can only be received.

excess.text:
Excessive focus on maintaining Prasada can lead to spiritual attachment, inability to engage with normal life challenges, and disconnection from reality.

Somatic: Fragility in the face of physical challenges, inability to handle harsh conditions, physical disconnection from reality.

Emotional: Emotional detachment from life’s challenges, inability to engage appropriately, emotional escape from necessary action.

Cognitive: Mental escapism from reality, inability to handle practical concerns, spiritual bypassing of necessary growth.

Psychic: The soul tries to manufacture or possess grace, grasping at what can only be received.
excess.vignette: Sam chases tranquility like a prize—meditating for the calm, grasping at grace, judging each day by how peaceful he managed to feel. The very pursuit of prasada keeps it away; grace, by its nature, can’t be seized, and his seizing is the unrest.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Absence of Prasada results in mental turbulence, confusion, and heaviness. There is no inner peace or clarity, only mental struggle.
facets.somatic: Poor health and vitality, dull physical appearance, lack of grace and ease, physical heaviness and tension.
facets.emotional: Mental and emotional turbulence, lack of peace, heaviness and burden, emotional confusion, absence of joy.
facets.cognitive: Mental confusion and fog, constant mental turbulence, lack of clarity, mental heaviness, inability to access wisdom.
facets.psychic: The soul, all self-effort and no surrender, leaves no opening through which grace might enter.

deficient.text:
Absence of Prasada results in mental turbulence, confusion, and heaviness. There is no inner peace or clarity, only mental struggle.

Somatic: Poor health and vitality, dull physical appearance, lack of grace and ease, physical heaviness and tension.

Emotional: Mental and emotional turbulence, lack of peace, heaviness and burden, emotional confusion, absence of joy.

Cognitive: Mental confusion and fog, constant mental turbulence, lack of clarity, mental heaviness, inability to access wisdom.

Psychic: The soul, all self-effort and no surrender, leaves no opening through which grace might enter.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no tranquility and no receptivity to any—churning constantly, closed to the possibility of a peace that might simply be given. The settled grace the teaching describes finds no opening in him; he’s too busy gripping to receive what only comes when the hands relax.

attributes.title: Kunti’s Prayer (Welcome Adversity)
attributes.aspect: Kunti’s prayers contain a radical teaching. She prays that calamities continue to come to her so she keeps seeing Krishna. The insight: prosperity tends to lead to forgetting; difficulty brings remembrance. The most spiritually fortunate are not always the materially fortunate. In daily life: reframe difficulties as opportunities for deepening connection rather than just suffering to escape.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / stance — welcoming adversity as grace · Valence: Virtue / stance · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Condition (adversity received as grace) · Key relation: The prayer that welcomes hardship; kin to Titikṣā (Dim 23).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / stance — welcoming adversity as grace
coordinate.key_relation: The prayer that welcomes hardship; kin to Titikṣā (Dim 23).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Difficulties reframed as spiritual opportunities. Each crisis deepens connection rather than destroying.
facets.somatic: Body endures with grace, learning through difficulty.
facets.emotional: Emotional transformation through trials.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees difficulties as gifts of spiritual deepening.
facets.psychic: The soul, like Kuntī, welcomes adversity as the very thing that keeps it turned toward the Divine.

balanced.text:
Difficulties reframed as spiritual opportunities. Each crisis deepens connection rather than destroying.

Somatic: Body endures with grace, learning through difficulty.

Emotional: Emotional transformation through trials.

Cognitive: Mind sees difficulties as gifts of spiritual deepening.

Psychic: The soul, like Kuntī, welcomes adversity as the very thing that keeps it turned toward the Divine.
balanced.vignette: Like Kunti asking for calamities so she’d never forget the divine, Ravi has come to meet adversity as a teacher rather than an enemy—not courting suffering, but welcoming what it reveals. The hard seasons, faced this way, have deepened him, and he no longer merely dreads them.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Masochistic seeking of difficulty. Spiritual martyrdom. Refusing legitimate well-being.
facets.somatic: Body in unnecessary suffering.
facets.emotional: Emotional addiction to crisis-mode.
facets.cognitive: Mental glorification of suffering.
facets.psychic: The soul courts suffering deliberately, mistaking self-imposed hardship for devotion.

excess.text:
Masochistic seeking of difficulty. Spiritual martyrdom. Refusing legitimate well-being.

Somatic: Body in unnecessary suffering.

Emotional: Emotional addiction to crisis-mode.

Cognitive: Mental glorification of suffering.

Psychic: The soul courts suffering deliberately, mistaking self-imposed hardship for devotion.
excess.vignette: Sam courts suffering masochistically—seeking out hardship to prove his spiritual mettle, performing ‘gratitude for the pain,’ romanticizing every difficulty. Kunti’s wisdom of welcoming adversity has curdled, in him, into a self-punishing pursuit of it, and the pursuit is its own affliction.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Difficulties met with despair, complaint, demands for relief. No spiritual reframe. Maximum suffering.
facets.somatic: Body fights every difficulty.
facets.emotional: Emotional complaint about all hardships.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees no purpose in difficulty.
facets.psychic: The soul flees all adversity, and in comfort forgets the Divine it sought only in distress.

deficient.text:
Difficulties met with despair, complaint, demands for relief. No spiritual reframe. Maximum suffering.

Somatic: Body fights every difficulty.

Emotional: Emotional complaint about all hardships.

Cognitive: Mind sees no purpose in difficulty.

Psychic: The soul flees all adversity, and in comfort forgets the Divine it sought only in distress.
deficient.vignette: Dan flees every adversity and learns from none—arranging his life to avoid all difficulty, resenting each hardship purely as an injustice. The teacher hidden in adversity is one he’s never let close enough to instruct him, and so the same lessons keep circling back, unlearned.

attributes.attribute: Four Steps: Vairagya, Gyan, Uparati, Shanti
attributes.title: Four Steps: Vairagya, Gyan, Uparati, Shanti
attributes.aspect: Four sequential and self-verifying steps. Each step’s authenticity is proven only by the next arising. False vairagya leaves no real gyan; false gyan leaves no real uparati; false uparati leaves no real shanti. Forge ahead only if previous step is verified.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a fourfold ladder to freedom · Valence: Cultivated sequence · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Sequence · Key relation: Dispassion → knowledge → cessation → peace; kin to Sādhana Chatuṣṭaya (Dim 23).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework — a fourfold ladder to freedom
coordinate.key_relation: Dispassion → knowledge → cessation → peace; kin to Sādhana Chatuṣṭaya (Dim 23).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: All four progressively unfolding—non-attachment naturally leading to inner knowing, naturally to total relaxation, naturally to absolute silence.
facets.somatic: Body progressively settling—from sense-dispersal to inner flame to deep rest to silence.
facets.emotional: Emotional unfolding—detachment, illumination, relaxation, peace.
facets.cognitive: Mental progression to total cessation, each stage verified by the next.
facets.psychic: The soul moves through dispassion, knowledge, withdrawal, and peace in their natural order, each preparing the next.

balanced.text:
All four progressively unfolding—non-attachment naturally leading to inner knowing, naturally to total relaxation, naturally to absolute silence.

Somatic: Body progressively settling—from sense-dispersal to inner flame to deep rest to silence.

Emotional: Emotional unfolding—detachment, illumination, relaxation, peace.

Cognitive: Mental progression to total cessation, each stage verified by the next.

Psychic: The soul moves through dispassion, knowledge, withdrawal, and peace in their natural order, each preparing the next.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has moved, over years, through the natural sequence—dispassion loosening his grip, knowledge clarifying his sight, cessation quieting his striving, and a deep peace settling in. Each step prepared the next; he didn’t force the ladder, and the ladder carried him.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Skipping steps—claiming silence without true relaxation, claiming knowing without real non-attachment. Pseudo progression.
facets.somatic: Body forced into appearing meditative without genuine readiness.
facets.emotional: Forced peace without authentic relaxation.
facets.cognitive: Claiming higher states without foundation.
facets.psychic: The soul rushes or forces the steps, grasping at peace before dispassion has ripened.

excess.text:
Skipping steps—claiming silence without true relaxation, claiming knowing without real non-attachment. Pseudo progression.

Somatic: Body forced into appearing meditative without genuine readiness.

Emotional: Forced peace without authentic relaxation.

Cognitive: Claiming higher states without foundation.

Psychic: The soul rushes or forces the steps, grasping at peace before dispassion has ripened.
excess.vignette: Sam rushes the four steps—forcing dispassion he doesn’t feel, claiming knowledge he hasn’t earned, performing a peace that isn’t there. Grasping at the summit, he skips the rungs, and the peace he announces is precisely the peace he lacks.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Stuck at first step or before it—no movement toward vairagya, complete outward dispersion.
facets.somatic: Body completely outward-dispersed.
facets.emotional: Emotional life entirely externally directed.
facets.cognitive: Mind perpetually scattered, no movement toward inner.
facets.psychic: The soul attempts none of the steps, and peace stays a word with no path beneath it.

deficient.text:
Stuck at first step or before it—no movement toward vairagya, complete outward dispersion.

Somatic: Body completely outward-dispersed.

Emotional: Emotional life entirely externally directed.

Cognitive: Mind perpetually scattered, no movement toward inner.

Psychic: The soul attempts none of the steps, and peace stays a word with no path beneath it.
deficient.vignette: Dan has taken none of the steps—no dispassion, no real knowledge, no cessation, no peace—the whole graded path untraveled. The sequence that leads from craving to tranquility is one he’s never set foot on; he’s still entirely at the bottom, and hasn’t noticed there’s a stair.

attributes.title: Three Paths: Bliss, Consciousness, Existence (Sat-Chid-Ananda)
attributes.aspect: The divine has three attributes—Existence (Sat), Consciousness (Chid), Bliss (Ananda). Three paths corresponding to these attributes lead to the same realization. Bliss path is visible (devotees), consciousness path is visible (mindful sages), existence path is invisible (hidden masters).

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: three doors to the one Reality via Being, Consciousness, and Bliss; kin to Satchidananda (Dim 28).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: three doors to the one Reality via Being, Consciousness, and Bliss; kin to Satchidananda (Dim 28).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: One’s natural path chosen wisely. Whichever attribute one cultivates, the other two follow automatically when realization occurs.
facets.somatic: Body shaped by the chosen path—blissful, conscious, or existential.
facets.emotional: Emotional life flowing through the chosen quality.
facets.cognitive: Mind aligned with chosen attribute, opening to the trinity.
facets.psychic: The soul approaches the Absolute through being, consciousness, or bliss, taking the door that fits it.

balanced.text:
One’s natural path chosen wisely. Whichever attribute one cultivates, the other two follow automatically when realization occurs.

Somatic: Body shaped by the chosen path—blissful, conscious, or existential.

Emotional: Emotional life flowing through the chosen quality.

Cognitive: Mind aligned with chosen attribute, opening to the trinity.

Psychic: The soul approaches the Absolute through being, consciousness, or bliss, taking the door that fits it.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has found his own doorway to the Absolute—for him it’s through consciousness, the witnessing awareness, though he honors that others reach it through being or through bliss. He’s taken the path suited to his nature, and it’s carried him toward the one reality all three approach.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forcing a path not natural to one’s being. Or claiming all three without realizing any.
facets.somatic: Body in wrong-path strain.
facets.emotional: Emotional confusion from mixed paths.
facets.cognitive: Mental confusion about which attribute is foundation.
facets.psychic: The soul absolutizes one door, insisting its way is the only way.

excess.text:
Forcing a path not natural to one’s being. Or claiming all three without realizing any.

Somatic: Body in wrong-path strain.

Emotional: Emotional confusion from mixed paths.

Cognitive: Mental confusion about which attribute is foundation.

Psychic: The soul absolutizes one door, insisting its way is the only way.
excess.vignette: Sam fixates on the bliss-path alone—chasing ananda, judging every practice by its sweetness, grasping at the joy while ignoring being and consciousness. He’s seized one of the three approaches as a drug, and the grasping stalls him at the threshold he keeps trying to force.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No path chosen, no attribute cultivated. Spiritual drifting without orientation.
facets.somatic: Body without spiritual practice direction.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without spiritual deepening.
facets.cognitive: Mind without spiritual purpose.
facets.psychic: The soul finds no door at all, the Absolute remaining an abstraction it never approaches.

deficient.text:
No path chosen, no attribute cultivated. Spiritual drifting without orientation.

Somatic: Body without spiritual practice direction.

Emotional: Emotional life without spiritual deepening.

Cognitive: Mind without spiritual purpose.

Psychic: The soul finds no door at all, the Absolute remaining an abstraction it never approaches.
deficient.vignette: Dan approaches the Absolute by no path at all—neither through being, nor consciousness, nor bliss, having never set out toward it in any way. The threefold doorway the teaching describes is one he’s never approached; the reality it leads to isn’t a destination he’s aiming for.

attributes.title: Instant Recipe for Mukti (20-Verse Liberation)
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra’s compressed teaching: One could get instant liberation if only one were to separate oneself from the body and remain effortlessly resting in pure Consciousness. The ‘recipe’: separate yourself from body-mind; rest in pure ‘am-ness’; simply BE without thinking ‘I am’. Nisarga Datta Maharaj reportedly attained enlightenment in less than three years through this sole sadhana.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — a means to freedom · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: a condensed, compact teaching for liberation.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — a means to freedom
coordinate.key_relation: a condensed, compact teaching for liberation.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Direct application: separate, rest, simply BE. Practiced daily for hours, leads to recognition.
facets.somatic: Body relaxed in being-recognition.
facets.emotional: Emotional peace from resting in pure existence.
facets.cognitive: Mind in pure ‘am-ness’ without conceptualizing it.
facets.psychic: The soul takes to heart that liberation can be pointed out in a few verses to a ripe seeker, and lets the simple truth land.

balanced.text:
Direct application: separate, rest, simply BE. Practiced daily for hours, leads to recognition.

Somatic: Body relaxed in being-recognition.

Emotional: Emotional peace from resting in pure existence.

Cognitive: Mind in pure ‘am-ness’ without conceptualizing it.

Psychic: The soul takes to heart that liberation can be pointed out in a few verses to a ripe seeker, and lets the simple truth land.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has taken a concise, essential teaching deeply to heart—working its few verses until they’ve reorganized how he sees—rather than drowning in a thousand texts. The compact recipe has borne fruit precisely because he’s actually applied it, not merely collected it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Repeating ‘I AM’ as mantra rather than simply remaining. Treating the recipe as technique.
facets.somatic: Body strained from repetition.
facets.emotional: Emotional anxiety about doing it right.
facets.cognitive: Mind making ‘I AM’ into another concept.
facets.psychic: The soul collects ‘instant’ teachings as a shortcut, skipping the ripening that makes them work.

excess.text:
Repeating ‘I AM’ as mantra rather than simply remaining. Treating the recipe as technique.

Somatic: Body strained from repetition.

Emotional: Emotional anxiety about doing it right.

Cognitive: Mind making ‘I AM’ into another concept.

Psychic: The soul collects ‘instant’ teachings as a shortcut, skipping the ripening that makes them work.
excess.vignette: Sam wants liberation from a formula by Friday—memorizing the twenty verses, expecting the mere recitation to free him, treating the concise teaching as a magic spell. He’s mistaken a pointer for a push-button, and the impatience is itself the bondage.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Identification with body, ego, qualifications. Never even attempting the recipe.
facets.somatic: Body identified with as ‘me.’
facets.emotional: Emotional life defines identity.
facets.cognitive: Mind takes ‘I am woman, I am 70, I am engineer’ as final.
facets.psychic: The soul dismisses the possibility of swift recognition, certain freedom must be endlessly far.

deficient.text:
Identification with body, ego, qualifications. Never even attempting the recipe.

Somatic: Body identified with as ‘me.’

Emotional: Emotional life defines identity.

Cognitive: Mind takes ‘I am woman, I am 70, I am engineer’ as final.

Psychic: The soul dismisses the possibility of swift recognition, certain freedom must be endlessly far.
deficient.vignette: Dan dismisses any concise path as too good to be true—certain liberation must be endlessly arduous, he ignores the essential teachings entirely. That the heart of it might fit in a few verses, if actually lived, is a possibility he’s ruled out, and so he never tries them.

attributes.title: The Pathless Path (No Steps Required)
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra’s radical teaching: there are no steps in the path. Most spiritual traditions prescribe gradations. Ashtavakra denies them. Even the Mahavakya ‘I am Brahman’ is inferior—because it presupposes ‘I’ looking at ‘Brahman.’ Beyond this, simply BE as ‘Sarvam khalvidam Brahma’ (all this is Brahman)—merge without observer-observed structure.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State / goal — liberation realized · Valence: The goal · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: the immediate, pathless recognition — no steps required; kin to Quantum Flight (Dim 27).
coordinate.key_relation: the immediate, pathless recognition — no steps required; kin to Quantum Flight (Dim 27).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition of pathlessness. No goal to reach because the destination is what one IS.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in life without spiritual-seeking.
facets.emotional: Emotional peace from not-seeking.
facets.cognitive: Mind beyond observer-observed structure.
facets.psychic: The soul senses that, since it is already what it seeks, the deepest truth is pathless — a recognition, not a journey.

balanced.text:
Recognition of pathlessness. No goal to reach because the destination is what one IS.

Somatic: Body engaged in life without spiritual-seeking.

Emotional: Emotional peace from not-seeking.

Cognitive: Mind beyond observer-observed structure.

Psychic: The soul senses that, since it is already what it seeks, the deepest truth is pathless — a recognition, not a journey.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to sense that the freedom he sought was never far off—that the pathless path is simply the recognition of what’s already the case, needing no journey to reach. He still practices, but lightly now, knowing the goal isn’t a destination but a present fact he keeps overlooking.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Claim of pathlessness as license for spiritual laziness. ‘I don’t need to do anything.’
facets.somatic: Body in passive sloth.
facets.emotional: Emotional avoidance disguised as pathlessness.
facets.cognitive: Mental complacency rationalized.
facets.psychic: The soul uses ‘there is no path’ to abandon all practice prematurely, a bypass for the unready.

excess.text:
Claim of pathlessness as license for spiritual laziness. ‘I don’t need to do anything.’

Somatic: Body in passive sloth.

Emotional: Emotional avoidance disguised as pathlessness.

Cognitive: Mental complacency rationalized.

Psychic: The soul uses ‘there is no path’ to abandon all practice prematurely, a bypass for the unready.
excess.vignette: Sam uses ‘there’s no path, you’re already free’ to justify doing nothing—no practice, no discipline, no work, all excused by a realization he’s understood as an idea and not as a fact. The pathless path has become his path of least resistance, and the sloth wears the robe of the highest teaching.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Endless path-walking, ladder-climbing, step-counting. Never arriving because always en-route.
facets.somatic: Body in eternal seeking.
facets.emotional: Emotional exhaustion from path-walking.
facets.cognitive: Mind perpetually on path, never resting.
facets.psychic: The soul, bound to steps and methods, cannot conceive of the immediate recognition the pathless path offers.

deficient.text:
Endless path-walking, ladder-climbing, step-counting. Never arriving because always en-route.

Somatic: Body in eternal seeking.

Emotional: Emotional exhaustion from path-walking.

Cognitive: Mind perpetually on path, never resting.

Psychic: The soul, bound to steps and methods, cannot conceive of the immediate recognition the pathless path offers.
deficient.vignette: Dan is certain that only a long, arduous path could ever work—decades of struggle, endless method—and so the immediate recognition the teaching points to is invisible to him. That freedom might be already the case, missed only for being looked past, is a possibility he can’t entertain.

attributes.title: Bhogi-Yogi Equivalence (Both On Same Plane)
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra’s profound psychological insight: the worldly enjoyer (bhogi) and the renouncing yogi are mentally identical—both fixated on enjoyment, one grasping, one rejecting. Both bind. Liberation requires going beyond both: the witness who neither grasps nor rejects. Renunciation is not a higher state than enjoyment—they are mirror images.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral insight · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: the non-difference of enjoyer and yogi in the realized — both on one plane.
coordinate.key_relation: the non-difference of enjoyer and yogi in the realized — both on one plane.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Witness attitude beyond both pursuit and rejection of enjoyment. Choiceless perception.
facets.somatic: Body engages with what arises, doesn’t pursue or reject.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from both grasping and renouncing.
facets.cognitive: Mind beyond bhogi-yogi duality.
facets.psychic: The soul sees that enjoyer and renouncer can stand on the same ground when both act without attachment.

balanced.text:
Witness attitude beyond both pursuit and rejection of enjoyment. Choiceless perception.

Somatic: Body engages with what arises, doesn’t pursue or reject.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from both grasping and renouncing.

Cognitive: Mind beyond bhogi-yogi duality.

Psychic: The soul sees that enjoyer and renouncer can stand on the same ground when both act without attachment.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to see that the enjoyer and the renunciant can meet on the same ground—that freedom isn’t about what you touch but how you hold it, so a full engagement and a deep letting-go are not opposed. He lives richly and grips nothing, bhogi and yogi at once.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Using doctrine to justify hedonism—‘I’m the witness, so anything is fine.’
facets.somatic: Body in indulgence claiming witness-state.
facets.emotional: Emotional license disguised as transcendence.
facets.cognitive: Mental rationalization of grasping.
facets.psychic: The soul uses the equivalence to justify indulgence, calling its bhoga a yoga.

excess.text:
Using doctrine to justify hedonism—‘I’m the witness, so anything is fine.’

Somatic: Body in indulgence claiming witness-state.

Emotional: Emotional license disguised as transcendence.

Cognitive: Mental rationalization of grasping.

Psychic: The soul uses the equivalence to justify indulgence, calling its bhoga a yoga.
excess.vignette: Sam invokes ‘the enjoyer and the yogi are on the same plane’ to justify pure indulgence—eating, grasping, consuming freely under cover of a non-duality he hasn’t realized. The subtle equivalence has become his license for a hedonism the teaching never sanctioned.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Either compulsive enjoyer or compulsive renunciate. Caught in the duality.
facets.somatic: Body either chasing or rejecting compulsively.
facets.emotional: Emotional life caught in approach-avoidance.
facets.cognitive: Mind locked in dualistic approach to objects.
facets.psychic: The soul splits the world into worldly and spiritual, missing the freedom available within ordinary enjoyment.

deficient.text:
Either compulsive enjoyer or compulsive renunciate. Caught in the duality.

Somatic: Body either chasing or rejecting compulsively.

Emotional: Emotional life caught in approach-avoidance.

Cognitive: Mind locked in dualistic approach to objects.

Psychic: The soul splits the world into worldly and spiritual, missing the freedom available within ordinary enjoyment.
deficient.vignette: Dan holds enjoyment and spirituality as flat opposites—all pleasure suspect, all renunciation holy—condemning the bhogi and idealizing a joyless yogi. That the two might meet in freedom, that one can be fully engaged and fully free, is an equivalence he rigidly denies.

attributes.title: Direct Perception Method (No Sadhana Needed)
attributes.aspect: Sri Atmananda’s distinctive teaching from Ashtavakra Gita: the Direct Perception Method requires no sadhana (preparatory practice). You are made to PERCEIVE Truth positively. Since your own Being is what you most directly experience, references to it must be reasonable. This is contrary to gradual-path teachings that require years of preparation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — a means to freedom · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: direct perception of the Self (aparokṣānubhūti); kin to the Direct Method (Dim 27).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — a means to freedom
coordinate.key_relation: direct perception of the Self (aparokṣānubhūti); kin to the Direct Method (Dim 27).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Direct recognition of one’s own Being as the most immediate experience. Truth is realized, not produced through sadhana.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as Self’s instrument, not as obstruction.
facets.emotional: Emotional life recognized as Self’s expression, not as enemy.
facets.cognitive: Mind directly perceives its own Source without effort.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes that awareness can know itself directly, without intermediary practice, here and now.

balanced.text:
Direct recognition of one’s own Being as the most immediate experience. Truth is realized, not produced through sadhana.

Somatic: Body recognized as Self’s instrument, not as obstruction.

Emotional: Emotional life recognized as Self’s expression, not as enemy.

Cognitive: Mind directly perceives its own Source without effort.

Psychic: The soul recognizes that awareness can know itself directly, without intermediary practice, here and now.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has learned to rest in direct recognition—seeing the Self not as a conclusion to be reached through long method but as the awareness already looking. The direct perception isn’t lazy shortcut; it’s an actual seeing, and it’s shown him that the sought was never absent.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Premature claim of ‘no sadhana needed’ without genuine recognition. Spiritual bypassing.
facets.somatic: Body still unprepared while claiming direct realization.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity continues despite claims.
facets.cognitive: Mental claim without actual perception.
facets.psychic: The soul uses ‘no sādhana needed’ to dismiss all discipline before it is ripe for the direct seeing.

excess.text:
Premature claim of ‘no sadhana needed’ without genuine recognition. Spiritual bypassing.

Somatic: Body still unprepared while claiming direct realization.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity continues despite claims.

Cognitive: Mental claim without actual perception.

Psychic: The soul uses ‘no sādhana needed’ to dismiss all discipline before it is ripe for the direct seeing.
excess.vignette: Sam claims direct realization to skip the work—‘no sadhana needed,’ he says, dispensing with the practice and discipline his actual state still requires. He’s grabbed the language of the direct method as an excuse, and the premature claim is precisely what keeps the direct seeing from opening.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Believing realization requires endless sadhana. Pure gradualism that postpones recognition indefinitely.
facets.somatic: Body in endless preparation without recognition.
facets.emotional: Emotional life in continual preparation mode.
facets.cognitive: Mind believes recognition is always future.
facets.psychic: The soul believes only long practice can ever yield truth, and never tries the direct look.

deficient.text:
Believing realization requires endless sadhana. Pure gradualism that postpones recognition indefinitely.

Somatic: Body in endless preparation without recognition.

Emotional: Emotional life in continual preparation mode.

Cognitive: Mind believes recognition is always future.

Psychic: The soul believes only long practice can ever yield truth, and never tries the direct look.
deficient.vignette: Dan believes only laborious method could ever work—decades of technique, endless practice—with no sense that the Self might be directly seen at all. The immediate recognition the direct method offers is off his map; he’s committed to a long road and never suspects the near one.

attributes.title: Easy Way (No Effort Required)
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra’s distinctive ‘easy way’: liberation comes not through effort but through recognition. Effort presupposes someone (the doer) who is striving—but the doer is precisely the false identity that needs to be seen through. Therefore, paradoxically, the path is easy: simply recognize what is already true. Yet it is the subtlest because effort itself becomes obstacle.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State / goal — liberation realized · Valence: The goal · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: liberation as effortless recognition — no effort required.
coordinate.key_relation: liberation as effortless recognition — no effort required.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition that liberation is easy because it is already accomplished. Effort transformed into receptive recognition.
facets.somatic: Body relaxed, not striving.
facets.emotional: Emotional peace from non-effort.
facets.cognitive: Mind receptive to what is already true.
facets.psychic: The soul discovers the effortless recognition beneath all its striving, the ease that was always available.

balanced.text:
Recognition that liberation is easy because it is already accomplished. Effort transformed into receptive recognition.

Somatic: Body relaxed, not striving.

Emotional: Emotional peace from non-effort.

Cognitive: Mind receptive to what is already true.

Psychic: The soul discovers the effortless recognition beneath all its striving, the ease that was always available.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has discovered the effortless quality of the Self—that the awareness he is requires no straining to be, only a relaxing into what’s already here. The ‘easy way’ isn’t laziness to him; it’s the recognition that the deepest thing can’t be manufactured, only allowed.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Laziness disguised as ‘easy way.’ Avoiding all spiritual work claiming non-effort.
facets.somatic: Body in passive sloth.
facets.emotional: Emotional avoidance disguised as non-doing.
facets.cognitive: Mental laziness rationalized as effortlessness.
facets.psychic: The soul mistakes laziness for the effortless way, using ‘no effort’ to avoid the work it still needs.

excess.text:
Laziness disguised as ‘easy way.’ Avoiding all spiritual work claiming non-effort.

Somatic: Body in passive sloth.

Emotional: Emotional avoidance disguised as non-doing.

Cognitive: Mental laziness rationalized as effortlessness.

Psychic: The soul mistakes laziness for the effortless way, using ‘no effort’ to avoid the work it still needs.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned ‘no effort required’ into a philosophy of sloth—skipping every practice, avoiding every discipline, calling his laziness ‘effortlessness.’ The genuine ease of the Self has become his excuse to do nothing, and the nothing binds him as surely as any striving.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Endless effort that creates more bondage. Never recognizing the always-already nature of Self.
facets.somatic: Body strained in eternal effort.
facets.emotional: Emotional exhaustion from striving.
facets.cognitive: Mind never settles into easy recognition.
facets.psychic: The soul believes only hard effort counts, and strains past the very ease it seeks.

deficient.text:
Endless effort that creates more bondage. Never recognizing the always-already nature of Self.

Somatic: Body strained in eternal effort.

Emotional: Emotional exhaustion from striving.

Cognitive: Mind never settles into easy recognition.

Psychic: The soul believes only hard effort counts, and strains past the very ease it seeks.
deficient.vignette: Dan is certain only hard effort could ever free him—grinding at technique, straining at practice, suspicious of anything that comes easily. The effortless recognition the teaching points to is, to him, too simple to be true, and so he keeps forcing a door that partly just opens.

attributes.title: Moksha (Liberation/Freedom)
attributes.aspect: Moksha is liberation from the cycle of birth and death, from suffering, and from illusion. It represents the ultimate goal of spiritual practice—freedom from bondage to the material world and ego.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State / goal — liberation (mokṣa) · Valence: The goal · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: The fourth puruṣārtha; the aim of the whole framework; kin to Niḥśreyasa (Dim 28).
coordinate.key_relation: The fourth puruṣārtha; the aim of the whole framework; kin to Niḥśreyasa (Dim 28).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: The pursuit of Moksha with proper understanding leads to decreasing attachment, increasing wisdom, freedom from fear and suffering, and a life lived with inner freedom.
facets.somatic: Physical health from decreasing stress and reactivity, body becomes a vehicle for spiritual life, freedom from disease caused by ignorance, light and ethereal quality, natural disengagement from harmful habits.
facets.emotional: Freedom from fear of death and suffering, decreasing emotional reactivity and attachment, emotional peace and bliss, freedom from guilt and shame, emotional detachment without coldness, increasing love and compassion.
facets.cognitive: Freedom from false beliefs and illusions, clarity about true nature of reality, transcendence of ego and self-identification, mental peace and stillness, wisdom about nature of existence.
facets.psychic: The soul orients its whole life toward liberation, the freedom that is its birthright and final good.

balanced.text:
The pursuit of Moksha with proper understanding leads to decreasing attachment, increasing wisdom, freedom from fear and suffering, and a life lived with inner freedom.

Somatic: Physical health from decreasing stress and reactivity, body becomes a vehicle for spiritual life, freedom from disease caused by ignorance, light and ethereal quality, natural disengagement from harmful habits.

Emotional: Freedom from fear of death and suffering, decreasing emotional reactivity and attachment, emotional peace and bliss, freedom from guilt and shame, emotional detachment without coldness, increasing love and compassion.

Cognitive: Freedom from false beliefs and illusions, clarity about true nature of reality, transcendence of ego and self-identification, mental peace and stillness, wisdom about nature of existence.

Psychic: The soul orients its whole life toward liberation, the freedom that is its birthright and final good.
balanced.vignette: Beneath his daily aims, Ravi is oriented toward genuine freedom—not escape, but the release from the deep bondage of his own grasping and fear. The orientation quietly steers his life; moksha, for him, is less a distant heaven than a present loosening he keeps choosing.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive focus on Moksha can lead to escapism, neglect of responsibilities, rejection of natural life, difficulty maintaining relationships, or spiritual bypassing.
facets.somatic: Neglect of physical health and body care, withdrawal from physical world and activity, weakness from lack of engagement, physical detachment and numbness, inability to function in material reality.
facets.emotional: Emotional withdrawal and coldness, loss of normal human relationships, emotional bypassing of psychological work, detachment that looks like enlightenment but is avoidance, lack of compassion for those still engaged.
facets.cognitive: Escapism from life responsibilities, dissociation from present experience, mental disconnection and spaciness, lack of interest in helping others, inability to bring wisdom into practical life.
facets.psychic: The soul turns mokṣa into one more object of craving, the grasping for freedom itself a bondage.

excess.text:
Excessive focus on Moksha can lead to escapism, neglect of responsibilities, rejection of natural life, difficulty maintaining relationships, or spiritual bypassing.

Somatic: Neglect of physical health and body care, withdrawal from physical world and activity, weakness from lack of engagement, physical detachment and numbness, inability to function in material reality.

Emotional: Emotional withdrawal and coldness, loss of normal human relationships, emotional bypassing of psychological work, detachment that looks like enlightenment but is avoidance, lack of compassion for those still engaged.

Cognitive: Escapism from life responsibilities, dissociation from present experience, mental disconnection and spaciness, lack of interest in helping others, inability to bring wisdom into practical life.

Psychic: The soul turns mokṣa into one more object of craving, the grasping for freedom itself a bondage.
excess.vignette: Sam grasps at liberation as one more acquisition—comparing his ‘progress,’ impatient for the prize, treating moksha as a trophy to be won and displayed. The very grasping for freedom is a fresh bondage, and he can’t see that the reaching is the thing that binds.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Ignoring Moksha leads to identifying only with material existence, endless pursuit of worldly pleasures without deeper meaning, inability to transcend ego.
facets.somatic: Physical attachment and identification, addictive behaviors and habits, disease and deterioration from material focus, inability to transcend physical needs, poor health from lack of spiritual grounding.
facets.emotional: Emotional emptiness beneath worldly pleasures, chronic dissatisfaction, inability to experience lasting happiness, emotional confusion and searching, emotional slavery to desires.
facets.cognitive: Mental identification with body and ego, no awareness of deeper purpose, endless worldly thinking, inability to transcend material concerns, mental confusion about meaning of life.
facets.psychic: The soul never conceives of liberation at all, settling for the small freedoms within its cage.

deficient.text:
Ignoring Moksha leads to identifying only with material existence, endless pursuit of worldly pleasures without deeper meaning, inability to transcend ego.

Somatic: Physical attachment and identification, addictive behaviors and habits, disease and deterioration from material focus, inability to transcend physical needs, poor health from lack of spiritual grounding.

Emotional: Emotional emptiness beneath worldly pleasures, chronic dissatisfaction, inability to experience lasting happiness, emotional confusion and searching, emotional slavery to desires.

Cognitive: Mental identification with body and ego, no awareness of deeper purpose, endless worldly thinking, inability to transcend material concerns, mental confusion about meaning of life.

Psychic: The soul never conceives of liberation at all, settling for the small freedoms within its cage.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no interest in freedom—content in his bondage, incurious about liberation, the whole notion of moksha striking him as irrelevant to a life he finds fine as it is. The release the tradition points to isn’t something he’s rejected; it’s a door he’s never noticed, in a wall he’s never questioned.

attributes.title: Samatvam (Equanimity)
attributes.aspect: Samatvam is the state of mental equilibrium where a person remains completely unbothered by contrasting life dualities like pleasure and pain, success and failure, gain and loss. It is the goal of yoga practice.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Virtue / state — equanimity (samatvam) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate / fruit · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition / fruit · Key relation: ‘Yoga is equanimity’ (Gītā 2.48); kin to Upekṣā (Dim 22) and Śama (Dim 8).
coordinate.key_relation: ‘Yoga is equanimity’ (Gītā 2.48); kin to Upekṣā (Dim 22) and Śama (Dim 8).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When equanimity is present, a person responds wisely to both favorable and unfavorable circumstances without emotional reactivity. There is profound inner peace regardless of external conditions.
facets.somatic: Steady and relaxed physical presence in all conditions, consistent energy levels, stable health despite circumstances, calm breathing and nervous system, poised physical bearing.
facets.emotional: Profound emotional peace in the face of pleasure or pain, emotional stability despite life changes, absence of emotional reactivity, equanimous response to success and failure, deep inner contentment.
facets.cognitive: Mental steadiness and peace, clarity of thought regardless of circumstances, wisdom in response, freedom from mental turbulence, mental peace as a stable baseline.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in equanimity, even-minded in gain and loss, the steadiness the Gītā calls yoga itself.

balanced.text:
When equanimity is present, a person responds wisely to both favorable and unfavorable circumstances without emotional reactivity. There is profound inner peace regardless of external conditions.
