This is genuinely fearful because we don’t know what’s on the other side. Yet without this leap, only book-knowledge accumulates.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital · Ontic mode: State / turning — the surrender into the unknown · Valence: The liberating leap · Guṇa: Sattva emerging · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Turning point · Key relation: The trusting leap beyond the known; kin to Śraddhā (Dim 23).
coordinate.ontic_mode: State / turning — the surrender into the unknown
coordinate.key_relation: The trusting leap beyond the known; kin to Śraddhā (Dim 23).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Readiness for the leap when grace presents the moment. Willingness to surrender even cherished spiritual concepts.
facets.somatic: Body ready to let go even of itself in moments of grace.
facets.emotional: Emotional courage to face the unknown without retreat.
facets.cognitive: Mind prepared to relinquish all known frameworks.
facets.psychic: The soul finds the courage to step beyond the known self into the trackless freedom beyond it.

balanced.text:
Readiness for the leap when grace presents the moment. Willingness to surrender even cherished spiritual concepts.

Somatic: Body ready to let go even of itself in moments of grace.

Emotional: Emotional courage to face the unknown without retreat.

Cognitive: Mind prepared to relinquish all known frameworks.

Psychic: The soul finds the courage to step beyond the known self into the trackless freedom beyond it.
balanced.vignette: When the path ran out and only the unknown lay ahead, Ravi leaped—leaving the secure and the certain for a trust he couldn’t justify, and found the ground rose to meet him. The willingness to step into the dark, when the light gives out, has been the making of him.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Reckless ‘leaping’ without preparation—spiritual recklessness that creates pathology.
facets.somatic: Body harm from premature leaps.
facets.emotional: Emotional breakdown from premature surrender.
facets.cognitive: Mental dissolution without integration.
facets.psychic: The soul romanticizes the leap, courting dramatic abandon rather than genuine surrender.

excess.text:
Reckless ‘leaping’ without preparation—spiritual recklessness that creates pathology.

Somatic: Body harm from premature leaps.

Emotional: Emotional breakdown from premature surrender.

Cognitive: Mental dissolution without integration.

Psychic: The soul romanticizes the leap, courting dramatic abandon rather than genuine surrender.
excess.vignette: Sam leaps recklessly and calls it faith—quitting jobs, ending things, flinging himself into chaos on a whim, mistaking impulsiveness for surrender to the unknown. The genuine leap has a groundedness he lacks; his is just falling, dressed up as trust, and he keeps landing hard.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Refusing the leap—accumulating knowledge endlessly without ever taking the existential step.
facets.somatic: Body never opens to the truly new.
facets.emotional: Emotional clinging to the known.
facets.cognitive: Mind accumulates without transformation.
facets.psychic: The soul clings to the known at all costs, refusing the leap that alone leads beyond it.

deficient.text:
Refusing the leap—accumulating knowledge endlessly without ever taking the existential step.

Somatic: Body never opens to the truly new.

Emotional: Emotional clinging to the known.

Cognitive: Mind accumulates without transformation.

Psychic: The soul clings to the known at all costs, refusing the leap that alone leads beyond it.
deficient.vignette: Dan clings to the known with white knuckles—never risking, never leaping, arranging his whole life to avoid the uncertainty that any real growth requires. The step into the unknown that the path eventually demands is one he can’t bring himself to take, and so he stays, safe and stuck, on the near shore.

attributes.title: All-Consuming Devotion to God (Bhakti)
attributes.aspect: All-consuming devotion is genuine bhakti—not a compartment of life but its pervasive quality. Every breath becomes prayer; every encounter becomes meeting the Divine; every action becomes offering. This devotion consumes all lower motivations.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: State — total, absorbing devotion · Valence: The fruit / the highest bhakti · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: Parā-bhakti; kin to Bhakti Yoga (Dim 10) and Bhakti-Samādhi (Dim 26).
coordinate.key_relation: Parā-bhakti; kin to Bhakti Yoga (Dim 10) and Bhakti-Samādhi (Dim 26).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Devotion pervades all of life as natural orientation, not as forced piety. Every moment becomes sacred.
facets.somatic: Body offerings become natural worship.
facets.emotional: Emotional life saturated with devotional warmth.
facets.cognitive: Mind oriented toward Divine in all engagements.
facets.psychic: The soul is consumed by love for the Divine, devotion carrying it past the separate self into union.

balanced.text:
Devotion pervades all of life as natural orientation, not as forced piety. Every moment becomes sacred.

Somatic: Body offerings become natural worship.

Emotional: Emotional life saturated with devotional warmth.

Cognitive: Mind oriented toward Divine in all engagements.

Psychic: The soul is consumed by love for the Divine, devotion carrying it past the separate self into union.
balanced.vignette: Ravi’s devotion has grown whole-hearted—a genuine, all-consuming love for the divine that’s reorganized his priorities around it, warm and total and real. The bhakti isn’t fanaticism; it’s a love that’s made him softer, more surrendered, and quietly more himself.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Sentimental devotion that neglects discrimination—blind emotionalism, dependence on guru-figures.
facets.somatic: Body in excessive ritualism.
facets.emotional: Emotional excess, ecstatic but ungrounded.
facets.cognitive: Mind abdicating discrimination in name of devotion.
facets.psychic: Devotion inflates into fevered emotionalism, the soul mistaking intensity of feeling for depth of love.

excess.text:
Sentimental devotion that neglects discrimination—blind emotionalism, dependence on guru-figures.

Somatic: Body in excessive ritualism.

Emotional: Emotional excess, ecstatic but ungrounded.

Cognitive: Mind abdicating discrimination in name of devotion.

Psychic: Devotion inflates into fevered emotionalism, the soul mistaking intensity of feeling for depth of love.
excess.vignette: Sam’s ‘devotion’ has tipped into fanaticism—the emotional excess, the intolerance of other paths, the ecstatic display mistaken for depth. The all-consuming love has consumed his discernment along with everything else, and the zeal has hardened into something narrow and a little frightening.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Devotion compartmentalized to special occasions—Sunday-only piety. The pervasive transforming quality absent.
facets.somatic: Body without devotional dimension in daily life.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without sacred warmth.
facets.cognitive: Mind functions secularly with occasional religious thought.
facets.psychic: The soul’s heart stays cold and closed, with no love large enough to dissolve it.

deficient.text:
Devotion compartmentalized to special occasions—Sunday-only piety. The pervasive transforming quality absent.

Somatic: Body without devotional dimension in daily life.

Emotional: Emotional life without sacred warmth.

Cognitive: Mind functions secularly with occasional religious thought.

Psychic: The soul’s heart stays cold and closed, with no love large enough to dissolve it.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s practice is entirely dry—all concept, no warmth, the heart shut, devotion a language he doesn’t speak. The whole-hearted love the path can open is closed to him, and without any warmth to soften it, his spirituality stays a cold and effortful thing.

attributes.title: Brahmavidya (Knowledge of Ultimate Reality)
attributes.aspect: Brahmavidya is the highest science of reality—the supreme knowledge that liberates. It is not intellectual information but existential transformation. To know it is to become it. It is the path treaded by the wise, discarding past sins and reaching the highest Purusha.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Framework / knowledge — the knowledge of Brahman · Valence: The liberating knowledge · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Transcendent · Causal status: Means / fruit · Key relation: The highest knowledge (parā vidyā, Dim 28); the direct liberator.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Framework / knowledge — the knowledge of Brahman
coordinate.key_relation: The highest knowledge (parā vidyā, Dim 28); the direct liberator.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Brahmavidya is pursued rightly, knowing and being merge. The seeker’s transformation is simultaneous with their understanding—each insight changes the entire being.
facets.somatic: Body becomes a living instrument of higher knowledge, healthy alignment between body and wisdom, vitality flowing from authentic understanding.
facets.emotional: Emotional integration with truth, deep peace from authentic knowing, freedom from emotional fragmentation between belief and being.
facets.cognitive: Knowledge and being are one, no gap between understanding and embodiment, wisdom that transforms moment to moment.
facets.psychic: The soul devotes itself to the knowledge of the Absolute, the highest science that ends the need for all others.

balanced.text:
When Brahmavidya is pursued rightly, knowing and being merge. The seeker’s transformation is simultaneous with their understanding—each insight changes the entire being.

Somatic: Body becomes a living instrument of higher knowledge, healthy alignment between body and wisdom, vitality flowing from authentic understanding.

Emotional: Emotional integration with truth, deep peace from authentic knowing, freedom from emotional fragmentation between belief and being.

Cognitive: Knowledge and being are one, no gap between understanding and embodiment, wisdom that transforms moment to moment.

Psychic: The soul devotes itself to the knowledge of the Absolute, the highest science that ends the need for all others.
balanced.vignette: Ravi’s knowledge of ultimate reality has stopped being information and become recognition—a lived sense of the ground beneath appearances that steadies how he meets everything. The Brahmavidya is real in him, not as a doctrine he holds but as a reality he increasingly sees from.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive intellectual pursuit of Brahmavidya without transformation creates a ‘well-learned man’ whose being remains untouched. He knows everything about Brahman but doesn’t know Brahman.
facets.somatic: Body remains heavy with old patterns despite intellectual knowledge, no embodied wisdom, mechanical existence.
facets.emotional: Emotional dryness from intellectualism, pride in scriptural knowledge, emotional disconnection from the heart center.
facets.cognitive: Mind crowded with information about ultimate reality, parasitic growth of memory at cost of being, accumulating knowledge that becomes a burden.
facets.psychic: The soul accumulates metaphysical knowledge as an end, erudition standing in for realization.

excess.text:
Excessive intellectual pursuit of Brahmavidya without transformation creates a ‘well-learned man’ whose being remains untouched. He knows everything about Brahman but doesn’t know Brahman.

Somatic: Body remains heavy with old patterns despite intellectual knowledge, no embodied wisdom, mechanical existence.

Emotional: Emotional dryness from intellectualism, pride in scriptural knowledge, emotional disconnection from the heart center.

Cognitive: Mind crowded with information about ultimate reality, parasitic growth of memory at cost of being, accumulating knowledge that becomes a burden.

Psychic: The soul accumulates metaphysical knowledge as an end, erudition standing in for realization.
excess.vignette: Sam wields ‘knowledge of Brahman’ as intellectual mastery—defending the metaphysics, correcting others, living entirely unchanged by the reality he can describe so well. The highest knowledge has become a finite possession he owns and is never moved by.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Absence of any orientation toward Brahmavidya keeps one identified with the temporary, with body and worldly knowledge alone—missing the highest possibility of human life.
facets.somatic: Body fully identified with as self, no transcendence orientation, gross consciousness only.
facets.emotional: Emotional life confined to worldly experiences, no contact with the source, emptiness despite accumulation.
facets.cognitive: Mind dominated by worldly knowledge alone, no awareness of the deathless dimension, ignorance of true Self.
facets.psychic: The soul never turns toward the ultimate knowledge, content with knowing only the perishable.

deficient.text:
Absence of any orientation toward Brahmavidya keeps one identified with the temporary, with body and worldly knowledge alone—missing the highest possibility of human life.

Somatic: Body fully identified with as self, no transcendence orientation, gross consciousness only.

Emotional: Emotional life confined to worldly experiences, no contact with the source, emptiness despite accumulation.

Cognitive: Mind dominated by worldly knowledge alone, no awareness of the deathless dimension, ignorance of true Self.

Psychic: The soul never turns toward the ultimate knowledge, content with knowing only the perishable.
deficient.vignette: For Dan there’s only the world of matter and fact—no ultimate reality to know, the whole idea of Brahmavidya empty mysticism. The knowledge that liberates isn’t something he’s failing to attain; it’s a domain he doesn’t believe exists, and so he’s never sought it.

attributes.title: Bhakti (Devotion vs Love)
attributes.aspect: Bhakti is unconditional reverence for life itself, undirected love that flows toward all existence. Unlike romantic love (which divides into beloved/unloved), devotion has no counterpart. It transforms the entire existence into the beloved.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Faculty / distinction — devotion distinguished from mere love · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Mechanism · Key relation: The capacity underlying Bhakti Yoga (Dim 10), distinguished from ordinary love.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Faculty / distinction — devotion distinguished from mere love
coordinate.key_relation: The capacity underlying Bhakti Yoga (Dim 10), distinguished from ordinary love.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Bhakti is mature, devotion flows toward all existence without exclusion. A stone, tree, river—anything becomes object of devotion. No jealousy or possessiveness arises because the whole is loved.
facets.somatic: Open heart, warmth flowing toward all beings, healthy body radiating universal care.
facets.emotional: Emotional unconditional love, freedom from possessive attachment, deep reverence for all life.
facets.cognitive: Mind united with the whole, no division between loved/unloved, devotional clarity.
facets.psychic: The soul’s devotion ripens from possessive love into selfless adoration that seeks the Beloved’s sake, not its own.

balanced.text:
When Bhakti is mature, devotion flows toward all existence without exclusion. A stone, tree, river—anything becomes object of devotion. No jealousy or possessiveness arises because the whole is loved.

Somatic: Open heart, warmth flowing toward all beings, healthy body radiating universal care.

Emotional: Emotional unconditional love, freedom from possessive attachment, deep reverence for all life.

Cognitive: Mind united with the whole, no division between loved/unloved, devotional clarity.

Psychic: The soul’s devotion ripens from possessive love into selfless adoration that seeks the Beloved’s sake, not its own.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to sense the difference between possessive love and true devotion—the one grasping, the other giving—and his love has ripened toward the selfless kind. He loves the people and the divine in his life without needing to own them, and the loving asks nothing back.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: When devotion is mistaken for love (as in ‘devotion to Rama against Krishna’), it becomes sectarian. Religious conflicts arise from love-phenomenon misunderstood as devotion.
facets.somatic: Body tense in defense of chosen deity, exclusive ritualism damaging health.
facets.emotional: Emotional jealousy disguised as devotion, hatred toward those of other paths, sectarian passions.
facets.cognitive: Mental exclusivism, fanaticism, debating superiority of one’s god, missing the universal.
facets.psychic: The soul’s devotion stays possessive, loving the Divine for what it gives.

excess.text:
When devotion is mistaken for love (as in ‘devotion to Rama against Krishna’), it becomes sectarian. Religious conflicts arise from love-phenomenon misunderstood as devotion.

Somatic: Body tense in defense of chosen deity, exclusive ritualism damaging health.

Emotional: Emotional jealousy disguised as devotion, hatred toward those of other paths, sectarian passions.

Cognitive: Mental exclusivism, fanaticism, debating superiority of one’s god, missing the universal.

Psychic: The soul’s devotion stays possessive, loving the Divine for what it gives.
excess.vignette: Sam’s ‘devotion’ is really need in disguise—clingy, demanding, jealous, a possessive love mistaken for spiritual bhakti. What he calls surrender to the beloved is really a grasping for reassurance, and the neediness masquerading as devotion strains every relationship it touches.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Absence of Bhakti creates a dry intellectual path with no warmth, no heart, no surrender. Cold philosophizing without transformation.
facets.somatic: Closed heart center, restricted breath, lack of emotional vitality.
facets.emotional: Emotional coldness, inability to surrender, mistrust of all that requires devotion.
facets.cognitive: Mind without heart-warmth, harsh intellectualism, no devotional unfolding.
facets.psychic: The soul has no devotion at all, facing existence without a thread of the heart’s longing.

deficient.text:
Absence of Bhakti creates a dry intellectual path with no warmth, no heart, no surrender. Cold philosophizing without transformation.

Somatic: Closed heart center, restricted breath, lack of emotional vitality.

Emotional: Emotional coldness, inability to surrender, mistrust of all that requires devotion.

Cognitive: Mind without heart-warmth, harsh intellectualism, no devotional unfolding.

Psychic: The soul has no devotion at all, facing existence without a thread of the heart’s longing.
deficient.vignette: Dan neither loves nor is devoted—cold to people and to any divine, the whole warm dimension of the heart shut down. The selfless love the teaching distinguishes from possessive love is one he’s never felt in either form; there’s simply no warmth there to refine.

attributes.title: Dhyana (Meditation as No-Music)
attributes.aspect: Dhyana is not an effort but the natural shadow of faith and devotion. It is the cessation of mental chatter, the inner silence in which presence with all existence becomes possible. It cannot be forced—it flowers when the inner ground is prepared.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — meditation as inner silence (‘no-music’) · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: Same as Dhyāna the limb (Dim 11); meditation as the ceasing of inner noise.
coordinate.key_relation: Same as Dhyāna the limb (Dim 11); meditation as the ceasing of inner noise.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When meditation arises spontaneously from devotion, it is effortless—the mind naturally becomes silent in the presence of the beloved (which is now all of existence).
facets.somatic: Body settled in natural stillness, nervous system at rest, breath slow and refined.
facets.emotional: Emotional silence beyond turbulence, peace as natural state, depth of feeling without agitation.
facets.cognitive: Mind naturally silent, no inner talk, awareness without object, presence to existence.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in meditation as a silence beyond all forms, the no-music in which the meditator dissolves.

balanced.text:
When meditation arises spontaneously from devotion, it is effortless—the mind naturally becomes silent in the presence of the beloved (which is now all of existence).

Somatic: Body settled in natural stillness, nervous system at rest, breath slow and refined.

Emotional: Emotional silence beyond turbulence, peace as natural state, depth of feeling without agitation.

Cognitive: Mind naturally silent, no inner talk, awareness without object, presence to existence.

Psychic: The soul rests in meditation as a silence beyond all forms, the no-music in which the meditator dissolves.
balanced.vignette: In deep meditation, Ravi has come to rest in a silence beyond all form—no image, no mantra, no inner ‘music,’ just a spacious, contentless awareness. The formless meditation has opened a stillness the earlier practices only pointed toward, and he abides there, quietly, more and more.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced meditation without devotional foundation becomes an arduous mental discipline that often fails or creates new tensions. The mind cannot be forced into silence.
facets.somatic: Tension from forced posture, headaches from straining, body rebelling against forced stillness.
facets.emotional: Frustration with practice, emotional suppression mistaken for peace, masochistic spirituality.
facets.cognitive: Mind fighting itself to become silent—creating more noise, forced concentration that creates strain.
facets.psychic: The soul grasps at meditative states, straining for silence and so disturbing it.

excess.text:
Forced meditation without devotional foundation becomes an arduous mental discipline that often fails or creates new tensions. The mind cannot be forced into silence.

Somatic: Tension from forced posture, headaches from straining, body rebelling against forced stillness.

Emotional: Frustration with practice, emotional suppression mistaken for peace, masochistic spirituality.

Cognitive: Mind fighting itself to become silent—creating more noise, forced concentration that creates strain.

Psychic: The soul grasps at meditative states, straining for silence and so disturbing it.
excess.vignette: Sam chases meditative experiences relentlessly—the visions, the lights, the blissful states—judging each session by its phenomena, addicted to the show. The formless silence the teaching points to is precisely what his grasping for experiences obscures; he’s after the music and misses the silence.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Without any meditation, life remains in constant chatter, surface-level engagement, no contact with deeper silence. Energy dissipates in endless mental movement.
facets.somatic: Restless body, chronic agitation, nervous system never finds rest.
facets.emotional: Constant emotional turbulence, no inner peace, reactive to every event.
facets.cognitive: Endless inner talk, scattered mind, no foundation for higher states.
facets.psychic: The soul never meditates, its surface never still enough to glimpse the depths.

deficient.text:
Without any meditation, life remains in constant chatter, surface-level engagement, no contact with deeper silence. Energy dissipates in endless mental movement.

Somatic: Restless body, chronic agitation, nervous system never finds rest.

Emotional: Constant emotional turbulence, no inner peace, reactive to every event.

Cognitive: Endless inner talk, scattered mind, no foundation for higher states.

Psychic: The soul never meditates, its surface never still enough to glimpse the depths.
deficient.vignette: Dan never meditates and knows no inner silence at all—the formless stillness beyond thought a dimension he’s never approached. The ‘no-music’ of deep meditation isn’t a state he’s failing to reach; it’s one he’s never once glimpsed, his inner world an unbroken noise.

attributes.title: Two Paths: Awareness and Methods (Samkhya and Yoga)
attributes.aspect: The two great paths to the divine: direct awareness (samkhya) where one simply witnesses all activities; or methods (yoga) where techniques create the conditions for awareness to arise. Most modern minds need methods first; awareness then flowers.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral (two valid roads) · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: the two roads — Sāṁkhya (knowledge) and Yoga (practice), one goal (Gītā 5).
coordinate.key_relation: the two roads — Sāṁkhya (knowledge) and Yoga (practice), one goal (Gītā 5).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Both paths used wisely according to one’s nature—methods to create conditions, awareness as the essence within methods. Neither rigid following of method nor abandoning of technique.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in methods that bring awareness, healthy practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional engagement with practice while awareness deepens.
facets.cognitive: Mind awake through methods, methods serving awareness.
facets.psychic: The soul honors both the path of pure discernment and the path of method, choosing as its temperament requires.

balanced.text:
Both paths used wisely according to one’s nature—methods to create conditions, awareness as the essence within methods. Neither rigid following of method nor abandoning of technique.

Somatic: Body engaged in methods that bring awareness, healthy practice.

Emotional: Emotional engagement with practice while awareness deepens.

Cognitive: Mind awake through methods, methods serving awareness.

Psychic: The soul honors both the path of pure discernment and the path of method, choosing as its temperament requires.
balanced.vignette: Ravi honors both roads—the path of clear seeing that dissolves ignorance and the path of disciplined practice that steadies the mind—using each as the moment requires. He neither dismisses knowledge as dry nor practice as mere effort; the two hands work together toward the one freedom.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mechanical obsession with methods without awareness—doing the technique without being present. Or grasping pure awareness without preparing the vessel.
facets.somatic: Body going through motions without the inner awareness.
facets.emotional: Emotional going-through-motions of practice without transformation.
facets.cognitive: Mental rituals without depth, or mental claims of awareness without ground.
facets.psychic: The soul absolutizes one path, dismissing the other as inferior.

excess.text:
Mechanical obsession with methods without awareness—doing the technique without being present. Or grasping pure awareness without preparing the vessel.

Somatic: Body going through motions without the inner awareness.

Emotional: Emotional going-through-motions of practice without transformation.

Cognitive: Mental rituals without depth, or mental claims of awareness without ground.

Psychic: The soul absolutizes one path, dismissing the other as inferior.
excess.vignette: Sam exalts his chosen path and scorns the other—all lofty knowledge and contempt for ‘mere technique,’ or all busy practice and suspicion of ‘armchair philosophy.’ In elevating one road he’s blinded himself to the other, and the imbalance has stalled him where the two would have carried him.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No path—neither methods nor awareness. Drifting in habitual unconsciousness, no transformation possible.
facets.somatic: Body asleep, mechanical existence.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity without method or awareness.
facets.cognitive: Mind in complete unconsciousness, no movement toward liberation.
facets.psychic: The soul follows neither, drifting without either the knowledge or the technique that frees.

deficient.text:
No path—neither methods nor awareness. Drifting in habitual unconsciousness, no transformation possible.

Somatic: Body asleep, mechanical existence.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity without method or awareness.

Cognitive: Mind in complete unconsciousness, no movement toward liberation.

Psychic: The soul follows neither, drifting without either the knowledge or the technique that frees.
deficient.vignette: Dan travels neither path—no clear seeing, no disciplined practice, the two great roads to freedom both untaken. The choice between knowledge and yoga, or their integration, is moot for him; he’s set out on neither, and so arrives, unsurprisingly, nowhere.

attributes.title: Bhuyo-Bhuya: Pramaa and Pramada (Right Knowing and Heedlessness)
attributes.aspect: Pramada is the spiritual disease of carelessness/heedlessness—forgetting one’s true nature, losing remembrance, slipping into automatic patterns. Even highly developed seekers fall through pramada. The antidote is apramada (heedfulness)—continuous remembrance and care.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral diagnostic · Guṇa: Sattva vs Tamas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: the oscillation of pramā (right knowing) and pramāda (heedlessness); kin to Pramāda (Dim 21).
coordinate.key_relation: the oscillation of pramā (right knowing) and pramāda (heedlessness); kin to Pramāda (Dim 21).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Continuous apramada—heedful attention throughout practice and life. Steady remembrance prevents loss of ground.
facets.somatic: Body in continuous mindful action.
facets.emotional: Emotional alertness without anxiety.
facets.cognitive: Mind in continuous heedful attention.
facets.psychic: The soul cultivates right knowing again and again, vigilant against the heedlessness that lets the truth slip.

balanced.text:
Continuous apramada—heedful attention throughout practice and life. Steady remembrance prevents loss of ground.

Somatic: Body in continuous mindful action.

Emotional: Emotional alertness without anxiety.

Cognitive: Mind in continuous heedful attention.

Psychic: The soul cultivates right knowing again and again, vigilant against the heedlessness that lets the truth slip.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has learned to keep returning to right awareness—catching himself when heedlessness creeps in, gently coming back, again and again, to clear seeing. The vigilance isn’t tense; it’s a patient, repeated homecoming, and the repetition is slowly wearing the groove of wakefulness deeper than the groove of drift.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Anxious hypervigilance disguised as apramada—exhausting forced attention.
facets.somatic: Body tense from forced vigilance.
facets.emotional: Emotional anxiety from constant alertness.
facets.cognitive: Mental exhaustion from forced heedfulness.
facets.psychic: The soul’s vigilance hardens into anxious self-monitoring.

excess.text:
Anxious hypervigilance disguised as apramada—exhausting forced attention.

Somatic: Body tense from forced vigilance.

Emotional: Emotional anxiety from constant alertness.

Cognitive: Mental exhaustion from forced heedfulness.

Psychic: The soul’s vigilance hardens into anxious self-monitoring.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned vigilance against heedlessness into an anxious scrupulosity—monitoring every lapse, punishing every drift, so tense with the effort of staying aware that the tension has become its own heedlessness. The watchfulness meant to free him exhausts him instead.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Chronic pramada—life lived in heedless drift. Spiritual ground constantly lost.
facets.somatic: Body in mechanical heedless action.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity from heedlessness.
facets.cognitive: Mind in chronic forgetfulness of higher truth.
facets.psychic: The soul lapses into chronic heedlessness, the truth glimpsed and forgotten, glimpsed and forgotten.

deficient.text:
Chronic pramada—life lived in heedless drift. Spiritual ground constantly lost.

Somatic: Body in mechanical heedless action.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity from heedlessness.

Cognitive: Mind in chronic forgetfulness of higher truth.

Psychic: The soul lapses into chronic heedlessness, the truth glimpsed and forgotten, glimpsed and forgotten.
deficient.vignette: Dan lives in unbroken heedlessness—no vigilance, no returning, the mind adrift from morning to night with no thread of awareness pulling it back. That right knowing must be reclaimed again and again against the pull of drift is a discipline he’s never practiced; he simply drifts.

attributes.title: Bhrigu’s Tapas (Progressive Truth-Discovery)
attributes.aspect: Bhrigu’s progressive inquiry through tapas: Varuna gives clue (‘that from which beings are born…’). Bhrigu doesn’t just remember—he goes and PRACTICES tapas to know. Each level reached through tapas: food, then prana, then mind, then intellect, then ananda (bliss). The model of authentic discovery vs. intellectual acceptance.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — a means to freedom · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: Bhṛgu’s graded inquiry to Ānanda (Taittirīya); kin to Bhrigu-Varuni Vidya (Dim 25).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — a means to freedom
coordinate.key_relation: Bhṛgu’s graded inquiry to Ānanda (Taittirīya); kin to Bhrigu-Varuni Vidya (Dim 25).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Authentic tapas-based discovery at each level. Not accepting teaching intellectually but realizing through practice.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in genuine tapas, discovering each level.
facets.emotional: Emotional commitment to discovery over hearsay.
facets.cognitive: Mind discovers through practice what cannot be merely learned.
facets.psychic: The soul, like Bhṛgu, discovers truth in stages through sustained inquiry, each level surrendered for the deeper.

balanced.text:
Authentic tapas-based discovery at each level. Not accepting teaching intellectually but realizing through practice.

Somatic: Body engaged in genuine tapas, discovering each level.

Emotional: Emotional commitment to discovery over hearsay.

Cognitive: Mind discovers through practice what cannot be merely learned.

Psychic: The soul, like Bhṛgu, discovers truth in stages through sustained inquiry, each level surrendered for the deeper.
balanced.vignette: Like Bhrigu inquiring step by step—is it food? breath? mind?—Ravi presses his self-inquiry deeper through patient discipline, each answer held and then seen past toward a truer one. The austerity isn’t self-punishment; it’s the sustained pressure that lets each layer give way to the next.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mechanical tapas without genuine inquiry—going through motions without breakthrough.
facets.somatic: Body in ritualistic tapas without realization.
facets.emotional: Emotional dryness in mechanical practice.
facets.cognitive: Mental tapas-performance without inquiry.
facets.psychic: The soul clings to an intermediate finding as final, stopping the inquiry too soon.

excess.text:
Mechanical tapas without genuine inquiry—going through motions without breakthrough.

Somatic: Body in ritualistic tapas without realization.

Emotional: Emotional dryness in mechanical practice.

Cognitive: Mental tapas-performance without inquiry.

Psychic: The soul clings to an intermediate finding as final, stopping the inquiry too soon.
excess.vignette: Sam does austerity for its own sake—the fasting, the hardship, the self-denial worn as a badge—or else stops his inquiry at a comfortable early answer and calls it the truth. The progressive discovery has become either grim mortification or a premature conclusion; either way, the deepening stops.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Accepting teachings intellectually without ever doing one’s own tapas-inquiry. Hearsay-spirituality.
facets.somatic: Body never engaged in genuine tapas.
facets.emotional: Emotional acceptance without discovery.
facets.cognitive: Mind takes teachings as given, no personal investigation.
facets.psychic: The soul never undertakes the sustained inquiry, and truth stays a rumor it has not tested.

deficient.text:
Accepting teachings intellectually without ever doing one’s own tapas-inquiry. Hearsay-spirituality.

Somatic: Body never engaged in genuine tapas.

Emotional: Emotional acceptance without discovery.

Cognitive: Mind takes teachings as given, no personal investigation.

Psychic: The soul never undertakes the sustained inquiry, and truth stays a rumor it has not tested.
deficient.vignette: Dan neither inquires nor disciplines himself—no sustained questioning, no austerity to give it force, the whole progressive discovery of truth untried. The patient, deepening inquiry that Bhrigu models is one he’s never begun; he’s asked no hard question and applied no pressure to find its answer.

attributes.title: Karma-Jnana Samuchchaya (Combined Action-Knowledge)
attributes.aspect: The crucial teaching: neither pure karma nor pure jnana suffices alone. Karma alone (action without wisdom) keeps in samsara; jnana alone (knowledge without grounding action) is dangerous incomplete realization. Both together—karma as the body, jnana as the soul—is the complete path.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Vital · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral (a debated synthesis) · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: the combined path of works and knowledge; kin to Vidya-and-Avidya (Dim 28).
coordinate.key_relation: the combined path of works and knowledge; kin to Vidya-and-Avidya (Dim 28).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Integrated path: action grounded in wisdom; wisdom expressed through action. Neither escape into knowledge nor lost in activity.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in karma supported by jnana.
facets.emotional: Emotional balance between action and contemplation.
facets.cognitive: Mind unifies action with knowledge.
facets.psychic: The soul weaves action and knowledge together, neither renouncing the world nor bound by it.

balanced.text:
Integrated path: action grounded in wisdom; wisdom expressed through action. Neither escape into knowledge nor lost in activity.

Somatic: Body engaged in karma supported by jnana.

Emotional: Emotional balance between action and contemplation.

Cognitive: Mind unifies action with knowledge.

Psychic: The soul weaves action and knowledge together, neither renouncing the world nor bound by it.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has woven his understanding and his action into one cloth—what he knows shaping what he does, what he does testing what he knows—neither a paralyzed knower nor a blind actor. The integration has made his life coherent; his insight and his conduct pull, at last, in the same direction.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Either over-emphasis on action alone or over-emphasis on knowledge alone. Imbalance toward one extreme.
facets.somatic: Body either compulsively active or completely withdrawn.
facets.emotional: Emotional life unbalanced toward one pole.
facets.cognitive: Mind either escapist-contemplative or compulsive-active.
facets.psychic: The soul over-balances toward one — action without wisdom, or wisdom without engagement.

excess.text:
Either over-emphasis on action alone or over-emphasis on knowledge alone. Imbalance toward one extreme.

Somatic: Body either compulsively active or completely withdrawn.

Emotional: Emotional life unbalanced toward one pole.

Cognitive: Mind either escapist-contemplative or compulsive-active.

Psychic: The soul over-balances toward one — action without wisdom, or wisdom without engagement.
excess.vignette: Sam splits knowledge from action—all insight and no changed behavior, or all busy doing and no wisdom to guide it. The combination the teaching urges eludes him; he keeps the knowing and the doing in separate rooms, and each, cut off from the other, goes lame.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Neither action nor knowledge cultivated. Mere drifting in samsara.
facets.somatic: Body without spiritual karma or jnana.
facets.emotional: Emotional drift without engagement.
facets.cognitive: Mind without action or knowledge orientation.
facets.psychic: The soul has neither living knowledge nor purified action, ungrounded in both.

deficient.text:
Neither action nor knowledge cultivated. Mere drifting in samsara.

Somatic: Body without spiritual karma or jnana.

Emotional: Emotional drift without engagement.

Cognitive: Mind without action or knowledge orientation.

Psychic: The soul has neither living knowledge nor purified action, ungrounded in both.
deficient.vignette: Dan has neither real knowledge nor skillful action—drifting through his days uninformed and ineffective, the wisdom that would guide him and the competence that would enact it both undeveloped. Neither hand works well, and they never work together.

attributes.title: Breath Stillness as Liberation (Niranjana State)
attributes.aspect: Lahiri Mahasay’s distinctive teaching: liberation comes through actual breath stillness, not philosophical insight alone. Through Kriya practice, breath naturally becomes slower, subtler, and eventually still. In this stillness, mind also stops; in mental stillness, the Self is recognized. Breath, mind, and consciousness are linked: still the breath and mind stops; stop the mind and Self is realized.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Psychic · Ontic mode: State / practice — the stainless stillness of stilled breath (niranjana) · Valence: The fruit · Guṇa: Sattva, beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: Kin to Prāṇāyāma (Dim 11) and Nāda (Dim 26).
coordinate.ontic_mode: State / practice — the stainless stillness of stilled breath (niranjana)
coordinate.key_relation: Kin to Prāṇāyāma (Dim 11) and Nāda (Dim 26).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Breath naturally slowing through Kriya. Stillness arising spontaneously, not forced. Liberation glimpsed in breath-pauses.
facets.somatic: Body needs less breath as it stills; subtle pranic balance.
facets.emotional: Emotional life still as breath stills.
facets.cognitive: Mind stops as breath stops; Self glimpsed.
facets.psychic: The soul finds that as the breath stills, the mind stills, and a stainless, liberated clarity opens.

balanced.text:
Breath naturally slowing through Kriya. Stillness arising spontaneously, not forced. Liberation glimpsed in breath-pauses.

Somatic: Body needs less breath as it stills; subtle pranic balance.

Emotional: Emotional life still as breath stills.

Cognitive: Mind stops as breath stops; Self glimpsed.

Psychic: The soul finds that as the breath stills, the mind stills, and a stainless, liberated clarity opens.
balanced.vignette: In deep absorption, Ravi’s breath sometimes grows so fine it nearly stops on its own—not forced, but naturally stilling as the mind settles into a spotless, spacious quiet. The breath-stillness isn’t his doing; it’s the body’s answer to a deeply quieted mind, and he rests in the clarity it opens.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forcing breath-holding (kumbhaka) creating pranic disorders. Mistaking breathlessness for realization.
facets.somatic: Body damaged by forced breath retention.
facets.emotional: Anxiety from forced practices.
facets.cognitive: Mental disturbance from improper practice.
facets.psychic: The soul forces the breath toward stillness, the straining defeating the calm it seeks.

excess.text:
Forcing breath-holding (kumbhaka) creating pranic disorders. Mistaking breathlessness for realization.

Somatic: Body damaged by forced breath retention.

Emotional: Anxiety from forced practices.

Cognitive: Mental disturbance from improper practice.

Psychic: The soul forces the breath toward stillness, the straining defeating the calm it seeks.
excess.vignette: Sam forces his breath to stop—straining at retention, holding past comfort, chasing the ‘niranjana state’ through sheer effort—and unsettles the very system he’s trying to still. The natural quieting has been replaced by a violent suppression, and the forcing keeps the stillness it seeks at bay.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Breath always heavy and gross. Body-mind never settled into the subtle stillness.
facets.somatic: Body in constant heavy breathing.
facets.emotional: Emotional turbulence reflected in breath.
facets.cognitive: Mind in chronic agitation matched by breath.
facets.psychic: The soul’s restless breath keeps the mind churning, the still clarity never approached.

deficient.text:
Breath always heavy and gross. Body-mind never settled into the subtle stillness.

Somatic: Body in constant heavy breathing.

Emotional: Emotional turbulence reflected in breath.

Cognitive: Mind in chronic agitation matched by breath.

Psychic: The soul’s restless breath keeps the mind churning, the still clarity never approached.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no awareness of his breath at all—it runs on autopilot, following his stress, never once observed, let alone stilled. The deep breath-stillness that opens in absorption is a state he’s never approached; his breathing and his mind churn on, unwatched, unquieted.

attributes.title: Samkhya Yoga as Stillness (Lahiri’s Definition)
attributes.aspect: Lahiri redefines Samkhya Yoga of the Gita not as philosophical Sankhya but as the practical state achieved through pranayama: peacefulness, steadiness, and contemplation of Formless Immovable Stillness. This is not theoretical sankhya but Kriya’s experiential samkhya. The expansion of Kriya naturally produces this state.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — a means to freedom · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: Lahiri’s definition of yoga as inner stillness; kin to Citta-Vṛtti-Nirodha (Dim 11).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — a means to freedom
coordinate.key_relation: Lahiri’s definition of yoga as inner stillness; kin to Citta-Vṛtti-Nirodha (Dim 11).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Pranayama-induced steadiness leading to contemplation of Formless. The Stillness becomes one’s nature.
facets.somatic: Body steady from pranayama.
facets.emotional: Emotional peace from pranic balance.
facets.cognitive: Mind contemplating Formless Immovable Stillness.
facets.psychic: The soul understands yoga as the stilling of movement, resting in the motionless awareness beneath all activity.

balanced.text:
Pranayama-induced steadiness leading to contemplation of Formless. The Stillness becomes one’s nature.

Somatic: Body steady from pranayama.

Emotional: Emotional peace from pranic balance.

Cognitive: Mind contemplating Formless Immovable Stillness.

Psychic: The soul understands yoga as the stilling of movement, resting in the motionless awareness beneath all activity.
balanced.vignette: For Ravi the goal has revealed itself as a profound stillness—the mind’s fluctuations settling until only clear, contentless awareness remains. He’s come to see yoga not as endless doing but as this quieting, and rests, increasingly, in the stillness that Lahiri called the whole point.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Endless pranayama without progression to Stillness. Technique without fruition.
facets.somatic: Body in eternal preparation.
facets.emotional: Emotional pursuit of technique-mastery.
facets.cognitive: Mind never settles into the Stillness.
facets.psychic: The soul grasps at stillness as a state to achieve, the grasping itself movement.

excess.text:
Endless pranayama without progression to Stillness. Technique without fruition.

Somatic: Body in eternal preparation.

Emotional: Emotional pursuit of technique-mastery.

Cognitive: Mind never settles into the Stillness.

Psychic: The soul grasps at stillness as a state to achieve, the grasping itself movement.
excess.vignette: Sam forces stillness like a clamp—suppressing every thought, wrestling the mind into a tense silence he mistakes for the goal. The natural quiet has become a violent one; beneath the clamped surface the agitation churns, and the forcing is the opposite of the stillness he’s after.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Theoretical Samkhya without pranayama. Philosophy without the body-base.
facets.somatic: Body uninvolved.
facets.emotional: Emotional life unconnected to practice.
facets.cognitive: Mind in concepts without grounding.
facets.psychic: The soul knows only ceaseless inner movement, never the stillness that is its ground.

deficient.text:
Theoretical Samkhya without pranayama. Philosophy without the body-base.

Somatic: Body uninvolved.

Emotional: Emotional life unconnected to practice.

Cognitive: Mind in concepts without grounding.

Psychic: The soul knows only ceaseless inner movement, never the stillness that is its ground.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s mind never stills for an instant—no quiet, no cessation, the fluctuations running unbroken from waking to sleep. The stillness that the teaching names as the very goal of yoga is a state he’s never once touched, and he can’t imagine a mind that isn’t churning.

attributes.title: Karma without Bondage (Tena Tyaktena Bhunjithah)
attributes.aspect: By renouncing through action, enjoy. Perform karma in this world (as enjoined) for a hundred years—thus action does not bind you. Renunciation is not non-action but action without attachment to fruits.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice / method — a means to freedom · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: ‘enjoy through renunciation’ (Īśāvāsya 1); kin to Karma Yoga (Dim 10) and Naiṣkarmya (Dim 16).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — a means to freedom
coordinate.key_relation: ‘enjoy through renunciation’ (Īśāvāsya 1); kin to Karma Yoga (Dim 10) and Naiṣkarmya (Dim 16).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Full engagement with action plus complete inner renunciation of fruits. Karma becomes liberation rather than bondage. 100 years of life lived authentically.
facets.somatic: Body fully engaged in dharmic action, healthy through purposeful activity.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from result-anxiety, joy in doing without attachment.
facets.cognitive: Mind acts wisely, doesn’t grasp outcomes, freedom in middle of activity.
facets.psychic: The soul enjoys the world by renouncing ownership of it, acting fully while claiming no fruit, and so stays free.

balanced.text:
Full engagement with action plus complete inner renunciation of fruits. Karma becomes liberation rather than bondage. 100 years of life lived authentically.

Somatic: Body fully engaged in dharmic action, healthy through purposeful activity.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from result-anxiety, joy in doing without attachment.

Cognitive: Mind acts wisely, doesn’t grasp outcomes, freedom in middle of activity.

Psychic: The soul enjoys the world by renouncing ownership of it, acting fully while claiming no fruit, and so stays free.
balanced.vignette: Ravi acts fully and enjoys freely, and somehow none of it binds him—because he’s let go of the frantic grip on outcomes, taking what comes as given rather than owned. He works and receives and releases, and the releasing is why the action leaves no residue.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Workaholic activity claiming non-attachment—endless busyness disguised as karma yoga, missing the inner renunciation.
facets.somatic: Body burned out from over-activity in name of duty.
facets.emotional: Hidden attachment to outcomes covered by spiritual rhetoric.
facets.cognitive: Mind constantly busy, claiming detachment while clinging.
facets.psychic: The soul uses ‘renounce and enjoy’ to rationalize grasping, the renunciation merely verbal.

excess.text:
Workaholic activity claiming non-attachment—endless busyness disguised as karma yoga, missing the inner renunciation.

Somatic: Body burned out from over-activity in name of duty.

Emotional: Hidden attachment to outcomes covered by spiritual rhetoric.

Cognitive: Mind constantly busy, claiming detachment while clinging.

Psychic: The soul uses ‘renounce and enjoy’ to rationalize grasping, the renunciation merely verbal.
excess.vignette: Sam invokes ‘act without attachment’ to justify not caring—phoning it in, skipping the effort, calling his half-heartedness non-attachment. The teaching of unbound action has become his excuse for unbound laziness, and the ‘detachment’ is really just indifference.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Either complete withdrawal (escapist non-action) or grasping action driven entirely by results. Both miss the karma-yoga balance.
facets.somatic: Either inactive body or compulsively driven body.
facets.emotional: Either depressive withdrawal or anxious result-focus.
facets.cognitive: Either escapist mind or grasping mind, both bound by karma.
facets.psychic: The soul acts always for the fruit, bound by every result it labors for.

deficient.text:
Either complete withdrawal (escapist non-action) or grasping action driven entirely by results. Both miss the karma-yoga balance.

Somatic: Either inactive body or compulsively driven body.

Emotional: Either depressive withdrawal or anxious result-focus.

Cognitive: Either escapist mind or grasping mind, both bound by karma.

Psychic: The soul acts always for the fruit, bound by every result it labors for.
deficient.vignette: Dan is bound by every action—gripping each outcome, devastated by each failure, inflated by each success, the fruits of his deeds shackled to his sense of self. That one could act fully and yet remain free of the results is a possibility he’s never lived; every deed binds him tighter.

attributes.title: Atmahatya (Self-Slayers/Killers of the Self)
attributes.aspect: Demoniac worlds covered with blinding darkness are inhabited by ‘slayers of the Self.’ These are not violent killers but those who deny their true Self through identification with body, ignorance, and worldly pursuit.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Affliction — the ‘slayers of the Self’ · Valence: Affliction (the deepest error) · Guṇa: Tamas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Distortion · Key relation: Those who slay the Self through ignorance (Īśāvāsya 3) — self-neglect as the true self-harm.
coordinate.key_relation: Those who slay the Self through ignorance (Īśāvāsya 3) — self-neglect as the true self-harm.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Honoring the Self—not identifying with body alone but living from awareness of the deeper reality. The Self is recognized, fed, nurtured.
facets.somatic: Body served the Self, not Self serving body. Healthy embodiment.
facets.emotional: Emotional connection to the deeper Self, not identified only with surface personality.
facets.cognitive: Mind oriented toward Self-realization, not just worldly knowledge.
facets.psychic: The soul heeds the Upaniṣad’s warning against slaying the Self through ignorance, and turns toward self-knowledge.

balanced.text:
Honoring the Self—not identifying with body alone but living from awareness of the deeper reality. The Self is recognized, fed, nurtured.

Somatic: Body served the Self, not Self serving body. Healthy embodiment.

Emotional: Emotional connection to the deeper Self, not identified only with surface personality.

Cognitive: Mind oriented toward Self-realization, not just worldly knowledge.

Psychic: The soul heeds the Upaniṣad’s warning against slaying the Self through ignorance, and turns toward self-knowledge.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has awakened to the Self as the one thing worth honoring—and so declines the great self-slaying the Isha warns of, the deadening of the spirit through willful immersion in the un-Self. Alive to what he most deeply is, he tends it rather than burying it under matter and distraction.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive identification with the Self as ‘I’—spiritual ego claiming superiority, neglect of body, denying ordinary life.
facets.somatic: Body neglected in name of Self-pursuit.
facets.emotional: Spiritual arrogance, contempt for those identified with body.
facets.cognitive: Self-claim without genuine realization.
facets.psychic: The soul moralizes the warning into morbid self-condemnation.

excess.text:
Excessive identification with the Self as ‘I’—spiritual ego claiming superiority, neglect of body, denying ordinary life.

Somatic: Body neglected in name of Self-pursuit.

Emotional: Spiritual arrogance, contempt for those identified with body.

Cognitive: Self-claim without genuine realization.

Psychic: The soul moralizes the warning into morbid self-condemnation.
excess.vignette: Sam actively slays the Self—drowning it in relentless materialism, sense-worship, and distraction, worshipping the un-Self as though it were everything. The Isha’s grim phrase fits him: he ‘kills the Self,’ not by dramatic violence but by a lifetime of turning wholly away from it toward the perishable.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Living as if there is no Self—total identification with body, senses, worldly pursuits. The Self is ‘slain’ through denial—the true tragedy.
facets.somatic: Body taken as ultimate identity, all energy in physical pursuits.
facets.emotional: Emotional life confined to bodily/social existence, no deeper dimension.
facets.cognitive: Mind entirely material-focused, the deeper Self denied, ignored, forgotten.
facets.psychic: The soul, neglecting the Self entirely, lives the very self-slaying the texts lament, unaware.

deficient.text:
Living as if there is no Self—total identification with body, senses, worldly pursuits. The Self is ‘slain’ through denial—the true tragedy.

Somatic: Body taken as ultimate identity, all energy in physical pursuits.

Emotional: Emotional life confined to bodily/social existence, no deeper dimension.

Cognitive: Mind entirely material-focused, the deeper Self denied, ignored, forgotten.

Psychic: The soul, neglecting the Self entirely, lives the very self-slaying the texts lament, unaware.
deficient.vignette: Dan doesn’t even know there’s a Self to slay—so wholly identified with body and world that the spiritual dimension has never once registered. Where the self-slayer at least turns away from something he half-knows, Dan lives in an unbroken forgetting, the very possibility of an inner Self having never dawned.

attributes.title: Atma Sakshatkar (Direct Self-Knowledge)
attributes.aspect: This Self cannot be attained by study of scriptures, by intellect, or by much hearing. He whom the Self chooses obtains him. To such, the Self reveals its true nature.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State / goal — liberation realized · Valence: The goal · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: ātma-sākṣātkāra — the direct seeing of the Self that liberates.
coordinate.key_relation: ātma-sākṣātkāra — the direct seeing of the Self that liberates.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Effortful preparation combined with grace—knowing one cannot force realization but must be ready when grace flows.
facets.somatic: Body prepared as vessel.
facets.emotional: Emotional surrender combined with effort.
facets.cognitive: Intellectual preparation along with humility before grace.
facets.psychic: The soul knows the Self directly, by immediate recognition rather than inference, the seeking ending in seeing.

balanced.text:
Effortful preparation combined with grace—knowing one cannot force realization but must be ready when grace flows.

Somatic: Body prepared as vessel.

Emotional: Emotional surrender combined with effort.

Cognitive: Intellectual preparation along with humility before grace.

Psychic: The soul knows the Self directly, by immediate recognition rather than inference, the seeking ending in seeing.
balanced.vignette: For Ravi the Self has become a matter of direct knowing—not an inference or a belief but an immediate recognition of the awareness he already is. The atma-sakshatkar isn’t a future attainment to him; it’s a present seeing, quiet and certain, that no argument gave and none can take away.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Pure intellectual effort claiming it will produce realization—contrary to the teaching.
facets.somatic: Body strained in self-effort.
facets.emotional: Anxious striving for realization.
facets.cognitive: Mind claiming to force what only grace gives.
facets.psychic: The soul chases the direct realization as a peak experience to capture.

excess.text:
Pure intellectual effort claiming it will produce realization—contrary to the teaching.

Somatic: Body strained in self-effort.

Emotional: Anxious striving for realization.

Cognitive: Mind claiming to force what only grace gives.

Psychic: The soul chases the direct realization as a peak experience to capture.
excess.vignette: Sam claims direct Self-realization—announces it, teaches it, wears it—while his reactive, grasping life quietly contradicts every word. He’s mistaken understanding the concept for the direct seeing, and the confident claim is the surest sign the seeing hasn’t happened.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Lazy waiting for grace without preparation—no practice, just hoping for spontaneous awakening.
facets.somatic: Body without practice.
facets.emotional: Sentimental waiting without preparation.
facets.cognitive: Mind without inquiry, just passive waiting.
facets.psychic: The soul knows the Self only as a concept it has read about, never as the immediate fact of awareness.

deficient.text:
Lazy waiting for grace without preparation—no practice, just hoping for spontaneous awakening.

Somatic: Body without practice.

Emotional: Sentimental waiting without preparation.

Cognitive: Mind without inquiry, just passive waiting.

Psychic: The soul knows the Self only as a concept it has read about, never as the immediate fact of awareness.
deficient.vignette: Dan knows the Self only as a word in a book—a concept he can define and has never once directly seen. The immediate recognition the teaching points to is entirely outside his experience; he has, at most, ideas about the Self, and takes the ideas for the thing.

attributes.title: Sarvadakshina (Total Sacrifice Including Self)
attributes.aspect: Sarvadakshina is the sacrifice of everything—not just possessions but oneself. The complete giving is the necessary preparation for the final stage of life. Gautama performed it in form (giving things) but not spirit (keeping the self). Nachiketa understood the true requirement: the self too must be offered.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital · Ontic mode: Practice — the sacrifice in which the self is offered · Valence: Virtue / the total offering · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: The all-giving sacrifice; kin to Yajña (Dim 28) and Atma-Nivedanam.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice — the sacrifice in which the self is offered
coordinate.key_relation: The all-giving sacrifice; kin to Yajña (Dim 28) and Atma-Nivedanam.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Wise total surrender—giving all possessions in proper time and surrendering even the self to truth. Final-stage preparation.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in genuine sacrifice without retention.
facets.emotional: Emotional readiness to surrender even attachment to self.
facets.cognitive: Mind committed to complete giving, not partial.
facets.psychic: The soul offers everything, including itself, as the final sacrificial gift, holding nothing back.

balanced.text:
Wise total surrender—giving all possessions in proper time and surrendering even the self to truth. Final-stage preparation.

Somatic: Body engaged in genuine sacrifice without retention.

Emotional: Emotional readiness to surrender even attachment to self.

Cognitive: Mind committed to complete giving, not partial.

Psychic: The soul offers everything, including itself, as the final sacrificial gift, holding nothing back.
balanced.vignette: In his deepest surrender, Ravi has offered everything—not just his possessions and deeds but the sense of a separate self doing the offering—holding nothing back. The total sacrifice isn’t a loss to him; it’s the ultimate release, and giving even the giver, he’s found an unexpected freedom.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Performative total sacrifice without inner readiness—rushing into renunciation without preparation, harming self and others.
facets.somatic: Body destroyed in premature renunciation.
facets.emotional: Emotional posturing without genuine surrender.
facets.cognitive: Mind claiming sacrifice while ego intact.
facets.psychic: The soul performs total sacrifice for show, the grand gesture feeding the ego it claims to give.

excess.text:
Performative total sacrifice without inner readiness—rushing into renunciation without preparation, harming self and others.

Somatic: Body destroyed in premature renunciation.

Emotional: Emotional posturing without genuine surrender.

Cognitive: Mind claiming sacrifice while ego intact.

Psychic: The soul performs total sacrifice for show, the grand gesture feeding the ego it claims to give.
excess.vignette: Sam performs total sacrifice—the ostentatious renunciation, the public martyrdom, the ‘I’ve given everything’ announced for its effect. The self he claims to have offered is running the whole display, and the grand gesture of giving all is one more thing the ego keeps for itself.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Gautama-like sacrifice—giving only useless things, keeping the valuable, never offering self. Form without spirit.
facets.somatic: Body in selective giving, keeping the best for self.
facets.emotional: Emotional manipulation of religious form.
facets.cognitive: Mind calculating what minimum to give while keeping the rest.
facets.psychic: The soul gives only what costs little, the self it most needs to offer kept safely back.

deficient.text:
Gautama-like sacrifice—giving only useless things, keeping the valuable, never offering self. Form without spirit.

Somatic: Body in selective giving, keeping the best for self.

Emotional: Emotional manipulation of religious form.

Cognitive: Mind calculating what minimum to give while keeping the rest.

Psychic: The soul gives only what costs little, the self it most needs to offer kept safely back.
deficient.vignette: Dan gives nothing—not his possessions, not his effort, not a fraction of his tight-held self—the whole idea of sacrifice foreign to a life organized around keeping. The total self-offering the teaching describes is not something he’s failed at; it’s a direction he’s never once faced.

attributes.title: Threefold Self-Offering (Gross-Subtle-Causal)
attributes.aspect: The complete self-offering happens in three stages corresponding to the three bodies/three worlds. Physical body, subtle body (mental/astral), and causal body (root tendencies) must each be surrendered. This is what sannyasa-vow includes—renouncing pleasures of all three worlds.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — offering the three bodies · Valence: Virtue / graded surrender · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: Offering the gross, subtle, and causal selves; kin to the three bodies (Dim 4).
coordinate.key_relation: Offering the gross, subtle, and causal selves; kin to the three bodies (Dim 4).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Systematic surrender of all three levels of self—physical attachments, mental/emotional patterns, and root karmic tendencies.
facets.somatic: Physical body surrendered—not destroyed but consecrated.
facets.emotional: Emotional/mental body surrendered—patterns released.
facets.cognitive: Causal/unconscious patterns surrendered through deep practice.
facets.psychic: The soul offers itself at every level — body, mind, and the root of identity — so that nothing of the separate self is withheld.

balanced.text:
Systematic surrender of all three levels of self—physical attachments, mental/emotional patterns, and root karmic tendencies.

Somatic: Physical body surrendered—not destroyed but consecrated.

Emotional: Emotional/mental body surrendered—patterns released.

Cognitive: Causal/unconscious patterns surrendered through deep practice.

Psychic: The soul offers itself at every level — body, mind, and the root of identity — so that nothing of the separate self is withheld.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has learned to offer himself at every level—the gross body and its deeds, the subtle mind and its thoughts, the causal sense of individuality itself—withholding nothing from the surrender. The threefold offering has hollowed out the ‘me’ at each layer, and the hollowing has, strangely, filled him.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced surrender at all levels without integration—psychological breakdown from premature dis-identification.
facets.somatic: Body damaged by forced renunciation.
facets.emotional: Emotional dissociation from forced detachment.
facets.cognitive: Causal disturbance from premature surrender.
facets.psychic: The soul offers the outer levels while clinging to the causal root, a partial surrender mistaken for total.

excess.text:
Forced surrender at all levels without integration—psychological breakdown from premature dis-identification.

Somatic: Body damaged by forced renunciation.

Emotional: Emotional dissociation from forced detachment.

Cognitive: Causal disturbance from premature surrender.

Psychic: The soul offers the outer levels while clinging to the causal root, a partial surrender mistaken for total.
excess.vignette: Sam offers the outer show and keeps the inner core—performing a surrender of his deeds while gripping his mind and his identity tight. He claims a total, threefold offering and has made only the safest, most visible gesture, and the partial giving, announced as complete, is its own deception.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Surface surrender only—giving up some physical things while subtle and causal levels remain in full grasp. Pseudo-renunciation.
facets.somatic: Token physical sacrifice.
facets.emotional: Emotional and mental patterns unchanged.
facets.cognitive: Causal tendencies still operating with full force.
facets.psychic: The soul offers nothing, its whole threefold self held tightly as its own.

deficient.text:
Surface surrender only—giving up some physical things while subtle and causal levels remain in full grasp. Pseudo-renunciation.

Somatic: Token physical sacrifice.

Emotional: Emotional and mental patterns unchanged.

Cognitive: Causal tendencies still operating with full force.

Psychic: The soul offers nothing, its whole threefold self held tightly as its own.
deficient.vignette: Dan offers nothing at any level—not the body, not the mind, not the sense of self—the whole graded surrender untried. The threefold self-offering the teaching describes is not a practice he’s begun; he keeps the whole of himself, gross and subtle and causal alike, firmly in his own hands.

attributes.title: Bhrigu’s Inquiry (Tapas as Brahman)
attributes.aspect: Bhrigu asks his father Varuna about Brahman. Varuna says: ‘That from which beings are born, by which they live, into which they return—desire to know That. That is Brahman.’ Bhrigu reaches Brahman through tapas, identifying Brahman successively with food, prana, mind, intellect, bliss.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — a means to freedom · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: ‘know tapas as Brahman’ (Taittirīya); kin to Bhrigu’s Tapas.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — a means to freedom
coordinate.key_relation: ‘know tapas as Brahman’ (Taittirīya); kin to Bhrigu’s Tapas.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Progressive inquiry through tapas (focused practice). Each level provisionally identified with Brahman until the deepest level (ananda) is reached.
facets.somatic: Body in disciplined inquiry.
facets.emotional: Emotional devotion to inquiry.
facets.cognitive: Mind progressively penetrating to subtler levels.
facets.psychic: The soul, through ardent inquiry, discovers tapas itself — the fervor of sustained seeking — as a face of Brahman.

balanced.text:
Progressive inquiry through tapas (focused practice). Each level provisionally identified with Brahman until the deepest level (ananda) is reached.

Somatic: Body in disciplined inquiry.

Emotional: Emotional devotion to inquiry.

Cognitive: Mind progressively penetrating to subtler levels.

Psychic: The soul, through ardent inquiry, discovers tapas itself — the fervor of sustained seeking — as a face of Brahman.
balanced.vignette: Through sustained inquiry and discipline, Ravi has arrived where Bhrigu arrived—the recognition that the very ground he sought through austerity is what he already is. The tapas led him not to some far attainment but home; Brahman, he found, was the seeker all along.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mechanical going-through levels without genuine tapas, or claiming arrival at ananda without preparation.
facets.somatic: Body without genuine practice.
facets.emotional: Mechanical inquiry without devotion.
facets.cognitive: Conceptual progression without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul mistakes the fervor for the goal, in love with its own intensity.

excess.text:
Mechanical going-through levels without genuine tapas, or claiming arrival at ananda without preparation.

Somatic: Body without genuine practice.

Emotional: Mechanical inquiry without devotion.

Cognitive: Conceptual progression without realization.

Psychic: The soul mistakes the fervor for the goal, in love with its own intensity.
excess.vignette: Sam either grinds at austerity for its own sake—the hardship a badge—or leaps to announce ‘tapas is Brahman, I’ve realized it’ from a discipline he’s barely begun. Either the means has swallowed the end or the end is claimed without the means, and the genuine inquiry stalls.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No inquiry into Brahman at all—accepting surface reality without question.
facets.somatic: Body in mechanical living.
facets.emotional: Emotional acceptance of surface life.
facets.cognitive: Mind without inquiry into fundamental questions.
facets.psychic: The soul brings no fervor to its seeking, and the inquiry that could reveal Brahman is never lit.

deficient.text:
No inquiry into Brahman at all—accepting surface reality without question.

Somatic: Body in mechanical living.

Emotional: Emotional acceptance of surface life.

Cognitive: Mind without inquiry into fundamental questions.

Psychic: The soul brings no fervor to its seeking, and the inquiry that could reveal Brahman is never lit.
deficient.vignette: Dan neither inquires nor disciplines himself toward any recognition—the sustained questioning that austerity fuels never undertaken. That the ground of reality might be discovered as one’s own deepest nature, through patient tapas, is a possibility he’s never pursued; he’s asked the question with no force behind it, or not at all.

attributes.title: Ego-Identity Test (Witnessing Capacity)
attributes.aspect: Five questions reveal one’s stage of spiritual development. They test the witnessing capacity—the actual evidence of progress. Without witnessing: thoughts are obeyed; desires only intensify; suffering is just endured; silence feels empty; peace depends on conditions. With witnessing: opposite of each.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / test — testing identity by the capacity to witness · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Mechanism · Key relation: Whatever can be witnessed is not the Self — the witness-test of identity.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / test — testing identity by the capacity to witness
coordinate.key_relation: Whatever can be witnessed is not the Self — the witness-test of identity.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Increasing capacity to witness thoughts, see through desires, question suffering, feel silence as full, find peace independent of conditions.
facets.somatic: Body actions no longer driven by every impulse.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns seen through rather than obeyed.
facets.cognitive: Mind has witnessing space between perception and reaction.
facets.psychic: The soul tests its freedom by its capacity to witness — to watch even its strongest reactions without being swept away.

balanced.text:
Increasing capacity to witness thoughts, see through desires, question suffering, feel silence as full, find peace independent of conditions.

Somatic: Body actions no longer driven by every impulse.

Emotional: Emotional patterns seen through rather than obeyed.

Cognitive: Mind has witnessing space between perception and reaction.

Psychic: The soul tests its freedom by its capacity to witness — to watch even its strongest reactions without being swept away.
balanced.vignette: When Ravi wants to know how deep his realization goes, he checks whether he can actually witness the ego—watch the pride flare, the fear grip, the ‘I’ assert itself—without being swept into it. The capacity to observe the ego rather than be it is his real measure, and it’s grown, quietly, over years.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Claiming high witnessing while showing reactive markers. Self-deception.
facets.somatic: Body still in reactive patterns despite claims.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity covered by spiritual rhetoric.
facets.cognitive: Mental claim of witnessing without actual capacity.
facets.psychic: The soul turns witnessing into cold detachment, watching from a defended distance.

excess.text:
Claiming high witnessing while showing reactive markers. Self-deception.

Somatic: Body still in reactive patterns despite claims.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity covered by spiritual rhetoric.

Cognitive: Mental claim of witnessing without actual capacity.

Psychic: The soul turns witnessing into cold detachment, watching from a defended distance.
excess.vignette: Sam is sure he can witness his ego—says so often—while every provocation still sweeps him instantly into reactivity, the ‘witnessing’ a story he tells about a capacity he doesn’t have. He’s mistaken the idea of witnessing for the act, and the confident claim fails the very test it names.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No witnessing capacity. Every thought obeyed, every desire pursued, suffering only endured, silence feels empty, peace conditional.
facets.somatic: Body completely driven by impulses.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactions are total identity.
facets.cognitive: Mind has no witness-space.
facets.psychic: The soul cannot witness at all, wholly merged with each reaction as it arises.

deficient.text:
