No witnessing capacity. Every thought obeyed, every desire pursued, suffering only endured, silence feels empty, peace conditional.

Somatic: Body completely driven by impulses.

Emotional: Emotional reactions are total identity.

Cognitive: Mind has no witness-space.

Psychic: The soul cannot witness at all, wholly merged with each reaction as it arises.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no witnessing capacity at all—the ego’s every movement is simply him, no distance from which to observe the pride or the fear or the grasping. The test of whether one can watch the ‘I’ rather than be it is one he can’t even attempt; there’s no vantage in him from which to look.

attributes.attribute: The Lived Experience of Practice Itself
attributes.title: The Lived Experience of Practice Itself

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Psychic · Ontic mode: State / practice — practice (sādhana) as lived · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Ground / bridge · Key relation: Genus — the lived act of practice of which all the disciplines below are forms.
coordinate.key_relation: Genus — the lived act of practice of which all the disciplines below are forms.

balanced.label: Balanced — living practice that transforms

facets.overview: Held in balance, sādhana is a living thing — done regularly but not rigidly, with sincerity rather than strain, and always pointed beyond its own technique toward the realization it serves. The practice gradually saturates ordinary life: the inquiry continues in the supermarket, the remembrance hums beneath the workday, the equanimity carries into conflict. The fruit is not a special meditative state walled off from living but a steady, deepening transformation of how one inhabits every hour — the body more at ease, the emotions more workable, the mind more often resting in its ground.

balanced.text: Held in balance, sādhana is a living thing — done regularly but not rigidly, with sincerity rather than strain, and always pointed beyond its own technique toward the realization it serves. The practice gradually saturates ordinary life: the inquiry continues in the supermarket, the remembrance hums beneath the workday, the equanimity carries into conflict. The fruit is not a special meditative state walled off from living but a steady, deepening transformation of how one inhabits every hour — the body more at ease, the emotions more workable, the mind more often resting in its ground.

excess.label: In excess — the practice as performance, ambition, or rigidity

facets.overview: Practice in excess turns the means into an idol. It can become rigid and mechanical — the technique performed perfectly and emptily, the form mistaken for the substance. It can become spiritual ambition — the striving for states, powers, and milestones, the subtle competitiveness, the practice that feeds the very ego it was meant to dissolve. And it can become bypass — a retreat into practice as avoidance of the relationships and responsibilities of ordinary life. The texts repeatedly warn that practice grasped by the ego becomes one more form of bondage, however holy its appearance.

excess.text: Practice in excess turns the means into an idol. It can become rigid and mechanical — the technique performed perfectly and emptily, the form mistaken for the substance. It can become spiritual ambition — the striving for states, powers, and milestones, the subtle competitiveness, the practice that feeds the very ego it was meant to dissolve. And it can become bypass — a retreat into practice as avoidance of the relationships and responsibilities of ordinary life. The texts repeatedly warn that practice grasped by the ego becomes one more form of bondage, however holy its appearance.

deficient.label: Deficient — no practice; the truth left as mere words

facets.overview: Where practice is deficient, the vision is collected as information and never embodied. The person reads, attends, discusses — and remains unchanged, because the bridge from understanding to realization was never walked. This is the most common condition: a head full of teachings and a life run by the same old grooves, the truth admired from a distance but never made one’s own. The tradition’s pragmatists — Lahiri among them — insist bluntly on this point: practice over words; it is the doing, not the discoursing, that transforms.

deficient.text: Where practice is deficient, the vision is collected as information and never embodied. The person reads, attends, discusses — and remains unchanged, because the bridge from understanding to realization was never walked. This is the most common condition: a head full of teachings and a life run by the same old grooves, the truth admired from a distance but never made one’s own. The tradition’s pragmatists — Lahiri among them — insist bluntly on this point: practice over words; it is the doing, not the discoursing, that transforms.

attributes.attribute: Witnessing as Path Out of Body
attributes.title: Witnessing as Path Out of Body
attributes.aspect: Witnessing as systematic practice in everyday activities—eating, walking, working—gradually breaks body-identification. The body acts; consciousness witnesses. This dual-awareness eventually leads to out-of-body experience and freedom.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna) · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: witnessing as the path out of body-identification; kin to the witness (Dim 27).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna)
coordinate.key_relation: witnessing as the path out of body-identification; kin to the witness (Dim 27).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Witnessing established in every activity—body acts, consciousness watches. Life becomes a drama in which one plays roles consciously while knowing oneself as the actor, not the role.
facets.somatic: Body performs its functions with awareness; healthy engagement without identification.
facets.emotional: Emotions arise as movements of body-mind; witness remains free.
facets.cognitive: Continuous witness-awareness, recognition of activities as not-Self.
facets.psychic: The soul witnesses the body from awareness, and the simple act of watching begins to loosen the identification with flesh.

balanced.text:
Witnessing established in every activity—body acts, consciousness watches. Life becomes a drama in which one plays roles consciously while knowing oneself as the actor, not the role.

Somatic: Body performs its functions with awareness; healthy engagement without identification.

Emotional: Emotions arise as movements of body-mind; witness remains free.

Cognitive: Continuous witness-awareness, recognition of activities as not-Self.

Psychic: The soul witnesses the body from awareness, and the simple act of watching begins to loosen the identification with flesh.
balanced.vignette: When pain flares in Ravi’s knee, he can rest as the awareness that notices the pain rather than the one drowning in it—witnessing the body from a step back. The witnessing doesn’t deny the ache; it just stops the ache from being the whole of him, and in that small gap he finds room to breathe.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Cold dissociative witnessing that becomes pathological—life seems flat, all engagement loses meaning. False detachment.
facets.somatic: Body movements robotic without true witnessing.
facets.emotional: Emotional flatness, inability to engage warmly.
facets.cognitive: Forced witness-claim creates split between observer and observed.
facets.psychic: The soul turns witnessing into cold dissociation, splitting from the body rather than freeing itself within it.

excess.text:
Cold dissociative witnessing that becomes pathological—life seems flat, all engagement loses meaning. False detachment.

Somatic: Body movements robotic without true witnessing.

Emotional: Emotional flatness, inability to engage warmly.

Cognitive: Forced witness-claim creates split between observer and observed.

Psychic: The soul turns witnessing into cold dissociation, splitting from the body rather than freeing itself within it.
excess.vignette: Karl has ‘witnessed his way out of the body’ into a cold dissociation—numb to its signals, neglecting its needs, floating above an embodied life he now finds beneath him. The path out of identification has become an exile from his own aliveness.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete identification with every activity—‘I am eating,’ ‘I am walking,’ ‘I am angry.’ No witness available, total absorption in role-play.
facets.somatic: Body’s activities completely identified with as self.
facets.emotional: Drowning in every emotion as ‘me,’ no freedom.
facets.cognitive: Mind fully identified with its contents, slavery to role-identification.
facets.psychic: The soul never steps back to watch, fused to the body and its every sensation.

deficient.text:
Complete identification with every activity—‘I am eating,’ ‘I am walking,’ ‘I am angry.’ No witness available, total absorption in role-play.

Somatic: Body’s activities completely identified with as self.

Emotional: Drowning in every emotion as ‘me,’ no freedom.

Cognitive: Mind fully identified with its contents, slavery to role-identification.

Psychic: The soul never steps back to watch, fused to the body and its every sensation.
deficient.vignette: Dan is welded to his body—its every sensation is him, its pains are catastrophes, its aging an assault—with no capacity to step back and simply witness. The awareness that could watch the body rather than be it is one he’s never once located.

attributes.title: Beyond Neti-Neti (Even Negation Superfluous)
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra transcends even the famous Neti-Neti method. For one established in his teaching, the concept of the world itself transforms—seeing the world doesn’t impact him. Therefore the need to negate (‘not this, not this’) becomes superfluous. The world is seen but no longer mistaken; the negation has already happened through recognition.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna) · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the stage where even negation becomes superfluous; kin to Neti-Neti (Dim 28).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna)
coordinate.key_relation: the stage where even negation becomes superfluous; kin to Neti-Neti (Dim 28).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Direct recognition beyond negation-practice. World continues to appear but is no longer mistaken.
facets.somatic: Body interacts with world without identification.
facets.emotional: Emotional response to world without binding.
facets.cognitive: Mind beyond positive affirmation and negative denial—simply recognizing.
facets.psychic: The soul rests where even the negating ‘not this’ falls away, abiding as what cannot be denied.

balanced.text:
Direct recognition beyond negation-practice. World continues to appear but is no longer mistaken.

Somatic: Body interacts with world without identification.

Emotional: Emotional response to world without binding.

Cognitive: Mind beyond positive affirmation and negative denial—simply recognizing.

Psychic: The soul rests where even the negating ‘not this’ falls away, abiding as what cannot be denied.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to a place where he no longer needs to negate anything—not the body, not the thoughts—because he simply rests, directly, as the awareness they appear in. The negation that once helped him has fallen away, superfluous; he’s home, and home needs no argument.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Dismissing Neti-Neti before having done the work. Skipping necessary steps.
facets.somatic: Body still entangled despite claims.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns unchanged.
facets.cognitive: Mind hasn’t actually transcended.
facets.psychic: The soul clings to the idea of going ‘beyond method,’ a subtle laziness dressed as the highest teaching.

excess.text:
Dismissing Neti-Neti before having done the work. Skipping necessary steps.

Somatic: Body still entangled despite claims.

Emotional: Emotional patterns unchanged.

Cognitive: Mind hasn’t actually transcended.

Psychic: The soul clings to the idea of going ‘beyond method,’ a subtle laziness dressed as the highest teaching.
excess.vignette: Sam has made a proud method out of ‘even negation is superfluous’—dismissing every practice, every inquiry, as beneath one who’s ‘already there,’ though he plainly isn’t. He’s grabbed the language of the summit to justify never climbing, and the ‘no method’ is his cleverest evasion.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Endless Neti-Neti practice as the highest method. Never transcending it.
facets.somatic: Body in perpetual negation-practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional energy spent on denial.
facets.cognitive: Mind always negating without breakthrough.
facets.psychic: The soul, still bound to its identifications, has no negation even begun, let alone transcended.

deficient.text:
Endless Neti-Neti practice as the highest method. Never transcending it.

Somatic: Body in perpetual negation-practice.

Emotional: Emotional energy spent on denial.

Cognitive: Mind always negating without breakthrough.

Psychic: The soul, still bound to its identifications, has no negation even begun, let alone transcended.
deficient.vignette: Dan needs endless negation and never arrives—forever setting aside ‘not this, not this’ as a mental exercise that stalls at the technique. That there’s a resting the negation was only ever pointing toward is an arrival he’s never reached; he’s still stuck in the pointing.

attributes.title: Witness Consciousness (Sakshi-Drishti as Radical)
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra’s distinctive witness teaching: you are not in the world; the world is in you. You witness the body, senses, mind, all experience. As witness, you are unaffected by what is witnessed. This is not effortful technique but direct recognition. The witness has never moved, never changed, never been touched.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna) · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: radical witness-seeing (sākṣi-dṛṣṭi); the Self as pure witness (Dim 27).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna)
coordinate.key_relation: radical witness-seeing (sākṣi-dṛṣṭi); the Self as pure witness (Dim 27).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Direct recognition of witness-status. All experience appears in unchanging awareness.
facets.somatic: Body witnessed, not identified with.
facets.emotional: Emotions witnessed without absorption.
facets.cognitive: Thoughts witnessed; the witness remains.
facets.psychic: The soul takes the radical stand as pure witness, recognizing it has only ever been the seer, never the seen.

balanced.text:
Direct recognition of witness-status. All experience appears in unchanging awareness.

Somatic: Body witnessed, not identified with.

Emotional: Emotions witnessed without absorption.

Cognitive: Thoughts witnessed; the witness remains.

Psychic: The soul takes the radical stand as pure witness, recognizing it has only ever been the seer, never the seen.
balanced.vignette: More and more, Ravi lives from the witness—the still awareness that watches his thoughts think and his moods move without being swept into any of them. The witnessing isn’t detachment from his life; it’s a spacious presence from which he meets it, freer and clearer than before.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Dissociated ‘witnessing’ that becomes cold detachment. Loss of healthy engagement.
facets.somatic: Body neglected in dissociated ‘witnessing.’
facets.emotional: Emotional coldness mistaken for witnessing.
facets.cognitive: Mental distance preventing engagement.
facets.psychic: The soul performs witnessing as a detached pose, watching from a defended distance.

excess.text:
Dissociated ‘witnessing’ that becomes cold detachment. Loss of healthy engagement.

Somatic: Body neglected in dissociated ‘witnessing.’

Emotional: Emotional coldness mistaken for witnessing.

Cognitive: Mental distance preventing engagement.

Psychic: The soul performs witnessing as a detached pose, watching from a defended distance.
excess.vignette: Sam uses ‘I am the witness’ to excuse a lofty coldness—observing his own life and everyone else’s from an uninvolved distance, above the fray, untouched. The witness consciousness has become his reason to stop caring, and the detachment is really a hiding place.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete identification with content. No witness recognized.
facets.somatic: Body fully identified with as self.
facets.emotional: Emotions taken as ultimate self-experience.
facets.cognitive: Mind fully absorbed in objects, no witnessing presence.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly the seen, swept into every experience with no witness recognized at all.

deficient.text:
Complete identification with content. No witness recognized.

Somatic: Body fully identified with as self.

Emotional: Emotions taken as ultimate self-experience.

Cognitive: Mind fully absorbed in objects, no witnessing presence.

Psychic: The soul is wholly the seen, swept into every experience with no witness recognized at all.
deficient.vignette: Dan is wholly the doer and sufferer—every action his, every feeling total, no witnessing presence anywhere behind the churn. The still awareness that watches, serene, while life happens is not something he’s failed to find; it’s one he’s never suspected exists.

attributes.attribute: Kapila Lesson: Sankhya for Daily Discrimination
attributes.title: Kapila Lesson: Sankhya for Daily Discrimination
attributes.aspect: Kapila Muni teaches his mother Devahuti the Sankhya philosophy — the discrimination between prakriti (matter, including body and mind) and purusha (consciousness, the witness). In daily life, this means developing the practical capacity to step back: ‘What is happening is in prakriti; I am the witness.’ Single most transformative cognitive shift available. Kapila taught his mother Devahuti the secret of Sankhya: prakriti and purusha. In daily life: develop the capacity to discriminate between consciousness (you) and material processes (events, body, mind). This single distinction transforms daily experience.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna) · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: Sāṁkhya discrimination for daily life (Kapila); kin to Viveka (Dim 23).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna)
coordinate.key_relation: Sāṁkhya discrimination for daily life (Kapila); kin to Viveka (Dim 23).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Sankhya-discrimination active in daily life. Witness-position maintained through life’s events.
facets.somatic: Body’s events witnessed, not identified with.
facets.emotional: Emotional life witnessed; one is not the emotion.
facets.cognitive: Mind’s contents recognized as prakriti, not Self.
facets.psychic: The soul uses Sāṅkhya’s discrimination daily, separating the watching Purusha from the moving play of nature.

balanced.text:
Sankhya-discrimination active in daily life. Witness-position maintained through life’s events.

Somatic: Body’s events witnessed, not identified with.

Emotional: Emotional life witnessed; one is not the emotion.

Cognitive: Mind’s contents recognized as prakriti, not Self.

Psychic: The soul uses Sāṅkhya’s discrimination daily, separating the watching Purusha from the moving play of nature.
balanced.vignette: Like Kapila teaching discrimination, Ravi has learned to sort, in the ordinary flow of his day, the seer from the seen—noticing when he’s fused with a mood and gently stepping back. The daily discrimination isn’t abstract; it’s a practical skill that keeps him from being run by every passing state.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Cold dissociation called witness-discrimination. Emotional disengagement from life.
facets.somatic: Body disconnected from experience.
facets.emotional: Emotional coldness mistaken for discrimination.
facets.cognitive: Mental distance preventing engagement.
facets.psychic: The soul over-intellectualizes the categories, sorting experience endlessly rather than resting as the witness.

excess.text:
Cold dissociation called witness-discrimination. Emotional disengagement from life.

Somatic: Body disconnected from experience.

Emotional: Emotional coldness mistaken for discrimination.

Cognitive: Mental distance preventing engagement.

Psychic: The soul over-intellectualizes the categories, sorting experience endlessly rather than resting as the witness.
excess.vignette: Sam can discriminate self from non-self beautifully on paper—dissecting the categories, mastering the Sankhya—and lives entirely fused with his moods regardless. The daily discrimination has stayed in his head, a dry philosophy that never once loosened his grip on a single feeling.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total identification with body, mind, events. No witness position. Maximum bondage.
facets.somatic: Body experiences are ‘me.’
facets.emotional: Emotions ARE the self.
facets.cognitive: Mind takes its contents as ultimate.
facets.psychic: The soul never discriminates seer from seen, taking the whole show of nature as simply itself.

deficient.text:
Total identification with body, mind, events. No witness position. Maximum bondage.

Somatic: Body experiences are ‘me.’

Emotional: Emotions ARE the self.

Cognitive: Mind takes its contents as ultimate.

Psychic: The soul never discriminates seer from seen, taking the whole show of nature as simply itself.
deficient.vignette: Dan makes no discrimination at all—the seer and the seen wholly merged, every mood taken as himself, every thought as fact. The practical skill of telling awareness from its contents is one he’s never developed, and so each passing state simply is, for him, the whole of reality.

attributes.title: Detached Perception (Sakshi-Drishti)
attributes.aspect: Detached perception is a transformed mode of seeing—not the absence of seeing but seeing without entanglement. One perceives reality, including emotional reality, without immediate reactive engagement. This creates space for wise response rather than blind reaction.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna) · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: detached perception — seeing as the uninvolved witness.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna)
coordinate.key_relation: detached perception — seeing as the uninvolved witness.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Perception becomes naturally detached—seeing clearly without reactive entanglement. Wise response replaces blind reaction.
facets.somatic: Body’s perceptions clear without immediate reactivity.
facets.emotional: Emotional perceiving without instant identification.
facets.cognitive: Mental clarity from non-reactive perception.
facets.psychic: The soul perceives with a quiet detachment, present to experience without being captured by it.

balanced.text:
Perception becomes naturally detached—seeing clearly without reactive entanglement. Wise response replaces blind reaction.

Somatic: Body’s perceptions clear without immediate reactivity.

Emotional: Emotional perceiving without instant identification.

Cognitive: Mental clarity from non-reactive perception.

Psychic: The soul perceives with a quiet detachment, present to experience without being captured by it.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has learned to perceive with a light detachment—seeing the argument, the insult, the temptation clearly without being instantly hooked by it. The detached perception gives him a beat of freedom; he sees, and in the small space before reacting, he can choose.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Detachment becoming dissociation—loss of warm engagement, cold disconnection.
facets.somatic: Body in dissociated ‘witness’ mode.
facets.emotional: Emotional coldness mistaken for detachment.
facets.cognitive: Mental distance that prevents genuine engagement.
facets.psychic: The soul’s detachment chills into indifference, a withdrawal that no longer cares.

excess.text:
Detachment becoming dissociation—loss of warm engagement, cold disconnection.

Somatic: Body in dissociated ‘witness’ mode.

Emotional: Emotional coldness mistaken for detachment.

Cognitive: Mental distance that prevents genuine engagement.

Psychic: The soul’s detachment chills into indifference, a withdrawal that no longer cares.
excess.vignette: Sam’s detached perception has gone cold—observing everything, including others’ suffering, from a clinical remove that he mistakes for equanimity. The healthy detachment has become an estrangement; he sees clearly and feels nothing, and calls the numbness peace.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total reactive identification—every perception immediately reacted to, no witness-space.
facets.somatic: Body in constant reactive states.
facets.emotional: Emotional storms triggered by every perception.
facets.cognitive: Mind has no space between perception and reaction.
facets.psychic: The soul perceives only through craving and aversion, every sight colored by what it wants.

deficient.text:
Total reactive identification—every perception immediately reacted to, no witness-space.

Somatic: Body in constant reactive states.

Emotional: Emotional storms triggered by every perception.

Cognitive: Mind has no space between perception and reaction.

Psychic: The soul perceives only through craving and aversion, every sight colored by what it wants.
deficient.vignette: Dan perceives nothing with detachment—every perception grabs him instantly, the insult wounding, the temptation pulling, no gap anywhere between seeing and reacting. The detached witnessing that would give him room to respond is a capacity he’s never once exercised.

attributes.title: Witnessing (Sakshi-Bhava)
attributes.aspect: Sakshi-bhava is dual-attention—aware of object and aware of being aware. Most experience is one-pointed (lost in object); witnessing maintains both perspectives, transcending both eventually.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna) · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the witness-attitude (sākṣi-bhāva) held through experience.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna)
coordinate.key_relation: the witness-attitude (sākṣi-bhāva) held through experience.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Witnessing becomes effortless—simultaneous awareness of experience and the experiencer. The witness is recognized as the unchanging space in which all experience arises.
facets.somatic: Body functions, but witness watches—neither identified nor dissociated.
facets.emotional: Emotions arise, witness observes—neither suppressed nor identified.
facets.cognitive: Thoughts move, witness sees—mind becomes transparent to awareness.
facets.psychic: The soul cultivates the witness-attitude, holding thoughts and feelings as passing weather it observes.

balanced.text:
Witnessing becomes effortless—simultaneous awareness of experience and the experiencer. The witness is recognized as the unchanging space in which all experience arises.

Somatic: Body functions, but witness watches—neither identified nor dissociated.

Emotional: Emotions arise, witness observes—neither suppressed nor identified.

Cognitive: Thoughts move, witness sees—mind becomes transparent to awareness.

Psychic: The soul cultivates the witness-attitude, holding thoughts and feelings as passing weather it observes.
balanced.vignette: Through his day Meera keeps a light witness-attitude—present in her tasks and also, quietly, aware of the one who’s present—so that the busyness never quite swallows her. The sakshi-bhava isn’t withdrawal; it’s a background presence that keeps her from getting lost in the foreground.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Cold detachment mistaken for witnessing—dissociation from life, inability to engage warmly. Or forced witnessing creating split between experiencer and experience.
facets.somatic: Body disconnected from life, robotic existence.
facets.emotional: Emotional coldness, inability to feel genuinely while ‘witnessing.’
facets.cognitive: Forced division between observer and observed, mental strain.
facets.psychic: The soul makes witnessing a constant self-monitoring that crowds out simple living.

excess.text:
Cold detachment mistaken for witnessing—dissociation from life, inability to engage warmly. Or forced witnessing creating split between experiencer and experience.

Somatic: Body disconnected from life, robotic existence.

Emotional: Emotional coldness, inability to feel genuinely while ‘witnessing.’

Cognitive: Forced division between observer and observed, mental strain.

Psychic: The soul makes witnessing a constant self-monitoring that crowds out simple living.
excess.vignette: Sam uses the witness-attitude to check out—watching his life from a detached distance as an excuse to not fully show up, to not commit, to not feel. The healthy witnessing has become an avoidance dressed as spirituality, and he’s absent from his own life in the name of being ‘the witness’ of it.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete identification with experience—lost in every emotion, every thought, no witness present. Slavery to state-contents.
facets.somatic: Body’s needs and reactions taking complete control.
facets.emotional: Drowned in emotions without witness, full reactive identification.
facets.cognitive: Mind lost in its own contents, no awareness of awareness, complete unconsciousness.
facets.psychic: The soul has no witnessing attitude, identified without remainder with each passing state.

deficient.text:
Complete identification with experience—lost in every emotion, every thought, no witness present. Slavery to state-contents.

Somatic: Body’s needs and reactions taking complete control.

Emotional: Drowned in emotions without witness, full reactive identification.

Cognitive: Mind lost in its own contents, no awareness of awareness, complete unconsciousness.

Psychic: The soul has no witnessing attitude, identified without remainder with each passing state.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no witness-attitude at all—wholly absorbed in whatever he’s doing or feeling, no quiet awareness standing behind the activity. The background presence that would keep him from being swallowed by the foreground is one he’s never cultivated; he’s all foreground, always.

attributes.title: Mirror Consciousness (Pure Witness)
attributes.aspect: Consciousness is like a mirror—it reflects all experiences but is never touched by them. The fiction is that what is reflected becomes part of us. Knowing oneself as the mirror, not the reflections, is liberation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna) · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: pure mirror-awareness — reflecting all, touched by none.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna)
coordinate.key_relation: pure mirror-awareness — reflecting all, touched by none.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Self known as mirror-like—reflecting all experiences without becoming any of them. Always virgin, always pure, never scarred by what passes through.
facets.somatic: Body experiences sensations, mirror-self reflects them without contamination.
facets.emotional: Emotions arise, mirror remains spotless—witnessing without identification.
facets.cognitive: Mind’s contents reflected, never absorbed. Always available as pure space.
facets.psychic: The soul rests as a mirror, reflecting all that passes without being stained or moved by any of it.

balanced.text:
Self known as mirror-like—reflecting all experiences without becoming any of them. Always virgin, always pure, never scarred by what passes through.

Somatic: Body experiences sensations, mirror-self reflects them without contamination.

Emotional: Emotions arise, mirror remains spotless—witnessing without identification.

Cognitive: Mind’s contents reflected, never absorbed. Always available as pure space.

Psychic: The soul rests as a mirror, reflecting all that passes without being stained or moved by any of it.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to reflect the world like a clean mirror—receiving what comes without the usual distortions of craving and fear, seeing more nearly what’s actually there. The mirror-consciousness isn’t passive; it’s a clarity that lets him respond to reality rather than to his projections onto it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Claiming mirror-consciousness intellectually while still emotionally reactive—pretense of detachment masking real identification.
facets.somatic: Body still tense and reactive despite intellectual claims.
facets.emotional: Suppressed emotions claiming witnessing—not real freedom.
facets.cognitive: Mental claim of mirror-status, dissociation mistaken for awareness.
facets.psychic: The soul prizes its ‘mirror-like’ clarity as an attainment, the pride dimming the very reflection.

excess.text:
Claiming mirror-consciousness intellectually while still emotionally reactive—pretense of detachment masking real identification.

Somatic: Body still tense and reactive despite intellectual claims.

Emotional: Suppressed emotions claiming witnessing—not real freedom.

Cognitive: Mental claim of mirror-status, dissociation mistaken for awareness.

Psychic: The soul prizes its ‘mirror-like’ clarity as an attainment, the pride dimming the very reflection.
excess.vignette: Sam claims his mind is ‘a pure mirror, reflecting without distortion’—while his perceptions are as warped by ego and desire as anyone’s. He’s mistaken the ideal for the attainment, and the confident claim of clarity is itself one of his distortions.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Complete identification with reflections—what is reflected is taken as the substance. All experiences become self, all changes felt as self-change.
facets.somatic: Body’s states felt as Self—every pain as ‘I am pain.’
facets.emotional: Every emotion is ‘I am angry/sad/happy’—total identification.
facets.cognitive: Every thought is ‘I think this’—mind taken as self.
facets.psychic: The soul is no mirror but a sponge, soaking up and clinging to every image that appears.

deficient.text:
Complete identification with reflections—what is reflected is taken as the substance. All experiences become self, all changes felt as self-change.

Somatic: Body’s states felt as Self—every pain as ‘I am pain.’

Emotional: Every emotion is ‘I am angry/sad/happy’—total identification.

Cognitive: Every thought is ‘I think this’—mind taken as self.

Psychic: The soul is no mirror but a sponge, soaking up and clinging to every image that appears.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s mind distorts everything it reflects—every perception bent by his fears, his cravings, his old wounds, so that he rarely sees what’s actually there. The clean mirror that reflects reality undistorted is a state he’s never approached; his glass is warped, and he takes the warp for the world.

attributes.title: Bhuyo-Bhuya Progressive Inquiry (Ladder)
attributes.aspect: The whole 7th chapter of Chhandogya is one continuous progressive teaching with 26 sequential meditations. Each is provisionally identified with Brahman, then transcended for a higher recognition. This forms a systematic ladder of inquiry that practitioners can climb step by step, ensuring no level is missed.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna) · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the ladder of progressive inquiry, again and again; kin to Bhṛgu’s inquiry (Dim 29).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna)
coordinate.key_relation: the ladder of progressive inquiry, again and again; kin to Bhṛgu’s inquiry (Dim 29).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Systematic engagement with the 26-step ladder. Each level fully recognized before moving to the next. Progressive deepening.
facets.somatic: Body engaged at each level appropriately.
facets.emotional: Emotional dimension explored at each rung.
facets.cognitive: Mind systematically penetrating from gross to subtle to infinite.
facets.psychic: The soul inquires again and again, climbing rung by rung as each level of self is recognized and surrendered.

balanced.text:
Systematic engagement with the 26-step ladder. Each level fully recognized before moving to the next. Progressive deepening.

Somatic: Body engaged at each level appropriately.

Emotional: Emotional dimension explored at each rung.

Cognitive: Mind systematically penetrating from gross to subtle to infinite.

Psychic: The soul inquires again and again, climbing rung by rung as each level of self is recognized and surrendered.
balanced.vignette: Ravi climbs his self-inquiry like a ladder—each insight a rung, held and then stepped past toward a deeper one, never mistaking a way-station for the top. The progressive inquiry has a patient momentum; he keeps going ‘more and more,’ and the more-and-more is carrying him steadily inward.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mechanical going-through levels without genuine realization at each. Checklist spirituality.
facets.somatic: Body without genuine practice at each level.
facets.emotional: Mechanical emotional engagement.
facets.cognitive: Conceptual progression without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on the ladder of stages, measuring its ascent rather than continuing to climb.

excess.text:
Mechanical going-through levels without genuine realization at each. Checklist spirituality.

Somatic: Body without genuine practice at each level.

Emotional: Mechanical emotional engagement.

Cognitive: Conceptual progression without realization.

Psychic: The soul fixates on the ladder of stages, measuring its ascent rather than continuing to climb.
excess.vignette: Sam rushes the ladder—skipping rungs, claiming the higher insights he hasn’t earned, impatient to be at the top. The progressive inquiry has become a grab for the summit, and in leaping past the middle steps he keeps falling back to the bottom.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Stuck at first level (Name) or completely outside the ladder. No systematic ascending inquiry.
facets.somatic: Body never engages spiritual inquiry.
facets.emotional: Emotional life unexplored systematically.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t engage progressive ascent.
facets.psychic: The soul never begins the inquiry, content at the lowest rung with no sense of any height above.

deficient.text:
Stuck at first level (Name) or completely outside the ladder. No systematic ascending inquiry.

Somatic: Body never engages spiritual inquiry.

Emotional: Emotional life unexplored systematically.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t engage progressive ascent.

Psychic: The soul never begins the inquiry, content at the lowest rung with no sense of any height above.
deficient.vignette: Dan climbs no ladder at all—no inquiry, no progression, no sense that self-knowledge deepens step by step. The patient ‘more and more’ that carries a seeker inward is a movement he’s never begun; he stands at the foot of a stair he’s never noticed.

attributes.title: Bhuyo-Bhuya (More and More - Progressive Inquiry)
attributes.aspect: Narada asks Sanatkumara to teach him beyond his vast learning. Sanatkumara teaches progressively: ‘meditate on name’ (vac), then ‘higher than name is speech,’ then ‘higher than speech is mind,’ then samkalpa, chitta, dhyana, vijnana, bala, anna, apa, tejas, akasha, smrti, asha, prana. Each step is ‘bhuyo-bhuya’—more and more inquiry. Finally arrives at Bhuma (Infinite).

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna) · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: ‘more and more’ — inquiry deepened by repetition.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna)
coordinate.key_relation: ‘more and more’ — inquiry deepened by repetition.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Continuous progressive inquiry—never settling at one level, always asking ‘is there more?’ until Bhuma is realized.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in continuous inquiry-practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional restlessness for deeper truth.
facets.cognitive: Mind never settles at any level, always seeking deeper.
facets.psychic: The soul deepens its inquiry ‘more and more,’ never resting in a penultimate answer.

balanced.text:
Continuous progressive inquiry—never settling at one level, always asking ‘is there more?’ until Bhuma is realized.

Somatic: Body engaged in continuous inquiry-practice.

Emotional: Emotional restlessness for deeper truth.

Cognitive: Mind never settles at any level, always seeking deeper.

Psychic: The soul deepens its inquiry ‘more and more,’ never resting in a penultimate answer.
balanced.vignette: Whenever Ravi’s inquiry stalls at a comfortable answer, he presses on—‘more, and more’—refusing to settle, questioning the questioner, deepening the seeing. The relentless return to the inquiry isn’t restlessness; it’s an honest refusal to stop short of what he’s actually looking for.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Endless inquiry as escape from realization—using ‘more and more’ to avoid commitment to any level.
facets.somatic: Body in eternal seeking without arrival.
facets.emotional: Emotional dissatisfaction without resolution.
facets.cognitive: Mind addicted to inquiry, never resting in any answer.
facets.psychic: The soul turns relentless inquiry into anxious dissatisfaction, unable to rest anywhere.

excess.text:
Endless inquiry as escape from realization—using ‘more and more’ to avoid commitment to any level.

Somatic: Body in eternal seeking without arrival.

Emotional: Emotional dissatisfaction without resolution.

Cognitive: Mind addicted to inquiry, never resting in any answer.

Psychic: The soul turns relentless inquiry into anxious dissatisfaction, unable to rest anywhere.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned ‘more and more’ into a compulsive spiritual seeking—never resting, never satisfied, forever chasing the next insight, the next teacher, the next depth. The healthy relentlessness has become a treadmill; he can’t arrive because arriving would end the seeking he’s addicted to.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Settling at first available answer—no progressive inquiry, taking surface understanding as complete.
facets.somatic: Body satisfied with shallow practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional satisfaction with surface understanding.
facets.cognitive: Mind doesn’t push deeper, accepts initial level as final.
facets.psychic: The soul asks once and stops, settling for the first easy answer it is given.

deficient.text:
Settling at first available answer—no progressive inquiry, taking surface understanding as complete.

Somatic: Body satisfied with shallow practice.

Emotional: Emotional satisfaction with surface understanding.

Cognitive: Mind doesn’t push deeper, accepts initial level as final.

Psychic: The soul asks once and stops, settling for the first easy answer it is given.
deficient.vignette: Dan takes the first easy answer and stops—no ‘more and more,’ no deepening, no return to the question once a comfortable resting place appears. The relentless inquiry that carries a seeker past every plateau is foreign to him; he’s camped on the ground floor and calls it the view from the top.

attributes.title: Neti-Neti (Not This, Not This)
attributes.aspect: Of the Self one can only say ‘not this, not this.’ For there is nothing higher than this. Negation as the method—any specific characterization is denied because Self exceeds all conceptual definition.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna) · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: knowing by negation (‘not this, not this’); same as Neti-Neti (Dim 28).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna)
coordinate.key_relation: knowing by negation (‘not this, not this’); same as Neti-Neti (Dim 28).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Systematic negation that reveals what cannot be negated. Self is realized through what it is NOT.
facets.somatic: Body negated as Self while still maintained.
facets.emotional: Emotions negated as Self while still felt.
facets.cognitive: Mind negated as Self—witness remains.
facets.psychic: The soul clears away every false identification — ‘not this, not this’ — until only the undeniable awareness remains.

balanced.text:
Systematic negation that reveals what cannot be negated. Self is realized through what it is NOT.

Somatic: Body negated as Self while still maintained.

Emotional: Emotions negated as Self while still felt.

Cognitive: Mind negated as Self—witness remains.

Psychic: The soul clears away every false identification — ‘not this, not this’ — until only the undeniable awareness remains.
balanced.vignette: When Ravi asks ‘who am I?’, he sets aside each answer—not the body, not the emotions, not the thoughts—until what remains is the awareness doing the setting-aside. The negation is a doorway; by clearing away what he isn’t, he arrives at what he is.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Nihilistic negation—‘I am nothing, life is nothing’ missing the witness that remains after negation.
facets.somatic: Body neglected through nihilistic denial.
facets.emotional: Emotional flatness from over-negation.
facets.cognitive: Mental nihilism, no positive realization following negation.
facets.psychic: The soul makes negation a barren denial, arriving at a blank rather than the fullness beyond all ‘this.’

excess.text:
Nihilistic negation—‘I am nothing, life is nothing’ missing the witness that remains after negation.

Somatic: Body neglected through nihilistic denial.

Emotional: Emotional flatness from over-negation.

Cognitive: Mental nihilism, no positive realization following negation.

Psychic: The soul makes negation a barren denial, arriving at a blank rather than the fullness beyond all ‘this.’
excess.vignette: Sam has turned ‘not this, not this’ into a nihilism—negating everything, valuing nothing, using the method to dismiss the whole world as unreal and himself along with it. The tool for finding the Self has become a solvent that dissolves meaning wholesale.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No negative method—identifying positively with body, mind, emotions, ego.
facets.somatic: Body affirmed as self.
facets.emotional: Emotions affirmed as self.
facets.cognitive: Mind affirmed as self without negation-purification.
facets.psychic: The soul clutches every passing form as ‘this is me,’ the cleansing ‘no’ never spoken.

deficient.text:
No negative method—identifying positively with body, mind, emotions, ego.

Somatic: Body affirmed as self.

Emotional: Emotions affirmed as self.

Cognitive: Mind affirmed as self without negation-purification.

Psychic: The soul clutches every passing form as ‘this is me,’ the cleansing ‘no’ never spoken.
deficient.vignette: Dan can’t set anything aside—he is his body, his moods, his thoughts, each identification total. The negating inquiry that would reveal the witness beneath is not something he’s failed at; it’s a move he’s never once made.

attributes.title: Ātma-Vichāra — Direct Self-Inquiry
attributes.aspect: Ātma-vichāra is the direct investigation into the nature of the self — the persistent turning of attention away from its objects and back toward the one who is aware, asking ‘who am I?’ or tracing every experience back to the ‘I’ to which it appears. Unlike practices that add something (a mantra, an image, a state), inquiry subtracts: it dissolves the false identifications until what remains is the simple, self-evident awareness that was looking all along. It is the most direct of practices and, for that reason, the one most easily turned into mere mental activity rather than living investigation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna) · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: direct self-inquiry — ‘Who am I?’; the heart of the Jñāna path.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna)
coordinate.key_relation: direct self-inquiry — ‘Who am I?’; the heart of the Jñāna path.

balanced.label: Balanced — living investigation that loosens identification

facets.overview: Sustained intense inquiry into one’s nature. Each identification examined and seen through.
facets.somatic: Body acknowledged as instrument, not self.
facets.emotional: Emotions recognized as movements, not self.
facets.cognitive: Mind seen as appearance, the witness is recognized.
facets.psychic: The soul turns to ask ‘Who am I?’ and follows the ‘I’-thought to its source; inquiry becomes the most direct movement back to its own ground.

balanced.text:
Sustained intense inquiry into one’s nature. Each identification examined and seen through.

Somatic: Body acknowledged as instrument, not self.

Emotional: Emotions recognized as movements, not self.

Cognitive: Mind seen as appearance, the witness is recognized.

Psychic: The soul turns to ask ‘Who am I?’ and follows the ‘I’-thought to its source; inquiry becomes the most direct movement back to its own ground.
balanced.vignette: Whenever the sense of ‘me’ flares—the hurt pride, the anxious grasping—Ravi turns and asks ‘who is this I?’, tracing the feeling back toward its source. The self-inquiry isn’t intellectual; it’s a lived turning-inward that, again and again, loosens the very ‘I’ it questions.

excess.label: In excess — inquiry as dry mental gymnastics; analysis replacing seeing

facets.overview: Endless intellectual inquiry without genuine penetration. Mental gymnastics.
facets.somatic: Body neglected in obsessive inquiry.
facets.emotional: Emotional dissociation through inquiry.
facets.cognitive: Mind playing word-games with ‘Who am I?’
facets.psychic: Inquiry becomes a mental loop; the soul turns ‘Who am I?’ into an obsessive thought rather than a sinking into the silence behind thought.

excess.text:
Endless intellectual inquiry without genuine penetration. Mental gymnastics.

Somatic: Body neglected in obsessive inquiry.

Emotional: Emotional dissociation through inquiry.

Cognitive: Mind playing word-games with ‘Who am I?’

Psychic: Inquiry becomes a mental loop; the soul turns ‘Who am I?’ into an obsessive thought rather than a sinking into the silence behind thought.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned self-inquiry into a mental loop—‘who am I? who am I?’ repeated as a mantra, analyzed, debated, kept safely in the head where it changes nothing. The direct turning-inward has become one more thought, and the questioner he keeps asking about is running the whole exercise.

deficient.label: Deficient — no self-investigation; identity assumed and never examined

facets.overview: Never asking the fundamental question. Identity assumed without examination.
facets.somatic: Body identification unchallenged.
facets.emotional: Emotional identity taken as final.
facets.cognitive: Mind operates with unexamined assumptions about identity.
facets.psychic: The soul never asks the question; identity is assumed and never examined, and the one inquiry that could free it goes unmade.

deficient.text:
Never asking the fundamental question. Identity assumed without examination.

Somatic: Body identification unchallenged.

Emotional: Emotional identity taken as final.

Cognitive: Mind operates with unexamined assumptions about identity.

Psychic: The soul never asks the question; identity is assumed and never examined, and the one inquiry that could free it goes unmade.
deficient.vignette: Dan never turns the question on himself—the ‘I’ that suffers and grasps taken utterly for granted, never once inquired into. The direct self-inquiry that could loosen his deepest knot is a practice he’s never attempted; the questioner has never questioned itself.

attributes.title: Kirtanam (Chanting/Singing the Divine Name)
attributes.aspect: Kirtanam is using the voice for chanting divine names, singing devotional songs, or speaking sacred truths. In daily life, this means transforming the use of speech itself — singing during chores, chanting while walking, using verbal energy for elevation rather than dispersal. Even soft chanting (japa) under the breath counts. The vibration physically affects the body. Kirtanam means singing/chanting — using one’s voice for divine names rather than gossip or complaint. In daily life: singing while cooking, walking, doing chores transforms the mood of these activities. Voice becomes instrument of devotion.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: chanting the Divine Name (kīrtanam); a limb of Bhakti.
coordinate.key_relation: chanting the Divine Name (kīrtanam); a limb of Bhakti.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Voice integrated with devotional sound throughout daily activities. Singing/chanting becomes natural background.
facets.somatic: Voice strengthened, body vibrating with sacred sound.
facets.emotional: Emotional mood elevated naturally throughout day.
facets.cognitive: Mind anchored through verbal devotion.
facets.psychic: The soul pours itself into sacred song, the chanted name carrying the heart past its own boundaries.

balanced.text:

Emotional: Emotional mood elevated naturally throughout day.

Cognitive: Mind anchored through verbal devotion.

Psychic: The soul pours itself into sacred song, the chanted name carrying the heart past its own boundaries.
balanced.vignette: When Ravi sings the divine name, something in his chest opens—the chanting carrying his heart past its usual guardedness into a warm, devotional absorption. The kirtan isn’t performance; it’s a doorway his heart steps through, and it leaves him softer for hours after.

excess.label: In excess

facets.somatic: Vocal strain from forced practice.
facets.emotional: Empty performance.
facets.cognitive: Mind disconnected from what mouth says.
facets.psychic: The soul performs the chant for show or fervor’s sake, mistaking emotional intensity for devotion.

excess.text:

Psychic: The soul performs the chant for show or fervor’s sake, mistaking emotional intensity for devotion.

The living, heart-opening practice has become a superstitious tic, and the mechanical repetition has squeezed the devotion right out of it.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Voice used for complaint, gossip, harsh words. Verbal energy dispersed in low-grade ways.
facets.somatic: Voice strained by complaint and anger.
facets.emotional: Emotional pollution from harsh speech.
facets.cognitive: Mind defined by negative talk.
facets.psychic: The soul never sings, the heart’s natural music silenced, with no sound to lift it.

Cognitive: Mind defined by negative talk.

Psychic: The soul never sings, the heart’s natural music silenced, with no sound to lift it.
deficient.vignette: Dan never sings, never chants, and the whole warm register of devotional sound is absent from his days—only the flat noise of the ordinary. The heart-opening the divine name can offer is a doorway he’s never approached; his practice, such as it is, stays dry and wordless.

attributes.title: Saintly Company (Satsang as Daily Need)
attributes.aspect: The Bhagavata emphasizes the transformative power of saintly company (satsang). Signs of a saint: their conversation gravitates toward the Divine; they have compassion for all; they are one-pointed yet inclusive. In daily life this means actively seeking elevating company (in-person or through books/recordings) and minimizing degrading company. Like associates like; you become what you keep company with. Bhagavata emphasizes: keep company of saints. Recognize them by how they talk — always speaking of the Divine, compassion for all. In daily life: consciously seek satsang (saintly company); minimize toxic company. The company you keep determines what you become.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: satsang — the company of the wise as daily need.
coordinate.key_relation: satsang — the company of the wise as daily need.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Daily company consciously cultivated toward saintly. Live presence, books, recordings — all chosen with awareness.
facets.somatic: Body increasingly in elevated environments.
facets.emotional: Emotional life nourished by saintly company.
facets.cognitive: Mind shaped by elevating association.
facets.psychic: The soul keeps the company of the wise and sincere, knowing the inner life is shaped by whom it sits near.

balanced.text:
Daily company consciously cultivated toward saintly. Live presence, books, recordings — all chosen with awareness.

Emotional: Emotional life nourished by saintly company.

Cognitive: Mind shaped by elevating association.

Psychic: The soul keeps the company of the wise and sincere, knowing the inner life is shaped by whom it sits near.
balanced.vignette: Ravi seeks out, regularly, the company of those who lift him toward what’s true—a weekly gathering, a wise friend, a community of practice—and returns from it steadied and clearer. He treats satsang as a genuine need, the way one treats food; good company shapes him, and he keeps himself in its way.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Isolation from ‘unspiritual’ people; cult-like exclusion. Spiritual snobbery.
facets.somatic: Body cut off from normal social engagement.
facets.emotional: Emotional isolation in spiritual identity.
facets.cognitive: Mental judgment of non-spiritual people.
facets.psychic: The soul makes satsang a social identity or dependency, leaning on company rather than ripening within.

excess.text:
Isolation from ‘unspiritual’ people; cult-like exclusion. Spiritual snobbery.

Somatic: Body cut off from normal social engagement.

Emotional: Emotional isolation in spiritual identity.

Cognitive: Mental judgment of non-spiritual people.

Psychic: The soul makes satsang a social identity or dependency, leaning on company rather than ripening within.
excess.vignette: Sam has become dependent on his spiritual community—unable to think a thought it hasn’t approved, terrified of leaving the fold, his identity wholly borrowed from the group. Good company has become a crutch that’s replaced his own discernment; the satsang meant to free him now defines him.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Surrounded entirely by toxic, degrading company. No elevating influences in daily life.
facets.somatic: Body in degrading environments.
facets.emotional: Emotional life shaped by negativity.
facets.cognitive: Mind shaped by lowest-common-denominator company.
facets.psychic: The soul keeps only company that pulls it downward, with no association that lifts.

deficient.text:

Psychic: The soul keeps only company that pulls it downward, with no association that lifts.
deficient.vignette: Dan keeps no company that lifts him—isolated, or surrounded by influences that pull him down, with no satsang to shape him toward the good. The nourishing power of holy company is something he’s never sought or felt, and he practices, when he does, entirely alone and adrift.

attributes.title: Dissociating Objects From Their Connections
attributes.aspect: Every object comes to us pre-coded with connections—possession, function, judgment, history. Dissociating objects from these accreted connections is a profound practice. The cup becomes simply a cup, not ‘my cup,’ ‘my mother’s cup,’ ‘the cup I bought.’ Pure perception emerges.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna) · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: dissociating objects from their habitual associations — fresh perception.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna)
coordinate.key_relation: dissociating objects from their habitual associations — fresh perception.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Objects perceived in their bare nature without accreted connections. Profound clarity in everyday perception.
facets.somatic: Body interacts with objects directly without burden of connections.
facets.emotional: Emotional relationship with objects free from history.
facets.cognitive: Mind perceives objects as they are, not as conceptually constructed.
facets.psychic: The soul learns to see each object freshly, freed from the web of craving and association that usually grips it.

balanced.text:
Objects perceived in their bare nature without accreted connections. Profound clarity in everyday perception.

Somatic: Body interacts with objects directly without burden of connections.

Emotional: Emotional relationship with objects free from history.

Cognitive: Mind perceives objects as they are, not as conceptually constructed.

Psychic: The soul learns to see each object freshly, freed from the web of craving and association that usually grips it.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has learned to see a thing freshly—the coffee cup, the familiar face, the daily task—stripped for a moment of its habitual associations, as if for the first time. The practice restores a quiet wonder; freed from the film of assumption, the ordinary world grows vivid again.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced dissociation creating cold distance from all objects—pathological detachment.
facets.somatic: Body in alienated relationship with all objects.
facets.emotional: Emotional disconnection from meaningful objects.
facets.cognitive: Mental cold-analysis without warmth.
facets.psychic: The soul over-analyzes its perceptions, dissecting experience until the living world goes flat.

excess.text:
Forced dissociation creating cold distance from all objects—pathological detachment.

Somatic: Body in alienated relationship with all objects.

Cognitive: Mental cold-analysis without warmth.

Psychic: The soul over-analyzes its perceptions, dissecting experience until the living world goes flat.
excess.vignette: Sam has practiced dissociating objects from their meanings so relentlessly that the world has gone flat and derealized—everything stripped of connection, floating and unreal, a vertigo he mistakes for insight. The freeing practice has become an estrangement from a world he can no longer feel connected to.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Every object encrusted with connections—‘my,’ ‘this means,’ ‘this is for.’ Reality buried under conceptual overlay.
facets.somatic: Body relates only through possession-claims.
facets.emotional: Emotional life entangled in object-histories.
facets.cognitive: Mind cannot perceive any object without conceptual baggage.
facets.psychic: The soul sees only through old associations, every object pre-wrapped in its conditioned meaning.

deficient.text:
Every object encrusted with connections—‘my,’ ‘this means,’ ‘this is for.’ Reality buried under conceptual overlay.

Somatic: Body relates only through possession-claims.

Emotional: Emotional life entangled in object-histories.

Cognitive: Mind cannot perceive any object without conceptual baggage.

Psychic: The soul sees only through old associations, every object pre-wrapped in its conditioned meaning.
deficient.vignette: Dan sees nothing freshly—every object buried under its habitual associations, every person filed under old assumptions, the world a set of fixed labels. The practice of seeing things stripped of their conditioned meanings is one he’s never tried; his world is entirely secondhand.

attributes.title: Pranava Sound (Internal Om)
attributes.aspect: Pranava (Om) is heard internally as actual sound as Kriya progresses. Different stages produce different inner sounds: bees-buzzing, flute, lute, bell, conch, thunder, lion’s roar. These are not metaphors but actual auditory experiences. The conches of the Mahabharata are Lahiri’s allegory for these inner sounds.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the internal OM (praṇava) as sound-support; kin to Praṇava (Dim 11).
coordinate.key_relation: the internal OM (praṇava) as sound-support; kin to Praṇava (Dim 11).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Inner Pranava sound naturally heard during deep practice. Each stage reveals more refined sounds.
facets.somatic: Body’s nervous system tuned to inner sound vibration.
facets.emotional: Emotional unification through sound resonance.
facets.cognitive: Mind absorbed in continuous inner sound, naturally one-pointed.
facets.psychic: The soul listens inward to the silent OM, the subtle internal sound that gathers the scattered mind.

balanced.text:
Inner Pranava sound naturally heard during deep practice. Each stage reveals more refined sounds.

Somatic: Body’s nervous system tuned to inner sound vibration.

Emotional: Emotional unification through sound resonance.

Cognitive: Mind absorbed in continuous inner sound, naturally one-pointed.

Psychic: The soul listens inward to the silent OM, the subtle internal sound that gathers the scattered mind.
balanced.vignette: In the quiet of practice, Ravi rests on an inner Om—a subtle vibration beneath the silence that gathers and steadies his whole system. The internal sound has become a thread he follows inward, and following it, the noise of his mind gradually falls away.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forcing hallucinatory sounds, mistaking imagination for real inner Pranava.
facets.somatic: Strain trying to hear sounds.
facets.emotional: Anxiety about not hearing.
facets.cognitive: Mind constructs phantom sounds.
facets.psychic: The soul chases inner sound-phenomena as attainments, fixating on the sound over the silence it opens.

excess.text:

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No awareness of inner sound dimension. Practice without this anchor.
facets.somatic: Body not yet sensitive to subtle sound.
facets.emotional: Missing the unification through sound.
facets.cognitive: Mind without sound-anchor.
facets.psychic: The soul never hears the inner sound, deaf to the subtle vibration beneath its noise.

deficient.text:
No awareness of inner sound dimension. Practice without this anchor.

Psychic: The soul never hears the inner sound, deaf to the subtle vibration beneath its noise.
deficient.vignette: Dan has never sat quietly enough to notice any inner sound at all—the subtle Om beneath the silence a dimension he’s passed over entirely. The internal anchor the practice offers is inaccessible to a mind that never grows quiet enough to listen.

attributes.title: Stages of Inner Sound (Nada Yoga in Kriya)
attributes.aspect: As Kriya advances, the practitioner hears progressively subtler inner sounds. Lahiri identifies the Mahabharata conches as these sounds: Panchajanya=bees/flute/lute/bell/thunder; Devadatta=bell sustain; Poundra=lion roar; Anantavijaya=Pranava unbroken; Sughosha=beautiful sound; Manipushpaka=pure sound. Each is a milestone of Kriya progress.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the graded inner sounds (nāda) in Kriyā; kin to Nāda (Dim 26).
coordinate.key_relation: the graded inner sounds (nāda) in Kriyā; kin to Nāda (Dim 26).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.somatic: Subtle auditory perception developing.
facets.emotional: Emotional refinement through sound stages.
facets.cognitive: Mind progressively absorbed in subtler sounds.
facets.psychic: The soul follows the inner sound through its deepening stages, drawn ever inward toward its source.

balanced.text:

balanced.vignette: As his practice has deepened, Ravi has heard the inner sound change and refine—coarser tones giving way to subtler ones—and he follows it patiently, letting each stage draw him inward. The nada isn’t a curiosity to him; it’s a ladder of sound leading toward stillness, and he climbs it rung by subtle rung.

excess.label: In excess

facets.somatic: Body strained trying to hear specific sounds.
facets.emotional: Emotional dependence on auditory experiences.
facets.cognitive: Mind grasping at sounds, missing the witness.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on cataloguing the sounds, collecting subtle experiences rather than dissolving into them.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No development of inner auditory dimension. Practice without this rich domain.
facets.somatic: Body without subtle sound-perception.
facets.emotional: Missing emotional resonance with inner sound.
facets.cognitive: Mind without the sound-anchor.
facets.psychic: The soul never accesses the inner soundscape, knowing only the clamor of the outer world.

attributes.title: OM as Akshara (Imperishable Syllable)
attributes.aspect: The goal that all Vedas declare, that all austerities aim at, that for which they live in brahmacharya—I tell briefly: it is OM. This syllable is Brahman; this syllable is supreme. Whoever knows it gets what he desires.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: OM as the imperishable syllable (akṣara); kin to Akshara Vidya (Dim 25).
coordinate.key_relation: OM as the imperishable syllable (akṣara); kin to Akshara Vidya (Dim 25).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: OM held as the supreme support, used as practice and recognition of Brahman.
facets.somatic: Body resonates with sacred sound.
facets.emotional: Emotional unification through Om.
facets.cognitive: Mind anchored in the imperishable syllable.
facets.psychic: The soul takes OM as the imperishable syllable in which the whole of reality is gathered, and rests in its vibration.

balanced.text:
OM held as the supreme support, used as practice and recognition of Brahman.

Somatic: Body resonates with sacred sound.

Emotional: Emotional unification through Om.

Cognitive: Mind anchored in the imperishable syllable.

Psychic: The soul takes OM as the imperishable syllable in which the whole of reality is gathered, and rests in its vibration.
balanced.vignette: Ravi contemplates Om as the imperishable—the one constant syllable beneath all the changing sounds and forms—and resting on it, he touches, faintly, the changeless it names. The Akshara isn’t just a syllable to him; it’s a pointer to what doesn’t perish, and the pointing steadies him amid all that does.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mechanical Om repetition without realization—chanting becomes substitute for direct realization.
facets.somatic: Vocal strain without inner resonance.
facets.emotional: Empty chanting without devotional warmth.
facets.cognitive: Mechanical mind during repetition.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on the syllable mechanically, repeating without the recognition it carries.

excess.text:
Mechanical Om repetition without realization—chanting becomes substitute for direct realization.

Somatic: Vocal strain without inner resonance.

Emotional: Empty chanting without devotional warmth.

Cognitive: Mechanical mind during repetition.

Psychic: The soul fixates on the syllable mechanically, repeating without the recognition it carries.
excess.vignette: Sam has literalized Om-as-imperishable into a magic word—convinced the mere sound is eternally potent, chanting it superstitiously, mistaking the syllable for the reality. The pointer to the changeless has become an object he clings to, and the clinging is precisely the perishable grasping it was meant to dissolve.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Missing the sacred sound dimension entirely—rationalist dismissal of mantra practice.
facets.somatic: No sacred vibration practice.
facets.emotional: Missing the unifying power of sacred sound.
facets.cognitive: Mind without sacred anchor.
facets.psychic: The soul has no sacred syllable to gather it, the imperishable unheard amid the perishable.

deficient.text:
Missing the sacred sound dimension entirely—rationalist dismissal of mantra practice.

Somatic: No sacred vibration practice.

Emotional: Missing the unifying power of sacred sound.

Cognitive: Mind without sacred anchor.

Psychic: The soul has no sacred syllable to gather it, the imperishable unheard amid the perishable.
deficient.vignette: For Dan Om is just a sound—a syllable people hum, pointing to nothing imperishable, meaning nothing beyond itself. The changeless reality the sacred syllable indicates is absent from his experience, so the word hovers, referring to a permanence he’s never sensed.

attributes.title: Om Meditation (Three Matras)
attributes.aspect: Meditating on Om with one matra (sound-unit) leads to human birth; with two matras to the lunar realm; with three matras one is identified with Purusha. The depth of Om-meditation determines its result.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: meditation on OM’s three mātras (A-U-M); the four states (Dim 4).
coordinate.key_relation: meditation on OM’s three mātras (A-U-M); the four states (Dim 4).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Deep three-matra Om meditation leading to absorption in Purusha. Wise depth of practice.
facets.somatic: Body’s resonance with sacred sound at deep level.
facets.emotional: Emotional unification through Om.
facets.cognitive: Mind absorbed in three matras, identified with Purusha.
facets.psychic: The soul meditates on OM’s three measures — waking, dream, sleep — and the silence that holds them.

balanced.text:
Deep three-matra Om meditation leading to absorption in Purusha. Wise depth of practice.

Somatic: Body’s resonance with sacred sound at deep level.

Emotional: Emotional unification through Om.

Cognitive: Mind absorbed in three matras, identified with Purusha.

Psychic: The soul meditates on OM’s three measures — waking, dream, sleep — and the silence that holds them.
balanced.vignette: Meditating on the three matras of Om—the A rising, the U sustaining, the M dissolving—Ravi feels the sound carry him through the waking, dreaming, and sleeping worlds toward their common ground. The structured meditation isn’t mechanical; each matra opens a layer, and the sequence draws him steadily deeper.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced advanced practice without preparation—pranic disturbance from forced Om-techniques.
facets.somatic: Pranic disorders from forced advanced practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional disturbance from rushed practice.
facets.cognitive: Mental complications from forced advanced meditation.
facets.psychic: The soul over-technicalizes the measures, analyzing the syllable rather than dissolving into it.

excess.text:
Forced advanced practice without preparation—pranic disturbance from forced Om-techniques.

Somatic: Pranic disorders from forced advanced practice.

Emotional: Emotional disturbance from rushed practice.

Cognitive: Mental complications from forced advanced meditation.

Psychic: The soul over-technicalizes the measures, analyzing the syllable rather than dissolving into it.
excess.vignette: Sam performs the three-matra meditation with rigid precision—obsessing over the exact durations, the perfect pronunciation—so fixated on the technique that the depth it’s meant to open never arrives. He’s polished the container and never tasted the contents.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Shallow Om practice or no Om practice—missing this profound technique.
facets.somatic: Body without sacred sound resonance.
facets.emotional: Missing devotional unification.
facets.cognitive: Mind without the Om-anchor for absorption.
facets.psychic: The soul never meditates on the sacred sound, its three states unexamined and unintegrated.

deficient.text:
Shallow Om practice or no Om practice—missing this profound technique.

Somatic: Body without sacred sound resonance.

Emotional: Missing devotional unification.

Cognitive: Mind without the Om-anchor for absorption.

Psychic: The soul never meditates on the sacred sound, its three states unexamined and unintegrated.
deficient.vignette: Dan chants Om, when he does, as a flat mechanical exercise—no sense of the three matras, no layers opening, no depth beneath the sound. The structured meditation that draws a practitioner through the worlds is, for him, just a syllable repeated because the practice says to.

attributes.title: Four Matras of OM (A-U-M-Silence)
attributes.aspect: OM consists of A (waking/Vaishvanara), U (dream/Taijasa), M (deep sleep/Prajna), and the silence after (Turiya). Meditating on Om as these four matras leads to realization.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the four mātras of OM (A-U-M-silence); maps the four states (Dim 4).
coordinate.key_relation: the four mātras of OM (A-U-M-silence); maps the four states (Dim 4).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Om meditation through all four matras, recognizing each state and the silence beyond.
facets.somatic: Body resonates with all four phases.
facets.emotional: Emotional unification through four-fold Om.
facets.cognitive: Mind integrates four states through Om practice.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in the fourth measure of OM — the silence beyond A-U-M — recognizing it as turīya, the ground.

balanced.text:
