Om meditation through all four matras, recognizing each state and the silence beyond.

Somatic: Body resonates with all four phases.

Emotional: Emotional unification through four-fold Om.

Cognitive: Mind integrates four states through Om practice.

Psychic: The soul rests in the fourth measure of OM — the silence beyond A-U-M — recognizing it as turīya, the ground.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to rest in the fourth matra—the silence after the Om fades, the soundless awareness the three sounds dissolve into—and finds there a stillness the sound only pointed toward. The silence after the syllable has become the real practice; he chants, and then rests in what remains.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mechanical Om-chanting without recognition of the four-fold structure.
facets.somatic: Mechanical chanting without resonance.
facets.emotional: Empty practice without devotion.
facets.cognitive: Mind missing the deep four-fold significance.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on the technical anatomy of the syllable, the silence missed for the sounds.

excess.text:
Mechanical Om-chanting without recognition of the four-fold structure.

Somatic: Mechanical chanting without resonance.

Emotional: Empty practice without devotion.

Cognitive: Mind missing the deep four-fold significance.

Psychic: The soul fixates on the technical anatomy of the syllable, the silence missed for the sounds.
excess.vignette: Sam chases ‘the silence after Om’ as an experience to attain—straining to produce the stillness, judging each sit by whether he reached it, grasping at the very silence that only comes when the grasping stops. He’s turned the fourth matra into a phenomenon to hunt, and the hunting fills it with noise.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No Om practice at all—missing this profound integration technique.
facets.somatic: Body without sacred sound.
facets.emotional: Missing the unifying practice.
facets.cognitive: Mind without the four-fold integration tool.
facets.psychic: The soul never reaches the silence, hearing at most the audible OM and never its soundless heart.

deficient.text:
No Om practice at all—missing this profound integration technique.

Somatic: Body without sacred sound.

Emotional: Missing the unifying practice.

Cognitive: Mind without the four-fold integration tool.

Psychic: The soul never reaches the silence, hearing at most the audible OM and never its soundless heart.
deficient.vignette: Dan chants Om and, when the sound fades, is immediately back in the mental churn—no silence, no soundless awareness, no fourth matra at all. The stillness the three sounds dissolve into is a state he’s never once touched; for him there’s only the noise, and then more noise.

attributes.title: Udgitha (Meditation on Om as the Sacred Singing)
attributes.aspect: Udgitha is the singing of Om—the sacred syllable as the essence of speech (Rik), prana (Saman), and life itself. ‘Ut’=above, ‘gi’=lifted, ‘tha’=established. The whole Chapter 1 develops profound meditations on Udgitha as transformation practice. The gods conquered the demons through prana-as-Udgitha alone, the only one demons couldn’t pierce with evil.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: OM as the sacred singing (udgītha); same as Udgitha Vidya (Dim 25).
coordinate.key_relation: OM as the sacred singing (udgītha); same as Udgitha Vidya (Dim 25).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Deep Udgitha meditation that lifts consciousness through sacred singing—Om as the breath of life itself, connecting individual prana to cosmic Prana.
facets.somatic: Body resonates with sacred vibration during Udgitha singing, healthy breath and voice.
facets.emotional: Emotional uplift through Udgitha, devotional warmth, inner singing.
facets.cognitive: Mind unified through Udgitha practice, consciousness lifted upward.
facets.psychic: The soul takes up OM as the udgītha, the sacred chant, and through its vibration gathers its powers toward the divine.

balanced.text:
Deep Udgitha meditation that lifts consciousness through sacred singing—Om as the breath of life itself, connecting individual prana to cosmic Prana.

Somatic: Body resonates with sacred vibration during Udgitha singing, healthy breath and voice.

Emotional: Emotional uplift through Udgitha, devotional warmth, inner singing.

Cognitive: Mind unified through Udgitha practice, consciousness lifted upward.

Psychic: The soul takes up OM as the udgītha, the sacred chant, and through its vibration gathers its powers toward the divine.
balanced.vignette: When Ravi chants Om as the sacred singing of the Udgitha, the sound moves through him and settles his whole system into a resonant, reverent stillness. The Udgitha isn’t a performance for him but a homecoming, and it tunes the rest of his day to a quieter key.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mechanical chanting without inner participation—Udgitha becomes empty ritual.
facets.somatic: Vocal strain without inner resonance.
facets.emotional: Empty performance, no devotional warmth.
facets.cognitive: Mechanical mind during practice, no transformation.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on the chant for its own sake, singing mechanically without the inward turning.

excess.text:
Mechanical chanting without inner participation—Udgitha becomes empty ritual.

Somatic: Vocal strain without inner resonance.

Emotional: Empty performance, no devotional warmth.

Cognitive: Mechanical mind during practice, no transformation.

Psychic: The soul fixates on the chant for its own sake, singing mechanically without the inward turning.
excess.vignette: Sam sings the Udgitha for its effect—the impressive resonance, the display of devotion—half-believing the correct performance will magically confer the fruit. The sacred singing has become a technique he executes, and the reverence that was its whole point has drained out of the exercise.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No sacred singing practice—missing this profound transformative technique.
facets.somatic: Voice without sacred dimension.
facets.emotional: Missing the unifying power of sacred sound.
facets.cognitive: Mind without the anchor of Udgitha.
facets.psychic: The soul has no sacred chant to gather it, its energies dispersed with nothing to recall them home.

deficient.text:
No sacred singing practice—missing this profound transformative technique.

Somatic: Voice without sacred dimension.

Emotional: Missing the unifying power of sacred sound.

Cognitive: Mind without the anchor of Udgitha.

Psychic: The soul has no sacred chant to gather it, its energies dispersed with nothing to recall them home.
deficient.vignette: Dan chants the Udgitha correctly and feels precisely nothing—mouth moving, mind elsewhere, no reverence, no resonance, no homecoming. The sacred singing that should open a doorway remains, for him, just a required noise, and the door stays shut.

attributes.title: Asato Ma Sad Gamaya (Lead from Unreal to Real)
attributes.aspect: From unreal lead me to real, from darkness lead me to light, from death lead me to immortality. The universal prayer for awakening.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the prayer ‘lead me from the unreal to the real’ (Bṛhadāraṇyaka).
coordinate.key_relation: the prayer ‘lead me from the unreal to the real’ (Bṛhadāraṇyaka).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Heartful invocation for awakening—asking to be led from illusion to reality, from darkness to light.
facets.somatic: Body in prayerful attitude of opening.
facets.emotional: Emotional yearning for awakening.
facets.cognitive: Mind invoking the movement toward Self-realization.
facets.psychic: The soul takes up the great prayer — from unreal to real, from darkness to light, from death to immortality — as its daily orientation.

balanced.text:
Heartful invocation for awakening—asking to be led from illusion to reality, from darkness to light.

Somatic: Body in prayerful attitude of opening.

Emotional: Emotional yearning for awakening.

Cognitive: Mind invoking the movement toward Self-realization.

Psychic: The soul takes up the great prayer — from unreal to real, from darkness to light, from death to immortality — as its daily orientation.
balanced.vignette: ‘Lead me from the unreal to the real’—Ravi prays it and means it, and lately it’s become more than words: a felt turning, day by day, from the shallow toward the deep, the false toward the true. The prayer has become a direction his whole life is quietly taking.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Demanding prayer—expecting immediate cosmic intervention without preparation.
facets.somatic: Body in demanding posture.
facets.emotional: Emotional demand rather than invocation.
facets.cognitive: Mental impatience with the path.
facets.psychic: The soul recites the prayer mechanically, the words running ahead of any real turning.

excess.text:
Demanding prayer—expecting immediate cosmic intervention without preparation.

Somatic: Body in demanding posture.

Emotional: Emotional demand rather than invocation.

Cognitive: Mental impatience with the path.

Psychic: The soul recites the prayer mechanically, the words running ahead of any real turning.
excess.vignette: Sam uses ‘lead me from the unreal to the real’ to dismiss the world as ‘unreal’—dodging responsibilities, waving away others’ suffering as illusion, hiding from life behind the prayer. The turning toward the real has become an excuse to disengage from the actual.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No prayer, no invocation—mechanical existence with no opening to transformation.
facets.somatic: Body without prayerful dimension.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without yearning for awakening.
facets.cognitive: Mind without invocation of higher movement.
facets.psychic: The soul never prays for the real, content to move among the unreal, the dark, and the mortal.

deficient.text:
No prayer, no invocation—mechanical existence with no opening to transformation.

Somatic: Body without prayerful dimension.

Emotional: Emotional life without yearning for awakening.

Cognitive: Mind without invocation of higher movement.

Psychic: The soul never prays for the real, content to move among the unreal, the dark, and the mortal.
deficient.vignette: Dan takes the unreal for the real without question—the shallow, the false, the passing all gripped as though they were bedrock, no prayer, no turning, no sense that there’s a ‘real’ to be led toward. The movement the prayer describes is one his life has never begun to make.

attributes.title: OM / Praṇava — the Sacred Sound
attributes.aspect: OM, the praṇava, is held by the tradition as the sound-form of the Absolute itself — the ‘imperishable syllable’ whose four parts (A, U, M, and the silence beyond them) correspond to the waking, dream, deep-sleep, and transcendent states. As practice, the sounding or inner repetition of OM gathers the scattered mind, harmonizes the breath, and opens, through its concluding silence, toward the wordless awareness it represents. It is at once the simplest of practices — a single syllable — and a complete map of consciousness held in sound.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the sacred sound OM (praṇava) itself; the emblem of Īśvara (Dim 11).
coordinate.key_relation: the sacred sound OM (praṇava) itself; the emblem of Īśvara (Dim 11).

balanced.label: Balanced — the sound gathering the mind and opening into silence

facets.overview: When sacred sound is used appropriately for one’s center, it creates inner fire that burns conditioning, awakens energy, transforms consciousness.
facets.somatic: Body resonating with sacred frequency, nervous system attuned, vital energy refined.
facets.emotional: Emotional purification through sound, devotional warmth and bliss.
facets.cognitive: Mind brought to focus by sacred sound, eventually transcending it into silence.
facets.psychic: The soul uses the sacred syllable to gather and lift itself; AUM becomes a sonic ladder by which scattered energy returns to its source.

balanced.text:
When sacred sound is used appropriately for one’s center, it creates inner fire that burns conditioning, awakens energy, transforms consciousness.

Somatic: Body resonating with sacred frequency, nervous system attuned, vital energy refined.

Emotional: Emotional purification through sound, devotional warmth and bliss.

Cognitive: Mind brought to focus by sacred sound, eventually transcending it into silence.

Psychic: The soul uses the sacred syllable to gather and lift itself; AUM becomes a sonic ladder by which scattered energy returns to its source.
balanced.vignette: Ravi begins his practice with a few slow rounds of Om, and the sacred sound resonates through his chest and settles his whole system into a devotional stillness. The syllable is a doorway he steps through gladly, and it opens the same way every time.

excess.label: In excess — mechanical repetition; the sound as superstition or noise

facets.overview: Mechanical repetition without devotion—mantra becomes empty noise. Or forced practice creating tension rather than transformation.
facets.somatic: Strained vocal chords from forced chanting, body without resonance.
facets.emotional: Empty ritualism, no devotional warmth.
facets.cognitive: Mechanical mind during repetition, no transformation.
facets.psychic: Repetition becomes mechanical; the soul chants without presence, mistaking the sound’s surface for its living power.

excess.text:
Mechanical repetition without devotion—mantra becomes empty noise. Or forced practice creating tension rather than transformation.

Somatic: Strained vocal chords from forced chanting, body without resonance.

Emotional: Empty ritualism, no devotional warmth.

Cognitive: Mechanical mind during repetition, no transformation.

Psychic: Repetition becomes mechanical; the soul chants without presence, mistaking the sound’s surface for its living power.
excess.vignette: Karl chants Om compulsively, counting rounds like currency, half-believing the sound alone will magically fix his life. The living sacred syllable has become a superstitious tic, and the mechanical repetition has drained the resonance it was meant to open.

deficient.label: Deficient — no sanctifying current; the mind left to its unbroken churn

facets.overview: Mind without sacred sound has no efficient way to gather energy, no sonic anchor for transformation, missing a powerful tool.
facets.somatic: Missing the somatic benefits of mantric vibration.
facets.emotional: Without the devotional sound dimension, drier practice.
facets.cognitive: No japa-anchor for the mind, harder concentration.
facets.psychic: Without any sacred sound, the soul has no easy means to collect itself, and the mind stays scattered for want of a center to return to.

deficient.text:
Mind without sacred sound has no efficient way to gather energy, no sonic anchor for transformation, missing a powerful tool.

Somatic: Missing the somatic benefits of mantric vibration.

Emotional: Without the devotional sound dimension, drier practice.

Cognitive: No japa-anchor for the mind, harder concentration.

Psychic: Without any sacred sound, the soul has no easy means to collect itself, and the mind stays scattered for want of a center to return to.
deficient.vignette: For Dan there’s no sacred sound in the day at all—no chant, no silence, no resonance, just the flat noise of notifications and traffic. A whole register of the sacred is simply missing from his life, and the doorway Om offers is one he’s never approached.

attributes.title: One-Pointedness (Daily Focus)
attributes.aspect: The Bhagavata’s strong teaching: one-pointedness. In modern distraction-saturated life, this is revolutionary. Choose one main practice, one main teacher’s lineage, one main scripture for sustained study. Don’t surface-scan across dozens. Depth produces results; surface-breadth produces only confusion. Daily life requires this single-pointed commitment in some area to bear fruit anywhere. Bhagavata insists: be one-minded. In daily life — modern distraction-addicted life — this is revolutionary. Choose one practice, one teacher, one path, and sustain. Surface-scanning across many produces nothing. Depth in one transforms.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: daily one-pointed focus (ekāgratā); kin to Dhāraṇā (Dim 11).
coordinate.key_relation: daily one-pointed focus (ekāgratā); kin to Dhāraṇā (Dim 11).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Sustained one-pointed commitment to main practice/path/teacher. Other things explored but not dispersing.
facets.somatic: Body’s daily practice consistent and one-pointed.
facets.emotional: Emotional commitment to single path.
facets.cognitive: Mind focused on one main direction.
facets.psychic: The soul gathers its attention to a single point, the scattered mind collected into steady focus.

balanced.text:
Sustained one-pointed commitment to main practice/path/teacher. Other things explored but not dispersing.

Somatic: Body’s daily practice consistent and one-pointed.

Emotional: Emotional commitment to single path.

Cognitive: Mind focused on one main direction.

Psychic: The soul gathers its attention to a single point, the scattered mind collected into steady focus.
balanced.vignette: When Priya needs to concentrate, she can—gathering her attention onto one thing and holding it there, at will, for as long as the task requires. The one-pointedness is available and flexible; she focuses deeply and then releases, the mind a well-trained instrument that comes when called.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Rigid sectarianism — only my teacher, my path, my scripture. Cult-like exclusion.
facets.somatic: Body refused other engagement.
facets.emotional: Emotional dependency on single source.
facets.cognitive: Mental rigidity around one path.
facets.psychic: The soul cramps around its focus, forcing a brittle concentration that strains and snaps.

excess.text:
Rigid sectarianism — only my teacher, my path, my scripture. Cult-like exclusion.

Somatic: Body refused other engagement.

Emotional: Emotional dependency on single source.

Cognitive: Mental rigidity around one path.

Psychic: The soul cramps around its focus, forcing a brittle concentration that strains and snaps.
excess.vignette: Ravi concentrates like a clenched fist—rigidly fixed, effortful, shattering at the first interruption and leaving him rattled. He’s mistaken tension for focus, and the white-knuckle grip he keeps on his attention makes it brittle precisely where it should be supple.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Spiritual scattering — sampling many traditions without depth in any. Spiritual tourism producing no transformation.
facets.somatic: Body in shifting practices.
facets.emotional: Emotional restlessness across paths.
facets.cognitive: Mind never settles into depth.
facets.psychic: The soul’s attention is hopelessly scattered, unable to rest on anything for long.

deficient.text:
Spiritual scattering — sampling many traditions without depth in any. Spiritual tourism producing no transformation.

Somatic: Body in shifting practices.

Emotional: Emotional restlessness across paths.

Cognitive: Mind never settles into depth.

Psychic: The soul’s attention is hopelessly scattered, unable to rest on anything for long.
deficient.vignette: Sara’s attention is confetti—unable to hold a single focus for more than a moment before it scatters, deep work slipping through her fingers. The gathered, one-pointed attention that real accomplishment requires is, for now, entirely beyond her.

attributes.title: Object of Meditation (Pure, Untainted, Griefless)
attributes.aspect: Everyone is a meditator—we constantly dwell on something. The four objects taught here—untainted space, pure beauty, clear transparency, griefless joy—shape consciousness toward liberation. You become what you meditate upon.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: choosing a pure, untainted, griefless object of meditation.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: choosing a pure, untainted, griefless object of meditation.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Conscious selection of meditation objects—dwelling on purity, transparency, joy, vastness. Mind becomes that on which it dwells.
facets.somatic: Body resonates with chosen quality—clear, light, healthy, joyful.
facets.emotional: Emotional shaping toward bliss, freedom from negative emotional dwelling.
facets.cognitive: Mind becoming pure, clear, transparent, blissful—taking the qualities of its object.
facets.psychic: The soul chooses a worthy object — pure, untroubled, griefless — giving the mind a clean place to rest.

balanced.text:
Conscious selection of meditation objects—dwelling on purity, transparency, joy, vastness. Mind becomes that on which it dwells.

Somatic: Body resonates with chosen quality—clear, light, healthy, joyful.

Emotional: Emotional shaping toward bliss, freedom from negative emotional dwelling.

Cognitive: Mind becoming pure, clear, transparent, blissful—taking the qualities of its object.

Psychic: The soul chooses a worthy object — pure, untroubled, griefless — giving the mind a clean place to rest.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has chosen a pure object for his meditation—luminous, untainted, free of grief—and resting his mind on it, he finds it lifts and clarifies him rather than stirring the usual muck. The object is well-chosen; it draws his attention upward, and his attention takes its color.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Rigid attachment to specific objects of meditation, missing the principle. Or using meditation objects as escape from facing reality.
facets.somatic: Body forced into meditative postures without inner readiness, physical strain.
facets.emotional: Forced positive feelings without addressing real emotions, spiritual escapism.
facets.cognitive: Attachment to meditation experiences, mistaking states for realization.
facets.psychic: The soul fusses over selecting the perfect object, the choosing itself becoming a distraction.

excess.text:
Rigid attachment to specific objects of meditation, missing the principle. Or using meditation objects as escape from facing reality.

Somatic: Body forced into meditative postures without inner readiness, physical strain.

Emotional: Forced positive feelings without addressing real emotions, spiritual escapism.

Cognitive: Attachment to meditation experiences, mistaking states for realization.

Psychic: The soul fusses over selecting the perfect object, the choosing itself becoming a distraction.
excess.vignette: Sam has grown attached to his meditation object—clinging to the pleasant image, the beloved form, collecting the experiences it produces, mistaking the raft for the shore. The pure object meant to be a doorway has become a thing he grasps, and the grasping stalls him at the threshold.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Unconscious meditation—dwelling on worry, anger, lust, grief without awareness. Becoming what one unconsciously meditates upon: anxious, angry, lustful, sad.
facets.somatic: Body shaped by negative dwelling—chronic tension, illness from stress.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns reinforced by unconscious dwelling—anger, sadness, anxiety.
facets.cognitive: Mind constantly dwelling on suffering, becoming the suffering, no awareness of meditation principle.
facets.psychic: The soul meditates on whatever drifts by, its attention captured by troubling, grief-laden content.

deficient.text:
Unconscious meditation—dwelling on worry, anger, lust, grief without awareness. Becoming what one unconsciously meditates upon: anxious, angry, lustful, sad.

Somatic: Body shaped by negative dwelling—chronic tension, illness from stress.

Emotional: Emotional patterns reinforced by unconscious dwelling—anger, sadness, anxiety.

Cognitive: Mind constantly dwelling on suffering, becoming the suffering, no awareness of meditation principle.

Psychic: The soul meditates on whatever drifts by, its attention captured by troubling, grief-laden content.
deficient.vignette: Dan can’t hold any object in the mind for ten seconds before it scatters—no steady focus, no pure object, no meditation that lifts. The clarifying practice of resting the mind on a griefless object is beyond him, because the mind he’d rest it with won’t stay still.

attributes.title: Atma-Nyaya (Spike-and-Bow Meditation)
attributes.aspect: The famous archery meditation: Take OM as the bow, the Atman (Self) as the arrow, Brahman as the target. With one-pointed concentration, shoot the arrow. Like the arrow becoming one with the target, become one with Brahman. This is one of the most direct meditation prescriptions in Upanishads.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the spike-and-bow meditation (ātma as arrow to the target Brahman); Muṇḍaka.
coordinate.key_relation: the spike-and-bow meditation (ātma as arrow to the target Brahman); Muṇḍaka.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Active practice of the archery meditation. OM-tension cultivated; Self-arrow shot toward Brahman target.
facets.somatic: Body still and focused like an archer.
facets.emotional: Emotional intensity of complete focus.
facets.cognitive: Mind one-pointed like arrow leaving the bow.
facets.psychic: The soul uses the spike-and-bow image to drive attention, like an arrow, straight into its target.

balanced.text:
Active practice of the archery meditation. OM-tension cultivated; Self-arrow shot toward Brahman target.

Somatic: Body still and focused like an archer.

Emotional: Emotional intensity of complete focus.

Cognitive: Mind one-pointed like arrow leaving the bow.

Psychic: The soul uses the spike-and-bow image to drive attention, like an arrow, straight into its target.
balanced.vignette: Ravi practices the spike-and-bow concentration with skill—the mind drawn to a single point like an arrow to its mark, held there steadily and released with ease. The technique gathers his scattered attention; applied well, it makes one-pointedness a thing he can summon rather than chase.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced concentration creating headaches, mental strain. Tension confused with focus.
facets.somatic: Body strained in forced focus.
facets.emotional: Emotional anxiety from forced concentration.
facets.cognitive: Mental tension that itself becomes obstacle.
facets.psychic: The soul strains the effort, the forced intensity defeating the steadiness it seeks.

excess.text:
Forced concentration creating headaches, mental strain. Tension confused with focus.

Somatic: Body strained in forced focus.

Emotional: Emotional anxiety from forced concentration.

Cognitive: Mental tension that itself becomes obstacle.

Psychic: The soul strains the effort, the forced intensity defeating the steadiness it seeks.
excess.vignette: Sam forces the concentration technique—straining at the point, gripping the focus, treating the practice as a feat of will—and gives himself a headache for his trouble. The gathering meant to be steady and soft has become a clench, and the clenching scatters what it means to hold.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Scattered concentration—arrow never focused, never shot. Meditation as drifting daydream.
facets.somatic: Body restless in supposed practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional dispersal during ‘meditation.’
facets.cognitive: Mind never gathers itself for the shot.
facets.psychic: The soul brings no directed effort at all, its attention slack and aimless.

deficient.text:
Scattered concentration—arrow never focused, never shot. Meditation as drifting daydream.

Somatic: Body restless in supposed practice.

Emotional: Emotional dispersal during ‘meditation.’

Cognitive: Mind never gathers itself for the shot.

Psychic: The soul brings no directed effort at all, its attention slack and aimless.
deficient.vignette: Dan has never practiced any concentration technique—the mind left to its native scatter, no method to gather it, no point to draw it to. The disciplined focusing the technique offers is one he’s never attempted; his attention has never been aimed at anything.

attributes.title: Bindu and Star Meditation
attributes.aspect: As Kriya advances, specific inner phenomena are perceived: Bindu (a point of inner light) and Star (a radiant star-like inner vision from throat to forehead). These are not visualizations but actual perceptions of subtle reality. Lahiri lists them as integral to the five-step essence of the entire Gita. They mark stages of progress and serve as anchors for further deepening.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: meditation on the inner point (bindu) and star of light.
coordinate.key_relation: meditation on the inner point (bindu) and star of light.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Inner phenomena naturally arising and serving as meditation anchors. Stable inner vision.
facets.somatic: Subtle body’s experiences becoming reliable.
facets.emotional: Emotional stability around inner phenomena, neither attached nor dismissive.
facets.cognitive: Mind absorbed in inner perception.
facets.psychic: The soul concentrates on the inner point of light, the bindu, gathering all awareness into a single radiant center.

balanced.text:
Inner phenomena naturally arising and serving as meditation anchors. Stable inner vision.

Somatic: Subtle body’s experiences becoming reliable.

Emotional: Emotional stability around inner phenomena, neither attached nor dismissive.

Cognitive: Mind absorbed in inner perception.

Psychic: The soul concentrates on the inner point of light, the bindu, gathering all awareness into a single radiant center.
balanced.vignette: Resting his attention on a point of light at the brow, Ravi feels his mind gather and brighten, the scattered thoughts converging toward the single luminous focus. The bindu meditation steadies and illumines him; the point of light becomes a still center his whole attention orbits.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Attachment to inner phenomena, treating them as goal rather than markers.
facets.somatic: Body strained trying to maintain inner visions.
facets.emotional: Emotional dependency on inner experiences.
facets.cognitive: Mind grasping at phenomena, missing the witness.
facets.psychic: The soul chases the visual phenomena, mistaking inner lights for the awareness behind them.

excess.text:
Attachment to inner phenomena, treating them as goal rather than markers.

Somatic: Body strained trying to maintain inner visions.

Emotional: Emotional dependency on inner experiences.

Cognitive: Mind grasping at phenomena, missing the witness.

Psychic: The soul chases the visual phenomena, mistaking inner lights for the awareness behind them.
excess.vignette: Sam chases the lights and stars of the meditation obsessively—straining to see them, judging each session by the brightness, hunting the visions rather than resting in the awareness behind them. He’s mistaken the phenomena for the practice, and the grasping obscures the very light he’s after.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No development of subtle perception. Inner dimension never accessed.
facets.somatic: Body without subtle sensitivity.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without inner dimension.
facets.cognitive: Mind without access to subtle perception.
facets.psychic: The soul never finds the inner point, its attention diffused across the whole field of mind.

deficient.text:
No development of subtle perception. Inner dimension never accessed.

Somatic: Body without subtle sensitivity.

Emotional: Emotional life without inner dimension.

Cognitive: Mind without access to subtle perception.

Psychic: The soul never finds the inner point, its attention diffused across the whole field of mind.
deficient.vignette: Dan sees no inner light and never has—the point, the star, the luminous focus of the meditation entirely outside his experience. The bindu practice that gathers and brightens the mind is inaccessible to someone whose inner world stays dull and dark.

attributes.title: Sandilya Vidya (Heart Meditation)
attributes.aspect: All this is Brahman. From it all comes, into it all returns. Tranquil, one should meditate on it as Self in the heart-cave—smaller than a grain of rice, vaster than the universe. Containing all karmas, all desires, all smells, all tastes.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: heart-meditation on Brahman (Śāṇḍilya); same as Sandilya Vidya (Dim 25).
coordinate.key_relation: heart-meditation on Brahman (Śāṇḍilya); same as Sandilya Vidya (Dim 25).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Heart meditation on the paradoxical Self—smaller than smallest, larger than largest. Tranquil contemplation.
facets.somatic: Body settled in heart-meditation.
facets.emotional: Emotional centering in the heart.
facets.cognitive: Mind absorbed in heart’s paradoxical infinity.
facets.psychic: The soul meditates on the tranquil Self in the heart, holding ‘all this is Brahman’ as a living conviction.

balanced.text:
Heart meditation on the paradoxical Self—smaller than smallest, larger than largest. Tranquil contemplation.

Somatic: Body settled in heart-meditation.

Emotional: Emotional centering in the heart.

Cognitive: Mind absorbed in heart’s paradoxical infinity.

Psychic: The soul meditates on the tranquil Self in the heart, holding ‘all this is Brahman’ as a living conviction.
balanced.vignette: Each morning Priya rests attention in the space of the heart, contemplating the Self that dwells there—luminous, small as a grain, vast as the sky. Over years it’s become a stable inner home she returns to, carrying a little of its steadiness into the noise of her day.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mechanical heart-visualization without depth, or grandiose claims about heart-realization.
facets.somatic: Forced heart-focus without genuine settling.
facets.emotional: Sentimentality without depth.
facets.cognitive: Visualization without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul turns the meditation into a slogan, asserting the identity while still gripped by fear.

excess.text:
Mechanical heart-visualization without depth, or grandiose claims about heart-realization.

Somatic: Forced heart-focus without genuine settling.

Emotional: Sentimentality without depth.

Cognitive: Visualization without realization.

Psychic: The soul turns the meditation into a slogan, asserting the identity while still gripped by fear.
excess.vignette: Ben has grown so attached to the blissful heart-states of his meditation that he retreats into them from every difficulty, using the inner cave as a hideout from an outer life he’d rather not face. The heart-meditation meant to root him has become a place to escape to.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No heart-practice—mind-dominated spirituality missing the heart-center entirely.
facets.somatic: Body without heart-awareness.
facets.emotional: Emotional heart unrecognized.
facets.cognitive: Pure mental approach missing heart-meditation.
facets.psychic: The soul never enters the heart’s meditation, seeing only multiplicity and conflict.

deficient.text:
No heart-practice—mind-dominated spirituality missing the heart-center entirely.

Somatic: Body without heart-awareness.

Emotional: Emotional heart unrecognized.

Cognitive: Pure mental approach missing heart-meditation.

Psychic: The soul never enters the heart’s meditation, seeing only multiplicity and conflict.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no felt inner refuge at all—his identity scattered entirely outward, no still center in the heart to come home to. The luminous Self dwelling in the heart-cave is, for him, an empty room he’s never once entered.

attributes.title: Akasha Vidya (Space Meditation)
attributes.aspect: Akasha (space/ether) is the highest—from akasha all things arise; into akasha all return. The space inside the heart-lotus is the same as the space outside. Meditate on space as Brahman.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: space-meditation on the all-pervading; same as Akasa Vidya (Dim 25).
coordinate.key_relation: space-meditation on the all-pervading; same as Akasa Vidya (Dim 25).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Recognition that the space within and without are one. Inner and outer space-meditation reveals fundamental unity.
facets.somatic: Body recognizes the space within as one with space without.
facets.emotional: Emotional spaciousness, freedom from claustrophobic identification.
facets.cognitive: Mind recognizes itself as space-like consciousness.
facets.psychic: The soul meditates on space as the emblem of Brahman, learning spaciousness as a way of being.

balanced.text:
Recognition that the space within and without are one. Inner and outer space-meditation reveals fundamental unity.

Somatic: Body recognizes the space within as one with space without.

Emotional: Emotional spaciousness, freedom from claustrophobic identification.

Cognitive: Mind recognizes itself as space-like consciousness.

Psychic: The soul meditates on space as the emblem of Brahman, learning spaciousness as a way of being.
balanced.vignette: Meditating on space, Priya has come to feel her awareness as something spacious and all-accommodating—room enough for whatever arises, like the sky holding the weather. It’s made her less cramped, less reactive; there’s space in her now where there used to be only pressure.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Concept of being ‘space’ without realization—identity-claim as new game.
facets.somatic: Body in dissociated ‘space’ identity.
facets.emotional: Emotional dissociation as ‘spaciousness.’
facets.cognitive: Conceptual space-identity without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on emptiness as a goal, mistaking blankness for living spaciousness.

excess.text:
Concept of being ‘space’ without realization—identity-claim as new game.

Somatic: Body in dissociated ‘space’ identity.

Emotional: Emotional dissociation as ‘spaciousness.’

Cognitive: Conceptual space-identity without realization.

Psychic: The soul fixates on emptiness as a goal, mistaking blankness for living spaciousness.
excess.vignette: Sam has ‘become the vast space’ so thoroughly that he floats through his days ungrounded and vague, losing his keys and his appointments, too spacious to bother with the concrete. He’s mistaken the spaced-out for the spacious.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Body experienced as solid, separate, no recognition of space-nature.
facets.somatic: Body feels solid, claustrophobic.
facets.emotional: Emotional contraction, no spaciousness.
facets.cognitive: Mind feels solid and bounded, no recognition of space-nature.
facets.psychic: The soul knows only the filled and solid, with no felt sense of the open space within.

deficient.text:
Body experienced as solid, separate, no recognition of space-nature.

Somatic: Body feels solid, claustrophobic.

Emotional: Emotional contraction, no spaciousness.

Cognitive: Mind feels solid and bounded, no recognition of space-nature.

Psychic: The soul knows only the filled and solid, with no felt sense of the open space within.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s inner world is cramped and crowded—no room, every worry pressed against the next. The accommodating inner space the meditation opens is foreign to him; his awareness is a small, full box, and always has been.

attributes.title: Nididhyasana (Deep Sustained Meditation)
attributes.aspect: Nididhyasana is sustained deep meditation on the truth understood through shravana and manana. Beyond intellectual conviction, it is the abiding contemplation that transforms understanding into realization. This is where actual liberation happens; the previous two stages merely prepare.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: deep sustained meditation (nididhyāsana); the third of śravaṇa-manana-nididhyāsana.
coordinate.key_relation: deep sustained meditation (nididhyāsana); the third of śravaṇa-manana-nididhyāsana.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Sustained deep meditation on Self-truth. Understanding becoming realization through abidance.
facets.somatic: Body in extended meditative absorption.
facets.emotional: Emotional stillness in meditation.
facets.cognitive: Mind dissolved in truth being meditated upon.
facets.psychic: The soul abides in sustained contemplation of the truth heard and reasoned, letting it ripen into direct realization.

balanced.text:
Sustained deep meditation on Self-truth. Understanding becoming realization through abidance.

Somatic: Body in extended meditative absorption.

Emotional: Emotional stillness in meditation.

Cognitive: Mind dissolved in truth being meditated upon.

Psychic: The soul abides in sustained contemplation of the truth heard and reasoned, letting it ripen into direct realization.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has learned to sustain his meditation—not just glimpsing the truth but abiding in it, the attention resting deeply and continuously in the recognition. The nididhyasana isn’t a technique to him but a settling-in; what he understood, he now increasingly lives from, held steady by the sustained absorption.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Withdrawal from life into endless meditation. Escape rather than realization.
facets.somatic: Body neglected in compulsive meditation.
facets.emotional: Emotional avoidance disguised as meditation.
facets.cognitive: Mental escape into meditative states.
facets.psychic: The soul strains for deep absorption, the grasping disturbing the very settling it seeks.

excess.text:
Withdrawal from life into endless meditation. Escape rather than realization.

Somatic: Body neglected in compulsive meditation.

Emotional: Emotional avoidance disguised as meditation.

Cognitive: Mental escape into meditative states.

Psychic: The soul strains for deep absorption, the grasping disturbing the very settling it seeks.
excess.vignette: Sam forces the deep meditation—straining to sustain the state, gripping at the absorption, judging each sit by its depth—and the grasping is precisely what breaks the continuity. He’s trying to hold the water by clenching his fist, and it keeps running out.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Intellectual conviction without abidance. Understanding never matures into realization.
facets.somatic: Body without sustained practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without transformation.
facets.cognitive: Mind stops at understanding, no deep meditation.
facets.psychic: The soul never sustains contemplation, the truth heard and reasoned but never deepened into knowing.

deficient.text:
Intellectual conviction without abidance. Understanding never matures into realization.

Somatic: Body without sustained practice.

Emotional: Emotional life without transformation.

Cognitive: Mind stops at understanding, no deep meditation.

Psychic: The soul never sustains contemplation, the truth heard and reasoned but never deepened into knowing.
deficient.vignette: Dan can’t sustain any meditation for more than a moment—the attention scattering the instant it settles, no continuity, no abiding. The deep, sustained absorption that ripens understanding into realization is a depth he’s never reached; his practice, when he tries, keeps slipping off the surface.

attributes.title: Dhyāna — Sustained Meditation
attributes.aspect: Dhyāna is the unbroken flow of attention toward a single object or ground — the deepening of momentary concentration into sustained absorption. In the path of knowledge it appears as nididhyāsana, the prolonged dwelling upon the realized truth until it saturates one’s being. Its mark is one-pointedness: the gathering of the ordinarily scattered mind into a single, steady current, held long enough that the agitations subside and the underlying stillness shows through.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: sustained meditation; same as Dhyāna the limb (Dim 11).
coordinate.key_relation: sustained meditation; same as Dhyāna the limb (Dim 11).

balanced.label: Balanced — steady absorption that stills the mind and reveals its ground

facets.overview: When Dhyana is practiced appropriately, a person develops increasing concentration, peace, and insight. Daily life is supported and enriched by regular meditation practice.
facets.somatic: Calmer nervous system from regular practice, healthier body from reduced stress, integrated body-mind, healthy meditation posture, vitality.
facets.emotional: Emotional regulation and peace from practice, healing of emotional patterns, increased equanimity, emotional depth.
facets.cognitive: Increased concentration and focus, mental clarity, insight and wisdom from practice, ability to direct attention, transformation of consciousness.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in sustained meditation; unbroken attention deepens into the absorption where the meditator dissolves into the meditated.

balanced.text:
When Dhyana is practiced appropriately, a person develops increasing concentration, peace, and insight. Daily life is supported and enriched by regular meditation practice.

Somatic: Calmer nervous system from regular practice, healthier body from reduced stress, integrated body-mind, healthy meditation posture, vitality.

Emotional: Emotional regulation and peace from practice, healing of emotional patterns, increased equanimity, emotional depth.

Cognitive: Increased concentration and focus, mental clarity, insight and wisdom from practice, ability to direct attention, transformation of consciousness.

Psychic: The soul rests in sustained meditation; unbroken attention deepens into the absorption where the meditator dissolves into the meditated.
balanced.vignette: Past the effort of concentration, Amara’s awareness begins to flow toward its object in an unbroken stream, sustained and easeful. Meditation has stopped being something she does and become something that happens; she settles, and the settling deepens on its own.

excess.label: In excess — chasing meditative states and experiences; striving for absorption

facets.overview: Excessive Dhyana becomes escapism, withdrawal from life responsibilities, addiction to meditative states, and spiritual bypass of necessary engagement.
facets.somatic: Physical withdrawal from healthy activity, neglect of body, isolation that damages health.
facets.emotional: Emotional escape into meditative states, avoidance of emotional work, false peace covering unresolved issues.
facets.cognitive: Mental escape from life challenges, dissociation disguised as meditation, addiction to inner states, spiritual bypassing.
facets.psychic: Meditation is forced and grasping; the soul strains for states and experiences, and the very effort prevents the settling it seeks.

excess.text:
Excessive Dhyana becomes escapism, withdrawal from life responsibilities, addiction to meditative states, and spiritual bypass of necessary engagement.

Somatic: Physical withdrawal from healthy activity, neglect of body, isolation that damages health.

Emotional: Emotional escape into meditative states, avoidance of emotional work, false peace covering unresolved issues.

Cognitive: Mental escape from life challenges, dissociation disguised as meditation, addiction to inner states, spiritual bypassing.

Psychic: Meditation is forced and grasping; the soul strains for states and experiences, and the very effort prevents the settling it seeks.
excess.vignette: Ben strains to ‘get into the meditative state,’ grasping at flow, judging each sit by whether he reached it—and the grasping is exactly what keeps it away. He’s chasing a stillness that only comes when he stops chasing.

deficient.label: Deficient — a perpetually scattered mind that never gathers or settles

facets.overview: Absence of Dhyana practice results in scattered mind, lack of inner development, no spiritual progress, and missing the transformative possibility of consciousness.
facets.somatic: Higher stress levels without practice, nervous system without regular calming, missing benefits of meditation for body.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity without practice, no developmental tool for emotional growth, missing emotional regulation benefits.
facets.cognitive: Scattered mind without training, no development of concentration, missing insights from contemplative practice, no transformation.
facets.psychic: Without meditation the soul stays scattered on the surface; lacking inner development, it is carried wherever the mind’s currents run.

deficient.text:
Absence of Dhyana practice results in scattered mind, lack of inner development, no spiritual progress, and missing the transformative possibility of consciousness.

Somatic: Higher stress levels without practice, nervous system without regular calming, missing benefits of meditation for body.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity without practice, no developmental tool for emotional growth, missing emotional regulation benefits.

Cognitive: Scattered mind without training, no development of concentration, missing insights from contemplative practice, no transformation.

Psychic: Without meditation the soul stays scattered on the surface; lacking inner development, it is carried wherever the mind’s currents run.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no inner life to speak of—no meditation, no reflection, no still stream of attention, only the outward churn of doing. The flowing sustained awareness of dhyana is uncharted country for him; he’s never once turned inward long enough to enter it.

attributes.title: Arcanam (Daily Worship/Ritual)
attributes.aspect: Arcanam is formal worship — daily lighting of lamps, offering of flowers, food, prayer at a household altar or temple. In modern life this can be as simple as five minutes daily before a small altar with photo/image of one’s chosen deity. The discipline of daily worship creates a sacred zone and anchor in daily life that strengthens all other practices. Arcanam is daily worship — lighting a lamp, offering flowers, ringing a bell, offering food. Even five minutes daily transforms a home. Creates a ‘sacred zone’ anchor in daily life. Outer worship strengthens inner orientation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: daily worship and ritual (arcanam); a limb of Bhakti.
coordinate.key_relation: daily worship and ritual (arcanam); a limb of Bhakti.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Daily arcanam practiced as anchor — simple, consistent, not elaborate but never skipped. Creates sacred rhythm.
facets.somatic: Body’s daily routine anchored by worship.
facets.emotional: Emotional refuge in daily worship-zone.
facets.cognitive: Mind oriented daily through ritual.
facets.psychic: The soul offers daily worship, the small ritual acts keeping the heart turned toward the sacred.

balanced.text:
Daily arcanam practiced as anchor — simple, consistent, not elaborate but never skipped. Creates sacred rhythm.

Somatic: Body’s daily routine anchored by worship.

Emotional: Emotional refuge in daily worship-zone.

Cognitive: Mind oriented daily through ritual.

Psychic: The soul offers daily worship, the small ritual acts keeping the heart turned toward the sacred.
balanced.vignette: Ravi’s daily worship—the small ritual, the offering, the bow—is done with a real and present heart, each gesture a genuine reaching toward the sacred. The arcanam isn’t empty form to him; the outer rite carries an inner devotion, and the two together steady and center his day.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Elaborate ritualism that becomes obsessive — multiple hours, complex procedures, anxiety if any step is missed.
facets.somatic: Body burdened by excessive ritual demands.
facets.emotional: Anxious perfectionism around ritual.
facets.cognitive: Mind fixated on ritual correctness.
facets.psychic: The soul reduces worship to mechanical ritual, the form preserved while the feeling drains away.

excess.text:
Elaborate ritualism that becomes obsessive — multiple hours, complex procedures, anxiety if any step is missed.

Somatic: Body burdened by excessive ritual demands.

Emotional: Anxious perfectionism around ritual.

Cognitive: Mind fixated on ritual correctness.

Psychic: The soul reduces worship to mechanical ritual, the form preserved while the feeling drains away.
excess.vignette: Sam performs his rituals with obsessive exactness and no heart—the precise gestures, the perfect offerings, the rubrics observed to the letter while the devotion they were meant to carry has quietly died. The worship has become a mechanical scrupulosity, correct and hollow.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No daily ritual practice. Spiritual life without anchor in physical action. Pure mental abstraction.
facets.somatic: Body without sacred zone in daily life.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without anchored sacred space.
facets.cognitive: Mind without ritual reminder.
facets.psychic: The soul performs no worship, its days unmarked by any turning toward the sacred.

deficient.text:
No daily ritual practice. Spiritual life without anchor in physical action. Pure mental abstraction.

Somatic: Body without sacred zone in daily life.

Emotional: Emotional life without anchored sacred space.

Cognitive: Mind without ritual reminder.

Psychic: The soul performs no worship, its days unmarked by any turning toward the sacred.
deficient.vignette: Dan performs no ritual and makes no offering—the whole practice of daily worship absent from his life, dismissed as superstition or simply never considered. The centering rhythm that regular sacred practice can give a life is one he’s never established; his days have no altar in them.

attributes.title: Narada Lesson: Daily Devotional Travel (Bhakti Movement)
attributes.aspect: Narada Muni, the divine sage, travels through all worlds carrying his veena and singing the divine name. He is the model of devotional movement — bhakti is not confined to temple but moves with one. In daily life, this means taking devotion with you everywhere: at work, in errands, in meetings. The work, market, meetings become sites of devotion through inner orientation. Narada travels the worlds singing ‘Narayana, Narayana,’ bringing devotion wherever he goes. In daily life: take devotion with you wherever you go. Be a ‘walking satsang.’ Devotion is portable; it goes with you to work, market, errands.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: Nārada’s ceaseless devotional movement — constant remembrance of God.
coordinate.key_relation: Nārada’s ceaseless devotional movement — constant remembrance of God.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Devotion carried through all daily activities. Workplace, market, errands all infused with the same orientation as one’s home altar.
facets.somatic: Body carries devotional energy everywhere.
facets.emotional: Emotional warmth maintained across contexts.
facets.cognitive: Mind not switching off devotion in ‘worldly’ contexts.
facets.psychic: The soul carries its devotion into movement and action, like Nārada, spreading and sustaining love through a life in motion.

balanced.text:
Devotion carried through all daily activities. Workplace, market, errands all infused with the same orientation as one’s home altar.

Somatic: Body carries devotional energy everywhere.

Emotional: Emotional warmth maintained across contexts.

Cognitive: Mind not switching off devotion in ‘worldly’ contexts.

Psychic: The soul carries its devotion into movement and action, like Nārada, spreading and sustaining love through a life in motion.
balanced.vignette: Like Narada moving ceaselessly through the worlds singing the divine name, Ravi keeps his devotion in motion—alive, fresh, carried from place to place and moment to moment rather than left to stagnate. The movement keeps his bhakti from going stale; his love stays current because he keeps it traveling.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Display of devotion in inappropriate contexts. Preaching to all encountered. Spiritual show-off.
facets.somatic: Body inappropriately demonstrative.
facets.emotional: Emotional exhibition.
facets.cognitive: Mind performing devotion for others.
facets.psychic: The soul mistakes restless activity for devotion, busy in the Lord’s name but inwardly scattered.

excess.text:
Display of devotion in inappropriate contexts. Preaching to all encountered. Spiritual show-off.

Somatic: Body inappropriately demonstrative.

Emotional: Emotional exhibition.

Cognitive: Mind performing devotion for others.

Psychic: The soul mistakes restless activity for devotion, busy in the Lord’s name but inwardly scattered.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned devotional movement into restless spiritual tourism—forever hopping between teachers, traditions, and retreats, mistaking the constant motion for depth. The healthy freshness has become an inability to ever settle and go deep; he’s always traveling and never arriving.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Devotion compartmentalized to specific places/times. Self-different at work, home, temple.
facets.somatic: Body switches modes contextually.
facets.emotional: Emotional life fragmented across contexts.
facets.cognitive: Mind compartmentalized.
facets.psychic: The soul’s devotion never moves into life, staying a private sentiment that touches nothing.

deficient.text:
Devotion compartmentalized to specific places/times. Self-different at work, home, temple.

Somatic: Body switches modes contextually.

Emotional: Emotional life fragmented across contexts.

Cognitive: Mind compartmentalized.

Psychic: The soul’s devotion never moves into life, staying a private sentiment that touches nothing.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s devotion, such as it is, has gone entirely stagnant—the same tepid habit, unmoving, unrefreshed, if it exists at all. The living, moving quality that keeps bhakti fresh is absent; his spiritual life sits still, and the stillness is the stillness of a stagnant pond, not a deep one.

attributes.title: Prayer to the Sun (Pushann Ekarshe)
attributes.aspect: The Truth’s face is veiled by a brilliant disc. O Sun, unveil it that I, whose dharma is truth, may behold the Purusha within. Prayer to the divine light that simultaneously reveals and conceals truth.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the prayer to the sun (Pūṣann Ekarṣe, Īśāvāsya); kin to Āditya Vidya (Dim 25).
coordinate.key_relation: the prayer to the sun (Pūṣann Ekarṣe, Īśāvāsya); kin to Āditya Vidya (Dim 25).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Prayer as authentic invocation—asking the cosmic intelligence to reveal what is hidden by its own brilliance. Devotional and philosophical at once.
facets.somatic: Body in prayerful posture without forced piety.
facets.emotional: Emotional warmth of devotion combined with truth-aspiration.
facets.cognitive: Mind invoking the unveiling of truth from within and without.
facets.psychic: The soul prays, like the Īśā’s dying seeker, for the golden veil to part and the truth behind the sun to be revealed.

balanced.text:
Prayer as authentic invocation—asking the cosmic intelligence to reveal what is hidden by its own brilliance. Devotional and philosophical at once.

Somatic: Body in prayerful posture without forced piety.

Emotional: Emotional warmth of devotion combined with truth-aspiration.

Cognitive: Mind invoking the unveiling of truth from within and without.

Psychic: The soul prays, like the Īśā’s dying seeker, for the golden veil to part and the truth behind the sun to be revealed.
balanced.vignette: Facing the morning sun, Ravi offers the ancient prayer—Pushann, the nourisher, the sole seer—and means it, the daily rite reconnecting him to a source larger than himself. The prayer to the sun isn’t superstition to him; it’s a small, sincere turning toward the light, and it orients his day.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mechanical ritual prayer without inner truth-seeking. Or excessive piety substituting for inquiry.
facets.somatic: Ritualistic body without inner participation.
facets.emotional: Sentimentality without genuine truth-seeking.
facets.cognitive: Mental piety without depth of inquiry.
facets.psychic: The soul recites the prayer as ritual formula, the longing behind it absent.

excess.text:
Mechanical ritual prayer without inner truth-seeking. Or excessive piety substituting for inquiry.

Somatic: Ritualistic body without inner participation.

Emotional: Sentimentality without genuine truth-seeking.

Cognitive: Mental piety without depth of inquiry.

Psychic: The soul recites the prayer as ritual formula, the longing behind it absent.
excess.vignette: Sam treats the sun-prayer as a magic transaction—convinced the exact words, said just so, will secure the blessing regardless of any inner sincerity. The heartfelt turning toward the light has hardened into a superstitious formula, and he waits for the ritual to do the work of the heart.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Pure intellectualism rejecting devotional dimension. Truth sought through reason alone, missing the prayerful aspect of inquiry.
facets.somatic: Body without devotional engagement.
facets.emotional: Emotional dryness in spiritual search.
facets.cognitive: Mind closed to the felt dimension of truth-realization.
facets.psychic: The soul never prays for the veil to lift, content to take the dazzling surface for the whole.

deficient.text:
Pure intellectualism rejecting devotional dimension. Truth sought through reason alone, missing the prayerful aspect of inquiry.

Somatic: Body without devotional engagement.

Emotional: Emotional dryness in spiritual search.

Cognitive: Mind closed to the felt dimension of truth-realization.

Psychic: The soul never prays for the veil to lift, content to take the dazzling surface for the whole.
deficient.vignette: Dan offers no prayer to anything—the sun a ball of gas, the morning a fact, no sacred reconnection anywhere in it. The daily turning toward a larger source that such a prayer offers is a gesture he’s never made; his mornings have no reverence in them.

attributes.title: Vandanam — Prayer as the Heart’s Offering
attributes.aspect: Vandanam is the offering of prayer — both the formal recitation of sacred verses and the informal, intimate conversation of the heart with the divine. As one of the nine limbs of devotion, it is the daily unburdening in which the devotee lays their concerns, their gratitude, and ultimately their very self before something larger than themselves; in its deepest reach it shades into ātma-nivedana, the total self-offering in which not only one’s words but one’s whole selfhood is surrendered. Sincerely practiced, prayer is the simplest and most available doorway of bhakti.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: prayer as the heart’s offering (vandanam); a limb of Bhakti.
coordinate.key_relation: prayer as the heart’s offering (vandanam); a limb of Bhakti.

balanced.label: Balanced — regular, heartfelt prayer that unburdens and opens the heart

facets.overview: Regular prayer practice — both formal and conversational. Heart unburdened daily through prayer.
facets.somatic: Body in prayer-posture (folded hands, bowed head) becoming familiar.
facets.emotional: Emotional release through regular prayer.
facets.cognitive: Mind learns to bring concerns to the Divine.
facets.psychic: The soul unburdens itself in prayer, laying its concerns and its very self before something larger; in its depth, prayer shades into total self-offering.

balanced.text:
Regular prayer practice — both formal and conversational. Heart unburdened daily through prayer.

Somatic: Body in prayer-posture (folded hands, bowed head) becoming familiar.

Emotional: Emotional release through regular prayer.

Cognitive: Mind learns to bring concerns to the Divine.

Psychic: The soul unburdens itself in prayer, laying its concerns and its very self before something larger; in its depth, prayer shades into total self-offering.
balanced.vignette: When Ravi prays, it’s a genuine offering of the heart—not a request list but a laying-down of himself before something larger, an opening rather than a transaction. The vandanam has become the tenderest part of his day; in it, he stops managing and simply offers.

excess.label: In excess — prayer reduced to constant petition: anxious asking, demands, and bargaining

facets.overview: Prayer as constant petition — endless asking for favors, demands, anxious bargaining.
facets.somatic: Body in anxious supplication.
facets.emotional: Emotional dependence on prayer-as-magic.
facets.cognitive: Mind treating prayer transactionally.
facets.psychic: Prayer shrinks to petition; the soul reduces the sacred conversation to anxious asking and bargaining, the relationship lost in the request.

excess.text:
Prayer as constant petition — endless asking for favors, demands, anxious bargaining.

Somatic: Body in anxious supplication.

Emotional: Emotional dependence on prayer-as-magic.

Cognitive: Mind treating prayer transactionally.

Psychic: Prayer shrinks to petition; the soul reduces the sacred conversation to anxious asking and bargaining, the relationship lost in the request.
excess.vignette: Sam’s prayer is performance—the eloquent phrases, the pious posture, the devotion displayed for its effect on himself and others. The heart’s offering has become a show, and the more beautifully he prays, the clearer it is that the offering never leaves his own hands.

deficient.label: Deficient — no prayer; the heart carrying its burdens alone, closed to the sacred

facets.overview: No prayer practice. Carrying burdens alone. No inner dialogue with the Sacred.
facets.somatic: Body never engaged in prayer-posture.
facets.emotional: Emotional isolation, no outlet.
facets.cognitive: Mind has no Divine listener.
facets.psychic: The soul prays not at all; carrying every burden alone, it stays closed to the relief and the larger relationship prayer would open.

deficient.text:
No prayer practice. Carrying burdens alone. No inner dialogue with the Sacred.

Somatic: Body never engaged in prayer-posture.

Emotional: Emotional isolation, no outlet.

Cognitive: Mind has no Divine listener.

Psychic: The soul prays not at all; carrying every burden alone, it stays closed to the relief and the larger relationship prayer would open.
deficient.vignette: Dan never prays—the whole gesture of offering the heart to something larger foreign to him, dismissed or simply unconsidered. The tender opening that prayer can be is a movement he’s never made; there’s no one he lays himself before, and no laying-down at all.

attributes.title: Pada-Sevanam (Service to the Divine)
attributes.aspect: Pada-sevanam means serving the feet of the Divine — symbolically serving the Lord, practically serving all beings as forms of the Divine. In daily life this means transforming ordinary acts of service (cooking for family, helping at work, caring for ill, even paying bills) into sacred offerings. The transformation is in the inner orientation, not the outer act. Pada-sevanam is service — serving the feet of the Lord, but also serving all beings as forms of the Divine. In daily life: every act of service becomes worship. Caring for family, helping colleagues, kindness to strangers — all become spiritual practice when offered to the Divine.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Physical · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: service to the Divine (pāda-sevanam); a limb of Bhakti.
coordinate.key_relation: service to the Divine (pāda-sevanam); a limb of Bhakti.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: All daily service infused with sacred orientation. Family duties, work, helping others — all become worship.
facets.somatic: Body energized by service-as-worship.
facets.emotional: Emotional warmth in all relationships through service-orientation.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees every act of service as devotional offering.
facets.psychic: The soul serves the Divine through humble service to its forms and creatures, devotion expressed as the hands’ work.

balanced.text:
All daily service infused with sacred orientation. Family duties, work, helping others — all become worship.

Somatic: Body energized by service-as-worship.

Emotional: Emotional warmth in all relationships through service-orientation.

Cognitive: Mind sees every act of service as devotional offering.

Psychic: The soul serves the Divine through humble service to its forms and creatures, devotion expressed as the hands’ work.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has found a real joy in serving the sacred—tending the temple, caring for the vulnerable, doing the humble work as an offering rather than for reward. The pada-sevanam has softened his ego; in serving something beyond himself, he’s found a gladness that his self-serving never gave him.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Self-sacrificing ‘service’ that depletes and resents. Codependent rescuing called devotion.
facets.somatic: Body burned out from over-service.
facets.emotional: Emotional martyrdom, hidden resentment.
facets.cognitive: Mind keeps score of service rendered.
facets.psychic: The soul performs service for recognition, the seva feeding the ego it claims to surrender.

excess.text:
Self-sacrificing ‘service’ that depletes and resents. Codependent rescuing called devotion.

Somatic: Body burned out from over-service.

Emotional: Emotional martyrdom, hidden resentment.

Cognitive: Mind keeps score of service rendered.

Psychic: The soul performs service for recognition, the seva feeding the ego it claims to surrender.
excess.vignette: Sam serves for the credit—the visible good works, the publicized humility, the service performed for the spiritual status it earns him. The offering has become a transaction with his own image, and the ‘servant of the divine’ is really serving himself.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: All actions performed only for self-benefit. Service refused or grudgingly given. No sense of sacred dimension to daily acts.
facets.somatic: Body engaged only in self-serving action.
facets.emotional: Emotional isolation from giving.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees no value in service.
facets.psychic: The soul renders no service, its devotion never reaching the hands and feet.

deficient.text:
All actions performed only for self-benefit. Service refused or grudgingly given. No sense of sacred dimension to daily acts.

Somatic: Body engaged only in self-serving action.

Emotional: Emotional isolation from giving.

Cognitive: Mind sees no value in service.

Psychic: The soul renders no service, its devotion never reaching the hands and feet.
deficient.vignette: Dan serves nothing beyond himself—no humble work offered to the sacred, no care given without expectation of return. The self-forgetting joy of service is a gladness he’s never tasted; his energy flows only toward his own advantage, and the deeper satisfaction eludes him.

attributes.attribute: Yagya Lesson: Daily Life as Sacred Offering
attributes.title: Yagya Lesson: Daily Life as Sacred Offering
attributes.aspect: The Yagya avatar embodies sacred offering. In daily life, the principle: every act can be transformed into yagya by inner orientation. Eating becomes yagya when food is mentally offered first. Work becomes yagya when results are released. Speech becomes yagya when words are offered as worship. The outer act is unchanged; the inner transformation is total. Yagya avatar personifies the spirit of sacrifice. In daily life: every act becomes yagya when offered as sacred. Eating becomes yagya (food offered first); work becomes yagya (offering of effort); even sleep becomes yagya (offering of body to rest).

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Interpersonal / cosmic · Causal status: Means · Key relation: daily life as sacred offering (yajña); kin to Karma Yoga (Dim 10).
coordinate.key_relation: daily life as sacred offering (yajña); kin to Karma Yoga (Dim 10).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: All daily acts transformed into yagya through inner orientation. Eating, working, speaking all sacred.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in continuous yagya.
facets.emotional: Emotional dimension of sanctity in all acts.
facets.cognitive: Mind orients every action as offering.
facets.psychic: The soul makes its whole day a yajña, each action offered, so that ordinary living becomes worship.

balanced.text:
All daily acts transformed into yagya through inner orientation. Eating, working, speaking all sacred.

Somatic: Body engaged in continuous yagya.

Emotional: Emotional dimension of sanctity in all acts.

Cognitive: Mind orients every action as offering.

Psychic: The soul makes its whole day a yajña, each action offered, so that ordinary living becomes worship.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to treat his ordinary work as a sacred offering—the daily tasks, the effort, the results all given rather than grasped, his whole life quietly turned into a kind of sacrifice. The yagya isn’t a ritual to him but an attitude, and it’s dignified the most mundane parts of his day.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Ritualism overwhelming actual living. Endless mental offerings preventing actual engagement.
facets.somatic: Body in constant ritual-mode.
facets.emotional: Emotional life burdened by offering-anxiety.
facets.cognitive: Mental over-ritualization.
facets.psychic: The soul turns the offering into rigid ritual accounting, the spirit lost in the bookkeeping.

excess.text:
Ritualism overwhelming actual living. Endless mental offerings preventing actual engagement.

Somatic: Body in constant ritual-mode.

Emotional: Emotional life burdened by offering-anxiety.

Cognitive: Mental over-ritualization.

Psychic: The soul turns the offering into rigid ritual accounting, the spirit lost in the bookkeeping.
excess.vignette: Sam makes a performance of ‘offering everything’—the ostentatious dedication, the announced sacrifice, the piety displayed while he keeps a tight grip on all that matters. The sacred offering has become theater, and the grand gesture of giving all is one more thing the ego keeps for itself.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Daily acts purely mechanical/transactional. No sacred dimension. Life as material process.
facets.somatic: Body in unsanctified activity.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without sacred dimension.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees no offering-potential.
facets.psychic: The soul lives unconsecrated, its actions a mere sequence offered to no one.

deficient.text:
Daily acts purely mechanical/transactional. No sacred dimension. Life as material process.

Somatic: Body in unsanctified activity.

Emotional: Emotional life without sacred dimension.

Cognitive: Mind sees no offering-potential.

Psychic: The soul lives unconsecrated, its actions a mere sequence offered to no one.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s life is pure taking—his work for his gain, his effort for his reward, no sense of any offering owed or given. The whole idea of daily life as sacred sacrifice is foreign to him; he consumes and accumulates, and senses no altar anywhere in his days.

attributes.title: Ashwamedha as Contemplation (Cosmic Sacrifice)
attributes.aspect: The Upanishadic Ashwamedha is not external sacrifice but the transformation of external ritual into inner contemplation. The sacrificial horse becomes the entire cosmos visualized; offering becomes recognition of one’s body as that horse. Each ritual act becomes spiritual practice.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: the horse-sacrifice read as inner, cosmic contemplation (Bṛhadāraṇyaka).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the horse-sacrifice read as inner, cosmic contemplation (Bṛhadāraṇyaka).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: External ritual transformed into inner contemplation. Every act becomes a mode of cosmic identification.
facets.somatic: Body’s actions all become contemplation, no separation between ritual and life.
facets.emotional: Emotional dimension of ritual deepened through contemplative transformation.
facets.cognitive: Mind transforms outer act into inner realization continuously.
facets.psychic: The soul reads the great sacrifice inwardly, offering its whole world as the horse offered to the all.

balanced.text:
External ritual transformed into inner contemplation. Every act becomes a mode of cosmic identification.

Somatic: Body’s actions all become contemplation, no separation between ritual and life.
