Emotional: Emotional dimension of ritual deepened through contemplative transformation.

Cognitive: Mind transforms outer act into inner realization continuously.

Psychic: The soul reads the great sacrifice inwardly, offering its whole world as the horse offered to the all.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to read the great horse-sacrifice inwardly—as the offering of his own ego, his own restless energies, into a larger whole. The Ashwamedha isn’t a ritual he performs but a contemplation he lives: the real sacrifice is the self, and he offers it a little more each day.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Pure intellectualization rejecting all external ritual—missing the embodied dimension.
facets.somatic: Body without ritual engagement, missing somatic aspect.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without ritual nourishment.
facets.cognitive: Mental abstraction without embodied practice.
facets.psychic: The soul over-literalizes the rite, lost in ceremonial detail rather than the surrender it figures.

excess.text:
Pure intellectualization rejecting all external ritual—missing the embodied dimension.

Somatic: Body without ritual engagement, missing somatic aspect.

Emotional: Emotional life without ritual nourishment.

Cognitive: Mental abstraction without embodied practice.

Psychic: The soul over-literalizes the rite, lost in ceremonial detail rather than the surrender it figures.
excess.vignette: Sam has literalized the cosmic sacrifice into grandiosity—casting himself as the cosmic emperor, the center of a vast rite, inflating a contemplative image into a fantasy of his own importance. The inner offering of ego has become, ironically, an aggrandizement of it.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Pure external ritualism without contemplative dimension—mechanical performance.
facets.somatic: Body in rituals without inner contemplation.
facets.emotional: Emotional ritualism without transformation.
facets.cognitive: Mind treats ritual as obligation rather than contemplation.
facets.psychic: The soul makes no inner offering, holding its world tightly as its own possession.

deficient.text:
Pure external ritualism without contemplative dimension—mechanical performance.

Somatic: Body in rituals without inner contemplation.

Emotional: Emotional ritualism without transformation.

Cognitive: Mind treats ritual as obligation rather than contemplation.

Psychic: The soul makes no inner offering, holding its world tightly as its own possession.
deficient.vignette: Dan offers nothing of himself—the inner sacrifice of ego that the great rite symbolizes is a movement he’s never made. The contemplation of the self as offering is foreign to him; he keeps the whole of himself, and the cosmic sacrifice remains, to him, an old and irrelevant myth.

attributes.title: Kriya Yoga (The Action Technique)
attributes.aspect: Kriya Yoga is the direct, practical technique of self-realization revealed by Mahavatar Babaji to Lahiri Mahasay. It is not philosophical inquiry but a concrete pranayama-meditation method that operates directly on the spine, breath, and attention. The technique works without intellectual mediation; results are felt, seen, and known directly in the body-mind.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the action-technique (kriyā yoga); tapas, svādhyāya, īśvara-praṇidhāna (Dim 11).
coordinate.key_relation: the action-technique (kriyā yoga); tapas, svādhyāya, īśvara-praṇidhāna (Dim 11).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Sustained daily Kriya practice with proper initiation. Direct experiential transformation; theoretical understanding follows experience.
facets.somatic: Body’s vital energy (prana) consciously circulated through spine; subtle anatomy activated.
facets.emotional: Emotional purification through pranic work, not just intellectual processing.
facets.cognitive: Mind progressively stilled by the technique itself, not by trying to stop thoughts.
facets.psychic: The soul takes up the inner technique that channels life-force along the spine, refining body and mind toward stillness.

balanced.text:
Sustained daily Kriya practice with proper initiation. Direct experiential transformation; theoretical understanding follows experience.

Somatic: Body’s vital energy (prana) consciously circulated through spine; subtle anatomy activated.

Emotional: Emotional purification through pranic work, not just intellectual processing.

Cognitive: Mind progressively stilled by the technique itself, not by trying to stop thoughts.

Psychic: The soul takes up the inner technique that channels life-force along the spine, refining body and mind toward stillness.
balanced.vignette: Ravi’s practice is woven through his ordinary week—a discipline he keeps, a text he studies, a habit of surrendering outcomes he can’t control—and the three strands together quietly transform him. The kriya-yoga works without his leaving his life to do it; the technique of action has become a way of living.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mechanical Kriya practice without devotional spirit, or excessive practice creating pranic imbalance.
facets.somatic: Body strained by over-practice, pranic disorders.
facets.emotional: Emotional dryness despite practice.
facets.cognitive: Mental obsession with technique without surrender.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on the technique and its phenomena, chasing energetic experiences rather than the silence.

excess.text:
Mechanical Kriya practice without devotional spirit, or excessive practice creating pranic imbalance.

Somatic: Body strained by over-practice, pranic disorders.

Emotional: Emotional dryness despite practice.

Cognitive: Mental obsession with technique without surrender.

Psychic: The soul fixates on the technique and its phenomena, chasing energetic experiences rather than the silence.
excess.vignette: Ben has turned the yoga of action into grim over-effort—punishing discipline, obsessive study, a white-knuckle ‘surrender’ that’s really control in disguise. He’s trying to force his way to freedom, and the forcing is precisely what bars the door.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Theoretical study without practice. Endless reading about Kriya without actual technique. Or practice without proper initiation.
facets.somatic: Body unchanged despite study.
facets.emotional: Emotional life untouched by theoretical knowledge.
facets.cognitive: Mind accumulating concepts without transformative practice.
facets.psychic: The soul practices no inner technique, its life-force dispersed and ungathered.

deficient.text:
Theoretical study without practice. Endless reading about Kriya without actual technique. Or practice without proper initiation.

Somatic: Body unchanged despite study.

Emotional: Emotional life untouched by theoretical knowledge.

Cognitive: Mind accumulating concepts without transformative practice.

Psychic: The soul practices no inner technique, its life-force dispersed and ungathered.
deficient.vignette: Sara admires the idea of a transformative practice and does none of it—no discipline, no study, no surrender, just drift. The engine of change sits idle for want of anyone to start it, and the technique of action goes entirely unused.

attributes.title: Tapas-Damah-Karma (Austerity, Self-Restraint, Action)
attributes.aspect: Austerity (tapas), self-restraint (damah), and action (karma) are the feet of this knowledge. Vedas are its limbs; truth is its abode. These three are foundational to the spiritual path.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: austerity, self-restraint, and action as the triad of purifying works.
coordinate.key_relation: austerity, self-restraint, and action as the triad of purifying works.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Balanced practice of austerity, sense-restraint, and right action. These three feet support the realization of Brahman.
facets.somatic: Body disciplined but not damaged, restrained but vital.
facets.emotional: Emotional discipline without suppression.
facets.cognitive: Mind tempered by tapas, restrained by damah, active in dharma.
facets.psychic: The soul weaves austerity, self-restraint, and selfless action into a single disciplined life.

balanced.text:
Balanced practice of austerity, sense-restraint, and right action. These three feet support the realization of Brahman.

Somatic: Body disciplined but not damaged, restrained but vital.

Emotional: Emotional discipline without suppression.

Cognitive: Mind tempered by tapas, restrained by damah, active in dharma.

Psychic: The soul weaves austerity, self-restraint, and selfless action into a single disciplined life.
balanced.vignette: Ravi keeps the three in balance—a purifying austerity he can bear, a self-restraint that governs without crushing, and an action offered rather than grasped. The three together temper him; austerity, restraint, and karma work as one discipline, and the discipline is quietly forging his character.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive austerity damaging body, harsh self-restraint creating repression, action without wisdom.
facets.somatic: Body damaged by extreme tapas.
facets.emotional: Suppressed emotions creating distortion.
facets.cognitive: Mental rigidity from excessive restraint.
facets.psychic: The soul over-emphasizes one — harsh austerity, or grim restraint — and the balance is lost.

excess.text:
Excessive austerity damaging body, harsh self-restraint creating repression, action without wisdom.

Somatic: Body damaged by extreme tapas.

Emotional: Suppressed emotions creating distortion.

Cognitive: Mental rigidity from excessive restraint.

Psychic: The soul over-emphasizes one — harsh austerity, or grim restraint — and the balance is lost.
excess.vignette: Sam overdoes one strand at the others’ expense—austerity tipped into self-punishment, or restraint into rigid repression, or action into compulsive doing. The balance the three were meant to hold has broken; one gear, driven too hard, grinds the others down.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No austerity, no restraint, action without ethics. The three feet missing—knowledge has no support.
facets.somatic: Body undisciplined, indulgent.
facets.emotional: No emotional discipline.
facets.cognitive: Mind without practice or restraint.
facets.psychic: The soul practices none of the three, its life without discipline, restraint, or dedicated work.

deficient.text:
No austerity, no restraint, action without ethics. The three feet missing—knowledge has no support.

Somatic: Body undisciplined, indulgent.

Emotional: No emotional discipline.

Cognitive: Mind without practice or restraint.

Psychic: The soul practices none of the three, its life without discipline, restraint, or dedicated work.
deficient.vignette: Dan practices none of the three—no austerity, no self-restraint, no offered action—leaving his character to whatever the day makes of it. The tempering discipline the three provide is one he’s never taken up; nothing in him is tested, governed, or refined.

attributes.title: Karma-Yoga — Daily Life as Sacred Offering
attributes.aspect: Karma-yoga transforms ordinary action into practice by changing not what one does but the spirit in which it is done: acting wholeheartedly while offering the fruits, so that work becomes a continual sacrifice (yajña) rather than a means of acquisition. Its genius is that it requires no withdrawal from life — the office, the kitchen, the duties of family all become the field of practice. Its discipline is the steady relinquishing of attachment to outcomes, which frees action from anxiety and turns the whole of daily life into a sacred offering.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, engaging the world · Causal status: Means · Key relation: daily life as sacred offering; same as Karma Yoga the path (Dim 10).
coordinate.key_relation: daily life as sacred offering; same as Karma Yoga the path (Dim 10).

balanced.label: Balanced — wholehearted action with the fruits offered, free of anxiety

facets.overview: Full engagement with action; full release of attachment to results. Krishna’s karma yoga lived daily.
facets.somatic: Body in full energetic action without strain of grasping.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from result-anxiety.
facets.cognitive: Mind acts while releasing outcomes.
facets.psychic: The soul acts wholeheartedly and releases the fruit; offering action itself as worship, it works in the world without being bound by results.

balanced.text:
Full engagement with action; full release of attachment to results. Krishna’s karma yoga lived daily.

Somatic: Body in full energetic action without strain of grasping.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from result-anxiety.

Cognitive: Mind acts while releasing outcomes.

Psychic: The soul acts wholeheartedly and releases the fruit; offering action itself as worship, it works in the world without being bound by results.
balanced.vignette: Ravi does his work well and offers the results—caring about the outcome without being chained to it, acting fully and grasping nothing. The karma-yoga has freed him within his ordinary life; he still works hard, but the frantic clutch at results has quietly let go.

excess.label: In excess — grim, joyless duty; or using ‘detachment’ to avoid real care

facets.overview: Pretended detachment that’s actually disengagement. Half-effort claimed as karma yoga.
facets.somatic: Body in partial engagement.
facets.emotional: Emotional uninvolvement masquerading as detachment.
facets.cognitive: Mental laziness disguised as result-release.
facets.psychic: ‘Non-attachment’ becomes an excuse for carelessness; the soul uses ‘I surrender the fruits’ to avoid genuine responsibility and effort.

excess.text:
Pretended detachment that’s actually disengagement. Half-effort claimed as karma yoga.

Somatic: Body in partial engagement.

Emotional: Emotional uninvolvement masquerading as detachment.

Cognitive: Mental laziness disguised as result-release.

Psychic: ‘Non-attachment’ becomes an excuse for carelessness; the soul uses ‘I surrender the fruits’ to avoid genuine responsibility and effort.
excess.vignette: Sam performs selfless action—the ostentatious dedication, the announced non-attachment—while inwardly gripping every outcome as tightly as ever. The offering of his work has become a show, and the ‘selflessness’ is one more thing his self is doing for effect.

deficient.label: Deficient — action wholly driven by craving for results; work as mere acquisition

facets.overview: Complete attachment to outcomes. Every result felt personally; every failure devastating.
facets.somatic: Body strained by attachment-driven action.
facets.emotional: Emotional roller-coaster tied to outcomes.
facets.cognitive: Mind obsessed with results.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly attached to outcomes; every result taken personally, every failure a wound, action becomes a treadmill of anxiety.

deficient.text:
Complete attachment to outcomes. Every result felt personally; every failure devastating.

Emotional: Emotional roller-coaster tied to outcomes.

Cognitive: Mind obsessed with results.

Psychic: The soul is wholly attached to outcomes; every result taken personally, every failure a wound, action becomes a treadmill of anxiety.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s every action is self-serving and outcome-bound—gripping each result, devastated by failure, inflated by success, no offering anywhere in it. That one could act fully and yet remain free of the fruits is a possibility he’s never lived; every deed binds him tighter.

attributes.title: Right Channelisation of Energy
attributes.aspect: Energy is a finite resource. Where we direct it determines what we become. Downward channelisation (sense-objects, gossip, anger, worry) dissipates and degrades. Upward channelisation (contemplation, service, devotion) accumulates and elevates. Skilled energy-channeling is essential to lived spirituality.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the right direction of vital energy; kin to the prāṇa vāyus (Dim 6).
coordinate.key_relation: the right direction of vital energy; kin to the prāṇa vāyus (Dim 6).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Energy consciously channeled toward higher purposes—not all upward (need life-function) but predominantly elevated.
facets.somatic: Body’s energy conserved and directed wisely.
facets.emotional: Emotional energy channeled toward devotion and meaningful engagement.
facets.cognitive: Mental energy directed toward wisdom-pursuits.
facets.psychic: The soul directs its vital energy toward worthy aims, the force that drives it given a sacred course.

balanced.text:
Energy consciously channeled toward higher purposes—not all upward (need life-function) but predominantly elevated.

Somatic: Body’s energy conserved and directed wisely.

Emotional: Emotional energy channeled toward devotion and meaningful engagement.

Cognitive: Mental energy directed toward wisdom-pursuits.

Psychic: The soul directs its vital energy toward worthy aims, the force that drives it given a sacred course.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has learned to direct his vital energy well—the drive that used to scatter into agitation now channeled into work, practice, and care. The right channelisation isn’t repression; it’s aim, and with his considerable force pointed rather than diffused, he accomplishes more and burns out less.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced upward-channeling that violates legitimate needs—ascetic burnout.
facets.somatic: Body neglected as ‘lower channel.’
facets.emotional: Emotional life dismissed.
facets.cognitive: Mental engagement only in ‘spiritual’ topics.
facets.psychic: The soul forces and manipulates its energy obsessively, straining the very current it would guide.

excess.text:
Forced upward-channeling that violates legitimate needs—ascetic burnout.

Somatic: Body neglected as ‘lower channel.’

Emotional: Emotional life dismissed.

Cognitive: Mental engagement only in ‘spiritual’ topics.

Psychic: The soul forces and manipulates its energy obsessively, straining the very current it would guide.
excess.vignette: Sam forces his energy aggressively—the intense practices, the manipulated breath, the willful pushing of his vitality in pursuit of dramatic states—and destabilizes the very system he’s driving. The channelling has become a forcing, and the forcing throws his whole energy off.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: All energy dissipated downward—sense-pursuits, gossip, worry. Maximum depletion.
facets.somatic: Body exhausted from downward channeling.
facets.emotional: Emotional life dissipated in low engagements.
facets.cognitive: Mind drained by trivia and worry.
facets.psychic: The soul lets its energy run wherever impulse leads, dissipated and ungoverned.

deficient.text:
All energy dissipated downward—sense-pursuits, gossip, worry. Maximum depletion.

Somatic: Body exhausted from downward channeling.

Emotional: Emotional life dissipated in low engagements.

Cognitive: Mind drained by trivia and worry.

Psychic: The soul lets its energy run wherever impulse leads, dissipated and ungoverned.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s energy dissipates in every direction—leaking into distraction, worry, and appetite, no channel, no aim, no conservation. The right direction of vitality that focuses a life is undeveloped in him; his considerable force scatters, and he’s perpetually depleted for it.

attributes.title: Harmonisation of Mind and Breath
attributes.aspect: Mind and breath are two faces of the same vital force. Their movements mirror each other—anxious thoughts produce shallow breath; deep breath calms the mind. Conscious harmonisation creates a powerful spiritual lever. Through breath, the mind is reached; through the mind, breath is regulated.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: harmonising mind and breath; kin to Prāṇāyāma (Dim 11) and the Vāsanā-Prāṇa link (Dim 26).
coordinate.key_relation: harmonising mind and breath; kin to Prāṇāyāma (Dim 11) and the Vāsanā-Prāṇa link (Dim 26).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Breath and mind in mutual harmony—each supporting the other. Deep slow breath, peaceful mind; calm mind, natural deep breath.
facets.somatic: Body breathes deeply and naturally, supporting calm.
facets.emotional: Emotional calm reflected in breath; calm breath supports emotional stability.
facets.cognitive: Mind peaceful, in resonance with deep breath.
facets.psychic: The soul harmonizes breath and mind, knowing that as one settles the other follows into stillness.

balanced.text:
Breath and mind in mutual harmony—each supporting the other. Deep slow breath, peaceful mind; calm mind, natural deep breath.

Somatic: Body breathes deeply and naturally, supporting calm.

Emotional: Emotional calm reflected in breath; calm breath supports emotional stability.

Cognitive: Mind peaceful, in resonance with deep breath.

Psychic: The soul harmonizes breath and mind, knowing that as one settles the other follows into stillness.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has felt directly how his mind and breath move together—how steadying the one settles the other—and works with the connection skillfully. When his thoughts race, he lengthens the breath; when the breath calms, the mind follows, the two harmonized into a single quieting.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced breath-control creating problems—pranayama without preparation, hyperventilation.
facets.somatic: Body strained from forced breath techniques.
facets.emotional: Emotional disturbance from improper breath-work.
facets.cognitive: Mental disturbance from forced techniques.
facets.psychic: The soul over-manipulates the breath to control the mind by force, the straining defeating the calm.

excess.text:
Forced breath-control creating problems—pranayama without preparation, hyperventilation.

Somatic: Body strained from forced breath techniques.

Emotional: Emotional disturbance from improper breath-work.

Cognitive: Mental disturbance from forced techniques.

Psychic: The soul over-manipulates the breath to control the mind by force, the straining defeating the calm.
excess.vignette: Sam over-manipulates his breath to force his mind—aggressive techniques, willful control, straining to bludgeon his thoughts into stillness through the breath. The gentle harmony has become a forcing, and the forcing agitates the very mind it means to calm.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Shallow disregulated breathing reflecting agitated mind. Self-perpetuating cycle of stress.
facets.somatic: Shallow chronic breathing, depleted prana.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity reflected in breath.
facets.cognitive: Mental agitation reinforced by shallow breath.
facets.psychic: The soul leaves breath and mind at odds, an agitated breath keeping the mind forever stirred.

deficient.text:
Shallow disregulated breathing reflecting agitated mind. Self-perpetuating cycle of stress.

Somatic: Shallow chronic breathing, depleted prana.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity reflected in breath.

Cognitive: Mental agitation reinforced by shallow breath.

Psychic: The soul leaves breath and mind at odds, an agitated breath keeping the mind forever stirred.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no idea his mind and breath are connected—the breath following his stress unnoticed, the link never used. The most available handle on his own state goes untouched; when his mind races, it never occurs to him that the breath could bring it home.

attributes.attribute: Sanak Kumara Lesson: Brahmacharya for Daily Conscience
attributes.title: Sanak Kumara Lesson: Brahmacharya for Daily Conscience
attributes.aspect: The First Avatar (Four Kumaras) teaches brahmacharya as the foundational quality. In daily life, this is not just sexual restraint but the broader principle of energy-conservation — not dissipating life-energy in trivial sense-pursuits, gossip, mental wandering. Energy conserved is energy available for the highest purpose. Purifies all four inner instruments. The Four Kumaras (Sanak, Sanandan, Sanatan, Sanatkumar) embody brahmacharya — not just celibacy but conserving energy for the highest purpose. In daily life: don’t dissipate energy carelessly; conserve for the most important. Purifies manas, buddhi, chit, ahankar.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: Brahmacharya for daily conscience (Sanaka); same as the yama (Dim 11).
coordinate.key_relation: Brahmacharya for daily conscience (Sanaka); same as the yama (Dim 11).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Conscious energy-conservation across all dimensions. Energy reserved for highest purpose.
facets.somatic: Body’s energy conserved through sensible self-discipline.
facets.emotional: Emotional energy not dispersed in trivial reactions.
facets.cognitive: Mental energy focused on what truly matters.
facets.psychic: The soul keeps a wise stewardship of its vital energy, conscience and continence steadying the whole life.

balanced.text:
Conscious energy-conservation across all dimensions. Energy reserved for highest purpose.

Somatic: Body’s energy conserved through sensible self-discipline.

Emotional: Emotional energy not dispersed in trivial reactions.

Cognitive: Mental energy focused on what truly matters.

Psychic: The soul keeps a wise stewardship of its vital energy, conscience and continence steadying the whole life.
balanced.vignette: Like Sanaka guarding the conscience, Ravi keeps a clean inner accounting—energy conserved, integrity intact, the small daily choices made with a working sense of right. His brahmacharya isn’t grim repression; it’s a wise stewardship of his force and a conscience kept clear, and both leave him steady.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Rigid brahmacharya damaging body, suppressing legitimate energy expression.
facets.somatic: Body harmed by excessive suppression.
facets.emotional: Emotional repression creating distortion.
facets.cognitive: Mental rigidity around restraint.
facets.psychic: The soul turns continence into grim repression, warring against its own vitality.

excess.text:
Rigid brahmacharya damaging body, suppressing legitimate energy expression.

Somatic: Body harmed by excessive suppression.

Emotional: Emotional repression creating distortion.

Cognitive: Mental rigidity around restraint.

Psychic: The soul turns continence into grim repression, warring against its own vitality.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned conscience into scrupulosity—policing every thought for impurity, tormented by minor lapses, his energy-conservation hardened into an anxious, joyless self-surveillance. The clean conscience has become a tyrant, and the vigilance is its own affliction.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total energy dissipation through indulgence in everything. Senses run free; mind disperses constantly.
facets.somatic: Body depleted by indulgence.
facets.emotional: Emotional life in constant dispersal.
facets.cognitive: Mind never gathers itself.
facets.psychic: The soul squanders its energy heedlessly, conscience dulled and reserves spent.

deficient.text:
Total energy dissipation through indulgence in everything. Senses run free; mind disperses constantly.

Somatic: Body depleted by indulgence.

Emotional: Emotional life in constant dispersal.

Cognitive: Mind never gathers itself.

Psychic: The soul squanders its energy heedlessly, conscience dulled and reserves spent.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no working conscience—the small daily choices made purely for advantage, his energy squandered on every appetite, no inner sense of ‘ought’ to check him. The clean conscience and conserved force that steady a life are absent; nothing in him keeps the accounts.

attributes.title: Varaha Lesson: Santosha (Contentment in Daily Life)
attributes.aspect: Varaha’s avatar teaches santosha (contentment in any situation). The boar-form Vishnu lifts the earth from the cosmic waters — symbolically, lifting consciousness above immediate difficulties. In daily life, santosha is not passivity but the capacity to remain inwardly content regardless of external circumstances. This is the foundation upon which all spiritual progress is built. Varaha lifts the earth from cosmic waters — symbol of santosha (contentment) in all situations. The lesson for daily life: like Varaha lifting earth, lift one’s perspective above difficulties. Remain contented whatever situation arises.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: Santoṣa — contentment in daily life (Varāha); same as the niyama (Dim 11).
coordinate.key_relation: Santoṣa — contentment in daily life (Varāha); same as the niyama (Dim 11).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Daily contentment cultivated across all situations. Inner stability not dependent on external fortune.
facets.somatic: Body relaxed in present circumstances.
facets.emotional: Emotional contentment as baseline.
facets.cognitive: Mind not constantly dissatisfied.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in contentment, lifting its world from the mire as Varāha lifted the earth, full where it once was wanting.

balanced.text:
Daily contentment cultivated across all situations. Inner stability not dependent on external fortune.

Somatic: Body relaxed in present circumstances.

Emotional: Emotional contentment as baseline.

Cognitive: Mind not constantly dissatisfied.

Psychic: The soul rests in contentment, lifting its world from the mire as Varāha lifted the earth, full where it once was wanting.
balanced.vignette: Kira’s life is ordinary and modest, and most evenings she feels, without irony, that she has enough—the contentment of Varaha’s lesson lived quietly. She wants things and works for them and isn’t tormented by their absence; the sufficiency is real, not resigned.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Apathetic ‘contentment’ that doesn’t address real problems. Passive acceptance.
facets.somatic: Body neglected in name of contentment.
facets.emotional: Emotional flatness called contentment.
facets.cognitive: Mind avoiding necessary engagement.
facets.psychic: The soul lets contentment slide into complacency, excusing stagnation as peace.

excess.text:
Apathetic ‘contentment’ that doesn’t address real problems. Passive acceptance.

Somatic: Body neglected in name of contentment.

Emotional: Emotional flatness called contentment.

Cognitive: Mind avoiding necessary engagement.

Psychic: The soul lets contentment slide into complacency, excusing stagnation as peace.
excess.vignette: Ben is so ‘content’ that nothing ever changes—the stalled life, the neglected health all accepted with a shrug dressed as serenity. His contentment has curdled into complacency, a spiritual-sounding excuse never to rise from the chair.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Chronic dissatisfaction. Nothing is enough. Every circumstance triggers complaint.
facets.somatic: Body in continuous tension from dissatisfaction.
facets.emotional: Emotional life of perpetual complaint.
facets.cognitive: Mind always finding fault.
facets.psychic: The soul is chronically discontent, its peace always one acquisition away.

deficient.text:
Chronic dissatisfaction. Nothing is enough. Every circumstance triggers complaint.

Somatic: Body in continuous tension from dissatisfaction.

Emotional: Emotional life of perpetual complaint.

Cognitive: Mind always finding fault.

Psychic: The soul is chronically discontent, its peace always one acquisition away.
deficient.vignette: Nothing is ever enough for Dana—each milestone disappointing on arrival, the horizon of ‘then I’ll be happy’ forever receding. She’s chronically, exhaustingly dissatisfied, and the discontent poisons even her good fortune.

attributes.title: Abhyasa (Sustained Practice)
attributes.aspect: Abhyasa is sustained practice—the persistent effort to remain in the yogic state. Patanjali says it must be done for a long time, without interruption, with reverence to become firmly grounded (Sutra 1.14).

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: sustained practice (abhyāsa); with Vairāgya, a wing of yoga (Dim 11).
coordinate.key_relation: sustained practice (abhyāsa); with Vairāgya, a wing of yoga (Dim 11).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Abhyasa is properly cultivated, a person practices consistently with dedication and patience. Long-term, uninterrupted, reverent practice creates stable progress.
facets.somatic: Disciplined physical practice routine, body that benefits from consistency, healthy practice habits.
facets.emotional: Emotional commitment to path, devotion to practice, patience with process.
facets.cognitive: Mental discipline of sustained practice, patience, focused effort, persistence.
facets.psychic: The soul gives itself to steady, long-continued practice, trusting that repetition alone bears fruit.

balanced.text:
When Abhyasa is properly cultivated, a person practices consistently with dedication and patience. Long-term, uninterrupted, reverent practice creates stable progress.

Somatic: Disciplined physical practice routine, body that benefits from consistency, healthy practice habits.

Emotional: Emotional commitment to path, devotion to practice, patience with process.

Cognitive: Mental discipline of sustained practice, patience, focused effort, persistence.

Psychic: The soul gives itself to steady, long-continued practice, trusting that repetition alone bears fruit.
balanced.vignette: Every morning, without drama, Meera practices—the same steady hour, year after year, through motivation and its absence alike. It’s the consistency, not the intensity, that’s transformed her; the quiet daily repetition has done what no burst of enthusiasm ever could.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive Abhyasa becomes obsessive practice, harming body through over-effort, rigid attachment to practice forms.
facets.somatic: Physical injury from over-practice, body damaged by extreme effort, burnout.
facets.emotional: Emotional rigidity about practice, anxiety if practice missed, obsessive devotion.
facets.cognitive: Mental obsession with practice forms, attachment to specific routines, anxiety about consistency.
facets.psychic: Practice becomes compulsive over-effort, the soul driving itself and mistaking strain for progress.

excess.text:
Excessive Abhyasa becomes obsessive practice, harming body through over-effort, rigid attachment to practice forms.

Somatic: Physical injury from over-practice, body damaged by extreme effort, burnout.

Emotional: Emotional rigidity about practice, anxiety if practice missed, obsessive devotion.

Cognitive: Mental obsession with practice forms, attachment to specific routines, anxiety about consistency.

Psychic: Practice becomes compulsive over-effort, the soul driving itself and mistaking strain for progress.
excess.vignette: Ben practices for hours a day and has let his job and relationships thin to make room, driving himself past rest and reason in pursuit of faster progress. The sustained practice has become a compulsion, and the life he’s neglecting is beginning to send the bill.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Lack of Abhyasa means no sustained practice, sporadic effort that builds no foundation, inconsistency preventing all progress.
facets.somatic: Inconsistent physical practice, no body development, sporadic effort yielding no results.
facets.emotional: Lack of emotional commitment, no devotional consistency, inconsistent engagement.
facets.cognitive: No mental discipline, inability to sustain effort, no foundation built, no progress possible.
facets.psychic: Practice done in fits and starts never accumulates, the soul’s gains leaking away between sessions.

deficient.text:
Lack of Abhyasa means no sustained practice, sporadic effort that builds no foundation, inconsistency preventing all progress.

Somatic: Inconsistent physical practice, no body development, sporadic effort yielding no results.

Emotional: Lack of emotional commitment, no devotional consistency, inconsistent engagement.

Cognitive: No mental discipline, inability to sustain effort, no foundation built, no progress possible.

Psychic: Practice done in fits and starts never accumulates, the soul’s gains leaking away between sessions.
deficient.vignette: Sara starts a practice every January and abandons it by February, the mat gathering dust till the next surge of resolve. Without consistency nothing accumulates; her potential stays theoretical for want of the one unglamorous thing—showing up, daily, anyway.

attributes.title: Rishabha Lesson: Personal Conduct (Acharana)
attributes.aspect: Rishabhadeva’s avatar teaches that spirituality manifests in everyday conduct (acharana). It is not theoretical knowledge but how you behave — how you speak to your servant, how you eat, how you keep your room, how you respond to inconvenience. Rishabha lived radical conduct that exemplified his teaching. In daily life: examine your micro-behaviors; that’s where your real spirituality shows. Rishabhadeva embodies the spirit of personal conduct (acharana). In daily life: how you conduct yourself moment-to-moment is your real spirituality. Theory means nothing without exemplary conduct in small daily things.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Practice / lesson — an avatar-modeled teaching · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, engaging the world · Causal status: Means / model · Key relation: personal conduct (ācaraṇa) as the ground of practice (Ṛṣabha).
coordinate.key_relation: personal conduct (ācaraṇa) as the ground of practice (Ṛṣabha).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Daily micro-conduct aligned with espoused values. No gap between belief and behavior in small things.
facets.somatic: Body’s micro-behaviors all aligned with values.
facets.emotional: Emotional responses consistent with values.
facets.cognitive: Mind’s conduct in every situation consistent.
facets.psychic: The soul tends its personal conduct as the ground of all practice, knowing character is the soil realization grows in.

balanced.text:
Daily micro-conduct aligned with espoused values. No gap between belief and behavior in small things.

Somatic: Body’s micro-behaviors all aligned with values.

Emotional: Emotional responses consistent with values.

Cognitive: Mind’s conduct in every situation consistent.

Psychic: The soul tends its personal conduct as the ground of all practice, knowing character is the soil realization grows in.
balanced.vignette: Like Rishabha modeling right conduct, Ravi governs himself well—his behavior consistent, his word reliable, his acharana a quiet example others trust. The personal conduct isn’t performed for approval; it’s simply who he’s become, and the integrity steadies everyone around him.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Perfectionism about conduct creating self-judgment and judgment of others.
facets.somatic: Body strained by perfectionism.
facets.emotional: Emotional self-criticism and criticism of others.
facets.cognitive: Mental obsession with right-conduct.
facets.psychic: The soul makes outward conduct a performance of virtue, the form preserved while the heart drifts.

excess.text:
Perfectionism about conduct creating self-judgment and judgment of others.

Somatic: Body strained by perfectionism.

Emotional: Emotional self-criticism and criticism of others.

Cognitive: Mental obsession with right-conduct.

Psychic: The soul makes outward conduct a performance of virtue, the form preserved while the heart drifts.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned personal conduct into rigid moralism—policing his behavior and everyone else’s, correcting, judging, the acharana hardened into a joyless legalism. The right conduct has become a cudgel, and the propriety serves his pride more than anyone’s good.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Gap between teaching and behavior. Espoused values not lived in micro-conduct.
facets.somatic: Body’s behaviors contradict claimed values.
facets.emotional: Emotional incongruence.
facets.cognitive: Mind has dual standards (high theory, low practice).
facets.psychic: The soul neglects its conduct, its aspirations rootless for want of a disciplined life.

deficient.text:
Gap between teaching and behavior. Espoused values not lived in micro-conduct.

Somatic: Body’s behaviors contradict claimed values.

Emotional: Emotional incongruence.

Cognitive: Mind has dual standards (high theory, low practice).

Psychic: The soul neglects its conduct, its aspirations rootless for want of a disciplined life.
deficient.vignette: Dan governs himself hardly at all—his conduct erratic, his word unreliable, no consistent acharana to make him trustworthy. The self-governance that right conduct requires is undeveloped; he does what’s convenient, and the people around him learn not to count on him.

attributes.attribute: Narasimha Lesson: Divine Protection in Crisis
attributes.title: Narasimha Lesson: Divine Protection in Crisis
attributes.aspect: The Narasimha story teaches: when ordinary protection fails, sincere devotion calls forth extraordinary divine response. Prahlada’s father tried every form of harm; nothing succeeded because Prahlada had pure faith. When the crisis reached its peak, Narasimha emerged from a pillar — utterly unexpected. In daily life: in deepest crisis, sincere prayer is never abandoned. Help comes, often in unexpected forms. Narasimha appears from a pillar when no one else can save Prahlada. The lesson: divine protection comes in seemingly impossible circumstances. When ordinary help fails, sincere call to the Divine is answered in unexpected forms.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / lesson — an avatar-modeled teaching · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, engaging the world · Causal status: Means / model · Key relation: trust in divine protection in crisis (Narasiṁha).
coordinate.key_relation: trust in divine protection in crisis (Narasiṁha).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Faith maintained through crises. The deepest difficulties become opportunities for divine grace to manifest.
facets.somatic: Body sustained through trials by faith.
facets.emotional: Emotional refuge in faith during crisis.
facets.cognitive: Mind anchored in trust even when help isn’t visible.
facets.psychic: The soul trusts that the Divine meets it in its deepest crisis, as Narasiṁha met Prahlāda, and so faces danger unafraid.

balanced.text:
Faith maintained through crises. The deepest difficulties become opportunities for divine grace to manifest.

Somatic: Body sustained through trials by faith.

Emotional: Emotional refuge in faith during crisis.

Cognitive: Mind anchored in trust even when help isn’t visible.

Psychic: The soul trusts that the Divine meets it in its deepest crisis, as Narasiṁha met Prahlāda, and so faces danger unafraid.
balanced.vignette: When the crisis came—sudden, overwhelming, beyond his own strength—Ravi found a genuine faith that he was held, and the faith carried him through where willpower would have failed. Like Prahlada calling and Narasimha appearing, he learned that grace meets the sincere cry in the very worst hour.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Passive waiting for miracles instead of doing one’s part.
facets.somatic: Body inactive expecting divine rescue.
facets.emotional: Emotional dependence on miracles.
facets.cognitive: Mental abdication of agency.
facets.psychic: The soul presumes upon protection, courting needless danger in the confidence of rescue.

excess.text:
Passive waiting for miracles instead of doing one’s part.

Somatic: Body inactive expecting divine rescue.

Emotional: Emotional dependence on miracles.

Cognitive: Mental abdication of agency.

Psychic: The soul presumes upon protection, courting needless danger in the confidence of rescue.
excess.vignette: Sam treats ‘divine protection in crisis’ as a rescue he can summon on demand—doing nothing to help himself, passively waiting for the miracle, gaming the promise of protection. The faith that opens to grace has become a fatalism that excuses all effort, and he sinks while waiting to be saved.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No faith in divine help. Crises met with despair. Self-reliance becomes isolation in difficulty.
facets.somatic: Body alone in crisis.
facets.emotional: Emotional despair without resource.
facets.cognitive: Mind without trust in higher help.
facets.psychic: The soul feels utterly unprotected, facing crisis as if entirely alone in an indifferent world.

deficient.text:
No faith in divine help. Crises met with despair. Self-reliance becomes isolation in difficulty.

Somatic: Body alone in crisis.

Emotional: Emotional despair without resource.

Cognitive: Mind without trust in higher help.

Psychic: The soul feels utterly unprotected, facing crisis as if entirely alone in an indifferent world.
deficient.vignette: When crisis strikes, Dan has nothing to lean on—no faith that he’s held, no sense of any protection beyond his own failing strength, only the raw terror of facing it alone. The trust that could steady him in the worst hour is one he’s never had, and so the worst hours find him unaccompanied.

attributes.attribute: Vamana Lesson: Humility Before the Divine
attributes.title: Vamana Lesson: Humility Before the Divine
attributes.aspect: Vamana’s story teaches humility at the height of prosperity. King Bali had conquered heaven itself, yet when Vamana (a small brahmin boy) asked for three steps of land, Bali agreed. When the Divine grew immense and covered all three worlds with three steps, Bali offered his head for the third step. The lesson: prosperity does not require pride; surrender what you possess when called. King Bali, even when prosperous, accepted Vamana’s three steps and offered his head when no other ground remained. The lesson: surrender what you possess when the Divine asks; humility at the height of success is the highest virtue.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / lesson — an avatar-modeled teaching · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, engaging the world · Causal status: Means / model · Key relation: humility before the Divine (Vāmana); kin to Mārdavam (Dim 24).
coordinate.key_relation: humility before the Divine (Vāmana); kin to Mārdavam (Dim 24).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Prosperity held with humility. What is given by the Divine is offered back when called. No clinging.
facets.somatic: Body engaged with prosperity without identification.
facets.emotional: Emotional non-attachment to status.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees possessions as Divine’s loan.
facets.psychic: The soul keeps a true humility before the Divine, like Vāmana’s small form that yet spanned the worlds.

balanced.text:
Prosperity held with humility. What is given by the Divine is offered back when called. No clinging.

Somatic: Body engaged with prosperity without identification.

Emotional: Emotional non-attachment to status.

Cognitive: Mind sees possessions as Divine’s loan.

Psychic: The soul keeps a true humility before the Divine, like Vāmana’s small form that yet spanned the worlds.
balanced.vignette: Like Vamana asking only three steps, Ravi meets the sacred with a genuine humility—small before what’s larger than him, unpuffed by his own gifts, unhurried to claim greatness. The humility isn’t self-abasement; it’s the honest smallness of someone who’s felt the vastness, and it makes him quietly luminous.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Performed ‘humility’ that’s actually fear of success. Refusing legitimate prosperity.
facets.somatic: Body refused legitimate prosperity.
facets.emotional: Emotional fear of success disguised as humility.
facets.cognitive: Mental pose of humility.
facets.psychic: The soul performs humility for admiration, a self-effacement that is its own subtle pride.

excess.text:
Performed ‘humility’ that’s actually fear of success. Refusing legitimate prosperity.

Somatic: Body refused legitimate prosperity.

Emotional: Emotional fear of success disguised as humility.

Cognitive: Mental pose of humility.

Psychic: The soul performs humility for admiration, a self-effacement that is its own subtle pride.
excess.vignette: Sam performs humility—the practiced self-deprecation, the announced smallness, the ‘I’m nothing’ that fishes for reassurance. The genuine lowliness before the divine has become a show, and the humbler he claims to be, the more the ego is running the display.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Pride at success. Refusal to surrender what one possesses. Maximum spiritual blindness at peak of prosperity.
facets.somatic: Body in arrogant display.
facets.emotional: Emotional pride that won’t surrender.
facets.cognitive: Mind takes possessions as inherent right.
facets.psychic: The soul stands in arrogance before life, no sense of anything greater to bow before.

deficient.text:
Pride at success. Refusal to surrender what one possesses. Maximum spiritual blindness at peak of prosperity.

Somatic: Body in arrogant display.

Emotional: Emotional pride that won’t surrender.

Cognitive: Mind takes possessions as inherent right.

Psychic: The soul stands in arrogance before life, no sense of anything greater to bow before.
deficient.vignette: Dan meets everything with an unhumbled self-importance—no smallness before anything larger, no sense of a vastness that dwarfs his gifts. The humility that opens a person to the divine is foreign to him; he stands, always, at the center of his own cosmos.

attributes.title: Parashurama Lesson: Righteous Anger (Vs. Personal Rage)
attributes.aspect: Parashurama, the warrior-brahmin, used force against corrupt rulers but his anger was always dharmic — for justice, not personal grievance. The lesson for daily life: not all anger is wrong. Anger about injustice, abuse, dharma-violation has its place. But ego-anger (someone offended me, I didn’t get my way) is destructive. Discriminate between these two qualitatively different forms. Parashurama destroyed corrupt Kshatriyas but his anger was dharmic, not personal. The lesson: anger has a place when it’s about injustice and dharma, not personal grievance. Discriminate between righteous indignation and ego-anger.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Practice / lesson — an avatar-modeled teaching · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, engaging the world · Causal status: Means / model · Key relation: righteous force vs personal rage (Paraśurāma); kin to Amarṣa (Dim 9) and Akrodha (Dim 24).
coordinate.key_relation: righteous force vs personal rage (Paraśurāma); kin to Amarṣa (Dim 9) and Akrodha (Dim 24).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Dharmic anger when warranted; personal anger seen through. Discrimination between the two.
facets.somatic: Body channels righteous indignation appropriately.
facets.emotional: Emotional response to injustice is healthy; personal grievance is examined.
facets.cognitive: Mind discriminates between dharmic and personal anger.
facets.psychic: The soul learns to wield clean, righteous anger in service of justice, distinct from the heat of personal rage.

balanced.text:
Dharmic anger when warranted; personal anger seen through. Discrimination between the two.

Somatic: Body channels righteous indignation appropriately.

Emotional: Emotional response to injustice is healthy; personal grievance is examined.

Cognitive: Mind discriminates between dharmic and personal anger.

Psychic: The soul learns to wield clean, righteous anger in service of justice, distinct from the heat of personal rage.
balanced.vignette: When Ravi encounters genuine injustice, a clean, righteous anger rises—and he uses it, like Parashurama’s axe, to act firmly against the wrong without personal venom. The anger serves justice, not his ego; it’s a fire aimed at the injustice, and once the wrong is met, it cools.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: All personal anger rebranded as ‘righteous.’ Self-deception about motivations.
facets.somatic: Body acts out personal rage claiming righteousness.
facets.emotional: Emotional ego-anger masquerading as dharma.
facets.cognitive: Mental self-deception about anger.
facets.psychic: The soul cloaks personal rage as righteousness, its fury serving ego under cover of principle.

excess.text:
All personal anger rebranded as ‘righteous.’ Self-deception about motivations.

Somatic: Body acts out personal rage claiming righteousness.

Emotional: Emotional ego-anger masquerading as dharma.

Cognitive: Mental self-deception about anger.

Psychic: The soul cloaks personal rage as righteousness, its fury serving ego under cover of principle.
excess.vignette: Sam dresses his personal rage as righteousness—the wounded pride, the petty grievance, the wish to dominate all recast as ‘standing for justice.’ The clean righteous anger has become a cover for the dirtiest kind, and he burns bridges convinced he’s serving the good.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Anger entirely suppressed including legitimate righteous anger about real injustice. Passive complicity.
facets.somatic: Body suppresses appropriate response.
facets.emotional: Emotional suppression of legitimate indignation.
facets.cognitive: Mental refusal to acknowledge dharmic anger.
facets.psychic: The soul cannot rouse even the clean anger that genuine wrong requires, a passivity before injustice.

deficient.text:
Anger entirely suppressed including legitimate righteous anger about real injustice. Passive complicity.

Somatic: Body suppresses appropriate response.

Emotional: Emotional suppression of legitimate indignation.

Cognitive: Mental refusal to acknowledge dharmic anger.

Psychic: The soul cannot rouse even the clean anger that genuine wrong requires, a passivity before injustice.
deficient.vignette: Dan can summon no anger even at real injustice—the exploitation, the cruelty, the wrong done in front of him met with a timid passivity he calls peace. The righteous anger that should move him to act is absent; he watches wrongs proceed and does nothing, and mistakes the paralysis for equanimity.

attributes.title: Rama Lesson: Maryada (Living Within Sacred Limits)
attributes.aspect: Rama is Maryada Purushottama — the supreme example of living within sacred limits (maryada). His exile to forest at father’s word, his treatment of wife, his rule as king — all exemplify dharmic limits voluntarily honored. In daily life: keep your word, honor commitments, follow dharmic protocols even when no one is watching. Sacred limits create dignity. Sri Rama is Maryada Purushottama — the perfect man living within sacred limits. The lesson: follow dharma even when difficult; honor one’s word; live with sacred limits even when free to transgress. Daily integrity matters.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice / lesson — an avatar-modeled teaching · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, engaging the world · Causal status: Means / model · Key relation: living within sacred limits (maryādā, Rāma); kin to Svadharma (Dim 16).
coordinate.key_relation: living within sacred limits (maryādā, Rāma); kin to Svadharma (Dim 16).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Living with sacred limits — keeping word, honoring commitments, following dharma without compulsion.
facets.somatic: Body operates within ethical bounds naturally.
facets.emotional: Emotional integrity from honored commitments.
facets.cognitive: Mind committed to dharmic limits.
facets.psychic: The soul lives within sacred limits, like Rāma, finding freedom in honored boundaries rather than in their breach.

balanced.text:
Living with sacred limits — keeping word, honoring commitments, following dharma without compulsion.

Somatic: Body operates within ethical bounds naturally.

Emotional: Emotional integrity from honored commitments.

Cognitive: Mind committed to dharmic limits.

Psychic: The soul lives within sacred limits, like Rāma, finding freedom in honored boundaries rather than in their breach.
balanced.vignette: Ravi lives within wise limits—the boundaries of duty, honor, and restraint that Rama embodied—not as a cage but as the banks that give his life a course. The maryada frees rather than confines him; keeping within the sacred limits, he’s spared the chaos of a life with no shape.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Rigid moralism — following rules mechanically without wisdom. Becoming a Pharisee.
facets.somatic: Body in rigid rule-following.
facets.emotional: Emotional rigidity.
facets.cognitive: Mental fixation on rules over spirit.
facets.psychic: The soul absolutizes the rules into rigid legalism, the letter killing the spirit.

excess.text:
Rigid moralism — following rules mechanically without wisdom. Becoming a Pharisee.

Somatic: Body in rigid rule-following.

Emotional: Emotional rigidity.

Cognitive: Mental fixation on rules over spirit.

Psychic: The soul absolutizes the rules into rigid legalism, the letter killing the spirit.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned living-within-limits into a rigid legalism—so bound by rules and propriety that the spirit they were meant to serve has suffocated, every boundary observed to the letter and none to the heart. The maryada has become a cage, and he mistakes the correctness for the virtue.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Breaking promises, ignoring commitments, transgressing dharma when convenient. No sacred limits.
facets.somatic: Body transgresses freely.
facets.emotional: Emotional pattern of betrayal.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees rules as obstacles.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes no limits at all, mistaking boundlessness for freedom.

deficient.text:
Breaking promises, ignoring commitments, transgressing dharma when convenient. No sacred limits.

Somatic: Body transgresses freely.

Emotional: Emotional pattern of betrayal.

Cognitive: Mind sees rules as obstacles.

Psychic: The soul recognizes no limits at all, mistaking boundlessness for freedom.
deficient.vignette: Dan lives within no limits at all—no boundary he won’t cross for advantage, no restraint, no sacred lines, his life a formless pursuit of whatever he wants. The maryada that gives a life shape and honor is absent; without banks, his river floods everywhere and arrives nowhere.

attributes.title: Apramatto Vibhavet (The Brahman Won by the Non-Negligent)
attributes.aspect: Brahman is realized only by those who are not negligent (apramatta)—the constantly alert. Negligence is the failure of awareness; non-negligence is the constant remembrance.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: vigilance — Brahman won by the non-negligent (Katha); antagonist to Pramāda (Dim 21).
coordinate.key_relation: vigilance — Brahman won by the non-negligent (Katha); antagonist to Pramāda (Dim 21).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Continuous alertness without strain—naturally awake to one’s own presence, not lapsing into unconsciousness.
facets.somatic: Body alert but relaxed, sustained presence.
facets.emotional: Emotional alertness without anxiety.
facets.cognitive: Mind constantly aware without exhausting itself.
facets.psychic: The soul stays vigilant and non-negligent, knowing the truth is won only by sustained, wakeful attention.

balanced.text:
Continuous alertness without strain—naturally awake to one’s own presence, not lapsing into unconsciousness.

Somatic: Body alert but relaxed, sustained presence.

Emotional: Emotional alertness without anxiety.

Cognitive: Mind constantly aware without exhausting itself.

Psychic: The soul stays vigilant and non-negligent, knowing the truth is won only by sustained, wakeful attention.
balanced.vignette: Ravi stays awake to his practice—non-negligent, vigilant, keeping the thread of attention through the ordinary distractions that make most seekers drift. The alertness isn’t tense; it’s a steady wakefulness, and it’s the reason the ground he gains tends to stay gained.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Hypervigilance disguised as alertness—exhausting forced attention, no relaxation.
facets.somatic: Body tense from forced vigilance.
facets.emotional: Anxious alertness, no rest.
facets.cognitive: Mental exhaustion from forced attention.
facets.psychic: The soul’s vigilance hardens into anxious hypervigilance that can never rest.

excess.text:
Hypervigilance disguised as alertness—exhausting forced attention, no relaxation.

Somatic: Body tense from forced vigilance.

Emotional: Anxious alertness, no rest.

Cognitive: Mental exhaustion from forced attention.

Psychic: The soul’s vigilance hardens into anxious hypervigilance that can never rest.
excess.vignette: Sam’s vigilance has become an anxious hyper-alertness—monitoring every lapse, punishing every drift, so tense with the effort of never being negligent that the tension is its own negligence. The wakefulness meant to free him has become an exhausting self-surveillance.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total negligence (pramada)—drifting through life unconsciously, half-asleep even when waking, no remembrance of presence.
facets.somatic: Body in mechanical functioning, no aliveness.
facets.emotional: Emotional sleepwalking, reactive without awareness.
facets.cognitive: Mind constantly distracted, no continuity of presence.
facets.psychic: The soul drifts in negligence, the truth slipping away again and again for want of attention.

deficient.text:
Total negligence (pramada)—drifting through life unconsciously, half-asleep even when waking, no remembrance of presence.

Somatic: Body in mechanical functioning, no aliveness.

Emotional: Emotional sleepwalking, reactive without awareness.

Cognitive: Mind constantly distracted, no continuity of presence.

Psychic: The soul drifts in negligence, the truth slipping away again and again for want of attention.
deficient.vignette: Dan is heedless through and through—no vigilance, no thread of attention, his practice drifting the moment it’s out of sight. The non-negligence by which, the teaching says, the Brahman is won, is a wakefulness he’s never sustained; he means to practice and forgets by noon.

attributes.title: Choiceless Awareness (Mind as Choice-Maker)
attributes.aspect: Mind functions through choice—dividing reality into pairs and choosing one. But the pairs are one continuum. To choose love is to choose hate’s eventual arrival. Either embrace both, or remain choiceless. Both dissolve the dividing mind.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: State — an attained condition · Valence: The fruit · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: choiceless awareness — prior to the choosing mind; kin to Choicelessness (Dim 28).
coordinate.key_relation: choiceless awareness — prior to the choosing mind; kin to Choicelessness (Dim 28).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Choiceless awareness—seeing all opposites as one continuum. Mind dissolves, existence appears as one. Life and death, love and hate accepted as one process.
facets.somatic: Body relaxed without internal conflict between preferences.
facets.emotional: Emotional acceptance of all polarities, peace beyond preference.
facets.cognitive: Mind beyond duality, choiceless witness, freedom from internal civil war.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in choiceless awareness, watching preferences arise and pass without being driven by them.

balanced.text:
Choiceless awareness—seeing all opposites as one continuum. Mind dissolves, existence appears as one. Life and death, love and hate accepted as one process.

Somatic: Body relaxed without internal conflict between preferences.

Emotional: Emotional acceptance of all polarities, peace beyond preference.

Cognitive: Mind beyond duality, choiceless witness, freedom from internal civil war.

Psychic: The soul rests in choiceless awareness, watching preferences arise and pass without being driven by them.
balanced.vignette: In stillness, Ravi has tasted a choiceless awareness—a resting in what is, prior to the mind’s endless picking and rejecting. It hasn’t made him passive; it’s freed him from the exhausting compulsion to control, so he can act cleanly when action is called for.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced ‘choicelessness’ pretending no preferences while internally still divided. False detachment.
facets.somatic: Body’s natural preferences suppressed.
facets.emotional: Pretended neutrality covering real choosing.
facets.cognitive: Intellectual choicelessness without genuine realization.
facets.psychic: The soul makes ‘choicelessness’ an excuse for passivity, refusing necessary decisions.

excess.text:
Forced ‘choicelessness’ pretending no preferences while internally still divided. False detachment.

Somatic: Body’s natural preferences suppressed.

Emotional: Pretended neutrality covering real choosing.

Cognitive: Intellectual choicelessness without genuine realization.

Psychic: The soul makes ‘choicelessness’ an excuse for passivity, refusing necessary decisions.
excess.vignette: Sam invokes ‘choiceless awareness’ to dodge decisions—refusing to choose a direction or take responsibility, all dressed as spiritual non-doership. The teaching about resting prior to the divided mind has become an excuse for drift.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Constant compulsive choosing—every event triggers choice between preferences. Maximum mental noise and suffering.
facets.somatic: Body in tension between preferring/rejecting.
facets.emotional: Emotional turmoil from constant choice-making.
facets.cognitive: Mind in perpetual civil war between opposites.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly identified with the choosing mind, swept by every like and dislike.

deficient.text:
Constant compulsive choosing—every event triggers choice between preferences. Maximum mental noise and suffering.

Somatic: Body in tension between preferring/rejecting.

Emotional: Emotional turmoil from constant choice-making.

Cognitive: Mind in perpetual civil war between opposites.

Psychic: The soul is wholly identified with the choosing mind, swept by every like and dislike.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s mind is a churning factory of choice—weighing, picking, rejecting, second-guessing, never resting for an instant in simple awareness. The choiceless ground beneath the choosing mind is a stillness he’s never touched; even his rest is a deciding.

attributes.title: Right Listening (Shravana)
attributes.aspect: Shravana is not casual hearing but total listening with one’s whole being. The mind goes silent; only receptivity remains. This kind of listening allows the secret teachings (TAT TWAMASI) to penetrate the disciple’s being like seeds entering a prepared womb.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: right listening to the teaching (śravaṇa) — the first move of Jñāna.
coordinate.key_relation: right listening to the teaching (śravaṇa) — the first move of Jñāna.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Becoming pure receptivity—the whole being listening, no thinking interfering. Teachings enter as seeds into ready ground.
facets.somatic: Body completely still in listening posture, every cell receptive.
facets.emotional: Emotional openness to teaching, surrender of resistance.
facets.cognitive: Mind completely silent during listening, total presence.
facets.psychic: The soul listens rightly to the truth, receptive and attentive, letting the teaching actually land.

balanced.text:
Becoming pure receptivity—the whole being listening, no thinking interfering. Teachings enter as seeds into ready ground.

Somatic: Body completely still in listening posture, every cell receptive.

Emotional: Emotional openness to teaching, surrender of resistance.

Cognitive: Mind completely silent during listening, total presence.

Psychic: The soul listens rightly to the truth, receptive and attentive, letting the teaching actually land.
balanced.vignette: When Ravi listens—to a teaching, a friend, a hard truth—he listens all the way, receptive and open, letting the words actually land rather than rehearsing his reply. The right listening has made him teachable; things get in, and getting in, they change him.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mechanical listening pretending to be total—physically present but mentally constructing arguments or comparing with own knowledge.
facets.somatic: Body present but tense, false receptivity.
facets.emotional: Hidden resistance behind apparent openness.
facets.cognitive: Mind silently arguing while pretending to listen.
facets.psychic: The soul listens to accumulate or to debate, the hearing feeding the intellect rather than the heart.

excess.text:
Mechanical listening pretending to be total—physically present but mentally constructing arguments or comparing with own knowledge.

Somatic: Body present but tense, false receptivity.

Emotional: Hidden resistance behind apparent openness.

Cognitive: Mind silently arguing while pretending to listen.

Psychic: The soul listens to accumulate or to debate, the hearing feeding the intellect rather than the heart.
excess.vignette: Sam ‘listens’ by swallowing whatever he hears whole—no discernment, no testing, an open-mouthed credulity that mistakes receptivity for wisdom. The right listening has tipped into passivity, and he’s become an echo of whoever spoke to him last.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Fragmentary listening—physical ears hearing while mind elsewhere. No transformation possible.
facets.somatic: Body present but attention scattered.
facets.emotional: No emotional opening to teaching.
facets.cognitive: Mind running parallel commentary, not receiving.
facets.psychic: The soul never truly listens, the truth bouncing off a closed or distracted mind.

deficient.text:
Fragmentary listening—physical ears hearing while mind elsewhere. No transformation possible.

Somatic: Body present but attention scattered.

Emotional: No emotional opening to teaching.

Cognitive: Mind running parallel commentary, not receiving.

Psychic: The soul never truly listens, the truth bouncing off a closed or distracted mind.
deficient.vignette: Dan doesn’t listen at all—already knowing, already answering, the words of others bouncing off a closed and busy mind. The receptive hearing that lets a teaching land is a capacity he’s never developed; he’s present at conversations and absent from them.

attributes.title: Right Thinking (Yes-Saying)
attributes.aspect: Right thinking is not no-saying but yes-saying. Most thinking starts with ‘no’—because no strengthens ego. Right thinking reverses this: yes is the starting point; reason is used to support yes; no comes only when truly impossible to say yes.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: affirmative right thinking — the ‘yes-saying’ mind.
coordinate.key_relation: affirmative right thinking — the ‘yes-saying’ mind.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Yes-orientation as foundation. Reason is used to find how to say yes. Receptivity replaces resistance. Profound relaxation in being.
facets.somatic: Body relaxed in yes-orientation, no chronic resistance-tension.
facets.emotional: Emotional openness, warmth, embracing.
facets.cognitive: Mind oriented toward acceptance, using reason to support yes-saying.
facets.psychic: The soul meets life with a fundamental yes, an affirmation that opens rather than resists.

balanced.text:
Yes-orientation as foundation. Reason is used to find how to say yes. Receptivity replaces resistance. Profound relaxation in being.

Somatic: Body relaxed in yes-orientation, no chronic resistance-tension.

Emotional: Emotional openness, warmth, embracing.

Cognitive: Mind oriented toward acceptance, using reason to support yes-saying.

Psychic: The soul meets life with a fundamental yes, an affirmation that opens rather than resists.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has trained his mind toward the affirmative—meeting situations with a constructive ‘yes, and’ rather than a reflexive ‘no,’ building where he used to block. The right thinking isn’t naive positivity; it’s a disciplined orientation toward the possible, and it’s slowly changed the weather of his mind.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Indiscriminate yes-saying—agreeing to everything, losing discrimination. Or saying yes while inwardly resisting.
facets.somatic: Body forced into accepting what damages it.
facets.emotional: Emotional doormat, accepting abuse.
facets.cognitive: Loss of healthy critical faculty, blanket affirmation.
facets.psychic: The soul’s positivity turns to forced denial of real difficulty, a yes that papers over the no.

excess.text:
Indiscriminate yes-saying—agreeing to everything, losing discrimination. Or saying yes while inwardly resisting.

Somatic: Body forced into accepting what damages it.

Emotional: Emotional doormat, accepting abuse.

Cognitive: Loss of healthy critical faculty, blanket affirmation.

Psychic: The soul’s positivity turns to forced denial of real difficulty, a yes that papers over the no.
excess.vignette: Sam forces relentless positivity—slapping a bright thought over every dark one before it can be felt, ‘good vibes only,’ the yes-saying become a suppression. The constructive thinking has curdled into a denial, and the unfelt negatives pool underground and run him from there.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Compulsive no-saying as identity—every question first met with denial. Ego built on rejection.
facets.somatic: Body in chronic resistance, tense, defensive.
facets.emotional: Emotional walls, rejection as identity.
facets.cognitive: Mind oriented toward finding what to reject, contraction.
facets.psychic: The soul meets life with chronic negation, a reflexive no that closes every door.

deficient.text:
Compulsive no-saying as identity—every question first met with denial. Ego built on rejection.

Somatic: Body in chronic resistance, tense, defensive.

Emotional: Emotional walls, rejection as identity.

Cognitive: Mind oriented toward finding what to reject, contraction.

Psychic: The soul meets life with chronic negation, a reflexive no that closes every door.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s mind runs on ‘no’—reflexively negative, cynical, finding the flaw and the obstacle in everything, the constructive thought never arising. The affirmative orientation that builds and opens is absent; his mind is a machine for finding reasons things won’t work.

attributes.title: Tantric Approach to Desire (Awareness Not Suppression)
attributes.aspect: Authentic non-attachment is not suppression or escape but the dissolution of attraction through complete awareness. Tantra moves into desire-objects with awareness—not indulgent but observant. The full conscious experience reveals the truth that breaks the spell.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: meeting desire with awareness, not suppression (the tantric approach).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: meeting desire with awareness, not suppression (the tantric approach).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Aware engagement with desire-objects until they reveal their true nature. Attraction dissolves through complete seeing, not suppression. Genuine vairagya.
facets.somatic: Body engages without bondage, knowing the nature of objects.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from compulsive attraction, neither suppression nor indulgence.
facets.cognitive: Mind clear about nature of objects through awareness, naturally non-grasping.
facets.psychic: The soul meets desire with awareness rather than suppression, letting the energy be seen and so transformed.

balanced.text:
Aware engagement with desire-objects until they reveal their true nature. Attraction dissolves through complete seeing, not suppression. Genuine vairagya.

Somatic: Body engages without bondage, knowing the nature of objects.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from compulsive attraction, neither suppression nor indulgence.

Cognitive: Mind clear about nature of objects through awareness, naturally non-grasping.

Psychic: The soul meets desire with awareness rather than suppression, letting the energy be seen and so transformed.
balanced.vignette: When desire arises, Ravi meets it with awareness rather than either indulgence or suppression—watching the craving fully, feeling it without obeying it, letting the very seeing loosen its grip. The desire, met this way, neither rules him nor gets crushed; it’s simply, consciously, held.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Indulgence claiming to be tantric awareness—using the doctrine to justify endless engagement without real awareness.
facets.somatic: Body in indulgence claiming awareness.
facets.emotional: Emotional indulgence without genuine seeing.
facets.cognitive: Self-deception about doing ‘tantric practice’ that is just indulgence.
facets.psychic: The soul uses ‘awareness not suppression’ to license indulgence, the watching merely nominal.

excess.text:
Indulgence claiming to be tantric awareness—using the doctrine to justify endless engagement without real awareness.

Somatic: Body in indulgence claiming awareness.

Emotional: Emotional indulgence without genuine seeing.

Cognitive: Self-deception about doing ‘tantric practice’ that is just indulgence.

Psychic: The soul uses ‘awareness not suppression’ to license indulgence, the watching merely nominal.
