excess.vignette: Sam has taken ‘meet desire with awareness, not suppression’ as license to indulge every appetite—the binge, the fling, the compulsion all excused as ‘conscious experience.’ The subtle practice of aware non-suppression has become a permission slip, and the ‘awareness’ is just a thin story over the indulgence.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Closed-eye renunciation that strengthens desire—escaping to monastery while mind obsesses with desires. False vairagya.
facets.somatic: Body suppressed while mind obsesses.
facets.emotional: Suppressed emotions seeking outlet.
facets.cognitive: Mind more obsessed with what is denied than what is enjoyed.
facets.psychic: The soul only suppresses its desires, the forced denial breeding the frustration it meant to escape.

deficient.text:
Closed-eye renunciation that strengthens desire—escaping to monastery while mind obsesses with desires. False vairagya.

Somatic: Body suppressed while mind obsesses.

Emotional: Suppressed emotions seeking outlet.

Cognitive: Mind more obsessed with what is denied than what is enjoyed.

Psychic: The soul only suppresses its desires, the forced denial breeding the frustration it meant to escape.
deficient.vignette: Dan meets every desire with brute suppression—forcing it down, warring against it, treating each craving as an enemy to be crushed. The aware, non-suppressive holding that the practice teaches is unknown to him; he only knows the war, and the war binds him as tightly as the desire would.

attributes.attribute: Belief I AM What Is Sought
attributes.title: Belief I AM What Is Sought (Mukta Abhimana)
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra’s second ‘explosive Moksha-capsule’: Mukta-abhimani mukto—he who considers himself liberated IS liberated; he who considers himself bound IS bound. This is not positive thinking but recognition: the only bondage is the conviction of bondage. Convince yourself of freedom and you are free.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: holding ‘I am what is sought’ (mukta-abhimāna) — identifying with the goal.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: holding ‘I am what is sought’ (mukta-abhimāna) — identifying with the goal.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Deep conviction—not affirmation—that one is already free. The recognition transforms experience.
facets.somatic: Body lived from conviction of freedom.
facets.emotional: Emotional life as expression of innate freedom.
facets.cognitive: Mind anchored in ‘I am free’ as recognition, not assertion.
facets.psychic: The soul takes the stand that it already is what it seeks, the conviction itself loosening the search.

balanced.text:
Deep conviction—not affirmation—that one is already free. The recognition transforms experience.

Somatic: Body lived from conviction of freedom.

Emotional: Emotional life as expression of innate freedom.

Cognitive: Mind anchored in ‘I am free’ as recognition, not assertion.

Psychic: The soul takes the stand that it already is what it seeks, the conviction itself loosening the search.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to a quiet, confident sense that what he’s been seeking he already, in his depth, is—not as an ego-boast but as a settling recognition that the sought and the seeker were never two. The conviction steadies his practice; he seeks now from fullness rather than from lack.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Affirmations chanted (‘I am free, I am free’) without inner conviction.
facets.somatic: Body unchanged by verbal claims.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity contradicting affirmations.
facets.cognitive: Mental repetition without genuine conviction.
facets.psychic: The ego appropriates the stand, ‘I am the free one’ becoming spiritual inflation.

excess.text:
Affirmations chanted (‘I am free, I am free’) without inner conviction.

Somatic: Body unchanged by verbal claims.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity contradicting affirmations.

Cognitive: Mental repetition without genuine conviction.

Psychic: The ego appropriates the stand, ‘I am the free one’ becoming spiritual inflation.
excess.vignette: Sam announces he’s already liberated—‘I AM the Self, I’ve arrived’—while reacting, grasping, and suffering exactly as before. He’s grabbed the confident conviction as an ego-trophy, and the premature ‘I’m free’ is the surest sign he isn’t.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Deep conviction of bondage. ‘I am limited, I am suffering’ as core identity.
facets.somatic: Body lived from conviction of bondage.
facets.emotional: Emotional life confirms bondage.
facets.cognitive: Mind anchored in ‘I am bound.’
facets.psychic: The soul is convinced it is only a bound, seeking creature, the sought-for forever elsewhere.

deficient.text:
Deep conviction of bondage. ‘I am limited, I am suffering’ as core identity.

Somatic: Body lived from conviction of bondage.

Emotional: Emotional life confirms bondage.

Cognitive: Mind anchored in ‘I am bound.’

Psychic: The soul is convinced it is only a bound, seeking creature, the sought-for forever elsewhere.
deficient.vignette: For Dan freedom is always elsewhere—far off, other, belonging to saints and not to him—and the notion that the sought might already be his own nature is unthinkable. He seeks, if he seeks at all, from a bottomless sense of lack, and the goal recedes precisely because he’s placed it outside himself.

attributes.attribute: Mind Control as By-Product of Self-Knowledge
attributes.title: Mind Control as By-Product of Self-Knowledge (Not Practice)
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra’s distinct teaching contradicting Patanjali: don’t try to control the mind through practice (chitta-vritti-nirodha). Through direct recognition of the Self, mind-control becomes automatic. Brahma the creator says: ‘No untruth comes from my mouth, my mind never wanders—not because I cultivated these qualities but because I hold Hari in my heart.’

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: mind-control as the by-product of Self-knowledge, not a separate practice.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: mind-control as the by-product of Self-knowledge, not a separate practice.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Self-knowledge naturally producing mental stillness. No fight with mind; only recognition.
facets.somatic: Body becomes calm as Self is recognized.
facets.emotional: Emotional stillness as natural consequence.
facets.cognitive: Mind becomes still without forcing—because the witnesser-witnessed structure dissolves.
facets.psychic: The soul sees that the mind quiets of itself as self-knowledge dawns, control a fruit rather than a forced aim.

balanced.text:
Self-knowledge naturally producing mental stillness. No fight with mind; only recognition.

Somatic: Body becomes calm as Self is recognized.

Emotional: Emotional stillness as natural consequence.

Cognitive: Mind becomes still without forcing—because the witnesser-witnessed structure dissolves.

Psychic: The soul sees that the mind quiets of itself as self-knowledge dawns, control a fruit rather than a forced aim.
balanced.vignette: Ravi noticed something curious: as his self-understanding deepened, his mind grew quiet on its own—not through force, but as a by-product of seeing clearly. He’s stopped fighting his thoughts and started understanding them, and the understanding does what the fighting never could.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Claim of mind-control without genuine recognition. Forced suppression masquerading as wisdom.
facets.somatic: Body tense from suppression.
facets.emotional: Emotional pressure from forcing stillness.
facets.cognitive: Mind in war with itself.
facets.psychic: The soul wages war on the mind directly, the very effort to control it stirring it further.

excess.text:
Claim of mind-control without genuine recognition. Forced suppression masquerading as wisdom.

Somatic: Body tense from suppression.

Emotional: Emotional pressure from forcing stillness.

Cognitive: Mind in war with itself.

Psychic: The soul wages war on the mind directly, the very effort to control it stirring it further.
excess.vignette: Sam wages open war on his mind—forcing stillness, suppressing thoughts, wrestling the restlessness into a tense silence that’s really just suppression. He’s mistaken the fight for the practice, and the harder he clamps down, the more the water he’s trying to still churns underneath.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Endless mind-control practice without Self-recognition. Or no practice and no recognition.
facets.somatic: Body in repetitive practice without breakthrough.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns unchanged despite practice.
facets.cognitive: Mind being whipped through exercises with no transformation.
facets.psychic: The soul neither knows the Self nor stills the mind, its thoughts running unchecked.

deficient.text:
Endless mind-control practice without Self-recognition. Or no practice and no recognition.

Somatic: Body in repetitive practice without breakthrough.

Emotional: Emotional patterns unchanged despite practice.

Cognitive: Mind being whipped through exercises with no transformation.

Psychic: The soul neither knows the Self nor stills the mind, its thoughts running unchecked.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s mind runs him entirely—no control, no quiet, no self-knowledge that might loosen its grip, just the ceaseless churn taken as reality. That the mind might settle as a by-product of understanding is an idea he’s never met; he doesn’t fight his thoughts, but only because he can’t see them.

attributes.title: Choiceless Perception (Beyond Good-Bad Judging)
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra’s distinctive teaching: perception itself can be choiceless. Not non-perception, but perception without judgment-overlay. Events happen; the witness sees them without categorizing as good/bad. This is not moral relativism but transcendence of dualistic categorization. The witnesser perceives what is without imposing preferences.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: State — an attained condition · Valence: The fruit · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: perception beyond good-bad judging — seeing without the verdict.
coordinate.key_relation: perception beyond good-bad judging — seeing without the verdict.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Perception without immediate good/bad overlay. Events recognized in their actual nature.
facets.somatic: Body’s perceptions clear of judgment-overlay.
facets.emotional: Emotional perceptions without immediate preference.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees without categorizing.
facets.psychic: The soul perceives without the constant overlay of good-bad judgment, seeing what is before labeling it.

balanced.text:
Perception without immediate good/bad overlay. Events recognized in their actual nature.

Somatic: Body’s perceptions clear of judgment-overlay.

Emotional: Emotional perceptions without immediate preference.

Cognitive: Mind sees without categorizing.

Psychic: The soul perceives without the constant overlay of good-bad judgment, seeing what is before labeling it.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to perceive without the reflexive labeling—seeing the situation, the person, the event freshly before the mind slaps ‘good’ or ‘bad’ on it. The choiceless perception gives him a clean look at what’s actually there, and the clean look lets him respond wisely rather than react.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced choicelessness that becomes moral indifference. Refusing necessary discrimination.
facets.somatic: Body accepting harm without protection.
facets.emotional: Emotional dissociation called witnessing.
facets.cognitive: Mental refusal to make even necessary distinctions.
facets.psychic: The soul uses ‘beyond judgment’ to dull its discernment, refusing to see real harm.

excess.text:
Forced choicelessness that becomes moral indifference. Refusing necessary discrimination.

Somatic: Body accepting harm without protection.

Emotional: Emotional dissociation called witnessing.

Cognitive: Mental refusal to make even necessary distinctions.

Psychic: The soul uses ‘beyond judgment’ to dull its discernment, refusing to see real harm.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned ‘beyond good and bad’ into an amoral bypass—refusing all judgment, excusing all behavior, dismissing ordinary discernment as ‘dualistic mind.’ The freedom from compulsive labeling has become a freedom from responsibility, and he calls his indifference to right and wrong enlightenment.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Every perception immediately categorized as good/bad/pleasant/unpleasant. Continuous judgment.
facets.somatic: Body in constant judgment-driven reactions.
facets.emotional: Emotional turbulence from constant evaluation.
facets.cognitive: Mind in perpetual binary-judging.
facets.psychic: The soul perceives only through judgment, every sight instantly sorted into good and bad.

deficient.text:
Every perception immediately categorized as good/bad/pleasant/unpleasant. Continuous judgment.

Somatic: Body in constant judgment-driven reactions.

Emotional: Emotional turbulence from constant evaluation.

Cognitive: Mind in perpetual binary-judging.

Psychic: The soul perceives only through judgment, every sight instantly sorted into good and bad.
deficient.vignette: Dan judges everything the instant he sees it—every person filed as friend or enemy, every event as good or bad for him, no perception free of the reflexive verdict. The clean seeing prior to the labeling is a capacity he’s never exercised; he never meets anything as it is, only as he’s already ruled it.

attributes.title: Forget Everything (Sarva Smriti Tyaga)
attributes.aspect: Ashtavakra’s instruction to forget everything: not amnesia but release of accumulated mental content as defining identity. Memories make ‘me’; concepts make ‘understanding’; scriptures make ‘knower.’ All these can be released. What remains when everything is forgotten? Pure awareness, which is one’s nature. Forgetting is not loss but liberation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna) · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: letting go of all memory (sarva-smṛti-tyāga) into the present.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna)
coordinate.key_relation: letting go of all memory (sarva-smṛti-tyāga) into the present.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Active releasing of mental content as identity-foundation. Memory remains functional but doesn’t define self.
facets.somatic: Body lives without burden of cumulative history.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns released as identity-defining.
facets.cognitive: Mind operates without accumulated content as ‘me.’
facets.psychic: The soul learns to set down its whole accumulated story, resting in the freshness before memory.

balanced.text:
Active releasing of mental content as identity-foundation. Memory remains functional but doesn’t define self.

Somatic: Body lives without burden of cumulative history.

Emotional: Emotional patterns released as identity-defining.

Cognitive: Mind operates without accumulated content as ‘me.’

Psychic: The soul learns to set down its whole accumulated story, resting in the freshness before memory.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has learned to set down the mental clutter—the grudges, the rehearsals, the running commentary—and meet each moment a little more freshly, unburdened by the whole weight of remembered self. The ‘forgetting’ isn’t amnesia; it’s a release, and released from the past’s accumulated grip, he’s lighter and more present.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced amnesia—trying to suppress memory rather than release identification with it.
facets.somatic: Body damaged through dissociation.
facets.emotional: Emotional repression rather than release.
facets.cognitive: Mental dissociation called transcendence.
facets.psychic: The soul mistakes mere blankness or avoidance for the sacred forgetting.

excess.text:
Forced amnesia—trying to suppress memory rather than release identification with it.

Somatic: Body damaged through dissociation.

Emotional: Emotional repression rather than release.

Cognitive: Mental dissociation called transcendence.

Psychic: The soul mistakes mere blankness or avoidance for the sacred forgetting.
excess.vignette: Sam has practiced ‘forgetting everything’ into a dissociative blankness—cutting himself off from his own history, his commitments, his relationships, calling the estrangement freedom. The release of mental clutter has become a flight from his own life, and the forgetting is really an abandonment.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total identification with accumulated content. Memory IS me; concepts ARE me.
facets.somatic: Body identified through accumulated history.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns from past ARE self.
facets.cognitive: Mind crowded with content all claimed as ‘me.’
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly bound by its remembered story, unable to set anything down.

deficient.text:
Total identification with accumulated content. Memory IS me; concepts ARE me.

Somatic: Body identified through accumulated history.

Emotional: Emotional patterns from past ARE self.

Cognitive: Mind crowded with content all claimed as ‘me.’

Psychic: The soul is wholly bound by its remembered story, unable to set anything down.
deficient.vignette: Dan forgets nothing—every grudge rehearsed, every slight retained, every moment of his past dragged into his present and coloring it. The capacity to set down the accumulated clutter and meet the moment fresh is one he’s never developed; he carries the whole weight, always.

attributes.attribute: Look Deeply at the World and Dispose of It
attributes.title: Look Deeply at the World and Dispose of It
attributes.aspect: Atmananda’s profound technique: don’t flee from world or cultivate forced dispassion. Look at the world deeply enough that you SEE through it. Look at passion deeply enough that it dissolves on its own. The ego itself contains the Real—it is only expanded mind. Morality is consequence of realization, not prerequisite.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna) · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: looking deeply at the world and setting it aside — seeing through to dispose of.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna)
coordinate.key_relation: looking deeply at the world and setting it aside — seeing through to dispose of.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Deep penetrating examination of every experience, every passion, every object. Through seeing, the spell is broken.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in deep examination, not avoidance.
facets.emotional: Emotions analyzed at their root, not suppressed.
facets.cognitive: Mind looks into every aspect of experience until it sees through.
facets.psychic: The soul looks so deeply into the world that it sees through it, and so can hold it lightly and set it down.

balanced.text:
Deep penetrating examination of every experience, every passion, every object. Through seeing, the spell is broken.

Somatic: Body engaged in deep examination, not avoidance.

Emotional: Emotions analyzed at their root, not suppressed.

Cognitive: Mind looks into every aspect of experience until it sees through.

Psychic: The soul looks so deeply into the world that it sees through it, and so can hold it lightly and set it down.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has learned to look hard at the things he clings to—the possession, the status, the outcome—see clearly how fleeting each is, and, seeing, loosen his grip. The practice isn’t cold; it’s a clarifying honesty that frees him to enjoy what comes without being owned by it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive analysis becoming intellectual neurosis. Endless dissection without resolution.
facets.somatic: Body in obsessive examination-mode.
facets.emotional: Emotional life as constant case-study.
facets.cognitive: Mind dissecting endlessly without integration.
facets.psychic: The soul’s ‘seeing through’ curdles into contempt, a premature disposal of a life still to be lived.

excess.text:
Excessive analysis becoming intellectual neurosis. Endless dissection without resolution.

Somatic: Body in obsessive examination-mode.

Emotional: Emotional life as constant case-study.

Cognitive: Mind dissecting endlessly without integration.

Psychic: The soul’s ‘seeing through’ curdles into contempt, a premature disposal of a life still to be lived.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned ‘look at the world and dispose of it’ into a bitter world-rejection—examining each thing only to condemn it as worthless, disposing of his engagements, his pleasures, his ties in a sweeping nihilism. The clarifying practice has become a contempt for a life he’s decided is beneath him.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Avoiding world and passion through repression or distraction. Never looking deeply enough.
facets.somatic: Body in avoidance patterns.
facets.emotional: Emotional suppression rather than analysis.
facets.cognitive: Mind flees from genuine examination.
facets.psychic: The soul never looks deeply, taking the world’s surface as final and clinging to it whole.

deficient.text:
Avoiding world and passion through repression or distraction. Never looking deeply enough.

Somatic: Body in avoidance patterns.

Emotional: Emotional suppression rather than analysis.

Cognitive: Mind flees from genuine examination.

Psychic: The soul never looks deeply, taking the world’s surface as final and clinging to it whole.
deficient.vignette: Dan looks at nothing deeply and disposes of nothing—gripping each possession, each outcome, each attachment as though it were permanent, never once examining its transience. The clarifying look that loosens the grip is one he’s never taken; he holds the whole fleeting world in a white-knuckled fist.

attributes.title: Mauna (Inner Silence)
attributes.aspect: Mauna is not merely external silence but the inner stillness of mind. It is the absence of mental chatter, the deep quietude in which true wisdom and Self-realization can emerge. It is a form of mental austerity.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: inner silence (mauna) — the stilling of speech and mind.
coordinate.key_relation: inner silence (mauna) — the stilling of speech and mind.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Mauna is practiced wisely, a person experiences both external restraint of speech and inner stillness of mind. There is appropriate use of silence as well as speech, with deep inner quietude.
facets.somatic: Calm nervous system, healthy body from reduced stress, physical ease from inner peace, balanced respiratory function from quietude.
facets.emotional: Emotional peace from inner silence, freedom from emotional chatter, depth of feeling from stillness, emotional healing through quietude.
facets.cognitive: Mental stillness and peace, freedom from inner chatter, deep wisdom emerging from silence, clarity from quietude, contemplative depth.
facets.psychic: The soul keeps an inner silence beneath its words, the still center from which speech and rest both arise.

balanced.text:
When Mauna is practiced wisely, a person experiences both external restraint of speech and inner stillness of mind. There is appropriate use of silence as well as speech, with deep inner quietude.

Somatic: Calm nervous system, healthy body from reduced stress, physical ease from inner peace, balanced respiratory function from quietude.

Emotional: Emotional peace from inner silence, freedom from emotional chatter, depth of feeling from stillness, emotional healing through quietude.

Cognitive: Mental stillness and peace, freedom from inner chatter, deep wisdom emerging from silence, clarity from quietude, contemplative depth.

Psychic: The soul keeps an inner silence beneath its words, the still center from which speech and rest both arise.
balanced.vignette: Ravi keeps a genuine inner silence—not just quiet lips but a quiet within, a resting from the ceaseless commentary that lets a deeper stillness through. The mauna isn’t withdrawal; it’s a spaciousness he carries even into conversation, and it’s made his few words weightier and his presence calmer.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive Mauna without wisdom becomes withdrawal from necessary communication, isolation, inability to engage with others, and avoidance disguised as spiritual practice.
facets.somatic: Physical withdrawal from interaction, isolation that damages health, social disengagement affecting well-being.
facets.emotional: Emotional withdrawal and isolation, inability to connect through expression, lonely emotional state, avoidance of genuine engagement.
facets.cognitive: Mental withdrawal from world, inability to communicate when needed, false spiritual pride about silence, avoidance of necessary engagement.
facets.psychic: The soul makes silence a rigid rule or a display, the outer quiet masking an inner noise.

excess.text:
Excessive Mauna without wisdom becomes withdrawal from necessary communication, isolation, inability to engage with others, and avoidance disguised as spiritual practice.

Somatic: Physical withdrawal from interaction, isolation that damages health, social disengagement affecting well-being.

Emotional: Emotional withdrawal and isolation, inability to connect through expression, lonely emotional state, avoidance of genuine engagement.

Cognitive: Mental withdrawal from world, inability to communicate when needed, false spiritual pride about silence, avoidance of necessary engagement.

Psychic: The soul makes silence a rigid rule or a display, the outer quiet masking an inner noise.
excess.vignette: Sam performs silence—the ostentatious quiet, the meaningful non-speaking, the mauna worn as a spiritual badge—while inside the commentary roars on louder than ever. The outer silence is a costume over an inner noise, and the performance of stillness is its own kind of talking.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Absence of Mauna creates constant mental chatter, exhausting noise of mind, inability to access deeper wisdom, and superficial engagement.
facets.somatic: Nervous system dysregulation from constant inner noise, stress-related illness, exhaustion from mental chatter.
facets.emotional: Emotional exhaustion from constant inner commentary, inability to feel deeply due to noise, emotional shallowness, no depth.
facets.cognitive: Constant mental chatter, inability to be still, exhausting inner monologue, no access to deeper wisdom, scattered thinking.
facets.psychic: The soul has no inner silence, its mind talking ceaselessly even when the mouth is shut.

deficient.text:
Absence of Mauna creates constant mental chatter, exhausting noise of mind, inability to access deeper wisdom, and superficial engagement.

Somatic: Nervous system dysregulation from constant inner noise, stress-related illness, exhaustion from mental chatter.

Emotional: Emotional exhaustion from constant inner commentary, inability to feel deeply due to noise, emotional shallowness, no depth.

Cognitive: Constant mental chatter, inability to be still, exhausting inner monologue, no access to deeper wisdom, scattered thinking.

Psychic: The soul has no inner silence, its mind talking ceaselessly even when the mouth is shut.
deficient.vignette: Dan knows no silence, inner or outer—the mouth and the mind both running ceaselessly, no rest from the commentary, no still point anywhere. The inner silence that lets a deeper stillness through is a country he’s never visited; even his quiet moments are loud.

attributes.title: Tapas (Austerity)
attributes.aspect: Tapas is austerity and disciplined effort that transforms consciousness. It exists in three forms: physical (worship, cleanliness, non-violence), verbal (truthful pleasant speech, scripture study), and mental (serenity, silence, self-control). It is the fire of transformation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Physical · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: austerity (tapas) — the heat that purifies; same as the niyama (Dim 11).
coordinate.key_relation: austerity (tapas) — the heat that purifies; same as the niyama (Dim 11).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Tapas is balanced and sattvic, a person practices appropriate physical, verbal, and mental discipline that purifies and transforms. There is healthy intensity without harm.
facets.somatic: Physical discipline that builds health, body strengthened through appropriate austerity, vitality from purifying practices, balanced physical refinement.
facets.emotional: Emotional purification through discipline, emotional strength from practice, healing through sustained effort, transformed emotional patterns.
facets.cognitive: Mental purification through discipline, clarity from practice, refined thinking, transformation of mental patterns through sustained effort.
facets.psychic: The soul takes up wholesome austerity, the disciplined heat that burns away impurity and tempers the will.

balanced.text:
When Tapas is balanced and sattvic, a person practices appropriate physical, verbal, and mental discipline that purifies and transforms. There is healthy intensity without harm.

Somatic: Physical discipline that builds health, body strengthened through appropriate austerity, vitality from purifying practices, balanced physical refinement.

Emotional: Emotional purification through discipline, emotional strength from practice, healing through sustained effort, transformed emotional patterns.

Cognitive: Mental purification through discipline, clarity from practice, refined thinking, transformation of mental patterns through sustained effort.

Psychic: The soul takes up wholesome austerity, the disciplined heat that burns away impurity and tempers the will.
balanced.vignette: Ravi keeps a discipline that costs him—the cold morning practice, the declined indulgence—not to punish himself but to temper himself, and he can feel it forging something. The heat of the effort purifies rather than harms.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive Tapas (rajasic or tamasic) leads to harmful self-mortification, ego-driven austerity for display, or destructive practices that damage rather than transform.
facets.somatic: Physical damage from extreme austerity, harm to body, stress-related illness, depleted physical state, harsh practices that injure.
facets.emotional: Emotional harshness toward self, masochistic practice, ego-driven discipline, harm to emotional well-being.
facets.cognitive: Mental rigidity about practice, pride in austerity, harsh self-judgment, extremism that distorts purpose.
facets.psychic: Austerity hardens into self-punishment, the soul mistaking mortification for purification.

excess.text:
Excessive Tapas (rajasic or tamasic) leads to harmful self-mortification, ego-driven austerity for display, or destructive practices that damage rather than transform.

Somatic: Physical damage from extreme austerity, harm to body, stress-related illness, depleted physical state, harsh practices that injure.

Emotional: Emotional harshness toward self, masochistic practice, ego-driven discipline, harm to emotional well-being.

Cognitive: Mental rigidity about practice, pride in austerity, harsh self-judgment, extremism that distorts purpose.

Psychic: Austerity hardens into self-punishment, the soul mistaking mortification for purification.
excess.vignette: Ben’s austerities have tipped into self-punishment—too little sleep, too little food, too much grim endurance, all worn as a badge. The purifying fire has become a slow burning of the very self it was meant to refine.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Absence of Tapas results in lack of discipline, no transformative effort, indulgence without refinement, and inability to progress on any path.
facets.somatic: Lack of physical discipline, poor health from indulgence, no transformative practice, physical decline from no effort.
facets.emotional: Emotional indulgence without transformation, no emotional growth, slavery to feelings, no refinement of emotional patterns.
facets.cognitive: No mental discipline, scattered mind without practice, no transformation of patterns, mental indulgence in any direction.
facets.psychic: The soul shuns all discipline, soft and unrefined, its impurities never burned away.

deficient.text:
Absence of Tapas results in lack of discipline, no transformative effort, indulgence without refinement, and inability to progress on any path.

Somatic: Lack of physical discipline, poor health from indulgence, no transformative practice, physical decline from no effort.

Emotional: Emotional indulgence without transformation, no emotional growth, slavery to feelings, no refinement of emotional patterns.

Cognitive: No mental discipline, scattered mind without practice, no transformation of patterns, mental indulgence in any direction.

Psychic: The soul shuns all discipline, soft and unrefined, its impurities never burned away.
deficient.vignette: Dan avoids every discomfort as a matter of course—no cold, no hunger, no effort he can dodge—and so nothing in him is ever tested or tempered. Without any friction, his character stays soft and unformed.

attributes.title: Sravanam (Listening to Sacred Stories)
attributes.aspect: Sravanam is the first of the nine devotional practices: actively listening to sacred stories, names, and pastimes of the Divine. In daily life, this means consciously choosing what one listens to — replacing gossip, complaint, and trivial talk with stories that elevate. The Bhagavatam itself is meant to be heard, not just read. Modern equivalent: podcasts, satsangs, recorded teachings consumed daily. Sravanam means listening — to scriptural stories, divine names, accounts of devotees. In daily life: listening replaces gossip; the type of input the ears receive shapes consciousness. ‘Bhakti washes away all karma. Must be listened for 7 days’ (the traditional Saptaha format).

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: listening to the sacred stories (śravaṇam) — a limb of Bhakti.
coordinate.key_relation: listening to the sacred stories (śravaṇam) — a limb of Bhakti.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Daily sacred listening woven into life — commute, chores, meals filled with elevating content. Consciousness gradually purified.
facets.somatic: Ears used for spiritual nourishment; body’s nervous system calmed by sacred sounds.
facets.emotional: Emotional life shaped by stories of devotion, courage, surrender — gradually emotional patterns transform.
facets.cognitive: Mind continuously fed sacred content; gossip and triviality lose their hold.
facets.psychic: The soul nourishes itself on sacred story, the hearing of the Divine’s deeds turning the heart toward it.

balanced.text:
Daily sacred listening woven into life — commute, chores, meals filled with elevating content. Consciousness gradually purified.

Somatic: Ears used for spiritual nourishment; body’s nervous system calmed by sacred sounds.

Emotional: Emotional life shaped by stories of devotion, courage, surrender — gradually emotional patterns transform.

Cognitive: Mind continuously fed sacred content; gossip and triviality lose their hold.

Psychic: The soul nourishes itself on sacred story, the hearing of the Divine’s deeds turning the heart toward it.
balanced.vignette: Ravi feeds regularly on the sacred stories—the lives of the saints, the old accounts of grace—and they steady and inspire him, keeping a larger world alive in his imagination. The sravanam isn’t idle listening; the stories seep in and slowly reshape what he aspires to.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Compulsive listening without inner digestion. Switching from one teacher’s audio to another endlessly. Spiritual entertainment.
facets.somatic: Body always plugged into something; never silent.
facets.emotional: Emotional dependency on constant input.
facets.cognitive: Mental hoarding of content without transformation.
facets.psychic: The soul consumes sacred stories as entertainment, the hearing never reaching the heart.

excess.text:
Compulsive listening without inner digestion. Switching from one teacher’s audio to another endlessly. Spiritual entertainment.

Somatic: Body always plugged into something; never silent.

Emotional: Emotional dependency on constant input.

Cognitive: Mental hoarding of content without transformation.

Psychic: The soul consumes sacred stories as entertainment, the hearing never reaching the heart.
excess.vignette: Sam consumes sacred stories passively—one after another, endlessly, a spiritual entertainment that leaves him unchanged, the listening a comfortable substitute for practice. He’s mistaken hearing about the path for walking it, and the stories have become one more thing to collect.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Ears filled with gossip, complaint, news-cycle, trivial entertainment. Mind shaped by what enters through hearing.
facets.somatic: Body’s nervous system disturbed by constant noise.
facets.emotional: Emotional turbulence from low-grade content.
facets.cognitive: Mind shaped by trivia and negativity.
facets.psychic: The soul fills its ears only with idle talk, no sacred story ever turning it inward.

deficient.text:
Ears filled with gossip, complaint, news-cycle, trivial entertainment. Mind shaped by what enters through hearing.

Somatic: Body’s nervous system disturbed by constant noise.

Emotional: Emotional turbulence from low-grade content.

Cognitive: Mind shaped by trivia and negativity.

Psychic: The soul fills its ears only with idle talk, no sacred story ever turning it inward.
deficient.vignette: Dan takes in no sacred input at all—no stories of grace, no accounts of the saints, nothing to keep a larger world alive in him. The nourishing, inspiring stream that such stories offer is absent from his diet, and his imagination has narrowed to the merely practical.

attributes.title: Smaranam (Remembering the Divine)
attributes.aspect: Smaranam is continuous remembrance — keeping the Divine in mind as a background thread throughout daily activities. Not formal meditation but the practice of returning attention to the Sacred at every available moment: while waiting, walking, between tasks, during sleep onset. The thread holds even when activities require attention elsewhere. Smaranam is continuous remembrance throughout the day. Not formal meditation but the ongoing background-thread of remembering the Sacred. While working, eating, conversing — the thread continues. Like Brahma whose mind ‘never wandered because he held Hari in his heart.’

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: remembering the Divine (smaraṇam) — a limb of Bhakti.
coordinate.key_relation: remembering the Divine (smaraṇam) — a limb of Bhakti.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Continuous background remembrance threading through all activities. The Divine is the constant inner companion.
facets.somatic: Body operates while consciousness rests in remembrance.
facets.emotional: Emotional security from continuous remembrance.
facets.cognitive: Mind has a constant return-home through remembrance.
facets.psychic: The soul keeps the Divine in continual remembrance, the thread of recollection running beneath the day.

balanced.text:
Continuous background remembrance threading through all activities. The Divine is the constant inner companion.

Somatic: Body operates while consciousness rests in remembrance.

Emotional: Emotional security from continuous remembrance.

Cognitive: Mind has a constant return-home through remembrance.

Psychic: The soul keeps the Divine in continual remembrance, the thread of recollection running beneath the day.
balanced.vignette: Through his day, Ravi keeps a quiet thread of remembrance—the divine held lightly in the back of his mind amid the tasks, a warm awareness he returns to between things. The smaranam isn’t effortful; it’s become a habit of the heart, and it keeps his ordinary hours quietly companioned.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced remembrance creating anxiety about forgetting. ‘Performance’ of mindfulness.
facets.somatic: Body strained trying to maintain remembrance.
facets.emotional: Anxiety about lapses in remembrance.
facets.cognitive: Mental pressure preventing natural remembrance.
facets.psychic: The soul makes remembrance an anxious compulsion, straining to never forget.

excess.text:
Forced remembrance creating anxiety about forgetting. ‘Performance’ of mindfulness.

Somatic: Body strained trying to maintain remembrance.

Emotional: Anxiety about lapses in remembrance.

Cognitive: Mental pressure preventing natural remembrance.

Psychic: The soul makes remembrance an anxious compulsion, straining to never forget.
excess.vignette: Sam remembers the divine compulsively—mechanically repeating the name, anxiously checking that he hasn’t forgotten, the remembrance become a scrupulous duty rather than a warm return. The living thread has hardened into an obligation, and the anxiety around it has crowded out the warmth.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Mind constantly forgetting the Sacred. Lost in trivia, problems, plans. Each spiritual reminder is rare and isolated.
facets.somatic: Body driven by automatic patterns.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without spiritual anchor.
facets.cognitive: Mind always elsewhere, never resting in the Sacred.
facets.psychic: The soul forgets the Divine entirely, its attention swallowed by the immediate and the urgent.

deficient.text:
Mind constantly forgetting the Sacred. Lost in trivia, problems, plans. Each spiritual reminder is rare and isolated.

Somatic: Body driven by automatic patterns.

Emotional: Emotional life without spiritual anchor.

Cognitive: Mind always elsewhere, never resting in the Sacred.

Psychic: The soul forgets the Divine entirely, its attention swallowed by the immediate and the urgent.
deficient.vignette: Dan never once thinks of the divine through his day—wholly absorbed in the tasks and the noise, no thread of remembrance, no warm return to anything sacred. The companioned quality that such remembrance can give a day is absent; his hours run on, entirely secular and alone.

attributes.title: Dasyam (Servant-Attitude)
attributes.aspect: Dasyam is the cultivated attitude of being the Divine’s servant — viewing oneself as nitya-krishna-dasa (eternally a servant of Krishna). In daily life this transforms work, responsibilities, and duties. The arrogance of ‘I am the doer’ dissolves; service becomes a privilege rather than burden. Cures both inflated self-importance and chronic resentment of duties. Dasyam is the attitude of being servant of the Divine. In daily life this transforms how one approaches work, family duties, responsibilities. ‘I am eternally a servant of Krishna’ (nitya-krishna-dasa). Every duty becomes sacred service. Cures arrogance and entitlement.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the servant-attitude (dāsyam) — a limb of Bhakti.
coordinate.key_relation: the servant-attitude (dāsyam) — a limb of Bhakti.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Servant-attitude pervading daily life. Tasks performed with dignity of sacred service, no false pride or resentment.
facets.somatic: Body engages in tasks with dignity of service.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from doer-pride and resentment.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees self as servant, not master.
facets.psychic: The soul takes the servant’s posture before the Divine, finding freedom in glad, humble service.

balanced.text:
Servant-attitude pervading daily life. Tasks performed with dignity of sacred service, no false pride or resentment.

Somatic: Body engages in tasks with dignity of service.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from doer-pride and resentment.

Cognitive: Mind sees self as servant, not master.

Psychic: The soul takes the servant’s posture before the Divine, finding freedom in glad, humble service.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has found a real gladness in the servant-attitude—offering his work and himself to the divine as a humble servant, and finding in the humility a freedom from the tyranny of his own importance. The dasyam has softened him; serving something larger, he’s lighter than when he served himself.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Self-erasing servitude — losing all healthy boundaries, becoming doormat. Codependent service.
facets.somatic: Body abused by excessive serving.
facets.emotional: Emotional self-erasure.
facets.cognitive: Mind has lost healthy individual capacity.
facets.psychic: The soul performs servitude for spiritual status, the humility a costume for pride.

excess.text:
Self-erasing servitude — losing all healthy boundaries, becoming doormat. Codependent service.

Somatic: Body abused by excessive serving.

Emotional: Emotional self-erasure.

Cognitive: Mind has lost healthy individual capacity.

Psychic: The soul performs servitude for spiritual status, the humility a costume for pride.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned the servant-attitude into a groveling self-abasement—forever unworthy, forever prostrate, a servility that’s really a kind of inverted pride in his own lowliness. The humble service has become a performance of worthlessness, and the display is its own ego.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Master-attitude in all things. Pride in achievements; resentment of any service. Entitlement.
facets.somatic: Body refuses humble tasks.
facets.emotional: Emotional entitlement and resentment of service.
facets.cognitive: Mind never sees self as servant.
facets.psychic: The soul will serve nothing greater than itself, the servant’s gladness unknown.

deficient.text:
Master-attitude in all things. Pride in achievements; resentment of any service. Entitlement.

Somatic: Body refuses humble tasks.

Emotional: Emotional entitlement and resentment of service.

Cognitive: Mind never sees self as servant.

Psychic: The soul will serve nothing greater than itself, the servant’s gladness unknown.
deficient.vignette: Dan serves nothing and no one but himself—no attitude of humble offering, no sense of being in service to anything larger, his energy flowing only toward his own advantage. The gladness of the servant-heart is a freedom he’s never tasted; he’s the master of a very small kingdom of one.

attributes.title: Sakhyam (Friendship with the Divine)
attributes.aspect: Sakhyam is cultivating friendship with the Divine — a relationship of intimate companionship rather than distant worship. Krishna is described as suhridam sarva-bhutanam, the great well-wishing friend of all beings. In daily life: confiding in the Divine as friend, joking and playing in mind, the sense of constant companionship. Profound cure for existential loneliness. Sakhyam is friendship with the Divine — like Arjuna with Krishna, intimate companionship. The Divine becomes ‘the great well-wishing friend’ (suhridam sarva-bhutanam). One is never alone. Cures existential loneliness.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: friendship with the Divine (sakhyam) — a limb of Bhakti.
coordinate.key_relation: friendship with the Divine (sakhyam) — a limb of Bhakti.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Genuine friendship-relationship with the Divine. Daily companionship through all situations.
facets.somatic: Body lived in continuous awareness of Divine company.
facets.emotional: Emotional intimacy with the Divine, freedom from loneliness.
facets.cognitive: Mind has constant Divine companion.
facets.psychic: The soul befriends the Divine, an intimate, trusting companionship that makes the sacred near and dear.

balanced.text:
Genuine friendship-relationship with the Divine. Daily companionship through all situations.

Somatic: Body lived in continuous awareness of Divine company.

Emotional: Emotional intimacy with the Divine, freedom from loneliness.

Cognitive: Mind has constant Divine companion.

Psychic: The soul befriends the Divine, an intimate, trusting companionship that makes the sacred near and dear.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to relate to the divine as a friend—intimate, easy, trusting, bringing his troubles and his joys to it as to a companion who knows him. The sakhyam has warmed his whole spiritual life; the divine is no longer a distant authority but a presence he confides in.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Familiar/casual relationship that loses reverence. Treating Divine as buddy without depth.
facets.somatic: Body too casual in approach.
facets.emotional: Emotional flippancy in relationship.
facets.cognitive: Mental loss of sacred dimension.
facets.psychic: The soul grows over-familiar, reducing the Divine to a buddy who exists to serve its wishes.

excess.text:
Familiar/casual relationship that loses reverence. Treating Divine as buddy without depth.

Somatic: Body too casual in approach.

Emotional: Emotional flippancy in relationship.

Cognitive: Mental loss of sacred dimension.

Psychic: The soul grows over-familiar, reducing the Divine to a buddy who exists to serve its wishes.
excess.vignette: Sam has grown over-familiar with the divine—presumptuous, casual to the point of contempt, treating the sacred as a buddy who owes him favors. The intimate friendship has curdled into a presumption that’s lost all reverence, and the familiarity has bred a subtle disrespect.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Distant, formal relationship with Divine. No intimacy. Spiritual life remains business-like.
facets.somatic: Body always in formal approach.
facets.emotional: Emotional distance from the Sacred.
facets.cognitive: Mind keeps the Divine at arm’s length.
facets.psychic: The soul has no felt friendship with the sacred, facing existence without a divine companion.

deficient.text:
Distant, formal relationship with Divine. No intimacy. Spiritual life remains business-like.

Somatic: Body always in formal approach.

Emotional: Emotional distance from the Sacred.

Cognitive: Mind keeps the Divine at arm’s length.

Psychic: The soul has no felt friendship with the sacred, facing existence without a divine companion.
deficient.vignette: For Dan the divine, if it exists, is utterly distant—a remote abstraction, an authority to fear or ignore, never a friend to confide in. The warm intimacy of divine friendship is foreign to him; there’s no companion in his depths, only the cold and the alone.

attributes.title: Trusteeship (Use What You Need, Hold Rest in Trust)
attributes.aspect: Suta Goswami’s foundational instruction for householders: live as trustee, not owner. Use what is necessary for legitimate maintenance; the rest is held in trust for the Divine and for those in need. This is not poverty or asceticism but conscious stewardship. Reverses ordinary consumer mentality without forcing renunciation. Suta Goswami’s daily-life instruction: ‘Do all action for the pleasure of the Lord. Whatever is necessary for maintenance, use that much; the rest hold as trustee.’ Revolutionary economics — one is not owner but steward.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: using only what one needs, holding the rest in trust; kin to Aparigraha (Dim 10).
coordinate.key_relation: using only what one needs, holding the rest in trust; kin to Aparigraha (Dim 10).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Living as trustee — using what’s needed, holding rest in trust for others/the Divine. Generosity natural.
facets.somatic: Body’s needs met without excess accumulation.
facets.emotional: Emotional security from non-possessive orientation.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees all possessions as held in trust.
facets.psychic: The soul holds its possessions as a trustee, using what it needs and stewarding the rest for the whole.

balanced.text:
Living as trustee — using what’s needed, holding rest in trust for others/the Divine. Generosity natural.

Somatic: Body’s needs met without excess accumulation.

Emotional: Emotional security from non-possessive orientation.

Cognitive: Mind sees all possessions as held in trust.

Psychic: The soul holds its possessions as a trustee, using what it needs and stewarding the rest for the whole.
balanced.vignette: Ravi holds what he has as a trustee—using what he genuinely needs and treating the rest as held in trust for others and for the larger good. The trusteeship has freed him from the anxiety of ownership; nothing is quite ‘his’ to clutch, and the not-clutching is a lightness.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Refusing to use even necessary things, ascetic miserliness disguised as trusteeship.
facets.somatic: Body deprived of legitimate needs.
facets.emotional: Emotional rigidity around possessions.
facets.cognitive: Mental hyperconcern about not using anything.
facets.psychic: The soul makes ‘trusteeship’ a rationalization for keeping everything, the trust merely nominal.

excess.text:
Refusing to use even necessary things, ascetic miserliness disguised as trusteeship.

Somatic: Body deprived of legitimate needs.

Emotional: Emotional rigidity around possessions.

Cognitive: Mental hyperconcern about not using anything.

Psychic: The soul makes ‘trusteeship’ a rationalization for keeping everything, the trust merely nominal.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned trusteeship into an anxious self-denial—refusing even what he needs, treating every use as a theft from others, so scrupulous about ‘holding in trust’ that he neglects himself. The freeing stewardship has become a joyless deprivation dressed as virtue.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Consumer mentality — accumulate, possess, claim, hoard. Maximum entanglement.
facets.somatic: Body driven by acquisition.
facets.emotional: Emotional identification with possessions.
facets.cognitive: Mind in constant calculation about more.
facets.psychic: The soul grasps all it has as personal property, the very idea of stewardship absent.

deficient.text:
Consumer mentality — accumulate, possess, claim, hoard. Maximum entanglement.

Somatic: Body driven by acquisition.

Emotional: Emotional identification with possessions.

Cognitive: Mind in constant calculation about more.

Psychic: The soul grasps all it has as personal property, the very idea of stewardship absent.
deficient.vignette: Dan treats everything he has as absolutely his own—to hoard, to spend, to keep, no sense that any of it is held in trust for anyone beyond himself. The lightness of the trustee, owning nothing finally, is foreign to him; he grips his possessions as extensions of himself, and the gripping is heavy.

attributes.attribute: Aspire to Long Life for Sacred Hearing
attributes.title: Aspire to Long Life for Sacred Hearing
attributes.aspect: Suta Goswami’s reframing of life-purpose: ‘Don’t live life for desire-fulfillment; live to attain satchitananda state.’ The Bhagavatam tells stories of the Lord’s 24 incarnations; one needs adequate time to hear, contemplate, integrate all this. Long life is desired not for more pleasure but for more spiritual unfolding. ‘Aspire to live a long life, to listen to the whole story of Bhagavan. Bhagavan took 24 lives.’ Reframes life-purpose: not desire-fulfillment but ample time for sacred listening, learning, devotional development.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: aspiring to long life for the sake of sacred hearing.
coordinate.key_relation: aspiring to long life for the sake of sacred hearing.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Life-aspiration reframed around spiritual unfolding. Long life desired for fuller devotional development.
facets.somatic: Body cared for as vehicle of spiritual development.
facets.emotional: Emotional life oriented around sacred learning.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees longevity in service of spirit.
facets.psychic: The soul wishes for length of days not to indulge but to keep hearing and serving the sacred.

balanced.text:
Life-aspiration reframed around spiritual unfolding. Long life desired for fuller devotional development.

Somatic: Body cared for as vehicle of spiritual development.

Emotional: Emotional life oriented around sacred learning.

Cognitive: Mind sees longevity in service of spirit.

Psychic: The soul wishes for length of days not to indulge but to keep hearing and serving the sacred.
balanced.vignette: Ravi values a long life not for its comforts but for its opportunity—more years to hear the truth, to practice, to deepen—and tends his health with that larger purpose in view. The aspiration isn’t clinging; it’s a wish for time to do the one thing that matters, and it dignifies even his morning walk.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Obsessive longevity-seeking, health-anxiety, ascetic body-preservation.
facets.somatic: Body in obsessive health protocols.
facets.emotional: Emotional anxiety about mortality.
facets.cognitive: Mental fixation on life-extension.
facets.psychic: The soul clings to long life out of fear of death, the aspiration soured by attachment.

excess.text:
Obsessive longevity-seeking, health-anxiety, ascetic body-preservation.

Somatic: Body in obsessive health protocols.

Emotional: Emotional anxiety about mortality.

Cognitive: Mental fixation on life-extension.

Psychic: The soul clings to long life out of fear of death, the aspiration soured by attachment.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned the wish for long life into a morbid life-extension obsession—the supplements, the anxious health-monitoring, the terror of death—clinging to the years for their own sake. The aspiration for time to hear the sacred has become a frantic grasping at mere survival.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Life pursued only for desire-fulfillment. Body abused; longevity not valued.
facets.somatic: Body abused through pleasure-pursuit.
facets.emotional: Emotional pursuit of momentary gratification.
facets.cognitive: Mind seeks only immediate satisfaction.
facets.psychic: The soul wastes its days on the trivial, its span never dedicated to anything sacred.

deficient.text:
Life pursued only for desire-fulfillment. Body abused; longevity not valued.

Somatic: Body abused through pleasure-pursuit.

Emotional: Emotional pursuit of momentary gratification.

Cognitive: Mind seeks only immediate satisfaction.

Psychic: The soul wastes its days on the trivial, its span never dedicated to anything sacred.
deficient.vignette: Dan squanders his life with no sense of its opportunity—the years spent on distraction and appetite, no aspiration to use his time for anything sacred, his health neglected and his days frittered. That a long life might be precious as a chance for the deepest hearing is a value he’s never held.

attributes.title: Reading Bhagavata in Saptaha (7-Day Format)
attributes.aspect: The traditional Bhagavata Saptaha is a 7-day continuous narration. In modern daily life, this principle adapts: schedule periods of immersion in sacred study — annual 7-day retreat; weekly multi-hour study; daily 30 minutes. The cumulative effect transforms life. Even non-Hindus benefit from immersive engagement with this remarkable text. The Bhagavatam is traditionally heard in 7-day intensive (saptaha) format. In daily life: schedule intensive immersion periods — annual retreat, weekly study, daily reading. Even just 30 minutes daily of reading Bhagavatam transforms life over months.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual, toward the Divine · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the seven-day reading of the Bhāgavata (saptāha).
coordinate.key_relation: the seven-day reading of the Bhāgavata (saptāha).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Regular immersion in sacred study — daily reading plus periodic intensive immersion. Cumulative transformation.
facets.somatic: Body in regular study posture and rhythm.
facets.emotional: Emotional life shaped by sacred stories.
facets.cognitive: Mind enriched by continuous engagement.
facets.psychic: The soul immerses itself in concentrated sacred reading, the seven-day saptāha steeping it in the Divine.

balanced.text:
Regular immersion in sacred study — daily reading plus periodic intensive immersion. Cumulative transformation.

Somatic: Body in regular study posture and rhythm.

Emotional: Emotional life shaped by sacred stories.

Cognitive: Mind enriched by continuous engagement.

Psychic: The soul immerses itself in concentrated sacred reading, the seven-day saptāha steeping it in the Divine.
balanced.vignette: When Ravi immerses himself in the seven-day reading of the sacred text, something in him is genuinely reordered—the concentrated dive into the teaching washing him, for that week, into a clearer and more devotional state. The saptaha isn’t ritual box-ticking; it’s a real immersion, and he surfaces changed.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Compulsive study without integration. Reading rather than living the teaching.
facets.somatic: Body always reading, never practicing.
facets.emotional: Emotional life around scripture without transformation.
facets.cognitive: Mental hoarding of content.
facets.psychic: The soul makes the reading a mechanical ritual to complete, the immersion never reaching the heart.

excess.text:
Compulsive study without integration. Reading rather than living the teaching.

Somatic: Body always reading, never practicing.

Emotional: Emotional life around scripture without transformation.

Cognitive: Mental hoarding of content.

Psychic: The soul makes the reading a mechanical ritual to complete, the immersion never reaching the heart.
excess.vignette: Sam completes the seven-day reading as a mechanical merit-earning ritual—racing through the verses, checking the box, convinced the mere completion confers the fruit regardless of any inner engagement. The transformative immersion has become a spiritual transaction, and the words pass through him untouched.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No regular sacred study. Spiritual life without textual nourishment. Disconnect from tradition.
facets.somatic: Body never engaged in sustained study.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without scriptural input.
facets.cognitive: Mind without sacred reading.
facets.psychic: The soul never immerses itself in sacred reading, the scriptures unopened and untasted.

deficient.text:
No regular sacred study. Spiritual life without textual nourishment. Disconnect from tradition.

Somatic: Body never engaged in sustained study.

Emotional: Emotional life without scriptural input.

Cognitive: Mind without sacred reading.

Psychic: The soul never immerses itself in sacred reading, the scriptures unopened and untasted.
deficient.vignette: Dan engages no sacred text at all—no immersion, no concentrated reading, no dive into the teaching that might reorder him. The washing that such a focused practice can give is one he’s never received; the sacred literature sits unopened, and he stays exactly as he was.

attributes.title: Attunement With Reality (Yoga as Resonance)
attributes.aspect: Attunement with Reality is the foundational orientation of all spiritual practice. Yoga is not seeking another world but tuning to the actual nature of THIS world correctly. Most suffering comes from being out-of-tune with reality—either denying it or imagining it differently than it is.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: yoga as resonance — attunement with reality.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: yoga as resonance — attunement with reality.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Living in continuous attunement to actual reality—what is, not what should be. Acceptance combined with engagement.
facets.somatic: Body in tune with natural rhythms, biological needs honored, no fighting with body.
facets.emotional: Emotional life in tune with what actually is, not denying or projecting.
facets.cognitive: Mind aligned with reality, no constant fight against what is.
facets.psychic: The soul attunes itself to the real, yoga as a resonance in which the inner string vibrates with the cosmic.

balanced.text:
Living in continuous attunement to actual reality—what is, not what should be. Acceptance combined with engagement.

Somatic: Body in tune with natural rhythms, biological needs honored, no fighting with body.

Emotional: Emotional life in tune with what actually is, not denying or projecting.

Cognitive: Mind aligned with reality, no constant fight against what is.

Psychic: The soul attunes itself to the real, yoga as a resonance in which the inner string vibrates with the cosmic.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has come to sense yoga as a kind of resonance—an attunement of himself to the deeper rhythm of reality, so that he moves with the grain of things rather than against it. When he’s in tune, life flows; the attunement isn’t passivity but a sensitive alignment, and it’s smoothed his whole way of being.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Fatalistic ‘acceptance’ of harmful conditions as ‘reality’—mistaking passive resignation for attunement.
facets.somatic: Body neglected as ‘this is just reality.’
facets.emotional: Emotional surrender to unacceptable situations.
facets.cognitive: Mental abdication of agency under guise of attunement.
facets.psychic: The soul forces a felt ‘attunement,’ chasing peak resonant states rather than living in tune.

excess.text:
Fatalistic ‘acceptance’ of harmful conditions as ‘reality’—mistaking passive resignation for attunement.

Somatic: Body neglected as ‘this is just reality.’

Emotional: Emotional surrender to unacceptable situations.

Cognitive: Mental abdication of agency under guise of attunement.

Psychic: The soul forces a felt ‘attunement,’ chasing peak resonant states rather than living in tune.
excess.vignette: Sam forces attunement—straining to be ‘in flow,’ manufacturing the resonance, so effortfully trying to align with reality that the trying is itself the discord. The natural resonance can’t be forced, and his forcing keeps him precisely out of the tune he’s grasping for.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Constant fight with reality—wanting things to be different, imagining alternate worlds. Maximum suffering.
facets.somatic: Body in chronic stress from fighting natural rhythms.
facets.emotional: Emotional turmoil from rejecting what is.
facets.cognitive: Mental civil war against reality, no peace possible.
facets.psychic: The soul lives wholly out of tune, jarring against reality at every turn.

deficient.text:
Constant fight with reality—wanting things to be different, imagining alternate worlds. Maximum suffering.

Somatic: Body in chronic stress from fighting natural rhythms.

Emotional: Emotional turmoil from rejecting what is.

Cognitive: Mental civil war against reality, no peace possible.

Psychic: The soul lives wholly out of tune, jarring against reality at every turn.
deficient.vignette: Dan is chronically out of tune with reality—resisting the grain of things, fighting what is, forever pushing against currents he refuses to feel. The resonant attunement that lets life flow is foreign to him; he meets the world as an obstacle, and the friction wears him down.

attributes.title: Right Relationships Between Things
attributes.aspect: All of existence is relational. There are no isolated entities; everything stands in relation to everything else. Wrong relationships (clinging, aversion, control, exploitation) create suffering. Right relationships (reverence, appropriateness, balance) create harmony.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: seeing the right relationships between things — harmony as knowledge.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: seeing the right relationships between things — harmony as knowledge.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: All relationships—with self, body, others, things, cosmos—established appropriately. Harmony as natural result.
facets.somatic: Right relationship with body—neither indulgent nor harsh.
facets.emotional: Right relationships with others—neither codependent nor isolated.
facets.cognitive: Mind in right relationship with thoughts—neither suppressing nor identified.
facets.psychic: The soul perceives the right relationship of each thing to each, and so moves through life in harmony with the whole.

balanced.text:
All relationships—with self, body, others, things, cosmos—established appropriately. Harmony as natural result.

Somatic: Right relationship with body—neither indulgent nor harsh.

Emotional: Right relationships with others—neither codependent nor isolated.

Cognitive: Mind in right relationship with thoughts—neither suppressing nor identified.

Psychic: The soul perceives the right relationship of each thing to each, and so moves through life in harmony with the whole.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has developed a feel for the right relationship between things—what belongs with what, what serves what, the fitting order of his priorities and his ties. The sense of right relationship gives his life coherence; things are, more and more, in their proper places, and the order is a quiet strength.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Rigid ‘correct relationship’ rules—formulaic ethics without genuine attunement.
facets.somatic: Body in mechanical ‘proper’ postures.
facets.emotional: Emotional relationships forced into formulas.
facets.cognitive: Mental rules without genuine wisdom.
facets.psychic: The soul over-systematizes relationships into rigid schemes, the living web reduced to a diagram.

excess.text:
Rigid ‘correct relationship’ rules—formulaic ethics without genuine attunement.

Somatic: Body in mechanical ‘proper’ postures.

Emotional: Emotional relationships forced into formulas.

Cognitive: Mental rules without genuine wisdom.

Psychic: The soul over-systematizes relationships into rigid schemes, the living web reduced to a diagram.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned the sense of right order into a compulsive systematizing—everything ranked, categorized, put in its rigid place, the living relationships flattened into a grid he controls. The feel for fitting order has hardened into a need to file reality, and the filing has replaced the feeling.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Wrong relationships everywhere—exploitative, codependent, hostile. Suffering pervasive.
facets.somatic: Body abused through wrong relationship.
facets.emotional: Toxic emotional relationships.
facets.cognitive: Mind in wrong relationship with itself and others.
facets.psychic: The soul sees only disconnected things, blind to the relationships that bind them into a whole.

deficient.text:
Wrong relationships everywhere—exploitative, codependent, hostile. Suffering pervasive.

Somatic: Body abused through wrong relationship.

Emotional: Toxic emotional relationships.

Cognitive: Mind in wrong relationship with itself and others.

Psychic: The soul sees only disconnected things, blind to the relationships that bind them into a whole.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no sense of right relationship at all—his priorities inverted, his ties tangled, the fitting order of things invisible to him. Without any feel for what belongs with what, his life is a jumble; nothing is in its place because he’s never sensed there were places.

attributes.title: Yoga as Balance (Samatvam Yoga Uchyate)
attributes.aspect: Yoga, in its essential meaning, is balance—not just physical postures but the equilibrium of one’s whole being. Body, breath, mind, emotion, spirit all in dynamic balance. When any dimension dominates, yoga is broken.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: ‘yoga is equanimity’ (samatvam, Gītā 2.48); kin to Samatvam (Dim 29).
coordinate.key_relation: ‘yoga is equanimity’ (samatvam, Gītā 2.48); kin to Samatvam (Dim 29).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Dynamic equilibrium across all dimensions—body, breath, mind, emotion, spirit in harmonious balance.
facets.somatic: Body balanced in posture, function, rest and activity.
facets.emotional: Emotional balance—neither suppression nor indulgence.
facets.cognitive: Mental equilibrium across all engagements.
facets.psychic: The soul lives the Gītā’s definition of yoga as evenness, equal-minded in success and failure alike.

balanced.text:
Dynamic equilibrium across all dimensions—body, breath, mind, emotion, spirit in harmonious balance.

Somatic: Body balanced in posture, function, rest and activity.

Emotional: Emotional balance—neither suppression nor indulgence.

Cognitive: Mental equilibrium across all engagements.

Psychic: The soul lives the Gītā’s definition of yoga as evenness, equal-minded in success and failure alike.
balanced.vignette: ‘Yoga is equanimity in action’—Ravi has come to live it, meeting success and failure, praise and blame from the same steady center while still acting fully. The balance isn’t withdrawal; it’s a poised engagement, and from it he works hard and stays unshaken, whichever way the results fall.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Rigid pursuit of perfect ‘balance’ becoming itself imbalance—obsessive equalization.
facets.somatic: Body forced into ‘balance’ that ignores natural variations.
facets.emotional: Emotional flatlining mistaken for balance.
facets.cognitive: Mental obsession with maintaining balance.
facets.psychic: The soul forces a flat composure, mistaking numbness for the living evenness of the sage.

excess.text:
Rigid pursuit of perfect ‘balance’ becoming itself imbalance—obsessive equalization.

Somatic: Body forced into ‘balance’ that ignores natural variations.

Emotional: Emotional flatlining mistaken for balance.

Cognitive: Mental obsession with maintaining balance.

Psychic: The soul forces a flat composure, mistaking numbness for the living evenness of the sage.
excess.vignette: Sam forces equanimity into a flat numbness—suppressing every high and low, refusing to feel the natural swing of things, mistaking a deadened indifference for the yogic balance. The living even-mindedness has become a clamped-down denial, and the suppression is its own hidden turbulence.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total imbalance—one dimension dominates at expense of others. No yoga possible.
facets.somatic: Body imbalanced—either over-exercise or sedentary, over-eat or starve.
facets.emotional: Emotional extremes without modulation.
facets.cognitive: Mental fixation on one engagement, neglect of others.
facets.psychic: The soul is tossed by every outcome, its inner balance hostage to results.

deficient.text:
Total imbalance—one dimension dominates at expense of others. No yoga possible.

Somatic: Body imbalanced—either over-exercise or sedentary, over-eat or starve.

Emotional: Emotional extremes without modulation.

Cognitive: Mental fixation on one engagement, neglect of others.

Psychic: The soul is tossed by every outcome, its inner balance hostage to results.
deficient.vignette: Dan is swept by every extreme—elated by success, crushed by failure, lifted and dropped by every turn of fortune with no steady center anywhere. The equanimity that yoga names as its very essence is a balance he’s never found; he rides the seesaw all the way up and all the way down.

attributes.title: Possessing Nothing (Aparigraha as Liberation)
attributes.aspect: We imagine we possess things—body, mind, relationships, objects, knowledge. But examination reveals we possess NOTHING; all is temporary use. Recognizing this releases the enormous burden of pseudo-ownership and opens lived experience to spontaneous freedom.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: non-possession as liberation (aparigraha); same as the yama (Dim 11).
coordinate.key_relation: non-possession as liberation (aparigraha); same as the yama (Dim 11).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Living with the recognition of non-possession. Using everything fully without claiming ownership. Profound lightness.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as temporary loan, used well without grasping.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from possessiveness in relationships.
facets.cognitive: Mind free from possession-claims about ideas, knowledge.
facets.psychic: The soul holds nothing as its own, and in possessing nothing finds itself unburdened and free.

balanced.text:
Living with the recognition of non-possession. Using everything fully without claiming ownership. Profound lightness.

Somatic: Body recognized as temporary loan, used well without grasping.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from possessiveness in relationships.

Cognitive: Mind free from possession-claims about ideas, knowledge.

Psychic: The soul holds nothing as its own, and in possessing nothing finds itself unburdened and free.
