balanced.vignette: Kira holds her possessions and her plans lightly, gives easily, and feels no clutch of dread around losing what she has. There’s a freedom in her that comes from not gripping; possessing little in spirit, she’s rich in ease.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced non-possession becoming pathological poverty, refusing legitimate use of things.
facets.somatic: Body deprived of legitimate needs in name of aparigraha.
facets.emotional: Emotional rejection of healthy attachments.
facets.cognitive: Mental rigidity around possession-rejection.
facets.psychic: The soul makes non-possession a performance of poverty, clinging to its very renunciation.

excess.text:
Forced non-possession becoming pathological poverty, refusing legitimate use of things.

Somatic: Body deprived of legitimate needs in name of aparigraha.

Emotional: Emotional rejection of healthy attachments.

Cognitive: Mental rigidity around possession-rejection.

Psychic: The soul makes non-possession a performance of poverty, clinging to its very renunciation.
excess.vignette: Determined to own nothing, Jon has renounced past the point of sense—refusing the help, the comfort, the ordinary security he actually needs—and calls the resulting deprivation ‘non-attachment.’ The freeing lightness has become a grim, self-punishing austerity.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total identification with possessions—‘this is mine’ claims about everything. Maximum bondage.
facets.somatic: Body experienced as ‘my body’ with intense ownership.
facets.emotional: Emotional possessiveness in all relationships.
facets.cognitive: Mental ownership of ideas, opinions, identities.
facets.psychic: The soul grasps and hoards, weighed down by possessions it mistakes for security.

deficient.text:
Total identification with possessions—‘this is mine’ claims about everything. Maximum bondage.

Somatic: Body experienced as ‘my body’ with intense ownership.

Emotional: Emotional possessiveness in all relationships.

Cognitive: Mental ownership of ideas, opinions, identities.

Psychic: The soul grasps and hoards, weighed down by possessions it mistakes for security.
deficient.vignette: Dana accumulates and can’t stop—more things, more savings, more control—each acquisition trailing a fresh anxiety about keeping it. She’s owned by what she owns, and the grasping that non-possession would free her from runs her entirely.

attributes.title: Renunciation (Sannyasa as Inward Movement)
attributes.aspect: Sannyasa is not external withdrawal but inner cessation of outward movement of consciousness. Every desire is movement outward; renunciation is the cessation of this movement that allows consciousness to fall back into the heart-cave.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: renunciation (sannyāsa) as an inward movement, not mere outer abandonment; kin to Nivritti (Dim 16).
coordinate.key_relation: renunciation (sannyāsa) as an inward movement, not mere outer abandonment; kin to Nivritti (Dim 16).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When renunciation is understood as cessation of outward movement, one can be in the world but not move outward. The consciousness rests in the heart-cave.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in world but not dispersed in it, energy conserved, vitality maintained.
facets.emotional: Emotional steadiness without external dependence, contentment from within.
facets.cognitive: Mind without compulsive outward movement, naturally inward, centered.
facets.psychic: The soul renounces inwardly, releasing ownership and outcome while remaining fully engaged.

balanced.text:
When renunciation is understood as cessation of outward movement, one can be in the world but not move outward. The consciousness rests in the heart-cave.

Somatic: Body engaged in world but not dispersed in it, energy conserved, vitality maintained.

Emotional: Emotional steadiness without external dependence, contentment from within.

Cognitive: Mind without compulsive outward movement, naturally inward, centered.

Psychic: The soul renounces inwardly, releasing ownership and outcome while remaining fully engaged.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has renounced inwardly while remaining fully in his life—letting go of his grip on outcomes, roles, and results even as he keeps his job and his family. The sannyasa is a movement of the heart, not a change of address; he’s renounced the clinging, not the world, and the inner freedom shows.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: External renunciation without inner transformation—escaping to Himalayas while the mind continues to desire. The world is renounced but the mind goes with it.
facets.somatic: Forced asceticism damaging body, neglect of legitimate physical needs.
facets.emotional: Emotional escape disguised as renunciation, suppressed desires creating tension, false saintliness.
facets.cognitive: Mind continues desiring while body is in monastery, dichotomy between outer and inner.
facets.psychic: The soul renounces only outwardly, dropping the robes and roles while the grasping continues within.

excess.text:
External renunciation without inner transformation—escaping to Himalayas while the mind continues to desire. The world is renounced but the mind goes with it.

Somatic: Forced asceticism damaging body, neglect of legitimate physical needs.

Emotional: Emotional escape disguised as renunciation, suppressed desires creating tension, false saintliness.

Cognitive: Mind continues desiring while body is in monastery, dichotomy between outer and inner.

Psychic: The soul renounces only outwardly, dropping the robes and roles while the grasping continues within.
excess.vignette: Sam has made a great show of outer renunciation—the robes, the abandoned career, the dramatic leaving—while inwardly as attached, as grasping, as ego-driven as ever. He’s changed his address and not his heart, and the costume of renunciation clothes an unrenounced self.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No renunciation at all means complete outward dispersal—energy constantly moving toward objects, consciousness never returning to center.
facets.somatic: Body exhausted by constant pursuit, energy dissipated, depletion.
facets.emotional: Emotional life constantly outward, no inner anchor, dependence on external.
facets.cognitive: Mind always elsewhere, never present, scattered in desires.
facets.psychic: The soul renounces nothing, clutching every possession, role, and result as its own.

deficient.text:
No renunciation at all means complete outward dispersal—energy constantly moving toward objects, consciousness never returning to center.

Somatic: Body exhausted by constant pursuit, energy dissipated, depletion.

Emotional: Emotional life constantly outward, no inner anchor, dependence on external.

Cognitive: Mind always elsewhere, never present, scattered in desires.

Psychic: The soul renounces nothing, clutching every possession, role, and result as its own.
deficient.vignette: Dan has renounced nothing—gripping his roles, his possessions, his outcomes with both hands, the inward letting-go the teaching describes never begun. The renunciation that frees a person while they stand in their own life is a movement he’s never made; he holds everything, tightly, always.

attributes.title: Heart Purification through Positive Accumulation
attributes.aspect: Heart purification is reversing the habit of negative accumulation. The same events that create misery can be approached positively—the person who was angry helped you study anger, helped you discover the witnessing capacity.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: purifying the heart by accumulating the positive (virtue and devotion).
coordinate.key_relation: purifying the heart by accumulating the positive (virtue and devotion).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Heart accumulates positives—gratitude, beauty, witnessing. Negative experiences are seen as opportunities for awareness. The heart becomes pure, capable of meditation.
facets.somatic: Open chest, healthy heart, easy breathing, relaxed nervous system.
facets.emotional: Emotional positivity not as denial but as transformation, gratitude flowing naturally, peace.
facets.cognitive: Mind oriented toward learning from every experience, freedom from victim-thinking.
facets.psychic: The soul purifies its heart by steadily accumulating good — kindness, service, devotion — until the dross is crowded out.

balanced.text:
Heart accumulates positives—gratitude, beauty, witnessing. Negative experiences are seen as opportunities for awareness. The heart becomes pure, capable of meditation.

Somatic: Open chest, healthy heart, easy breathing, relaxed nervous system.

Emotional: Emotional positivity not as denial but as transformation, gratitude flowing naturally, peace.

Cognitive: Mind oriented toward learning from every experience, freedom from victim-thinking.

Psychic: The soul purifies its heart by steadily accumulating good — kindness, service, devotion — until the dross is crowded out.
balanced.vignette: Ravi purifies his heart by cultivation—deliberately growing the positive qualities, the gratitude, the goodwill, the devotion, crowding out the negative by filling the space with the good. The positive accumulation isn’t naive; it’s a gardener’s patience, and slowly the weeds have less and less room.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: False positivity that denies legitimate pain—spiritual bypassing where one refuses to acknowledge real hurt, claiming gratitude for everything mechanically.
facets.somatic: Body holding suppressed emotions while face shows positivity, somatic symptoms.
facets.emotional: Emotional denial mistaken for positivity, suppressed grievances, fake spirituality.
facets.cognitive: Forced positive thinking that doesn’t address real issues, denial mechanism.
facets.psychic: The soul keeps a proud ledger of its merits, the accounting feeding the ego it would purify.

excess.text:
False positivity that denies legitimate pain—spiritual bypassing where one refuses to acknowledge real hurt, claiming gratitude for everything mechanically.

Somatic: Body holding suppressed emotions while face shows positivity, somatic symptoms.

Emotional: Emotional denial mistaken for positivity, suppressed grievances, fake spirituality.

Cognitive: Forced positive thinking that doesn’t address real issues, denial mechanism.

Psychic: The soul keeps a proud ledger of its merits, the accounting feeding the ego it would purify.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned cultivating virtue into spiritual materialism—collecting practices and qualities like merit badges, tracking his ‘progress,’ accumulating the good for the status it confers. The heart-purification has become an acquisition project, and the ego is quietly feeding on the very virtues meant to thin it.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Chronic negative accumulation—every slight is remembered, gratitude is impossible, heart becomes diseased with collected hurts.
facets.somatic: Heart problems, chest tension, body manifesting accumulated resentment.
facets.emotional: Bitter heart, chronic resentment, inability to forgive, emotional toxicity.
facets.cognitive: Mind ruminating on grievances, victim-mentality, life as constant suffering.
facets.psychic: The soul makes no effort at purification, the heart left cluttered with its old accumulations.

deficient.text:
Chronic negative accumulation—every slight is remembered, gratitude is impossible, heart becomes diseased with collected hurts.

Somatic: Heart problems, chest tension, body manifesting accumulated resentment.

Emotional: Bitter heart, chronic resentment, inability to forgive, emotional toxicity.

Cognitive: Mind ruminating on grievances, victim-mentality, life as constant suffering.

Psychic: The soul makes no effort at purification, the heart left cluttered with its old accumulations.
deficient.vignette: Dan cultivates nothing—no gratitude grown, no goodwill practiced, no positive quality deliberately accumulated, the heart left to whatever weeds the day sows. The patient cultivation that purifies a heart by filling it with the good is a practice he’s never begun; his inner garden runs wild.

attributes.title: Nyasa (Cosmic Placement Practice)
attributes.aspect: Nyasa is the meditative practice of identifying parts of one’s body with corresponding parts of the cosmic/divine body (e.g., dawn=head, sun=eyes, wind=breath, fire=mouth). Through systematic placement, the subject-object distinction collapses. This converts every ritual act into contemplation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: cosmic placement (nyāsa) — installing the divine in the body.
coordinate.key_relation: cosmic placement (nyāsa) — installing the divine in the body.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Systematic Nyasa practice—each body part recognized as cosmic. Worshipper becomes worshipped through placement-identification.
facets.somatic: Body experienced as cosmos in miniature—each part with cosmic correspondence.
facets.emotional: Emotional unity with cosmic being through bodily identification.
facets.cognitive: Mind dissolves subject-object distinction through placement contemplation.
facets.psychic: The soul places the divine powers consciously throughout its body and life, consecrating the whole as sacred ground.

balanced.text:
Systematic Nyasa practice—each body part recognized as cosmic. Worshipper becomes worshipped through placement-identification.

Somatic: Body experienced as cosmos in miniature—each part with cosmic correspondence.

Emotional: Emotional unity with cosmic being through bodily identification.

Cognitive: Mind dissolves subject-object distinction through placement contemplation.

Psychic: The soul places the divine powers consciously throughout its body and life, consecrating the whole as sacred ground.
balanced.vignette: In his practice, Ravi places the sacred deliberately through his body and his day—a touch, a breath, an intention that consecrates the ordinary, making his very hands and moments an offering. The nyasa isn’t superstition; it’s a way of remembering the sacred in the flesh, and it’s re-enchanted his embodied life.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Mechanical placement-ritualism without genuine identification—going through motions.
facets.somatic: Body in mechanical posture without inner correspondence.
facets.emotional: Emotional flatness despite ritual performance.
facets.cognitive: Conceptual placement without realization of unity.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on the ritual mechanics of placement, the consecration becoming empty technique.

excess.text:
Mechanical placement-ritualism without genuine identification—going through motions.

Somatic: Body in mechanical posture without inner correspondence.

Emotional: Emotional flatness despite ritual performance.

Cognitive: Conceptual placement without realization of unity.

Psychic: The soul fixates on the ritual mechanics of placement, the consecration becoming empty technique.
excess.vignette: Sam performs the placement-ritual mechanically—the exact touches, the precise gestures, the rite executed to the letter with no felt sense of the sacred it’s meant to invoke. The consecrating practice has become an empty choreography, and the divine it should place through his body is entirely absent from it.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No cosmic-body correspondence recognized—body taken as isolated biological entity only.
facets.somatic: Body felt only as personal organism, no cosmic dimension.
facets.emotional: Emotional isolation from cosmic process.
facets.cognitive: Mind cannot conceive of body as cosmos-in-miniature.
facets.psychic: The soul consecrates nothing, body and life left profane and unhallowed.

deficient.text:
No cosmic-body correspondence recognized—body taken as isolated biological entity only.

Somatic: Body felt only as personal organism, no cosmic dimension.

Emotional: Emotional isolation from cosmic process.

Cognitive: Mind cannot conceive of body as cosmos-in-miniature.

Psychic: The soul consecrates nothing, body and life left profane and unhallowed.
deficient.vignette: Dan consecrates nothing—no sense of placing the sacred through his body or his day, the whole practice of hallowing the ordinary foreign to him. The re-enchantment that such placement offers is one he’s never known; his body and his hours stay flatly, entirely secular.

attributes.title: Smriti (Memory as Bridge)
attributes.aspect: Smriti is the faculty of retention that bridges time. Lower smriti chains us to past experiences and conditioning. Higher smriti (Brahma-smriti) maintains continuous remembrance of Brahman through all activities. The shift from lower to higher smriti is fundamental practice.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: memory (smṛti) as the bridge of continuity; kin to Smṛti (Dim 20).
coordinate.key_relation: memory (smṛti) as the bridge of continuity; kin to Smṛti (Dim 20).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Memory cultivated toward higher purpose—remembering the Divine through all activities. Bondage-memory progressively replaced.
facets.somatic: Body’s habit-memory progressively dharmic.
facets.emotional: Emotional memory transformed toward devotion.
facets.cognitive: Mind continuously remembers higher reality.
facets.psychic: The soul uses memory as a bridge, carrying its learning and recollection of the Divine forward without being bound by the past.

balanced.text:
Memory cultivated toward higher purpose—remembering the Divine through all activities. Bondage-memory progressively replaced.

Somatic: Body’s habit-memory progressively dharmic.

Emotional: Emotional memory transformed toward devotion.

Cognitive: Mind continuously remembers higher reality.

Psychic: The soul uses memory as a bridge, carrying its learning and recollection of the Divine forward without being bound by the past.
balanced.vignette: Ravi uses memory as a bridge—recalling a moment of grace, a line of teaching, an earlier clarity to carry him across the dry stretches back toward the sacred. The smriti isn’t nostalgia; it’s a working thread, and remembering what he’s known keeps him connected to it when the immediate sense of it fades.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Obsessive memorization as spiritual practice—accumulating recitation without transformation.
facets.somatic: Body in mechanical recitation.
facets.emotional: Emotional attachment to memorized contents.
facets.cognitive: Mind crowded with memorized material.
facets.psychic: The soul becomes lost in memory, ruminating on what was rather than living what is.

excess.text:
Obsessive memorization as spiritual practice—accumulating recitation without transformation.

Somatic: Body in mechanical recitation.

Emotional: Emotional attachment to memorized contents.

Cognitive: Mind crowded with memorized material.

Psychic: The soul becomes lost in memory, ruminating on what was rather than living what is.
excess.vignette: Sam is lost in memory—forever reliving past spiritual highs, comparing every present moment to a remembered peak, so caught in what was that he misses what is. The bridge of memory has become a place he’s stuck on, and the recollection of grace has crowded out its present possibility.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Forgetful living without continuous remembrance of higher reality. Bondage-memories dominate.
facets.somatic: Body following old conditioning automatically.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity from past memories.
facets.cognitive: Mind without continuous Self-remembrance.
facets.psychic: The soul’s thread of memory frays, unable to carry learning or remembrance forward at all.

deficient.text:
Forgetful living without continuous remembrance of higher reality. Bondage-memories dominate.

Somatic: Body following old conditioning automatically.

Emotional: Emotional reactivity from past memories.

Cognitive: Mind without continuous Self-remembrance.

Psychic: The soul’s thread of memory frays, unable to carry learning or remembrance forward at all.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no memory of the sacred to bridge from—no recalled grace, no retained teaching, no earlier clarity to draw on in the dry times. The thread of remembrance that could carry him back is one he’s never spun; when the immediate sense fades, there’s simply nothing to return to.

attributes.title: Brahmacharya for Realization
attributes.aspect: Brahmacharya in its essential meaning is the channeling of all life-energy toward Brahman. Mere celibacy is a small part; the whole includes diet, association, speech, study, work, sleep—all directed toward the highest. This comprehensive discipline conserves and refines energy for realization.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: continence directed to realization; same as the yama (Dim 11).
coordinate.key_relation: continence directed to realization; same as the yama (Dim 11).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: All dimensions of life integrated toward Brahman-realization. Comprehensive energy-direction.
facets.somatic: Body’s energy conserved through balanced disciplines.
facets.emotional: Emotional energy directed toward devotion.
facets.cognitive: Mental energy focused on highest pursuit.
facets.psychic: The soul conserves its vital energy for the sake of realization, the gathered force fueling the inner work.

balanced.text:
All dimensions of life integrated toward Brahman-realization. Comprehensive energy-direction.

Somatic: Body’s energy conserved through balanced disciplines.

Emotional: Emotional energy directed toward devotion.

Cognitive: Mental energy focused on highest pursuit.

Psychic: The soul conserves its vital energy for the sake of realization, the gathered force fueling the inner work.
balanced.vignette: Kavi conserves his vital energy for the goal—neither repressing his desires nor squandering himself on every impulse—so his force is available for the deep work of realization. The brahmacharya isn’t grim; it’s a wise husbanding of power, and the gathered energy fuels his practice.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced extreme renunciation in name of Brahmacharya—creating breakdown.
facets.somatic: Body damaged through extreme celibacy and asceticism.
facets.emotional: Emotional repression creating distortion.
facets.cognitive: Mental rigidity around Brahmacharya rules.
facets.psychic: The soul turns continence into grim repression, at war with its own vitality.

excess.text:
Forced extreme renunciation in name of Brahmacharya—creating breakdown.

Somatic: Body damaged through extreme celibacy and asceticism.

Emotional: Emotional repression creating distortion.

Cognitive: Mental rigidity around Brahmacharya rules.

Psychic: The soul turns continence into grim repression, at war with its own vitality.
excess.vignette: Ben’s continence has hardened into a war on the body—every desire a threat, every pleasure a fall—until his practice has gone rigid and joyless. The discipline meant to conserve his energy for the goal is starving it, and the life-denial has drained the vitality it claimed to protect.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Energy dissipated in all directions—no integration toward highest purpose.
facets.somatic: Body’s energy scattered in compulsive pursuits.
facets.emotional: Emotional life dispersed in many directions.
facets.cognitive: Mind without focusing principle.
facets.psychic: The soul squanders its energy, leaving no reserve for the realization it claims to seek.

deficient.text:
Energy dissipated in all directions—no integration toward highest purpose.

Somatic: Body’s energy scattered in compulsive pursuits.

Emotional: Emotional life dispersed in many directions.

Cognitive: Mind without focusing principle.

Psychic: The soul squanders its energy, leaving no reserve for the realization it claims to seek.
deficient.vignette: Dan spends himself on every appetite as it arises, with no governor at all, and comes to his practice depleted, wondering where his energy went. Nothing is conserved for the goal, so nothing accumulates; the force that realization requires is given away, daily, before it can gather.

attributes.title: Shatsampatti (Six-fold Foundation Wealth)
attributes.aspect: Shatsampatti is the spiritual seeker’s six-fold wealth: 1) Sama - control of mind from objects; 2) Dama - control of senses; 3) Uparati - withdrawal from external practices when no longer needed; 4) Titiksha - patient endurance of heat-cold, pleasure-pain; 5) Samadhana - one-pointed concentration; 6) Shraddha - faith in teacher and scripture. Together, foundational treasure.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: the sixfold foundation-wealth (ṣaṭ-sampatti); same as the six treasures (Dim 23).
coordinate.key_relation: the sixfold foundation-wealth (ṣaṭ-sampatti); same as the six treasures (Dim 23).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: All six qualities developed in balanced way. Solid foundation for genuine spiritual inquiry.
facets.somatic: Body disciplined through dama and titiksha.
facets.emotional: Emotional stability through sama, samadhana.
facets.cognitive: Mental foundation of shraddha and viveka complete.
facets.psychic: The soul cultivates the six inner treasures — calm, control, withdrawal, endurance, faith, focus — as the wealth that steadies the path.

balanced.text:
All six qualities developed in balanced way. Solid foundation for genuine spiritual inquiry.

Somatic: Body disciplined through dama and titiksha.

Emotional: Emotional stability through sama, samadhana.

Cognitive: Mental foundation of shraddha and viveka complete.

Psychic: The soul cultivates the six inner treasures — calm, control, withdrawal, endurance, faith, focus — as the wealth that steadies the path.
balanced.vignette: Ravi cultivates the six-fold foundation—calm, restraint, withdrawal, forbearance, faith, and focus—and the six together give his practice a stable base the way wealth gives a life security. The virtues aren’t abstractions to him; they’re a foundation he’s laid, brick by brick, and everything else rests on them.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Rigid pursuit of qualities as virtue-collection rather than living foundation.
facets.somatic: Body forced into perfectionism around qualities.
facets.emotional: Emotional self-evaluation against the six.
facets.cognitive: Mental measuring of self against six qualities obsessively.
facets.psychic: The soul forces the six, the cultivation becoming strained and brittle.

excess.text:
Rigid pursuit of qualities as virtue-collection rather than living foundation.

Somatic: Body forced into perfectionism around qualities.

Emotional: Emotional self-evaluation against the six.

Cognitive: Mental measuring of self against six qualities obsessively.

Psychic: The soul forces the six, the cultivation becoming strained and brittle.
excess.vignette: Sam forces the six virtues grimly—manufacturing calm, white-knuckling restraint, performing a faith he doesn’t feel—until the qualities that should arise naturally have become one more set of demands. The foundation-wealth, forced, has turned into its own strain.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: None of the six qualities cultivated. Foundationless spirituality.
facets.somatic: Body undisciplined, no dama or titiksha.
facets.emotional: Emotional instability, no sama.
facets.cognitive: Mind without shraddha or samadhana.
facets.psychic: The soul cultivates none of the treasures, its practice without scaffolding and quick to collapse.

deficient.text:
None of the six qualities cultivated. Foundationless spirituality.

Somatic: Body undisciplined, no dama or titiksha.

Emotional: Emotional instability, no sama.

Cognitive: Mind without shraddha or samadhana.

Psychic: The soul cultivates none of the treasures, its practice without scaffolding and quick to collapse.
deficient.vignette: Dan lacks the six-fold foundation entirely—no calm, no restraint, no faith, no focus—and so his practice, whenever he attempts it, has no base to stand on. The foundational wealth that makes deeper work possible is undeveloped; he builds on sand, and it keeps sliding.

attributes.title: Yonimudra (Inner Vision Practice)
attributes.aspect: Yonimudra is a specific Kriya technique where the senses are sealed and inner vision opens. Combined with Pranayam, tongue-to-palate (kechari), bindu/sound listening, and star-meditation—these five form the operative essence of the entire Gita according to Lahiri. The technique is not philosophical but technical—body-engineering for spiritual experience.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the inner-vision seal (yoni-mudrā); kin to the mudrās (Dim 26).
coordinate.key_relation: the inner-vision seal (yoni-mudrā); kin to the mudrās (Dim 26).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Proper yonimudra practice with initiation. Inner visions arise naturally.
facets.somatic: Body’s sensory channels sealed; inner perception activated.
facets.emotional: Emotional withdrawal from outer engagement, inner peace.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees inner reality directly.
facets.psychic: The soul turns the senses inward through the seal, withdrawing perception to behold the light within.

balanced.text:
Proper yonimudra practice with initiation. Inner visions arise naturally.

Somatic: Body’s sensory channels sealed; inner perception activated.

Emotional: Emotional withdrawal from outer engagement, inner peace.

Cognitive: Mind sees inner reality directly.

Psychic: The soul turns the senses inward through the seal, withdrawing perception to behold the light within.
balanced.vignette: Turning his senses inward with the practice, Ravi finds an inner vision opening—a subtle attention to the light and space within that the outer world usually drowns out. The yoni-mudra is a threshold for him; sealing the outer, he discovers an inner landscape he’d never had the quiet to notice.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forcing visions, attachment to inner phenomena, mistaking imagination for sight.
facets.somatic: Eye strain from forced practice.
facets.emotional: Attachment to inner visions.
facets.cognitive: Mind constructs imagined visions.
facets.psychic: The soul chases the inner visions the practice yields, mistaking phenomena for realization.

excess.text:
Forcing visions, attachment to inner phenomena, mistaking imagination for sight.

Somatic: Eye strain from forced practice.

Emotional: Attachment to inner visions.

Cognitive: Mind constructs imagined visions.

Psychic: The soul chases the inner visions the practice yields, mistaking phenomena for realization.
excess.vignette: Sam chases inner visions through the practice obsessively—straining for lights and images, judging each session by its phenomena, hunting the spectacle rather than resting in the awareness behind it. The inward-turning has become a grab for experiences, and the grasping obscures the very vision he’s after.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Practice without proper initiation, or no practice at all. Inner vision dimension closed.
facets.somatic: Body not engaged in subtle sensory withdrawal.
facets.emotional: Emotional life entirely external.
facets.cognitive: Mind without access to inner vision.
facets.psychic: The soul never turns the senses inward, its perception streaming wholly outward.

deficient.text:
Practice without proper initiation, or no practice at all. Inner vision dimension closed.

Somatic: Body not engaged in subtle sensory withdrawal.

Emotional: Emotional life entirely external.

Cognitive: Mind without access to inner vision.

Psychic: The soul never turns the senses inward, its perception streaming wholly outward.
deficient.vignette: Dan never turns his senses inward at all—the gaze always out, on the world and the screen, the inner landscape never once explored. The inner vision the practice opens is a dimension he’s never entered; there is, for him, only the outer, and no threshold in.

attributes.title: Sushumna Activation (Central Channel)
attributes.aspect: Through Kriya practice, the Sushumna (central channel along the spine) becomes activated. Energy moves up from the base toward the head. Lahiri identifies Arjuna’s bow Gandiva as the activated Sushumna. This is concrete energy work—not metaphor. The practitioner experiences energy moving in specific patterns.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: activating the central channel (suṣumnā); kin to the nāḍīs (Dim 6).
coordinate.key_relation: activating the central channel (suṣumnā); kin to the nāḍīs (Dim 6).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Gradual, balanced sushumna activation through proper Kriya practice. Energy flowing smoothly.
facets.somatic: Body experiences subtle energy movements along spine.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns transformed through energy work.
facets.cognitive: Mind clarified by sushumna activation.
facets.psychic: The soul awakens the central channel, energy rising along the spine to gather toward the higher centers.

balanced.text:
Gradual, balanced sushumna activation through proper Kriya practice. Energy flowing smoothly.

Somatic: Body experiences subtle energy movements along spine.

Emotional: Emotional patterns transformed through energy work.

Cognitive: Mind clarified by sushumna activation.

Psychic: The soul awakens the central channel, energy rising along the spine to gather toward the higher centers.
balanced.vignette: In deep practice, Ravi senses his energy gathering and rising up the central channel—a subtle, clarifying current that lifts his whole state toward stillness and light. The sushumna-activation isn’t forced; it’s what happens when the energy is balanced and the mind quiet, and it draws him upward on its own.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced kundalini practices creating disturbance. Sushumna activation without preparation.
facets.somatic: Body in pranic distress.
facets.emotional: Emotional volatility from forced activation.
facets.cognitive: Mental disturbance from improper energy work.
facets.psychic: The soul forces the activation, straining for energetic experiences and destabilizing itself.

excess.text:
Forced kundalini practices creating disturbance. Sushumna activation without preparation.

Somatic: Body in pranic distress.

Emotional: Emotional volatility from forced activation.

Cognitive: Mental disturbance from improper energy work.

Psychic: The soul forces the activation, straining for energetic experiences and destabilizing itself.
excess.vignette: Sam forces his energy up the central channel aggressively—the violent breathwork, the strained locks, the willful pushing—chasing dramatic kundalini experiences and destabilizing himself in the process. The natural rising has become a dangerous forcing, and the forcing throws his whole system into disarray.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Sushumna dormant. Energy in side channels (ida-pingala) only. Spiritual progress slow.
facets.somatic: Body without central energy activation.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns unchanged.
facets.cognitive: Mind without the clarifying effect of sushumna.
facets.psychic: The soul never engages the central channel, its energy diffuse and earthbound.

deficient.text:
Sushumna dormant. Energy in side channels (ida-pingala) only. Spiritual progress slow.

Somatic: Body without central energy activation.

Emotional: Emotional patterns unchanged.

Cognitive: Mind without the clarifying effect of sushumna.

Psychic: The soul never engages the central channel, its energy diffuse and earthbound.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no awareness of any central channel or rising energy at all—the subtle energetic dimension of practice entirely off his map. The clarifying current the activation opens is inaccessible to someone who’s never sensed his own subtle energy, let alone its rising.

attributes.title: Kechari Mudra (Tongue Practice)
attributes.aspect: Kechari mudra—the practice of folding the tongue back into the nasopharynx—is part of advanced Kriya. It is said to release amrita (nectar of immortality) and to facilitate breath-stillness and meditation. Lahiri counts it among the five essential techniques. Requires proper preparation and instruction.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the tongue-lock seal (khecharī); same as Khechari Mudra (Dim 26).
coordinate.key_relation: the tongue-lock seal (khecharī); same as Khechari Mudra (Dim 26).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Gradual development of kechari through proper instruction. Tongue practice supporting deeper meditation.
facets.somatic: Tongue gradually trained without force.
facets.emotional: Emotional sweetness associated with practice.
facets.cognitive: Mind helped to deeper stillness through practice.
facets.psychic: The soul, through the advanced seal, turns awareness inward and tastes the subtle nectar of absorption.

balanced.text:
Gradual development of kechari through proper instruction. Tongue practice supporting deeper meditation.

Somatic: Tongue gradually trained without force.

Emotional: Emotional sweetness associated with practice.

Cognitive: Mind helped to deeper stillness through practice.

Psychic: The soul, through the advanced seal, turns awareness inward and tastes the subtle nectar of absorption.
balanced.vignette: In deep practice, Ravi rests the tongue back and up, and a subtle inner sweetness opens—a refinement of a very old technique, approached gently over years. The seal is a quiet doorway to a subtler layer, and he steps through it without force or fanfare.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced tongue-stretching or surgical alterations (which some have done). Damaging body for technique.
facets.somatic: Body damaged by forced practice or surgery.
facets.emotional: Emotional fixation on physical technique.
facets.cognitive: Mental obsession with kechari rather than its purpose.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on the feat and the ‘nectar’ as sensation, chasing experience over stillness.

excess.text:
Forced tongue-stretching or surgical alterations (which some have done). Damaging body for technique.

Somatic: Body damaged by forced practice or surgery.

Emotional: Emotional fixation on physical technique.

Cognitive: Mental obsession with kechari rather than its purpose.

Psychic: The soul fixates on the feat and the ‘nectar’ as sensation, chasing experience over stillness.
excess.vignette: Sam, chasing the advanced form, has taken to stretching and forcing the tongue back aggressively, obsessed with an attainment he’s read about. The subtle seal has become a grasping, sometimes harmful ordeal, the refinement lost in the forcing.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No tongue practice. Missing this aspect of Kriya engineering.
facets.somatic: Body without this supportive technique.
facets.emotional: Missing emotional benefits.
facets.cognitive: Practice lacking this particular support.
facets.psychic: The soul never seals its awareness inward, attention leaking out through the senses.

deficient.text:
No tongue practice. Missing this aspect of Kriya engineering.

Somatic: Body without this supportive technique.

Emotional: Missing emotional benefits.

Cognitive: Practice lacking this particular support.

Psychic: The soul never seals its awareness inward, attention leaking out through the senses.
deficient.vignette: Dan has never explored any subtle tongue or palate practice—the seals, the refinements, the delicate inner work entirely outside his map. He practices only the gross and physical, and the subtle doorways stay closed because he doesn’t know they’re there.

attributes.title: Pratibodha-Viditam (Self-Knowledge in Every Cognition)
attributes.aspect: When Brahman is known as the inner Self in every state of consciousness, it is known truly. Not as a separate object to be known, but as the constant witness in every act of cognition.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna) · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: Self-knowledge in every cognition (pratibodha-viditam, Kena) — awareness known in each act of knowing.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna)
coordinate.key_relation: Self-knowledge in every cognition (pratibodha-viditam, Kena) — awareness known in each act of knowing.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Continuous awareness of the witness in every act of perception—Brahman recognized as ‘me’ in every cognition.
facets.somatic: Body’s perceptions all backed by witnessing presence.
facets.emotional: Emotional states all seen against the unchanging awareness.
facets.cognitive: Every thought witnessed by the unchanging consciousness.
facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the Self in every act of knowing, awareness known in and through each cognition.

balanced.text:
Continuous awareness of the witness in every act of perception—Brahman recognized as ‘me’ in every cognition.

Somatic: Body’s perceptions all backed by witnessing presence.

Emotional: Emotional states all seen against the unchanging awareness.

Cognitive: Every thought witnessed by the unchanging consciousness.

Psychic: The soul recognizes the Self in every act of knowing, awareness known in and through each cognition.
balanced.vignette: Ravi has begun to catch it in the very act of knowing—that in each perception, each thought, the same awareness is present, lighting the cognition from within. The Self, he’s found, isn’t hidden behind experience but shining in every moment of it, and noticing this in cognition after cognition has become his practice.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced ‘witnessing’ technique becoming mechanical—missing the natural always-already presence of the witness.
facets.somatic: Strained body in ‘witnessing’ posture.
facets.emotional: Forced emotional detachment.
facets.cognitive: Mental gymnastics of trying to witness, missing the natural presence.
facets.psychic: The soul turns this into a clever idea to assert, missing the immediate recognition it points to.

excess.text:
Forced ‘witnessing’ technique becoming mechanical—missing the natural always-already presence of the witness.

Somatic: Strained body in ‘witnessing’ posture.

Emotional: Forced emotional detachment.

Cognitive: Mental gymnastics of trying to witness, missing the natural presence.

Psychic: The soul turns this into a clever idea to assert, missing the immediate recognition it points to.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned ‘the Self is known in every cognition’ into an intellectual formula he recites and analyzes—a clever metaphysical point he’s mastered and never once actually noticed in a live moment of knowing. The living recognition has become a doctrine he owns and is never moved by.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No awareness of the witness—every cognition takes the cognized object as ultimate. Complete identification.
facets.somatic: Body identified with whatever it experiences.
facets.emotional: Each emotion is ‘me,’ no witnessing presence.
facets.cognitive: Mind fully absorbed in objects, no recognition of constant witness.
facets.psychic: The soul knows only the objects of cognition, never the awareness present in every one.

deficient.text:
No awareness of the witness—every cognition takes the cognized object as ultimate. Complete identification.

Somatic: Body identified with whatever it experiences.

Emotional: Each emotion is ‘me,’ no witnessing presence.

Cognitive: Mind fully absorbed in objects, no recognition of constant witness.

Psychic: The soul knows only the objects of cognition, never the awareness present in every one.
deficient.vignette: Dan never notices the knower in his knowing—wholly absorbed in the objects of perception, the awareness that lights each cognition taken utterly for granted. That the Self might be recognized in every act of knowing is a subtlety he’s never approached; he knows a thousand things and never the knower.

attributes.attribute: Yoga as Steady Holding of Senses
attributes.title: Yoga as Steady Holding of Senses
attributes.aspect: When the five senses, mind, and intellect are still—this is the highest state, called yoga. Steady control of the senses is yoga. One must be careful, for yoga can be lost.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: yoga as the steady holding of the senses (Katha); kin to Pratyāhāra (Dim 11).
coordinate.key_relation: yoga as the steady holding of the senses (Katha); kin to Pratyāhāra (Dim 11).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Steady stilling of senses and mind without suppression. Yoga as continuously available state through careful practice.
facets.somatic: Body still, senses gathered.
facets.emotional: Emotional stillness, depth.
facets.cognitive: Mind and intellect quietly held in yoga.
facets.psychic: The soul holds the senses steady, the firm but supple restraint that keeps the mind available for the inner work.

balanced.text:
Steady stilling of senses and mind without suppression. Yoga as continuously available state through careful practice.

Somatic: Body still, senses gathered.

Emotional: Emotional stillness, depth.

Cognitive: Mind and intellect quietly held in yoga.

Psychic: The soul holds the senses steady, the firm but supple restraint that keeps the mind available for the inner work.
balanced.vignette: Ravi holds his senses steady—able to keep them gathered and quiet when depth is needed, not letting every sound and sight drag his attention away. The steady holding of the senses isn’t repression; it’s a trained composure, and it lets him rest inward while the world clamors outside.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced stillness creating rebound, violent suppression of senses.
facets.somatic: Body forced rigid.
facets.emotional: Suppressed senses creating distortion.
facets.cognitive: Mental violence against natural movement.
facets.psychic: The soul clamps the senses harshly, the forced restraint becoming its own affliction.

excess.text:
Forced stillness creating rebound, violent suppression of senses.

Somatic: Body forced rigid.

Emotional: Suppressed senses creating distortion.

Cognitive: Mental violence against natural movement.

Psychic: The soul clamps the senses harshly, the forced restraint becoming its own affliction.
excess.vignette: Sam clamps his senses shut by force—rigidly repressing every sensory pull, warring against sound and sight and taste, mistaking the tense shutting-down for control. The steady holding has become a violent suppression, and the clenching agitates the very stillness it means to protect.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No discipline of senses—they pull in all directions, mind scattered, no yoga.
facets.somatic: Body driven by sense-objects.
facets.emotional: Emotional dispersal.
facets.cognitive: Scattered mind, no yogic gathering.
facets.psychic: The soul lets the senses run free, dragged outward by every object with no restraint.

deficient.text:
No discipline of senses—they pull in all directions, mind scattered, no yoga.

Somatic: Body driven by sense-objects.

Emotional: Emotional dispersal.

Cognitive: Scattered mind, no yogic gathering.

Psychic: The soul lets the senses run free, dragged outward by every object with no restraint.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s senses run him entirely—dragged by every stimulus, unable to hold his attention steady for a moment against the outward pull. The composed holding of the senses that depth requires is a capacity he’s never developed; the world tugs, and he goes, every time.

attributes.title: Guru’s Test (Yama’s Three-Day Silence)
attributes.aspect: The genuine spiritual seeker is tested by the master’s apparent indifference. Yama’s three-day absence; the temptations he later offers Nachiketa (sons, wealth, kingdoms, celestial damsels)—all are tests. The disciple’s patient waiting and refusal of substitutes reveal true readiness.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: the teacher’s test of readiness (Yama’s three-day silence, Katha).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the teacher’s test of readiness (Yama’s three-day silence, Katha).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Patient endurance of testing, refusing substitutes for ultimate truth. Steady aspiration through delays and temptations.
facets.somatic: Body waits patiently, fasts willingly, endures discomfort.
facets.emotional: Emotional steadiness through perceived rejection, no offense at delays.
facets.cognitive: Mind refuses substitutes, holds clear vision of ultimate goal.
facets.psychic: The soul, like Naciketas waiting three days at Yama’s door, proves its earnestness through patient, steady resolve.

balanced.text:
Patient endurance of testing, refusing substitutes for ultimate truth. Steady aspiration through delays and temptations.

Somatic: Body waits patiently, fasts willingly, endures discomfort.

Emotional: Emotional steadiness through perceived rejection, no offense at delays.

Cognitive: Mind refuses substitutes, holds clear vision of ultimate goal.

Psychic: The soul, like Naciketas waiting three days at Yama’s door, proves its earnestness through patient, steady resolve.
balanced.vignette: When the teacher tested Ravi—the delay, the silence, the withholding, like Yama making Nachiketa wait three days—he persevered, patient and undeterred, and the perseverance itself proved his readiness. He met the test not as an insult but as part of the teaching, and the meeting deepened him.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Masochistic seeking of suffering as ‘test,’ creating false trials to display worthiness.
facets.somatic: Body forced into unnecessary suffering.
facets.emotional: Performative endurance for show.
facets.cognitive: Mind manufacturing tests, missing genuine readiness.
facets.psychic: The soul performs earnestness for the teacher’s approval, the patience a display.

excess.text:
Masochistic seeking of suffering as ‘test,’ creating false trials to display worthiness.

Somatic: Body forced into unnecessary suffering.

Emotional: Performative endurance for show.

Cognitive: Mind manufacturing tests, missing genuine readiness.

Psychic: The soul performs earnestness for the teacher’s approval, the patience a display.
excess.vignette: Sam bristles at any test—entitled to instruction, impatient with delay, insulted by the teacher’s withholding, demanding the fruit without the trial. The testing that’s meant to ripen him he experiences as an affront, and the entitlement is precisely the unreadiness the test reveals.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Demanding immediate response from teachers, taking offense at delay, accepting substitutes for truth.
facets.somatic: Body unable to wait or fast.
facets.emotional: Emotional offense at perceived rejection.
facets.cognitive: Mind grabbing at substitutes instead of ultimate truth.
facets.psychic: The soul has no patience for the test, abandoning the threshold before the teaching is given.

deficient.text:
Demanding immediate response from teachers, taking offense at delay, accepting substitutes for truth.

Somatic: Body unable to wait or fast.

Emotional: Emotional offense at perceived rejection.

Cognitive: Mind grabbing at substitutes instead of ultimate truth.

Psychic: The soul has no patience for the test, abandoning the threshold before the teaching is given.
deficient.vignette: Dan gives up at the first obstacle—the delay, the difficulty, the smallest withholding sending him away for good. The perseverance that a real test requires is absent; he abandons the path whenever it stops being easy, and so the deeper teaching, which only comes after the trial, never reaches him.

attributes.title: Senses Outward, Self Inward (Parak-Pratyak)
attributes.aspect: By creation’s design, senses go outward—everyone naturally looks externally. Only the rare heroic seeker (dhira), wanting immortality, turns the senses inward to behold the Pratyak-Atman (inward Self). This reversal of the natural current requires great courage.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: the senses turned outward vs the Self within (parāk / pratyāk, Katha); kin to the inward turn (Dim 28).
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: the senses turned outward vs the Self within (parāk / pratyāk, Katha); kin to the inward turn (Dim 28).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Heroic reversal of senses’ outward flow—turning the gaze within to behold the Self. Going against the natural current.
facets.somatic: Body’s senses consciously turned inward at appropriate times.
facets.emotional: Emotional courage to face inner reality, not flee to external.
facets.cognitive: Mind heroic enough to turn against natural outward tendency.
facets.psychic: The soul reverses the outward flow, drawing the senses’ attention back toward the inward Self.

balanced.text:
Heroic reversal of senses’ outward flow—turning the gaze within to behold the Self. Going against the natural current.

Somatic: Body’s senses consciously turned inward at appropriate times.

Emotional: Emotional courage to face inner reality, not flee to external.

Cognitive: Mind heroic enough to turn against natural outward tendency.

Psychic: The soul reverses the outward flow, drawing the senses’ attention back toward the inward Self.
balanced.vignette: Against the mind’s natural outward pull, Ravi has learned to turn his attention inward—the senses run out toward objects, and he, gently, runs the awareness the other way, back toward its source. The inward turn isn’t a withdrawal from life; it’s a homecoming, and he can make it at will now, even amid the noise.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Total rejection of outward life—pathological introversion, dissociation from world.
facets.somatic: Body completely withdrawn from external functioning.
facets.emotional: Emotional dissociation from all external engagement.
facets.cognitive: Mind cut off from outer reality, isolation as ‘spirituality.’
facets.psychic: The soul forces the inward turn into a rejection of all outer life, a brittle withdrawal.

excess.text:
Total rejection of outward life—pathological introversion, dissociation from world.

Somatic: Body completely withdrawn from external functioning.

Emotional: Emotional dissociation from all external engagement.

Cognitive: Mind cut off from outer reality, isolation as ‘spirituality.’

Psychic: The soul forces the inward turn into a rejection of all outer life, a brittle withdrawal.
excess.vignette: Sam has turned so far inward that the outer world has gone dim and neglected—withdrawn into his interior, the senses shut, the balance the teaching urges tipped into a dissociative retreat. The inward turn has become an escape, and he’s abandoned a life still happening outside him.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Senses always rushing outward, no inward turning ever attempted. Natural creation-pattern unbroken.
facets.somatic: Body always engaged externally, never inward.
facets.emotional: Emotional life entirely externally directed.
facets.cognitive: Mind never turns inward, knows only outer objects.
facets.psychic: The soul’s senses run wholly outward, the inward Self never sought.

deficient.text:
Senses always rushing outward, no inward turning ever attempted. Natural creation-pattern unbroken.

Somatic: Body always engaged externally, never inward.

Emotional: Emotional life entirely externally directed.

Cognitive: Mind never turns inward, knows only outer objects.

Psychic: The soul’s senses run wholly outward, the inward Self never sought.
deficient.vignette: Dan’s attention runs only outward—flung after every object, never once turned back toward the awareness doing the looking. The inward turn against the senses’ outward flow is a movement he’s never made; he’s all searchlight and never once the source of the light.

attributes.title: Approach to Guru (Tadvijnanartham)
attributes.aspect: For knowing that Truth, one must approach a guru with samit (fuel) in hand—a knower of Brahman, well-established in scriptures. Such approach requires humility, readiness to serve.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: the right approach to the teacher (tadvijñānārtham, Muṇḍaka); kin to Śraddhā (Dim 23).
coordinate.key_relation: the right approach to the teacher (tadvijñānārtham, Muṇḍaka); kin to Śraddhā (Dim 23).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Approaching the wise teacher with humility, ready to serve and learn. Discrimination plus surrender.
facets.somatic: Body in attitude of service and reverence.
facets.emotional: Emotional openness to teaching with discrimination.
facets.cognitive: Mind ready to receive while still discerning teacher’s genuineness.
facets.psychic: The soul approaches a true teacher humbly, fuel in hand, ready to serve and to learn.

balanced.text:
Approaching the wise teacher with humility, ready to serve and learn. Discrimination plus surrender.

Somatic: Body in attitude of service and reverence.

Emotional: Emotional openness to teaching with discrimination.

Cognitive: Mind ready to receive while still discerning teacher’s genuineness.

Psychic: The soul approaches a true teacher humbly, fuel in hand, ready to serve and to learn.
balanced.vignette: Ravi approaches his teacher rightly—humble, serving, genuinely wanting to know, offering himself as a student rather than demanding as a customer. The right approach opens the transmission; because he comes to learn rather than to be impressed, what the teacher has actually reaches him.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Blind teacher-worship, mindless surrender to anyone claiming knowledge.
facets.somatic: Body in slavish service.
facets.emotional: Emotional dependence on guru.
facets.cognitive: Mental abdication, blind belief.
facets.psychic: The soul approaches the teacher to test or to be seen, the humility absent.

excess.text:
Blind teacher-worship, mindless surrender to anyone claiming knowledge.

Somatic: Body in slavish service.

Emotional: Emotional dependence on guru.

Cognitive: Mental abdication, blind belief.

Psychic: The soul approaches the teacher to test or to be seen, the humility absent.
excess.vignette: Sam has dissolved into servile guru-dependence—no judgment of his own, every thought deferred, his whole self handed over in a submission that’s abolished his discernment. The right humility before a teacher has become a self-erasure, and in surrendering his mind entirely he’s lost the very faculty the teaching was meant to sharpen.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Refusing all teachers, claiming self-sufficiency. Missing the crucial transmission.
facets.somatic: Body never in service-attitude.
facets.emotional: Emotional pride preventing learning.
facets.cognitive: Mental arrogance, no genuine learning.
facets.psychic: The soul approaches no teacher at all, certain it can find the way alone.

deficient.text:
Refusing all teachers, claiming self-sufficiency. Missing the crucial transmission.

Somatic: Body never in service-attitude.

Emotional: Emotional pride preventing learning.

Cognitive: Mental arrogance, no genuine learning.

Psychic: The soul approaches no teacher at all, certain it can find the way alone.
deficient.vignette: Dan approaches no teacher—too proud to be a student, certain he can figure it all out alone, dismissing every guru as fraud or crutch. The humble, serving approach that opens transmission is one he refuses on principle, and so the guidance that could save him years never has a chance to reach him.

attributes.title: Tapas-Brahmacharya-Shraddha (Three Pillars)
attributes.aspect: Through truth, austerity, right knowledge, brahmacharya (continence), and meditation is this Self perceived. By those who have purified themselves through inner discipline.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: the three pillars — austerity, continence, and faith.
coordinate.key_relation: the three pillars — austerity, continence, and faith.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Integrated practice of truth, austerity, knowledge, continence, meditation—the multi-faceted preparation.
facets.somatic: Body disciplined yet vital, brahmacharya conserved.
facets.emotional: Emotional purification through practice.
facets.cognitive: Mind clear through integrated practice.
facets.psychic: The soul stands on the three pillars — disciplined heat, conserved energy, living faith — and the practice holds firm.

balanced.text:
Integrated practice of truth, austerity, knowledge, continence, meditation—the multi-faceted preparation.

Somatic: Body disciplined yet vital, brahmacharya conserved.

Emotional: Emotional purification through practice.

Cognitive: Mind clear through integrated practice.

Psychic: The soul stands on the three pillars — disciplined heat, conserved energy, living faith — and the practice holds firm.
balanced.vignette: Ravi keeps the three pillars firm—a tempering austerity, a conserving self-restraint, and a steady faith—and the three together hold his practice up the way three legs hold a stool. None dominates; austerity, brahmacharya, and shraddha work as one foundation, and the foundation is solid.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Extreme practices in any one direction—harsh celibacy, brutal tapas, rigid truth.
facets.somatic: Body damaged by extreme practice.
facets.emotional: Emotional suppression through harsh discipline.
facets.cognitive: Mental rigidity from extremism.
facets.psychic: The soul over-leans on one pillar, harsh austerity or blind faith, and the balance is lost.

excess.text:
Extreme practices in any one direction—harsh celibacy, brutal tapas, rigid truth.

Somatic: Body damaged by extreme practice.

Emotional: Emotional suppression through harsh discipline.

Cognitive: Mental rigidity from extremism.

Psychic: The soul over-leans on one pillar, harsh austerity or blind faith, and the balance is lost.
excess.vignette: Sam overdoes one pillar and neglects the rest—all grim austerity and no faith, or all fervent faith and no restraint—and the imbalance tips the whole structure. The three that were meant to hold each other up now lean, one overbuilt and the others crumbling, and the practice wobbles.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No spiritual discipline of any kind—life of indulgence, no preparation for Self-realization.
facets.somatic: Undisciplined body, depleted vitality.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without purification.
facets.cognitive: Mind without practice or preparation.
facets.psychic: The soul has none of the three, its practice without discipline, reserve, or conviction.

deficient.text:
No spiritual discipline of any kind—life of indulgence, no preparation for Self-realization.

Somatic: Undisciplined body, depleted vitality.

Emotional: Emotional life without purification.

Cognitive: Mind without practice or preparation.

Psychic: The soul has none of the three, its practice without discipline, reserve, or conviction.
deficient.vignette: Dan has none of the three pillars—no austerity, no restraint, no real faith—and so his practice, whenever he attempts it, has nothing to stand on. The foundation the three provide is unbuilt; the structure of a spiritual life has no legs, and it never rises.

attributes.title: Daharakasha (Small Space in the Heart)
attributes.aspect: Within the city of Brahman (the body) is the small lotus of the heart. Within it is a small space (daharakasha). What is in this small space contains all this—heaven, earth, fire, wind, sun, moon, lightning, stars. All your desires, all your knowledge are there. It is unaffected by old age and death.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: meditation on the small space in the heart (daharākāśa); same as Dahara Vidya (Dim 25).
coordinate.key_relation: meditation on the small space in the heart (daharākāśa); same as Dahara Vidya (Dim 25).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Heart-space meditation reveals the infinite within—the heart-cave contains all worlds. Genuine inner discovery.
facets.somatic: Body becomes vehicle to access inner space, heart center activated.
facets.emotional: Emotional discovery of vast inner space, emotional contentment.
facets.cognitive: Mind enters the heart-space and discovers infinity within.
facets.psychic: The soul discovers the vast space within the small cave of the heart, the whole cosmos found within.

balanced.text:
Heart-space meditation reveals the infinite within—the heart-cave contains all worlds. Genuine inner discovery.

Somatic: Body becomes vehicle to access inner space, heart center activated.

Emotional: Emotional discovery of vast inner space, emotional contentment.

Cognitive: Mind enters the heart-space and discovers infinity within.

Psychic: The soul discovers the vast space within the small cave of the heart, the whole cosmos found within.
balanced.vignette: In the small space within the heart, Amara has found something impossibly large—an inner vastness that, contemplated daily, has become a sanctuary she carries everywhere. However cramped the outer day, she can drop into that spacious inner room, and it holds her.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced visualization claiming heart-space discovery, mistaking imagination for realization.
facets.somatic: Body strained in forced visualization.
facets.emotional: Sentimental imagination without genuine discovery.
facets.cognitive: Concept-based visualization without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on heart-experiences and sensations, chasing the symbol over the awareness.

excess.text:
Forced visualization claiming heart-space discovery, mistaking imagination for realization.

Somatic: Body strained in forced visualization.

Emotional: Sentimental imagination without genuine discovery.

Cognitive: Concept-based visualization without realization.

Psychic: The soul fixates on heart-experiences and sensations, chasing the symbol over the awareness.
excess.vignette: Ben loves the vast inner space of the meditation so much that he’s retreated into it from the world, neglecting the relationships and duties that feel small beside it. The inner sanctuary has become an exit, and he lives more and more within it and less among people.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No awareness of inner space—the heart-cave unknown, all attention turned outward.
facets.somatic: Body never settled enough to discover inner space.
facets.emotional: Emotional heart unrecognized.
facets.cognitive: Mind constantly outward, missing infinite within.
facets.psychic: The soul never enters the heart-space, all its attention turned outward.

deficient.text:
No awareness of inner space—the heart-cave unknown, all attention turned outward.

Somatic: Body never settled enough to discover inner space.

Emotional: Emotional heart unrecognized.

Cognitive: Mind constantly outward, missing infinite within.

Psychic: The soul never enters the heart-space, all its attention turned outward.
deficient.vignette: Dan has no felt inner refuge; his whole identity is flung outward into the world’s demands, with no small inner space to retreat to. The vast sanctuary within the heart is, for him, an address that doesn’t exist, and so he faces every storm entirely exposed.

attributes.title: Shravana (Hearing the Truth)
attributes.aspect: Shravana is the first stage of learning—hearing truth from the realized teacher. It is not academic listening but receptive presence to the teaching, especially the great pronouncements (Mahavakyas). The student must be qualified through Sadhana Chatushtaya before shravana bears fruit.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: hearing the truth (śravaṇa) — the first of śravaṇa-manana-nididhyāsana.
coordinate.key_relation: hearing the truth (śravaṇa) — the first of śravaṇa-manana-nididhyāsana.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Receptive, sustained hearing of truth from competent teacher. Whole being listens.
facets.somatic: Body still and present during teaching.
facets.emotional: Emotional openness to receive.
facets.cognitive: Mind quiet, receptive, attentive.
facets.psychic: The soul hears the liberating truth from a worthy source, the first and indispensable step of the path.

balanced.text:
Receptive, sustained hearing of truth from competent teacher. Whole being listens.

Somatic: Body still and present during teaching.

Emotional: Emotional openness to receive.

Cognitive: Mind quiet, receptive, attentive.

Psychic: The soul hears the liberating truth from a worthy source, the first and indispensable step of the path.
balanced.vignette: When Ravi hears the truth taught—the real teaching, clearly given—he receives it fully, letting it land and take root rather than merely noting it and moving on. The shravana isn’t passive; it’s a deep, attentive taking-in, and what he hears this way begins its slow work of changing how he sees.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Endless hearing without contemplation or assimilation. Mere accumulation of teachings.
facets.somatic: Body always in classes without integration.
facets.emotional: Emotional dependence on teacher.
facets.cognitive: Mind hoarding teachings without digestion.
facets.psychic: The soul hears to accumulate doctrine, the listening feeding the intellect rather than the heart.

excess.text:
Endless hearing without contemplation or assimilation. Mere accumulation of teachings.

Somatic: Body always in classes without integration.

Emotional: Emotional dependence on teacher.

Cognitive: Mind hoarding teachings without digestion.

Psychic: The soul hears to accumulate doctrine, the listening feeding the intellect rather than the heart.
excess.vignette: Sam collects teachings the way others collect stamps—hearing everything, retaining much, applying none, the shravana become an intellectual acquisition that leaves his life untouched. He’s mistaken the volume of what he’s heard for the depth of what he’s realized, and the hearing has become its own end.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Distracted, partial hearing. Teachings don’t penetrate. No foundation for further practice.
facets.somatic: Body present but attention elsewhere.
facets.emotional: No emotional opening to teaching.
facets.cognitive: Mind constructs arguments while pretending to listen.
facets.psychic: The soul never hears the truth at all, the teaching never reaching its ears.

deficient.text:
Distracted, partial hearing. Teachings don’t penetrate. No foundation for further practice.

Somatic: Body present but attention elsewhere.

Emotional: No emotional opening to teaching.

Cognitive: Mind constructs arguments while pretending to listen.

Psychic: The soul never hears the truth at all, the teaching never reaching its ears.
deficient.vignette: Dan doesn’t really hear the teaching at all—present at the discourse, absent in attention, the truth bouncing off a closed and busy mind. The deep receptive hearing that lets a teaching take root is a capacity he’s never developed; the words pass him by, unheard and unplanted.

attributes.title: Manana (Contemplation / Reflection)
attributes.aspect: Manana is sustained reflection on what has been heard. It removes doubts through reasoning, examines apparent contradictions, integrates the teaching with experience. Without manana, shravana remains mere information. The teaching becomes one’s own through this stage.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: reflection on what is heard (manana) — the second of the three.
coordinate.key_relation: reflection on what is heard (manana) — the second of the three.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Sustained reflection on teaching. Doubts addressed through careful reasoning. Teaching becomes one’s own understanding.
facets.somatic: Body settled in contemplative posture.
facets.emotional: Emotional engagement with teaching.
facets.cognitive: Mind active in productive inquiry, doubt-removal.
facets.psychic: The soul reflects deeply on what it has heard, reasoning until doubt dissolves and the truth grows clear.

balanced.text:
Sustained reflection on teaching. Doubts addressed through careful reasoning. Teaching becomes one’s own understanding.

Somatic: Body settled in contemplative posture.

Emotional: Emotional engagement with teaching.

Cognitive: Mind active in productive inquiry, doubt-removal.

Psychic: The soul reflects deeply on what it has heard, reasoning until doubt dissolves and the truth grows clear.
balanced.vignette: After hearing a teaching, Ravi turns it over—reflecting, questioning, testing it against his own experience until it stops being borrowed and becomes his own understanding. The manana is where the teaching gets metabolized; through the patient reflection, what he heard becomes what he knows.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Over-analysis preventing assimilation. Endless intellectual mapping.
facets.somatic: Body in tension from over-analysis.
facets.emotional: Emotional dryness from over-intellectualization.
facets.cognitive: Mind generates more questions than it answers.
facets.psychic: The soul over-reasons endlessly, contemplation becoming a maze of analysis with no exit.

excess.text:
Over-analysis preventing assimilation. Endless intellectual mapping.

Somatic: Body in tension from over-analysis.

Emotional: Emotional dryness from over-intellectualization.

Cognitive: Mind generates more questions than it answers.

Psychic: The soul over-reasons endlessly, contemplation becoming a maze of analysis with no exit.
excess.vignette: Sam’s reflection never stops—turning every teaching over endlessly, spinning it into ever-finer conceptual distinctions, so absorbed in thinking about the truth that he never rests in it. The manana meant to metabolize the teaching has become a mental treadmill, and the analysis has replaced the realization.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Heard teachings accepted without reflection. Doubts unaddressed; integration impossible.
facets.somatic: Body without contemplative engagement.
facets.emotional: Emotional resistance to deep examination.
facets.cognitive: Mind passive, no genuine inquiry.
facets.psychic: The soul never reflects on what it hears, the truth received but never made its own.

deficient.text:
Heard teachings accepted without reflection. Doubts unaddressed; integration impossible.

Somatic: Body without contemplative engagement.

Emotional: Emotional resistance to deep examination.

Cognitive: Mind passive, no genuine inquiry.

Psychic: The soul never reflects on what it hears, the truth received but never made its own.
deficient.vignette: Dan reflects on nothing he hears—the teaching taken in, if at all, and never turned over, never tested, never made his own. The contemplation that metabolizes a teaching into understanding is a step he skips; whatever he hears stays borrowed and inert, and changes nothing.

attributes.title: Vairagya (Dispassion/Detachment) [Yoga Sutra Context]
attributes.aspect: Vairagya is dispassion—the consciousness of mastery over desire for seen and revealed objects. Patanjali distinguishes lower vairagya (mastery over visible objects) from higher (transcendence of all qualities of nature).

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: dispassion (vairāgya); with Abhyāsa, a wing of yoga; same as Vairāgya (Dim 23).
coordinate.key_relation: dispassion (vairāgya); with Abhyāsa, a wing of yoga; same as Vairāgya (Dim 23).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Vairagya is established, the yogi can engage with the world without being controlled by desire. Lower vairagya frees from sensory objects; higher vairagya transcends the gunas themselves.
facets.somatic: Body free from compulsive sensory seeking, healthy engagement without bondage.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from desire-driven life, peace from non-grasping, equanimity.
facets.cognitive: Mental freedom from desires, clarity, mastery over the pull of objects, eventual transcendence of all attachment.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in dispassion, free from craving without suppression, holding the world’s pleasures lightly.

balanced.text:
When Vairagya is established, the yogi can engage with the world without being controlled by desire. Lower vairagya frees from sensory objects; higher vairagya transcends the gunas themselves.
