Somatic: Body free from compulsive sensory seeking, healthy engagement without bondage.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from desire-driven life, peace from non-grasping, equanimity.

Cognitive: Mental freedom from desires, clarity, mastery over the pull of objects, eventual transcendence of all attachment.

Psychic: The soul rests in dispassion, free from craving without suppression, holding the world’s pleasures lightly.
balanced.vignette: Kira wants things—she’s human—and she’s not ruled by the wanting. She can enjoy what comes and release what goes, holding her desires loosely enough that their absence doesn’t devastate her. Her dispassion is not coldness but freedom: she’s stopped needing the world to be a certain way in order to be all right.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Misunderstood Vairagya becomes aversion and rejection, harsh disengagement from life, cold withdrawal mistaken for dispassion.
facets.somatic: Physical withdrawal from healthy engagement, neglect of body, ascetic damage.
facets.emotional: Emotional coldness disguised as detachment, false dispassion, rejection of warmth.
facets.cognitive: Mental rejection rather than transcendence, false superiority, escape from engagement.
facets.psychic: The soul forces a cold detachment, renunciation driven by aversion rather than understanding.

excess.text:
Misunderstood Vairagya becomes aversion and rejection, harsh disengagement from life, cold withdrawal mistaken for dispassion.

Somatic: Physical withdrawal from healthy engagement, neglect of body, ascetic damage.

Emotional: Emotional coldness disguised as detachment, false dispassion, rejection of warmth.

Cognitive: Mental rejection rather than transcendence, false superiority, escape from engagement.

Psychic: The soul forces a cold detachment, renunciation driven by aversion rather than understanding.
excess.vignette: Karl has renounced desire so thoroughly that he’s renounced life. Pleasure is suspect, attachment a weakness, warmth a leak—and behind the disciplined detachment is a man gone cold and grey, mistaking the suppression of his heart for its liberation. His ‘dispassion’ is really a quiet war on being alive.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Absence of Vairagya means being driven by every desire, no mastery over impulses, slavery to seen and revealed objects, no foundation for yoga.
facets.somatic: Body controlled by desires and impulses, no mastery, sensory slavery.
facets.emotional: Emotional bondage to desires, no peace from grasping, suffering from attachment.
facets.cognitive: Mental slavery to wanting, no freedom, perpetual seeking without satisfaction, no yogic foundation.
facets.psychic: The soul is ruled by craving, mistaking the next pleasure for the home it longs for.

deficient.text:
Absence of Vairagya means being driven by every desire, no mastery over impulses, slavery to seen and revealed objects, no foundation for yoga.

Somatic: Body controlled by desires and impulses, no mastery, sensory slavery.

Emotional: Emotional bondage to desires, no peace from grasping, suffering from attachment.

Cognitive: Mental slavery to wanting, no freedom, perpetual seeking without satisfaction, no yogic foundation.

Psychic: The soul is ruled by craving, mistaking the next pleasure for the home it longs for.
deficient.vignette: Dan is at the mercy of every craving. The desire arises and he obeys—the purchase, the fling, the impulse followed the instant it appears—with no space between wanting and grabbing. The mind is simply ruled by its appetites, and he mistakes the obedience for freedom.

attributes.title: Kaivalya (Liberation/Aloneness)
attributes.aspect: Kaivalya is the ultimate goal of yoga—the absolute isolation/aloneness of pure consciousness from all that is not itself. The Self (Purusha) abides in its own true nature, freed from identification with prakriti.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State / goal — liberation as aloneness (kaivalya) · Valence: The goal · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: The final aim of the Yoga path (Dim 11); the Self resting in itself.
coordinate.key_relation: The final aim of the Yoga path (Dim 11); the Self resting in itself.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Kaivalya is achieved, consciousness rests in its own pure nature, free from all identification. This is the final liberation Patanjali points to.
facets.somatic: Body fully recognized as not-self, instrument used without identification, complete freedom from body-identification.
facets.emotional: Complete emotional liberation, freedom from all conditional happiness/unhappiness, abiding bliss.
facets.cognitive: Pure consciousness aware of itself, complete freedom from mental modifications, ultimate liberation, mind fully transcended.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in kaivalya, the aloneness of pure awareness abiding in its own nature, free of all entanglement.

balanced.text:
When Kaivalya is achieved, consciousness rests in its own pure nature, free from all identification. This is the final liberation Patanjali points to.

Somatic: Body fully recognized as not-self, instrument used without identification, complete freedom from body-identification.

Emotional: Complete emotional liberation, freedom from all conditional happiness/unhappiness, abiding bliss.

Cognitive: Pure consciousness aware of itself, complete freedom from mental modifications, ultimate liberation, mind fully transcended.

Psychic: The soul rests in kaivalya, the aloneness of pure awareness abiding in its own nature, free of all entanglement.
balanced.vignette: After the long work, Ravi has come to rest in a profound aloneness that isn’t loneliness—the Self standing free in its own nature, needing nothing, complete. The kaivalya isn’t isolation from others; it’s an inner sufficiency so total that his relationships, freed of need, have actually grown warmer.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Premature claims of Kaivalya without realization create spiritual delusion, dangerous self-deception, false transcendence claims.
facets.somatic: Body neglect from false transcendence claims, ungrounded existence.
facets.emotional: Emotional bypassing claiming liberation, false transcendence of legitimate human emotions.
facets.cognitive: Spiritual delusion claiming realization, dangerous self-deception, ego-claim of awakening.
facets.psychic: The soul grasps at kaivalya as a state to attain, the seeking itself an entanglement.

excess.text:
Premature claims of Kaivalya without realization create spiritual delusion, dangerous self-deception, false transcendence claims.

Somatic: Body neglect from false transcendence claims, ungrounded existence.

Emotional: Emotional bypassing claiming liberation, false transcendence of legitimate human emotions.

Cognitive: Spiritual delusion claiming realization, dangerous self-deception, ego-claim of awakening.

Psychic: The soul grasps at kaivalya as a state to attain, the seeking itself an entanglement.
excess.vignette: Sam announces he’s attained kaivalya—the final liberation, the isolated freedom—while reacting, needing, and suffering exactly as before. He’s grabbed the name of the summit from the foothills, and the very claiming, needy of recognition, betrays how far the aloneness-in-freedom still is.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Absence of any aspiration toward Kaivalya means no orientation toward ultimate freedom, life lived in complete bondage without higher purpose.
facets.somatic: Body completely identified with as self, no transcendence orientation.
facets.emotional: Total emotional bondage, no aspiration for freedom, complete enslavement to conditions.
facets.cognitive: Mind completely identified with contents, no awareness of pure consciousness, no liberation orientation.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly entangled in the play of nature, the standalone awareness never glimpsed.

deficient.text:
Absence of any aspiration toward Kaivalya means no orientation toward ultimate freedom, life lived in complete bondage without higher purpose.

Somatic: Body completely identified with as self, no transcendence orientation.

Emotional: Total emotional bondage, no aspiration for freedom, complete enslavement to conditions.

Cognitive: Mind completely identified with contents, no awareness of pure consciousness, no liberation orientation.

Psychic: The soul is wholly entangled in the play of nature, the standalone awareness never glimpsed.
deficient.vignette: Dan is bound at every point—dependent, grasping, defined entirely by his relationships and circumstances, with no felt sense of a Self that could stand free in its own nature. The kaivalya that crowns the whole path isn’t a freedom he’s failing to reach; it’s one he’s never once glimpsed as even possible.
